sa_verse: ॐ तपः स्वाध्यायनिरतं तपस्वी वाग्विदां वरम्। नारदं परिपप्रच्छ वाल्मीकिर्मुनिपुङ्गवम्॥
en: The ascetic Vālmīki asked Nārada, the best of sages and foremost of those conversant with words, ever engaged in austerities and Vedic studies.

sa_verse: कोन्वस्मिन् साम्प्रतं लोके गुणवान् कश्च वीर्यवान्। धर्मज्ञश्च कृतज्ञश्च सत्यवाक्यो दृढत्नतः॥
en: Who at present in this world is like crowned with qualities, and with prowess, knowing duty, and grateful, and truthful, and firm in vow.

sa_verse: चारित्रेण च को युक्तः सर्वभूतेषु को हितः। विद्वान् कः कः समर्थश्च कश्चैकप्रियदर्शनः॥
en: Who is qualified by virtue of his character, and who is engaged in the welfare of all creatures? Who is learned and capable. Who alone is ever lovely to behold?

sa_verse: आत्मवान् को जितक्रोधो द्युतिमान् कोऽनसूयकः। कस्य बिभ्यति देवाश्च जातरोषस्य संयुगे॥
en: Who has subdued his heart, and controlled his anger, is endowed with splendour, and devoid of malice; and whom enraged in battle, do even the gods, fear?

sa_verse: एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं परं कौतूहलं हि मे। महर्षे त्वं समर्थोऽसि ज्ञातुमेवंविधं नरम्॥
en: I have great curiosity to hear of such a person. You can, O Maharşi,* know about a man of this description. *A great saint. The word, however, signifies one belonging to a particular order of saints.

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा चैतत्रिलोकज्ञो वाल्मीकेर्नारदो वचः। श्रूयतामिति चामन्त्र्य प्रहष्टो वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Hearing Vālmīki's words, Nărada, cognizant of the three worlds, said with alacrity, “listen to me”!

sa_verse: बहवो दुर्लभाश्चैव ये त्वया कीर्तिता गुणाः। मुने वक्ष्याम्यहं बुद्ध्वा तैर्युक्तः श्रूयतां नर।७।।
en: Rare as are the qualities mentioned by you, I will, O sage, having duly considered, describe to you a person endued with them.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकुवंशप्रभवो रामो नाम जनैः श्रुतः। नियतात्मा महावीर्यो श्रुतिमान् धृतिमान् वशी॥
en: There is one sprung from the line of Ikşvāku, known by the name of Rāma. He is of subdued soul; and is exceedingly powerful; effulgent; endowed with patience; having senses under control.

sa_verse: बुद्धिमान् नीतिमान् वाग्मी श्रीमाञ्छत्रुनिबर्हणः। विपुलांसो महाबाहुः कम्बुग्रीवो महाहनुः॥
en: He is intelligent; learned in morality; eloquent; crowned with grace; the slayer of foes: broad-shouldered; possessed of mighty arms, a conch-shaped neck, fleshy jaws.

sa_verse: महोरस्को महेष्वासो गूढजत्रुररिंदमः। आजानुबाहुः सुशिराः सुललाटः सुविक्रमः॥
en: And a broad chest; a powerful bowman; the repressor of foes; having plump shoulder-blades; of arms reaching down to his knees; with a beautiful head, and a graceful forehead; and endowed with excellent might.

sa_verse: समः समविभक्ताङ्गः स्निग्धवर्णः प्रतापवान्। पीनवक्षा विशालाक्षो लक्ष्मीवाञ्छुभलक्षणः॥
en: And having symmetrical limbs, and of a cool hue; and possessed of prowess; and having a well-developed chest; with expansive eyes; crowned with auspiciousness and favourable marks.

sa_verse: धर्मज्ञः सत्यसंधश्च प्रजानां च हिते रतः। यशस्वी ज्ञानसम्पन्नः शुचिर्वश्यः समाधिमान्॥
en: Knowing duty; firm in promise; always engaged in the good of his subjects; of accomplished renown; furnished with knowledge; pure in body and spirit; obedient towards superiors; versed in self-knowledge.

sa_verse: प्रजापतिसमः श्रीमान् धाता रिपुनिषूदनः। रक्षिता जीवलोकस्य धर्मस्य परिरक्षिता॥
en: Narada says-Like Prajapati himself; blessed with prosperity; protecting all; the destroyer of enemies, and protector of all living beings.

sa_verse: रक्षिता स्वस्य धर्मस्य स्वजनस्य च रक्षिता। वेदवेदाङ्गतत्त्वज्ञो धनुर्वेदे च निष्ठितः॥
en: Practising all the duties of his class; and preserving those cleaving to him; versed in the profundities of the Vedas and the Vedāngas; accomplished in archery.

sa_verse: सर्वशास्त्रार्थतत्वज्ञः स्मृतिमान् प्रतिभानवान्। सर्वलोकप्रियः साधुरदीनात्मा विचक्षणः॥
en: Well versed in the dicta of all the sciences; brilliant; gifted with a good memory; the darling of all; unreproved; of unvanquished spirit; proficient in every branch of learning.

sa_verse: सर्वदाभिगतः सद्भिः समुद्र इव सिन्धुभिः। आर्यः सर्वसमश्चैव सदैव प्रियदर्शनः॥
en: Ever resorted to by the good persons even as the ocean is by the rivers; worthy of being honoured; having an equal regard for all; and capable of filling the heart with ever new sensations.

sa_verse: स च सर्वगुणोपेतः कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः। समुद्र इव गाम्भीर्ये धैर्येण हिमवानिव॥
en: Crowned with all sort of qualities, he enhances the joys of Kausalyā; being like that of the sea in gravity, and that of the Himavat in patience.

sa_verse: विष्णुना सदृशो वीर्ये सोमवत्प्रियदर्शनः। कालाग्निसदृशः क्रोधे क्षमया पृथिवीसमः॥
en: In prowess, he is like as Vişnu, and boasts of the personal attractions of the Moon. In anger he resembles the fire raging at the time of dissolution; and in forgiveness, he is like that of the Earth.

sa_verse: धनदेन समस्त्यागे सत्ये धर्म इवापरः। तमेवं गुणसम्पन्नं रामं सत्यपराक्रमम्॥ ज्येष्ठं ज्येष्ठगुणैर्युक्तं प्रियं दशरथः सुतम्। प्रकृतीनां हितैर्युक्तं प्रकृतिप्रियकाम्यया॥ यौवराज्येन संयोक्तुमैच्छत् प्रीत्या महीपतिः।
en: In giving away, he is like the Bestower of riches Kubera, and in truth, he is like another Dharma. Desirous of doing that which would be acceptable to his subjects, king Dasaratha, from fuilness of affection, wished to install as his associate in the kingdom his beloved and meritorious eldest son. Rāina who of infallible prowess, and endued with sterling virtues, and ever intent on the welfare of the people. The king Dasaratha with great pleasure wished to install as the hairapparent of the kingdom.

sa_verse: स सत्यवचनात् राजा धर्मपाशेन संयतः। विवासयामास सुतं रामं दशरथः प्रियम्॥
en: Bound by the ties of duty in consequence of his promise, king Dasaratha banished his favourite son Rāma.

sa_verse: स जगाम वनं वीरः प्रतिज्ञामनुपालयन्। पितुर्वचननिर्देशात् कैकेय्याः प्रियकारणात्॥
en: In pursuance of his father's promise, and with the view of compassing the pleasure of Kaikeyī, that heroic one, commanded by his sire, repaired to the forest.

sa_verse: तं व्रजन्तं प्रियो भ्राता लक्ष्मणोऽनुजगाम ह। स्नेहाद् विनयसम्पन्नः सुमित्रानन्दवर्धनः॥ भ्रातरं दयितो भ्रातुः सौभ्रात्रमनुदर्शयन्।
en: On the eve of his departure for the forest, that enhancer of Sumitrā's joy and favourite of his brother (Rāma), his dear brother Lakşmaņa, endowed with humility, displaying brotherliness, followed him out of affection.

sa_verse: रामस्य दयिता भार्या नित्यं प्राणसमाहिता॥ जनकस्य कुले जाता देवमायेव निर्मिता। सर्वलक्षणसम्पन्ना नारीणामुत्तमा वधूः॥ सीताप्यनुगता रामं शशिनं रोहिणी यथा। पौरैरनुगतो दूरं पित्रा दशरथेन च॥
en: As Rohini followed with the moon, Rāma's beloved spouse, sprung in Janaka's line-like as an embodiment of Divine power-sear (Rāma) as life itself, and engaged in acts of good, and furnished with every auspicious mark, and the best of wives, followed Rāma. Having been followed far by his father Dasaratha along with the citizens.

sa_verse: शृङ्गवेरपुरे सुतं गङ्गाकूले व्यसर्जयत्। गुहमासाद्य धर्मात्मा निषादाधिपतिं प्रियम्॥ गुहेन सहितो रामो लक्ष्मणेन च सीतया।
en: Rāma met with the virtuous and beloved king of the Nişādas; and then in company with Guha, Laksmana, and Sita, Rama dismissed his charioteer on the banks of the Ganges at Srigaverapura.

sa_verse: ते वनेन वनं गत्वा नदीस्तीर्वा बहूदकाः॥ चित्रकूटमनुप्राप्य भरद्वाजस्य शासनात्। रम्यमावसथं कृत्वा रममाणा वने त्रयः॥
en: Then wending from one forest to another, and having crossed many broad rivers, they, in accordance with Bharadvăja's directions, arrived at the Citrakūta; and constructing a romantic abode, the three began to live there.

sa_verse: देवगन्धर्वसंकाशास्तत्र ते न्यवसन् सुखम्। चित्रकूटं गते रामे पुत्रशोकातुरस्तदा॥ राजा दशरथः स्वर्ग जगाम विलपन् सुतम्।
en: They spent their days in delight, in the company of gods and Gandharvas. And when Rāma had reached the Citrakuța, king Dasaratha, distressed on account of his son, went to heaven, bewailing the latter.

sa_verse: गते तु तस्मिन् भरतो वसिष्ठप्रमुखैर्द्विजैः॥ नियुज्यमानो राज्याय नैच्छद् राज्यं महाबलः। स जगाम वनं वीरो रामपादप्रसादकः॥
en: When king Dasaratha had passed away heaven, the mighty Bharata, although pressed by the Brāhmaṇas headed by Vasiştha, to rule the kingdom, did not wish for dominion. And that hero went the forest, with the view of propitiating Rāma.

sa_verse: गत्वा तु स महात्मानं रामं सत्यपराक्रमम्। अयाचद् भ्रातरं राममार्यभावपुरस्कृतः॥
en: Having come to the high-souled Rāma, with truth for his prowess, he besought his brother, with every mark of respect.

sa_verse: त्वमेव राजा धर्मज्ञ इति रामं वचोऽब्रवीत्। रामोऽपि परमोदारः सुमुखः सुमहायशाः॥ न चैच्छत् पितुरादेशाद् राज्यं रामो महाबलः। पादुके चास्य राज्याय न्यासं दत्त्वा पुनः पुनः॥ निवर्तयामास ततो भरतं भरताग्रजः।
en: Bharata said, You are only the king conversant with Dharma. And the exceedingly generous, illustrious and mighty Rāma of a cheerful countenance did not wish for the kingdom, in consonance with his father's injunction. And having made over to Bharata, as his substitute on the throne, his own sandals, Bharata's elder brother repeatedly forbade him.

sa_verse: स काममनवाप्यैव रामपादावुपस्पृशन्॥ नन्दिग्रामेऽकरोद् राज्यं रामागमनकाङ्क्षया।
en: And then Bharata, finding his desire not fulfilled, touched Rama's feet, and began to rule at Nandigrāma, expecting the return of Rāma.

sa_verse: गते तु भरते श्रीमान् सत्यसंधो जितेन्द्रियः॥ रामस्तु पुनरालक्ष्य नागरस्य जनस्य च। तत्रागमनमेकाग्रो दण्डकान् प्रविवेश ह॥
en: And when the auspicious Bharata, firm in his promise and of subdued sense, had gone away, Rāma again perceiving there the influx of citizens and others, eagerly entered Dandaka.

sa_verse: प्रविश्य तु महारण्यं रामो राजीवलोचनः । विराधं राक्षसं हत्वा शरभङ्गं ददर्श ह ॥ सुतीक्ष्णं चाप्यगस्त्यं च अगस्त्यभ्रातरं तथा।
en: Having entered that mighty vast, the lotuseyed Rāma slew the Räkşasa Virädha, and saw Sarabhanga, Sutiksna, Agastya and Agastya's brother.

sa_verse: अगस्त्यवचनाच्चैव जग्राहैन्द्रं शरासनम्॥ खङ्गं च परमप्रीतस्तूणी चाक्षयसायको।
en: He then, advised by Agastya, gladly possessed himself of Indra's bow, the inexhaustible arrows, the scimitar, and the quiver.

sa_verse: वसतस्तस्य रामस्य वने वनचरैः सह॥ ऋषयोऽभ्यागमन् सर्वे वधायासुररक्षसाम्।
en: While Rāma was dwelling there with the rangers of forests, the sages came to him in a body, for the destruction of the Asuras and Raksasas.

sa_verse: स तेषां प्रतिशुश्राव राक्षसानां तदा वने॥ प्रतिज्ञातश्च रामेण वधः संयति राक्षसाम्। ऋषीणामग्निकल्पानां दण्डकारण्यवासिनाम्॥
en: Thereupon in the presence of those ascetics like the flaming fire, inhabiting the Daņdaka forest, he promised to slay those Räkşasas in battle.

sa_verse: तेन तत्रैव वसता जनस्थाननिवासिनी। विरूपिता शूर्पणखा राक्षसी कामरूपिणी॥
en: It was while he was living there that, that dweller of Janasthāna, the Rākṣasī Śūrpanakhā, capable of assuming any form at will, was disfigured.

sa_verse: ततः शूर्पणखावाक्यादुद्युक्तान् सर्वराक्षसान्। खरं त्रिशिरसं चैव दूषणं चैव राक्षसम्॥ निजधान रणे रामस्तेषां चैव पदानुगान्। वने तस्मिन् निवसता जनस्थाननिवासिनाम्॥ रक्षसां निहतान्यासन् सहस्राणि चतुर्दश।
en: It was while living there in the society of the inhabitants of Janasthāna, that Rāma slew in battle the Rākşasas Khara and Trisiras and Dusana, together with their followers, who all had been stirred up by the words of Śūrpanakhā. And fourteen thousand Räksasas were slain in that battle.

sa_verse: ततो ज्ञातिवधं श्रुत्वा रावणः क्रोधमूर्च्छितः।॥ सहायं वरयामास मारीचं नाम राक्षसम्।
en: Having learnt of the destruction of his relatives, Rāvaņa wrought into frenzy by anger, sought the help of a Rākşasa named Mārīca.

sa_verse: चार्यमाणः सु.हुशो मारीचेन स रावणः॥ न विरोधो बलवता क्षमो रावण तेन ते। अनादृत्य तु तद्वाक्यं रावणः कालचोदितः॥ जगाम सहमारीचस्तस्याश्रमपदं तदा।
en: And although strongly dissuaded by Mārīca, saying. You ought not to enter into hostilities with that powerful one. Do you, therefore, O Ravana, excuse me! yet, disregarding those words of his, Ravana, urged on by Fate, went into that asylum in company with Mārīca.

sa_verse: तेन मायाविना दूरमपवाह्य नृपात्मजौ ॥ जहार भार्या रामस्य गृधं हत्वा जटायुषम्।
en: That one (Mārīca) commanding illusions, having drawn far the king's sons (Rāma and Lakşmaņa), he (Rāvana) carried away Rāma's wife, slaying the vulture Jatayu.

sa_verse: गृधं च निहतं दृष्ट्वा हृतां श्रुत्वा च मैथिलीम् ॥ राघवः शोकसंतप्तो विललापाकुलेन्द्रियः।
en: And beholding the vulture slain and learning of the kidnapping of Mithila's daughter, the descendant of Raghu, deprived of sense, bewailed in grief.

sa_verse: ततस्तेनैव शोकेन गृधं दग्ध्वा जटायुषम्॥ मार्गमाणो वने सीतां राक्षसं संददर्श ह। कबन्धं नाम रूपेण विकृतं घोरदर्शनम्॥ तं निहत्य महाबाहुर्ददाह स्वर्गतश्च सः।
en: Having with unassuaged sorrow burnt the vulture Jațāyu, as he was searching for Sità in that forest, he fell in with a Rākṣasa, Kabandha by name, of a dreadful and deformed shape. Having slain him, the mighty-armed one burnt his body,-and thereupon he went to heaven.

sa_verse: स चास्य कथयामास शबरी धर्मचारिणीम्॥ श्रमणां धर्मनिपुणामभिगच्छेति राघव।
en: And the Rākşasa addressed Rama saying, O descendant of Raghu, Do you repair to the ascetic, Śabarī, conversant with all systems of morality.

sa_verse: सोऽभ्यगच्छन्महातेजाः शबरीं शत्रुसूदनः॥ शबर्या पूजितः सम्यग् रामो दशरथात्मजः।
en: Repairing to Sabarī, that destroyer of foes, gifted with exceeding energy, Rāma, the son of Dasaratha, highly honoured by Sabari.

sa_verse: पम्पातीरे हनुमता सङ्गतो वानरेण ह॥ हनुमद्वचनाच्चैव सुग्रीवेण समागतः। सुग्रीवाय च तत्सर्वं शंसद्रामो महाबलः।५९।।
en: Than he met with Hanuman on the banks of the Pampā. Then, agreeably to Hanumān's advice, the exceedingly powerful Rāma saw Sugrīva. And detailed to him all specially touching Sitā.

sa_verse: सुग्रीवश्चापि तत्सर्वं श्रुत्वा रामस्य वानरः॥ चकार सख्यं रामेण प्रीतश्चैवाग्निसाक्षिकम्।
en: Then the monkey Sugrīva, having heard all from Rāma was well pleased with Rāma, and in the presence of fire, made friends with him.

sa_verse: प्रतिज्ञातं च रामेण तदा वालिवधं प्रति ॥ वालिनश्च बलं तत्र कथयामास वानरः। सुग्रीवः शङ्कितश्चासीनित्यं वीर्येण राघवे॥
en: Then Ráma vowed that he would slay Vālī. Thereupon the monkey described to Raghava the prowess of Vālī, and he feared lest Räma should not prove a match for Válī.

sa_verse: राघवप्रत्ययार्थं तु दुन्दुभेः कायमुत्तमम्। दर्शयामास सुग्रीवो महापर्वतन्निभम्॥
en: And with the view of convincing Raghava (as to Vali's might), Sugriva showed to him the huge corpse of Dundubhi, resembling a hill.

sa_verse: उत्स्मयित्वा महाबाहुः प्रेक्ष्य चास्थि महाबलः। पादाङ्गुष्ठेन चिक्षेप सम्पूर्ण दशयोजनम्॥
en: And looking at the skeleton, Rāma endued with exceeding prowess, smiling the while, with his toe cast it off at the distance of full ninety miles.

sa_verse: बिभेद च पुनस्तालान् सप्तैकेन महेषुणा। गिरिं रसातलं चैव जनयन् प्रत्ययं तदा॥
en: With a single mighty shaft he pierced seven palmyra palms, a hill, and the sixth nether worlds, carrying conviction to Sugriva.

sa_verse: ततः प्रीतमनास्तेन विश्वस्तः स महाकपिः। किष्किन्धां रामसहितो जगाम च गुहां तदा॥
en: Thus convinced, the mighty monkey well pleased went with Rāma towards the cave called Kiskindha.

sa_verse: ततोऽगर्जद्धरिवरः सुग्रीवो हेमपिङ्गलः। तेन नादेन महता निर्जगाम हरीश्वरः॥ अनुमान्य तदा तारां सुग्रीवेण समागतः। निजघान च तत्रैनं शरेणैकेन राघवः॥
en: And having arrived there, that best of monkeys, Sugrīva of a tawny and golden hue, set up loud roars. And at those mighty sounds, out came the lord of monkeys; and having obtained Tārā's consent came before Sugrīva for battle. Then Rāghava killed Vālī on the spot with a single shaft.

sa_verse: ततः सुग्रीववचनाद्धत्वा बालिनमाहवे। सुग्रीवमेव तद्राज्ये राघवः प्रत्यपादयत्॥
en: And, in compliance with Sugrīva's request, having slain Vālī in battle, Raghava conferred the kingdom on Sugriva.

sa_verse: स च सर्वान् समानीय वानरान् वानरर्षभः। दिशः प्रस्थापयामास दिदृक्षुर्जनकात्मजाम्।७१ ।।
en: Then that best of monkeys having summoned all the various monkeys, sent them in various directions in search of Janaka's daughter.

sa_verse: ततो गृध्रस्य वचनात् सम्पातेर्हनुमान् बली। शतयोजनविस्तीर्णं पुप्लुवे लवणार्णवम्॥
en: Then at the suggestion of the vulture Sampāti, the mighty Hanuman crossed the salt sea extending for a hundred Yojanas (900 miles).

sa_verse: तंत्र लङ्कां समासाद्य मुरीं रावणपालिताम्। ददर्श सीतां ध्यायन्तीमशोकवनिकां गताम्॥
en: Thereby arriving at the city of Lankā, ruled by Rāvana, he found Sītā in the midst of an Asoka garden, absorbed in thought.

sa_verse: निवेदयित्वाभिज्ञानं प्रवृत्तिं विनिवेद्य च। समाश्वास्य च वैदेहीं मर्दयामास तोरणम्॥
en: Then having shown her the sign, he rtlated to her all about the friendship between Rāma and Sugrīva, and having convinced Videha's daughter, he smashed the gate of the place.

sa_verse: पञ्च सेनाग्रगान् हत्वा सप्त मन्त्रिसुतानपि। शूरमक्षं च निक्षिप्य ग्रहणं समुपागमत्॥
en: Then having slain five generals, and seven counsellors' sons, and crushed the heroic Aksa, he was bound fast (by the arms of Indrajit.)

sa_verse: अस्त्रेणोन्मुक्तमात्मानं ज्ञात्वा पैतामहाद् वरात्। मर्षयन् राक्षसान् वीरो यन्त्रिणस्तान् यदृच्छया॥
en: Then knowing that in virtue of the grandsire's boon, he was free, he forgave those Raksasas that were leading him (to Ravana.)

sa_verse: ततो दग्ध्वा पुरीं लङ्कामृते सीतां च मैथिलीम्। रामाय प्रियमाख्यातुं पुनरायान्महाकपिः। ७७।।
en: Then having burnt down the city of Lankā, with the exception of the place occupied by Mithila's daughter, the mighty one returned, with the intention of delivering the glad tidings to Rama.

sa_verse: सोऽभिगम्य महात्मानं कृत्वा रामं प्रदक्षिणम्। न्यवेदयदमेयात्मा दृष्टा सीतेति तत्त्वतः। ७८ ।।
en: That one of immeasurable soul having come before the high-souled Rāma, and circled him addressed him, saying,— I have truly seen Sītā.

sa_verse: ततः सुग्रीवसहितो गत्वा तीरं महोदधेः। समुद्र क्षोभयामास शरैरादित्यसंनिभैः॥
en: Thereafter accompanied by Sugrīva, Rāma repaired to the shore of the mighty ocean, and with shafts resembling the sun, vexed the deep.

sa_verse: दर्शयामास चात्मानं समुद्रः सरितां पतिः। समुद्रवचनाच्चैव नलं सेतुमकारयत्॥
en: Then that lord of rivers, the Ocean, showed himself. And agreeably to the advice of the Ocean, Nala constructed a bridge (over the water.)

sa_verse: तेन गत्वा पुरी लङ्कां हत्वा रावणमाहवे। रामः सीतामनुप्राप्य परां व्रीडामुपागमत् ॥
en: By that bridge Rama went to the city of Lankā, and slew Rāvana in battle. And, having recovered Sītā, Rāma experienced high shame (in consequence of Sītā's having lived so long in Ravana's place.)

sa_verse: तामुवाच ततो रामः परुषं जनसंसदि। अमृष्यमाणा सा सीता विवेश ज्वलनं सती॥
en: Language towards Sītā in the presence of all. Incapable of bearing it, the chaste Sītā entered flaming fire.

sa_verse: ततोऽग्निवचनात् सीतां ज्ञात्वा विगतकल्मषाम्। कर्मणा तेन महता त्रैलोक्यं सचराचरम्॥ सदेवर्षिगणं तुष्टं राघवस्य महात्मनः।
en: Thereupon assured by Agni as to the sinlessness of Sītā, Rāma became exceedingly pleased, and was honoured by all the deities. And at that great act of Rāma's the three worlds with all that was mobile and immobile in them, along with the sages and gods, were well pleased with the mighty-souled Rāghava.

sa_verse: बभौ रामः सम्प्रहृष्टः पूजितः सर्वदैवतैः५५८४ ।। अभिषिच्य च लङ्कायां राक्षसेन्द्रं विभीषणम्। कृतकृत्यस्तदा रामो विज्वरः प्रमुमोद ह॥
en: Then installing that foremost of Rākşasas, Vibhișana, on the throne of Lankā, Rāma was perfectly easy, and rejoiced exceedingly.

sa_verse: देवताभ्यो वरं प्राप्य समुत्थाप्य च वानरान्। अयोध्या प्रस्थितो रामः पुष्पकेण सुहृद्वृतः॥
en: Then Rāma, obtaining a boon from the celestials, revived the monkeys fallen in battle, and surrounded by friends, set out for Ayodhyā on the Puspaka.

sa_verse: भरद्वाजाश्रमं गत्वा रामः सत्यपराक्रमः। भरतस्यान्तिके रामो हनूमन्तं व्यसर्जयत् ॥
en: And repairing to Bharadvāja's hermitage, Rāma, having truth for his prowess, despatched Hanuman to Bharata.

sa_verse: पुनराख्यायिकां जल्पन् सुग्रीवसहितस्तदा। पुष्पकं तत् समारुह्य नन्दिग्रामं ययौ तदा॥
en: Then talking over past affairs, accompanied with Sugriva, Rama, mounted on the Puspaka, and departed for Nandigrāma.

sa_verse: नन्दिग्रामे जटां हित्वा भ्रातृभिः सहितोऽनघः। रामः सीतामनुप्राप्य राज्यं पुनरवाप्तवान्॥
en: Having arrived at Nandigrāma, the sinless Rāma sheared himself of his matted locks along with his brothers, and having regained Sītā, got back his kingdom

sa_verse: प्रहृष्टमुदितो रेकस्तुष्टः पुष्टः सुधार्मिकः। निरामयो रोगश्च दुर्भिक्षभयवर्जितः॥
en: During his reign) his subjects will enjoy happiness, and contentment, and become hale, and grow in righteousness, and be devoid of mental disquietude and disease, and free from the fear of famine.

sa_verse: न पुत्रमरणं केचिद् द्रक्ष्यन्ति पुरुषाः क्वचित्। नार्यश्चाविधवा नित्यं भविष्यन्ति पतिव्रताः॥
en: Where a no person is to witness his son's death, and women will be ever chaste, and never bear widow-hood.

sa_verse: न चाग्निजं भयं किंचिन्नाप्सु मज्जन्ति जन्तवः। न वातजं भयं किंचिन्नापि ज्वरकृतं तथा॥ न चापि क्षुद्भयं तत्र न तस्करभयं तथा। नगराणि च राष्ट्राणि धनधान्ययुतानि च॥ नित्यं प्रमुदिताः सर्वे यथा कृतयुगे तथा।
en: And no fear of conflagration (will exercise people), nor creatures be drowned in water. And no danger will come from hunger, or from thieves. And all will live happily as at the Krta age.

sa_verse: अश्वमेधशतैरिष्ट्वा तथा बहुसुवर्णकैः॥ गवां कोट्ययुतं दत्त्वा विद्वद्भयो विधिपूर्वकम्। असंख्येयं धनं दत्त्वा ब्राह्मणेभ्यो महायशाः॥ राजवंशाञ्छतगुणान् स्थापयिष्यति राघवः। चातुर्वयं च लोकेऽस्मिन् स्वे स्वे धर्मे नियोक्ष्यति।।
en: Having performed with countless gold an hundred horse-sacrifices, and bestowed with due rites ayutas and kotis* of kine on learned persons, and countless wealth famous Brāhmaṇas, Rāghava will establish an bundred royal families, and will employ each of the four castes in its own duties. Ayuta is ten thousand; and koti is ten millions.

sa_verse: दशवर्षसहस्राणि दशवर्षशतानि च। रामो राज्यमुपासित्वा ब्रह्मलोकं प्रयास्यति॥
en: And having reigned for ten thousand and as many hundred years. Rāma will depart for the regions of Brahmā.

sa_verse: इदं पवित्रं पापघ्नं पुण्यं वेदैश्च सम्मितम्। यः पठेत् रामचरितं सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥
en: He that reads this sacred, sin-destroying, merit-bestowing history of Rāma like that of the Veda itself, becomes cleansed from all sin.

sa_verse: एतदाख्यानमायुष्यं पठन् रामायणं नरः। सपुत्रपौत्रः सगणः प्रेत्य स्वर्गे महायते॥
en: And the man that reads this Rāmāyaṇa conferring length of days, after death, is on honoured in heaven, along with his sons, and grandsons, and relations.

sa_verse: पठन् द्विजो वागृषभत्वमीयात् स्यात् क्षत्रियो भूमिपतित्वमीयात्। ज्जनश्च शूद्रोऽपि महत्त्वमीयात्॥
en: If a Brāhmaṇa reads it, he attains excellence in speech; if a Kșatriya, he will acquire lordship over landed possessions, if a Vaisya, abundance of wealth in trade; and if a Sudra, greatness.

sa_verse: नारदस्य तु तद् वाक्यं श्रुत्वा वाक्यविशारदः। पूजयामास धर्मात्मा सह शिष्यो महामुनिम्॥
en: Hearing those words of Nārada, that one of virtuous soul, skilled in speech, together with his disciples, worshipped that great sage.

sa_verse: यथावत् पूजितस्तेन देवर्षि रदस्तथा। आपृच्छयैवाभ्यनुज्ञातः स जगाम विहायसम्॥
en: Having received due honours, that Devarși Nārada taking leave of him went to his ethereal regions.

sa_verse: स मुहूर्तं गते तस्मिन् देवलोकं मुनिस्तदा। जगाम तमसातीरं जाह्नव्यास्त्वविदूरतः॥
en: And when Nārada had left for the celestial regions, that holy person went to the banks of the Tamasā not very far from Jāhnavī.

sa_verse: स तु तीरं समासाद्य तमसाया मुनिस्तदा। शिष्यमाह स्थितं पार्श्वे दृष्ट्वा तीर्थमकर्दमम्॥
en: And having arrived at the banks of that river, the pious one, observing a holy spot devoid of mud, spoke to his disciple standing by.

sa_verse: अकर्दममिदं तीर्थे भरद्वाज निशामय। रमणीयं प्रसन्नाम्बु सन्मनुष्यमनो यथा।५।।
en: Saying-O Bharadvāja, behold this holy spot devoid of mud. And it is beautiful, and contains pleasant waters, even like the minds of good men.

sa_verse: न्यस्यतां कलशस्तात दीयतां वल्कलं मम। इदमेवावगाहिष्ये तमसातीर्थमुत्तमम्॥
en: O child, do you, put down your pitcher, and give me my bark. I will bathe even in this Tamasā, the best of holy spots.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो भरद्वाजो वाल्मीकेन महात्मना। प्रायच्छत मुनेस्तस्य वल्कलं नियतो गुरुः॥
en: Thus accosted by the high-souled Vālmīki, Bharadvāja ever intent upon serving his spiritual guide, presented the sage his bark.

sa_verse: स शिष्याहस्तदादाय वल्कलं नियतेन्द्रियः। विचचार ह पश्यंस्तत् सर्वतो विपुलं वनम्।।।।।
en: That one of subdued senses, having received his bark from his disciple, began to range around, surveying that extensive forest.

sa_verse: तस्याभ्याशे तु मिथुनं चरन्तमनपायिनम्। ददर्श भगवांस्तत्र क्रौञ्चयोश्चारुनिःस्वनम्॥
en: In the vicinity of the forest, that worshipful one espied a pair of Krauñcas, emitting melodious notes, and ranging around never parting from one another.

sa_verse: तस्मात् तु मिथुनादेकं पुमांसं पापनिश्चयः। जघान वैरनिलयो निषादस्तस्य पश्यतः॥
en: At this juncture, a wicked-minded fowler, singling out the male, without any cause of hostility, slew him in the very presence of the holy man.

sa_verse: तं शोणितपरीताङ्ग चेष्टमानं महीतले। भार्या तु निहतं दृष्ट्वा रुराव करुणां गिरम्॥
en: Observing him struggling on the earth, bathed in blood, his female companion began to bewail in piteous accents.

sa_verse: वियुक्ता पतिना तेन द्विजेन सहचारिणा। ताम्रशीर्षेण मत्तेन पत्रिणा सहितेन वै॥
en: At the prospect of her separation from her copper-crested oviparous husband, engaged in sport with extended plumage.

sa_verse: तथाविधं द्विजं दृष्ट्वा निषादेन निपातितम्। ऋषेर्धर्मात्मनस्तस्य कारुण्यं समपद्यत॥
en: Finding the bird in that condition thus brought down by the fowler, the pity of that pious and righteous-souled Rși was excited exceedingly.

sa_verse: ततः करुणवेदित्वादधर्मोऽयमिति द्विजः। निशाम्य रुदती क्रौञ्चीमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then considering it to be an unrighteous deed, with a heart moved with pity, that twice-born one, beholding the weeping Krauñci, spoke these words.

sa_verse: मा निषाद प्रतिष्ठां त्वमगमः शाश्वतीः समाः। यत् क्रौञ्चमिथुनादेकमवधीः काममोहितम्॥
en: O fowler, since you have slain one of a pair of Krauñcas, you shall never attain prosperity (respect)!

sa_verse: तस्येत्थं ब्रुवतश्चिन्ता बभूव हृदि वीक्षतः। शोकार्तेनास्य शकुनेः किमिदं व्याहृतं मया॥
en: Having uttered this, he got worried within himself, What is this that I have said, afflicted with grief for the bird?

sa_verse: चिन्तयन् स महाप्राज्ञश्चकार मतिमान्मतिम्। शिष्यं चैवाब्रवीद् वाक्यमिदं स मुनिपुङ्गवः॥
en: Resolving thus in his mind, that highly-wise one and best of sages addressed his disciple, saying.

sa_verse: पादबद्धोऽक्षरसमस्तन्त्रीलयसमन्वितः। शोकार्तस्य प्रवृत्तो मे श्लोको भवतु नान्यथा॥
en: This speech that I have uttered is of equal bound with metre and accents; it has rhythm and is capable of being chanted according to measure to stringed accompaniment. Let it therefore go forth as a sloka!

sa_verse: शिष्यस्तु तस्य ब्रुवतो मुनेर्वाक्यमनुत्तमम्। प्रतिजग्राह संतुष्टस्तस्य तुष्टोऽभवन्मुनि ॥
en: When the sage had spoken thus, his disciple, well pleased, assented to his excellent speech; and thereat that pious person was gratified.

sa_verse: सोऽभिषेकं ततः कृत्वा तीर्थे तस्मिन् यथाविधि। तमेव चिन्तयत्रर्थमुपावर्तत वै मुनिः॥
en: Then having duly performed his ablutions at that holy spot, the reverend sage retraced his steps, pondering over the incidents touching the sloka.

sa_verse: भरद्वाजस्ततः शिष्यो विनीतः श्रुतवान् गुरोः। कलशं पूर्णमादाय पृष्ठतोऽनुजगाम ह॥
en: His disciple also, accomplished in learning, and of meek demeanour, followed Vālmīki, carrying on his back a pitcher filled with water.

sa_verse: स प्रविश्याश्रमपदं शिष्येण सह धर्मवित्। उपविष्टः कथाश्चान्याश्चकार ध्यानमास्थितः॥
en: Having entered the hermitage along with his disciple, conversant with righteousness engaged in diverse kinds of talk, revolved in his mind the circumstances connected with the verses.

sa_verse: आजगाम ततो ब्रह्मा लोककर्ता स्वयं प्रभुः। चतुर्मुखो महातेजा द्रष्टुं तं मुनिपुङ्गवम् ॥
en: It came to pass that desirous of seeing the best of sages, there arrived, that lord and creator of all, the effulgent Brahmā, furnished with four countenances.

sa_verse: वाल्मीरिरथ तं दृष्ट्वा सहसोत्थाय वाग्यतः। प्राञ्जलिः प्रयतो भूत्वा तस्थौ परमविस्मितः॥
en: Beholding him Vālmīki rose up suddenly and, wondering greatly, humbly and silently stood before him with clasped hands.

sa_verse: पूजयामास तं देवं पाद्याासनवन्दनैः। प्रणम्य विधिवच्चैनं पृष्ट्वा चैव निरामयम् ॥
en: And duly bending low in reverence, Vālmīki offered that deity water to wash his feet, and other things for reception.

sa_verse: अथोपविश्य भगवानासने परमार्चिते। वाल्मीकये च ऋषये संदिदेशासनं ततः॥
en: Having sat-down on a highly-honoured seat, that worshipful one enquired after the welfare of that sage Vālmīki knowing no deterioration, and then asked him to be seated.

sa_verse: ब्रह्मणा समनुज्ञातः सोऽप्युपाविशदासने। उपविष्टे तदा तस्मिन् साक्षाल्लोकपितामहे ॥ तद्गतेनैव मनसा वाल्मीकिानमास्थितः। पापात्मना कृतं कप्टं वैरग्रहणबुद्धिना॥ यत् तादृशं चारुरवं क्रौञ्चं हन्यादकारणात्। शोचन्नेव पुनः क्रौञ्चीमुपश्लोकमिमं जगौ।
en: Having been seated in the presence of the Grand-sire of all, Vālmīki, his mind running upon the self-same subject, became plunged in thought. What a sin has been committed by the wicked-minded one, incited by hostile feeling, that he without cause slew that sweetly-singing Kraunca! And thereupon, again lamenting that female Krauñca, he, in grief of heart, mentally recited those verses.

sa_verse: पुनरन्तर्गतमना भूत्वा शोकपरायणः। तमुवाच ततो ब्रह्मा प्रहसन् मुनिपुङ्गवम्॥ श्लोक एवास्त्वयं बद्धो नात्र कार्या विचारणा। मच्छन्दादेव ते ब्रह्मन् प्रवृत्तेयं सरस्वती॥
en: Then smiling, Brahmā spoke to the excellent sage,-Those verses of your which you have composed a sloka shall attain celebrity: no doubt need be entertained on this head, It is because I had intended so, that those verses had come out of your lips.

sa_verse: रामस्य चरितं कृत्स्नं कुरु त्वमृषिसत्तम। धर्मात्मनो भगवतो लोके रामस्य धीमतः॥ वृत्तं कथय धीरस्य यथा ते नारदाच्छुतम्।
en: Do you now, O best of saints, compose the entire history of Rāma. Do you relate to the world the history of the righteous-souled and intelligent Rāma crowned with qualities. And do you, as you have heard it from Närada.

sa_verse: रहस्यं च प्रकाशं च यद् वृत्तं तस्य धीमतः॥ रामस्य सहसौमित्रे राक्षसानां च सर्वशः। वैदेह्याश्चैव यत् वृत्तं प्रकाशं यदि वा रहः॥ तच्चाप्यविदितं सर्व विदितं ते भविष्यति।
en: Relate all what is known, and all what is unknown to the world, O wise one, concerning Rāma, and Laksmana, and Videha's daughter, and all the Rākşasas. And even what is not known to Nārada, shall be unfolded to you.

sa_verse: न ते वागनृता काव्ये काचिदत्र भविष्यति॥ कुरु रामकथां पुण्यां श्लोकबद्धां मनोरमाम्।
en: No words of yours in this poem shall contain an untruth sacred. Therefore, now compose into verses. This delightful story of Rama.

sa_verse: यावत् स्थास्यन्ति गिरयः सरितश्च महीतले॥ तावद् रामायणकथा लोकेषु प्रचरिष्यति।
en: As long as the mountains and the seas exist on earth, the history of Rāmāyaṇa will remain prevalent among men.

sa_verse: यावद् रामस्य च कथा त्वत्कृता प्रचरियति ॥ तावदूर्ध्वमधश्च त्वन मल्लोकेषु निवत्स्यसि।
en: As long as this story of Rāmāyaṇa shall retain its currency, you shall reside both in this world and in mine.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा भगवान् ब्रह्मा तत्रैवान्तरधीयत। ततः सशिष्यो भगवान् मुनिर्विस्मयमाययौ ॥
en: Having said this, the Lord Brahmä disappeared there. And thereat the sage and his disciples marvelled greatly.

sa_verse: तस्य शिष्यास्ततः सर्वे जगुः श्लोकमिमं पुनः। मुहुर्मुहुः प्रीयमाणाः प्राहुश्च भृशविस्मिताः॥
en: His disciples sang those verses again and again; and, momentarily experiencing pleasure, recited it with exceeding wonder.

sa_verse: समाक्षरैश्चतुर्भिर्यः पादैर्गीतो महर्षिणा। सोऽनुव्याहरणाद् भूयः शोकः श्लोकत्वमागतः॥
en: Those verses, composed of equal accents, and furnished with four parts, have been sung by the great saint, have, in virtue of frequent repetition, been associated with a world of pathos and have attained the eminence of a sloka.

sa_verse: तस्य बुद्धिरियं जाता महर्षे वितात्मनः। कृत्स्नं रामायणं काव्यमीदृशैः करवाण्यहम्॥
en: Now it is the intention of that illustrious and self-centred sage to compose the entire Rāmāyaṇa in this metre.

sa_verse: स्तदास्य रामस्य चकार कीर्तिमान्। समाक्षरैः श्लोकशतैर्यशस्विनो यशस्करं काव्यमुदारदर्शनः।॥
en: The great ascetic Vālmīki of gracious appearance and unparalleled renown composed a poetry consisting of hundreds of verses in melodious measure, couching the significance of the history of Rāma.

sa_verse: तदुपगतसमाससंधियोगं सममधुरोपनतार्थवाक्यबद्धम्। रघुवरचरितं मुनिप्रणीतं दशशिरसश्च वधं निशामयध्वम्॥
en: Listen to the annals of the foremost of Raghu's race, and the destruction of the Tenheaded one composed by the ascetic, with Samāsas, Sandhis, Prakrtis, and Pratayas; and lucid with sweet and equally-accented words.

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा वस्तु समग्रं तद्धर्मार्थसहितं हितम्। व्यक्तमन्वेषते भूयो यद् वृत्तं तस्य धीमतः॥
en: Having heard the entire prevalent history of the intelligent Rāma, capable of conferring religious merit and the two other cognate objects, as well as emancipation, Vālmīki again sought to get insight into it.

sa_verse: उपस्पृश्योदकं सम्यमुनिः स्थित्वा कृताञ्जलिः। प्राचीनाग्रेधु दर्भेषु धर्मेणान्वेषते गतिम् ॥
en: And, sitting himself facing the east on a cushion of kuća grass, and sipping water according to the ordinance, he addressed himself to the contemplation of the subject through Yoga.

sa_verse: रामलक्ष्मणसीताभी राज्ञा दशरथेन च। सभार्येण सराष्ट्रेण यत् प्राप्तं तत्र तत्त्वतः॥ हसितं भाषितं चैव गतिर्यावच्च चेष्टितम्। तत् सर्वं धर्मवीर्येण यथावत् सम्प्रपश्यति॥
en: And by virtue of his Yoga powers, he clearly observed before him Rāma, and Lakşmana, and Sītá, and Dasaratha together with his wives in his kingdom, laughing, and talking, and acting, and bearing themselves as in real life.

sa_verse: स्त्रीतृतीयेन च तथा यत् प्राप्तं चरता वने। सत्यसंधेन रामेण तत् सर्वं चान्ववैक्षत ॥
en: He also saw all that was endured by Rāma firm in promise, with his wife as the third accompanist roaming in the forest

sa_verse: ततः पश्यति धर्मात्मा तत् सर्वं योगमास्थितः। पुरा यत् तत्र निर्वृत्तं पाणावामलकं यथा॥
en: And like an embolic myrobalan on his palm, that righteous-souled sage by virtue of his yoga, perceived all that had happened as well as all that would happen in the past.

sa_verse: तत् सर्वं तत्त्वतो दृष्ट्वा धर्मेण स महामतिः। अभिरामस्य रामस्य तत् सर्वं कर्तुमुद्यतः॥
en: Having truly seen everything by virtue of his contemplation, that magnanimous one set about recording the charming Rāma's history.

sa_verse: जन्म रामस्य सुमहद्वीर्यं सर्वानुकूताम्। लोकस्य प्रियतां शान्ति सौम्यतां सत्यशीलताम्॥
en: Rama's birth, his mighty prowess, and kindness to all, and popularity, and forbearance, and goodness, and truthfulness.

sa_verse: नाना चित्राः कथाश्चान्या विश्वामित्रसहायने। जानक्याश्च विवाहं च धनुषश्च विभेदनम्॥ रामरामविवादं च गुणान् दाशरथेस्तथा। तथाभिषेकं रामस्य कैकेय्या दुष्टभावताम्॥ विघातं चाभिषेकय रामस्य च विवासनम्। राज्ञः शोकं विलापं च परलोकस्य चाश्रयम् ॥ प्रकृतीनां विषादं च प्रकृतीनां विसर्जनम्। निषादाधिपसंवादं सूतोपावर्तनं तथा॥
en: And the wonderful converse he had with Visvāmitra; wedding of Janaka's daughter; and breaking the bow; the hostilities of Rāma with Rāma (Paraśurāma); and the noble qualities of Dasaratha's son: and Rama's installation; and the enmity of Kaikeyī; and the obstacle in the way of the installation; and the exile of Rāma; and the kings grief, lamentations, and departure for the other regions; and the grief of the subjects, and their dismissal (by Rāma to Ayodhyā); and the tidings of the lord of Nişādas, and the charioteer's return.

sa_verse: गङ्गायाश्चापि संतारं भरद्वाजस्य दर्शनम्। भरद्वाजाभ्यनुज्ञानाच्चित्रकूटस्य दर्शनम्॥
en: And the swimming of the Gangā and Rāma sinter view with Bharadvāja; and his arrival at Citrakūta in consonance with Bharadvāja's injunction.

sa_verse: वास्तुकर्मनिवेशं च भरतागमनं तथा। प्रसादनं च रामस्य पितुश्च सलिलक्रियाम् ॥
en: And Rāma's building a mansion there and sojourn; and Bharata's arrival, and his propitiation of Rāma; and Rāma's offering oblations to the manes of his father.

sa_verse: पादुकााभिषेकं च नन्दिग्रामनिवासनम्। दण्डकारण्यगमनं विराधस्य वधं तथा॥
en: And the installation of the sandals; and Bharata's dwelling at Nandigrāma: and Rāma's removal to Dandaka and killing of Virādha.

sa_verse: दर्शनं शरभङ्गस्य सुतीक्ष्णेन समागमम्। अनसूयासमाख्यां च अङ्गरागस्य चार्पणम्॥
en: And Rāma's interview with Sarabhanga and meeting with Sutiksna; and Sita's companionship with Anasūyā, and the latter's painting the former.

sa_verse: दर्शनं चाप्यगस्त्यस्य धनुषो ग्रहणं तथा। शूर्पणख्याश्च संवाद विरूपकरणं तथा॥
en: And Rāma's interview with Agastya, and his obtaining the bow from him; and the dialogue of Śūrpanakha and her disfigurement.

sa_verse: वधं खरत्रिशिरसोरुत्थानं रावणस्य च। मारीचस्य वधं चैव वैदेह्या हरणं तथा॥
en: And the slaughter of Khara and Trisiras; and the exertions of Rāvana; the killing of Mārīca, and the carrying away of Vaidehī.

sa_verse: राघवस्य विलापं च गृध्रराजनिबर्हणम्। कबन्धदर्शनं चैव पम्पायाश्चापि दर्शनम्॥
en: Rāghava's lamentations, and the death of the king of vultures; Rāma's encounter with Kabandha, and his view of Pampā.

sa_verse: शबरीदर्शनं चैव फलमूलाशनं तथा। प्रलापं चैव पम्पाया हनूमद्दर्शनं तथा॥
en: Rāma's interview with Śabarī, and his subsistence there on fruits and roots; Rāma's lamentations at Pampā, and meeting with Hanumān.

sa_verse: ऋष्यमूकस्य गमनं सुग्रीवेण समागमम्। प्रत्ययोत्पादनं सख्यं वालिसुग्रीवविग्रहम्॥
en: The former's sojourn to the Rşyamūka, and meeting with Sugrīva; Rāma's raising the confidence of Sugrīva and his friendship with the latter; and the encounter between Vāli and Sugriva.

sa_verse: वालिप्रमथनं चैव सुग्रीवप्रतिपादनम्। ताराविलापं समयं वर्षरात्रनिवासनम्॥ कोपं राघवसिंहस्य बलानामुपसंग्रहम्। दिशः प्रस्थापनं चैव पृथिव्याश्च निवेदनम्॥
en: The destruction of Vâli, and the installation of Sugrīva and Tara's lamentations%3B the understanding between Rāma and Sugrīva as to the time for commencing the march. Rāma's stay at the wet night, and the ire of Rama the lion of Raghu's race; the levying of forces; and the despatch of envoys in deferent directions; and the assignment by Sugrīva of different quarters to the monkeys.

sa_verse: अङ्गुलीयकदानं च ऋक्षस्य बिलदर्शनम्। प्रायोपवेशनं चैव सम्पातेश्चापि दर्शनम् ॥
en: The making over of his ring by Rāma to Hanuman; Rksa's discovery of the cave. The abstemious abode of the monkeys on the shore of the ocean; Hanuman's interview with Sampāti.

sa_verse: पर्वतारोहणं चैव सागरस्यापि लङ्घनम्। समुद्रवचनाच्चैव मैनाकस्य च दर्शनम् ॥ राक्षसीतर्जनं चैव च्छायाग्राहस्य दर्शनम्। सिंहिकायाश्च निधनं लङ्कामलयदर्शनम् ॥
en: Hanuman's ascension of the mountain, and his bounding over the main; and his sight of the Maināka at the injunction of Ocean; the roaring of Raksasis; Hanuman's meeting with the Rākşasa Chāyāgrāha. Hanumān's destruction of Simhikāyā; and Hanuman's sight of Lankā.

sa_verse: रात्रौ लङ्काप्रवेशं च एकस्यापि विचिन्तनम्। आपानभूमिगमनमवरोधस्य दर्शनम् ॥ दर्शनं रावणस्यापि पुष्पकस्य च दर्शनम्। अशोकवनिकायानं सीतायाश्चापि दर्शनम्॥
en: His entrance by night into Lankā; his ascertaining of conduct in times of helplessness; his journey to the Āpāna ground; and his sight of his car Puşpaka; his walk to the Asoka forest, and sight there of Şītā.

sa_verse: अभिज्ञानप्रदानं च सीतायाश्चापि भाषणम्। राक्षसीतर्जनं चैव त्रिजटास्वप्नदर्शनम्॥
en: His presentation of the ring to Sītā and converse with her; and the roaring of the Rākṣasis; and the dreaming of the dream by Trijatā.

sa_verse: मणिप्रदानं सीताया वृक्षभङ्ग तथैव च। राक्षसीविद्रवं चैव किंकराणां निबर्हणम् ॥
en: Sīta's handing a gem to Hanumān; and the breaking down of trees; and the fight of the Rakasasis, and the slaughter of the salves.

sa_verse: ग्रहणं वायुसूनोश्च लङ्कादाहाभिगर्जनम्। प्रतिप्लवनमेवाथ मधूनां हरणं तथा॥
en: The wind-god's son being taken captive; and his terrible roars while burning down Lankā; and his bounding back over the ocean; and the forcible possession of honey.

sa_verse: राघवाश्वासनं चैव मणिनिर्यातनं तथा। संगमं च समुद्रेण नलसेतोश्च बन्धनम् ॥
en: Hanumān's consoling Rāghava, and handing him the gem; Rāma's interview with ocean; and Nala's constructing the bridge.

sa_verse: प्रतारं च समुद्रस्य रात्रौ लङ्कावरोधनम्। विभीषणेन संसर्गं वधोपायनिवेदनम्॥
en: The army's crossing of the ocean; and the nightly siege of Lankā; and Rāma, interview with Bibhishana; the communication as to the means of destruction.

sa_verse: कुम्भकर्णस्य निधनं मेघनादनिबर्हणम्। रावणस्य विनाशं च सीतावाप्तिमरेः पुरे॥
en: And the killing of Kumbhakarna and Meghanāda; and the destruction of Răvaņa, and the recovery of Sītā in the enemy's city.

sa_verse: विभीषणाभिषेकं च पुष्पकस्य च दर्शनम्। अयोध्यायाश्च गमनं भरद्वाजसमागमम्॥
en: And the coronation of Vibhīşaņa, and the sight of Puspaka; Rama's return towards Ayodhyā, and meeting with Bharadvāja.

sa_verse: प्रेषणं वायुपुत्रस्य भरतेन समागमम्। रामाभिषेकाभ्युदयं सर्वसैन्यविसर्जनम्। स्वराष्ट्ररञ्जनं चैव वैदेह्याश्च विसर्जनम्॥
en: Despatch of Hanumān; and Rāma's meeting with Bharata; and the installation of Rāma; and the dismissal of all the forces.

sa_verse: अनागतं च यत् किंचिद् रामस्य वसुधातले। तच्चकारोत्तरे काव्ये वाल्मीकिर्भगवानृषिः॥
en: Rāma's pleasing his subjects and renunciation of Sītā,-alleles besides concerning Rāma on earth, that has not yet taken place-have been dealt with by the worshipful sage in the following poem. earth, that has not yet taken place-have been dealt with by the worshipful sage in the following poem.

sa_verse: प्राप्तराज्यस्य रामस्य वाल्मीकिर्भगवानृषिः। चकार चरितं कृत्स्नं विचित्रपदमर्थवत्॥
en: When Rāma had obtained his kingdom that worshipful sage , Vālmīki, composed the entire history (of that hero in) excellent metre and fraught with high meaning.

sa_verse: चतुर्विंशत्सहस्राणि श्लोकानामुक्तवानृषिः। तथा सर्गशतान् पञ्च षट्काण्डानि तथोत्रम्॥
en: The saint recited twenty-four thousand slokas; and it consists of five hundred sections, and is divided into six Kāndas with the Uttara.

sa_verse: कृत्वा तु तन्महाप्राज्ञः सभविष्यां सहोत्तरम्। चिन्तयामास को न्वेतत् प्रयुञ्जीयादिति प्रभुः॥
en: And having composed it, including as well future incidents to happen afterwards, that lord relected as to who should publish the same before assemblies.

sa_verse: तस्य चिन्तयमानस्य महर्षे वितात्मनः। अगृहीतां ततः पादौ मुनिवेषौ कुशीलवौ॥
en: And as that great sage of purified soul was thus pounding, in came Kuśi and Lava, in the guise of the sons of ascetics, and touched his feet.

sa_verse: कुशीलवौ तु धर्मज्ञौ राजपुत्रौ यशस्विनौ। भ्रातरौ स्वरसम्पन्नौ ददर्शाश्रमवासिनौ ॥ स तु मेधाविनौ दृष्ट्वा वेदेषु परिनिष्ठितौ। वेदोपबृंहणार्थाय तावग्राहयत प्रभुः॥ काव्यं रामायणं कृत्स्नं सीतायाश्चरितं महत्। पौलस्त्यवधमित्येवं चकार चरितव्रतः।७।।
en: And he found those illustrious princes the brothers Kusi and lava, knowing morality, and living in a hermitage, and endowed with rythmic voice, well versed in the Vedas. And finding them of a retentive endowment, and initiated into Vedic studies, that lord thought them how to interpret the Vedas, and that vow-observing one taught them the great Rāmāyaṇa in full, treating of Sītā's great life, and the destruction of Paulastya (Rāvana).

sa_verse: पाठ्ये गेये च मधुरं प्रमाणैस्त्रिभिरन्वितम्। जातिभिः सप्तभिर्युक्तं तन्त्रीलयसमन्वितम्॥ रसैः शृङ्गारकरुणहास्यरौद्रभयानकैः। वीरादिभी रसैर्युक्तं काव्यमेतदगायताम्॥ तौ तु गान्धर्वतत्त्वज्ञौ स्थानमूर्च्छनकोविदौ। भ्रातरौ स्वरसम्पन्नौ गन्धर्वाविव रूपिणौ॥ रूपलक्षणसम्पन्नौ मधुरस्वरभाषिणौ। बिम्बादिवोत्थितौ बिम्बौ रामदेहात् तथापरौ॥
en: Those sweet-voiced brothers, resembling Gandharvas in grace, accomplished in music and dancing, and cognizant of Sthāna and Mürchaņā, began to time to the accompaniment of stringed instruments, and fraught with the sentiments of love, pathos, risibility, the irascible, the terrible, and the heroic. And knowing the characteristics that go to make up the Drama, and gifted with mellifluous voices, those blameless princes, coming from Rāma's body, and resembling him (like two images of one body, they were two images of made of Rama's body).

sa_verse: तौ राजपुत्रौ कात्स्न्येन धर्नामाख्यानमुत्तमम्। वाचोविधेयं तत्सर्वं कृत्वा काव्यमनिन्दितौ ॥ ऋषीणां च द्विजातीनां साधूनां च समागमे। यथोपदेशं तत्त्वज्ञौ जगतुः सुसमाहितौ ॥
en: Even as the reflection of the solar or the lunar disc resembles that disc; got by heart that excellent and moral story in its entirety; and those princes versed in the Fine arts, with a concentrated mind chanted it as they had learnt it, in the assemblies of ascetics and Brāhmaṇas and good men.

sa_verse: महात्मानौ महाभागौ सर्वलक्षणलक्षितौ। तौ कदाचित् समेतानामृषीणां भावितात्मनाम्॥ मध्येसभं समीपस्थाविदं काव्यमगायताम्। तच्छुत्वा मुनयः सर्वे वाष्पपर्याकुलेक्षणाः॥ साधु साध्विति तावूचुः परं विस्मयमागताः। ते प्रीतिमनसः सर्वे मुनयो धर्मवत्सला:॥
en: Once upon a time, those high-souled and pious ones, furnished with every auspicious mark, chanted this poem in an assembly of ascetics of purified souls. Having heard this music, all the ascetics were seized with surprise, and with eyes flooded with tears, exclaimed, Well done! Well done! And well pleased, those saints cherishing Duty.

sa_verse: प्रशशंसुः प्रशस्तव्यौ गायमानौ कुशीलवौ। अहो गीतस्य माधुर्थ श्लोकानां च विशेषतः॥
en: Praised the praiseworthy Kuśi and Lava as they sang, saying,-Ah! what charming music! What sweetness of the verses!

sa_verse: चिरनिर्वृत्तमप्येतत् प्रत्यक्षमिव दर्शितम्। प्रविश्य तावुभौ सुष्टु तथाभावमगायताम्॥ सहितौ मधुरं रक्तं सम्पन्नं स्वरसम्पदा।
en: All this happened long ago, yet it seems as if we saw it before us. And unified with the theme, both of them singing together sweetly, and at a high pitch, by means of şadja and the other notes, they entranced the audience.

sa_verse: एवं प्रशस्यमानौ तौ तप:श्लाघ्यैर्महर्षिभिः॥ संरक्ततरमत्यर्थं मधुरं तावगायताम्।
en: They two thus went on sweetly singing at a high pitch, praised by those mighty sages priding in their asceticism.

sa_verse: प्रीतः कश्चिन्मुनिस्ताभ्यां संस्थितः कलशं ददौ॥ प्रसन्नो वल्कलं कश्चित् ददौ ताभ्यां महायशाः। अन्यः कृष्णाजिनमदाद् यज्ञसूत्रं तथापरः॥
en: Some one in the assembly pleased with them presented them with a water-pitcher; and some one of high fame, being delighted, made them a present of a bark garment; and some one gave them a dark deer skin and some holy thread.

sa_verse: कश्चित् कमण्डलुं प्रादान्मौञ्जीमन्यो महामुनिः। बृसीमन्यस्तदा प्रादात् कौपीनमपरो मुनिः॥
en: Some gave a kamandalu (An earthen or wooden water pot used by an ascetic) and some great saint conferred on them a muñja (grass) made girdle; and some person granted them a brsi (The seat of an ascetic), and some a kaupina (a small piece of cloth worn by ascetics.)

sa_verse: ताभ्यां ददौ तदा हृष्टः कुठारमपरो मुनिः। काषायमपरो वस्त्रं चीरमन्यो ददौ मुनिः॥
en: Then some ascetic, well-pleased, gave them an axe; and some a saffron, another a casual clothings.

sa_verse: जटाबन्धनमन्यस्तु काष्टरज्जु मुदान्वितः। यज्ञभाण्डमृषिः कश्चित् काष्ठभारं तथापरः॥ औदुम्बरी बृसीमन्यः स्वस्ति केचित् तदावदन्। आयुष्यमपरे प्राहुर्मुदा तत्र महर्षयः॥ ददुश्चैवं वरान् सर्वे मुनयः सत्यवादिनः।
en: And some, a thread for tying up their matted locks; and some gladly gave a twine for binding faggots with, and some ascetic presented them with a sacrificial pot; and some, a quantity of fire-wood; and some, a seat made of udumbara (The glamorous fig tree). And some exclaimed, 'Svasti'; and some joyfully cried, “May you be long-lived!" And all those ascetics of truthful speech conferred on them blessings.

sa_verse: आश्चर्यमिदमाख्यानं मुनिना सम्प्रकीर्तितम्॥ परं कवीनामाधारं समाप्तं च यथाक्रमम्। अभिगीतमिदं गीतं सर्वगीतिषु कोविदौ॥ आयुयं पुष्टिजननं सर्वश्रुतिमनोहरम्।
en: The sages said Wonderful is the story! And, O you accomplished in all kinds of music! beautifully have you chanted and finished this poem, charming ear and heart, and conferring long life and prosperity, which will afford themes to poets.

sa_verse: प्रशस्यमानौ सर्वत्र कदाचित्तत्र गायकौ॥ रथ्यासु राजमार्गेषु ददर्श भरताग्रजः। स्ववेश्म चानीय ततो भ्रातरौ स कुशीलवौ॥ पूजयामास पूजाहौं रामः शत्रुनिवर्हणः। आसीनः काञ्चने दिव्ये स च सिंहासने प्रभुः॥ उपोपविष्टैः सचिवैर्धातृभिश्च समन्वितः। दृष्ट्वा तु रूपसम्पन्नौ विनीतौ भ्रातरावुभौ ॥ उवाच लक्ष्मणं रामः शत्रुघ्नं भरतं तथा। श्रूयतामेतदाख्यानमनयोर्देववर्चसोः॥ विचित्रार्थपदं सम्यग्गायकौ समचोदयत्।
en: And admired everywhere, on one occasion those singers were seen by Bharata's elder brother, in a street of Ayodhyā, sparsely scattered with stalls. And having had the brothers Kusi and Lava brought under his roof, that destroyer of enemies, Rāma, accorded those ones worthy of honour, a respectful reception. And having seated himself on a throne of excellent gold, in the midst of his brothers and counsellors, that lord, Rāma, beholding both the brothers, handsome and of modest demeanour, spoke to Laksmana, Bharata and Satrughna, saying, Do you listen to the story, fraught with excellent sense and composed in excellent measure, as sung by these ones endowed with the divine afflatus. And then he ordered the singers to begin.

sa_verse: तौ चापि मधुरं रक्तं स्वचित्तायतनिःस्वनम्॥ तन्त्रीलयवदत्यर्थं विश्रुतार्थमगायताम्। ह्लादयत् सर्वगात्राणि मनांसि हृदयानि च। श्रोत्राश्रयसुखं गेयं तद् बभौ जनसंसदि॥
en: Thereupon causing the down of the audience to stand on end, and ravishing their minds and hearts, they began to sing melodiously and distinctly and in as high a pitch as they could command, and in strains rivalling the notes of a Vīņā. And that song of their enchanted the ear of that assembly.

sa_verse: इमौ मुनी पार्थिवलक्षणान्वितौ कुशीलवौ चैव महातपस्विनौ। ममापि तद् भूतिकरं प्रचक्षते महानुभावं चरितं निबोधत॥
en: Rāma said, Although these Kuśa and Lava, of rigid penances, look like ascetics, yet they bear on their persons the signs of royalty. And, besides, the story conduces to my fame. Do you, therefore, listen to that history fraught with great worth!

sa_verse: ततस्तु तौ रामवचः प्रचोदितावगायतां मार्गविधानसम्पदा। स चापि रामः परिषद्गतः शनैर्बुभूषयासक्तमना बभूव॥
en: And then commanded by Rāma, they began to chant according to the Märga mode, and Rāma seated in the midst of his court, was drawn to the music, anxious for the perpetuation of his history.

sa_verse: सर्वापूर्वमियं येषामासीत् कृत्स्ना वसुंधरा। प्रजापतिमुपादाय नृपाणां जयशालिनाम्॥ येषां स सगरो नाम सागरो येन खानितः। षष्टिपुत्रसहस्राणि यं यान्तं पर्यवारयन्॥ इक्ष्वाकूणामिदं तेषां राज्ञा वंशे महात्मनाम्। महदुतान्नमाख्यानं रामायणमिति श्रुतम्॥
en: This great story of Rämāyana treats of those victorious kings commencing with Prajāpati, and having Ikşvāku for their founder, who ruled the entire earth as no other kings had done so before them, and in whose line Sagara was born Sagara who dug the ocean, and whom, while out in progress, his sixty-thousand sons followed.

sa_verse: तदिदं वर्तयिष्यावः सर्वं निखिलमादितः। धर्मकामार्थसहितं श्रोतव्यमनसूयता ॥
en: We shall now chant the entire history of that dynasty from the beginning. Do you, with minds free from ill will, listen to that story conferring merit, profit and pleasure.

sa_verse: कोशलो नाम मुदितः स्फीतो जनपदो महान्। निविष्टः सरयूतीरे प्रभूतधनधान्यवान् ॥
en: There is on the banks of the Sarayū a great and flourishing country called Kośala abounding in corn and wealth, in which the inhabitants passed their days pleasantly.

sa_verse: अयोध्या नाम नगरी तत्रासील्लोकविश्रुता। मनुना मानवेन्द्रेण या पुरी निर्मिता स्वयम्॥
en: The capital of that country was Ayodhyā famed among men, which was founded by Manu himself that foremost of men.

sa_verse: आयता दश च द्वे च योजनानि महापुरी। श्रीमती त्रीणि विस्तीर्णा सुविभक्तमहापथा॥
en: That beautiful and mighty city was twelve Yojanas in length and Three in breadth; and was intersected outside with spacious roads laid out orderly.

sa_verse: राजमार्गेण महता सुविभक्तेन शोभिता। मुक्तपुष्पावकीर्णेन जलसिक्तेन नित्यशः॥
en: And scattered with blown blossoms, and regularly sprinkled with water; the well-arranged broad high-ways looked beautiful.

sa_verse: तां तु राजा दशरथो महाराष्ट्रविवर्धनः। पुरीमावासयामास दिवि देवपतिर्यथा॥
en: That one bringing prosperity into great kingdoms, King Dasaratha, lived in that city, like that of the lord of the deities inhabiting the celestial regions.

sa_verse: कपाटतोरणवतीं सुविभक्तान्तरापणाम्। सर्वयन्त्रायुधवतीमुषितां सर्वशिल्पिभिः॥
en: And the city was furnished with doors and gates, and well-arranged rows of shops. And it contained all kinds of instruments and arms, and was inhabited by all classes of artizans.

sa_verse: सूतमागधसम्बाधां श्रीमतीमतुलप्रभाम्। उच्चाट्टालध्वजवती शतनीशतसंकुलाम्॥
en: And that graceful and matchlessly brilliant city abounded in eulogists and genealogists. And it was crowned with stately edifices with flags, and guarded by hundreds of Satāghnis. (A weapon commonly described as a stone set round with iron spiles).

sa_verse: वधूनाटकसंधैश्च संयुक्तां सर्वतः पुरीम्। उद्यानाम्रवणोपेतां महतीं सालमेखलाम्॥
en: And the mighty city contained theatres for females, and gardens, and mango-groves; and was enclosed by a wall of såla trees.

sa_verse: दुर्गगम्भीरपरिखां दुर्गामन्यैर्दुरासदाम्। वाजिवारणसम्पूर्णा गोभिरुष्टैः खरैस्तथा॥
en: And encircled by a deep moat, the city was not accessible for enemies. And it abounded with elephants and horses, and kine and camels and asses.

sa_verse: सामन्तराजसंधैश्च बलिकर्मभिरावृताम्। नानादेशनिवासैश्च वणिग्भिरुपशोभिताम्॥
en: It was thronged with neighbouring kings come to pay tribute, and inhabited by merchants from various countries.

sa_verse: प्रासादै रत्नविकृतैः पर्वतैरिव शोभिताम्। कूटागारैश्च सम्पूर्णामिन्द्रस्येवामरावतीम्॥
en: And adorned with mountain like palaces glittering with gems, and filled with sportingplaces for females, and like to Indra's Amarāvati.

sa_verse: चित्रामष्टापदाकारां वरनारीगणायुताम्। सर्वरत्नसमाकीर्णा विमानगृहशोभिताम्॥
en: The city was wonderful to behold, gleaming with gold-burnished ornaments, and inhabited by troops of courtezans, and abounding in all kinds of gems, and graced with royal palaces.

sa_verse: गृहगाढामविच्छिद्रां समभूमौ निवेशिताम्। शालितण्डुलसम्पूर्णामिक्षुकाण्डरसोदकाम्॥
en: And it abounded in paddy and rice, and its water was sweet as the juice of the sugar-cane.

sa_verse: दुन्दुभीभिर्मृदङ्गैश्च वीणाभिः पणवैस्तथा। नादितां भृशमत्यर्थं पृथिव्यां तामनुत्तमाम्॥
en: And it resounded with the sounds of Dundubhis and Mrdargas and Viņās and Panavas.

sa_verse: विमानमिव सिद्धानां तपसाधिगतं दिवि। सुनिवेशितवेश्मान्तां नरोत्तमसमावृताम्॥
en: And that foremost spot of all the earth was like an aerial car obtained in heaven by the Siddhas, through force of ascetic austerities, and thronged with the best specimens of humanity.

sa_verse: ये च बाणैर्न विध्यन्ति विविक्तमपरापरम्। शब्दवेध्यं च विततं लघुहस्ता विशारदाः॥ सिंहव्याघ्रवराहाणां मत्तानां नदतां वने। हन्तारो निशितैः शस्त्रैर्बलाद् बाहुबलैरपि ॥ तादृशानां सहस्रेस्तामभिपूर्णां महारथैः। पुरीमावासयामास राजा दशरथस्तदा॥
en: That city was filled by king Dasaratha with thousands of such Mahārathas light-handed and accomplished in fight, as could by force of arms or sharpened shafts slaughter infuriated lions and tigers and boars roaming in the forest; yet as would not pierce with arrows persons lorn or abandoned or hiding or fugitive. * A warrior coping with ten thousand persons, and protecting both his charioteer and steeds. A warrior fighting ten thousand Maharathas.

sa_verse: तामग्निमद्भिर्गुणवद्भिरावृतां द्विजोत्तमैर्वेदषडङ्गपारगैः। सहस्रदैः सत्यरतैर्महात्मभि महर्षिकल्पैर्ऋषिभिश्च केवलैः॥
en: It abounded mostly in excellent Brāhmaṇas, lighting the sacrificial fire, and crowned with qualities, and versed in the Vedas and the Vedāngas, and giving away thousands, and ever abiding by truth, and high-souled, and resembling might ascetics.

sa_verse: तस्यां पुर्यामयोध्यायां वेदवित् सर्वसंग्रहः। दीर्घदर्शी महातेजाः पौरजानपदप्रियः॥
en: In that city of Ayodhyā resided king Dasaratha, versed in the Vedas, commanding all resources, far-sighted, of mighty prowess, dear to the inhabitants both rural and urban.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकूणामतिरथो यज्वा धर्मपरो वशी। महर्षिकल्पो राजर्षिस्त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतः॥
en: He was an Athiratha in the Ikşvāku line, performing sacrifices, engaged in the performance of duties, self-controlled like a Maharși, a royal saint famed in the three worlds.

sa_verse: बलवान् निहतामित्रो मित्रवान् विजितेन्द्रियः। धनैश्च संचयैश्चान्यैः शक्रवैश्रवणोपमः॥
en: He possessed of strength, the destroyer of foes, having friends, of subdued senses, comparable to ſakra and Vaiśravaņa by virtue of accumulated riches and other possessions.

sa_verse: यथा मनुर्महातेजा लोकस्य परिरक्षिता। तथा दशरथो राजा लोकस्य परिरक्षिता।४॥
en: As the highly-powerful Manu protected the people, Dasaratha did the same.

sa_verse: तेन सत्याभिसंधेन त्रिवर्गमनुतिष्ठता। पालिता सा पुरी श्रेष्ठा इन्द्रेणेवामरावती॥
en: As Indra rules Amarāvatī, that one firm in promise, and following duty, profit, and pleasure, ruled that best of cities.

sa_verse: तस्मिन् पुरवरे हृष्टा धर्मात्मानो बहुश्रुताः। नरास्तुष्टा धनैः स्वैः स्वैरलुब्धाः सत्यवादिनः॥
en: In that excellent city, the men were happy and righteous-souled, and widely-read, and each contented with his possessions, and devoid of covetousness, and speaking the truth.

sa_verse: नाल्पसंनिचयः कश्चिदासीत् तस्मिन् पुरोत्तमे। कुटुम्बी यो ह्यसिद्धार्थो गवाश्वधनधान्यवान्॥
en: In that prime of cities, there was none who had not at his command a plenteous supply of the good things, and there was no householder who was not well off in horses and kine, and corn and wealth.

sa_verse: कामी वा न कदर्यो वा नृशंसः पुरुषः क्वचित्। द्रष्टुं शक्यमयोध्यायां नाविद्वान् न च नास्तिकः॥
en: And one could see nowhere in Ayodhyā persons given up to lust, or unsightly, or crooked-minded, or unlettered, or atheistical.

sa_verse: सर्वे नराश्च नार्यश्च धर्मशीला: सुसंयताः। मुदिताः शीलवृत्ताभ्यां महर्षय इवामलाः॥
en: And all the men and all the women were of excellent character, and subdued senses, and a happy frame of mind, and both on respect of occupation and conduct spotless like to the Maharsis.

sa_verse: नाकुण्डली नामुकुटी नास्रग्वी नाल्पभोगवान्। नामृष्टो न नलिप्ताङ्गो नासुगन्धश्च विद्यते ॥
en: And all wore ear-rings and tiaras and garlands, and abundantly enjoyed the good things of life. And all were clean, daubing their limbs, and perfuming their persons.

sa_verse: नामृष्टभोजी नादाता नाप्यनङ्गदनिष्कधृक् । नाहस्ताभरणो वापि दृश्यते नाप्यनात्मवान्॥
en: And feeding on pure food, and giving away, and wearing Angadas (A bracelet worn upon the upper arm) and Niskas (An ornament for the breast or neck.) and hand-ornaments, and repressing passions.

sa_verse: नानाहिताग्नि यज्वा न क्षुद्रो वा न तस्करः। कश्चिदासीदयोध्यायां न चावृत्तो न संकरः॥
en: There were not on Ayodhyā persons not lighting the sacrificial fire, or not performing sacrifices, or mean-minded, or thieving, or engaged in improper occupations, or of impure descent.

sa_verse: स्वकर्मनिरता नित्यं ब्राह्मणा विजितेन्द्रियाः। दानाध्ययनशीलाश्च संयताश्च प्रतिग्रहे ॥
en: The Brāhmaṇas of subdued senses were always engaged in the performance of their own duties, giving away in charity, and studying, and receiving gifts with discrimination.

sa_verse: नास्तिको नानृती वापि न कश्चिदबहुश्रुतः। नासूयको न चाशक्तो नाविद्वान् विद्यते क्वचित्॥
en: None of them was atheistical or untruthful or be reft of hearing scriptures or detracting or incompetent or illiterate.

sa_verse: नाषडङ्गविदन्नास्ति नाव्रतो नासहस्रदः। नदीनः क्षिप्तचित्तो वा व्यथितो वापि कश्चन ॥
en: There was no Brāhmaṇa who was not versed in the Vedas and Vedangas, or poor-spirit, or of insane mind, or afflicted.

sa_verse: कश्चित्ररो वा नारी वा नाश्रीमान् नाप्यरूपवान्। द्रष्टुं शक्यमयोध्यायां नापि राजन्यभक्तिमान्॥
en: And no man and no woman was seen devoid of grace or beauty, or lacking in reverence for their monarch.

sa_verse: वर्णेष्वर्यचतुर्थेषु देवतातिथिपूजकाः। कृतज्ञाच वदान्याच शूरा विक्रमसंयुताः॥
en: The four orders with Brāhmanas at their head contained persons serving gods and guests, and endowed with gratitude, and munificent, and heroic, and possessing prowess.

sa_verse: दीर्घायुषो नराः सर्वे धर्म सत्यं च संश्रिताः। सहिताः पुत्रपौत्रैश्च नित्यं स्त्रीभिः पुरोत्तमे॥
en: And the men were long-lived; and ever abode by duty and truth; and lived in that best of cities, always surrounded by sons and grandsons and wives.

sa_verse: क्षत्रं ब्रह्ममुखं चासीत् वैश्याः क्षत्रमनुव्रताः। शूद्राः स्वकर्मनिरतास्त्रीन् वर्णानुपचारिणः॥
en: The Kşatriyas were obedient the Brahmanas, and the Vaisyas followed the Kșatriyas, and the sadras, occupied with their proper vocations, ministered to the three other orders.

sa_verse: सा तेनेक्ष्वाकुनाथेन पुरी सुपरिरक्षिता। यथा पुरस्तान्मनुना मानवेन्द्रेण धीमता॥
en: That city was ably govered by Dasaratha that lord of Ikşvāku's line, even as that foremost of men, the intelligent Manu, had governed it before him.

sa_verse: योधानामग्निकल्पानां पेशलानाममर्षिणाम्। सम्पूर्णा कृतविद्यानां गुहा केसरिणामिव।२१।।
en: And as a mountain-cavern abounds with lions, it was filled with warriors resembling flaming fire, of straight ways, unforbearing, and of accomplished learning. to

sa_verse: काम्बोजविषये जातैर्बाहीकैश्च हयोत्तमैः। वनायुजैनदीजैश्च पूर्णा हरिहयोत्तमैः॥
en: And the city abounded with excellent horses sprung in Kamboja, and Vahlika, and Vanayu, and the banks of the Sindhu, and like to that best of horses, Hari's charger.

sa_verse: विन्ध्यपर्वतजैर्मतैः पूर्णा हैमवतैरपि। मदान्वितैरतिबलैर्मातङ्गैः पर्वतोपमैः॥
en: With fierce elephants sprung on the Vindhya mountain, and the Himavat, filled with juice, and of exceeding strength, and resembling hills.

sa_verse: ऐरावतकुलीनैश्च महापद्मकुलैस्तथा। अञ्जनादपि निष्क्रान्तैमिनादपि च द्विपैः॥ भद्रैर्मन्दैमूंगैश्चैव भद्रमन्द्रमृगैस्तथा। भद्रमन्द्रर्भद्रमृगैर्मुगमन्द्रश्च सा पुरी॥ नित्यमनैः सदा पूर्णा नागैरचलसंनिभैः। सा योजने द्वे च भूयः सत्यनामा प्रकाशते।॥
en: And with Bhadra, Mandra, and Mrga* elephants; and those sprung from the mixture of the three, and from the mixture of Bhadra and Mandra, and froin Bhadra and Mrga, and from Mrga and Mandra, superior like to Airāvata, and coming from Mahapaama, Anjana, and Vamana breeds, fierce, and looking like hills. And that city was over two Yojanas; and truly it was called Ayodhya. (incapable of being conquered.) *(Those elephants whose limbs are contracted are bhadras; those whose bodies are fat, slack, and contracted are Mandras: and those whose bodies are lean and large are Mrgas.)

sa_verse: तां पुरी स महातेजा राजा दशरथो महान्। शशास शमितामित्रो नक्षत्राणीव चन्द्रमाः॥ तां सत्यनामां दृढतोरणार्गलां गृहैविचित्रैरुपशोभितां शिवाम्। पुरीमयोध्यां नृसहस्रसंकुला शशास वै शक्रसमो महीपतिः॥
en: Repressing enemies, that city was governed by the great and the exceedingly. And that lord of earth resembling Sakra govemed that city of Ayodhya bearing a true name furnished with strong gates and bolts, and auspicious, and graced with excellent edifices, and teeming with thousands.

sa_verse: तस्यामात्या गुणैरासत्रिक्ष्वाकोः सुमहात्मनः। मन्त्रज्ञाश्चेङ्गितज्ञाश्च नित्यं प्रियहिते रताः॥ अष्टौ बभूवुर्वीरस्य तस्यामात्या यशस्विनः। शुचयश्चानुरक्ताश्च राजकृत्येषु नित्यशः॥
en: That high-souled one of Ikşvāku's line had competent counsellors, capable of administering business, of diving into the motives of others, and ever intent upon the good of the monarch. And that heroic king had eight famous Counsellors, pure and devoted to the royal service.

sa_verse: धृष्टिर्जयन्तो विजयः सुराष्ट्रो राष्ट्रवर्धनः। अकोपो धर्मपालश्च सुमन्त्रश्चाष्टमोऽर्थवित्॥
en: They were, Dhrsti, Jayanta, and Vijaya, and Surăştra, and Răstravardhana, and Akopa and Dharmapala, and Sumantra the cight, conversant with profit.

sa_verse: ऋत्विजौ द्वावभिमतौ तस्यास्तामृषिसत्तमौ। वसिष्ठो वामदेवश्च मन्त्रिणश्च तथापरे॥
en: And he had two family priests beloved to him; viz., those foremost of saints. Vasistha and Vamadeva.

sa_verse: सुयज्ञोऽप्यथ जाबालिः काश्यपोऽप्यथ गौतमः। मार्कण्डेयस्तु दीर्घायुस्तथा का.यायनो द्विजः॥
en: And he had other counsellors besides, viz; Suyajna, and Javali, and Kasyapa, and Gautama, and the long-lived Markandeya, and the regenerate Katyayana.

sa_verse: एतैर्ब्रह्मर्षिभिनित्यमृत्विजस्तस्य पौर्वकाः। विद्याविनीता हीमन्तः कुशला नियतेन्द्रियाः॥ श्रीमन्तश्च महात्मानः शस्त्रज्ञा दृढविक्रमाः। कीर्तिमन्तः प्रगिहिता यथावचनकारिणः।७।। तेजः क्षमायशः प्राप्ताः स्मितपूर्वाभिभाषिणः। क्रोधात् कामार्थहेतोर्वा न ब्रुयुरनृतं वचः।८।।
en: Ever associated in counsel with these Brahmarsis, his priests and counsellors serving the dynasty from father to son, were learned yet modest. and bashful, and conversant with policy, and of subdued-sense, and auspicious, and highsouled, and accomplished in the art of arms, and of high renown, and cautious, and acting according to their word, and possessing energy, forgiveness and fame and ever precluding their speech with a smile, and never committing themselves to a lie either from anger or interest or desire.

sa_verse: तेषामविदितं किंचित् स्वेषु नास्ति परेषु वा। क्रियमाणं कृतं वापि चारेणापि चिकीर्षितम्॥ कुशला व्यवहारेषु सौहृदेषु परीक्षिताः। प्राप्तकालं यथा दण्डं धारयेयुः सुतेष्वपि॥
en: And they were not unknown to what was done or sought to be done in the midst of their own or a hostile party. And they were adept in intercourse with people, and well-tried in friendship by the monarch.

sa_verse: कोशसंग्रहणे युक्ता बलस्य च परिग्रहे। अहितं चापि पुरुषं न हिंस्युरविदूषकम्॥
en: And they were ever busy in replenishing the exchequer and in levying troops. And they did not cherish ill will even towards enemies, when innocent.

sa_verse: वीराश्च नियतोत्साहा राजशास्त्रमनुष्ठिताः। शुचीनां रक्षितारश्च नित्यं विषयवासिनाम्॥
en: And they were heroic, and ever high-spirited, following policy, and protecting those citizens that were pure.

sa_verse: ब्रह्मक्षत्रमहिंसन्तस्ते कोशं समपूरयन्। सुतीक्ष्णदण्डाः सम्प्रेक्ष्य पुरुषस्य बलाबलम्॥
en: And not bearing ill will towards Brahmaņas and Khatriyas, and filling the treasury, by inflicting punishments according lo the offences of the persons guilty.

sa_verse: शुचीनामेकबुद्धीनां सर्वेषां सम्पजानताम्। नासीत्पुरे वा राष्ट्रे वा मृषावादी नरः क्वचित्॥ क्वचिन्न दुष्टस्तत्रासीत् परटाररतिर्नरः। प्रशान्तं सर्वमेवासीद् राष्ट्र पुरवरं च तत्॥
en: And during the time when those pure ones of one mind presided over the justice of the kingdom, there was neither in the city nor the provinces any that was a liar, or 'wicked, or going after others' wives. And peace reigned all around the city and the provinces.

sa_verse: सुवाससः सुवेषाश्च ते च सर्वे शुचिव्रताः। हितार्थाश्च नरेन्द्रस्य जाग्रतो नयचक्षुषा॥
en: And the ministers wore excellent raiment, and ornaments, and were engaged in observing pure Vows, and ever kept their eye of policy open in the interest of the monarch.

sa_verse: गुरोर्गुणगृहीताश्च प्रख्याताश्च पराक्रमैः। विदेशेष्वपि विज्ञाताः सर्वतो बुद्धिनिश्चयाः॥
en: And the king considered them as crowned with virtues, and they were famed on account of their prowess, concluding unerringly in consequence of their intelligence of other countries. And in all climes and times they could manifest their noble qualities.

sa_verse: अभितो गुणवन्तश्च न चासन् गुणवर्जिताः। संधिविग्रहतत्त्वज्ञाः प्रकृत्या सम्पदान्विताः॥
en: They inculcated noble qualities in all aspects and were not devoid of virtues. And they were cognizant of war and peace, and possessed of goodness, passion and ignorance.

sa_verse: मन्त्रसंवरणे शक्ताः शक्ताः सूक्ष्मासु बुद्धिषु। नीतिशास्त्रविशेषज्ञाः सततं प्रियवादिनः॥
en: And they could keep their counsel and judge of things finely, and were well-versed in the art of policy, and ever fair-spoken.

sa_verse: ईदृशैस्तैरमात्यैश्च राजा दशरथोऽनघः। उपपन्नो गुणोपेतैरन्वशासत् वसुन्धराम्॥
en: Surrounded by such counseliors endowed with various qualities, the faultless king Dasaratha ruled the earth.

sa_verse: अवेक्ष्यमाणश्चारेण प्रजा धर्मेण रक्षयन्। प्रजानां पालनं कुर्वनधर्म परिवर्जयन्॥
en: And gathering intelligence by means of spies, and righteously protecting the subjects, and preserving the people, and not sacrificing his duties.

sa_verse: विश्रुतस्त्रिषु लोकेषु वदान्यः सत्यसंगरः। स तत्र पुरुषव्याघ्रः शशास पृथिवीमिमाम्॥
en: Famed over the three worlds, and munificent, and linn in promise in battle, the tiger among men ruled over this earth.

sa_verse: नाध्यगच्छद्विशिष्टं वा तुल्यं वा शत्रुमात्मनः। मित्रवानतसामन्तः प्रतापहतकण्टकः। स शशास जगत् राजा दिवि देवपतिर्यथा॥
en: Nor did he ever meet with a foe that was either his equal or superior. And possessed of friends, and having obedient commanders, and extricating his thorns by his might, that king ruled the earth, even as the lord of celestials rules heaven.

sa_verse: तैर्मन्त्रिभिर्मन्त्रिहितोनिविष्ठैर्वुतोऽनुरक्तैः कुशलैः समर्थैः। स पार्थिवो दीप्तिमवाप युक्तस्तेजोमयैर्गोभिरिवोदितोऽर्कः॥
en: And surrounded by those counsellors studious of his welfare, and bearing affection towards him, and clever, and competent, that king, by virtue of his prowess in subduing others, resembled the sun surrounded by his rays.

sa_verse: तस्य चैवंप्रभावस्य धर्मज्ञस्य महात्मनः। सुतार्थं तप्यमानस्य नासीद् वंशकरः सुतः॥
en: And although engaged in austerities with the view of having sons born to him, the powerful and high-souled king, had no son capable of perpetuating his race.

sa_verse: चिन्तयानस्य तस्यैवं बुद्धिरासीन्महात्मनः। सुतार्थं वाजिमेधेन किमर्थं न यजाम्यहम्॥
en: Mentally turning the matter over, the highsouled one thought, Why do I not celebrate a horse-sacrifice with the intention of obtaining a son?

sa_verse: स निश्चितां मतिं कृत्वा यष्टव्यमिति बुद्धिमान्। मन्त्रिभिः सह धर्मात्मा सर्वैरपि कृतात्मभिः॥ ततोऽब्रवीन्महातेजाः सुमन्त्रं मन्त्रिसत्तम। शीघ्रमानय मे सर्वान् गुरूंस्तान् सपुरोहितान्॥
en: That highly-energetic, pious and intelligent monarch, in consultation with all his counsellors of sedate minds, having made up his mind to celebrate the sacrifice, said to that best of counsellors, Sumantra, Do you speedily summon my spiritual guides, along with the family priests.

sa_verse: ततः सुमन्त्रस्त्वरितं गत्वा त्वरितविक्रमः। समानयत् स तान् सर्वान् समस्तान् वेदपारगान्॥
en: Thereupon, going out speeding, Sumantra of swift movements called together all the spiritual guides, as well as others versed in the Vedic ritual.

sa_verse: सुयज्ञं वामदेवं च जाबालिमथ काश्यपम्। पुरोहितं वसिष्ठं च ये चाप्यन्ये द्विजोत्तमाः॥ तान् पूजयित्वा धर्मात्मा राजा दशरथस्तदा। इदं धर्मार्थसहितं श्लक्ष्णं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: They were Suyajña, and Vămadeva, and Jabali, and Kasyapa, and Vasistha, and other principal twice-born ones. And having paid homage to them, the virtuous king Dasaratha then spoke to them these sweet words.

sa_verse: मम लालप्यमानस्य सुतार्थं नास्ति वै सुखम्। तदर्थं हयमेधेन यक्ष्यामीति मतिर्मम॥
en: Consistent with duty and interest,-Ever pining on account of a son, I know no happiness,-therefore it is my intention that I should celebrate a horse sacrifice.

sa_verse: तदहं यष्टुमिच्छामि शास्त्रदृष्टेन कर्मणा। कथं प्राप्स्याम्यहं कामं बुद्धिरत्र विचिन्त्यताम्॥
en: I intend to celebrate it according to the ordinance. Do you, therefore, consider how I may attain my object.

sa_verse: ततः साध्विति तद्वाक्यं ब्राह्मणाः प्रत्यपूजयन्। वसिष्ठप्रमुखाः सर्वे पार्थिवस्य मुखेरितम्॥
en: Thereat, the Brāhmaṇas with Vasiştha at their head, exclaiming well! Well 1 approved the words that had fallen from the lips of the monarch.

sa_verse: ऊचुश्च परमप्रीताः सर्वे दशरथं वचः। सम्भाराः सम्ध्रियन्तां ते तुरगश्च विमुच्यताम् ॥ सरय्वाश्चोत्तरे तीरे यज्ञभूमिर्विधीयताम्। सर्वथा प्राप्स्यसे पुत्रानभिप्रेतांश्च पार्थिव ॥ यस्य ते धार्मिकी बुद्धिरियं पुत्रार्थमागता।
en: And exceedingly pleased, they asked Daſaratha,-“Do you order the necessary articles, loose the horse, and prepare the sacrificial ground on the north bank of the Sarayû." And, king, since with the intention of obtaining offspring your purpose so piously, you will surely obtain sons after your heart.

sa_verse: ततस्तुष्टोऽभवद् राजा श्रुत्वैतद् द्विजभाषितम्॥ अमात्यानब्रवीद् राजा हर्षव्याकुललोचनः। सम्भाराः सम्भ्रियन्तां मे गुरूणां वचनादिह ॥ समर्थाधिष्ठितश्चाश्वः सोपाध्यायो विमुच्यताम्। सरय्वाश्चोत्तरे तीरे यज्ञभूमिर्विधीयताम्॥ शान्तयश्चापि वर्धन्तां यथाकल्पं यथाविधि। शक्यः प्राप्तुमयं यज्ञः सर्वेणापि महीक्षिता ॥ नापराधो भवेद् कष्टो यद्यस्मिन् क्रतुसत्तमे। छिद्रं हि मृगयन्ते स्म विद्वांसो ब्रह्मराक्षसा ॥
en: And hearing these words of the regenerate ones, the king was highly gratified. And with eyes expanded in delight, he asked his ministers, Do to procure the necessary sacrificial articles, according to the injunction of my spiritual preceptors; and loose a horse protected by a competent person, and followed by one of the chief family priests; and prepare the sacrificial ground on the north bank of the Sarayū; and in due order and according to the ordinance perform the śānti rites required to secure an uninterrupted completion to the ceremony. This ceremony is incapable of being celebrated by every king. Particular care should be taken that the sacrifice is not defective on account of any serious omission; inasmuch as with learned Brahma-Rākṣasas ever on the look-out to spy shortcomings in the ceremony.

sa_verse: विधिहीनस्य यज्ञस्य सद्यः कर्ता विनश्यति। तद्यथा विधिपूर्वं मे क्रतुरेष समाप्यते॥
en: The performer thereof speedily perishes, should anything take place not consonant to the ordinance. And do you possessed of ability so arrange, that this sacrifice may be completed in harmony with the ritual.

sa_verse: तथा विधानं क्रियतां समर्थाः साधनेष्विति। तथेति चाब्रुवन् सर्वे मन्त्रिणः प्रतिपूजिताः॥
en: Thus addressed with due respect, the counsellors listened to the word of the monarch, and said, So be it.

sa_verse: पार्थिवेन्द्रस्य तद् वाक्यं यथापूर्वं निशम्य ते। तथा द्विजास्ते धर्मज्ञा वर्धयन्तो नृपोत्तमम्॥ अनुज्ञातास्ततः सर्वे पुनर्जग्मुर्यथागतम्।
en: Then hearing those words of that best king again, as heard before, conversant with their duty, having blessed the monarch, taking permission of the king, they returned to their respective quarters.

sa_verse: विसर्जयित्वा तान् विप्रान् सचिवानिदमब्रवीत्॥ ऋत्विग्भिरुपसंदिष्टो यथावत् क्रतुराप्यताम्।
en: Dismissing those Brāhmaṇas, the king spoke to his ministers, saying, Do you, even as the family priests have ordered, arrange for the sacrifice!

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वानृपशार्दूलः सचिवान् समुपस्थितान्॥ विसर्जयित्वा स्वं वेश्म प्रविवेश महामतिः।
en: Having said this, that mighty-minded tiger among nen dismissed his ministers, and himself entered into the inner apartment.

sa_verse: ततः स गत्वा ताः पत्नीनरेन्द्रो हृदयंगमाः॥ उवाच दीक्षां विशत यक्ष्येऽहं सुतकारणात् ।
en: And coming there, that lord of men said to his favourite wives, Do you know it for certain that in order to obtain a son I am going to perform a sacrifice.

sa_verse: तासां तेनातिकान्तेन वचनेन सुवर्चसाम्। मुखपद्मान्यशोभन्त पद्मानीव हिमात्यये॥
en: And hearing those sweet words, the countenances of those shining dames looked resplendent, like lotuses after the cold season is over.

sa_verse: ऋत्विग्भिरुपदिष्टोऽयं पुरावृत्तो मया श्रुतः। सनत्कुमारो भगवान् पूर्वं कथितवान् कथाम्॥ ऋषीणां संनिधौ राजंस्तव पुत्रागमं प्रति।
en: This horse-sacrifice is enjoined by the family priests; and I have myself heard the following story celebrated in ancient chronicle. And what the worshipful Sanatkumāra had said formerly in the presence of the saints, applies, O king, to the case of your having a son.

sa_verse: काश्यपस्य च पुत्रोऽस्ति विभाण्डक इति श्रुतः॥ ऋष्यशृङ्ग इति ख्यातस्तस्य पुत्रो भविष्यति। स वने नित्यसंवृद्धो मुनिर्वनचरः सदा ॥
en: Kaśyapa has a son known by the name of Vibhāņdaka. He will get a son called Rșyasſiga. And he will grow up and pass his days in the forests.

sa_verse: तस्यैवं वर्तमानस्य काल: समभिवर्तत॥ अग्नि शुश्रूषमाणस्य पितरं च यशस्विनम्। एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु रोमपातः प्रतापवान्॥ अङ्गेषु प्रथितो राजा भविष्यति महाबलः। तस्य व्यतिक्रमाद् राज्ञो भविष्यति सुदारुणा ॥ अनावृष्टिः सुघोरा वै सर्वलोकभयावहा।
en: And he spent some time in serving the sacrificial fire and his famous sire. At this time, the powerful Romapāda of exceeding strength will be famed as king of the Angas. And in consequence of some default on his part, there will occur in his kingdom a terrible and dreadful drought, capable of striking terror in the all.

sa_verse: अनावृष्टयां तु वृत्तायां राजा दुःखसमन्वितः॥ ब्राह्मणाञ्छ्रुतसंवृद्धान् समानीय प्रवक्ष्यति। भवन्तः श्रुतकर्माणो लोकचारित्रवेदिनः॥ समादिशन्तु नियमं प्रायश्चित्तं यथा भवेत्।
en: And filled with grief on account of this drought, the king will call about him Vedaaccomplished Brāhmaṇas, and speak to them, saying, You are conversant with the Vedic ritual and the social duties. Do therefore, tell me how to expiate for this evil.

sa_verse: इत्युक्तास्ते ततो राज्ञा सर्वे ब्राह्मणसत्तमाः॥ वक्ष्यन्ति ते महीपालं ब्राह्मणा वेदपारगाः।
en: Thus accosted by the king, those excellent Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vedas, will say to the ruler of earth.

sa_verse: विभाण्डकसुतं राजन् सर्वापायैरिहानय ॥ आनाय्य तु महीपाल ऋष्यशृङ्ग सुसत्कृतम्। विभाण्डकसुतं राजन् ब्राह्मणं वेदपारगम्। प्रयच्छ कन्यां शान्तां वै विधिना सुसमाहितः॥
en: Do you, O monarch, by all means, bring Vibhandaka's son. And having, Oking, brought that Brāhmaṇa versed in the Vedas, Vibhandaka's son Rsyaśộnga, and duly honoured him, do you, O monarch, with a concentrated mind, perform the betrothal of your daughter śāntā, with him according to the ordinance.

sa_verse: तेषां तु वचनं श्रुत्वा राजा चिन्तां प्रपत्स्यते। केनोपायेन वै शक्यमिहानेतुं स वीर्यवान्॥
en: And hearing those words of theirs, the king will began to think as to how he can bring over that one endowed with energy.

sa_verse: ततो राजा विनिश्चित्य सह मन्त्रिभिरात्मवान्। पुरोहितममात्यांश्च प्रेषयिष्यति सत्कृतान्॥
en: Then in consultation with his counsellors, the prudent king having come to a conclusion will, honouring them duly, desire his priest and his courtiers to set out in quest of Rsyaśộnga.

sa_verse: ते तु राज्ञो वचः श्रुत्वा व्यथिता विनताननाः। न गच्छेम ऋषेीता अनुनेयन्ति तं नृपम्॥
en: Thereupon hearing the king's words, with aggrieved hearts, and with heads hanging down, they will beseech the monarch, saying, Afraid of the saint, Vibhāndaka, we shall not be able to repair thither.

sa_verse: वक्ष्यन्ति चिन्तयित्वा ते तस्योपायांश्च तान् क्षमान्। आनेयामो वयं विप्रं न च दोषो भविष्यति॥
en: Anon hitting upon the appropriate means, they say, 'We will search for the Vipra, and no blame shall accrue to us.'

sa_verse: एवमङ्गाधिपेनैव गणिकाभिषेः सुतः। आनीतोऽवर्षयत् देवः शान्ता चास्मै प्रदीयते॥
en: Thus by help of courtesans, the saint's son was brought by the lord of the Angas. And then the god (Indra) poured down showers; and the king conferred on him śānta.

sa_verse: अथ हृष्टो दशरथः सुमन्त्रं प्रत्यभाषत। यथर्घ्यशृङ्गस्त्वानीतो येनोपायेन सोच्यताम्॥
en: Thereupon king Dasaratha, well pleased, asked Sumantra, Do you now tell me by what means Rşyaśộnga was brought over (by the lord of the Angas).

sa_verse: सुमन्त्रश्चोदितो राज्ञा प्रोवाचेदं वचस्तदा। यथर्घ्यशृङ्गस्त्वानीतो येनोपायेन मन्त्रिभिः॥
en: Thus asked by the king, Sumantra said these words-I will relate to you how the counsellors brought Rşyaśộnga. Do you listen with your counsellors!

sa_verse: रोमपादमुवाचेदं सहामात्यः पुरोहितः। उपायो निरपायोऽयमस्माभिरभिचिन्तितः॥
en: The priest together with the counsellors spoke to Romapada, saying, 'The means that we have hit upon can never fail of effect.'

sa_verse: ऋष्यशृङ्गो वनचरस्तपः स्वाध्यायसंयुतः। अनभिज्ञस्तु नारीणां विषयाणां सुखस्य च॥
en: Rşyaśộnga has been brought up in woods; and is engaged in austerities and the study of the Vedas. He is ignorant of the pleasure that ensues from contact with women,

sa_verse: इन्द्रियार्थैरभिमतैनरचित्तप्रमाथिभिः। पुरमानाययिष्यामः क्षिप्रं चाध्यवसीयताम्॥
en: By help of things agreeably ministering to the sense, and ravishing the soul, we shall bring him to the city. Do you, therefore, arrange for them!

sa_verse: गणिकास्तत्र गच्छन्तु रूपवत्यः स्वलंकृताः। प्रलोभ्य विविधोपायैरानेयन्तीह सत्कृताः॥
en: Let courtezans of comely presence, clad in ornaments, repair thither. And if well treated, they will by various means bring him here.'

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा तथेति राजा च प्रत्युवाच पुरोहितम्। पुरोहितो मन्त्रिणश्च तदा चक्रुश्च ते तथा॥
en: Hearing this, the king said to the priest, 'So be it!' Then the priest and courtiers acted accordingly.
