en: On beholding the peaks of Citrakuța, one reaps welfare, and ignorance does not envelope one's mind.

sa_verse: ऋषयस्तत्र बहवो विहृत्य शरदां शतम्। तपसा दिवमारूढाः कपालशिरसा सह॥
en: There innumerable saints with the hair of their heads rendered white like skulls, having spent hundreds of years, have through austerities ascended heaven.

sa_verse: प्रविविक्तमहं मन्ये तं वासं भवतः सुखम्। इह वा वनवासाय वस राम मया सह ॥
en: That solitary spot I deem as one which will make a happy residence for you. O Rāma, do you, for living out the term of your banishment, dwell either here or with me.

sa_verse: स रामं सर्वकामैस्तं भरद्वाजः प्रियातिथिम्। सभार्यं सह च भ्रात्रा प्रतिजग्राह हर्षयन्॥
en: Bharadvāja entertained his welcome guest, Rāma, along with his wife and brother by extending towards them every rite of hospitality.

sa_verse: तस्य प्रयागे रामस्य तं महर्षिमुपेयुषः। प्रपन्ना रजनी पुण्या चित्राः कथयत: कथाः॥
en: At Prayāga Rāma having obtained the company of that Maharși, and discoursing on a variety of sacred topics, the night came on.

sa_verse: सीतातृतीयः काकुत्स्थः परिश्रान्तः सुखोचितः। भरद्वाजाश्रमे रम्ये तां रात्रिमवसत् सुखम्॥
en: And with Sītā for the third, Kåkutstha brought up in luxury, being fatigued, pleasantly spent that night at the romantic hermitage of Bharadvāja.

sa_verse: प्रभातायां तु शर्वर्यां भरद्वाजमुपागमत्। उवाच नरशार्दूलो मुनिं ज्वलिततेजसम्॥
en: When the night had passed away and day dawned, (Rāma) approached Bharadvāja, and that chief of men addressed that ascetic of flaming energy.

sa_verse: शर्वरीं भगवनद्य सत्यशील तवाश्रमे। उषिताः स्मोऽह वसतिमनुजानातु नो भवान्॥
en: O reverend sire of truthful character, we have here in your asylum spent the night. Do you now permit us to set forward (for Citrakuta).

sa_verse: रात्र्यां तु तस्यां व्युष्टायां भरद्वाजोऽब्रवीदिदम्। मधुमूलफलोपेतं चित्रकूटं व्रजेति ह॥ वासमौपयिकं मन्ये तव राम महाबल। नानानगगणोपेतः किन्नरोरगसेवितः॥ मयूरनादाभिरतो गजराजनिषेवितः। गम्यतां भवता शैलश्चित्रकूटः स विश्रुतः॥
en: On Rāma having passed the night there, Bharadvāja spoke to him, “Do you race your steps to Citrakuța filled with delicious fruits and roots. I deem that, О mighty Rāma, as a fit abode for you, being, as it is, furnished with various kinds of trees, inhabited by crowds of Kinnars, resounding with the cries of peacocks, and frequented by gigantic elephants.

sa_verse: पुण्यश्च रमणीयश्च बहुमूलफलायुतः। तत्र कुञ्जरयूथानि मृगयूथानि चैव हि ॥ विचरन्ति वनान्तेषु तानि द्रक्ष्यसि राघव। सरित्प्रस्रवणप्रस्थान् दरीकन्दरनिर्झरान्। चरतः सीतया सार्धं नन्दिष्यति मनस्तव।॥
en: Do you repair to the famed Citrakuţa, holy, fair to the view, and abounding in countless fruits and roots. And in those forests range herds of elephants and deer; and these, O Rāghava, you will behold. And ranging with Sītā rivers and rills and plateau's, caverns and fountains, your mind will experience delight.

sa_verse: विनोदयन्तं च सुखं परं शिवम्। मृगैश्च मत्तैर्बहुभिश्च कुञ्जरैः सुरम्यमासाद्य समावसाश्रयम्॥
en: Delighted with the notes of joyous Koyastivas and coels, and the cries of deer and coundess mad elephants, do you, arriving at the auspicious mountain, reside at the romantic asylum.

sa_verse: उषित्वा रजनीं तत्र राजपुत्रावरिंदमौ। महर्षिमभिवाद्याथ जग्मतुस्तं गिरिं प्रति॥
en: Having spent the night there, those princes, repressors of their foes, after paying their obeisance to the Maharși, set out for the mountain.

sa_verse: तेषां स्वस्त्ययनं चैव महर्षिः स चकार ह। प्रस्थितान् प्रेक्ष्य तांश्चैव पिता पुत्रानिवौरसान् ॥
en: Seeing them about to set forth, that Maharși performed a propitiatory ceremony for them, even as a father does on behalf of the sons begot from his own loins.

sa_verse: ततः प्रचक्रमे वक्तुं वचनं स महामुनिः। भरद्वाजो महातेजा रामं सत्यपराक्रमम्॥
en: And that mighty ascetic, Bharadvāja having truth for his prowess, addressed them, saying.

sa_verse: गङ्गायमुनयोः संधिमादाय मनुजर्षभ। कालिन्दीमनुगच्छेतां नदी पश्चान्मुखाश्रिताम्॥
en: O best of men, do you coming to the confluence of the Gangā and the Yamunā proceed along the Kalindi river flowing westwards.

sa_verse: अथासाद्य तु कालिन्दी प्रतिस्रोतः समागताम्। तस्यास्तीर्थं प्रचरितं प्रकामं प्रेक्ष्य राघव। तत्र यूयं प्लवं कृत्वा तरतांशुमती नदीम्॥
en: Arriving at the Kalindi running in a contrary direction, you will, O Rāghava, behold a goodly bathing place well-worn by foot-passengers. There constructing a raft, do you cross over the river who is the daughter of the Sun.

sa_verse: स पन्थाश्चित्रकूटस्य गतस्य बहुशो मया। रम्यो मार्दवयुक्तश्च दावैश्चैव विवर्जितः॥
en: I went to Citrakuța many a time by that road, which is beautiful, sandy, and free from forestfire.

sa_verse: इति पन्थानमादिश्य महर्षिः संन्यवर्तत। अभिवाद्य तथेत्युक्त्वा रामेण विनिवर्तितः॥
en: Having directed the way, the Maharsi paused. Thereupon Rāma, saying, “So be it," asked him to stop.

sa_verse: उपावृत्ते मुनौ तस्मिन् रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्। कृतपुण्याः स्म भद्रं ते मुनिर्यन्नोऽनुकम्पते॥
en: On the ascetic turning away, Rāma spoke to Lakṣmaṇa, “We had surely acquired religious merit, good betide you, since, the ascetic has shown compassion to us.

sa_verse: इति तौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ मन्त्रयित्वा मनस्विनौ। सीतामेवाग्रतः कृत्वा कालिन्दी जग्मतुर्नदीम्॥
en: Having thus conversed with each other, those foremost of men endowed with intelligence, place Sītā in their front, proceeded towards the rive Kalindi.

sa_verse: अथासाद्यतुकालिन्दी शीघ्रस्रोतस्विनी नदीम्। चिन्तामापेदिरे सद्यो नदीजलतितीर्षवः॥
en: Having arrived at the Kālindī of rapid currents, they desirous of crossing over, began to think (as to the means).

sa_verse: तौ काष्ठसंघाटमथो चक्रतुः सुमहाप्लवम्। शुष्कैर्वंयैः समाकीर्णमुशीरैश्च समावृतम्॥ ततो वैतसशाखाश्च जम्बुशाखाश्च वीर्यवान्। चकार लक्ष्मणश्छित्त्वा सीतायाः सुखमासनम्॥
en: Then with heaps of dry wild wood covered with grass, they constructed a large raft. And the puissant Lakşmaņa tearing twigs from the ratan and the rose-apple, made a comfortable seat for Sita.

sa_verse: तत्र श्रियमिवाचिन्त्यां रामो दाशरथिः प्रियाम्। ईषत्स लज्जमानां तामध्यारोपयत प्लवम्॥ पार्वे तत्र च वैदेह्या वसने भूषणानि च। प्लवे कठिनकाजंच रामश्चके समाहितः॥
en: Then Dasaratha's son, Rāma, made his bashful wife (in power) inconceivable like to Śrī herself, ascend the raft, and carefully laid beside Vaidehī her attires and ornaments as well as the hoe and the basket.

sa_verse: आरोप्य सीतां प्रथमं संघाटं परिगृह्य तौ ततः प्रतेरतुर्यत्तौ प्रीतौ दशरथात्मजौ॥
en: And first having placed Sītā on the raft, those sons of Daśaratha ascended themselves, and with glad heares began carefully to cross (the stream)..

sa_verse: कालिन्दीमध्यमायाता सीता त्वेनामवन्दत। स्वस्ति देवि तरामि त्वां पारयेन्मे पतिव्रतम्॥ यक्ष्ये त्वां गोसहस्रेण सुराघटशतेन च। स्वस्ति प्रत्यागते रामे पुरीमिक्ष्वाकुपालिताम्॥
en: Having come near the middle of the Kalindi, Sītā prayed to her, saying, 'Hail to you, O goddess! I cross you. If my husband can successfully perform his vow, I will worship you with a thousand cows and an hundred vessels of wine, hail to you, upon Rāma's return to the city ruled by Ikşvāki

sa_verse: कालिन्दीमथ सीता तु याचमाना कृताञ्जलिः। तीरमेवाभिसम्प्राप्ता दक्षिणं वरवर्णिनी॥
en: Having thus prayed to Kālindī with joined hands, that virtuous lady, Sītā, reached the southern bank thereof.

sa_verse: ततः प्लवेनांशुमती शीघ्रगामूर्मिमालिनीम्। तीरजैर्बहुभिवृक्षैः संतेरुर्यमुना नदीम्॥
en: And by means of that raft they crossed that fleet-coursing daughter of the Sun, heaving with billows, the river Yamunā with her banks abounding with innumerable trees.

sa_verse: तेषु ते प्लवमुत्सृज्य प्रस्थाय यमुनावनात्। श्यामं न्यग्रोधमासेदुः शीतलं हरितच्छदम्॥
en: Then renouncing the raft, and passing by the woods adjoining the Yamunā, they came upon a banian Syāma by name, of cool shade and verdant foliage.

sa_verse: न्यग्रोधं समुपागम्य वैदेही चाभ्यवन्दत। नमस्तेऽस्तु महावृक्ष पारयेन्मे पतिव्रतम्॥ कौसल्यां चैव पश्येम सुमित्रां च यशस्विनीम्। इति सीताञ्जलिं कृत्वा पर्यगच्छन्मनस्विनी॥
en: On coming to the banian, Vaidehī saluted it saying, “O mighty tree, I bow to you. May my husband fulfil his vow; and may we behold Kausalyā and the illustrious Sumitrā.” Having thus prayed with joined hands, the intelligent Sītā went away.

sa_verse: अवलोक्य ततः सीतामायाचन्तीमनिन्दिताम्। दयितां च विधेयां च रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥
en: Seeing the blameless and beloved Sītā ever conducting herself properly, praying, Rāma said to Laksmana.

sa_verse: सीतामादाय गच्छ त्वमग्रतो भरतानुज । पृष्ठतोऽनुगमिष्यामि सायुधो द्विपदां वर॥
en: Do you, O younger brother of Bharata, taking Sītā with you, go forward. O best of men, furnished with weapons, I will go in your wake.

sa_verse: यद् यत् फलं प्रार्थयते पुष्पं वा जनकात्मजा। तत् तत् प्रयच्छ वैदेह्या यत्रास्या रमते मनः।२८।।
en: Do you procure Vaidehī with whatever fruits or flowers may please 'her and she may wish to have.

sa_verse: एकैकं पादपं गुल्म लतां वा पुष्पशालिनीम्। अदृष्टरूपां पश्यन्ती रामं पप्रच्छ साऽबला॥
en: Seeing every tree and shrub crowned with blossoms unseen before, that one belonging to the softer see questioned Rāma about it.

sa_verse: रमणीयान् बहुविधान् पादपान् कुसुमोत्करान्। सीतावचनसंरब्ध आनयामास लक्ष्मणः॥
en: And hearing Sita's words, Laksmana brought to her (fruits and flowers) of beautiful and flower-scattering trees of divers kinds.

sa_verse: विचित्रवालुकजलां हंससारसनादिताम्। रेमे जनकराजस्य सुता प्रेक्ष्य तदा नदीम्॥
en: And beholding streams with water flowing over glittering sands and resounding with cranes of various kinds, the daughter of king Janaka felt exceeding delight.

sa_verse: क्रोशमात्रं ततो गत्वा भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। बहून् मेध्यान् मृगान् हत्वा चेस्तुर्यमुनावने ॥
en: And having proceeded just a Krosa, those brothers, Rāma and Lakşmaņa, having killed many a sacred deer, began to range in the woods of the Yarnunā.

sa_verse: विहृत्य ते बर्हिणपूगनादिते शुभे वने वारणवानरायुते। समं नदीवप्रमुपेत्य सत्वरं निवासमाजग्मुरदीनदर्शनाः॥
en: And having disported in the beautiful woods resounding with multitudes of peacocks and inhabited by elephants and monkeys, they looking as lively as ever, coming to the level banks of the river, took up their quarters there.

sa_verse: अथ राज्यां व्यतीतायामवसुप्तमनन्तरम्। प्रबोधयामास शनैर्लक्ष्मणं रघुपुङ्गवः॥
en: When the night had been spent, that best of Raghus gently awakened Lakşmaņa from his light sleep.

sa_verse: सौमित्रे शृणु वन्यानां वल्गु व्याहरतां स्वनम्। सम्प्रतिष्ठामहे कालः प्रस्थानस्य परंतप॥
en: O Sumiträ's son, do you hear the dulcet notes of the birds in the woods. Let us proceed. O repressor of foes, the time of our departure is present.

sa_verse: प्रसुप्तस्तु ततो भ्रात्रा समये प्रतिबोधितः। जहौ निद्रां च तन्द्रां च प्रसक्तं च परिश्रमम्॥
en: Awakened at the proper time, Rāma's brother left off sleep and drowsiness and clinging fatigue.

sa_verse: तत उत्थाय ते सर्वे स्पृष्ट्वा नद्याः शिवं जलम्। पन्थानमृषिभिर्जुष्टं चित्रकूटस्य तं ययुः॥
en: Then they all arising touched the sacred waters of the river, and began to proceed on the way to Citrakuța inhabited by ascetics.

sa_verse: ततः सम्प्रस्थितः काले रामः सौमित्रिणा सह। सीतां कमलपत्राक्षीमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Setting out in season with Sumitra's son, he of eyes resembling lotus-petais spoke these words to Sītā.

sa_verse: आदीप्तानिव वैदेहि सर्वतः पुष्पितान् नगान्। स्वैः पुष्पैः किंशुकान् पश्य मालिनः शिशिरात्यये॥ पश्य भल्लातकान् बिल्वान् नरैरनुपसेवितान्। फलपुष्पैरवनतान् नूनं शक्ष्याम जीवितुम्॥
en: O Vaidehi, behold these flowering trees, the Kinsukas in spring appearing engarlanded with their own flowers, and as if flaming., Do you behold the Bhallatakas and Bilvas bending beneath their fruits and flowers, with no man to enjoy them. Surely, we: shall be able to live here.

sa_verse: पश्य द्रोणप्रमाणानि लम्बमानानि लक्ष्मण। मधूनि मधुकारीभिः सम्भृतानि नगे नगे॥
en: Behold, O Laksmana, these honeycombs measuring about a Droņa have been hung up on trees by the bees.

sa_verse: एष क्रोशति नत्यूहस्तं शिखी प्रतिकूजति। रमणीये वनोद्देशे पुष्पसंस्तरसंकटे॥
en: In the charming woods overarched by flowers, the Datyuha cries, and is responded to by the peacock.

sa_verse: मातङ्गयूथानुसृतं पक्षिसंघानुनादितम्। चित्रकूटमिमं पश्य प्रवृद्धशिखरं गिरिम्॥
en: Do you behold Citrakuta frequented by mad elephants and resonant with the voice of multitudes of birds, the mountain with its towering summits.

sa_verse: समभूमितले रम्ये दुमैर्बहुभिरावृते। पुण्ये रंस्यामहे तात चित्रकूटस्य कानने॥
en: O child, we will disport in the sacred woods of Citrakuta with fine level plains, and covered with divers trees.

sa_verse: ततस्तौ पादचारेण गच्छन्तौ सह सीतया। रम्यमासेदतुः शैलं चित्रकूटं मनोरमम् ॥
en: Then they proceeding on foot along with Sītā, arrived at the charming and beautiful mountain Citrakuta.

sa_verse: तं तु पर्वतमासाद्य नानापक्षिगणायुतम्। बहुमूलफलं रम्यं सम्पन्नसरसोदकम्॥
en: Arriving at the mountain inhabited by birds of various kinds, abounding in fruits and roots, and furnished with watery expanses, (Răma) said.

sa_verse: मनोज्ञोऽयं गिरिः सौम्य नानादुमलतायुतः। बहुमूलफलो रम्यः स्वाजीवः प्रतिभाति मे॥
en: O amiable one, methinks this beautiful hill furnished with innumerable fruits and roots, is fraught with subsistence.

sa_verse: मुनयश्च महात्मानो वसन्त्यस्मिशिलोच्चये। अयं वासो भवेत् तात वयमत्र वसेमहि ॥
en: And the hill is inhabited by high-souled ascetics. Let this, O child, be our abode. We will dwell here.

sa_verse: इति सीता च रामश्च लक्ष्मणश्च कृताञ्जलिः। अभिगम्याश्रमं सर्वे वाल्मीकिमभिवादयन्॥
en: Then Ráma, Lakşmana and Sītā with joined hands presented themselves at the asylum of Vālmīki and saluted him.

sa_verse: तान् महर्षिः प्रमुदितः पूजयामास धर्मवित्। आस्यतामिति चोवाच स्वागतं तं निवेद्य च॥
en: Thereat the Maharși cognizant of morality said to them, "Be seated!” and addressed Rāma, saying, “Has your journey been a pleasant one?”

sa_verse: ततोऽब्रवीन्महाबाहुर्लक्ष्मणं लक्ष्मणाग्रजः। संनिवेद्य यथान्यायमात्मानमृषये प्रभुः॥
en: Then having duly acquainted the saint with matters pertaining to himself, that lord, the mighty-armed elder brother of Laksmana, said to the latter.

sa_verse: लक्ष्मणानय दारूणि दृढानि च वराणि च। कुरुष्वावसथं सौम्य वासे मेऽभिरतं मनः॥
en: O Lakşmaņa, bring you wood good and strong. O amiable one, construct a dwelling. My mind is set upon staying here.

sa_verse: तस्य तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा सौमित्रिर्विविधान् गुमान्। आजहार ततश्चके पर्णशालामरिंदमः॥
en: Hearing his words, Sumitra's son procured woods of various descriptions, and then that subduer of foes reared a cottage thatched with leaves.

sa_verse: तां निष्ठितां बद्धकटां दृष्ट्वा रामः सुदर्शनाम्। शुश्रूषमाणमेकाग्रमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Beholding that goodly dwelling walled with wood furnished with doors, Rāma addressed these words and Lakşmaņa, intent upon ministering to his brother.

sa_verse: ऐणेयं मांसमाहृत्य शालां यक्ष्यामहे वयम्। कर्तव्यं वास्तुशमनं सौमित्रे चिरजीविभिः॥
en: Procuring meat, we will worship the deity presiding over this dwelling. O Sumitrā's son, those who wish to live long, should pacify the household gods.

sa_verse: मृगं हत्वानय क्षिप्रं लक्ष्मणेह शुभेक्षण। कर्तव्यः शास्त्रदृष्टो हि विधिर्धर्ममनुस्मर॥
en: O Lakşmaņa graced with auspicious eyes, do you killing deer, swiftly bring it here. It behoves us to observe the rules prescribed by the scriptures. Do you follow the ordinance.

sa_verse: भ्रातुर्वचनमाज्ञाय लक्ष्मणः परवीरहा। चकार च यथोक्तं हि तं रामः पुनरब्रवीत्॥
en: Acquainted with the words of his brother, Lakşmaņa, slayer of hostile heroes did as he was told. Thereupon Rāma addressed him again.

sa_verse: ऐणेयं श्रपयस्वैतच्छालां यक्ष्यामहे वयम्। त्वर सौम्यमुहूर्तोऽयं ध्रुवश्च दिवसो ह्ययम्॥
en: Do you cook this meat. We will worship the presiding deities of this mansion. Bestir yourself, the moment is mild and the day is styled Dhruva.

sa_verse: स लक्ष्मणः कृष्णमृगं हत्वा मेध्यं प्रतापवान्। अथ चिक्षेप सौमित्रिः समिद्धे चातवेदसि॥
en: Then Lakşmana the son of Sumitrā endowed with vigour, having slain a sacred black deer, threw it into flaming fire.

sa_verse: तत् तु पक्वं समाज्ञाय निष्टप्तं छिन्नशोणितम्। लक्ष्मणः पुरुषव्याघ्रमथ राघवमब्रवीत्॥
en: And seeing it well scorched and hot and free from blood, Lakşmaņa spoke to that foremost of men, Rāghava, saying.

sa_verse: अयं सर्वः समस्ताङ्गः शृतः कृष्णमृगो मया। देवता देवसंकाश यजस्व कुशलो ह्यसि॥ रामः स्नात्वा तु नियतो गुणवाञ्जपकोविदः। संग्रहेणाकरोत् सर्वान् मन्त्रान् सत्रावसानिकान्॥ इष्ट्वा देवगणान् सर्वान् विवेशावसथं शुचिः। बभूव च मनोह्वादो रामस्यामिततेजसः॥
en: Here is the entire black deer roasted by me, capable of serving any purpose. Do you, O you that resemble a celestial, worship the gods. Having performed his ablutions, Rāma conversant with the ritual and possessing a knowledge of Japa, restraining his senses, performed all the mantras that are necessary for completing a sacrifice; and having in a pure spirit communed with all the deities, entered the habitation.

sa_verse: वैश्वदेवबलिं कृत्वा रौद्रं वैष्णवमेव च। वास्तुसंशमनीयानि मङ्गलानि प्रवर्तयन्॥
en: And (this having been done), Rāma of immeasurable energy rejoiced exceedingly. And sacrificing to Vaisva, Rudra and Visnu, he performed some ceremonies for removing malign influences from the abode.

sa_verse: जपं च न्यायतः कृत्वा स्नात्वा नद्यां यथाविधि। पापसंशमनं रामश्चकार बलिमुत्तमम्॥ वेदिस्थलविधानानि चैत्यान्यायतनानि च। आश्रमस्यानुरूपाणि स्थापयामास राघवः॥
en: Having duly performed Japa and bathed is consonance with the ordinance, Rāma made an excellent sacrifice for removing sin. And then Rāghava established a dais, and a Caitya proportionate to the abode.* The N.W.P. text reads:, "And in that romantic. forest abounding in various kinds of beast and birds, with trees bearing a profusion of variegated flowers, and resounding with the roars of beasts and serpents, those ones and, who had conquered their senses, began to live happily.",

sa_verse: वन्यैर्माल्यैः फलैर्मूलैः पक्वैाँसैर्यथाविधि। अद्भिर्जपैश्च वेदोक्तैर्दभैश्च ससमित्कुशैः।। तौ तर्पयित्वा भूतानि राघवौ सह सीतया। तदा विविशतुः शालां सुशुभां शुभलक्षणौ।।
en: And having with garlands made of wild flowers, fruits roots, meat cooked according to the ordinance, water, Fapa as prescribed by the Veda, grass, and faggot, worshipped the spirits, those descendants of Raghu, the auspicious Rāma and Lakşmaņa, in company with Sītă entered the graceful mansion.]

sa_verse: तां वृक्षपर्णच्छदनां मनोज्ञां यथाप्रदेशं सुकृतां निवाताम्। वासाय सर्वे विविशुः समेताः सभां यथा देवगणाः सुधर्माम्॥
en: And as the celestials enter the hall entitled Sudharmă, they together with the view of dwelling in it, entered the mansion beautiful to behold, thatched with the leaves of trees, built at a convenient site, well-made, and keeping out the wind.

sa_verse: सुरम्यमासाद्य तु चित्रकूट नदी च तां माल्यवती सुतीर्थाम्। ननन्द हृष्टो मृगपक्षिजुष्टां जहौ च दुःखं पुरविप्रवासात्॥
en: And having come to the charming Citrakuta and the river Mālyavatī furnished with excellent bathing places, and haunted by beasts and fowls, they rejoiced with glad hearts, and forsook the grief incident to their exile from the city.

sa_verse: कथयित्वा तु दुःखार्तः सुमन्त्रेण चिरं सह। रामे दक्षिणकूलस्थे जगाम स्वगृहं गुहः॥
en: Having for a long time conversed with Sumantra, Guha distressed at heart on Rāma reaching the southern bank, retraced his steps homewards.

sa_verse: भरद्वाजाभिगमनं प्रयागे च सभाजनम्। आ गिरेर्गमनं तेषां तत्रस्थैरभिलक्षितम्॥ अनुज्ञातः सुमन्त्रोऽथ योजयित्वा हयोत्तमान्। अयोध्यामेव नगरी प्रययौ गाढदुर्मनाः॥
en: Learning from envoys at (Śrngaverapura) all about Rāma's visit to Bharadvāja at Prayāga and his reception (at Bharadvāja's place) as well as their destined journey (to Citrakuta), Sumantra, taking the permission (of Guha), yoked those excellent horses and with a heavy heart directed his course to the city of Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: स वनानि सुगन्धीनि सरितश्च सरांसि च। पश्यन् यत्तो ययौ शीघ्रं ग्रामाणि नगराणि च॥
en: And beholding perfumed woods and rivers and watery expanses and towns and villages, he eagerly proceeded on his way.

sa_verse: ततः सायाह्नसमये द्वितीयेऽहनि सारथिः। अयोध्यां समनुप्राप्य निरानन्दां ददर्श ह॥
en: And on the third day at dusk the charioteer arriving at Ayodhyā saw it bereft of happiness.

sa_verse: स शून्यामिव निःशब्दां दृष्ट्वा परमदुर्मनाः। सुमन्त्रश्चिन्तयामास शोकवेगसमाहतः॥ कच्चिन सगजा साश्वा सजना सजनाधिपा। रामसंतापदुःखेन दग्धा शोकाग्निना पुरी।७।।
en: And beholding it empty and still, Sumantra afflicted with exceeding sorrow, and overwhelmed with grief, thought, “Perhaps the city with her elephants and horses and men and king has been consumed by the fire of grief on account of Rāma."

sa_verse: इति चिन्तापरः सूतो वाजिभिः शीघ्रयायिभिः। नगरद्वारमासाद्य त्वरितः प्रविवेश ह॥
en: Having thus reflected, the charioteer drawing up to the city-gate by means of those fleetcoursing horses, speedily entered the city.

sa_verse: सुमन्त्रमभिधावन्तः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः। क्व राम इति पृच्छन्तः सूतमभ्यद्रवन् नराः॥
en: Thereupon, people by hundreds and thousands rushed after the charioteer, Sumantra, asking, "Where is Rāma?”

sa_verse: तेषां शशंस गङ्गायामहमापृच्छय राघवम्। अनुज्ञातो निवृत्तोऽस्मि धार्मिकेण महात्मना ॥ ते तीर्णा इति विज्ञाय वाष्पपूर्णमुखा नराः। अहो धिगिति निःश्वस्य हा रामेति विचुक्रुशुः॥ शुश्राव च वचस्तेषां वृन्दं वृन्दं च तिष्ठताम्। हताः स्म खलु ये नेह पश्याम इति राघवम्॥
en: To them he replied, “Having asked Rāghava on the Gangā and being permitted by him, I have been sent away by that high-souled righteous one." Learning that they (Rāma and the rest) had crossed over (the Ganga), the men with tears in their eyes, sighed forth “O fie!" and began to bewail, exclaiming, “Ah Rāma." And he heard crowds exclaim, Not seeing Rāma in the car, we cease to exist.

sa_verse: दानयज्ञविवाहेषु समाजेषु महत्सु च। न द्रक्ष्यामः पुनर्जातु धार्मिकं राममन्तरा॥
en: We shall no longer see the righteous Rāma in the midst of mighty assemblies engaged in charity, sacrifice or nuptial rites.

sa_verse: किं समर्थे जनस्यास्य किं प्रियं किं सुखावहम्। इति रामेण नगरं पित्रेव परिपालितम्॥
en: What was necessary for this body? What was dear to them and what did they delight in?, (constantly revolving all this in his mind), Rāma ruled this city even as father.

sa_verse: वातायनगतानां च स्त्रीणामन्वन्तरापणम्। राममेवाभितप्तानां शुश्राव परिदेवनाम्॥
en: Then proceeding past the stalls, Sumantra heard the lamentations of females at windows, burning in grief for Rāma.

sa_verse: स राजमार्गमध्येन सुमन्त्रः पिहिताननः। यत्र राजा दशरथस्तदेवोपययौ गृहम्॥
en: With his face muffled, Sumantra proceeded on the highway towards the palace of Daśaratha.

sa_verse: सोऽवतीर्य रथाच्छीघ्रं राजवेश्म प्रविश्य च। कक्ष्याः सप्ताभिचक्राम महाजनसमाकुलाः॥
en: Swiftly alighting from the car and entering the royal residence, he went past seven apartments thronged with people.

sa_verse: हम्यैर्विमानैः प्रासादैरवेक्ष्याथ समागतम्। हाहाकारकृता नार्यो रामादर्शनकर्शिताः॥
en: And beholding Sumantra returned to the city crowned with edifices, seven-storied houses, and palatial mansions, the women, stricken with the absence of Rama, set up a cry of "Oh" and "Alas."

sa_verse: आयतैर्विमलैत्रैरश्रुवेगपरिप्लुतैः। अन्योन्यमभिवीक्षन्ते व्यक्तमार्ततराः स्त्रियः॥ ततो दशरथस्त्रीणां प्रासादेभ्यस्ततस्ततः। रामशोकाभितप्तानां मन्दं शुश्राव जल्पितम्॥
en: Waxing still more aggrieved, the females looked at each other with their expansive and transparent eyes fast flooded with tears. And then he heard the talk, as toned down it proceeded from the royal mansions, of the wives of Dasaratha afflicted with grief for Rāma.

sa_verse: सह रामेण निर्यातो विना राममिहागतः। सूतः किं नाम कौसल्या क्रोशन्ती प्रतिवक्ष्यति॥
en: Going in company with Rāma, and returning without him, what will the charioteer answer Kausalya bewailing (for her son)?

sa_verse: यथा च मन्ये दुर्जीवमेवं न सुकरं ध्रुवम्। आच्छिद्य पुत्रे निर्याते कौसल्या यत्र जीवति॥
en: Surely life is miserable, yet is incapable of being renounced, since, although her son leaving (the installation) has gone away, yet Kausalyā still lives.

sa_verse: स प्रविश्याष्टमीं कक्ष्यां राजानं दीनमातुरम्। पुत्रशोकपरिधूनमपश्यत् पाण्डुरे गृहे॥
en: And entering the eighth apartment he beheld in a gloomy chamber the king distressed and in a pitiable plight, woe-begone for grief for his son.

sa_verse: अभिगम्य तमासीनं राजानमभिवाद्य च। सुमन्त्रो रामवचनं यथोक्तं प्रत्यवेदयत्॥
en: Thereupon presenting himself before the monarch, Sumantra saluted him and then conveyed to the king the words of Rāma as he had uttered them.

sa_verse: स तूष्णीमेव तच्छ्रुत्वा राजा विद्रुतमानसः। मूर्छितो न्यपतद् भूमौ रामशोकाभिपीडितः॥
en: Hearing them silently, the monarch with his mind exceedingly wrought, dropped down to the ground in a swoon, afflicted with grief for Rāma.

sa_verse: ततोऽन्तःपुरमाविद्धं मूर्छिते पृथिवीपतौ। उच्छ्रित्य बाहू चुक्रोश नृपतौ पतिते क्षितौ॥
en: On the lord of earth swooning away and falling to the ground, the inmates of the inner apartment raising their burst into lamentations. arms of an

sa_verse: सुमित्रया तु सहिता कौसल्या पतितं पतिम्। उत्थापयामास तदा वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Kausalyā availing herself of the aid of Sumantra, raised up her fallen lord and addressed him, saying.

sa_verse: इमं तस्य महाभाग दूतं दुष्करकारिणः। वनवासादनुप्राप्तं कस्मान्न प्रतिभाषसे॥
en: This, O eminently virtuous one, is the envoy of that one an exceedingly arduous achievement returned from the forest. Why do you not accost him?

sa_verse: अद्येममनयं कृत्वा व्यपत्रपसि राघव। उत्तिष्ठ सुकृतं तेऽस्तु शोके न स्यात् सहायता।॥
en: O descendant of Raghu, you are ashamed today, having done this wrong. Do you rise: merit be yours (arising from this act.) Let not your adherents come to naught (because of your sorrow).

sa_verse: देव यस्या भयाद् रामं नानुपृच्छसि सारथिम्। नेह तिष्ठति कैकेयी विश्रब्धं प्रतिभाष्यताम्॥
en: O worshipful one, she from fear of whom you do not speak to the charioteer, Kaikeyi, is not here. Do you therefore speak to him without fear.

sa_verse: सा तथोक्त्वा महाराज कौसल्या शोकलालसा। धरण्यां निपपाताशु बाष्पविप्लुतभाषिणी॥
en: Having said this to the monarch, Kausalyā overwhelmed with grief, with her voice oppressed with the vapour begot of emotion, all on a sudden fell to the earth.

sa_verse: विलपन्ती तथा दृष्ट्वा कौसल्यां पतितां भुवि । पतिं चावेक्ष्य ताः सर्वाः समन्ताद् रुरुदुः स्त्रियः॥
en: Behoiding Kausalyā fallen on the ground bewailing, as also their husband, the ladies seated around, began to Lament,

sa_verse: ततस्तमन्तःपुरनादमुत्थितं समीक्ष्य वृद्धास्तरुणाश्च मानवाः। स्त्रियश्च सर्वा रुरुदुः समन्ततः पुरं तदासीत् पुनरेव संकुलम्॥
en: Hearing the sounds of wailing arise from the inner apartment, old and young as well as females, set up lamentations all round; and the city was again filled with them.

sa_verse: प्रत्याश्वस्तो यदाराजा मोहात् प्रत्यागतस्मृतिः। तदाजुहाव तं सूतं रामवृत्तान्तकारणात्॥
en: When having been ministered to and when his senses has returned after the swoon, the king summoned Sumantra for hearing tidings of Rama.

sa_verse: तदा सूतो महाराज कृताञ्जलिरुपस्थितः। राममेवानुशोचन्तं दुःखशोकसमन्वितम्॥ वृद्धं परमसंतप्तं नवग्रहमिव द्विपम्। विनिःश्वसन्तं ध्यायन्तमस्वस्थमिव कुञ्जरम्॥ राजा तु रजसा सूतं ध्वस्ताङ्ग समुपस्थितम्। अश्रुपूर्णमुखं दीनमुवाच परमार्तवत्॥
en: Thereupon the charioteer with joined hands spoke to the mighty monarch, lamenting for Rāma, influenced by grief and sorrow, aged, burning in grief, like a newly-taken elephant, sighing heavily, plunged in thought, and resembling an elephant that is indisposed. Then the king like one exceedingly distressed, spoke to the charioteer, who had presented himself, covered over with dust, with tears starting from his eyes, and in pitiable guise.

sa_verse: क्व नु वत्स्यति धर्मात्मा वृक्षमूलमुपाश्रितः। सोऽत्यन्तसुखितः सूत किमशिष्यति राघवः॥
en: Where stays that righteous one, taking refuge under a tree? lapped in luxury, what, O charioteer, will Rāghava feed on?

sa_verse: दुःखस्यानुचितो दुःखं सुमन्त्र शयनोचितः। भूमिपालात्मजो भूमौ शेते कथमनाथवत्॥
en: Unworthy of privations, and worthy of excellent beds, how, O Sumantra, a king's son, he is sleeping on the ground like one forlorn?

sa_verse: यं यान्तमनुयान्ति स्म पदातिरथकुञ्जराः। स वत्स्यति कथं रामो विजनं वनमाश्रितः॥
en: How is Rāma passing his days in the lone forest, he who when he went out used to be followed by foot-men and elephants?

sa_verse: व्यालैर्मृगैराचरितं कृष्णसर्पनिवेषितम्। कथं कुमारौ वैदेह्या सार्धं वनमुपाश्रितौ ॥
en: How are the princes in company with Vaidehi living in the woods, ranged by serpents and beasts and inhabited by black snakes?

sa_verse: सुकुमार्या तपस्विन्या सुमन्त्र सह सीतया। राजपुत्रौ कथं पादैरवरुह्य रथाद् गतौ॥
en: How, O Sumantra, having descended from the car, the princes along with the tender and unfortunate Sītā are proceeding on foot?

sa_verse: सिद्धार्थः खलु सूत त्वं येन दृष्टौ ममात्मजौ। वनान्तं प्रविशन्तौ तावश्विनाविव मन्दरम्॥
en: O charioteer, surely you are blessed, for you have beheld my sons enter the forest, like the Asvins entering the Mandāra hill.

sa_verse: किमुवाच वचो रामः किमुवाच च लक्ष्मणः। सुमन्त्र वनमासाध किमुवाच च मैथिली॥
en: And what did Rāma say? And what did Laks mana? And, O Sumantra, arriving at the forest what did Mithila's daughter?

sa_verse: आसितं शयितं भुक्तं सूत रामस्य कीर्तय। जीविष्याम्ययमेतेन ययातिरिव साधुषु ॥
en: Do you, O charioteer, describe to me what Rāma lives on and where he lies down. Hearing this, I shall live, like Yayati in the midst of saints.

sa_verse: इति सूतो नरेन्द्रेण चोदितः सज्जमानया। उवाच वाचा राजानं स बाष्पपरिबद्धया॥
en: Thus urged by the king, the charioteer spoke to the king with his voice faltering and suppressed with the vapour of grief.

sa_verse: अब्रवीन्मे महाराज धर्ममेवानुपालयन्। अञ्जलिं राघवः कृत्वा शिरसाभिप्रणम्य च॥ सूत मद्वचनात् तस्य तातस्य विदितात्मनः। शिरसा वन्दनीयस्य वन्द्यौ पादौ महात्मनः॥ सर्वमन्तःपुरं वाच्यं सूत मद्वचनात् त्वया। आरोग्यमविशेषेण यथार्हमभिवादनम्॥
en: O mighty monarch observing morality, Rāghava with joined hands and bowing down his head, said, “O charioteer, do you in my name salute with your head the feet of my high-souled sire worthy of being saluted, and famed (in the world).” And, O charioteer, do you in my name as each deserves salute the ladies of the inner apartment and communicate to them tidings of my health.

sa_verse: माता च मम कौसल्या कुशलं चाभिवादनम्। अप्रमादं च वक्तव्या ब्रूयाश्चैनामिदं वचः॥ धर्मनित्या यथाकालमग्न्यगारपरा भव। देवि देवस्य पादौ च देववत् परिपालय॥
en: Having saluted my mother Kausalyā and conveyed to her news of my welfare, as well as of my unswerving adherence to morality, do you tell her these words, Do you remaining steady in virtue, at the proper hour bestir yourself in behalf of the chamber of sacrificial fire. And, O revered one, do you minister to the feet of that worshipful one, (the king), as if they were very deities.

sa_verse: अभिमानं च मानं च त्यक्त्वा वर्तस्व मातृषु। अनुराजानमार्यां च कैकेयीमम्ब कारय॥
en: Banishing pride and self-love, do you bear yourself towards my mothers. And, O mother, do you show respect to Kaikeyi, who is followed by the king himself.

sa_verse: कुमारे भरते वृत्तिर्वर्तितव्या च राजवत्। अप्यज्येष्ठा हि राजानो राजधर्ममनुस्मर॥
en: You should behave towards Bharata as one should towards one's king. Kings (although juveline), are really senior by virtue of their royalty; and do you remember the duties touching sovereigns.

sa_verse: भरतः कुशलं वाच्यो वाच्यो मद्वचनेन च। सर्वास्वेव यथान्यायं वृत्तिं वर्तस्व मातृषु॥
en: Do you communicate to Bharata news of my welfare and say in my name, Do you behave properly with all your mothers.

sa_verse: वक्तव्यश्च महाबाहुरिक्ष्वाकुकुलनन्दनः। पितरं यौवराज्यस्थो राज्यस्थमनुपालय॥
en: And to that delight of the Ikṣvāku line do you further say, Having been installed as the heirapparent, be you, looking upon the king as the supreme authority in the state, obedient to him.

sa_verse: अतिक्रान्तवया राजा मा स्मैनं व्यपरोरुधः। कुमारराज्ये जीवस्व तस्यैवाज्ञाप्रवर्तनात्॥
en: Do you not deprive the king of authority, as he has grown old. O Prince, do you grant him satisfaction in the kingdom and do you proclaim his mandates.

sa_verse: अब्रवीच्चापि मां भूयो भृशमश्रूणि वर्तयन्। मातेव मम माता ते द्रष्टव्या पुत्रगर्धिनी। ॥ इत्येवं मां महाबाहुब्रुवन्नेव महायशाः। रामो राजीवपत्राक्षो भृशमश्रूण्यवर्तयत्॥
en: And shedding copious tears, he again addressed me, saying, Do you look upon my mother as on your own proud of her son. Having said this, the mighty-armed and illustrious Rāma furnished with eyes resembling lotus-petals, shed plenteous tears.

sa_verse: लक्ष्मणस्तु सुसंक्रुद्धो निःश्वसन् वाक्यमब्रवीत्। केनायमपराधेन राजपुत्रो विवासितः॥
en: Thereupon Lakşmaņa waxing wroth, breathing hard, said, For what fault of his has the Prince been banished?

sa_verse: राज्ञा तु खलु कैकेय्या लघु चाश्रुत्य शासनम्। कृतं कार्यमकार्यं वा वयं येनाभिपीडिताः॥
en: Observing the worthless command of Kaikeyſ, whether the king has done well or ill, we have been exceedingly pained thereby.

sa_verse: यदि प्रव्राजितो रामो लोभकारणकारितम्। वरदाननिमित्तं वा सर्वथा दुष्कृतं कृतम्॥
en: Whether Rāma has been exiled through Kaikeyi's lust for dominion or through the exigencies of the bestowal of the boon, there cannot be any doubt that the king has acted most unrighteously.

sa_verse: इदं तावद् यथाकाममीश्वरस्य कृते कृतम्। रामस्य तु परित्यागे न हेतुमुपलक्षये॥
en: Even if this has been done in harmony with the wish of the Lord, I do not perceive any reason for the banishment of Rāma.

sa_verse: असमीक्ष्य समारब्धं विरुद्धं बुद्धिलाघवात्। जनयिष्यति संक्रोशं राघवस्य विवासनम्॥
en: The king through lack of sense has done herulessly what is opposed to morality; and this banishment of Rāma will but bring upon him woe here and hereafter.

sa_verse: अहं तावन्महाराजे पितृत्वं नोपलक्षये। भ्राता भर्ता च बन्धुश्च पिता च मम राघवः॥
en: Fatherhood find I none in the monarch. My brother and feeder and friend and father is even Rāghava.

sa_verse: सर्वलोकप्रियं त्यक्त्वा सर्वलोकहिते रते। सर्वलोकोऽनुरज्येत कथं चानेन कर्मणा॥
en: Renouncing one that is universally dear, and that is ever engaged in the good of all, how by such an act will the king please the people?

sa_verse: सर्वप्रजाभिरामं हि रामं प्रव्रज्य धार्मिकम्। सर्वलोकविरोधेन कथं राजा भविष्यति ॥
en: How by banishing the righteous Rāma dear to all the subjects, and thus withstanding the wishes of all, will he retain his royalty?

sa_verse: जानकी तु महाराज निःश्वसन्ती तपस्विनी। भूतोपहतचित्तेव विष्ठिता विस्मृता स्थिता॥
en: O mighty monarch, Janaki also, unfortunate that she is, sighing heavily, stood wildered like one that had been possessed.

sa_verse: अदृष्टपूर्वव्यसना राजपुत्री यशस्विनी। तेन दुःखेन रुदती नैव मां किंचिदब्रवीत्॥
en: And not having experienced any calamity before, the famed princes weeping because of this misfortune, told me nothing.

sa_verse: उद्वीक्षमाणा भर्तारं मुखेन परिशिष्यता। मुमोच सहसा बाष्पं प्रयान्तमुपवीक्ष्य सा॥
en: Looking up to her lord with a blank countenance, she suddenly let fall tears, perceiving the destruction of the subject.

sa_verse: तथैव रामोऽश्रुमुखः कृताञ्जलिः स्थितोऽब्रवील्लक्ष्मणबाहुपालितः। तथैव सीता रुदती तपस्विनी निरीक्षते राजरथं तथैव माम्॥
en: Thus said Rāma ministered to by Lakşmaņa, with a tearful countenance; and thus stood the unfortunate and weeping Sītå beholding the royal car and myself.

sa_verse: मम त्वश्वा निवृत्तस्य न प्रावर्तन्त वर्त्मनि। उष्णमश्रु विमुञ्चन्तो रामे सम्प्रस्थिते वनम्॥ उभाभ्यां राजपुत्राभ्यामथ कृत्वाहमञ्जलिम्। प्रस्थितो रथमास्थाय तदुःखमपि धारयन्॥
en: On Rāma having gone to the forest, my horses as I turned away, did not proceed vigorously on the way, and shed warm tears. And having (done homage) to both the princes by joining my hands, I turned my back, bearing as best I could that load of sorrow.

sa_verse: गुहेन सार्धं तत्रैव स्थितोऽस्मि दिवसान् बहून्। आशया यदि मां रामः पुनः शब्दापयेदिति ॥
en: Indulging in the hope that Rāma might again summon me by any of the envoys (of Guha left there), I stayed there with Guha for many days.

sa_verse: विषये ते महाराज महाव्यसनकर्शिताः। अपि वृक्षाः परिम्लानाः सपुष्पाकुरकोरकाः॥ उपतप्तोदका नद्यः पल्वलानि सरांसि च। परिशुष्कपलाशानि वनान्युपवनानि च॥
en: In your dominions, O monarch, exercised by the calamity that has befallen Rāma, even trees bearing blossoms and buds and sprouts look sad; the rivers and pools and liquid lapses have their waters dried up; and the woods and groves have their foliage withered.

sa_verse: न च सर्पन्ति सत्त्वानि व्याला न प्रचरन्ति च। रामशोकाभिभूतं त निष्कूजमिव तद्वनम्॥
en: Creatures do not move and beasts of prey cease to range about; and the forest appears to be dumb, stupefied by grief on account of Rāma.

sa_verse: लीनपुष्करपत्राश्च नद्यश्च कलुषोदकाः। संतप्तपद्माः पद्मिन्यो लीनमीनविहंगमाः॥ जलजानि च पुष्पाणि माल्यानि स्थलजानि च। नातिभान्त्यल्पगन्धीनि फलानि च यथापुरम् ॥
en: Streams containing lotuses with their leaves shrivelled, have their waters stained; and lotuses have their leaves burnt; and fished and (aquatic) birds have grown lean. And flowers both on land and water have been deprived of their freshness and fragrance; and they no longer retain their former condition.

sa_verse: अत्रोद्यानानि शून्यानि प्रलीनविहगानि च। न चाभिरामानारामान् पश्यामि मनुजर्षभ॥
en: The gardens are idle with their birds drooping. And, O best of men, I do not find the bowers beautiful (as before).

sa_verse: प्रविशन्तमयोध्यायां न कश्चिदभिनन्दति। नरा राममपश्यन्तो निःश्वसन्ति मुहुर्मुहुः॥
en: When I entered Ayodhyā, none greeted me. And not seeing Rāma, the people sigh momentarily.

sa_verse: देव राजरथं दृष्ट्वा विना राममिहागतम्। दूरादश्रुमुखः सर्वो राजमार्गे गतो जनः॥
en: And, O revered one, seeing the royal car returned hither without Rāma, the people on the highways from grief appear with tearful countenance.

sa_verse: हम्र्यैर्विमानैः प्रासादैरवेक्ष्य रथमागतम्। हाहाकारकृता नार्यो रामादर्शनकर्शिताः॥
en: And from mansions, cars, and lordly edifices, ladies seeing the car come back, set up a chorus of 'Ah' and 'Alas,' afflicted with the absence of Rama.

sa_verse: आयतैर्विमलैनॆत्रैरश्रुवेगपरिप्लुतैः। अन्योन्यमभिवीक्षन्तेऽव्यक्तमार्ततराः स्त्रियः॥
en: And becoming more distressed that ever, the fair sex with their expansive and clear eyes filled with tears, began to eye each other indistinctly.

sa_verse: नामित्राणां न मित्राणामुदासीनजनस्य च। अहमार्ततया कंचिद् विशेषं नोपलक्षये॥
en: And in consequence of the general grief that prevailed, I could not perceive any difference between friends and foes and persons indifferent.

sa_verse: अप्रहृष्टमनुष्या च दीननागतुरंगमा। आर्तस्वरपरिम्लानविनिःश्वसितनि:स्वना ॥ निरानन्दा महाराज रामप्रव्राजनातुरा। कौसल्या पुत्रहीनेव अयोध्या प्रतिभाति मे॥
en: O mighty monarch, distressed in consequence of the exile of Rāma, men appear sunk in dejection, and elephants and horses are spiritless; and seized with cheerlessness, they utter doleful sounds and heave profound sighs. Ayodhyā appears to me joyless like Kausalyă deprived of her son.

sa_verse: सूतस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा वाचा परमदीनया। बाष्पोपहतया सूतमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Hearing the words of the charioteer, the king like an exceedingly forlorn, addressed the former in words lost in the vapour of sorrow.

sa_verse: कैकेय्या विनियुक्तेन पापाभिजनभावया। मया न मन्त्रकुशलैर्वृद्धैः सह समर्थितम्॥
en: Exhorted by Kaikeyi of a sinful country, born in a sinful race and cherishing sinful designs, I did not take counsel with aged people capable of offering advice.

sa_verse: न सुहृद्भिर्न चामात्यैर्मन्त्रयित्वा सनैगमैः। मयायमर्थः सम्मोहात् स्त्रीहेतोः सहसा कृतः॥
en: Without consulting with friends or courtiers or persons versed in the Vedas, I have in the interest of a woman rashly done this thing through ignorance.

sa_verse: भवितव्यतया नूनमिदं वा व्यसनं महत्। कुलस्यास्य विनाशाय प्राप्तं सूत यदृच्छया॥
en: Meseems, O charioteer, for the purpose of destroying this line entirely, this mighty disaster has surely befallen us through the influence of Destiny.

sa_verse: सूत यद्यस्ति ते किंचिन्मयापि सुकृतं कृतम्। त्वं प्रापयाशु मां रामं प्राणाः संत्वरयन्ति माम्॥
en: O charioteer, if I have ever done you any good do you immediately take me to Rāma: my life urges me on (in this direction).

sa_verse: यद्यद्यापि ममैवाज्ञा निवर्तयतु राघवम्। न शक्ष्यामि विना रामं मुहूर्तमपि जीवितुम्॥
en: Or let my command make Rāghava turn back. I cannot live for a moment without Rāma.

sa_verse: अथवापि महाबाहुर्गतो दूरं भविष्यति। मामेव रथमारोप्य शीघ्रं रामाय दर्शय॥
en: But if that mighty-armed one has proceeded far, do you placing me on a car speedily show me to Rāma.

sa_verse: वृत्तदंष्ट्रो महेष्वासः क्वासौ लक्ष्मणपूर्वजः। यदि जीवामि साध्वेनं पश्येयं सीतया सह॥
en: Where is that elder brother of Lakşmaņa of a mighty bow, furnished with teeth resembling Kunda flowers? If I live so long, I will behold him in company with Sītā.

sa_verse: लोहिताक्षं महाबाहुमामुक्तमणिकुण्डलम्। रामं यदि न पश्येयं गमिष्यामि यमक्षयम्॥ अतो नु किं दुःखतरं योऽहमिक्ष्वाकुनन्दनम्। इमामवस्थामापनो नेह पश्यामि राघवम्॥
en: What can be sadder than this, that reduced to such a pass, I cannot see here that descendant of Ikşvāku, Rāghava?

sa_verse: हा राम रामानुज हा हा वैदेहि तपस्विनि। न मां जानीत दुःखेन म्रियमाणमनाथवत्॥
en: Ah Rāma! Ah you younger brother of Rāma! Ah you unfortunate Vaidehī! You do not know that I am through grief lamenting like one deserted.

sa_verse: स तेन राजा दुःखेन भृशमर्पितचेतनः। अवगाढः सुदुष्पारं शोकसागरमब्रवीत्॥
en: Deprived of his consciousness through that sorrow of his, the king said.

sa_verse: रामशोकमहावेगः सीताविरहपारगः। श्वसितोर्मिमहावर्तो वाष्पवेगजलाविलः॥ बाहुविक्षेपमीनोऽसौ विक्रन्दितमहास्वनः। प्रकीर्णकेशशैवाल: कैकेयीवडवामुखः॥ ममाश्रुवेगप्रभवः कुब्जावाक्यमहाग्रहः। वरवेलो नृशंसाया रामप्रव्राजनायतः॥ यस्मिन् बत निमग्नोऽहं कौसल्ये राघवं विना। दुस्तरो जीवता देवि मयायं शोकसागरः॥ अशोभनं योऽहमिहाद्य राघवं दिदृक्षमाणो न लभे सलक्ष्मणम्। इतीव राजा विलपन् महायशाः पपात तूर्णं शयने स मूर्च्छितः॥
en: I have plunged myself into this ocean of woe hard to cross, with grief for Rāma as its mighty tide; separation from Sītā, its other shore; sighs heaved, its furious billows and whirlpools; tears, rivers that rush into it; tossing of the arms, its fishes; lamentations it roar; my hair flung about, its moss; Kaikeyi, its submarine fire; my fastflowing tears, its current; the words of the humpbacked one, its terrific ravenous animals; the boon, its continents; and the exile of Rama, its expanse. And, O Kausalyā, without Rāghava, I shall sink in this ocean. O exalted dame, living, it is hard for me to cross over this ocean. It is surely owing to my sin that to-day wishing to behold Rāghava and Lakşmaņa, I do not get them (before me). Having thus lamented, the illustrious king all of a sudden dropped to the earth in a swoon.

sa_verse: इति विलपति पार्थिवे प्रणष्टे करुणतरं द्विगुणं च रामहेतोः। वचनमनुनिशम्य तस्य देवी भयमगमत् पुनरेव राममाता॥
en: On the king swooning away lamenting, that exalted lady, Rāma's mother, hearing his words doubly bitter and more piteous than ever uttered for Rāma, was seized with fresh apprehension.

sa_verse: इति विलपति पार्थिवे प्रणष्टे करुणतरं द्विगुणं च रामहेतोः। वचनमनुनिशम्य तस्य देवी भयमगमत् पुनरेव राममाता॥
en: On the king swooning away lamenting, that exalted lady, Rāma's mother, hearing his words doubly bitter and more piteous than ever uttered for Rāma, was seized with fresh apprehension.

sa_verse: ततो भूतोपसृष्टेव वेपमाना पुनः पुनः। धरण्यां गतसत्त्वेव कौसल्या सूतमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then like one possessed by an evil spirit, and trembling again and again, Kausalyä lying down on the ground like one dead, spoke to the charioteer, saying.

sa_verse: नय मां यत्र काकुत्स्थ: सीता यत्र च लक्ष्मणः। तान् विना क्षणमप्यद्य जीवितुं नोत्सहे ह्यहम्॥
en: Do you take me where Kākutstha is and Sītā and Lakşınaņa: without them, I cannot live for a moment.

sa_verse: निवर्तय रथं शीघ्रं दण्डकान् नय मामपि। अथ तान् नानुगच्छामि गमिष्यामि यमक्षयम्॥
en: Do you without delay turn the car. Do you take me also to Dandaka. If I do not follow them, I shall repair to the mansion of Yama.

sa_verse: बाष्पवेगोपहतया स वाचा सज्जमानया। इदमासयन् देवीं सूतः प्राञ्जलिरब्रवीत्॥ त्यज शोकं च मोहं च सम्भ्रंमं दुःखजं तथा। व्यवधूय च संतापं वने वत्स्यति राघवः॥
en: Thereat the cahrioteer with joined hands comforted that exalted lady with ready words faltering and choked with rising vapour, "Do you leave grief and sorrow and the violent emotion. Renouncing grief, Rāghava is living in the woods."

sa_verse: विजनेऽपि वने सीता वासं प्राप्य गृहेष्विव। विस्रम्भं लभतेऽभीता राम विन्यस्तमानसा॥
en: And even in the lonely woods, Sītā as if remaining at home, having fixed her thoughts on Rāma, is living a life of love.

sa_verse: नास्या दैन्यं कृतं किंचित् सुसूक्ष्मपि लक्ष्यते। उचितेव प्रवासानां वैदेही प्रतिभाति मे॥
en: And there appears not the least trace of any distress afflicting her; and Vaidehi seems to me as if she were meant (by Nature) for a life away from home.

sa_verse: नगरोपवनं गत्वा यथा स्म रमते पुरा। तथैव रमते सीता निर्जनेषु वनेष्वपि॥
en: And as forinerly going to urban villas she disported, she disports now even in the lonely forest.

sa_verse: बालेव रमते सीताबालचन्द्रनिभानना। रामा रामे ह्यदीनात्मा विजनेऽपि वने सती॥
en: And although living in the lone forest, that one of a countenance resembling the infant moon, sports merrily like a girl, in the garden represented by Rāma's self.

sa_verse: तद्गतं हृदयं यस्यास्तदधीनं च जीवितम्। अयोध्या हि भवेदस्या रामहीना तथा वनम्॥
en: Ayodhyā without Rāma would have seemed a wilderness to her whose heart is fixed on him and whose very life depends upon him.

sa_verse: परिपृच्छति वैदेही ग्रामांश्च नगराणि च। गतिं दृष्ट्वा नदीनां च पादपान् विविधानपि॥ रामं वा लक्ष्मणं वापि दृष्ट्वा जानाति जानकी। अयोध्या क्रोशमात्रे तु विहारमिव साश्रिता।१३।।
en: Vaidehī is now asking (Rāma) concerning the villages and towns (in their way); and observing various trees and the courses of the rivers, Jānaki, asking Råma Laksmana (for information), is learning all about them. And Sītā sports as she used to do in arbours stationed at the distance of only one krosa from Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: इदमेव स्मराम्यस्याः सहसैवोपजल्पितम्। कैकेयीसंश्रितं जल्पं नेदानी प्रतिभाति माम्॥
en: This only I remember; but all that she had from sudden impulse communicated to me or concerning Kaikeyſ, does not rise into consciousness.

sa_verse: ध्वंसयित्वा तु तद् वाक्यं प्रमादात् पर्युपस्थितम्। ह्लादनं वचनं सूतो देव्या मधुरमब्रवीत्॥
en: Suppressing this topic which had come up through heedlessness, the charioteer spoke sweet words cheering up that noble lady.

sa_verse: अध्वना वातवेगेन सम्भ्रमेणातपेन च। न विगच्छति वैदेह्याश्चन्द्रांशुसदृशी प्रभा॥
en: Neither through the fatigue of travel, nor the influence of the wind, nor excitement, nor the sun, has Vaidehi's lustre resembling the lunar light suffered any diminution.

sa_verse: सदृशं शतपत्रस्य पूर्णचन्द्रोपमप्रभम्। वदनं तद् वदान्याया. वैदेह्या न विकम्पते ॥
en: The countenance of that fair-speaking one resembling the lotus and comparable to the full moon in splendour, has not waxed pale.

sa_verse: अलक्तरसरक्ताभावलक्तरसवर्जितौ। अद्यापि चरणौ तस्याः पद्मकोशसमप्रभौ॥
en: Her feet now without your dye of the liquid lac, but naturally furnished with the roseate hue of the same, are gorgeous like lotus buds.

sa_verse: नूपुरोत्कृष्टलीलेव खेलं गच्छति भामिनी। इदानीमपि वैदेही तद्रागान्यस्तभूषणा ॥
en: Still Vaidehī decked in ornaments from affection for Rāma, goes gracefully rebuking with her bangles the wavy gait (of cranes).

sa_verse: गजं वा वीक्ष्य सिंह वा व्याघ्र वा वनमाश्रिता। नाहारयति संत्रासं बाहू रामस्य संश्रिता॥
en: Supporting herself on Rāma's arm, Sītă arrived at the forest, is not inspired with fear on beholding either an elephant, or a lion, or a tiger.

sa_verse: न शोच्यस्ते न चात्मा ते शोच्यो नापि जनाधिपः। इदं हि चरितं लोके प्रतिष्ठास्यति शाश्वतम्॥
en: Therefore they are not to be bewailed, nor your own self, nor the lord of men. This history of Rāma will endure for ever in this world.

sa_verse: विधूय शोकं परिहृष्टमानसा महर्षियाते पथि सुव्यवस्थिताः। वने रता वन्यफलाशनाः पितुः शुभां प्रतिज्ञा प्रतिपालयन्ति ते॥
en: Renouncing grief and with cheerful hearts, well established in the life led by the Maharşis, they living in the forest on wild fruits as their sustenance, are maintaining the noble promise of their sire.

sa_verse: तथापि सूतेन सुयुक्तवादिना निवार्यमाणा सुतशोककर्शिता। न चैव देवी विरराम कूजितात् प्रियेति पुत्रेति च राघवेति च ॥
en: Consoled by the truth-telling yet sweetspeeched charioteer, that lady oppressed with grief for her son, ceased not to wail loudly, "My beloved,” “My son," "Rāghava."

sa_verse: वनं गते धर्मरते रामे रमयतां वरे। कौसल्या रुदती चार्ता भर्तारमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: On that foremost of those capable of charming people, the righteous Rāma, repairing to the forest, Kausalyā crying in grief said to her husband.

sa_verse: यद्यपि त्रिषु लोकेषु प्रथितं ते महद् यशः। सानुक्रोशो वदान्यश्च प्रियवादी च राघवः॥
en: Your great fame has spread over the three worlds; and the descendant of Raghu is kind, munificent and fair-speaking.

sa_verse: कथं नरवर श्रेष्ठ पुत्रौ तौ सह सीतया। दुःखितौ सुखसंवृद्धौ वने दुःखं सहिष्यतः॥
en: Why then have you forsaken those foremost of men along with Sītā? Brought up in happiness, and now brought to misery, how can they bear it?

sa_verse: सा नूनं तरुणी श्यामा सुकुमारी सुखोचिता। कथमुष्णं च शीतं च मैथिली विसहिष्यते॥
en: How can the youthful daughter of Mithilā of slender make, tender, and deserving of happiness, bear heat and cold?

sa_verse: भुक्त्वाशनं विशालाक्षी सूपदंशान्वितं शुभम्। वन्यं नैवारमाहारं कथं सीतोपभोक्ष्यते॥
en: Having formerly partaken of (excellent) rice with curries, how will Sita feed on wild rice?

sa_verse: गीतवादित्रनिर्घोषं श्रुत्वा शुभसमन्विता। कथं क्रव्यादसिंहानां शब्दं श्रोष्यत्यशोभनम्॥
en: Having heard excellent vocal and instrumental music, how will Sītă hear the frightful cries of carnivorous lions?

sa_verse: महेन्द्रध्वजसंकाशः क्व नु शेते महाभुजः। भुजं परिघसंकाशमुपाधाय महाबलः॥
en: Resembling the gonfalon of the great Indra, where sleeps the mighty-armed and exceedingly powerful Råma, making his arm like to a mace his pillow?

sa_verse: पद्मवर्णं सुकेशान्तं पद्मनिःश्वासमुत्तमम्। कदा द्रक्ष्यामि रामस्य वदनं पुष्करेक्षणम्॥
en: When shall I behold Rāma's countenance hued like the lotus, with his hair ending beautifully (in curls), and his breath impregnated with the fine perfume of the lotus, and his eyes resembling lotus leaves?

sa_verse: वज्रसारमयं नूनं हृदयं मे न संशयः। अपश्यन्त्या न तं यद् वै फलतीदं सहस्रधा॥
en: Surely my heart, without doubt, is made of the essence of the thunderbolt, since not beholding him, it is not cracked in a thousand fragments.

sa_verse: यत् त्वया करुणं कर्म व्यपोह्य मम बान्धवाः। निरस्ताः परिधावन्ति सुखार्हाः कृपणा वने॥
en: It is because of your sad act that mine own, being thwarted, although worthy of happiness, are miserably ranging the wilderness.

sa_verse: यदि पञ्चदशे वर्षे राघवः पुनरेष्यति। जह्याद् राज्यं च कोशं च भरतो नोपलक्ष्यते॥
en: If after the expiration of the five and ten years, Rāghava does return, it does not appear likely that Bharata will renounce the kingdom and the exchequer.

sa_verse: भोजयन्ति किल श्राद्धे केचित् स्वानेव बान्धवान्। ततः पश्चात् समीक्षन्ते कृतकार्या द्विजोत्तमान्॥ तत्र ये गुणवन्तश्च विद्वांसश्च द्विजातयः। न पश्चात् तेऽभिमन्यन्ते सुधामपि सुरोपमाः॥
en: Some on the occasions of the Sradha (first) feast their own friends, and having done this, they mind the choice Brāhmaṇas. But those twice-born ones that are meritorious, learned, and like to celestials, do not at the last moment regard even viands resembling ambrosia.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणेष्वपि वृत्तेषु भुक्तशेष द्विजोत्तमाः। नाभ्युपेतुमलं प्राज्ञाः शृङ्गच्छेदमिवर्षभाः॥
en: Brāhmaṇas of high respectability endowed with wisdom never bear being entertained after the other twice-born ones have been feasted, even as bulls never quietly bear to have their horns cut off.

sa_verse: एवं कनीयसा भ्रात्रा भुक्तं राज्यं विशाम्पते। भ्राता ज्येष्ठो वरिष्ठश्च किमर्थं नावमन्यते॥
en: Why will not an elder brother and one who has sterling merits, O monarch, disregard a kingdom which has been thus enjoyed by his younger brother?

sa_verse: न परेणाहृतं भक्ष्यं व्याघ्रः खादितुमिच्छति। एवमेव नरव्याघ्रः परलीढं न मंस्यते॥
en: A tiger does not like to feed on food procured by others. Even so that tiger-like personage does not regard anything that has come to be tasted by another.

sa_verse: हविराज्यं पुरोडाशः कुशा यूपाश्च खादिराः। नैतानि यातयामानि कुर्वन्ति पुनरध्वरे ॥
en: Clarified butter, sacrificial cakes, Kusa, stakes of catechu having been used in one sacrifice cannot be used in another.

sa_verse: तथा ह्यात्तमिदं राज्यं हृतसारां सुरामिव। नाभिमन्तुमलं रामो नष्टसोममिवाध्वरम्॥
en: Even so this kingdom which has been already enjoyed, like to liquor deprived of its essence or a sacrifice whose soma has been eaten, cannot be accepted by Rāma.

sa_verse: नैवंविधमसत्कारं राघवो मर्षयिष्यति। बलवानिव शार्दूलो वालधेरभिमर्शनम्॥
en: Such an ill treatment Rāghava will not put up with, even as a powerful tiger cannot bear the rubbing of its tail (by another).

sa_verse: नैतस्य सहिता लोका भयं कुर्युर्महामृधे। अधर्मं त्विह धर्मात्मा लोकं धर्मेण योजयेत्॥
en: This world aided by the gods fear to encounter him in high conflict; but he restrains himself, thinking any such action on his part as unrighteous. Indeed, that righteous one brings back people to morality.

sa_verse: नन्वसौ काञ्चनैर्बाणैर्महावीर्यो महाभुजः। युगान्त इव भूतानि सागरानपि निर्दहेत्॥
en: Surely, that mighty-armed one endowed with exceeding prowess, can with his golden shafts burn all creatures and the oceans, like the Day at the dissolution of all.

sa_verse: स तादृशः सिंहबलो वृषभाक्षो नरर्षभः। स्वयमेव हतः पित्रा जलजेनात्मजो यथा॥
en: But such a man of men, possessed of leonine strength, and graced with the eyes of a bull, has been destroyed by his father, like a fish destroying his offspring.

sa_verse: द्विजातिचरितो धर्मः शास्त्रे दृष्टः सनातनैः। यदि ते धर्मनिरते त्वया पुत्रे विवासिते॥
en: If you had believed in the morality prescribed in the scriptures, and which is followed by the twice-born ones, you would not have banished your son. But disregarding such morality, you have banished your virtuous son.

sa_verse: गतिरेका पतिर्नार्या द्वितीया गतिरात्मजः। तृतीया ज्ञातयो राजश्चतुर्थी नैव विद्यते॥
en: One of the refuges of a woman is her husband, a second is her son, and a third is her relatives; and a fourth she has none.

sa_verse: तत्र त्वं मम नैवासि रामश्च वनमाहितः। न वनं गन्तुमिच्छामि सर्वथा हा हता त्वया॥
en: But you cease to be mine; and Rāma has been sent to the woods. I do not like to go into the forest; so I am entirely undone by you.

sa_verse: हतं त्वया राष्ट्रमिदं सराज्यं हताः स्म सर्वाः सह मन्त्रिभिश्च। हता सपुत्रास्मि हताश्चं पौराः सुतश्च भार्या च तव प्रहृष्टौ ॥
en: This kingdom, your own kingdom, has been destroyed by you; destroyed are all along with the counsellors; destroyed am I with my son; and destroyed are the citizens: your son and your wife are alone delighted.

sa_verse: इमां गिरं दारुणशब्दसंहितां निशम्य रामेति मुमोह दुःखितः। ततः स शोकं प्रविवेश पार्थिवः स्वदुष्कृतं चापि पुनस्तथास्मरत्॥
en: Hearing these words uttered in heart-rending accents, Dasaratha exceedingly distressed, became senseless. And being afflicted with grief, he again remembered his evil act.

sa_verse: एवं तु क्रुद्धया राजा राममात्रा सशोकया। श्रावितः परुषं वाक्यं चिन्तयामास दुःखितः॥ चिन्तयित्वा स च नृपो मोहव्याकुलितेन्द्रियः। अथ दीर्पण कालेन संज्ञामाप परंतपः॥
en: Thus harshly addressed by the indignant mother of Rāma, the king aggrieved was plunged in thought. Having thought for a long while, that repressor of foes, the king, who had lost his senses through grief, regained consciousness.

sa_verse: स संज्ञामुपलभ्यैव दीर्घमुष्णं च निःश्वसन्। कौसल्या पार्श्वतो दृष्ट्वा ततश्चिन्तामुपागमत्॥
en: Having regained his senses, he sighing hot and hard, seeing Kausalyā beside him, was again lost in thought.

sa_verse: तस्य चिन्तयमानस्य प्रत्यभात् कर्म दुष्कृतम्। यदनेन कृतं पूर्वमज्ञानाच्छब्दवेधिना॥
en: As he was thinking, the sinful act which he had through ignorance formerly committed by means of the shaft which hits by sounds, rose up in his recollection,

sa_verse: अमानास्तेन शोकेन रामशोकेन च प्रभुः। द्वाभ्यामपि महाराजः शोकाभ्यामभितप्यते॥
en: Afflicted with this grief as well as that on account of Rāma, that lord, the king, burned in these two several griefs.

sa_verse: दह्यमानस्तु शोकाभ्यां कौसल्यामाह दुःखितः। वेपमानोऽञ्जलिं कृत्वा प्रसादार्थमवाङ्मुखः। ॥
en: Burning in grief and distressed, he trembling and with joined hands, with his head hanging down, addressed Kausalyā, with the view of pacifying her.

sa_verse: प्रसादये त्वां कौसल्ये रचितोऽयं मयाञ्जलिः। वत्सला चानृशंसा च त्वं हि नित्यं परेष्वपि॥
en: I deprecate your displeasure. O Kausalyä with joined hands. You are ever affectionate and did not treat harshly even enemies.

sa_verse: कौसल्या व्यसृजद् भर्ता तु खलु नारीणां गुणवान् निर्गुणोऽपि वा। धर्मं विमृशमानानां प्रत्यक्षं देवि दैवतम्॥
en: Verily to women cognizant of virtue, a husband, whether he has any merits or not, is a very deity.

sa_verse: सा त्वं धर्मपरा नित्यं दृष्टलोकपरावरा। नार्हसे विप्रियं वक्तुं दुःखितापि सुदुःखितम्॥
en: Ever virtuous, you, that have seen both the virtuous and the vicious, although aggrieved, ought not to say anything unpleasant to me who am weighed down with woe.

sa_verse: तद् वाक्यं करुणं राज्ञः श्रुत्वा दीनस्य भाषितम्। बाष्पं प्रणालीव नवोदकम्॥
en: Having heard these piteous words of the distressed king, Kausalyā uttered words even as a water-way lets out fresh accession of rain.

sa_verse: सा मूर्ध्नि बद्ध्वा रुदती राज्ञः पद्ममिवाञ्जलिम्। सम्भ्रमादब्रवीत् त्रस्ता त्वरमाणाक्षरं वचः॥
en: And weeping, she drew on her head the joined hands of the king resembling lotuses; and then flurried spoke these words hurriedly informed with extreme affection.

sa_verse: प्रसीद शिरसा याचे भूमौ निपतितास्मि ते। याचितास्मि हता देव क्षन्तव्याहं नहि त्वया॥
en: Be you propitious; I beseech with (bended) head. I bow to you, falling on the ground. O reverend one, besought by you, I shall be undone. I do not deserve to be forgiven by you.

sa_verse: नैषा हि सा स्त्री भवति श्लाघनीयेन धीमता। उभयोर्लोकयोर्लोके पत्या या सम्प्रसाद्यते॥
en: She cannot be reckoned a gentlewoman, who is propitiated by her intelligent husband, worthy of being extolled in both worlds.

sa_verse: जानामि धर्मे धर्मज्ञ त्वां जाने सत्यवादिनम्। पुत्रशोकार्तया तत्तु मया किमपि भाषितम्॥
en: I know duty, O righteous one; I know that you are truth-telling. And it is because I was exceedingly distressed on account of my son that I spoke harshly to you.

sa_verse: शोको नाशयते धैर्यं शोको नाशयते श्रुतम्। शोको नाशयते सर्वं नास्ति शोकसमो रिपुः॥
en: Sorrow destroys patience, sorrow destroys knowledge of the scriptures, sorrow destroys every things; there is no enemy like to sorrow.

sa_verse: शक्यमापतितः सोढुं प्रहारो रिपुहस्ततः। शोढुमापतितः शोकः सुसूक्ष्मोऽपि न शक्यते॥
en: One can falling down bear ever beating from an enemy; but one cannot falling down bear ever so little sorrow.

sa_verse: वनवासाय रामस्य पञ्चरात्रोऽत्र गण्यते। यः शोकहतहर्षायाः पञ्चवर्षोपमो मम॥
en: This is the fifth night of the banishment of Rāma, as calculated by me; and to me rendered cheerless by sorrow, this interval has assumed the proportions of five years.

sa_verse: तं हि चिन्तयमानायाः शोकोऽयं हृदि वर्धते। नदीनामिव वेगेन समुद्रसलिलं महत्॥
en: And fostered by thought on my part, this grief increases in my bosom, like the mighty waters of the ocean increased by the vehement discharge of rivers.

sa_verse: एवं हि कथयन्त्यास्तु कौसल्यायाः शुभं वचः। मन्दरश्मिरभूत् सूर्यो रजनी चाभ्यवर्तत॥ अथ प्रह्लादितो वाक्यैर्देव्या कौसल्यया नृपः। शोकेन चसमाक्रान्तो निद्राया वशमेयिवान्॥
en: As Kausalyā was thus speaking auspiciously, the rays of the sun grew milder, and the night arrived. Cheered up by the words of Kausalya, the king overcome by grief, felt the influence of sleep.

sa_verse: प्रतिबुद्धो मुहूर्तेन शोकोपहतचेतनः। अथ राजा दशरथः स चिन्तामभ्यपद्यत ॥
en: Starting in a moment from sleep, king Dasaratha deprived (almost) of his consciousness by grief, (again) became a prey to thought.

sa_verse: रामलक्ष्मणयोश्चैव विवासाद् वासवोपमम्। आपेदे उपसर्गस्तं तमः सूर्यमिवासुरम्॥
en: I consequence of the exile of Rāma and Laks mana, the king resembling Våsava was overpowered by grief, like the darkness of Rāhu enveloping the sun. was own

sa_verse: सभार्ये हि गते रामे कौसल्यां कोसलेश्वरः। विवक्षुरसितापाङ्गी स्मृत्वा दुष्कृतमात्मनः॥
en: Then on Răma's having gone along with his wife, the lord of the Kosalas, remembering his own misdeed, felt anxious to communicate himself to that lady having her eyes furnished with dark outer corners.

sa_verse: स राजा रजनी षष्ठी रामे प्रवाजिते वनम्। अर्धरात्रे दशरथः सोऽस्मरद् दुष्कृतं कृतम्॥
en: And on the sixth night after Rāma had repaired to the forest, the king Dasaratha, when it midnight, remembered his unrighteous act.

sa_verse: स राजा पुत्रशोकार्तः स्मृत्वा दुष्कृतमात्मनः। कौसल्यां पुत्रशोकार्तामिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: And then to Kausalyā aggrieved on account of her son, her spoke these words.

sa_verse: यदाचरति कल्याणि शुभं वा यदि वाऽशुभम्। तदेव लभते भद्रे कर्ता कर्मजमात्मनः॥
en: As are the actions of one, O auspicious one, whether good otherwise, the consequences, O gentle lady, reaped by the doer of them.

sa_verse: कश्चिदाम्रवणं छित्त्वा पलाशांश्च निषिञ्चति। पुष्पं दृष्ट्वा फले गृध्नुः स शोचति फलागमे॥ अविज्ञाय फलं यो हि कर्म त्वेवानुधावति। स शोचेत् फलवेलायां यथा किंशुकसेचकः॥
en: He that cutting down a mango grove, waters Palasa trees, beholding the flowers (blooming), will covet fruits; and grieve when their season arrives. The person that without apprehending (the principle of causation) rushed to action, grieves at the season of fruits, even like him that waters kinsuka trees (hewing down his mango grove). or are

sa_verse: सोऽहमाम्रवणं छित्वा पलाशांश्च न्यषेचयम्। राम फलागमे त्यक्त्वा पश्चाच्छोचामि दुर्मतिः॥
en: And is this way, I fool that I am having hewn down my grove of mangoes and watered Palāsas, having renounced Räma in the season of fruit, is grieving in the end.

sa_verse: लब्धशब्देन कौसल्ये कुमारेण धनुष्मता। कुमारः शब्दवेधीति मया पापमिदं कृतम्॥
en: Having, O Kausalyā, earned the expression, 'The Prince can pierce his aim by sound alone,', I, a prince and bowman, did this offence.

sa_verse: तदिदं मेऽनुसम्प्राप्तं देवि दुःखं स्वयंकृतम्। सम्मोहादिह बालेन यथा स्याद् भक्षितं विषम्॥
en: Therefore, O noble dame, I have myself brought this misfortune on me, like a child who has eaten poison through ignorance.

sa_verse: यथान्यः पुरुषः कश्चित् पलाशैर्मोहितो भवेत्। एवं मयाप्यविज्ञातं शब्दवेध्यमिदं फलम्॥
en: Even like another person fascinated by the sight of some Palasa trees (and doing as mentioned above), I (did this act) not fore knowing the consequence that would follow my shooting by sound.

sa_verse: देव्यनूढा त्वमभवो युवराजो भवाम्यहम्। ततः प्रावृडनुप्राप्ता मम कामविवर्धिनी॥
en: O lady, you were then unwedded; and I was a youthful prince. And it was at this time that the rainy season increasing my desire set in.

sa_verse: अपास्य हि रसान् भौमांस्तप्त्वा च जगदंशुभिः। परेताचरितां भीमां रविराचरते दिशम् ॥
en: Drawing moisture from the ground and heated the earth by his rays, the Sun goes to the dreadful quarter whither repair the dead.

sa_verse: उष्णमन्तर्दधे सद्यः स्निग्धा ददृशिरे घनाः। ततो जहषिरे सर्वे भेकसारङ्गबर्हिणः॥ क्लिनपक्षोत्तराः स्नाताः कृच्छ्रादिव पतत्रिणः। वृष्टिवातावधूताग्रान् पादपानभिपेदिरे॥
en: The heat was immediately dispelled, and the gelid clouds snowed themselves; and frogs and Sārangas and peacocks began to rejoice; and, finding it unpleasant, the feathered ones bathed and with the surface of their plumage shrunk up from the wet, took refuge in trees shaken by the wind and rain.

sa_verse: पतितेनाम्भसाऽऽच्छन्नः पतमानेन चासकृत्। आबभौ मत्तसारङ्गस्तोयराशिरिवाचलः॥
en: And the hill graced by maddened Sārangas, covered by showers falling simultaneously looked like a mass of waters.

sa_verse: पाण्डुरारुणवर्णानि स्रोतांसि विमलान्यपि। सुस्रुवुर्गिरिधातुभ्यः सभस्मानि भुजंगवत्॥
en: And the waters although unstained, being mixed up with mineral substances and ashes from the mountains, flowed in serpentine torrents black and red.

sa_verse: तस्मिन्नतिसुखे काले धनुष्मानिषुमान् रथी। व्यायामकृतसंकल्पः सरयूमन्वगां नदीम्॥ निपाने महिषं रात्रौ गजं वाभ्यागतं मृगम्। अन्यद्वाश्वापदं किंचिज्जिघांसुरजितेन्द्रियः॥
en: At such a sweet hour, I intent upon taking exercise, taking my bow and arrows and mounted on my car, sallied out for the river Sarayū, with the intention that I with my senses under control, should in the watery expense slay any buffalo, elephant, or any other beast that mighty have come there in the night.

sa_verse: अथान्धकारे त्वौषं जले कुम्भस्य पूर्यतः। अचक्षुर्विषये घोषं वारणस्येव नर्दतः॥
en: And (coming there) while it was so dark that : nothing could be discovered, I heard sounds of a filling pitcher proceeding from the waters; resembling the roars of an elephant.

sa_verse: ततोऽहं शरमुद्धृत्य दीप्तमाशीविषोपमम्। शब्दं प्रति गजप्रेप्सुरभिलक्ष्यमपातयम्॥
en: Thereupon raising up my shaft flaming and like to a serpent of virulent poison, I desirous of hunting the (imaginary) elephant, let fly my shaft in the direction of the sound.

sa_verse: अमुञ्चं निशितं बाणमहमाशीविषोपमम्। तत्र वागुषसि व्यक्ता प्रादुरासीद् वनौकसः॥ हा हेति पततस्तोये बाणाद् व्यथितमर्मणः। तस्मिन्निपतिते भूमौ वागभूत् तत्र मानुषी॥
en: Thereupon from the spot whereto the sharpened shaft resembling a poisonous snake had been discharged by me in the twilight, proceeded cries of 'Oh' and 'Alas' uttered by a forester pierced to the quick by the arrow, and falling into the water. And when he had dropped down, words spoken by a human being became audible.

sa_verse: कथमस्मद्विधे शस्त्रं निपतेच्च तपस्विनी। प्रविविक्तां नदी रात्रावुदाहारोऽहमागतः॥
en: Why does the weapon light upon me? I had come to this lone stream for procuring water.

sa_verse: इषुणाभिहतः केन कस्य वापकृतं मया। ऋषेहि न्यस्तदण्डस्य वने वन्येन जीवतः॥ कथं नु शस्त्रेण वधो मद्विधस्य विधीयते। जटाभारधरस्यैव वल्कलाजिनवाससः॥ को वधेन ममार्थी स्यात् किं वास्यापकृतं मया। एवं निष्फलमारब्धं केवलानर्थसंहितम्॥
en: By whom have I been wounded by this arrow? To whom have I done wrong? And how can the slaying of one like me bearing a load of matted locks, and wearing bark and deer-skin, who subsist on what the forest yields and never injures others, be sanctioned by the scriptures? Who can serve any purpose by slaying me? And how can I have injured such an one? Such a purposeless act cannot but end in evil.

sa_verse: न क्वचित् साधु मन्येत नेमं तथानुशोचामि जीवितक्षयमात्मनः॥ मातरं पितरं चोभावनुशोचामि मद्वधे। तदेतन्मिथुनं वृद्धं चिरकालभृतं मया॥ मयि पञ्चत्वमापन्ने कां वृत्तिं वर्तयिष्यति। वृद्धौ च मातापितरावहं चैकेषुणा हतः॥ केन स्म निहताः सर्वे सुबालेनाकृतात्मना।
en: This can never be reckoned as righteous even like to violating the chastity of a preceptor's wife. I do not so much lament my end as I lament it on account of my father and mother. To what will the old couple, who have ever been maintained by me, betake themselves when I am gone? My father and mother are old, and I their only son is slain. What boy is it of uncontrolled senses that has killed us all?

sa_verse: यथैव गुरुतल्पगम्। तां गिरं करुणं श्रुत्वा मम धर्मानुकाक्षिणः॥ कराभ्यां सशरं चापं व्यथितस्यापतद् भुवि। तस्याहं करुणं श्रुत्वा ऋषेर्विलपतो निशि॥ सम्भ्रान्तः शोकवेगेन भृशमासं विचेतनः।
en: Hearing his piteous words, I ever anxious to follow virtue, was exceedingly pained, and the bow with its arrow fixed fell down from my hand to the earth.

sa_verse: तं देशमहमागम्य दीनसत्त्वः सुदुर्मनाः॥ अपश्यमिषुणा तीरे सरय्वास्तापसं हतम्। अवकीर्णजटाभारं प्रविद्धकलशोदकम्॥ पांसुशोणितदिग्धाङ्ग शयानं शल्यवेधितम्। स मामुद्वीक्ष्य नेत्राभ्यां त्रस्तमस्वस्थचेतनम्॥ इत्युवाच वचः क्रूरं दिधक्षनिव तेजसा।
en: Hearing in the night, the pathetic words of the saint thus lamenting, I became frightened, and was deprived of my senses through excess of grief. And coming to the quarter, I exceedingly unnerved and with an excited mind, discovered on the banks of the Sarayū an ascetic wounded with a shaft, with his matted locks scattered about, his pitcher of water lying by, his body smeared with blood and dust and afflicted by the dare. Gazing with his eyes at me who was extremely agitated and ill at ease, he said these words sternly, as if consuming me with his energy.

sa_verse: किं तवापकृतं राजन् वने निवसता मया॥ जिहीर्घरम्भो गुर्वर्थं यदहं ताडितस्त्वया।
en: What wrong, O monarch, had I residing in the woods done you, that coming to procure water for my parents, I have been thus afflicted by. you?

sa_verse: बाणेन मर्मण्यभिहते मयि ॥ द्वावन्धौ निहतौ वृद्धौ माता जनयिता च मे।
en: By piercing my marrow with a shaft, you have slain both my aged and blind father and mother.

sa_verse: तौ नूनं दुर्बलावन्धौ मत्प्रतीक्षौ पिपासितौ॥ चिरमाशां कृतां कष्टां तृष्णां संधारयिष्यतः।
en: Surely, they feeble and blind, who afflicted by thirst are remaining in expectation of me, will now bear (the stress and tension of) the expectation as well as the parching thirst.

sa_verse: एकेन खलु न नूनं तपसो वास्ति फलयोगः श्रुतस्य वा॥ पिता यन्मां न जानीते शयानं पतितं भुवि।
en: Surely asceticism and study carry no fruit with them, since I lying low on the ground, my father knows nothing about it.

sa_verse: जानन्नपि च किं कुर्यादशक्तश्चापरिक्रमः।॥ भिद्यमानमिवाशक्तस्त्रातुमन्यो नगो नगम्।
en: And what could he do, even if he knew it, being as he is incapable and unable to go about? One tree cannot rescue another that is being battered (by the winds).

sa_verse: पितुस्त्वमेव मे गत्वा शीघ्रमाचक्ष्व राघव॥ न त्वामनुदहेत् क्रुद्धो वनमग्निरिवैधितः।
en: Do you, O descendant of Raghu, yourself going to my father, speedily inform of him this that has occurred. But take care that like a fire waxing furious consuming a woods, he in his ire do not burn you.

sa_verse: इयमेकपदी राजन् यतो मे पितुराश्रमः॥ तं प्रसादय गत्वा त्वं न त्वा संकुपितः शपेत्।
en: This narrow way, O king, will lead you to my father's dwelling. Do you going there, pacify him, so that getting wroth he may not curse you.

sa_verse: विशल्यं कुरु मां राजन् मर्म मे निशितः शरः॥ रुणद्धि मृदु सोत्सेधं तीरमम्बुरयो यथा।
en: Do you (now), O king, take out the arrow. Your sharpened shaft afflicts my marrow, like the tide of a river wearing away a hollowheaving sand bank.

sa_verse: सशल्यः क्लिश्यते प्राणैर्विशल्यो विनशिष्यति।४६।। इति मामविशच्चिन्ता तस्य शल्यापकर्षणे। दुःखितस्य च दीनस्य मम शोकातुरस्य च॥ लक्षयामास स ऋषिश्चिन्तां मुनिसुतस्तदा।
en: But touching the extracting of the arrow, this thought perplexed me: 'If the arrow is left alone, it pains; if extracted, death ensues.' As I was distressed, aggrieved an afflicted with sorrow, the son of the ascetic perceived my anxiety.

sa_verse: ताम्यमानं स मां कृच्छादुवाच परमार्थवित्॥ सीदमानो विवृत्ताङ्गोऽचेष्टमानो गतः क्षयम्। संस्तभ्य शोकं धैर्येण स्थिरचित्तो भवाम्यहम्॥ ब्रह्महत्याकृतं तापं हृदयादपनीयताम्। न द्विजातिरहं राजन्मा भूत् ते मनसो व्यथा॥
en: Thereupon that one well versed in the scriptures sinking motionless, with his eyes rolling upwards, and waxing extremely weak, said with difficulty, 'Restraining sorrow, I by dint of patience become calm. Do you remove from your mind the grief caused by the consciousness of having slain a Brāhmaṇa. O king, I belong not to the twice-born race: let not your mind be pained.

sa_verse: शूद्रायामस्मि वैश्येन जातो नरवराधिप। इतीव वदतः कृच्छाद् बाणाभिहतमर्मणः॥ विघूर्णतो विचेष्टस्य वेपमानस्य भूतले। तस्य त्वाताम्यमानस्य तं बाणमहमुद्धरम्। स मामुद्वीक्ष्य संत्रस्तो जहौ प्राणांस्तपोधनः॥
en: O lord of the foremost men, I was begot by a Vaisya on a Sudra woman.' As he, his vitals afflicted with the shaft, hi eyes rolling, inert and trembling on the ground, with his limbs drawn in, was speaking with difficulty, I drew out the arrow. Thereupon, looking at me, the ascetic, growing affrighted, gave up the ghost.

sa_verse: जलाड़गात्रं तु विलप्य कृच्छं मर्मवणं संततमुच्छसन्तम्। ततः सरय्वां तमहं शयानं समीक्ष्य भद्रे सुभृशं विषण्णः॥
en: On beholding him with his body dripping with water, and mortally wounded, and breathing hard without respite, after he had bewailed his mortal wound, lying on the banks of the Sarayü, I lamented him and was, O gentle lady, greatly aggrieved.

sa_verse: वधमप्रतिरूपं तु महर्षेस्तस्य राघवः। विलपन्नेव धर्मात्मा कौसल्यामिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Remembering the extraordinary death of the Maharsi, that righteous descendant of Raghu, lamenting his son, thus spoke to Kausalyā.

sa_verse: तदज्ञानान्महत्पापं कृत्वा संकुलितेन्द्रियः। एकस्त्वचिन्तयं बुद्ध्या कथं नु सुकृतं भवेत्॥
en: Having unwittingly committed that great sin, I, with my senses oppressed through grief, thought within myself as to how I could mend it.

sa_verse: ततस्तं घटमादाय पूर्णं परमवारिणा। आश्रमं तमहं प्राप्य यथाख्यातपथं गतः॥
en: Then taking up the pitcher filled with excellent water, I went by the way mentioned and (at last) reached the asylum.

sa_verse: तत्राहं दुर्बलावन्धौ वृद्धावपरिणायकौ। अपश्यं तस्य पितरौ लूनपक्षाविव द्विजौ॥ तन्निमित्ताभिरासीनौ कथाभिरपरिश्रमौ। तामाशां मत्कृते हीनावुपासीनावनाथवत्॥
en: There I found his aged, infirm, forlorn, parents, without any one to help them in moving about, like to birds whose wings have been severed, keeping up a talk about (their son) without experiencing any fatigue, and like helpless ones feeding on a hope which had been blasted by me.

sa_verse: शोकोपहतचित्तश्च भयसंत्रस्तचेतनः। तच्चाश्रमपदं गत्वा भूयः शोकमहं गतः॥
en: My senses overpowered by grief, and my consciousness almost lost through apprehension, I, arrived at the hermitage, again overwhelmed with sorrow.

sa_verse: पदशब्दं तु मे श्रुत्वा मुनिर्वाक्यमभाषत। किं चिरायसि मे पुत्र पानीयं क्षिप्रमानय॥
en: Hearing my footsteps, the ascetic said, “Why, my son, delay you? Bring the drink at once.

sa_verse: यनिमित्तमिदं तात सलिले क्रीडितं त्वया। उत्कण्ठिता ते मातेयं प्रविश क्षिप्रमाश्रमम्॥
en: Your mother, O child, was exceedingly anxious in consequence of your sporting in the waters. Do you speedily enter the asylum.

sa_verse: यद् व्यलीकं कृतं पुत्र मात्रा ते यदि वा मया। न तन्मनसि कर्तव्यं त्वया तात तपस्विना॥
en: O child, it behove you not to take to heart any unkind action that, O son, may have been done to you of high fame either by your mother or myself.

sa_verse: त्वं गतिस्त्वगतीनां च चक्षुस्त्वं हीनचक्षुषाम्। समासक्तास्त्वयि प्राणाः कथं त्वं नाभिभाषसे॥
en: You are the resource of these helpless ones; you are the eyes of these bereft of their sight. was Our lives are bound up with you. Why do not answer?'

sa_verse: मुनिमव्यक्तया वाचा तमहं सज्जमानया। हीनव्यञ्जनया प्रेक्ष्य भीतचित्त इवाब्रुवम्॥ मनसः कर्म चेष्टाभिरभिसंस्तभ्य वाग्बलम्। आचचक्षे त्वंहं तस्मै पुत्रव्यसनजं भयम्॥
en: Seeing the ascetic with a choked utterance indistinctly speaking thus with the letters not articulated clearly, I, dashed in spirits, yet concealing the real state of my mind by assuming a doughty tongue, communicated to him the danger that had befallen him in consequence of the calamity of his son:

sa_verse: क्षत्रियोऽहं दशरथो नाहं पुत्रो महात्मनः। सज्जनावमतं दुःखमिदं प्राप्तं स्वकर्मजम् ॥
en: I am a Ksatriya, Dasaratha (by name), and no son of yours, O magnanimous one. I have come by a misfortune in consequence of an act blamed by the good.

sa_verse: भगवंश्चापहस्तोऽहं सरयूतीरमागतः। जिघांसुः श्वापदं किंचिनिपाने वागतं गजम्॥
en: O revered one, desirous of killing some beast of prey, and elephant (or some other), come to the waters, I went to the banks of the Sarayū bow in hand.

sa_verse: ततस्तस्यैव वचनादुपेत्य परितप्यतः। स मया सहसा बाण उद्धृतो ममतस्तदा॥
en: Then coming forward, I in accordance with the direction given by him as he lay in agony, suddenly extracted the arrow from his vitals.

sa_verse: स चोद्धृतेन बाणेन सहसा स्वर्गमास्थितः। भगवन्तावुभौ शोचन्नन्धाविति विलप्य च॥
en: And as soon as the arrow had been extracted, he ascended heaven, O reverend sir, lamenting and bewailing you, both grown old.

sa_verse: अज्ञानाद् भवतः पुत्रः सहसाभिहतो मया। शेषमेवं गते यत्स्यात् तत् प्रसीदतु मे मुनिः॥
en: It is through ignorance that I suddenly wounded your son. This having been past, do you favour me with telling me what is now to be done, O ascetic.

sa_verse: स तच्छ्रुत्वा वचः क्रूरं मया तदघशंसिना। नाशकत् तीव्रमायासं स कर्तुं भगवानृषिः॥
en: Having heard these cruel words, the worshipful ascetic could well by his curse consume me to ashes.

sa_verse: स बाष्पपूर्णवदनो निःश्वसशोकमूर्च्छितः। मामुवाच महातेजाः कृताञ्जलिमुपस्थितम्॥
en: With eyes flooded with tears, and well nigh deprived of his senses by grief, that highly energetic one said to me standing with joined hands.

sa_verse: यद्येतदशुभं कर्म न स्म मे कथयेः स्वयम्। फलेन्मूर्धा स्म राजन् सद्यः शतसहस्रधा॥
en: If, O king, you had not of yourself immediately communicated to us this unpleasant news, your head would have been reduced to a thousand flaws.

sa_verse: क्षत्रियेण वधो राजन् वानप्रस्थे विशेषतः। ज्ञानपूर्वं कृतः स्थानाच्च्यावयेदपि वज्रिणम्॥
en: Not to speak of Kștriyas, I can even drag the very wielder of the thunderbolt himself from his position, if he knowingly kills one, in especial, that has assumed the Vånaprastha mode of life.

sa_verse: सप्तधा तु भवेन्मूर्धा मुनौ तपसि तिष्ठति। ज्ञानाद् विसृजतः शस्त्रं तादृशे ब्रह्मवादिनि॥
en: Your head would have been severed in seven, if you had discharged the weapon knowingly at such an ascetic staying in austerities and versed in the Vedas.

sa_verse: अज्ञानाद्धि कृतं यस्मादिदं ते तेन जीवसे। अपि ह्यकुशलं न स्याद् राघवाणां कुतोभवान् ॥
en: It is because you have done this through ignorance that you (still) live, else the race itself of the Rāghavas should be not, and where are you?

sa_verse: ते नय नौ नृप तं देशमिति मां चाभ्यभाषत। अद्य तं द्रष्टुमिच्छावः पुत्रं पश्चिमदर्शनम्॥
en: He then said, 'Do you, o king, take us to the scene. To day will we look our last on our son besmeared with blood, his deer-skin garb falling off (from his body), lying senseless on the earth, and come under the subjection of the lord of righteousness.'

sa_verse: रुधिरेणावसिक्ताङ्गं प्रकीर्णाजिनवाससम्। शयानं भुवि निःसंज्ञं धर्मराजवशं गतम्॥ अथाहमेकस्तं देशं नीत्वा तौ भृशदुःखितौ। अस्पर्शयमहं पुत्रं तं मुनि सह भार्यया॥
en: Thereupon I alone taking them exceedingly disconsolate to the spot, made the ascetic and his wife touch their son.

sa_verse: तौ पुत्रमात्मनः स्पृष्ट्वा तमासाद्य तपस्विनौ। निपेततुः शरीरेऽस्य पिता चैनमुवाच ह॥
en: And having approached their son and touched him, ascetics fell on his person, and then his father addressed him thus.

sa_verse: नाभिवादयसे माऽद्य न च मामभिभाषसे। किं च शेषे तु भूमौ त्वं वत्स किं कुपितो ह्यसि।।३०।
en: You salute me not to-day, nor did you speak to me. Why, my child, did you lie down on the ground? Art you angry (with us)?

sa_verse: नन्वहं तेऽप्रियः पुत्र मातरं पश्य धार्मिकीम्। किं च नालिङ्गसे पुत्र सुकुमार वचो वद॥
en: If, my son, you do not feel kindly to me, do you look up to your virtuous mother. And why, O son, do you not embrace me? Do you speak tender words.

sa_verse: कस्य वा पररात्रेऽहं श्रोष्यामि हृदयङ्गमम्। अधीयानस्य मधुरं शास्त्रं वान्यद् विशेषतः॥
en: At the small hours, form whom engaged in study, shall I hear the scriptures sweetly read in a way coming home to the listener's mind?

sa_verse: को मां संध्यामुपास्यैव स्नात्वा हुतहुताशनः। श्लाघयिष्यत्युपासीनः पुत्रशोकभयादितम् ॥
en: Who, having performed his daily devotions and offered oblation to the sacrificial fire, will bathe me, afflicted with grief for my son?

sa_verse: कन्दमूलफलं हृत्वा यो मां प्रियमिवातिथिम्। भोजयिष्यत्यकर्मण्यमप्रग्रहमनायकम्॥
en: Who procuring Kandas, fruits and roots, will feed me like an welcome guest, incapable of and doing anything and furnishing provisions, and without any one to take care of myself.?

sa_verse: इमामन्धां च वृद्धां च मातरं ते तपस्विनीम्। कथं पुत्र भरिष्यामि कृपणां पुत्रगर्धिनीम्॥
en: And, my son, how will I maintain this blind ascetic mother of yours, proud of her son, who is passing her days in misery?

sa_verse: तिष्ठ मा मा गमः पुत्र यमस्य सदनं प्रति। श्वो मया सह गन्तासि जनन्या च समेधितः॥
en: Do you stay my son, in my behalf. Tomorrow you will go to Yama's mansion with me and your mother.

sa_verse: उभावपि च शोकार्तावनाथौ कृपणौ वने। क्षिप्रमेव गमिष्यावस्त्वया हीनौ यमक्षयम्॥
en: Distressed with grief and rendered miserable in the forest, both of us deprived of you shall soon repair to the abode of Yama.

sa_verse: ततो वैवस्वतं दृष्ट्वा तं प्रवक्ष्यामि भारतीम्। क्षमतां धर्मराजो मे बिभृयात् पितरावयम्॥
