en: Seeing Vivasvata's son, I will say to him, Do you, O lord of justice forgive me, and let this my son, continue to maintain us, his parents.

sa_verse: दातुमर्हति धर्मात्मा लोकपालो महायशाः। ईदृशस्य ममाक्षय्यामेकामभयदक्षिणाम्॥
en: It behove you, O righteous and illustrious guardian of the words, to confer on me reduced to such a pass this one enduring dakşiņā capable of removing our fear.

sa_verse: अपापोऽसि यथा पुत्र निहतः पापकर्मणा। तेन सत्येन गच्छाशु ये लोकास्त्वस्त्रयोधिनाम्॥ यां हि शूरा गतिं यान्ति संग्रामेष्वनिवर्तिनः। हतास्त्वभिमुखाः पुत्र गतिं तां परमां व्रज॥
en: You, my son, are sinless, although slain by this one who has done an unrighteous act; and by the force of this truth , do you repair to the world of warriors. Do you, O son, go the supreme way that is gone by heroes who without turning back from the fight, are slain in open encounter.

sa_verse: यां गतिं सगरः शैब्यो दिलीपो जनमेजयः। नहुषो धुन्धुमारश्च प्राप्तास्तां गच्छ पुत्रक॥
en: Do you, O son, go the way that has been gone by Sagara and Saivya and Dilīpa and Janamejaya and Nahusa and Dhundhumāra.

sa_verse: या गतिः सर्वभूतानां स्वाध्यायात् तपसश्च या। भूमिदस्याहिताग्नेश्च एकपत्नीव्रतस्य च ॥ गोसहस्रप्रदातॄणां गुरुसेवाभृतामपि। देहन्यासकृतां या च तां गतिं गच्छ पुत्रक॥
en: Do you, O son, go the way that is gone by all creatures, even by ascetics engaged in the study of the Veda, by bestowers of lands, by those performing fire-sacrifices, by individuals each devoted to a single wife, men giving away a thousand kine, persons tending their preceptors, and individuals renouncing lives by fasting.

sa_verse: न हि त्वस्मिन् कुले जातो गच्छत्यकुशलां गतिम्। स तु यास्यति येन त्वं निहतो मम बान्धवः॥
en: He that is born in such a race cannot come by any evil case. Such a condition be his that has taken the life of you, my friend.'

sa_verse: एवं स कृपणं तत्र पर्यदेवयतासकृत्। तथोक्त्वा कर्तुमुदकं प्रवृत्तः सह भार्यया॥
en: Having thus piteously wept, he along with his wife set about performing the watery rites on behalf of his son.

sa_verse: स तु दिव्येन रूपेण मुनिपुत्रः स्वकर्मभिः। स्वर्गमध्यारुहत् क्षिप्रं शक्रेण सह धर्मवित्॥
en: Thereupon speedily assuming a celestial shape, the virtuous son of the ascetic by his own actions ascended heaven in company with Sakra.

sa_verse: आबभाषे च तौ वृद्धौ शक्रेण सह तापसः। आश्वस्य च मुहूर्तं तु पितरं वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then (returning) along with Sakra, the ascetic comforting his aged parents, addressed them, sa ing.

sa_verse: स्थानमस्मि महत् प्राप्तो भवतोः परिचारणात्। भवन्तावपि च क्षिप्रं मम मूलमुपैष्यथः॥
en: I have attained a high state in consequence of having served you. Do you also without delay come to me.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा तु दिव्येन विमानेन वपुष्मता। मामुवाच महातेजाः आरुरोह दिवं क्षिप्रं मुनिपुत्रो जितेन्द्रियः॥
en: Having said this, the ascetic's son of restrained sense ascended heaven by means of an excellent and commodious car.

sa_verse: स कृत्वाथोदकं तूर्णं तापसः सह भार्यया। कृताञ्जलिमुपस्थितम्॥
en: Having performed the watery rites, the highly energetic ascetic along with his wife speedily said to me staying with joined hands.

sa_verse: अद्यैव जहि मां राजन् मरणे नास्ति मे व्यथा। यः शरेणैकपुत्रं मां त्वमकार्षीरपुत्रकम्॥
en: Do you, O monarch, slay me on the instant. I do not grieve to die', you have by your shaft rendered me who had an only son, absolutely sonless.

sa_verse: त्वयापि च यदज्ञानानिहतो मे स बालकः। तेन त्वामपि शप्स्येऽहं सुदुःखमतिदारुणम्॥ पुत्रव्यसनजं दुःखं यदेतन्मम साम्प्रतम्। एवं त्वं पुत्रशोकेन राजन् कालं करिष्यसि ॥
en: Since this sorrow arising from the calamity that has befallen my son, is at present mine (through your instrumentality), I curse you. You shall even in this way find your death from grief born of separation from your son.

sa_verse: अज्ञानात्तु हतो यस्मात् क्षत्रियेण त्वया मुनिः। तस्मात् त्वां नाविशत्याशु ब्रह्महत्या नराधिप॥ त्वामप्येतादृशो भावः क्षिप्रमेव गमिष्यति। जीवितान्तकरो घोरो दातारमिव दक्षिणाम्॥
en: As you a Kşatriya has through ignorance slain an ascetic, the sin, O lord of men, of slaying a Brāhmaṇa will not envelope you speedily; but you shall shortly come by this dreadful and mortal condition, like a donor of Dakşiņās (coming by the things given away).'

sa_verse: एवं शापं मयि न्यस्य विलप्य करुणं बहु। चितामारोप्य देहं तन्मिथुनं स्वर्गमभ्ययात्॥
en: Having thus inflicted on me the curse and piteously lamented long, the coupe ascended the funeral pile and went to heaven.

sa_verse: तदेतच्चिन्तयानेन स्मृतं पापं मया स्वयम्। तदा बाल्यात् कृतं देवि शब्दवेध्यनुकर्षिणा॥
en: O noble dame, the crime that I hitting by help of sound, had committed in my boyhood, has reverted to my recollection in course of thought.

sa_verse: तस्यायं कर्मणो देवि विपाकः समुपस्थितः। अपथ्यैः सह सम्भुक्ते व्याधिरनरसे यथा॥ तस्मान्मामागतं भद्रे तस्योदारस्य तद् वचः।
en: O exalted lady, even as a disease generated by one's taking rice with unhealthy curry, this danger is imminent in consequence of that act. O gentle one, the words of that noble-minded person are about to be verified in me.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा स रुदंस्त्रस्तो भार्यामाह तु भूमिपः॥ यदहं पुत्रशोकेन संत्यजिष्यामि जीवितम्। चक्षुभ्यां त्वां न पश्यामि कौसल्ये त्वं हि मां स्पृश।।
en: Having said this and weeping, the king said to his wife, “I shall renounce life through grief for my son. And I shall no more behold you with my eyes. Do you, O Kausalyā, touch me."

sa_verse: यमक्षयमनुप्राप्ता द्रक्ष्यन्ति नहि मानवाः। यदि मां संस्पृशेद् रामः सकृदन्वारभेत वा॥
en: People going to the mansion of Yama no more behold (their friends): If Rama touch me directly or otherwise, obtain the exchequer, and be installed as the heir-apparent, Meseems, I may yet live.

sa_verse: धनं वा यौवराज्यं वा जीवेयमिति मे मतिः। न तन्मे सदृशं देवि यन्मया राघवे कृतम्॥ सदृशं तत्तु तस्यैव यदनेन कृतं मयि।
en: O noble lady, what I have done to Rāghava in not surely like myself; but what (on the other hand) he has done by me is worthy of him.

sa_verse: दुर्वृत्तमपि कः पुत्रं त्यजेद् भुवि विचक्षणः॥ कश्च प्रव्राज्यमानो वा नासूयेत् पितरं सुतः।
en: What sensible man for sake his son, albeit he may be wicked? And what son being banished, does not bear ill will towards his father?

sa_verse: चक्षुषा त्वां न पश्यामि स्मृतिर्मम विलुप्यते॥ दूता वैवस्वतस्यैते कौसल्ये त्वरयन्ति माम्।
en: But I do not see you with my eyes, and my memory fails. These envoys of Vivasvata's son, OKausalya, urge speed upon me.

sa_verse: अतस्तु किं दुःखतरं यदहं जीवितक्षये॥ नहि पश्यामि धर्मज्ञं रामं सत्यपराक्रमम्।
en: What can be an object of greater regret than that I during my last moments cannot behold the righteous Rāma having truth for prowess?

sa_verse: तस्य दर्शनजः शोकः सुतस्याप्रतिकर्मणः॥ उच्छोषयति वै प्राणान् वारि स्तोकमिवातपः।
en: Even as the sun dried up a drop of water, grief for not seeing my son of incomparable acts dried my spirits.

sa_verse: न ते मनुष्या देवास्ते ये चारुशुभकुण्डलम्॥ मुखं द्रक्ष्यन्ति रामस्य वर्षे पञ्चदशे पुनः।
en: Those are not men, those are gods who in the fifteenth year shall again behold Rāma's countenance graced with elegant and burnished ear-rings.

sa_verse: पद्मपत्रेक्षणं सुभ्र सुदंष्ट्रं चारुनासिकम्॥ धन्या द्रक्ष्यन्ति रामस्य ताराधिपसमं मुखम्।
en: O you of graceful eye-brows, blessed are they who shall behold Rāma's countenance furnished with eyes resembling lotus-petals, with excellent teeth and a shapely nose, like to the lord of the stars himself.

sa_verse: सदृशं शारदस्येन्दोः फुल्लस्य कमलस्य च॥ सुगन्धि मम रामस्य धन्या द्रक्ष्यन्ति ये मुखम्। निवृत्तवनवासं तमयोध्यां पुनरागतम्॥ द्रक्ष्यन्ति सुखिनो रामं शुक्रं मार्गगतं तथा।
en: Blessed are they that shall behold that fragrant face of his like to the autumnal moon, or the fullblown lotus. Thrice-blessed they who with delighted hearts, shall behold Rāma returned from the forest and come back to Ayodhyā, like to Sukra crowning the zenith?

sa_verse: कौसल्ये चित्तमोहेन हृदयं सीदतेतराम्॥ वेदये न च संयुक्ताशब्दस्पर्शरसानहम्। चित्तनाशाद् विपद्यन्ते सर्वाण्येवेन्द्रियाणि हि। क्षीणस्नेहस्य दीपस्य संरक्ता रश्मयो यथा॥
en: O Kausalyā, my heart is weighed down with grief; and I do not perceive objects of hearing, feeling, or taste. My senses are growing dim in consequence of the mental stupor, like the rays of a lamp reduced to smoke, becoming dim when the oil has been exhausted.

sa_verse: अयमात्मभवः शोको मामनाथमचेतनम्। संसाधयति वेगेन यथा कूलं नदीरयः॥
en: As the violence of a river wears away its banks, my grief occasioned through my own agency is destroying me, who am helpless and insensible.

sa_verse: हा राघव महाबाहो हा ममायासनाशन। हा पितृप्रिय मे नाथ हा ममासि गतः सुत । ७५ ।।
en: O mighty-armed Rāghava! O you remover of my troubles! O you that do delight in your father! you are my stay, O my son, that have gone away.

sa_verse: हा कौसल्ये न पश्यामि हा सुमित्रे तपस्विनि। हा नृशंसे ममामित्रे कैकेयि कुलपांसनि॥
en: O Kausalyā, I do not see. O wretched Sumitrā! O cruel one, you enemy of mine, you Kaikeyī, who have befouled your line!

sa_verse: इति मातुश्च रामस्य सुमित्रायाश्च संनिधौ। राजा दशरथः शोचञ्जीवितान्तमुपागमत्॥
en: Having thus lamented in presence of Rāma's mother and Sumitrā, king Daſaratha breathed his last.

sa_verse: तथा तु दीनः कथयन् नराधिपः प्रियस्य पुत्रस्य विवासनातुरः। स्तदा जहौ प्राणमुदारदर्शनः। ७८ ।।
en: Thus that distressed lord of men, smitten with 1e exile of his beloved son, that one possessed fa gracious presence, when the night had been alf spent, wrought up with the violence of his notion, departed this life.

sa_verse: ततस्तु स्तुवतां तेषां सूतानां पाणिवादकाः। अपदानान्युदाहृत्य पाणिवादान्यवादयन्॥
en: And as the bards hymned the monarch, palmplayers celebrating the deeds of the kings of the Raghu race, began to play with their palms.

sa_verse: तेन शब्देन विहगाः प्रतिबुद्धाश्च सस्वनुः। शाखास्थाः पञ्जरस्थाश्च ये राजकुलगोचराः॥ व्यहृताः पुण्यशब्दाश्च वीणानां चापि निःस्वनाः। आशीर्गेयं च गाथानां पूरयामास वेश्म तत्॥
en: And awakened by those sounds, birds on boughs and in cages worthy of the royal race, uttered notes. And the sacred words uttered by these), the notes of Vinas, and the valedictory songs of singers filled that mansion.

sa_verse: ततः शुचिसमाचाराः पर्युपस्थानकोविदाः। स्त्रीवर्षवरभूयिष्ठा उपतस्थुर्यथापुरा॥
en: And as on former occasions, men practising purity and well up in serving, with numerous women and eunuchs entered appearance.

sa_verse: हरिचन्दनसम्पृक्तमुदकं काञ्चनैर्घटैः। आनिन्युः स्नानशिक्षाज्ञा यथाकालं यथाविधि॥
en: Persons acquainted with the ceremonials connected with bath, according to the ordinance and in due time, in golden vessels brought water impregnated with Hair sandal powder.

sa_verse: मङ्गलालम्भनीयानि प्राशनीयान्युपस्करान्। उपानिन्युस्तथा पुण्याः कुमारीबहुलाः स्त्रियः॥
en: Pure females together with many virgins brought kine &c, which were to be touched, Ganges water for sipping, mirrors, cloths, ornaments and other areicles.

sa_verse: सर्वलक्षणसम्पन्नं सर्वं विधिवदर्चितम्। सर्वं सुगुणलक्ष्मीवत् तदभूदाभिहारिकम्॥
en: All the things that were procured (for presentation to the monarch) were worshipped in accordance with prescription, were furnished with auspicious marks, and were of excellent virtues and possessed of auspiciousness.

sa_verse: ततः सूर्योदयं यावत् सर्वं परिसमुत्सुकम्। तस्थावनुपसम्प्राप्तं किंस्विदित्युपशङ्कितम्॥
en: As along as the sun did not rise, all these people remained expecting the presence of the king; but then they were alarmed as to what had occurred.

sa_verse: अथ याः कोसलेन्द्रस्य शयनं प्रत्यनन्तराः। ताः स्त्रियस्तु समागम्य भर्तारं प्रत्यबोधयन्॥
en: Those ladies that were beside the bed of the lord of Kosala, were consoling their husband.

sa_verse: अथाप्युचितवृत्तास्ता विनयेन नयेन च। नह्यस्य शयनं स्पृष्ट्वा किंचिदप्युपलेभिरे॥
en: And engaged in tending the monarch with mild and pliant words, those females knowing the condition of sleep, feeling him as he lay in his bed, did not perceive any action in the evermoving pulse.

sa_verse: ताः स्त्रियः स्वप्नशीलज्ञाश्चेष्टां संचलनादिषु। ता वेपथुपरीताश्च राज्ञः प्राणेषु शङ्किताः॥ प्रतिस्रोतस्तृणाग्राणां सदृशं संचकाशिरे। अथ संदेहमानानां स्त्रीणां दृष्ट्वा च पार्थिवम्। यत् तदाशङ्कितं पापं तदा जज्ञे विनिश्चयः॥
en: Thereupon apprehensive for the king's life, they began to tremble like a blade of grass on a torrent. Filled with doubts at sight of the king, the ladies at last concluded that what had been apprehended (by the monarch) had certainly taken place.

sa_verse: कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च पुत्रशोकपराजिते। प्रसुप्ते न प्रबुध्येते यथा कालसमन्विते ॥
en: Overcome by grief for their sons, Sumitrā and Kausalyā were sleeping as if they were dead, and had not awaked.

sa_verse: निष्प्रभासा विवर्णा च सन्ना शोकेन संनता। न व्यराजत कौसल्या तारेव तिमिरावृता ॥
en: Deprived of lustre, pallid, stricken by sorrow, and lying with her limbs contracted, Kausalya looked like a star enveloped in darkness.

sa_verse: कौसल्यानन्तरं राज्ञः सुमित्रा तदनन्तरम्। न स्म विभ्राजते देवी शोकाश्रुलुलितानना॥
en: After Kausalyā, the king, and after him, Sumitrā; and with her countenance faded from grief this noble lady did not look particularly lovely.

sa_verse: ते च दृष्ट्वा तदा सुप्ते उभे देव्यौ च तं नृपम्। सुप्तमेवोद्गतप्राणमन्तःपुरममन्यत॥
en: Finding these two ladies asleep and the king seeming as if sleeping, the inmates of the inner apartment showed themselves as if their lives had departed out of them.

sa_verse: ततः प्रचुकुशुर्दीनाः सस्वरं ता वराङ्गनाः। करेणेव इवारण्ये स्थानप्रच्युतयूथपाः॥
en: Thereupon exceedingly distressed, those paragons of their sex, like she-elephants in the forest deprived of their leader of the herd, broke out into wailing.

sa_verse: तासामाक्रन्दशब्देन सहसोद्गतचेतने। कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च त्यक्तनिद्रे बभूवतुः॥
en: At the sounds of their lamentations, both Kausalya and Sumitrā suddenly regaining consciousness, awoke from their sleep.

sa_verse: कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च दृष्ट्वा स्पृष्ट्वा च पार्थिवम्। हा नाथेति परिकृष्य पेततुर्धरणीतले॥
en: And Kausalya and Sumitrā looking at the king and feeling him, fell down to the earth, exclaiming, "Ah lord.”

sa_verse: सा कोसलेन्द्रदुहिता चेष्टमाना महीतले। न भ्राजते रजोध्वस्ता तारेव गगनच्युता॥
en: As the daughter of Kosala's lord rolled on the ground, she covered with dust did not appear in all her loveliness, like a star fallen from the sky to the earth below.

sa_verse: नृपे शान्तगुणे जाते कौसल्यां पतितां भुवि। अपश्यंस्ताः स्त्रियः सर्वा हतां नागवधूमिव ॥
en: And when the king had departed this life, the women saw Kausalyā fallen on the earth like the slain mate of an elephant.

sa_verse: ततः सर्वा नरेन्द्रस्य कैकेयीप्रमुखाः स्त्रियः। रुदत्यः शोकसंतप्ता निपेतुर्गतचेतनाः॥
en: Then all the wives of the monarch headed by Kaikeyī, burning with grief and weeping, were well nigh rendered senseless.

sa_verse: ताभिः स बलवान् नादः क्रोशन्तीभिरनुद्रुतः। येन स्फीतीकृतो भूयस्तद् गृहं समनादयत्॥
en: And the loud sounds emitted by these, mingling with those (who had been lamenting before them), attained greater proportions and filled the hall.

sa_verse: तत् परित्रस्तसम्भ्रान्तपर्युत्सुकजनाकुलम्। सर्वतस्तुमुलाक्रन्दं परितापार्तबान्धवम्॥ सद्योनिपतितानन्दं दीनं विक्लवदर्शनम्। बभूव नरदेवस्य सद्म दिष्टान्तमीयुषः॥
en: The mansion of the king became filled with people exceedingly excited and frightened, eager to know all about the matter, became filled with lamentations, with friends afflicted with distress, its joy instantaneously vanished, a scene of distress and dole.

sa_verse: अतीतमाज्ञाय तु पार्थिवर्षभं यशस्विनं तं परिवार्य पत्नयः। भृशं रुदत्यः करुणं सुदुः :खिताः प्रगृह्य बाहू व्यलपन्ननाथवत्॥
en: Knowing that the lord of earth had departed, his wives surrounding that illustrious one, smitten with excess of sorrow and weeping bitterly and piteously, holding the king's hands indulged in lamentations, like forlorn ones.

sa_verse: तमग्निमिव संशान्तमम्बुहीनमिवार्णवम्। गतप्रभमिवादित्यं स्वर्गस्थं प्रेक्ष्य भूमिपम्॥ कौसल्या बाष्पपूर्णाक्षी विविधं शोककर्शिता। उपगृह्य शिरो राज्ञः कैकेयी प्रत्यभाषत ॥
en: Seeing the king had ascended heaven, like to a fire that has cooled, or an ocean deprived of its waters, or the sun shorn of his splendour, Kausalyā afflicted with woe, taking on her lap the head of the king, with tears in her eyes, said.

sa_verse: सकामा भव कैकेयि भुक्ष्व राज्यमकण्टकम्। त्यक्त्वा राजानमेकाना नृशंसे दुष्टचारिणि ॥ विहाय मां गतो रामो भर्ता च स्वर्गतो मम। विपथे सार्थहीनेव नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे॥
en: O Kaikeyī, attain you your wished: do you enjoy this kingdom rid of your thorn. O cruel one, O you of wicked ways, you that forsaking the king had set your heart (on having your son crowned), Rāma had gone away, forsaking me; and now my lord has ascended heaven. I can no longer bear to live, like one left lone in a wilderness by her companions.

sa_verse: भर्तारं तु परित्यज्य का स्त्री दैवतमात्मनः। इच्छेज्जीवितुमन्यत्र कैकेय्यास्त्यक्तधर्मणः॥
en: What other woman except Kaikeyi lost to righteousness, having lost her deity, her lord, wishes to carry on existence in another's kingdom?

sa_verse: न लुब्धो बुध्यते दोषान् किंपाकमिव भक्षयन्। कुब्जानिमित्तं कैकेय्या राघवाणां कुलं हतम्॥ अनियोगे नियुक्तेन राज्ञा रामं विवासितम्। सभार्य जनकः श्रुत्वा परितप्स्यत्यहं यथा।७।।
en: As a covetous person taking poison (through anger or some other passion), does not consider himself guilty, (so Kaikeyī) having done this evil through Manthara's incitement, does not bring her guilt home to her mind. It is through the instrumentality of the humpbacked woman that this race of the Rāghavas has been destroyed by Kaikeyi. Hearing that the king being made to do an unrighteous action, has banished Rāma together with his wife, king Janaka will be filled with grief as I have been.

sa_verse: स मामनाथां विधवां नाद्य जानाति धार्मिकः। रामः कमलपत्राक्षो जीवनाशमितो गतः॥
en: That virtuous one does not know that to-day I have become helpless and been widowed. Răma of eyes resembling lotus-petals has living been removed from my sight.

sa_verse: विदेहराजस्य सुता तथा चारुतपस्विनी। दुःखस्यानुचिता दुःखं वने पर्युद्विजिष्यति॥
en: The fair daughter of Videha's king unworthy of hardship, in ascetic guise is leading a life of trouble and terror in the woods.

sa_verse: नदतां भीमघोषाणां निशासु मृगपक्षिणाम्। निशम्यमाना संत्रस्ता राघवं संश्रयिष्यति॥
en: Hearing at night the dreadful roars of birds and beasts crying, she exceedingly frightened takes shelter with Rāghava.

sa_verse: वृद्धश्चैवाल्पपुत्रश्च वेदेहीमनुचिन्तयन्। सोऽपि शोकसमाविष्टो नूनं त्यक्ष्यति जीवितम्॥ साहमचैव दिष्टान्तं गमिष्यामि पतिव्रता। इदं शरीरमालिङ्गय प्रवेक्ष्यामि हुताशनम्॥
en: Old and having an only daughter, he revolving in his mind thoughts of Vaidehi, shall, smitten with grief, surely renounce his life. I ever faithful to my lord will die this very day, embracing this body; I will enter fire.

sa_verse: तां ततः सम्परिष्वज्य विलपन्ती तपस्विनीम्। व्यपनिन्युः सुदुःखाता कौसल्यां व्यावहारिकाः॥
en: As embracing the (dead) body, that unfortunate lady was bewailing, the courtiers had the distressed (queen) removed from there.

sa_verse: तैलद्रोण्यां तदामात्याः संवेश्य जगतीपतिम्। राज्ञः सर्वाण्यथादिष्टाश्चक्रुः कर्माण्यनन्तरम्॥
en: Then placing the corpse of the king in a (capacious) pan with oil, the courtiers performed the mourning rites of the monarch.

sa_verse: न तु संकालनं राज्ञो विना पुत्रेण मन्त्रिणः। सर्वज्ञाः कर्तुमीषुस्ते ततो रक्षन्ति भूमिपम्॥ तैलद्रोण्यां शायितं तं सचिवैस्तु नराधिपम्। हा मृतोऽयमिति ज्ञात्वा स्त्रियस्ताः पर्यदेवयन्॥
en: But well versed in everything, the counsellors, in the absence of his son, did not perform the funeral obsequies of the king; and therefore they placed his body stretched in the pan of oil. Alas! at length concluding it for certain that the king was dead, the ladies burst out into lamentaions.

sa_verse: बाहूनुच्छ्रित्य कृपणा नेत्रप्रस्रवणैर्मुखैः। रुदत्यः शोकसंतप्ताः कृपणं पर्यदेवयन्॥
en: And raising their arms, with tears trickling down their faces, they in dire affliction and extremely exercised with grief, lamented.

sa_verse: हा महाराज रामेण संततं प्रियवादिना। विहीनाः सत्यसंधेन किमर्थं विजहासि नः॥
en: O monarch, why do you forsake us, who have been already deprived of Rāma ever speaking fair and firm in promise?

sa_verse: कैकेय्या दुष्टभावाया राघवेण विवर्जिताः। कथं सपत्न्या वत्स्यामः समीपे विधवा वयम्॥
en: Renounced by Rāma, how shall we, rendered widows, stay with the wicked Kaikeyi, co-wife with us?

sa_verse: स हि नाथः स चास्माकं तव च प्रभुरात्मवान्। वनं रामो गतः श्रीमान् विहाय नृपतिश्रियम्॥
en: That one of free soul is our master, as he is the lord of yourself. Rāma has gone to the woods, forsaking regal dignity.

sa_verse: त्वया तेन च वीरेण विना व्यसनमोहिताः। कथं वयं निवत्स्यामः कैकेय्या च विदूषिताः॥
en: Deprived of you as well as that hero, and overwhelmed with misfortune, how shall we live reprimanded by Kaikeyi?

sa_verse: यया च राजा रामश्च लक्ष्मणश्च महाबलः। सीतया सह संत्यक्ताः सा कमन्यं न हास्यति ॥
en: She that has renounced the king, Rāma, and Lakşmaņa along with Sītā, whom can such a one not renounce?

sa_verse: ता बाष्पेण च संवीताः शोकेन विपुलेन च। व्यचेष्टन्त निरानन्दा राघवस्य वरस्त्रियः॥
en: Thus with tears in their eyes, the wives of that descendant of Raghu, joyless and convulsed with a huge passion, displayed signs of sorrow.

sa_verse: निशा नक्षत्रहीनेव स्त्रीव भर्तृविवर्जिता। पुरी नाराजतायोध्या हीना राज्ञा महात्मना॥ बाष्पपर्याकुलजना हाहाभूतकुलाङ्गना। शून्यचत्वरवेश्मान्ता न बभ्राज यथापुरम्॥
en: Like a night without stars, like a fair one forsaken by her husband, the city of Ayodhyā without the magnanimous monarch did not appear delightful as it had done before, with the populace filled with tears, the ladies uttering exclamations of distress, and the terraces and courts deserted.

sa_verse: गते तु शोकात् त्रिदिवं नराधिपे महीतलस्थासु नृपाङ्गनासु च। निवृत्तचारः सहसा गतो रविः प्रवृत्तचारा रजनी ह्युपस्थिता॥
en: On the lord of men having ascended heaven from grief, and the wives of the king remaining on the earth, the sun, his journey done, set, and the night began her course.

sa_verse: नारोचयस्ते सुहृदः समागताः। इतीव तस्मिशयने न्यवेशयन् विचिन्त्य राजानमचिन्त्यदर्शनम्॥
en: The idea of consuming the king's corpse in the absence of his son did not recommended itself to the assembled. adherents (of the departed). Thinking this, they in that way laid the king endowed with an inconceivably dignified presence.

sa_verse: गतप्रभा द्यौरिव भास्करं विना व्यपेतनक्षत्रगणेव शर्वरी। पुरी बभासे रहिता महात्मना कण्ठास्रकण्ठाकुलमार्गचत्वरा ॥
en: And with her terraces overflowing with tears that flooded the throats of the mourners, the city appeared like the welkin without its splendour in the absence of the sun, or the night with the stars enveloped.

sa_verse: नराश्च नार्यश्च समेत्य संघशो विगर्हमाणा भरतस्य मातरम्। तदा नगाँ नरदेवसंक्षये बभूवुरार्ता न च शर्म लेभिरे॥
en: And on on the demise of that illustrious personage, in the city men and women in multitudes, censuring Bharata's mother, became extremely distressed, and did not attain peace of mind.

sa_verse: आक्रन्दिता निरानन्दा सास्रकण्ठजनाविला। अयोध्यायामवतता सा व्यतीयाय शर्वरी॥
en: At length the weary night in Ayodhya rendered cheerless cheerless by lamentations, and populous with men with voice choked with tears, was spent.

sa_verse: व्यतीतायां तु शर्वर्यामादित्यस्योदये ततः। समेत्य राजकर्तारः सभामीयुर्द्विजातयः॥ मार्कण्डेयोऽथ मौद्गल्यो वामदेवश्च कश्यपः। कात्यायनो गौतमश्च जाबालिश्च महायशाः॥ एते द्विजाः सहामात्यैः पृथग्वाचमुदीरयन्। वसिष्ठमेवाभिमुखाः श्रेष्ठं राजपुरोहितम्॥
en: And when the night had departed and the sun had arisen, those officers of the royal house-hold belonging to the twice-born order, Märkandeya, Maudgalya, Vāmadeva, Kaśyapa, Kātyāyana, Gautama, and the highly famous Jābāli. assembled together along with the counsellors, spoke each on different topics.

sa_verse: अतीता शर्वरी दुःखं या नो वर्षशतोपमा। अस्मिन् पञ्चत्वमापने पुत्रशोकेन पार्थिवे॥
en: Then facing the royal priest, the eminent Vasistha, they said, "That night that had appeared like to an hundred years has at last been painfully passed.

sa_verse: स्वर्गस्थश्च महाराजो रामश्चारण्यमाश्रितः। लक्ष्मणश्चापि तेजस्वी रामेपैव गतः सह॥ उभौ भरतशत्रुघ्नौ केकयेषु परंतपौ। पुरे राजगृहे रम्ये मातामहनिवेशने॥
en: The king racked by sorrow for his son having breathed his last, the mighty monarch has ascended heaven, Rāma has taken refuge in the woods, the energetic Lakşmaņa has gone with Rama, and does Bharata and Satrughna, repressors of foes, are staying in Kekaya in pleasant Rājagrha, the abode of their maternal grandfather.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकूणामिहाद्यैव कश्चिद् राजा विधीयताम्। अराजकं हि नो राष्ट्र विनाशं समवाप्नुयात् ।।
en: Do you select some on this very day from the descendants of Ikşvāku, to be king here. Verily does a kingdom go to ruin, when without a king.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे विद्युन्माली महास्वनः। अभिवर्षति पर्जन्यो महीं दिव्येन वारिणा॥
en: He that goes garlanded with lightning, and has a mighty voice, even the Cloud, does not with Skye shower drench the Earth in a kingdom without a king.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे बीजमुष्टिः प्रकीर्यते। अराजके पितुः पुत्रो भार्या वा वर्तते वशे॥
en: In a kingless kingdom no one sows corn. In a kingless kingdom the son does not obey his sire, or the wife her husband.

sa_verse: अराजके धनं नास्ति नास्ति भार्याप्यराजके। इदमत्याहितं चान्यत् कुतः सत्यमराजके॥
en: A kingless kingdom possesses no wealth, and wives are hard to keep in such a place. This great fear attends a kingless country. And where is other morality (besides that detailed above) to be found at such a place?

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे कारयन्ति सभां नराः। उद्यानानि च रम्याणि हृष्टाः पुण्यगृहाणि च॥
en: In a kingless country men do not form themselves into associations, nor do they, inspired with cheerfulness, make elegant gardens or sacred edifices.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे यज्ञशीला द्विजातयः। सत्राण्यन्वासते दान्ता ब्राह्मणाः संशितव्रताः॥ नाराजके जनपदे महायज्ञेषु यज्वनः। ब्राह्मणा वसुसम्पूर्णा विसृजन्त्याप्तदक्षिणाः॥
en: In a kingless country, the twice-born ones do not celebrate sacrifices. In a kingless country, in mighty sacrifices wealthy Brāhmaṇas do not confer (on the officiating priests) the Dakșiņās (which they receive according to the ordinance).

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे प्रहृष्टनटनर्तकाः। उत्सवाश्च समाजाश्च वर्धन्ते राष्ट्रवर्धनाः॥
en: In a kingless country, neither neither social gatherings, nor festivities characterised by the presence of merry theatrical managers and performers, increase.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे सिद्धार्था व्यवहारिणः। कथाभिरभिरज्यन्ते कथाशीलाः कथाप्रियैः॥
en: In a kingless country disputants cannot decide their point; nor are persons given to hearing Puranic recitations pleased by those delighting in the practice.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे तूद्यानानि समागताः। सायाह्ने क्रीडितुं यान्ति कुमार्यो हेमभूषिताः॥
en: In a kingless country, bevies of virgins decked in gold do not repair to gardens for purposes of sport.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे धनवन्तः सुरक्षिताः। शेरते विवृतद्वाराः कृषिगोरक्षजीविनः॥
en: In a kingless country, the wealthy are not well protected; nor do shepherds and cultivators sleep with their doors open.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे वाहनैः शीघ्रवाहिभिः। नरा निर्यान्त्यरण्यानि नारीभिः सह कामिनः॥
en: In a kingless country pleasure-seeking people do not in company with females go to the woods by means of swift vehicles.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे बद्धघण्टा विषाणिनः। अटन्ति राजमार्गेषु कुञ्जराः षष्ठिहायनाः॥
en: In a kingless country long-tusked elephants sixty years old, bearing bells on their necks, do not walk the high-way.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे शरान् संततमस्यताम्। श्रूयते तलनिर्घोष इष्वस्त्राणामुपासने॥
en: In a kingless country one hears not the clappings of persons engaged in shooting arrows constantly.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे वणिजो दूरगामिनः। गच्छन्ति क्षेममध्वानं बहुपण्यसमाचिताः॥
en: In a kingless country traders coming from distant lands, loaded with various kinds of merchandise, do not with safety go along the roads.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे चरत्येकचरो वशी। भावयन्नात्मनात्मानं यत्रसायं गृहो मुनिः॥
en: In a kingless country the ascetic with his subdued senses, himself his sole protector, who makes his quarters wherever evening overtakes him, cannot walk contemplating the Deity.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे योगक्षेमः प्रवर्तते। न चाप्यराजके सेना शत्रून् विषहते युधि॥
en: In a kingless country, one cannot protect what one has, or procure what one has not. In a kingless country, the forces cannot bear the onslaught of the foe.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे हृष्टैः परमवाजिभिः। नराः संयान्ति सहसा रथैश्च प्रतिमण्डिताः॥
en: In a kingless country men cannot at will go on excellent and high-mettled horses and ornamented cars.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे नराः शास्त्रविशारदाः। संवदन्तोपतिष्ठन्ते वनेपूपवनेषु वा॥
en: In a kingless country persons well versed in learning can not engage in controversy, repairing to woods and groves.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे माल्यमोदकदक्षिणाः। देवताभ्यर्चनार्थाय कल्प्यन्ते नियतैर्जनैः॥ नाराजके जनपदे चन्दनागुरुरुषिताः। राजपुत्रा विराजन्ते वसन्ते इव शाखिनः॥
en: In a kingless country, persons with intent hearts do not offer garlands, sweets, and Daksinās, for worshipping the gods. In a kingless country princes smeared with sandal and aguru, do not look graceful like trees in spring.

sa_verse: यथा ह्यनुदका नद्यो यथा वाप्यतृणं वनम्। अगोपाला यथा गावस्तथा राष्ट्रमराजकम्॥
en: Even as a river without water, a wood without grass, a herd of kine without a keeper, is a kingdom without a king.

sa_verse: ध्वजो रथस्य प्रज्ञानं धूमो ज्ञानं विभावसोः। तेषां यो नो ध्वजो राजा स देवत्वमितो गतः॥
en: The sign of a car is its pennon, of fire is smoke, and our banner the king, has gone to heaven.

sa_verse: नाराजके जनपदे स्वकं भवति कस्यचित्। मत्स्या इव जना नित्यं भक्षयन्ति परस्परम्॥
en: In a kingless country a person hardly preserves his life; and like fishes people eat up one another.

sa_verse: ये हि सम्भिन्नमर्यादा नास्तिकाश्छिन्नसंशयाः। तेऽपि भावाय कल्पन्ते राजदण्डनिपीडिताः॥
en: Even those heretics who having disregarded the dignity of social morality had met with chastisement at the hands of the king, their fear removed, give themselves airs.

sa_verse: यथा दृष्टिः शरीरस्य नित्यमेव प्रवर्तते। तथा नरेन्द्रो राष्ट्रस्य प्रभवः सत्यधर्मयोः॥
en: As the sight is engaged in the welfare of the body, the king, that fountain of truth and religion, is engaged in compassing the good of the kingdom.

sa_verse: राजा सत्यं च धर्मश्च राजा कुलवतां कुलम्। राजा माता पिता चैव राजा हितकारो नृणाम्॥
en: The king is truth, the king is morality, the king is the racial dignity of those possessed of the same, the king is the father, the king is the ON 68 RĀMĀYAŅAM mother, the king compasses the welfare of men.

sa_verse: यमो वैश्रवणः शक्रो वरुणश्च महाबलः। विशिष्यन्ते नरेन्द्रेण वृत्तेन महता ततः॥
en: By virtue of magnanimity of character, a king surpasses Yama and Vaiśravaṇa and Śakra and Varuņa endowed with mighty strength.

sa_verse: अहो तम इवेदं स्यान प्रज्ञायेत किंचन। राजा चेन भवेल्लोके विभजन् साध्वसाधुनी॥ जीवत्यपि महाराजे तवैव वचनं वयम्। नातिक्रमामहे सर्वे वेलां प्राप्येव सागः ॥
en: If there were not a king in this world to adjudge fair and foul, darkness would overspread (the face of the earth) and people could not distinguish anything whatever. As the ocean keeps within its continent, we even while the monarch lived, did not disregard your words.

sa_verse: स नः समीक्ष्य द्विजवर्य वृत्तं नृपं विना राष्ट्रमरण्यभूतम्। कुमारमिक्ष्वाकुसुतं तथान्यं त्वमेव राजानमिहाभिषेचय॥
en: Do you, O best of Brāhmaṇas, beholding our acts rendered nugatory, and this empire became a wilderness for want of a king, install that descendant of Ikşvāku or any other as king of this realm.

sa_verse: तेषां तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा वसिष्ठः प्रत्युवाच ह। मित्रामात्यजनान् सर्वान् ब्राह्मणांस्तानिदं वचः॥
en: Hearing their words, Vasiştha said to the Brāhmaṇas and the adherents and counsellors (of the king)

sa_verse: यदसौ मातुलकुले दत्तराज्यः परं सुखी। भरतो वसति भ्रात्रा शत्रुघ्नेन मुदान्वितः॥
en: Bharata, on whom the king has conferred the kingdom, along with his brother Satrughna, is living happily in the house of his maternal uncle.

sa_verse: तच्छीघ्रं जवना दूता गच्छन्तु त्वरितं हयैः। आनेतुं भ्रातरौ वीरौ किं समीक्षामहे वयम्॥
en: Let envoys by means of fleet coursers speedily repair thither and bring those heroic brothers. What shall we decide?

sa_verse: गच्छन्त्विति ततः सर्वे वसिष्ठं वाक्यमब्रुवन्। तेषां तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा वसिष्ठो वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Let them go", said all to Vasistha. Hearing their words, Vasistha spoke to them, saying.

sa_verse: एहि सिद्धार्थ विजय जयन्ताशोकनन्दन। श्रूयतामितिकर्तव्यं सर्वानेव ब्रवीमि वः॥
en: Come here, you Siddhārtha, and Vijaya, and Jayanta, and Asokanandana. Do you hear. I well tell you what you are to do.

sa_verse: पुरं राजगृहं गत्वा शीघ्रं शीघ्रजवैर्हयैः। त्यक्तशोकैरिदं वाच्यः शासनाद् भरतो मम ॥
en: First going speedily to the royal residence by means of swift horses, do you, renouncing grief, by my order speak these words of mine to Bharata.

sa_verse: पुरोहितस्त्वां कुशलं प्राह सर्वे च मन्त्रिणः। त्वरमाणश्च निर्याहि कृत्यमात्ययिकं त्वया।७॥
en: The priest as well as the counsellors have enquired after your welfare. Do you at once set out. A business is at hand that brooks no delay on your part.

sa_verse: मा चास्मै प्रोषितं रामं मा चास्मै पितरं मृतम्। भवन्तः शंसिषुर्गत्वा राघवाणामितः क्षयम्॥
en: But you must on no account communicate to him the exile of Rāma, or the demise of the monarch, or the destruction that has befallen the Raghu race through this occasion.

sa_verse: कौशेयानि च वस्त्राणि भूषणानि वराणि च। क्षिप्रमादाय राज्ञश्च भरतस्य च गच्छत॥
en: Do you, speedily taking silk apparel and excellent ornaments for king Bharata, set off.

sa_verse: दत्तपथ्यशना दूता जग्मुः स्वं स्वं निवेशनम्। केकयांस्ते गमिष्यन्तो हयानारुह्य सम्मतान्॥
en: Thereupon, having been furnished with the necessaries for the journey, they bound for Kekaya went to their respective abodes, mounted on goodly horses.

sa_verse: ततः प्रास्थानिकं कृत्वा कार्यशेषमनन्तरम्। वसिष्ठेनाभ्यनुज्ञाता दूताः संत्वरितं ययुः॥
en: Then having supplied themselves with the necessaries of the journey, the envoys in consonance with Vasiştha's injunction, speedily went away.

sa_verse: न्यन्तेनापरतालस्य प्रलम्बस्योत्तरं प्रति। निषेवमाणास्ते जग्मुर्नदी मध्येन मालिनीम्॥
en: Having proceeded by the west of Aparatāla, they in the middle crossed the Mālinſ, and went towards the north of Pralamba.

sa_verse: ते हास्तिनपुरे गङ्गां तीर्त्वा प्रत्यङ्मुखा ययुः। पाञ्चालदेशमासाद्य मध्येन कुरुजाङ्गलम्॥
en: Then crossing the Gangā at Hastināpura, and arriving at Pāñcāla, they proceeded west ward through Kurujāngala.

sa_verse: सरांसि च सुफुल्लानि नदीश्च विमलोदकाः। निरीक्षमाणा जग्मुस्ते दूताः कार्यवशाद् द्रुतम्॥
en: And beholding watery expan'ses filled with blown blossoms, and rivers containing pellucid waters, the envoys proceeded apace on account of the errand they had on hand.

sa_verse: ते प्रसन्नोदकां दिव्यां नानाविहगसेविताम्। उपातिजग्मुर्वेगेन शरदण्डां जलाकुलाम्॥
en: And they darted past the Saradanda overflowing with delightful water, beautiful, and haunted by fowls of various kinds.

sa_verse: निकूलवृक्षमासाद्य दिव्यं सत्योपयाचनम्। अभिगम्याभिवाद्यं तं कुलिङ्गां प्राविशन् पुरीम्॥
en: Then on the western bank of the stream, coming upon a tree called Satyopayācana presided by a deity, and bowing down to the tree and going round it, they entered the city of Kulinga.

sa_verse: अभिकालं ततः प्राप्य तेजोऽभिभवनाच्च्युताः। पितृपैतामहीं पुण्यां तेरुरिक्षुमती नदीम्॥
en: And having passed Tejobhibhavana and arrived at Abhikāla, they crossed the sacred stream Ikşumatī belonging to the Ikşvākus for generations.

sa_verse: अवेक्ष्याञ्जलिपानांश्च ब्राह्मणान् वेदपारगान्। ययुर्मध्येन बाह्रीकान् सुदामानं च पर्वतम्॥
en: Beholding here Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vedas drinking water with their joined hands, they went through Vahllika towards the mount Sudaman.

sa_verse: विष्णोः पदं प्रेक्ष्यमाणा विपाशां चापि शाल्मलीम्। नदीर्वापीतटाकानि पल्वलानि सरांसि च॥ पश्यन्तो विविधांश्चापि सिंहान् व्याघ्रान् मृगान् द्विपान्। ययुः पथातिमहता शासनं भर्तुरीप्सवः॥
en: There seeing the foot-print of Visnu, they desirous of doing the bidding of their master, proceeded a long way, viewing the Vipasa and the Salmali and rivers and tanks and pools and ponds and sheets of water and various kinds of lions and tigers and deer and elephants.

sa_verse: ते श्रान्तवाहना दूता विकृष्टेन सता पथा। गिरिव्रजं पुरवरं शीघ्रमासेदुरञ्जसा ॥
en: And with their vehicles fatigued, the ambassadors, on account of the great distance of the way; speedily reached that best of cities, Girivraja.

sa_verse: भर्तुः प्रियार्थं कुलरक्षणार्थं भर्तुश्च वंशस्य परिग्रहार्थम्। अहेडमानास्त्वरया स्म दूता रात्र्यां तु ते तत्पुरमेव याताः॥
en: And for pleasing their master, for the preservation of the people, and enabling Bharata to assume the reins of government, those envoys, casting aside negligence, swiftly entered the city in the night.

sa_verse: यामेव रात्रिं ते दूताः प्रविशन्ति स्म तां पुरीम्। भरतेनापि तां रात्रि स्वप्नो दृष्टोऽयमप्रियः॥
en: The very same night that the envoys entered the city, Bharata saw an evil dream.

sa_verse: व्युष्टामेव तु तां रात्रिं दृष्ट्वा तं स्वप्नमप्रियम्। पुत्रो राजाधिराजस्य सुभृशं पर्यतप्यत॥
en: And seeing that evil dream during the short hours, the son of that king of kings exceedingly burned in grief.

sa_verse: तप्यमानं तमाज्ञाय वयस्याः प्रियवादिनः। आयासं विनयिष्यन्तः सभायां चक्रिरे कथाः॥
en: And finding him aggrieved, his sweet. speeched associates, endeavouring to chase the heaviness, began to converse on a variety of subjects.

sa_verse: वादयन्ति तदा शान्ति लासयन्त्यपि चापरे। नाटकान्यपरे स्माहुर्हास्यानि विविधानि च॥ ।
en: Some played on instruments; some for the purpose of pacifying his mind, danced (the courtezans); others performed scenes variously fraught with the sentiment of mirth.

sa_verse: स तैर्महात्मा भरतः सखिभिः प्रियवादिभिः। गोष्ठीहास्यानि कुर्वद्भिर्न प्राहृष्यत राघवः॥
en: But although his friends intending to ally his agitation set about enacting passages calculated to amuse family circles, that magnanimous descendant of Raghu did not indulge in laughter.

sa_verse: तमब्रवीत् प्रियसखो भरतं सखिभिर्वृतम्। सुहृद्भिः पर्युपासीनः किं सखे नानुमोदसे॥
en: Then a dear friend addressed Bharata, as he sat surrounded by his friends, “Surrounded by your friends, why do you not, my friend, join in the mirth?"

sa_verse: एवं ब्रुवाणं सुहृदं भरतः प्रत्युवाच ह। शृणु त्वं यनिमित्तं मे दैन्यमेतदुपागतम्॥ स्वप्ने पितरमद्राक्षं मलिनं मुक्तमूर्धजम्। पतन्तमद्रिशिखरात् कलुषे गोमये हृदे॥
en: Thus asked by his friend, Bharata answered, “Listen why this depression has overtaken me. In a dream I beheld my father, pale, with his hair loosely flowing about, plunging from the summit of a mountain into a dirty pool filled with cowdung.”

sa_verse: प्लवमानश्च मे दृष्टः स तस्मिन् गोमये हृदे। पिबन्नञ्जलिना तैलं हसन्निव मुहुर्मुहुः॥
en: And I saw him floating on a sink of cowdung, and yet with a momentary laugh drinking oil by means of his joined hands.

sa_verse: ततस्तिलोदनं भुक्त्वा पुनः पुनरधःशिराः। तैलेनाभ्यक्तसर्वाङ्गस्तैलमेवान्वगाहत॥
en: Then feeding on rice mixed with sesame, he again and again hanging his head down, dives into oil with his limbs rubbed with oil.

sa_verse: स्वप्नेऽपि सागरं शुष्कं चन्द्रं च पतितं भुवि। उपरुद्धां च जगतीं तमसेव समावृताम्॥ औपवाह्यस्य नागस्य विषाणं शकलीकृतम्। सहसा चापि संशान्ता ज्वलिता जातवेदसः॥ अवदीर्णां च पृथिवीं शुष्कांश्च विविधान् दुमान् । अहं पश्यामि विध्वस्तान् सधूमांश्चैव पर्वतान्॥
en: And in my dream I saw the ocean dried up, and the moon fallen on the earth, and the earth as if invaded by enfolding darkness, and the tusk of the elephant on which the monarch rides falling in fragments, and flaming fire suddenly extinguished, and the earth rent, and the trees withered, and all the mountains belching smoke.

sa_verse: पीठे कार्णायसे चैव निषण्णं कृष्णवाससम्। प्रहरन्ति स्म राजानं प्रमदाः कृष्णपिङ्गलाः॥
en: I saw the king seated on a sable seat of iron, clad in a sable garb; and women black and yellow beating him.

sa_verse: त्वरमाणश्च धर्मात्मा रक्तमाल्यानुलेपनः। रथेन खरयुक्तेन प्रयार्ता दक्षिणामुखः॥
en: And bearing a garland of red flowers, with his body daubed with red sandal, he was fast proceeding to the south in a car yoked with asses.

sa_verse: प्रहसन्तीव राजानं प्रमदा रक्तवासिनी। प्रकर्षन्ती मया दृष्टा राक्षसी विकृतानना॥
en: And women clad in red garment were laughing at him, and a grim-visaged Rākşasa was seen by me as dragging him.

sa_verse: एवमेतन्मया दृष्टमिमां रात्रिं भयावहाम्। अहं रामोऽथवा राजा लक्ष्मणो वा मरिष्यति॥
en: This was the dream that I saw this terrible night. Either I, or Rama, or the king, or Lakşmaņa is to breathe our last.

sa_verse: नरो यानेन यः स्वप्ने खरयुक्तेन याति हि। अचिरात्तस्य धूम्राग्रं चितायां सम्प्रदृश्यते॥ एतन्निमित्तं दीनोऽहं न वचः प्रतिपूजये। शुष्यतीव च मे कण्ठो न स्वस्थमिव मे मनः॥
en: The smoke of the funeral pyre of him will be shortly visible that goes in the car yoked with asses. It is for this reason that I am poor of spirit, and that I do not respond to your words. Further, my throat is parched, and my mind ill at ease.

sa_verse: न पश्यामि भयस्थानं भयं चैवोपधारये। भ्रष्टश्च स्वरयोगो मे छाया चापगता मम। जुगुप्सु इव चात्मानं न च पश्यामि कारणम्॥
en: Ground of fear find I none, yet am I subject to fear. My voice is untuned, and my grace fled, and I begin to despise my life, nor know I the reason why.

sa_verse: इमां च दुःस्वप्नगतिं निशम्य हि त्वनेकरूपामवितर्कितां पुरा। भयं महत्वद् हृदयान याति मे विचिन्त्य राजानमचिन्त्यदर्शनम्॥
en: Bringing to mind this various-looking dream which I had not thought of before, and remembering the king of incomprehensible presence, this fear goes not from my heart.

sa_verse: भरते ब्रुवति स्वप्नं दूतास्ते क्लान्तवाहनाः। प्रविश्यासह्यपरिखं रम्यं राजगृहं पुरम्॥
en: Bharata was speaking thus when the envoys with their vehicles fatigued, entered the splendid royal residence surrounded by a strong rampart.

sa_verse: समागम्य च राज्ञा ते राजपुत्रेण चार्चिताः। राज्ञः पादौ गृहीत्वा च तमूचुर्भरतं वचः॥
en: Presenting themselves before the king, they, well received by him as well as the prince, bowed down to the feet of the monarch.

sa_verse: पुरोहितस्त्वां कुशलं प्राह सर्वे च मन्त्रिणः। त्वरमाणश्च निर्याहि कृत्यमात्ययिकं त्वया॥
en: Then they addressed Bharata, saying, The priest and the counsellors have enquired after your welfare. Do you set out speedily. A business is on hand that Brooke no delay on your part.

sa_verse: इमानि च महार्हाणि वस्त्राण्याभरणानि च। प्रतिगृह्य विशालाक्ष मातुलस्य च दापय॥
en: And, O you of expansive eyes, do you take these costly raiment's and ornaments, and present them to your maternal uncle and grandfather.

sa_verse: अत्र विंशतिकोट्यस्तु नृपतोर्मातुलस्य ते। दशकोट्यस्तु सम्पूर्णास्तथैव च नृपात्मज ॥
en: Twenty Kotis are intended for the king and complete ten for your maternal uncle, O son of the monarch.

sa_verse: प्रतिगृह्य तु तत् सर्वं स्वनुरक्तः सुहज्जने। दूतानुवाच भरतः कामैः सम्प्रतिपूज्य तान्।६।।
en: Taking all these, Bharata attached to his relatives, made the articles over to them; and receiving the envoys with goodly gifts, spoke to them.

sa_verse: कच्चित् स कुशली राजा पिता दशरथो मम। कच्चिदारोग्यता राम लक्ष्मणे च महात्मनि॥
en: Is my father, king Dasaratha, well? And is it well with Rama and the high-souled Laksmana?

sa_verse: आर्या च धर्मनिरता धर्मज्ञा धर्मवादिनी। अरोगा चापि कौसल्या माता रामस्य धीमतः॥
en: And is the mother of the intelligent Rāma, the revered Kausalyā, conversant with virtue and ever practising it, well?

sa_verse: कच्चित् सुमित्रा धर्मज्ञा जननी लक्ष्मणस्य या। शत्रुघ्नस्य च वीरस्य अरोगा चापि मध्यमा॥
en: Is the virtuous Sumitrā, mother of Lakşmaņa and of the heroic Śatrughna, well?

sa_verse: आत्मकामा सदा चण्डी क्रोधना प्राज्ञमानिनी। अरोगा चापि मे माता कैकेयी किमुवाच ह॥
en: And that wrathful one ever intent on her interest and setting immense store by her wisdom, my mother Kaikeyī, is it also well with her?

sa_verse: एवमुक्तास्तु ते दूता भरतेन महात्मना। ऊचुः सम्प्रश्रितं वाक्यमिदं तं भरतं तदा॥
en: Thus addressed by the magnanimous Bharata, the envoys spoke to him these humble and brief words.

sa_verse: कुशलास्ते नरव्याघ्र येषां कुशलमिच्छसि। श्रीश्च त्वां वृणुते पद्मा युज्यतां चापि ते रथः॥
en: Those of whose welfare you are enquiring after, are, O foremost of men, all well. Śrī seated on the lotus asks for you. Let your car be yoked.

sa_verse: भरतश्चापि तान् दूतानेवमुक्तोऽभ्यभाषत। आपृच्छेऽहं महाराजं दूताः संत्वरयन्ति माम्॥
en: Thus addressed, Bharata said to the envoys, “Let me tell the king that the envoys are urging speed on me.”

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा तु तान् दूतान् भरतः पार्थिवात्मजः। दूतैः संचोदितो वाक्यं मातामहमुवाच ह ॥
en: Having said this, that son of the king, Bharata, communicated to his maternal grand-father what the envoys had told him.

sa_verse: राजन् पितुर्गमिष्यामि सकाशं दूतचोदितः। पुनरप्यहमेष्यामि यदा मे त्वं स्मरिष्यसि ॥
en: Asked by the envoys, I shall, O monarch, go to my father. I shall come again whenever you will remember me.

sa_verse: भरतेनैवमुक्तस्तु नृपो मातामहस्तदा। तमुवाच शुभं वाक्यं शिरस्याघ्राय राघवम्॥
en: Thus accosted by Bharata, his maternal grandfather, the king, smelling Bharata's head, spoke these auspicious words to that descendant of Raghu.

sa_verse: गच्छ तातानुजाने त्वां कैकेयी सुप्रजास्त्वया। मातरं कुशलं ब्रूयाः पितरं च परंतप॥
en: Go, my child; I permit you. Kaikeyi is mother of a worthy son through you. Do you O subduer of foes, communicate our welfare to both your father and your mother.

sa_verse: पुरोहितं च कुशलं ये चान्ये द्विजसत्तमाः। तौ च तात महेष्वासौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ॥
en: Do you likewise communicate the same to the priest and the other principal Brāhmaṇas; as also, my child, to those mighty bowmen, the brothers Rāma and Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: तस्मै हस्त्युत्तमांश्चित्रान् कम्बलानजिनानि च। सत्कृत्य केकयो राजा भरताय ददौ धनम्॥
en: Then honouring Bharata, king Kekaya conferred on Bharata wealth consisting of choice elephants maintained (at the palace) and woollen sheets and deerskins.

sa_verse: अन्तःपुरेऽतिसंवृद्धान् व्याघ्रवीर्यबलोपमान्। दंष्ट्रायुक्तान् महाकायाशुनश्चोपायनं ददौ॥
en: The king presented him with dogs brought up in the inner apartment, resembling tigers in strength and prowess, furnished with teeth representing weapons, and large of body.

sa_verse: रुक्मनिष्कसहस्र द्वे षोडशाश्वशतानि च। सत्कृत्य केकयीपुत्रं केकयो धनमादिशत्॥
en: Honouring the son of Kaikeyi, Kekaya gave him two thousand nishkas and six hundred : horses.

sa_verse: तदामात्यानभिप्रेतान् विश्वास्यांश्च गुणान्वितान्। ददावश्वपतिः शीघ्रं भरतायानुयायिनः॥
en: And for following Bharata, Aśvapati without delay assigned a number of goodly, trustworthy, and qualified courtiers.

sa_verse: ऐरावतानैन्द्रशिरान् नागान् वै प्रियदर्शनान्। खराशीघ्रान् सुसंयुक्तान् मातुलोऽस्मै धनं ददौ॥
en: Bharata's maternal uncle conferred on Bharata wealth in the shape of graceful elephants sprung in the īrāvat mountain and the country called Indrasira; as well as fleet and well-broken horses.

sa_verse: स दत्तं केकयेन्द्रेण धनं तन्नाभ्यनन्दत। भरतः केकयीपुत्रो गमनत्वरया तदा॥
en: But owing to the hurry of his departure, Bharata the son of Kaikeyi did not appear to be so very much gratified with the gifts.

sa_verse: बभूव ह्यस्य हृदये चिन्ता सुमहती तदा। त्वरया चापि दूतानां स्वप्नस्यापि च दर्शनात् ॥
en: Owing to his having seen the dream and the post haste speed of the envoys, a mighty anxiety was present in his heart.

sa_verse: स स्ववेश्माभ्यतिक्रम्य नरनागाश्वसंकुलम्। प्रपेदे सुमहच्छीमान् राजमार्गमनुत्तमम्॥
en: Then issuing from his abode, that one possessed of exceeding grace passed the goodly thoroughfare thronged with men, horses, and elephants.

sa_verse: अभ्यतीत्य ततोऽपश्यदन्तःपुरमनुत्तमम्। ततस्तद् भरतः श्रीमानाविवेशानिवारितः॥
en: Having left it behind, Bharata saw (before him) the inner apartment (of the king); and thereupon the handsome Bharata entered it without let.

sa_verse: स मातामहमापृच्छ्य मातुलं च युधाजितम्। रथमारुह्य भरत: शत्रुघ्नसहितो ययौ॥
en: Then after speaking with his maternal grandfather and uncle, Yudhajit, Bharata ascending a car, set out with Satrughna.

sa_verse: रथान् मण्डलचक्रांश्च योजयित्वा परः शतम्। उष्ट्रगोऽश्वखरै त्या भरतं यान्तमन्वयुः॥
en: Thereat servants by means of an hundred cars, furnished with circular wheels, and yoked with camels, oxen, horses, and asses, followed Bharata.

sa_verse: बलेन गुप्तो भरतो महात्मा सहायकस्यात्मसमैरमात्यैः। गुहाद् ययौ सिद्ध इवेन्द्रलोकात्॥
en: Protected by the forces and the courtiers of his maternal grandfather dear to him like his ownself, the magnanimous Bharata who had his foes removed, taking with him his brother, Śatrughna, departed from the abode (of the king), like Siddha issuing from the regions of Indra.

sa_verse: स प्राङ्मुखो राजगृहादभिनिर्याय वीर्यवान्। ततः सुदामां द्युतिमान् संतीर्यावेक्ष्य तां नदीम्॥ ह्रादिनी दूरपारं च प्रत्यक्स्रोतस्तरङ्गिणीम्। शतद्रुमतरच्छीमान् नदीमिक्ष्वाकुनन्दनः॥
en: Issuing from the palace, the blazing Bharata endowed with prowess went in an easterly direction, and seeing before him the river called Sudāmā, crossed it. Then the auspicious descendant of Iksvāku crossed the broad Hrādinſ coursing westwards, as also the river Satadru.

sa_verse: ऐलधाने नदीं तीर्वा प्राप्य चापरपर्वतान्। शिलामाकुर्वती ती। आग्नेयं शल्यकर्षणम्॥
en: Then crossing a river at Eladhana,' and coming to Aparaparvata”, he crossed the Sila? and the Akurvatī, and arrived at Agneya* and Salyakarsanam. 1. Some read Eladhani. 2. Some read Apharaparyata. 3. The river, according to some is so named because it transforms the surrounding objects to stone. 4. Some make Agneya an epithet to Salyakarsana, meaning, relating to the south-east.

sa_verse: सत्यसंधः शुचिर्भूत्वा प्रेक्षमाणः शिलावहाम्। अभ्यगात् स महाशैलान् वनं चैत्ररथं प्रति ॥
en: And having purified himself and seen Silavaha,' that one of truthful purposes passed the Mahacailas,' and entered the forest of Caitraratha. 1. According to Tirtha, Silavaha is a strcam having Āgneya on one side and Salyakarşaņa on the other. 2. Lit, mighty mountains.

sa_verse: सरस्वतीं च गङ्गां च युग्मेन प्रतिपद्य च। उत्तरान् वीरमत्स्यानां भारुण्डं प्राविशद् वनम्॥
en: Then coming upon the confluence of the Ganga and the Sarasvati, Bharata entered the forest of Bhārunda lying to the north* of Vīramatsya. *Uttarān, lit. north. Some text read uttaram. It would mean countries lying to the north of Viramatsya.

sa_verse: शीतीकृत्वा तु गात्राणि क्लान्तानाश्वास्य वाजिनः। तत्र स्नात्वा च पीत्वा च प्रायादादाय चोदकम्॥ राजपुत्रो महारण्यमनभीक्ष्णोपसेवितम्। भद्रो भद्रेण यानेन मारुतः खमिवात्यगात्॥
en: Then cooling the limbs of the fatigued horses (with water) and refreshing them, Bharata himself bathed there and drank of the water; and then resumed the march, furnished with the water. Then the gentle prince by means of an excellent car, like to the wind-god himself, entered the mighty forest inhabited by various races of men.

sa_verse: भागीरथी दुष्प्रतरां सोंऽशुधाने महानदीम्। उपायात् राघवस्तूर्णं प्राग्वटे विश्रुते पुरे॥
en: Seeing that the mighty river Gangā was hard to cross at Ansudhāna, Bharata speedily went to the famous city of Prāgvața.

sa_verse: स गङ्गां प्राग्वटे ती, समायात् कुटिकोष्ठिकाम्। सबलस्तां स तीथि समगाद् धर्मवर्धनम्॥
en: Having crossed the Gangă at Prāgvața, he went over to the Kuţikoșthika. Having with his forces crossed that river, he proceeded to Dharmavardhana.

sa_verse: तोरणं दक्षिणार्धेन जम्बूप्रस्थं समागमत्। वरूथं च ययौ रम्यं ग्रामं दशरथात्मजः॥
en: Then proceeding by the south of Toraņa, he came to Jambuprastha. Then Dasaratha's son went to the beautiful village of Varūtha.

sa_verse: तत्र रम्ये वने वासं कृत्वासौ प्राङ्मुखो ययौ। उद्यानमुज्जिहानायाः प्रियका यत्र पादपाः॥
en: Having for a while stayed at that romantic wood, he proceeded eastwards, and presented himself at the villa of Ujjihānāya, where abound trees (called) Priyaka.

sa_verse: स तांस्तु प्रियकान् प्राप्य शीघ्रानास्थाय वाजिनः। अनुज्ञाप्याथ भरतो वाहिनीं त्वरितो ययौ॥
en: Coming to the Priyakas, Bharata speedily yoking the horses, set out without delay, issuing his orders to the forces.

sa_verse: वासं कृत्वा सर्वतीर्थे ती चोत्तानिकां नदीम्। अन्या नदीश्च विविधैः पार्वतीयैस्तुरङ्गमैः॥ हस्तिपृष्ठकमासाद्य कुटिकामप्यवर्तत। ततार च नरव्याघ्रो लोहित्ये च कपीवतीम्॥ एकसाले स्थाणुमती विनते गोमती नदीम्। कलिङ्गनगरे चापि प्राप्य सालवनं तदा ॥ भरतः क्षिप्रमागच्छत् सुपरिश्रान्तवाहनः। वनं च समतीत्याशु शर्वर्यामरुणोदये॥ अयोध्यां मनुना राज्ञा निर्मितां स ददर्श ह। तां पुरी पुरुषव्याघ्रः सप्तरात्रोषितः पथि॥
en: Then sojourning at Sarvatirtha and crossing the river there flowing north-wards as well as others abounding in various kinds of mountainous horses, Bharata arrived Hastiprşthaka. And at Lohitya that foremost of men crossed the Kapivati, at Ekasāla, the Sthanumati, and at Vinaya the Gomati. And Bharata arriving at a forest of Sala trees in the city of Kalinga, speedily passed it with his forces way-worn. And having passed the forest swiftly over night, he at sunrise saw Ayodhyā built by king Manu. Having spent seven nights on the journey, that chief of men beheld Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: अयोध्यामग्रतो दृष्ट्वा सारथिं चेदमब्रवीत्। एषा नातिप्रतीता मे पुण्योद्याना यशस्विनी॥ अयोध्या दृश्यते दूरात् सारथे पाण्डुमृत्तिका। यज्विभिर्गुणसम्पन्नैर्ब्राह्मणैर्वेदपारगैः॥ भूयिष्ठमृद्धैराकीर्णा राजर्षिवरपालिता। अयोध्यायां पुरा शब्दः श्रूयते तुमुलो महान्॥ समन्तानरनारीणां तमद्य न शृणोम्यहम्।
en: Before him and thus addressed the charioteer, O charioteer, from the distance Ayodhyā at seemeth like a mass of black earth, albeit she boasts of sacrificial priests crowned with every virtue, Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vedas and opulent people, and albeit she is maintained by Rājarșis. Formerly one could hear a great and mighty tumult all round Ayodhyā proceeding from men and women; but I do not hear this today.

sa_verse: उद्यानानि हि सायाह्ने क्रीडित्वोपरतैनरैः॥ समन्ताद् विप्रधावद्भिः प्रकाशन्ते ममान्यथा। तान्यद्यानुरुदन्तीव परित्यक्तानि कामिभिः॥
en: The gardens where from persons having sported in the evening used to rush out (at day break) wear a different aspect now. Forsaken by the pleasure-seekers, the garden appear to weep.

sa_verse: अरण्यभूतेव पुरी सारथे प्रतिभाति माम्। सह्यत्र यानैर्दृश्यन्ते न गर्न च वाजिभिः। निर्यान्तो वाभियान्तो वा नरमुख्या यथा पुरा॥
en: O charioteer, the city appears to me like a wilderness. And I do not as formerly behold the flower of the city leaving or entering her on cars or elephants or horses.

sa_verse: उद्यानानि पुरा भान्ति मत्तप्रमुदितानि च। जनानां रतिसंयोगेष्वत्यन्तगुणवन्ति च॥ तान्येतान्यद्य पश्यामि निरानन्दानि सर्वशः। स्रस्तपर्णैरनुपथं विक्रोशद्भिरिव दुमैः॥
en: Gardens which, frequented by people inebriated with the honey of love, met together for purposes of sport, looked charmingly blooming, I find as utterly void of cheerfulness, with the trees as if lamenting with tremulous leaves.

sa_verse: नाद्यापि श्रूयते शब्दो मत्तानां मृगपक्षिणाम्। सरक्तां मधुरां वाणी कलं व्याहरतां बहु॥
en: Still do I not hear the inarticulate though sweet and delicious voice of beasts and birds crying in chorus.* *Some text read muhus, momentarily.

sa_verse: चन्दनागुरुसम्पृक्तो धूपसम्मूर्छितोऽमलः। प्रवाति पवनः श्रीमान् किं नु नाद्य यथा पुरा ॥
en: Why, as before, does not the blameless* and bland wind blow mixed with the perfume of sandal and faint with Dhūpa? *Some texts read atula in place of amala, incomparable.

sa_verse: भेरीमृदङ्गवीणानां कोणसंघट्टितः पुनः। किमद्य शब्दो विरतः सदादीनगतिः पुरा॥
en: And why, again, have the sounds of Vīņās and Mļdangas developed by beating sticks, which continually flew in a never-ceasing vigorous courses, ceased to-day?

sa_verse: अनिष्टानि च पापानि पश्यामि विविधानि च। निमित्तान्यमनोज्ञानि तेन सीदति मे मनः॥
en: I witness various evil, unsightly, and unpleasant* omens, and hence my mind is depressed. *The commentator renders amongnani, inauspicious. I venture to differ from him.,

sa_verse: सर्वथा कुशलं सूत दुर्लभं मम बन्धुषु। तथा ह्यसति सम्मोहे हृदयं सीदतीव मे॥
en: O charioteer, complete good fortune with my friends is hardly to be realised. But although no apparent cause exists for my depression of spirits, yet my heart droopeth.

sa_verse: विषण्णः श्रान्तहृदयस्त्रस्तः संलुलितेन्द्रियः। भरतः प्रविवेशाशु पुरीमिक्ष्वाकुपालिताम्॥
en: Then Bharata depressed and cast down and with his senses afflicted, speedily entered the city ruled by Ikşvāku.

sa_verse: द्वारेण वैजयन्तेन प्राविशच्छ्रान्तवाहनः। द्वा:स्थैरुत्थाय विजयं पृष्ठस्तैः सहितो ययौ॥
en: And Bharata having his vehicles fatigued, entered by the Vaijayanta* gate, and went on, followed by the gate-keepers, who enquired after his welfare. *Vijayantena. Another meaning is, By the gate resembling that of Indra's palace.

sa_verse: स त्वनेकाग्रहृदयो द्वा:स्थं प्रत्यर्च्य तं जनम्। सूतमश्वपतेः क्लान्तमब्रवीत् तत्र राघवः॥ किमहं त्वरयानीत: कारणेन विनानघ। अशुभाशङ्कि हृदयं शीलं च पततीव मे॥
en: Then courteously telling the warders to desist, he with an agitated heart spoke to the tired charioteer of Aśvapati, “O sinless one, why have I been brought in this haste without any (adequate) reason?" My heart apprehended some evil; and my disposition gives way.

sa_verse: श्रुता नु यादृशाः पूर्वं नृपतीनां विनाशने। आकारांस्तानहं सर्वानिह पश्यामि सारथे॥
en: O charioteer, I see around me all those signs which I had heard told as occurring on the occasion of he death of monarchs.

sa_verse: सम्मार्जनविहीनानि परुषाण्युपलक्षये। असंयतकवाटानि श्रीविहीनानि सर्वशः॥ बलिकर्मविहीनानि धूपसम्मोदनेन च। अनाशितकुटुम्बानि प्रभाहीनजनानि च॥ अलक्ष्मीकानि पश्यामि कुटिम्बिभवनान्यहम्। अपेतमाल्यशोभानि असम्मृष्टाजिराणि च॥ देवागाराणि शून्यानि न भान्तीह यथा पुरा।
en: I behold the houses of the citizens unswept and unsightly with the doors flung open, and destitute of grace, without sacrifices and other religious ceremonies, without the incense of Dhūpa, the citizens fasting, and the people appearing with faded countenance, displaying no flags, etc., The abodes of the deities are not decorated with garlands, and their courts are unclean; and remaining vacant, they look. no longer beautiful as formerly.

sa_verse: देवताः प्रविद्धाश्च यज्ञगोष्ठास्तथैव च॥ माल्यापणेषु राजन्ते नाद्य पण्यानि वा तथा। दृश्यन्ते वणिजोऽप्यद्य न यथापूर्वमत्र वै॥ ध्यानसंविग्नहदया नष्टव्यापारयन्त्रिताः।
en: The images are not worshipped; and the places of sacrifice are in a like condition. And garlands are not displayed in the shops where they are sold. And traders looking anxious, with their business stopped, do not look as before.

sa_verse: देवायतनचैत्येषु दीनाः पक्षिगास्तथा॥ मलिनं चाश्रुपूर्णाक्षं दीनं ध्यानपरं कृशम्। सस्त्र.पुरां च पश्याम जनमुत्कण्ठितं पुरे॥
en: And in temples and Caityas birds and beasts appear dispirited. And in the city I behold men and women pale and woe-begone and emaciated and anxious, with tears filling their eyes.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा भरतः सूतं तं दीनमानसः। तान्यनिष्टान्ययोध्यायां प्रेक्ष्य राजगृहं ययौ॥
en: Having said this to the charioteer, Bharata, beholding these inauspicious sights, entered the palace of the king with a depressed heart.

sa_verse: तां शून्यशृङ्गाटकवेश्मरथ्यां रजोरुणद्वारकवाटयन्त्राम्। दृष्ट्वा पुरीमिन्द्रपुरीप्रकाशां दुःखेन सम्पूर्णतरो बभूव॥
en: Beholding the city resembling the city of Indra, with her crossings and houses and roads void of people, and the doors and hinges covered with dust, Bharata was filled with greater grief.

sa_verse: बभूव पश्यन्मनसोऽप्रियाणि यान्यन्यदा नास्य पुरे बभूवुः। अवाक्शिरा दीनमना न हृष्टः पितुर्महात्मा प्रविवेश वेश्म ॥
en: And witnessing many unpleasant things which he had never seen during the life of the monarch, that high-souled one entered the mansion of his father, bending his head, depressed in spirits, and with his mind extremely aggrieved.

sa_verse: अपश्यंस्तु ततस्तत्र पितरं पितुरालये। जगाम भरतो द्रष्टुं मातरं मातुरालये॥
en: Not having seen his father in his father's quarters, Bharata went to his mother's apartment for seeing her. *The text is evidently corrupt. From the sequel it appears that Bharata has not seen the king at that time. That commentator, however, passes over the point in silence, which is to be accounted for by the slovenliness of the way of though characterising the Sanskrit commentators in general. An English commentator would have considered the matter as of very great importance, but his Hindu brother takes it quietly, and passes on with indifference.

sa_verse: अनुप्राप्तं तु तं दृष्ट्वा कैकेयी प्रोषितं सुतम्। उत्पपात तदा हृष्टा त्यक्त्वा सौवर्णमासनम्॥
en: On seeing her son before her after his sojourn from home, Kaikeyi delighted, rose up from her golden seat.

sa_verse: स प्रविश्यैव धर्मात्मा स्वगृहं श्रीविवर्जितम्। भरतः प्रेक्ष्य जग्राह जनन्याश्चरणौ शुभौ॥
en: On entering his own quarter which he found deprived of grace, the virtuous Bharata took hold of his mother's auspicious feet.

sa_verse: तं मूर्ध्नि समुपाघ्राय परिष्वज्य यशस्विनम्। अङ्के भरतमारोप्य प्रष्टुं समुपक्रमे॥
en: Then smelling the crown of his head and embracing him and taking that illustrious one on her lap, Kaikeyi addressed him, saying.

sa_verse: अद्य ते कतिचिद् रात्र्यश्च्युतस्यार्यकवेश्मनः। अपि नाध्वश्रमः शीघ्रं रथेनापततस्तव॥
en: How many days hence did you leave the residence of the revered one? Have you felt any fatigue on the way incident to the car proceeding swiftly?

sa_verse: आर्यकस्ते सुकुशली युधाजिन्मातुलस्तव। प्रवासाच्च सुखं पुत्र सर्वं मे वक्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: And is the revered one will, and your maternal uncle, Yuddhajit? And, my son, have you passed your time pleasantly during your sojourn? It behoves you to tell me all this.

sa_verse: एवं पृष्ठस्तु कैकेय्या प्रियं पार्थिवनन्दनः। आचष्ट भरतः सर्व मात्र राजीवलोचनः।७।।
en: Thus asked that son of the king, Bharata, furnished with eyes resembling lotuses, told his mother that all was well.

sa_verse: अद्य मे सप्तमी रात्रिश्च्युतस्याकवेश्मनः। अम्बायाः कुशली तातो युधाजिन्मातुलश्च मे॥
en: Seven nights hence I took leave of that revered one's residence. My mother's sire is well, as also my maternal uncle, Yuddhajit.

sa_verse: यन्मे धनं च रत्नं च ददौ राजा परंतपः। परिश्रान्तं पथ्यभवत् ततोऽहं पूर्वमागतः॥ राजवाक्यहरैर्दूतैस्त्वर्यमाणोऽहमागतः। यदहं प्रष्टुमिच्छामि तदम्बा वक्तुमर्हति ॥
en: My vehicles had got tired in consequence of bearing the wealth and jewels which that subduer of foes, the king, had bestowed on me. And it is for this reason that I have come in advance of them. Urged by the royal emissaries, I have come here so swiftly. But it behoves my mother to tell me what I wish to ask.

sa_verse: शून्योऽयं शयनीयस्ते पर्यङ्को हेमभूषितः। न चायमिक्ष्वाकुजनः प्रहृष्टः प्रतिभाति मे॥
en: This sleeping bedstead of yours adorned with gold is empty. I do not see the race of Ikşvāku in their usual good spirits.

sa_verse: राजा भवति भूयिष्ठमिहाम्बाया निवेशने। तमहं नाद्य पश्यामि द्रष्टुमिच्छनिहागतः॥
en: The king generally remains in this apartment of my mother. But coming here with the desire of seeing him, I do not today find him at this place.

sa_verse: पितुर्ग्रहीष्ये पादौ च तं ममाख्याहि पृच्छतः। आहोस्विदम्बाज्येष्ठायाः कौसल्याया निवेशने॥
en: I would take the feet of my father. Do you tell me who ask you. Is he in the apartment of my eldest mother, Kausalya?

sa_verse: तं प्रत्युवाच कैकेयी प्रियवद् घोरमप्रियम्। अजानन्तं प्रजानन्ती राज्यलोभेन मोहिता॥
en: Blinded by the lust of dominion, and looking upon that as desirable (to Bharata) which was exceedingly disagreeable (to him), Kaikeyi replied to him, who did not know what had transpired.

sa_verse: या गतिः सर्वभूतानां तां गतिं ते पिता गतः। राजा महात्मा तेजस्वी यायजूकः सतां गतिः॥
en: That high-souled and energetic one ever engaged in sacrifice, the refuge of the good, your father, the king, has come by the state which pertains to all creatures.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा भरतो वाक्यं धर्माभिजनवाञ्छुचिः। पपात सहसा भूमौ पितृशोकबलार्दितः॥ हा हतोऽस्मीति कृपणां दीनां वाचमुदीरयन्। निपपात महाबाहुर्बाहू विक्षिप्य वीर्यवान्॥
en: Hearing these words, Bharata of pure ways sprung from a righteous race, smite with the vehemence of sorrow on account of his father, suddenly fell down to the earth. And exclaiming in the anguish of spirit and in the excess of grief, the words, "Ah me! I am undone!" that mightyarmed one endowed with prowess, fell down, tossing about his arms.

sa_verse: ततः शोकेन संवीतः पितुर्मरणदुःखितः। विललाप महातेजा भ्रान्ताकुलितचेतनः॥
en: Then, overwhelmed with and distressed at the death of his father, that highly sorrow energetic one, with his senses distracted, indulged in lamentations.

sa_verse: एतत् सुरुचिरं भाति पितुर्मे शयनं पुरा। शशिनेवामलं रात्रौ गगनं तोयदात्यये॥ तदिदं न विभात्यद्य विहीनं तेन धीमता। व्योमेव शशिना हीनमप्शुष्क इव सागरः॥
en: This bed of my father used to look like the speckless welkin at night crowned with the moon, after the clouds have gone off. But to-day, deprived of that intellignet one, it ceases to shine, like the firmament without the moon or the sea devoid of its waters.

sa_verse: बाष्पमुत्सृज्य कण्ठेन स्वात्मना परिपीडितः। प्रच्छाद्य वदनं श्रीमद् वस्त्रेण जयतां वरः॥
en: Exclaiming with tears trickling down, that foremost of victorious ones, extremely afflicted at heart, wept, muffling his graceful countenance.

sa_verse: तमार्तं देवसंकाशं समीक्ष्य पतितं भुवि। निकृत्तमिव सालस्य स्कन्धं परशुना वने॥ माता मातङ्गसंकाशं चन्दार्कसदृशं सुतम्। उत्थापयित्वा शोकार्तं वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Seeing that one resembling a celestial fallen to the earth, stricken with sorrow, like to a bough of the Sāla that had been severed in the wood by an axe, his mother raising up he distressed son like a mad elephant or the sun or the moon, addressed him.

sa_verse: उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ किं शेषे राजन्नत्र महायशः। त्वद्विधा नहि शोचन्ति सन्तः सदसि सम्मताः॥
en: Arise, arise. Why do you lie down, O illustrious son of the king? Persons like you having their senses under perfect control, and approved by men of culture, do not grieve.

sa_verse: दानयज्ञाधिकारा हि शीलश्रुतितपोनुगा। बुद्धिस्ते बुद्धिसम्पन्न प्रभेवार्कस्य मन्दिरे॥
en: O you endo!. ed with understanding, like the halo of the Sun in the solar disc, your sense, entitled to dispensing gifts and celebrating sacrifices, ever follows morals your Śruti and asceticism.

sa_verse: स रुदित्वा चिरं कालं भूमौ परिविवृत्य च। जननी प्रत्युवाचेदं शोकैर्बहुभिरावृतः॥
en: Having wept for a long while with his body rolling on the earth, Bharata afflicted with manifold grief, answered his mother, saying.

sa_verse: अभिषेक्ष्यति रामं तु राजा यज्ञं नु यक्ष्यते। इत्यहं कृतसंकल्पो हृष्टो यात्रामयासिषम्॥
en: The king will install Rāma and celebrate a sacrifice' concluding this for certain, I had joyfully gone from hence.

sa_verse: तदिदं ह्यन्यथाभूतं व्यवदीर्णं मनो मम। पितरं यो न पश्यामि नित्यं प्रियहिते रतम्॥
en: But it has fallen out otherwise. That I do not behold my father ever engaged in the dear welfare (of his subjects) cleaves my heart mother.

sa_verse: अम्ब केनात्यगाद् राजा व्याधिना मय्यनागते। धन्या रामादयः सर्वे यैः पिता संस्कृतः स्वयम्॥
en: Of what ailment has the king breathed his last during my absence? Blessed are Rāma and others who have personally performed my sire's last rites.

sa_verse: न नूनं मां महाराजः प्राप्तं जानाति कीर्तिमान्। उपजिभ्रेत् तु मां मूर्ध्नि तात: संनाम्य सत्वरम्॥
en: Surely the renowned monarch does not know that I have come. (If he had done so), pressing down my head, my father would at once have smelt it.

sa_verse: क्व स पाणिः सुखस्पर्शस्तातस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः। यो हि मां रजसा ध्वस्तमभीक्ष्णं परिमार्जति॥
en: Where is now the soft hand of that energetic one which used to rub my person when it was covered with dust?

sa_verse: यो मे भ्राता पिता बन्धुर्यस्य दासोऽस्मि सम्मतः। तस्य मां शीघ्रमाख्याहि रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः॥
en: Do you now without delay convey the news of my arrival to the vigorous Rāma, who is at once my father, brother and friend, and whose beloved servant I am.

sa_verse: पिता हि भवति ज्येष्ठो धर्ममार्यस्य जानतः। तस्य पादौ ग्रहीष्यामि स हीदानी गतिर्मम ॥
en: The eldest brother of one that is noble and cognisant of morality, becomes his father. I shall take hold of his feet: he is now my refuge.

sa_verse: धर्मविद् धर्मशीलश्च महाभागो दृढव्रतः। आर्ये किमब्रवीद् राजा पिता मे सत्यविक्रमः॥ पश्चिमं साधुसंदेशमिच्छामि श्रोतुमात्मनः।
en: And, O noble lady, what did that virtuous one cognizant of virtue, that pre-eminently pious one, firm in his vow, and having truth for prowess, even my father, say? I wish to hear news concerning us relative to the last moments of the monarch.

sa_verse: सिद्धार्थास्तु नरा राममागतं सह सीतया। लक्ष्मणं च महाबाहुं द्रक्ष्यन्ति पुनरागतम्॥
en: Your father like a mighty elephant fast bound with a cord, said to me these words during his last moments, "Blessed are they that shall see Rāma and the mighty-armed Lakşmaņa returned along with Sītā.”

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा विषसादैव द्वितीयाप्रियशंसनात्। विषण्णवदनो भूत्वा भूयः पप्रच्छ मातरम्॥
en: Hearing this, Bharata apprehending a second misfortune was deeply moved; and with a sad countenance, he again asked his mother.

sa_verse: क्व चेदानीं च धर्मात्मा कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्राता सीतया च समागतः॥
en: Where has gone that righteous-souled one, the enchancer of Kausalya's joy, along with Laks maņa and Sītā?

sa_verse: तथा पृष्टा यथान्यायमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे। मातास्य युगपद्वाक्यं विप्रियं प्रियशंसया॥
en: Thus questioned, his mother at the same time duly said in words which although highly unwelcome, she took as agreeable to Bharata.

sa_verse: स हि राजसुतः पुत्र चीरवासा महावनम्। दण्डकान् सह वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणानुचरो गतः॥
en: O son, that son of the king wearing bark has repaired to the forest of Dandaka, along with Vaidehi and followed by Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा भरतस्त्रस्तो भ्रातुश्चारित्रशङ्कया। स्वस्य वंशस्य माहात्म्यात् प्रष्टुं समुपचक्रमे॥
en: Hearing this, Bharata apprehending some moral lapse on the part of his brother from the dignity of his race, asked in agitation.

sa_verse: कच्चिन्न ब्राह्मणधनं हृतं रामेण कस्यचित्। कच्चिन्नाढ्यो दरिद्रो वा तेनापापो विहिंसितः॥
en: Has Rāma deprived any Brāhmaṇa of his wealth? Or has he wronged any innocent person, whether rich or poor?

sa_verse: कच्चिन्न परदारान् वा राजपुत्रोऽभिमन्यते। कस्मात् स दण्डकारण्ये भ्राता रामो विवासितः॥
en: Has the fancy of the prince gone after the wife of another? For what reason has brother Rāma been banished?

sa_verse: एवमुक्ता तु कैकेयी भरतेन महात्मना। उवाच वचनं हृष्टा वृथापण्डितमानिनी॥
en: Thus asked by the magnanimous Bharata, Kaikeyi vainly pluming herself on her wisdom, joyfully said.

sa_verse: न ब्राह्मणधनं किंचिद्धृतं रामेण कस्यचित्। कश्चिन्नाढ्यो दरिद्रो वा तेनापापो विहिंसितः। न रामः परदारान् स चक्षुामपि पश्यति॥
en: Rāma has deprived no Brāhmaṇa of his property, nor has any innocent person rich or otherwise been wronged by him, nor does he ever with his eyes look at the wife of another.

sa_verse: मया तु पुत्र श्रुत्वैव रामस्येहाभिषेचनम्। याचितस्ते पिता राज्यं रामस्य च विवासनम्॥
en: O son, as soon as I heard of Råma's (coming) installation, I asked for the kingdom to be conferred on you, and Rāma to be banished.

sa_verse: स स्ववृत्तिं समास्थाय पिता ते तत् तथाकरोत्। रामस्तु सहसौमित्रिः प्रेषितः सह सीतया॥ तमपश्यन् प्रियं पुत्रं महीपालो महायशाः। पुत्रशोकपरिवूनः पञ्चत्वमुपपेदिवान्॥
en: Thereupon, he, staying by his promise, did accordingly: Rāma has been banished along with Sumitrā's son and Sītā. Not seeing his beloved son, the illustrious lord of earth, stricken by grief on his account, has breathed his last.

sa_verse: त्वया त्विदानी धर्मज्ञ राजत्वमवलम्ब्यताम्। त्वत्कृते हि मया सर्वमिदमेवंविधं कृतम्॥
en: Do you now, O you cognizant of duty, take charge of the kingdom. I have done all this in your interests.

sa_verse: मा शोकं मा च संतापं धैर्यमाश्रय पुत्रक। त्वदधीना हि नगरी राज्यं चैतदनामयम्॥
en: Do not give way to sorrow. O son, assume patience. This city is subject to you, as well as this peaceful kingdom.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठमुख्यैः सहितो द्विजेन्द्रैः। मात्मानमुक्मभिषेचयस्व॥
en: Having with the help of the principal Brāhmaṇas headed by Vasiştha, duly performed the funeral obsequies of the king, do you, without suffering your energy to depart, install yourself in the kingdom.

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा च स पितुर्वत्तं भ्रातरौ च विवासितौ। भरतो दुःखसंतप्त इदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Hearing of his father's death and the exile of his brothers, Bharata burning in grief, said these words.

sa_verse: किं नु कार्यं हतस्येह मम राज्येन शोचतः। विहीनस्याथ पित्रा च भ्रात्रा पितृसमेन च॥
en: Deprived of my father as well as my brother like to a father, what shall I bewailing them and undone by you do with the kingdom?

sa_verse: दुःखे मे दुःखमकरोत्रणे क्षारमिवाददाः। राजानं प्रेतभावस्थं कृत्वा रामं च तापसम्॥
en: You, like one throwing alkali on a sore, have, by bringing about the death of the king and making Rāma an ascetic, heaped grief on grief.

sa_verse: कुलस्य त्वमभावाय कालरात्रिरिवागता। अङ्गारमुपगूह्य स्म पिता मे नावबुद्धवान् ॥
en: You have come like the fatal night for the destruction of this race. Not under-standing it, my father embraced live coal.

sa_verse: मृत्युमापादितो राजा त्वया मे पापदर्शिनि। सुखं परिहतं मोहाद् कुलेऽस्मिन् कुलपांसनि ॥
en: O you that have your gaze fixed on sin, it is through you that my sovereign has come by death; and that, O stainer of the line, this race has by your infatuation been deprived of its happiness.

sa_verse: त्वां प्राप्य हि पिता मेऽद्य सत्यसंधो महायशाः। तीव्रदुःखाभिसंतप्तो वृत्तो दशरथो नृपः॥
en: Having got you, my illustrious sire intent upon truth, king Dasaratha, afflicted by extreme sorrow, has departed this life.

sa_verse: विनाशितो महाराजः पिता मे धर्मवत्सलः। कस्मात् प्रवाजितो रामः कस्मादेव वनं गतः॥
en: Why has the king, my father, ever loving virtue, been deprived of life? Why has Rāma been banished; and wherefore has he gone to the woods?

sa_verse: कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च पुत्रशोकाभिपीडिते। . दुष्करं यदि जीवेतां प्राप्य त्वां जननी मम॥
en: Afflicted with grief for their son, even if Kausalya and Sumitrā live, it will be hard for them to do so in the presence of you, my mother.

sa_verse: नन्वार्योऽपि च धर्मात्मात्वयि वृत्तिमनुत्तमाम्। वर्तते गुरुवृत्तिज्ञो यथा मातरि वर्तते॥
en: Surely the noble and virtuous Råma, well knowing his duties towards his superiors, regards you as highly as he does his own mother.

sa_verse: तथा ज्येष्ठा हि मे माता कौसल्या दीर्घदर्शिनी। त्वयि धर्म समास्थाय भगिन्यामिव वर्तते॥
en: In the same way, my eldest mother, the farsighted Kausalyā, doing her duties by you, bears herself like a sister.

sa_verse: तस्याः पुत्रं महात्मानं चीरवल्कलवाससम्। प्रस्थाप्य वनवासाय कथं पापे न शोचसे॥
en: Why, O sinful one, having sent to the woods her magnanimous son, clad in bark, do you not grieve?

sa_verse: अपापदर्शिनं शूरं कृतात्मानं यशस्विनम्। प्रव्राज्य चीरवसनं किं नु पश्यसि कारणम्॥
en: Having exiled the famous and heroic Rāma seeing no sin, clad in bark, what benefit do you perceive as yours?

sa_verse: लुब्धाया विदिती मन्ये न तेऽहं राघवं यथा। तथा ह्यनर्थो राज्यार्थं त्वयानीतो महानयम्॥
en: I deem that you did not know how very highly I regarded Rāghava; and therefore it is that you have brought this mighty calamity.

sa_verse: अहं हि पुरुषव्याघ्रावपश्यन् रामलक्ष्मणौ। केन शक्तिप्रभावेण राज्यं रक्षितुमुत्सहे॥
en: Not seeing those chiefs of men, Rāma and Lakşmaņa, by virtue of whose energy can I venture to rule the kingdom?

sa_verse: तं हि नित्यं महाराजो बलवन्तं महौजसम्। उपाश्रितोऽभूद् धर्मात्मा मेरुमैरुवनं यथा ॥
en: The monarch was ever protected by that powerful one possessed of mighty energy, as Meru is protected by its forest.

sa_verse: सोऽहं कथमिमं भारं महाधुर्यसमुद्यतम्। दम्यो धुरमिवासाद्य सहेयं केन चौजसा ॥
en: Like a calf burdened with a load capable of being borne by a mighty ox, by what energy shall I bear this burden which was borne by an exceedingly strong person?

sa_verse: अथवा मे भवेच्छक्तिर्योगैर्बुद्धिबलेन वा। सकामां न करिष्यामि त्वामहं पुत्रगर्धिनीम्॥
en: And even if this strength be mine through yoga or vigour of intellect, I will not crown with success the hopes of you, proud of your son.

sa_verse: न मे विकाङ्क्षा जायेत त्यक्तुं त्वां पापनिश्चयाम्। यदि रामस्य नावेक्षा त्वयि स्यान्मातृवत् सदा॥
en: Nor, had Rāma not always regarded you as his mother, would I hesitate to renounce you, whose heart has been set on sin.

sa_verse: उत्पन्ना तु कथं बुद्धिस्तवेयं पापदर्शिनी। साधुचारित्रविभ्रष्टे पूर्वेषां नो विगर्हिता॥
en: O you that views unrighteousness, O you that have fallen off from the way of the good, how could such thoughts unworthy of our line, arise in your mind?

sa_verse: अस्मिन् कुले हि सर्वेषां ज्येष्ठो राज्येऽभिषिच्यते। अपरे भ्रातरस्तस्मिन् प्रवर्तन्ते समाहिताः॥
en: In this race, the eldest brother of al} is installed in the kingdom: the other brothers remain under him.

sa_verse: न हि मन्ये नृशंसे त्वं राजधर्ममवेक्षसे। गतिं वा न विजानासि राजवृत्तस्य शाश्वतीम्॥
en: O fell one, you did not, I think, know the morality of kings, or the consequence that attends its faithful observance.

sa_verse: सततं राजपुत्रेषु ज्येष्ठो राजाभिषिच्यते। राज्ञामेतत् समं तत् स्यादिक्ष्वाकूणां विशेषतः॥
en: Of all the princes, the eldest is always installed as the king. Even this is the accepted principle of all sovereigns, specially the descendants of Ikşvāku.

sa_verse: तेषां धर्मैकरक्षाणां कुलचारित्रशोभिनाम्। अद्य चारित्रशौटीर्यं त्वां प्राप्य विनिवर्तितम्॥
en: But to-day the pride of character of those whose virtue was their sole concern,* and who shone in the character of their line, has been humbled through they instrumentality. *Another interpretation is;, Whose virtue is their sole protector.

sa_verse: तवापि सुमहाभागे जनेन्द्रकुलपूर्वके। बुद्धिमोहः कथमयं सम्भूतस्त्वयि गर्हितः॥
en: And how, O highly exalted lady, O you that born in a royal race, could such reprehensible fatuity take possession of your senses?

sa_verse: न तु कामं करिष्यामि तवाहं पापनिश्चये। यया व्यसनमारब्धं जीवितान्तकरं मम॥
en: But, O you bent upon sin, I will by no means fulfil the wish of you by whom has been brought in the first instance this calamity calculated to end my existence.

sa_verse: एष त्विदानीमेवाहमप्रियार्थं तवानघे। निवर्तयिष्यामि वनाद् भ्रातरं स्वजनप्रियम्॥
en: Further, O sinless one, I will do this at present was to displease you: I will bring from the forest my brother dear to his relatives.

sa_verse: निवर्तयित्वा रामं च तस्याहं दीप्ततेजसः। दासभूतो भविष्यामि सुस्थितेनान्तरात्मना॥
en: And having brought back Rāma, I will in a perfectly contented spirit, became the slave of that one of flaming energy.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा भरतो महात्मा प्रियेतरैर्वाक्यगणैस्तुदंस्ताम्। शोकार्दितश्चापि ननाद भूयः सिंहो यथा मन्दरकन्दरस्थः॥
en: The high-souled Bharata afflicted (his mother) with multitudes of words causing pain; and distressed with grief, emitted sounds like to a lion in the cave of Mandāra.

sa_verse: तां तथा गर्हयित्वा तु मातरं भरतस्तदा। रोषेण महताविष्टः पुनरेवाब्रवीद् वचः॥
en: Having thus reproved his mother in great wrath, Bharata said again.

sa_verse: राज्याद् भ्रंशस्व कैकेयि नृशंसे दुष्टचारिणि। परित्यक्तासि धर्मेण मा मृतं रुदती भव॥
en: Do you bear yourself from this kingdom, O heartless and wicked Kaikeyī. Having been lost to righteousness, do not you lament me dead.

sa_verse: किं नु तेऽदूषयद् रामो राजा वा भृशधार्मिकः। ययोर्मृत्युर्विवासश्च त्वत्कृते तुल्यमागतौ॥
en: What qualities of yours were taxed by the king or the exceedingly virtuous Rāma, that both of them simultaneously have come by death and exile respectively through your agency?

sa_verse: भ्रूणहत्यामसि प्राप्ता कुलस्यास्य विनाशनात्। कैकेयी नरकं गच्छ मा च तातसलोकताम्॥
en: O Kaikeyī, you have been guilty of homicide in consequence of the destruction of this race. Do you go to hell, never to the world attained by my father.

sa_verse: यत्त्वया हीदृशं पापं कृतं घोरेण कर्मणा। सर्वलोकप्रियं हित्वा ममाप्यापादितं भयम्॥
en: Since, renouncing Rāma dear to all, you have committed this sin by your grim act and brought me into fear.

sa_verse: त्वत्कृते मे पिता वृत्तो रामश्चारण्यमाश्रितः। अयशो जीवलोके च त्वयाहं प्रतिपादितः॥
en: Through you has my father met with his end, and Rāma has taken refuge in the woods; and it is through you that I have come to ignominy among all creatures.

sa_verse: मातृरूपे ममामित्रे नृशंसे राज्यकामुके। न तेऽहमभिभाष्योऽस्मि दुर्वृत्ते पतिघातिनि।७।।
en: O you enemy of mine in the guise of a mother! O fell one! O you that covetest the kingdom! do you not speak to me, O wicked wretch!

sa_verse: कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च याश्चान्या मम मातरः। दुःखेन महताविष्टास्त्वां प्राप्य कुलदूषिणीम्॥
