en: O slayer of your husband! it is because of you, stainer of your race, that Kausalyā, Sumiträ, and other mothers of mine have been overwhelmed with a mighty sorrow.

sa_verse: न त्वमश्वपतेः कन्या धर्मराजस्य धीमतः। राक्षसी तत्र जातासि कुलप्रध्वंसिनी पितुः॥ यत् त्वया धार्मिको रामो नित्यं सत्यपरायणः। वनं प्रस्थापितो वीरः पितापि त्रिदिवं गतः॥ यत् प्रधानासि तत् पापं मयि पित्रा विना कृते। भ्रातृभ्यां च परित्यक्ते सर्वलोकस्य चाप्रिये॥
en: You are never the daughter of the pious and intelligent Aśvapati, you are a Rākṣasī born in that race, you that have destroyed my father's race, inasmuch as the virtuous and heroic Rāma ever observing truth has been sent to the woods and my father has ascended heaven through your agency; inasmuch as you that have perpetrated this arch offence, have laid this sin on me, who have been forsaken by my sire, renounced by my brothers, and come by universal disfavour.

sa_verse: कौसल्यां धर्मसंयुक्तां वियुक्तां पापनिश्चये। कृत्वा कं प्राप्स्यसे ह्यद्य लोकं निरयगामिनि॥
en: O you of sinful ways, having separated the virtuous Kausalya (from her son), what world, O you that repairest to hell, shall be your portion?

sa_verse: किं नावबुध्यसे क्रूरे नियतं बन्धुसंश्रयम्। ज्येष्ठं पितृसमं रामं कौसल्यायात्मसम्भवम्॥
en: Does you not, O tortuous one, know that that one sprung from Kucalya's, self, my eldest brother Räma, who is like a father to me, has ever been the refuge of his friends?

sa_verse: अङ्गप्रत्यङ्गजः पुत्रो हृदयाच्चाभिजायते। तस्मात् प्रियतरो मातुः प्रिया एव तु बान्धवाः॥
en: A son born from all the limbs of his mother, comes out from her heart, and therefore it is that he is far dearer to his mother, albeit her friends are dear to her.* *Angapratyangaja: at., born from the limbs principal and minor. The commentator recounts the face, the breast, the abdomen, the hands and the feet as constituting the former; and eyes, fingers, etc., as the latter.

sa_verse: तावर्धदिवसं श्रान्तौ दृष्ट्वा पुत्रौ महीतले। रुरोद पुत्रशोकेन बाष्पपर्याकुलेक्षणम्॥
en: And, when the day had been half spent, seeing her sons fatigued on earth, she stricken with grief for them, began to weep with tears flooding her eyes.

sa_verse: अधस्ताद् व्रजतस्तस्याः सुरराज्ञो महात्मनः। बिन्दवः पतिता गात्रे सूक्ष्माः सुरभिगन्धिनः॥
en: And it came to pass that as the magnanimous sovereign of the celestials was passing below, her fine and fragrant tear drops fell on his person.

sa_verse: निरीक्षमाणस्तां शक्रो ददर्श सुरभिं स्थिताम्। आकाशे विष्टितां दीनां रुदतीं भृशदुःखिताम्॥।
en: Turning his eyes up, Śakra found Surabhi stationed in the sky, distressed and weeping in extreme anguish of spirit.

sa_verse: तां दृष्ट्वा शोकसंतप्तां वज्रपाणिर्यशस्विनीम्। इन्द्रः प्राञ्जलिरुद्विग्नः सुरराजोऽब्रवीद् वचः॥
en: Seeing that illustrious one burning in grief, the wielder of the thunder-bolt, Indra the lord of the celestials, waxing anxious, with joined hands said.

sa_verse: भयं कच्चिन्न चास्मासु कुतश्चिद् विद्यते महत्। कुतो निमित्तः शोकस्ते ब्रूहि सर्वहितैषिणि ॥
en: Is there any great danger in any quarter? Do you, O you that are intent on the welfare of all, say, whence is this grief of yours?

sa_verse: एवमुक्ता तु सुरभिः सुरराजेन धीमता। प्रत्युवाच ततो धीरा वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदा॥
en: Thus addressed by the intelligent king of the celestials, that one well skilled in speech, the sedate Surabhi answered.

sa_verse: शान्तं पापं न वः किंचित् कुतश्चिदमराधिप। अहं तु मग्नौ शौचामि स्वपुत्रौ विषमे स्थितौ॥ एतौ दृष्ट्वा कृशौ दीनौ सूर्यरश्मिप्रतापितौ। बध्यमानौ बलीवौं कर्षकेण दुरात्मना॥
en: Auspiciousness, O lord of celestials ! No sin is yours. But I mourn my sons fallen into evil plight, having seen them lean, distressed, and burnt by the rays of the sun, Balivardas afflicted by the wicked-minded ploughman.

sa_verse: मम कायात् प्रसूतौ हि दुःखितौ भारपीडितौ। यौ दृष्ट्वा परितप्येऽहं नास्ति पुत्रसमः प्रियः॥
en: Seeing those that have been born of my body, cast down and in trouble, I grieve: there is none that is dear like to a son.

sa_verse: यस्याः पुत्रसहस्रेस्तु कृत्स्नं व्याप्तमिदं जगत्। तां दृष्ट्वा रुदतीं शक्रो न सुतान् मन्यते परम्॥
en: Seeing her the whole earth is filled with whose sons by thousands, weeping, Indra understood that none is an object of greater affection than a son.

sa_verse: इन्द्रो ह्यश्रुनिपातं तं स्वगात्रे पुण्यगन्धिनम्। सुरभिं मन्यते दृष्ट्वा भूयसी तामिहेश्वरः॥
en: And that lord, Indra, also considered Surabhi of a person breathing a sacred odour, whose tears had dropped on his body, as the foremost being on earth.

sa_verse: समाप्रतिमवृत्ताया लोकधारणकाम्यया। श्रीमत्या गुणमुख्यायाः स्वभावपरिचेष्टया॥ यस्याः पुत्रसहस्राणि सापि शोचति कामधुक् । किं पुनर्या विना रामं कौसल्या वर्तयिष्यति॥
en: Even that one yielding whatever is asked, auspicious, crowned with the choicest virtues,

although having all natural functions, showing equal kindness to all, and of unequalled character, who maintain all creatures, she who has a thousand sons, grieves (for her sons). How can then Kausalyā carry on existence without Rāma?

sa_verse: अहं त्वपचितिं भ्रातुः पितुश्च सकलामिमाम्। वर्धनं यशसश्चापि करिष्यामि न संशयः॥
en: I shall for my part completely minister to my brother and father, and shall without doubt, increase my fame.

sa_verse: आनाय्य च महाबाहुं कोसलेन्द्रं महाबलम्। स्वयमेव प्रवेक्ष्यामि वनं मुनिनिषेचितम्॥
en: Bringing hither the mighty-armed lord of Kosala endowed with immense strength, I myself will repair to the forest inhabited by ascetics.

sa_verse: नाहं पापसंकल्पे पापे पापं त्वया कृतम्। शक्तो धारयितुं पौरैर श्रुकण्ठैर्निरीक्षितः॥
en: O you that have delivered yourself over to iniquity, I, looked at by the citizens with their throats oppressed with the vapour grief, cannot bear this (burden of) sin heaped on my by you.

sa_verse: सात्वमग्नि प्रविश वा स्वयं वा विश दण्डकान्। रज्जु बद्ध्वाथवा कण्ठे नहि तेऽन्यत् परायणम्॥
en: Do you enter fire, or dive into Dandaka, or wound a cord round your neck: other desirable way there is none for you.

sa_verse: अहमप्यवनों प्राप्ते रामे सत्यपराक्रमे। कृतकृत्यो भविष्यामि विप्रवासितकल्मषः॥
en: On Rāma having truth for prowess, obtaining the earth, I, my disgrace removed, shall be blest.

sa_verse: इति नाग इवारण्ये तोमराङ्कुशतोदितः। पपात भुवि संक्रुद्धो निःश्वसन्निव पन्नगः॥
en: Grieving thus, Bharata, like an elephant in the forest afflicted with a tomara or ankusa,* fell to the earth sighing like an enraged snake. *Names of instruments.

sa_verse: संरक्तनेत्रः शिथिलाम्बरस्तथा विधूतसर्वाभरणः परंतपः। बभूव भूमौ पतितो नृपात्मजः। शचीपतेः केतुरिवोत्सवक्षये॥
en: With his eyes reddened, and his cloth falling off, and his ornaments cast away, that subduer of foes, the king's son fallen on the earth, resembled a banner of Sakra after the festival is over.

sa_verse: दीर्घकालात् समुत्थाय संज्ञां लब्ध्वा स वीर्यवान्। नेत्राभ्यामश्रुपूर्णाभ्यां दीनामुद्वीक्ष्य मातरम्॥ सोऽमात्यमध्ये भरतो जननीमभ्यकुत्सयत्। राज्यं न कामये जातु मन्त्रये नापि मातरम्॥ अभिषेकं न जानामि योऽभूद् राज्ञा समीक्षितः। विप्रकृष्टे ह्यहं देशे शत्रुघ्नसहितोऽभवम्॥
en: Arising after a long while he had regained his consciousness, Bharata endowed with prowess, regarding his distressed mother with eyes filled with tears, began to tax her in the midst of the courtiers: “I had never desired the kingdom. I had never consulted my mother. Nor did I know the installation that had been thought of by the monarch. I was then living in a far country in company with Satrughna."

sa_verse: वनवासं न जानामि रामस्याहं महात्मनः। विवासनं च सौमित्रेः सीतायाश्च यथाभवत्॥
en: I did not know the banishment of the magnanimous Rāma to the woods, or the exile of Sumitra's son; nor did I know how Sītā came to be banished.

sa_verse: तथैव क्रोशतस्तस्य भरतस्य महात्मनः। कौसल्या शब्दमाज्ञाय सुमित्रां चेदमब्रवीत्॥
en: As the high-souled Bharata was thus wailing, Kausalyā recognizing his voice spoke to Sumitra.

sa_verse: आगतः क्रूरकार्यायाः कैकेय्या भरतः सुतः। तमहं द्रष्टुमिच्छामि भरतं दीर्घदर्शिनम्॥
en: Come is Bharata, the son of that one of crooked ways. I am desirous of seeing the farsighted Bharata.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा सुमित्रां तां विवर्णवदना कृशा। प्रतस्थे भरतो यत्र वेपमाना विचेतना॥ स तु राजात्मजश्चापि शत्रुघ्नसहितस्तदा। प्रतस्थे भरतो येन कौसल्याया निवेशनम्॥
en: Having said this to Sumitrā, that one emaciated and with a pallid countenance, trembling and almost deprived of sensation, went to where Bharata, was. And it came to pass that the king's son, Bharata, along with Śatrughna had just then by the way that led to the same, been proceeding to Kausalyā's quarter.

sa_verse: ततः शत्रुघ्नभरतौ कौसल्या प्रेक्ष्य दुःखितौ। पर्यष्वजेतां दुःखार्ती पतितां नष्टचेतनाम्॥ रुदन्तौ रुदती दुःखात् समेत्यार्या मनस्विनी। भरतं प्रत्युवाचेदं कौसल्या भृशदुःखिता॥
en: Then Śatrughna and Bharata seeing the aggrieved Kausalyā, embraced the stricken and fallen lady wellnigh deprived of her senses. Thereupon, weeping from grief, the noble and intelligent Kausalyà exceedingly afflicted, embracing them as they indulged in sorrow, said to Bharata.

sa_verse: इदं ते राज्यकामस्य राज्यं प्राप्तमकण्टकम्। सम्प्राप्तं बत कैकेय्या शीघ्रं क्रूरेण कर्मणा॥
en: Thus have you, that had desired the kingdom, received it rid of its thorn. Alas! it has been speedily obtained through the crooked act of Kaikeyi.

sa_verse: प्रस्थाप्य चीरवसनं पुत्रं मे वनवासिनम्। कैकेयी के गुणं तत्र पश्यति क्रूरदर्शिनी॥
en: What is the good that is perceived by Kaikeyi of tortuous sight in sending away my son clad in bark to the woods?

sa_verse: क्षिप्रं मामपि कैकेयी प्रस्थापयितुमर्हति। हिरण्यनाभो यत्रास्ते सुतो मे सुमहायशाः॥
en: It behoves Kaikeyi to send me also speedily to where is staying my illustrious son furnished with a gold-gleaming navel.

sa_verse: अथवा स्वयमेवाहं सुमित्रानुचरा सुखम्। अग्निहोत्रं पुरस्कृत्य प्रस्थास्ये यत्र राघवः॥
en: Or first performing the fire-sacrifice, I followed by Sumitrā, will myself happily seek the way by which Rāghava (has gone to the woods).

sa_verse: कामं वा स्वयमेवाद्य तत्र मां नेतुमर्हसि। यत्रासौ पुरुषव्याघ्रस्तप्यते मे सुतस्तपः॥
en: Or it behoves you to yourself bring me to the place where that foremost of men, my son, is performing austerities.

sa_verse: इदं हि तव विस्तीर्णं धनधान्यसमाचितम्। हस्त्यश्वरथसम्पूर्ण राज्यं निर्यातितं तया॥
en: This spacious kingdom abounding in corn and wealth, and filled with elephants, horses, and cars, is yours, having been conferred on you (by Kaikeyi).

sa_verse: इत्यादिबहुभिर्वाक्यैः क्रूरैः सम्भर्त्तितोऽनघः। विव्यथे भरतोऽतीव व्रणे तुद्येव सूचिना॥
en: Thus reproached by many a harsh word, the sinless Bharata felt exceeding pain like to that produced by pricking a sore with a needle.

sa_verse: पपात चरणौ तस्यास्तदा सम्भ्रान्तचेतनः। विलप्य बहुधासंज्ञो लब्धसंज्ञस्तदाभवत्॥ एवं विलपमानां तां प्राञ्जलिर्भरतस्तदा। कौसल्या प्रत्युवाचेदं शोकैर्बहुभिरावृताम् ॥
en: Exceedingly agitated, he fell at Kausalya's feet, lamenting much, and well nigh deprived of his Bharata then regained his consciousness, and with joined hands answered Kausalya lamenting thus and overcome with excess of sorrow, saying.

sa_verse: आर्ये कस्मादजानन्तं गर्हसे मामकल्मषम्। विपुलां च मम प्रीति स्थितां जानासि राघवे॥
en: O noble lady, what for do you censure me who am without sin and who know nothing of this? You know that profuse is my delight in Rāghava.

sa_verse: कृतशास्त्रानुगा बुद्धिर्मा भूत् तस्य कदाचन। सत्यसंधः सतां श्रेष्ठो यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May the sense of him never follow scripture, that had approved the exile into the woods of that foremost of the good, the noble Rāma intent senses. on truth!

sa_verse: प्रेष्यं पापीयसां यातु सूर्यं च प्रति मेहतु। हन्तु पादेन गाः सुप्ता यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May such a person as had approved the exile to the woods of the noble one undergo servitude at the hands of the sinful, answer the calls of nature facing the Sun, and kick a sleeping cow with his feet!

sa_verse: कारयित्वा महत् कर्म भर्ता भृत्यमनर्थकम्। अधर्मो योऽस्य सोऽस्यास्तु यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of the noble one into the woods come by the sin that is reaped by a master who withholds salary from servants after the ceremony is over!

sa_verse: परिपालयमानस्य राज्ञो भूतानि पुत्रवत्। ततस्तु दुह्यतां पापं यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May that one that had approved the exile of the noble Rāma to the woods, be guilty of the sin that is his that injures a sovereign engaged in ruling his subjects like sons!

sa_verse: बलिषड्भागमुद्धत्य नृपस्यारक्षितुः प्रजाः। अधर्मो योऽस्य सोऽस्यास्तु यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May the person that had approved the exile of the noble one to the woods, reap the sin of his that having taken a sixth pare of their incomes, does not protect his subjects!

sa_verse: संश्रुत्य च तपस्विभ्यः सत्रे वै यज्ञदक्षिणाम्। तां चापलतां पापं यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of the noble one to the woods, come by the demerit that is his that denies Dakșiņās to ascetics in a sacrifice, after having promised them the same!

sa_verse: हस्त्यश्वरथसम्बाधे युद्धे शस्त्रसमाकुले। मास्म कार्षीत् सतां धर्म यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile into the woods of the noble one, never discharge the duty of the good in the field thronged with elephants and horses and cars, and bristling with arms!

sa_verse: उपदिष्टं सुसूक्ष्मार्थं शास्त्रं यत्नेन धीमता। स नाशयतु दुष्टात्मा यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May the wicked wight that had approved the exile of the noble one to the woods bring to naught the subtle scriptural knowledge which has been carefully taught him by his intelligent preceptor!

sa_verse: मा च तं व्यूढबाह्वसं चन्द्रभास्करतेजसम्। द्राक्षीद् राज्यस्थमासीनं यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May that one that had approved the exile of the noble (Rāma) to the woods never behold the long-armed and broad-shouldered (Rāghava) resembling the Sun and the Moon in energy (on the throne)!

sa_verse: पायसं कृसरं छागं वृथा सोऽश्नातु निघृणः। गुरुंश्चाप्यवजानातु यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May that one void of aversion, that had approved of the exile of the noble one to the woods feed on Payasa, Krsara, and goat's flesh, which have not been offered in sacrifice; and may he insult his spiritual precept.

sa_verse: गवां स्पृशतु पादेन गुरून् परिवदेत च। मित्रे दुह्येत सोऽत्यर्थं यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: Or! May he that had approved the exile of the noble one to the woods touch a cow with his feet, revile his superiors, and injure his friends!

sa_verse: विश्वासात् कथितं किंचित् परिवादं मिथः क्वचित्। विवृणोतु स दुष्टात्मा यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May that one of wicked soul that had approved of the exile of the noble one into the woods divulge any defects (of another's character) that have been communicated to him in strict confidence!

sa_verse: अकर्ता चाकृतज्ञश्च त्यक्तश्च निरपत्रपः। लोके भवतु विद्विष्टो यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of Rāma to the woods, never repay any good act, be ungrateful, abandoned by all, the object of universal execration.

sa_verse: पुत्रैर्दासैश्च भृत्यैश्च स्वगृहे परिवारितः। स एको मृष्टमश्नातु यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved of the exile into the woods of the noble one, eat alone in his home, although surrounded by sons and wives and servitors!

sa_verse: अप्राप्य सदृशान् दाराननपत्यः प्रमीयताम्। अनवाप्य क्रियां धर्त्यां यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that haå approved of the exile of the noble one, die without obtaining wives worthy of him, without sons, without any good works performed!

sa_verse: मात्मनः संततिं द्राक्षीत् स्वेषु दारेषु दुःखितः। आयुःसमग्रमप्राप्य यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of the noble one into the woods, never see his own offspring, and not attaining ripe age, may he mourn his wives!

sa_verse: राजस्त्रीबालवृद्धानां वधे यत् पापमुच्यते। भृत्यत्यागे च यत्पापं तत्पापं प्रतिपद्यताम्॥
en: And may he come by the sin that is reaped by slaying a king, a woman, a boy, or an old man, or by renouncing those who have claims on his maintenance!

sa_verse: लाक्षया मधुमांसेन लोहेन च विषेण च। सदैव बिभृयाद् भृत्यान् यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of the noble one into the woods always feed his family with lac, honey, meat, iron, and poison!

sa_verse: संग्रामे समुपोढे च शत्रुपक्षभयंकरे। पलायमानो बध्येत यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May that one that had approved the exile of the noble one into the woods, while flying from the engagement while it is waxing furious with the foe, come to be killed !

sa_verse: कपालपाणिः पृथिवीमटतां चीरसंवृतः। भिक्षमाणो यथोन्मत्तो यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved of the exile of the noble one into the woods, clad in tatters with a skuil in hand, like a madman range the earth abegging!

sa_verse: मद्यप्रसक्तो भवतु स्त्रीष्वक्षेषु च नित्यशः। कामक्रोधाभिभूतश्च यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of the noble one into the woods, be always addicted to wine, to women, and to dice; and may he be swayed by lust, anger, etc!

sa_verse: मास्य धर्मे मनो भूयादधर्मं स निषेवताम्। अपात्रवर्षी भवतु यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved of the exile of that noble one into the woods, never relish virtue, and go after unrighteousness; and may he shower his wealth on the unworthy!

sa_verse: संचितान्यस्य वित्तानि विविधानि सहस्रशः। दस्युभिर्विप्रलुप्यन्तां यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of the noble one into the woods, have his various accumulated riches by thousand carried off by robbers!

sa_verse: उभे संध्ये शयानस्य यत् पापं परिकल्प्यते। तच्च पापं भवेत् तस्य यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥ यदग्निदायके पापं यत् पापं गुरुतल्पगे। मित्रद्रोहे च यत् पापं तत् पापं प्रतिपद्यताम्॥
en: May he that had approved of the exile into the woods of that noble one, come by the demerit that is his who sleeps during both the twilight's! May he reap the sin that is reaped by an incendiary or him that Leith with his preceptor's wife; or him that wrongs his friend!

sa_verse: देवतानां पितृणां च मातापित्रोस्तथैव च। मा स्म कार्षीत् स शुश्रूषां यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः।॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of that noble one into the woods, never serve the gods or the manes of the ancestors or his father and mother!

sa_verse: सतां लोकात् सतां कीर्त्याः सज्जुष्टात् कर्मणस्तथा। भ्रश्यतु क्षिप्रमद्यैव यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: may he that had approved the exile of that noble one into the woods, be speedily shut out from the world of the good, from their fame, and from their acts!

sa_verse: अपास्य मातृशुश्रूषामनर्थे सोऽवतिष्ठताम्। दीर्घबाहुमहावक्षा यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of that long armed and broad breasted one, renouncing the ministration of his mother, turn his thoughts to evil!

sa_verse: बहुभृत्यो दरिद्रश्च ज्वररोगसमन्वितः। समायात् सततं क्लेशं यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of the noble one, becoming improved the exile of the noble one, becoming impoverished yet having to support a large family, and suffering from fever, be always miserable! an

sa_verse: आशामाशंसमानानां दीनानामूलचक्षुषाम्। अर्थिनां वितथां कुर्याद् यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile of that noble one render fruitless the hopes of poor people cherishing them and looking up (wistfully).

sa_verse: मायया रमतां नित्यं पुरुषः पिशुनोऽशुचिः। राज्ञो भीतस्त्वधर्मात्मा यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile into the woods of the noble one remaining (always) wicked, cruel and impure, leading unrighteous life and being in (continual) fear of his sovereign, maintain himself by deceit for ever and a day!

sa_verse: ऋतुस्नातां सती भार्यामृतुकालानुरोधिनीम्। अतिवर्तेत दुष्टात्मा यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः५२।।
en: May that wicked person that had approved the exile into the woods of that noble one disregard his chaste wife remaining near, after she has performed her ablutions at the end of her season.

sa_verse: विप्रलुप्तप्रजातस्य दुष्कृतं ब्राह्मणस्य यत्। तदेतत् प्रतिपद्येत यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May his that had approved the banishment into the woods of that noble one be the sin that is incurred by a Brāhmana that has rendered himself sonless.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणायोद्यतां पूजां विहन्तु कलुषेन्द्रियः। बालवत्सां च गां दोग्धुर्यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May that one of befouled senses that had approved of the exile into the woods of that noble one disturb the worship of Brāhmaṇa and milch a cow that has a calf.

sa_verse: धर्मदारान् परित्यज्य परदारान् निषेवताम्। त्यक्तधर्मरतिर्मूढो यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May that foolish person that had approved the exile of that noble one forsaking all pleasure in virtue, seek others wives, renouncing his own wedded with sacred rites!

sa_verse: पानीयदूषके पापं तथैव विषदायके। यत्तदेकः स लभतां यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile into the woods of that noble one come by the sin that attaches to a wine biber or one that administers poison to another!

sa_verse: तृषार्तं सति पानीये विप्रलम्भेन योजयन्। यत् पापं लभते तत् स्याद् यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved of the exile into the woods of the noble one bear the sin that is his that serves a thirsty soul with deceit.

sa_verse: भक्त्या विवदमानेषु मार्गमाश्रित्य पश्यतः। तेन पापेन युज्येत यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः॥
en: May he that had approved the exile into the woods of that noble one reap the demerit of them that from devotion to their respective faiths wrangle from their own points of view, as well as that of them that listen to the disputation!

sa_verse: एवमाश्वासयन्नेव दुःखार्तोऽनुपपात ह। विहीनां पतिपुत्राभ्यां कौसल्या पार्थिवात्मजः॥
en: Having thus consoled Kausalyā bereft of her son and husband, the prince afflicted with distress fell down.

sa_verse: तदा तं शपथैः कष्टैः शपमानमचेतनम्। भरतं शोकसंतप्तं कौसल्या वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then Kausalyā addressed the aggrieved Bharaia (well-nigh) deprived of his senses, swearing strong oaths, saying.

sa_verse: मम दुःखमिदं पुत्र भूयः समुपजायते। शपथैः शपमानो हि प्राणानुपरुणत्सि मे॥
en: O son, it grieves me more that you are afflicting my heart by taking oaths.

sa_verse: दिष्ट्या न चलितो धर्मादात्मा ते सहलक्षणः। वत्स सत्यप्रतिज्ञो हि सतां लोकानवाप्स्यसि ॥ इत्युक्त्वा चाङ्कमानीय भरतं भ्रातृवत्सलम्। परिष्वज्य महाबाहुं रुरोद भृशदुःखिता॥
en: Lucky it is that graced with auspicious marks, you swerves not from virtue. My child, for this reason you will attain the regions of pious person, drawing into her lap Bharata attached to his brother, and embracing the mighty armed one, gave way to grief.

sa_verse: एवं विलपमानस्य दुःखार्तस्य महात्मनः। मोहाच्च शोकसंरम्भाद् बभूव लुलितं मनः॥
en: And bewailing thus, the mind of the high souled (Bharata) wrought with sorrow, was overcome with the burden of grief.

sa_verse: लालप्यमानस्य विचेतनस्य प्रणष्टबुद्धेः पतितस्य भूमौ। मुहुर्मुहुनिःश्वसतश्च दीर्घ सा तस्य शोकेन जगाम रात्रिः :॥
en: And fallen the ground, lamenting, senseless, with his intellect overpowered, and momentarily heaving sighs, Bharata passed away night in grief.

sa_verse: तमेवं शोकसंतप्तं भरतं कैकयीसुतम्। उवाच वदतां श्रेष्ठो वसिष्ठः श्रेष्ठवागृषिः॥
en: As Bharata, the son of Kaikeyi was thus burning in grief, that foremost of those skilled in speech, the saint Vasistha, endowed with excellence of language, said.

sa_verse: अलं शोकेन भद्रं ते राजपुत्र महायशः। प्राप्तकालं नरपतेः कुरु संयानमुत्तमम्॥
en: Do not weep, good betide you, O illustrious prince. Do you perform the funereal rites of the departed king.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठस्य वचः श्रुत्वा भरतो धरणीं गतः। प्रेतकृत्यानि सर्वाणि कारयामास धर्मवित्॥
en: Hearing Vasistha's words, Bharata cognisant of duty, bowed down to the ground and despatched the ministers for performing the last rites.

sa_verse: उदधृत्य तैलसंसेकात् स तु भूमौ निवेशितम्। आपीतवर्णवदनं प्रसुप्तमिव भूमिपम्॥ संवेश्य शयने चाग्र्ये नानारत्नपरिष्कृते। ततो दशरथं पुत्रो विललाप सुदुःखितः॥
en: And raising from under the oil the body of the king with a sallow countenance, and appearing to be asleep. Bharata placed it on the ground upon a couch in front adorned with various gems. Then overwhelmed with grief, Dasaratha's son bewailed him thus.

sa_verse: किं ते व्यवसितं राजन् प्रोषिते मय्यनागते। विवास्य रामं धर्मज्ञं लक्ष्मणं च महाबलम्॥
en: O king, what was it that you had intended to do, I absent and away from home, by banishing righteous Rāma and the powerful Lakşmana?

sa_verse: क्व यास्यसे महाराज हित्वेमं दु:खितं जनम्। हीनं पुरुषसिंहेन रामेणाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ॥
en: Whither wend you, O mighty monarch, forsaking these aggrieved people, who have already been deprived of the lion like Rāma energetic in action?

sa_verse: योगक्षेमं तु तेऽव्यग्रं कोऽस्मिन् कल्पयिता पुरे। त्वयि प्रयाते स्वस्तात रामे च वनमाश्रिते॥
en: O father, you having ascended heaven and Rāma having taken refuge in the woods, who now in this city of yours shall protect what the people possess and secure to them what they have not?

sa_verse: विधवा पृथिवी राजंस्त्वया हीना न राजते। हीनचन्द्रव रजनी नगरी प्रतिभाति माम्॥
en: Widowed in consequence of losing you, this earth does not look graceful. The city appears to me like the night deprived of the Moon

sa_verse: एवं विलपमानं तं भरतं दीनमानसम्। अब्रवीद् वचनं भूयो वसिष्ठस्तु महामुनिः॥
en: As Bharata was lamenting thus in dejected mood, the mighty ascetic Vasiştha again addressed him, saying.

sa_verse: प्रेतकार्याणि यान्यस्य कर्तव्यानि विशाम्पतेः। तान्यव्यग्रं महाबाहो क्रियतामविचारितम्॥
en: O mighty armed one, do you without indulging in any reflections, perform those funeral ceremonies of the monarch which ought to be performed.

sa_verse: तथेति भरतो वाक्यं वसिष्टस्याभिपूज्य तत्। ऋत्विक्पुरोहिताचार्यास्त्वरयामास सर्वशः॥
en: Thereupon honouring his words by saying, "So be it,” he urged speed upon all the Rtvigs, priests, and Acāryas.

sa_verse: ये त्वग्नयो नरेन्द्रस्य अग्न्यगाराद् बहुष्कृताः। ऋत्विग्भिर्याजकैश्चैव ते हूयन्ते यथाविधि॥
en: And then those that had brought the king's corpse out side from the fire chamber, instructed by the Rtvigs and priests according to the ordinance began to offer oblations into the fire.

sa_verse: हिरण्यं च सुवर्णं च वासांसि विविधानि च। प्रकिरन्तो जना मार्गे नृपतेरग्रतो ययुः॥
en: And scattering gold and silver and various kinds of cloth, on the way people went in front of the king.

sa_verse: चन्दनागुरुनिर्यासान् सरलं पद्मकं तथा। देवदारूणि चाहृत्य क्षेपयन्ति तथापरे॥ गन्धानुच्चावचांश्चान्यांस्तत्र गत्वाथ भूमिपम्। तत्र संवेशयामासुश्चितामध्ये तमृत्विजः॥
en: Others procuring sandal, aguru and other sarala, padmaka and devadari, cast it (on the earth). And drawing near the king there, Rtvigs laid various other fragrant substances on the funeral pile.

sa_verse: तदा हुताशनं हुत्वा जेपुस्तस्य तदृत्विजः। जगुश्च ते यथाशास्त्रं तत्र सामानि सामगाः॥
en: Then offering oblations into the fire, the Rtvigs began to recite japa; and as laid down in the scriptures, Sāma-singers chanted Sämans.

sa_verse: शिबिकाभिश्च यानैश्च यथार्ह तस्य योषितः। नगरान्निर्ययुस्तत्र वृद्धैः परिवृतास्तथा॥ प्रसव्यं चापि तं चक्रुर्ऋत्विजोऽग्निचितं नृपम्। स्त्रियश्च शोकसंतप्ता: कौसल्याप्रमुखास्तदा॥
en: By means of litters and other conveyances, each mounted according to her rank, the wives of the king went out from the city, surrounded by aged men. And Rtvijas went round the corpse on the king who performed many sacrifices leaving it on the left side.

sa_verse: क्रौञ्चीनामिव नारीणां निनादस्तत्र शुश्रुवे। आर्तानां करुणं काले क्रोशन्तीनां सहस्रशः॥
en: And kindling with grief, the females also headed by Kausalyā (circumambulated the pyre). And then there was heard the wail of women distressed with grief weeping piteously by thousands like to Krauñcis.

sa_verse: ततो रुदन्त्यो विवशा विलप्य च पुनः पुनः। यानेभ्यः सरयूतीरमवतेरुपाङ्गनाः॥
en: Weeping again and again with their sense lost, the wives of the king alighted from the car on the banks of the Sarayu.

sa_verse: कृत्वोदकं ते भरतेन साधु नृपाङ्गना मन्त्रिपुरोहिताश्च। पुरं प्रविश्याश्रुपरीतनेत्रा भूमौ दशाहं व्यनयन्त दुःखम्॥
en: Having performed the watery rites, the wives of the monarch as well as the counsellors and priests, in company with Bharata entering the city with tears in their eyes, spent ten days* in mourning lying down on the ground. *The Smrti: lays down twelve days of mourning for kings, and sixteen for Khatriyas. Parasara, however, fixes ten days for Ksatriyas in general.

sa_verse: ततो दशाहेऽतिगते कृतशौचो नृपात्मजः। द्वादशेऽहनि सम्प्राप्ते श्राद्धकर्माण्यकारयत्॥
en: When the ten days had gone by, the king's son, his uncleanness (consequent on the demise of his father) removed, performed the sraddha on the twelfth day.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणेभ्यो धनं रत्नं ददावन्नं च पुष्कलम्। वासांसि च महार्हाणि रत्नानि विविधानि च। बास्तिकं बहु शुक्लं च गाश्चापि बहुशस्तदा॥ दासीर्दासांश्च यानानि वेश्मानि सुमहान्ति च। ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददौ पुत्रो राज्ञस्तस्यौर्ध्वदेहिकम्॥
en: On the occasion on the ceremonies for the welfare of the departed Spirit, the son of the king conferred on Brāhmaṇas wealth and gems, and rice in abundance, and herds of goats, and silver in profusion, and countless kine, and maidservants and man-servants, and vehicles and spacious mansions.

sa_verse: ततः प्रभातसमये दिवसे च त्रयोदशे। विललाप महाबाहुर्भरतः शोकमूर्च्छितः॥
en: And on the thirteenth day, the long armed Bharata, overwhelmed with grief, burst into lamentation.

sa_verse: शब्दापिहितकण्ठश्च शोधनार्थमुपागतः। चितामूले पितुर्वाक्यमिदमाह सुदुःखितः॥ तात यस्मिन् निसृष्टोऽहं त्वया भ्रातरि राघवे। तस्मिन् वनं प्रव्रजिते शून्ये त्यक्तोऽस्म्यहं त्वया॥
en: And coming to the foot of the funeral pyre for gathering the bones of the departed, he overcome with grief, with his throat obstructed with the sounds of lamentation, said, “my father, on brother Rāma, to whom I had been consigned by you, having gone to the forest, I have been cast by you into vacancy.

sa_verse: यस्या गतिरनाथायाः पुत्रः प्रव्राजितो वनम्। तामम्बां तात कौसल्यां त्यक्त्वात्वं व गतोनृप।७।।
en: My father, forsaking forlorn mother Kausalya, whose stay her son, had gone to the forest, where have you gone, Oking?

sa_verse: दृष्ट्वा भस्मारुणं तच्च दग्धास्थि स्थानमण्डलम्। पितुः शरीरनिर्वाणं निष्टनन् विषसाद ह॥
en: Seeing the spot where lay the bones of his father mixed with ashes and embers Bharata looking at the place where occurred the dissolution of his father's fame, carried away by emotion, indulged in sorrow.

sa_verse: स तु दृष्ट्वा रुदन् दीनः पपात धरणीतले। उत्थाप्यमानः शक्रस्य यन्त्रध्वज इवोच्छ्रितः॥
en: And seeing this, he exceedingly distressed, crying fell down to the ground. And raised up (by others) he looked like an uplifted banner of Sakra bound to an engine.

sa_verse: अभिपेतुस्ततः सर्वे तस्यामात्याः शुचिव्रतम्। अन्तकाले निपतितं ययातिमृषयो यथा॥
en: His counsellors rushed towards that one of pure vows, like the saints making towards Yayati as he was falling on the extinction of his merit.

sa_verse: शत्रुघ्नश्चापि भरतं दृष्ट्वा शोकपरिप्लुतम्। विसंज्ञो न्यपतद् भूमौ भूमिपालमनुस्मरन्॥
en: Seeing Bharata plunged in grief, Satrughna remembering the king, fell down to the ground deprived of consciousness.

sa_verse: उन्मत्त इव निश्चित्तो विललाप सुदुःखितः। स्मृत्वा पितुर्गुणाङ्गानि तानि तानि तदा तदा॥
en: And devoid of sense and like a madman, he in grief of heart began to lament remembering all the virtues of his father again and again.

sa_verse: मन्थराप्रभवस्तीव्रः कैकेयीग्राहसंकुलः। वरदानमयोऽक्षोभ्योऽमज्जयच्छोकसागरः॥
en: This terrible sea of grief owing its origin to Mantharā, containing its ferocious aquatic animal in the shape of Kaikeyī, and incapable of being disturbed in consequence of the bestowal of the boon drowns (us).

sa_verse: सुकुमारं च बालं च सततं लालितं त्वया। क तात भरतं हित्वा विलपन्तं गतो भवान् ॥
en: O father, where have you gone, leaving the tender and youthful Bharata fondled by you, to lament (your loss).

sa_verse: ननु भोज्येषु पानेषु वस्त्रेष्वाभरणेषु च। प्रवारयति सर्वान् नस्तनः कोऽद्य करिष्यति॥
en: You did use to confer on us eatables and drinkable and attires and ornaments. Who will now do so?

sa_verse: अवदारणकाले तु पृथिवी नावदीर्यते। विहीना या त्वया राज्ञा धर्मज्ञेन महात्मना ॥
en: Deprived of you, the high souled king cognisant of duty, the earth albeit her time of riving is come, is not yet riven.

sa_verse: पितरि स्वर्गमापन्ने रामे चारण्यमाश्रिते। किं मे जीवितसामर्थ्य प्रवेक्ष्यामि हुताशनम्॥
en: My father having gone to heaven and Rāma having sought the woods, how can I live? I will enter fire.

sa_verse: हीनो भ्रात्रा च पित्रा च शून्यामिक्ष्वाकुपालिताम्। अयोध्यां न प्रवेक्ष्यामि प्रवेक्ष्यामि तपोवनम्॥
en: Bereft of my brother and sire, I will not enter the empty Ayodhyā governed by the Ikşvākus. I will repair to the forest of asceticism.

sa_verse: तपोर्विलपितं श्रुत्वा व्यसनं चाप्यवेक्ष्य तत्। भृशमार्ततरा भूयः सर्व एवानुगामिनः॥
en: Hearing his lamentations and seeing that disaster, all the followers became all the more distressed.

sa_verse: ततो विषण्णौ श्रान्तौ च शत्रुघ्नभरतावुभौ। धरायां स्म व्यचेष्टेतां भग्नशृङ्गविवर्षभौ॥
en: Then depressed and exhausted, both Šatrughna and Bharata rolled on the earth like two bulls with their horns fractured,

sa_verse: ततः प्रकृतिमान् वैद्यः पितुरेषां पुरोहितः। वसिष्ठो भरतं वाक्यमुत्थाप्य तमुवाच ह॥
en: Then the all-knowing priest of their father possessed of sterling worth, Vasistha, raising Bharata, said to him.

sa_verse: त्रयोदशोऽयं दिवसः पितुर्वृत्तस्य ते विभो। सावशेषास्थिनिचये किमिह त्वं विलम्बसे ॥
en: O Lord this is the thirteenth day since the cremation of your sire. Why did you delay, when you have to collect the bones?

sa_verse: त्रीणि द्वन्द्वानि भूतेषु प्रवृत्तान्यविशेषतः। तेषु चापरिहार्येषु नैवं भवितुमर्हसि॥
en: Three couples* pertain in especial to all creatures and these being inevitable, you ought not to bear yourself thus. *Hunger and thirst, ignorance and grief, sickness and death.

sa_verse: सुमन्त्रश्चापि शत्रुघ्नमुत्थाप्याभिप्रसाद्य च। श्रावयामास तत्त्वज्ञः सर्वभूतभवाभवौ॥
en: And Sumantra also versed in the nature of things, raising up Šatrughna and pacifying him, discoursed the birth and heath of all beings.

sa_verse: उत्थितौ तौ नरव्याघ्रौ प्रकाशेते यशस्विनौ। वर्षातपपरिग्लानौ पृथगिन्द्रध्वजाविव ॥
en: Being raised up, those renowned chiefs of men looked like Indra's banner stained by shine and shower.

sa_verse: अश्रूणि परिमृद्गन्तौ रक्ताक्षौ दीनभाषिणौ। अमात्यास्त्वरयन्ति स्म तनयौ चापराः क्रियाः॥
en: And as the princes stood there shedding tears, with reddened eyes, and speaking sadly, the courers urged nem on in behalf of the rites that remained.

sa_verse: अथ यात्रां समीहन्तं शत्रुघ्ने लक्ष्मणानुजः। भरतं शोकसंतप्तमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then ſatrughna, the younger brother of Lakşmaņa spoke to Bharata burning in grief as he was revolving in his mind the (intended) journey (to Rāma).

sa_verse: गतिर्यः सर्वभूतानां दुःखे किं पुनरात्मनः। स रामः सत्त्वसम्पन्नः स्त्रिया प्रव्राजितो वनम् ॥
en: Need it be said that Rāma is the refuge both of himself and all creatures in trouble? That Rāma possessed of strength has been exiled into the woods by a woman!

sa_verse: बलवान् वीर्यसम्पन्नो लक्ष्मणो नाम योऽप्यसौ। किं न मोचयते रामं कृत्वापि पितृनिग्रहम्॥
en: And powerful and having prowess, why did not Laksmana deliver Rāma by checking our father?

sa_verse: पूर्वमेव तु विग्राह्यः समवेक्ष्य नयानयौ। उत्पथं यः समारूढो नार्या राजा वशं गतः॥
en: The king bent upon following an unrighteous course through the influence of a woman, should, the justice or otherwise of the measure being fully weighed, at the very outset have been checked.

sa_verse: इति सम्भाषमाणे तु शत्रुघ्ने लक्ष्मणानुजे। प्रारद्वारेऽभूत् तदा कुब्जा सर्वाभरणभूषिता॥ लिप्ता चन्दनसारेण राजवस्त्राणि बिभ्रती। विविधं विविधैस्तैस्तैर्भूषणैश्च विभूषिता॥
en: As Lakşmaņa's younger brother Satrughna was speaking thus, appeared there at the door in front the hump backed one adorned with every kind of ornament, with her body besmeared with sandal paste, wearing regal apparel, and variously decked out with a variety of ornaments.

sa_verse: मेखलादामभिश्चित्रैरन्यैश्च वरभूषणैः। बभासे बहुभिर्बद्धा रज्जुभिरिव वानरी॥
en: And adorned with elegant zones, and divers other superb ornaments, she looked like a female monkey tethered with a rope.

sa_verse: तां समीक्ष्य तदा द्वा:स्थो भृशं पापस्य कारिणीम्। गृहीत्वाकरुणं कुब्जां शत्रुघ्नाय न्यवेदयत्॥
en: At that time seeing that one of horrible misdeeds, (Bharata) who stood near the door, seizing the hump backed one ruthlessly, took her to Satrughna, and said.

sa_verse: यस्याः कृते वने रामो न्यस्तदेहश्च वः पिता। सेयं पापा नृशंसा च तस्याः कुरु यथामति।।।।
en: That one through whom Rāma has gone to the wood and our father has renounced his body this is that wicked and remorseless one. Do you deal with her as you like.

sa_verse: शत्रुघ्नश्च तदाज्ञाय वचनं भृशदुःखितः। अन्तःपुरचरान् सर्वानित्युवाच धृतव्रतः॥
en: At Bharata's command Satrughna observing vows waxing exceedingly aggrieved, addressed all the inmates of the inner apartment, saying.

sa_verse: तीव्रमुत्पादितं दुःखं भ्रातृणां मे तथा पितुः। यथा सेयं नृशंसस्य कर्मणः फलमश्नुताम्॥
en: This one has occasioned intense misery to my father and brothers Let her now take the fruit of her fell deed.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा च तेनाशु सखीजनसमावृता। गृहीता बलवत् कुब्जा सा तद् गृहमनादयत्॥
en: Having said this, he furiously fell upon the hump backed one surrounded by her maids. Thereupon she with her cries made the chamber resound.

sa_verse: ततः सुभृशसंतप्तस्तस्याः सर्वः सखीजनः। क्रुद्धमाज्ञाय शत्रुघ्नं व्यपलायत सर्वशः॥
en: Concluding Śatrughna fired with rage, her associates extremely pained, fled away in all directions.

sa_verse: अमन्त्रयत कृत्स्नश्च तस्याः सर्वः सखीजनः। यथायं समुपक्रान्तो निःशेष नः करिष्यति॥
en: Then her companions in a body took counsel of each other, saying, “considering the way in which he has entered upon it, he will annihilate us quite."

sa_verse: सानुक्रोशां वदान्यां च धर्मज्ञां च यशस्विनीम्। कौसल्यां शरणं यामः सा हि नोऽस्ति ध्रुवा गतिः।।
en: Let us therefore seek the protection of the tender hearted generous, pious and illustrious Kausalyā. Even she is our sure refuge.

sa_verse: सच रोषेण संवीतः शत्रुघ्नः शत्रुशासनः। विचकर्ष तदा कुब्जां क्रोशन्तीं पृथिवीतले॥
en: Overpowered with rage, that chastiser of foes, Śatrughna, dragged the shrieking hump backed one to the ground.

sa_verse: तस्यां ह्याकृष्यमाणायां मन्थरायां ततस्ततः। चित्रं बहुविधं भाण्डं पृथिव्यां तद्व्यशीर्यत ॥
en: As Mantharā was pushed this way and that, her various ornaments were scattered over the floor.

sa_verse: तेन भाण्डेन विस्तीर्णं श्रीमद् राजनिवेशनम्। अशोभत तदा भूयः शारदं गगनं यथा॥
en: And strewn with those ornaments, the graceful chamber of the palace looked like the autumnal firmament.

sa_verse: स बली बलवत् क्रोधाद् गृहीत्वा पुरुषर्षभः। कैकेयीमभिनिर्भय॑ बभाषे परुषं वचः॥
en: And that foremost of men possessed of strength holding her, began to reprove Kaikeyī with harsh speech.

sa_verse: तैर्वाक्यैः परुषैर्दुःखैः कैकेयी भृशदुःखिता। शत्रुघ्नभयसंत्रस्ता पुत्रं शरणमागता॥
en: Extremely hurt by those rough words, Kaikeyi terrified on account of Satrughna, took refuge with her son.

sa_verse: तं प्रेक्ष्य भरतः क्रुद्धं शत्रुघ्नमिदमब्रवीत्। अवध्याः सर्वभूतानां प्रमदाः क्षम्यतामिति॥
en: Thereupon casting his eyes on Satrughna, Bharata said, “A woman is incapable of being slain by any. Do you therefore excuse her.”

sa_verse: हन्यामहमिमां पापां कैकेयीं दुष्टचारिणीम्। यदि मां धार्मिको रामो नासूयेन्मातृघातकम्॥
en: I myself would have slain this wicked Kaikeyi of impious deeds, if the righteous Rāma should not be displeased with me on account of my slaying my mother.

sa_verse: इमामपि हतां कुब्जां यदि जानाति राघवः। त्वां च मां चैव धर्मात्मा नाभिभाषिष्यते ध्रुवम्॥
en: If Rāghava knows that the hump-backed one has been slain, he sure' will speak neither with you nor me.

sa_verse: भरतस्य वचः श्रुत्वा शत्रुघ्नो लक्ष्मणानुजः। न्यवर्तत ततो दोषात् तां मुमोच च मूर्च्छिताम्॥
en: Hearing Bharata's words, Satrughna, younger brother to Lakşmaņa, refrained from that wrong and set free the hump-backed woman in a swoon.

sa_verse: सा पादमूले कैकेय्या मन्थरा निपपात ह। निःश्वसन्ती सुदुःखार्ता कृपणं विललाप ह॥
en: Thereat, sighing hard in exceeding grief, Mantharā flung herself and Kaikeyi's feet, weeping piteously.

sa_verse: शत्रुघ्नविक्षेपविमूढसंज्ञां समीक्ष्य कुब्जां भरतस्य माता। शनैः समाश्वासयदार्तरूपां कौचीं विलग्नामिव वीक्षमाणाम्॥
en: Seeing the hump-backed one deprived of her senses in consequence of the pushing she had received at the hands of Satrughna, Bharata's mother consoled that distressed woman, who appeared like a Krauñci that had been entrapped.

sa_verse: ततः प्रभातसमये दिवसेऽथ चतुर्दशे। समेत्य राजकर्तारो भरतं वाक्यमब्रुवन्॥
en: Then on the morning of the fourteenth day, the ministers of the king assembled addressed Bharata in the following words.

sa_verse: गतो दशरथः स्वर्गं यो नो गुरुतरो गुरुः। रामं प्रव्राज्य वै ज्येष्ठं लक्ष्मणं च महाबलम्॥ त्वमद्य भव नो राजा राजपुत्र महायशः। संगत्या नापराध्नोति राज्यमेतदनायकम्॥
en: Having exiled his eldest son and the exceedingly strong Lakşmaņa, Dasaratha who was the superior of our superiors has gone to heaven. Do you, therefore, O illustrious prince, become our king. Having been permitted by the king you will commit no fault (by doing so), as this kingdom is without a master.

sa_verse: आभिषेचनिकं सर्वमिदमादाय राघव। प्रतीक्षते त्वां स्वजनः श्रेणयश्च नृपात्मज ॥
en: O Raghava, having procured all these necessaries for the installation, the counsellor and othe, as well as the citizens wait, О king's son.

sa_verse: राज्यं गृहाण भरत पितृपैतामहं ध्रुवम्। अभिषेचय चात्मानं पाहि चास्मान् नरर्षभ॥
en: Do you, O Bharata, take charge of this secure kingdom bequeathed by your father and grandfather. Do you, O foremost of men, have yourself sprinkled, and rule over us.

sa_verse: आभिषेचनिकं भाण्डं कृत्वा सर्वं प्रदक्षणिम्। भरतस्तं जनं सर्वं प्रत्युवाच धृतव्रतः॥
en: Thereupon, having gone round all the things procured for the sprinkling, Bharata from in his vows addressed those persons, saying.

sa_verse: ज्येष्ठस्य राजता नित्यमुचिता हि कुलस्य नः। नैवं भवन्तो मां वक्तुमर्हन्ति कुशला जनाः॥
en: In our line it is ever fit for the first-born alone to perform the task of government. It does not behove you who are wise to say so to me.

sa_verse: रामः पूर्वो हि नो भ्राता भविष्यति महीपतिः। अहं त्वरण्ये वत्स्यामि वर्षाणि नव पञ्च च॥
en: Certainly Rāma our eldest brother shall become the king; and I will abide in the forest for five all nine years.

sa_verse: युज्यतां महती सेना चतुरङ्गमहाबला। आनयिष्याम्यहं ज्येष्ठं भ्रातरं राघवं वनात्॥
en: Do you array the grand and mighty my consisting of fourfold forces. I will bring back from the forest my eldest brother Rāghava.

sa_verse: आभिषेचनिकं चैव सर्वमेतदुपस्कृतम्। पुरस्कृत्य गमिष्यामि रामहेतोर्वनं प्रति॥ तत्रैव तं नरव्याघ्रमभिषिच्य पुरस्कृतम्। आनयिष्यामि वै रामं हव्यवाहमिवाध्वरात्॥
en: And taking all these necessaries for the investiture in front, I will go in the direction of the forest for Rāma. And sprinkling that chief of men on the spot, I will placing him in our front, bring Rāma back, like to fire brought in from the sacrifice.

sa_verse: न सकामां करिष्यामि स्वामिमां मातृगन्धिनीम्। वने वत्स्याम्यहं दुर्गे रामो राजा भविष्यति॥
en: I will never fulfil the desire of this lady proud of her son. I will inhabit the impracticable woods, and Rāma shall become the monarch.

sa_verse: क्रियतां शिल्पिभिः पन्थाः समानि विषमाणि च। रक्षिणश्चानुसंयातु पथि दुर्गविचारकाः॥
en: Let workmen lay out level roads in uneven tracts; and let those men that are adepts in threading places difficult to pass through, follow us.

sa_verse: एवं सम्भाषमाणं तं रामहेतोपात्मजम्। प्रत्युवाच जनः सर्वः श्रीमद्वाक्यमनुत्तमम्॥
en: When the prince had spoken thus in behalf of Rāma, all those persons answered him in these excellent words.

sa_verse: एवं ते भाषमाणस्य पद्मा श्रीरुपतिष्ठताम्। यस्त्वं ज्येष्ठे नृपसुते पृथिवीं दातुमिच्छसि ॥
en: For saying this, may Lakşmi seated on the lotus remain at they side; inasmuch you wish to confer the earth on the eldest son of the monarch!

sa_verse: अनुत्तमं तद्वचनं नृपात्मजः प्रभाषितं संश्रवणे निशम्य च। प्रहर्षजास्तं प्रति बाष्पविन्दवो निपेतुरार्यानननेत्रसम्भवाः॥
en: Hearing that graceful speech of the king's son, tear-drops. begot of delight began to trickle from their eyes and adorned those noble countenances.

sa_verse: ऊचुस्ते वचनमिदं निशम्य हृष्टाः सामात्याः सपरिषदो वियातशोकाः। पन्थानं नरवरभक्तिमान् जनश्च व्यादिष्टस्तव वचनाच्च शिल्पिवर्गः॥
en: Their grief removed, with cheerful hearts, the counsellors, courtiers, and others hearing that speech (of the prince), said, “O best of men, according to your order artizans as well as people cherishing a high regard for you have been directed to lay out a read."

sa_verse: अथ भूमिप्रदेशज्ञाः सूत्रकर्मविशारदाः। स्वकर्माभिरताः शूराः खनका यन्त्रकास्तथा॥ कर्मान्तिकाः स्थपतयः पुरुषा यन्त्रकोविदाः। तथा वर्धकयश्चैव मार्गिणो वृक्षतक्षकाः॥ सूपकाराः सुधाकारा वंशचर्मकृतस्तथा। समर्था ये च द्रष्टारः पुरतश्च प्रतस्थिरे॥
en: Then set out in advance persons having a knowledge of the humidity or otherwise of the soil, men skilled in making tents, brave delivers engaged on their proper work; those capable of constructing canals and water courses, people on pay, car-makers, men preparing machines, carpenters, those intended to guard the ways, pioneers, cooks, perfumers, makers of wickerware and able guides.

sa_verse: स तु हर्षात् तमुद्देशं जनौघो विपुलः प्रयान्। अशोभत महावेगः सागरस्येव पर्वणि॥
en: And as the mighty throng began to proceed, it resembled the swell of the sea on the occasion of a parva.

sa_verse: ते स्ववारं समास्थाय वर्मकर्मणि कोविदाः। करणैर्विविधोपेतैः पुरस्तात् सम्प्रतस्थिरे॥
en: And number of men skilled in roadconstructing went before furnished with various implements.

sa_verse: लता वल्लीश्च गुल्मांश्च स्थाणूनश्मन एव च। जनास्ते चक्रिरे मार्ग छिन्दन्तो विविधान् दुमान्॥
en: And hewing away boughs, plants and shrubs and woody projections, stones, and diverse trees, they went on preparing a road.

sa_verse: अवृक्षेषु च देशेषु केचिद् वृक्षानरोपयन्। केचित् कुठारैष्टकैश्च दात्रैश्छिन्दन् क्वचित्क्वचित्।७।। अपरे वीरणस्तम्बान् बलिनो बलवत्तराः। विधमन्ति स्म दुर्गाणि स्थलानि च ततस्ततः॥ अपरेऽपूरयन् कूपान् पांसुभिः श्वभ्रमायतम्। निम्नभागांस्तथैवाशु समांश्चक्रुः समन्ततः॥
en: They set up trees where there were none, and at places they felled trees by means of axes, tankas, and daos, others possessed of greater strength and more powerful, with their hands uprooted masses of Vīrana and here and there levelled a rising ground. And other filled up with dust wells and capacious hollows, and speedily levelled deep places all around.

sa_verse: बबन्धुर्बन्धनीयांश्च क्षोद्यान् संवुक्षुदुस्तथा। बिभिदुर्भेदनीयांश्च तांस्तान् देशान् नरास्तदा॥
en: And those men threw bridges wherever they became necessary, and broke the earth wherever such a process was required, and excavated whenever such a process was required, and excavated whenever it was necessary.

sa_verse: अचिरेण तु कालेन परिवाहान् बहूदकान्। चक्रुर्बहुविधाकारान् सागरप्रतिमान् बहून्॥
en: In a short time, they made places poor of water overflow with many and various expanses resembling the ocean.

sa_verse: निर्जलेषु च देशेषु खानयामासुरुत्तमान्। उदपानान् बहुविधान् वेदिकापरिमण्डितान्॥
en: And in tracts void of water, they digged divers receptacles of water, decorated with daises.

sa_verse: ससुधाकुट्टिमतलः प्रपुष्पितमहीरुहः। मत्ता ष्टद्विजगणः पताकाभिरलंकृतः॥ चन्दनोदकसंसिक्तो नानाकुसुमभूषितः। बहशोभत सेनायाः पन्थाः सुरपथोपमः॥
en: And the way of the army, furnished with pavements of bricks and clay, with trees bearing blossoms, eloquent with the tunes of birds, decorated with pennons, sprinkled with sandal showers, and garnished with flowers of various kinds, looked exceedingly beautiful, like to the way of the celestials.

sa_verse: आज्ञाप्याथ यथाज्ञप्ति युक्तास्तेऽधिकृता नराः। रमणीयेषु देशेषु बहुस्वादुफलेषु च ॥ यो निवेशस्त्वभिप्रेतो भरतस्य महात्मनः। भूयस्तं शोभयामासुभूषाभिभूषणोपमम् ॥
en: Then having received the command of Bharata, the men that were in charge of the tents, ordered (the workmen) to pitch the tents, and when they had been pitched at a romantic spot filled with tasteful fruits, in consonance with the injunction of the high-souled Bharata, the men decorated splendidly the tents which themselves were like the ornaments of the road.

sa_verse: नक्षत्रेषु प्रशस्तेषु मुहूर्तेषु च तद्विदः। निवेशान् स्थापयामासुर्भरतस्य महात्मनः॥
en: Under an auspicious star and at a favourable hour, men well up in the work set the encampment of the high-souled Bharata.

sa_verse: बहुपांसुचयाश्चापि परिखाः परिवारिताः। तत्रेन्द्रनीलप्रतिमाः प्रतोलीवरशोभिताः॥ प्रासादमालासंयुक्ताः सौधप्राकारसंवृताः। पताकाशोभिताः सर्वे सुनिर्मितमहापथाः॥ विसर्पद्भिरिवाकाशे विटङ्काग्रविमानकैः। समुच्छ्रितैर्निवेशास्ते बभुः शक्रपुरोपमाः॥
en: And the tents surrounded by an entrenchment paved with dust, containing images made of sapphires, graced with goodly thoroughfares, lined with edifices, encircled by towers and walls, decorated with streamers, having wellmade high-ways, and appearing like celestial daises and containing stately mansions dovecotes, resembled the metropolis of Śakra himself.

sa_verse: जाह्नवीं तु समासाद्य विविधदुमकाननाम्। शीतलामलपानीयां महामीनसमाकुलाम्॥ सचन्द्रतारागणमण्डितं यथा नभः क्षपायाममलं विराजते। नरेन्द्रमार्गः स तदा व्यराजत क्रमेण रम्यः शुभशिल्पिनिर्मितः॥
en: And passing by the Jāhnavi abounding in various trees and woods, of cool and crystal waters, and filled with mighty fishes, that way of the chief of men constructed by artizans, looked more and more lovely as it proceeded, as the unclouded sky looks beautiful at night, adorned with innumerable stars.

sa_verse: ततो नान्दीमुखीं रात्रि भरतं सूतमागधाः। तुष्टुवुः सविशेषज्ञाः स्तवैर्मङ्गलसंस्तवैः॥
en: Then seeing that the night in which had been performed the auspicious preliminary ceremonies, was about to be spent, eulogists and genealogists hymned Bharata with consecrated hymns.

sa_verse: सुवर्णकोणाभिहतः प्राणदद्यामदुन्दुभिः। दध्मुः शङ्खांश्च शतशो वाद्यांश्चोच्चावचस्वरान्॥
en: And then sounded the kettle-drum, beaten by a golden stick, announcing the departure of the night; and people sounded conchs and other instruments by hundreds furnished with soft and loud sounds.

sa_verse: स तूर्यघोषः सुमहान् दिवमापूरयन्निव। भरतं शोकसंतप्तं भूयः शोकैररन्धयत्॥
en: And as if filling the heavens, those powerful blasts of the trumpet repeatedly rendered Bharata burning in grief the more aggrieved.

sa_verse: ततः प्रबुद्धो भरतस्तं घोषं संनिवर्त्य च। नाहं राजेति चोक्त्वा तं शत्रुघ्नमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then awaking and silencing those sounds with saying, “I am not the king," he said to Satrughna.

sa_verse: पश्य शत्रुघ्न कैकेय्या लोकस्यापकृतं महत्। विसृज्य मयि दुःखानि राजा दशरथो गतः॥
en: Behold, O Satrughna, in what a mighty wrong the people are engaged, on account of Kaikeyi. The king Dasaratha has gone away throwing down on me (the burthen of) this misery.

sa_verse: तस्यैषा धर्मराजस्य धर्ममूला महात्मनः। परिभ्रमति राजश्रीनौरिवाकर्णिका जले॥
en: The royal grace founded in righteousness of that magnanimous and virtuous monarch is wandering even like a boat on water having no helmsman.

sa_verse: यो हि नः सुमहान् नाथः सोऽपि प्रव्राजितो वने। अनया धर्ममुत्सृज्य मात्रा मे राघवः स्वयम्।७।।
en: And he who is our mighty master has been banished into the woods by this mother of mine, who had (in doing so) renounced virtue.

sa_verse: इत्येवं भरतं वीक्ष्य विलपन्तमचेतनम्। कृपणा रुरुदुः सर्वाः सुस्वरं योषितस्तदा॥
en: Seeing Bharata lamenting thus senseless, the ladies afflicted with sorrow began to wail in winsome accents.

sa_verse: तथा तस्मिन् विलपति वसिष्ठो राजधर्मवित्। सभामिक्ष्वाकुनाथस्य प्रविवेश महायशाः॥ शातकुम्भमयीं रम्यां मणिहेमसमाकुलाम्। सुधर्मामिव धर्मात्मा सगणः प्रत्यपद्यत ॥ स काञ्चनमयं पीठं स्वस्त्यास्तरणसंवृतम्। अध्यास्त सर्ववेदज्ञो दूताननुशशास च॥
en: As Bharata was mourning thus, the highly famous and virtuous Vasistha accompanied by his disciples entered the court of the Ikşvāku king: built of entire gold, charming, dazzling with gems and gold: like to Sudharmä its. Sitting down on a golden seat furnished with a elegant cover, that one versed in all the Vedas commanded the envoys, saying.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणान् क्षत्रियान् योधानमात्यान् गणवल्लभान्। क्षिप्रमानयताव्यग्राः कृत्यमात्ययिकं हि नः॥ सराजपुत्रं शत्रुघ्नं भरतं च यशस्विनम्। युधाजितं सुमन्त्रं च ये च तत्र हिता जनाः॥
en: Do you speedily with collected minds bring hither Brāhmaṇas and Kștriyas and warriors and counsellors and generals of forces and Satrughna with the other princes, and the famous Bharata and Yuddhajit* and Sumantra and others that are engaged in our welfare. *One of the counsellors.

sa_verse: ततो हलहलाशब्दो महान् समुदपद्यत। रथैरश्वैर्गजैश्चापि जनानामुपगच्छताम्॥
en: Then there arose a mighty hubbub occasioned by people coming up in cars, horses and elephants.

sa_verse: ततो भरतमायान्तं शतक्रतुमिवामराः। प्रत्यनन्दन् प्रकृतयो यथा दशरथं तथा॥
en: When Bharata arrived, the subjects rejoiced as they used to rejoice on Daſaratha's arrival; and as rejoiced the immortals on the arrival of him of an hundred sacrifices.

sa_verse: हृद इव तिमिनागसंवृतः स्तिमितजलो मणिशङ्खशर्करः। दशरथसुतशोभिता सभा सदशरथेव बभूव सा पुरा॥
en: And then the court resembling a moveless ocean containing whales and serpents,* and gems and conchs and gold-mines, being graced with the presence of Dasaratha's son, looked splendid as it formerly did with that of Dasaratha himself. *Nāga may also mean hypopotamus.

sa_verse: तामार्यगणसम्पूर्णां भरतः प्रग्रहां सभाम्। ददर्श बुद्धिसम्पन्नः पूर्णचन्द्रां निशामिव॥
en: And then the intelligent Bharata surveyed that assembly filled with noble, and worthy personages, resembling the night of the full moon.

sa_verse: आसनानि यथान्यायमार्याणां विशतां तदा। वस्त्राङ्गरागप्रभया द्योतिता सा सभोत्तमा॥
en: And that august assembly was ablaze with the brilliant hues proceeding from the attires of the honourable persons seated according to rank.

sa_verse: सा विद्वज्जनसम्पूर्णा सभा सुरुचिरा तथा। अदृश्यत घनापाये पूर्णचन्द्रेव शर्वरी॥
en: And that splendid assembly filled with learned people looked like he night of the full moon after the clouds have dispersed.

sa_verse: राज्ञस्तु प्रकृतीः सर्वाः स सम्प्रेक्ष्य च धर्मवित्। इदं पुरोहितो वाक्यं भरतं मृदु चाब्रवीत्॥
en: And seeing all the subjects of the sovereign gathered together, the priest cognizant of virtue softly spoke to Bharata.

sa_verse: तात राजा दशरथः स्वर्गतो धर्ममाचरन्। धनधान्यवतीं स्फीतां प्रदाय पृथिवीं तव॥
en: Your child, leaving to you this prosperous carth abounding in corn and wealth, king Dasaratha having performed his duties, has gone to heaven.

sa_verse: रामस्तथा सत्यवृत्तिः सतां धर्ममनुस्मरन्। ना जहात् पितुरादेशं शशी ज्योत्स्नामिवोदितः॥
en: And Rāma of truthful character observing the virtue of the righteous has not set aside his father's commands. even as the risen moon does not renounce the moonlight.

sa_verse: पित्रा भ्रात्रा च ते दत्तं राज्यं निहतकण्टकम्। तद् भुझ्व मुदितामात्यः क्षिप्रमेवाभिषेचय॥ उदीच्याश्च प्रतीच्याश्च दाक्षिणात्याश्च केवलाः। कोट्यापरान्ताः सामुद्रा रत्नान्युपहरन्तु ते॥
en: Loved by the courtiers, do you, having been installed, enjoy this kingdom conferred on you by your father and mother, rid of its thorn. Let princes throned as well as those without thrones, from east, and west, north and south, and also persons ranging the sea, procure countless gems (for presenting them to you.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा भरतो वाक्यं शोकेनाभिपरिप्लुतः। जगाम मनसा रामं धर्मज्ञो धर्मकाया॥
en: Hearing this speech, Bharata cognizant of virtue, filled with sorrow, mentally repaired to Rāma desirous of reaping merit.

sa_verse: सबाष्पकलया वाचा कलहंसस्वरो युवा। विललाप सभामध्ये जगहें च पुरोहितम्॥
en: Then in words choked with the melodious voice of a swan, lamented and taxed the priest, in the midst of the assembly.

sa_verse: चरितब्रह्मचर्यस्य विद्यास्नातस्य धीमतः। धर्मे प्रयतमानस्य को राज्यं मद्विधो हरेत्॥
en: Who like myself ever deprives one that has led a Brahmacharya mode of life, that is endowed with understanding and performed his bath after having acquired learning, and that is always intent on righteousness, of one's kingdom?

sa_verse: कथं दशरथाज्जातो भवेद् राज्यापहारकः। राज्यं चाहं च रामस्य धर्म वक्तुमिहार्हसि ॥
en: How begot of Dasaratha, shall I deprive Rāma of his kingdom. It behove you to speak righteousness before this assembly.

sa_verse: ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठश्च धर्मात्मा दिलीपनहुषोपमः। लब्धुमर्हति काकुत्स्थो राज्यं दशरथो यथा॥
en: First-born, and foremost in merit, righteoussouled, and comparable to Dilipa and Nahuşa, Kākutstha deserves the kingdom, just as Dasaratha did.

sa_verse: अनार्यजुष्टमस्वयं कुर्यां पापमहं यदि। इक्ष्वाकूणामहं लोके भवेयं कुलपांसनः॥
en: If I commit myself to this sin dishonourable and calculated to bring me to perdition, I shall in this world bring disgrace on the race of the Ikşvākus.

sa_verse: यद्धि मात्रा कृतं पापं नाहं तदपि रोचये। इहस्थो वनदुर्गस्थं नमस्यामि कृताञ्जलिः॥
en: I do not at all relish the sin that has been committed by mother. Remaining here with joined hands I bow down to Rāma gone to the forest fastness.

sa_verse: राममेवानुगच्छामि स राजा द्विपदां वरः। त्रयाणामपि लोकानां राघवो राज्यमर्हति ॥
en: I will follow Rāma. That best of men is the king. Rāghava deserves the dominion of the three worlds themselves.

sa_verse: तद्वाक्यं धर्मसंयुक्तं श्रुत्वा सर्वे सभासदः। हर्षान्मुमुचुरश्रूणि रामे निहितचेतसः॥
en: Hearing words informed with those righteousness, the entire assembly with their minds intent upon Rāma, from joy shed tears.

sa_verse: यदि त्वार्यं न शक्ष्यामि विनिवर्तयितुं वनात्। वने तत्रैव वत्स्यामि यथार्यो लक्ष्मणस्तथा॥
en: If I fail to bring back the noble one from the forest, I will like the exalted Lakşmaņa remain even in that forest.

sa_verse: सर्वोपायं तु वर्तिष्ये विनिवर्तयितुं बलात्। समक्षमार्यमिश्राणां साधूनां गुणवर्तिनाम्॥
en: I will in presence of this mixed assembly of pious and honourable persons following every perfection, adopt every expedient to bring back Rāma.

sa_verse: विष्टिकर्मान्तिकाः सर्वे मार्गशोधकदक्षकाः। प्रस्थापिता मया पूर्व यात्रा च मम रोचते॥
en: I have already despatched beforehand persons serving for love as well as those for money, and layers of roads and their keepers, and I intend setting out now.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा तु धर्मात्मा भरतो भ्रातृवत्सलः। समीपस्थमुवाचेदं सुमन्त्रं मन्त्रकोविदम्॥
en: Having said this, the virtuous Bharata attached to his brother spoke to Sumantra skilled in counsel, who was by, saying.

sa_verse: तूर्णमुत्थाय गच्छ त्वं सुमन्त्र मम शासनात्। यात्रामाज्ञापय क्षिप्रं बलं चैव समानय ॥
en: Up, and go, O Sumantra at my command. Do you make known this journey and bring the forces.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तः सुमन्त्रस्तु भरतेन महात्मना। प्रहृष्टः सोऽदिशत् सर्वं यथासंदिष्टमिष्टवत्॥
en: Thus accosted by the magnanimous Bharata, Sumantra with a cheerful heart issued orders concerning everything desirable as he was ordered.

sa_verse: ताः प्रहृष्टाः प्रकृतयो बलाध्यक्षा बलस्य च। श्रुत्वा यात्रां समाज्ञप्तां राघवस्य निवर्तने॥
en: Hearing that the army had been ordered to march forth for bringing back Rāma, the subjects as well as the generals of the forces became exceedingly delighted.

sa_verse: ततो योधाङ्गनाः सर्वा भर्तृन् सर्वान् गृहे गृहे। सज्जं तु तद् यात्रागमनमाज्ञाय त्वरयन्ति स्म हर्षिताः॥
en: Hearing of the journey to Rāma, for bringing him back the wives of the soldiers apprised of the order that had been issued to the latter, being exceedingly delighted, hurried on.

sa_verse: ते हयैर्गोरथैः शीघ्रं स्यन्दनैश्च मनोजवैः। सह योषिद्बलाघ्यक्षा बलं सर्वमचोदयन्॥
en: And the generals expeditiously despatched their forces with warriors by means of horses and cares and cars fleet as the mind.

sa_verse: बलं दृष्ट्वा भरतो गुरुसंनिधौ। रथं मे त्वरयस्वेति सुमन्त्रं पार्श्वऽब्रवीत्॥
en: Seeing those force marshalled, Bharata staying near his preceptor, said to Sumantra who was at his side, “Do you speedily bring my car”

sa_verse: भरतस्य तु तस्याज्ञां परिगृह्य प्रहर्षितः। रथं गृहीत्वोपययौ युक्तं परमवाजिभिः॥
en: In obedience to the mandate of Bharata, Sumantra exceedingly rejoiced, appeared with the car yoked with superb steeds.

sa_verse: स राघवः सत्यधृतिः प्रतापवान् ब्रुवन् सुयुक्तं दृढसत्यविक्रमः। गुरुं महारण्यगतं यशस्विनं प्रसादयिष्यन् भरतोऽब्रवीत् तदा॥
en: Then that powerful descendant of Raghu of truthful character, and having unswerving truth for his prowess, Bharata, having said what was fit, spoke words calculated to gladden his illustrious superior gone to the mighty forest.

sa_verse: तूर्णं त्वमुत्थाय सुमन्त्र गच्छ बलस्य योगाय बलप्रधानान्। आनेतुमिच्छामि हि तं वनस्थं प्रसाद्य रामं जगतो हिताय॥
en: O Sumantra, arise you speedily and, your desire fully attained, go by my command, and tell the chiefs of the army, and our principal adherents to array the forces.

sa_verse: गाज्ञापितः सम्परिपूर्णकामः। शशास सर्वान् प्रकृतिप्रधानान् बलस्य मुख्यांश्च सुहृज्जनं च॥ ततः समुत्थाय कुले कुले ते राजन्यवैश्या वृषलाश्च विप्राः। अयूयुजन्नुष्ट्ररथान् खरांश्च नागान् हयांश्चैव कुलप्रसूतान्॥
en: Thereat rising, Rājanyas and Vaisyas, and Vņşalas; and Vipras in every house began to yoke camels and cars and mules and elephants and excellent steeds.

sa_verse: ततः समुत्थाय कुले कुले ते राजन्यवैश्या वृषलाश्च विप्राः। अयूयुजन्नुष्ट्ररथान् खरांश्च नागान् हयांश्चैव कुलप्रसूतान्॥
en: Thereat rising, Rājanyas and Vaisyas, and Vņşalas; and Vipras in every house began to yoke camels and cars and mules and elephants and excellent steeds.

sa_verse: ततः समुत्थितः कल्यमास्थाय स्यन्दनोत्तमम्। प्रययौ भरतः शीघ्रं रामदर्शनकाम्यया॥
en: Rising with the morrow, Bharata anxious to behold Rāma, speedily set out ascending an excellent car.

sa_verse: अग्रतः प्रययुस्तस्य सर्वे मन्त्रिपुरोहिताः। अधिरुह्य हयैर्युक्तान् रथान् सूर्यरथोपमान्॥
en: Before him went the councillors and priests, ascending cars resembling that of the Sun yoked with steeds.

sa_verse: नवनागसहस्राणि कल्पितानि यथाविधिः। अन्वयुर्भरतं यान्तमिक्ष्वाकुकुलनन्दनम्॥
en: And a thousand elephants duly consecrated went in the wake of that son of the Ikşvāku line as he was proceeding.

sa_verse: षष्टी रथसहस्राणि धन्विनो विविधायुधाः। अन्वयुर्भरतं यान्तं राजपुत्रं यशस्विनम् ॥
en: And six thousand with bow-men furnished with various weapons followed the illustrious prince Bharata as he was proceeding.

sa_verse: शतं सहस्राण्यश्वानां समारूढानि राघवम्। अन्वयुर्भरतं यान्तं राजपुत्रं यशस्विनम्॥
en: And a hundred thousand horses mounted (by riders) went in the wake of that descendant of Raghu intent upon truth and having his senses under control.

sa_verse: कैकेयी च सुमित्रा च कौसल्या च यशस्विनी। रामानयनसंतुष्टा ययुर्यानेन भास्वता॥
en: Kaikeyi and Sumitrā and the highly famous Kausalyā rejoicing at the prospect of the bringing of Rāma, went in an effulgent car. cars

sa_verse: प्रयाताश्चार्यसंघाता रामं द्रष्टुं सलक्ष्मणम्। तस्यैव च कथाश्चित्राः कुर्वाणा हृष्टमानसाः॥
en: And the honourable persons (belonging to all the three orders) went with the object of beholding Rāma in company with Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: मेघश्याम महाबाहुं स्थिरसत्त्वं दृढव्रतम्। कदा द्रक्ष्यामहे रामं जगतः शोकनाशनम्॥
en: They with glad hearts variously conversed with each other, “When shall we see the mighty armed Rāma sable like to a cloud, of steady strength, firm in vows, the remover of the world's grief?

sa_verse: दृष्ट एव हि नः शोकमपनेष्यति राघवः। तमः सर्वस्य लोकस्य समुद्यन्निव भास्करः॥
en: As soon as we shall see him, Rāghava will remove our sorrow; even as the Sun arising dispels the darkness of the entire world.

sa_verse: इत्येवं कथयन्तस्ते सम्प्रहृष्टाः कथाः शुभाः। परिष्वजानाश्चान्योन्यं ययुर्नागरिकास्तदा॥
en: Thus cheerfully carrying on an auspicious talk, the citizens embracing each other went along.

sa_verse: ये च तत्रापरे सर्वे सम्मता ये च नैगमाः। रामं प्रतिययुर्हृष्टाः सर्वाः प्रकृतयः शुभाः॥
en: And all other, and the foremost merchants as well as all the principal classes, joyfully went in quest of Rāma.

sa_verse: मणिकाराश्च ये केचित् कुम्भकाराश्च शोभनाः। सूत्रकर्मविशेषज्ञा ये च शस्त्रोफ्जीविनः॥ मायूरकाः क्राकचिका वेधका रोचकास्तथा। दन्तकाराः सुधाकारा ये च गन्धोपजीविनः॥ सुवर्णकाराः प्रख्यातास्तथा कम्बलकारकाः। स्नापकोष्णोदका वैद्या धूपकाः शौण्डिकास्तथा॥ रजकास्तुत्रवायाश्च ग्रामघोषमहत्तराः। शैलूषाश्च सह स्त्रीभिर्यान्ति कैवर्तकास्तथा॥ समाहिता वेदविदो ब्राह्मणा वृत्तसम्मताः। गोरथैर्भरतं यान्तमनुजग्मुः सहस्रशः॥ सुवेषाः शुद्धवसनास्ताम्रपृष्टानुलेपिनः। सर्वे ते विविधैर्यानैः शनैर्भरतमन्वयुः॥
en: And number of gem-cutters, and goodly manufacturers, bathers in tepid water, shampooers, physicians, makers of Dhūpas, and wine-sellers, washermen, and tailors, and actors in numbers with females, and Kaivartas, and persons versed in Vedas having their minds in control, and Brāhmaṇas of reputed character, and persons well dressed and attired in pure habits, with their bodies daubed with coppery unguents, by thousands followed Bharata on carts.

sa_verse: प्रहृष्टमुदिता सेना सान्वयात् कैकयीसुतम्। भ्रातुरानयने यातं भरतं भ्रातृवत्सलम्॥
en: All these gradually followed Bharata by means of excellent vehicles. And the army delighted and in high spirits went in the wake of Kaikeyi's son attached to his brother, going to bring back his brother.

sa_verse: ते गत्वा दूरमध्वानं रथयानाश्वकुञ्जरैः। समासेदुस्ततो गङ्गां शृङ्गवेरपुरं प्रति॥ यत्र रामसखा वीरो गुहो ज्ञातिगणैर्वृतः। निवसत्यप्रमादेन देशं तं परिपालयन्॥
en: Going far by means of cars, vehicles, elephants, and horses, they arrived at the Gangā near Śrngaverapura, where was peacefully staying that friend of Rāma, the heroic Guha, surrounded by his relatives ruling the realm.

sa_verse: उपेत्य तीरं गङ्गायाश्चक्रवाकैरलंकृतम्। व्यवतिष्ठत सा सेना भरतस्यानुयायिनी॥ निरीक्ष्यानुत्थितां सेनां तां च गङ्गां शिवोदकाम्। भरतः सचिवान् सर्वानब्रवीद् वाक्यकोविदः॥
en: Having come to the banks of the Gangā graced with Cakravākas, the army which was following Bharata halted. Seeing the army inactive as well as the Gangā of sacred waters, Bharata versed in speech spoke to the courtiers.

sa_verse: निवेशयत् मे सैन्यमभिप्रायेण सर्वतः। विश्रान्ताः प्रतरिष्यामः श्व इमां सागरणमाम्॥
en: Do you communicating to all our intentions, encamp the army. Having been fatigued, we shall cross the ocean-going Gangā on the morrow.

sa_verse: दातुं च तावदिच्छामि स्वर्गतस्य महीपतेः। और्ध्वदेहनिमित्तार्थमवतीर्योदकं नदीम्॥
en: Having crossed the stream, I am anxious to offer its water to the monarch, who has gone to heaven in behalf of his spiritual body.

sa_verse: तस्यैवं ब्रुवतोऽमात्यास्तथेत्युक्त्वा समाहिताः। न्यवेशयंस्तांश्छन्देन स्वेन स्वेन पृथक् पृथक् ॥
en: When he had said this, the courtiers with collected minds saying, “Be it so,” disposed their forces, each according to his wish.

sa_verse: निवेश्य गङ्गामनु तां महानदी चमूं विधानैः परिबर्हशोभिनीम्। उवास रामस्य तदा महात्मो विचिन्तमानो भरतो निवर्तनम्॥
en: Having on the mighty stream, Gangā, quartered his forces furnished with all appliances for the journey, Bharata remained there, revolving the means of making the high-souled Rāma turn back.

sa_verse: ततो निविष्टां ध्वजिनी गङ्गामन्वाश्रितां नदीम्। निषादराजो दृष्ट्वैव ज्ञातीन् स परितोऽब्रवीत्॥
en: Seeing the forces with banners flying quartered on the banks of the river Gangā, and engaged in carious occupations, the lord of the Nisadas, Guha, said to his relatives ranged around.

sa_verse: महतीयमितः सेना सागराभा प्रदृश्यते। नास्यान्तमवगच्छामि मनसापि विचिन्तयन्॥
en: This mighty host here appears like an ocean. I do not find its end even by thinking of it in my mind.

sa_verse: यदा नु खलु दुर्बुद्धिर्भरतः स्वयमागतः। स एष हि महाकायः कोविदारध्वजो रथे॥
en: Surely the foolish Bharata has come hither himself: on his car appears the huge Kovidāra banner.

sa_verse: सम्पन्नां श्रियमन्विच्छंस्तस्य राज्ञः सुदुर्लभाम्। भरतः कैकयीपुत्रो हन्तुं समधिगच्छति ॥
en: Desirous of taking complete possession of the rare regal fortune of that sovereign (Rāma), Kaikeyi's son, Bharata, comes to destroy him.

sa_verse: भर्ता चैव सखा चैव रामो दाशरथिर्मम। तस्यार्थकामाः संनद्धा गङ्गानूपेऽत्र तिष्ठत॥
en: Rāma the son of Daśaratha is both my maintainer and friend. Do you in his interests, donning on your mail, wait on the banks of the river.

sa_verse: तिष्ठन्तु सर्वदाशाश्च गङ्गामन्वाश्रिता नदीम्। बलयुक्ता नदीरक्षा मांसमूलफलाशनाः॥
en: And stationed on the river Gangā, let my powerful retainers subsisting on fruits and roots and meat, be prepared for opposing Bharata's passages over the river.

sa_verse: नावां शतानां पञ्चानां कैवर्तानां शतं शतम्। संनद्धानां तथा यूनां तिष्ठन्त्वित्यभ्यचोदयत्॥
en: And let hundreds upon hundreds of Kaivarta youths accoutred in mail remain in each of five hundred barks., Guha issued this order.

sa_verse: यदि तुष्टस्तु भरतो रामस्येह भविष्यति। इयं स्वस्तिमती सेना गङ्गामद्य तरिष्यति॥
en: But if Bharata be well disposed towards Rāma, this host shall today safely cross the Ganga.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वोपायनं गृह्य मत्स्यमांसमधूनि च। अभिचक्राम भरतं निषादाधिपतिर्मुहः॥
en: Having said this, the lord of the Nişādas, Guha, taking a present of flesh, fish and honey, went out for interviewing Bharata.

sa_verse: तमायान्तं तु सम्प्रेक्ष्य सूतपुत्रः प्रतापवान्। भरतायाचचक्षेऽथ समयज्ञो विनीतवत्॥
en: Seeing Guha approaching, the powerful son of the charioteer knowing season, humbly informed Bharata of it, saying.

sa_verse: एष ज्ञातिसहस्रेण स्थपतिः परिवारितः। कुशलो दण्डकारण्ये वृद्धो भ्रातुश्च ते सखा॥ तस्मात् पश्यतु काकुत्स्थ त्वां निषादाधिपो गुहः। असंशयं विजानीते यत्र तौ रामलक्ष्मणौ॥
en: This lord (that approaches) surrounded by his relatives, is very potent in Dandaka and is an old friend of your brother. Therefore let Guha, the lord of the Nişādas, see you, O Kākutstha. He indubitably knows where Rāma and Lakşmaņa are.

sa_verse: एतत् तु वचनं श्रुत्वा सुमन्त्राद् भरतः शुभम्। उवाच वचनं शीघ्रं गुहः पश्यतु मामिति ॥
en: Hearing these wise words of Sumantra, Bharata at once said, "Let Guha see me."

sa_verse: लब्ध्वानुज्ञां सम्प्रहृष्टो ज्ञातिभिः परिवारितः। आगम्य भरतं प्रह्वो गुहो वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Receiving permission, Guha, right glad, appeared before Bharata, bending low, and said.

sa_verse: निष्कुटश्चैव देशोऽयं वञ्चिताश्चापि ते वयम्। निवेदयाम ते सर्वं स्वके दाशगृहे वस॥
en: This place is your home. But you have stolen a march over us. We dedicate all this to you. Do you reside in the abode of your servant.

sa_verse: अस्ति मूलफलं चैतनिषादैः स्वयमर्जितम्। आर्द्र शुष्कं तथा मांसं वन्यं चोच्चावचं तथा॥
en: Here are fruits and roots gathered by the Nişādas and meat dry and moist and various other produces of the forest.

sa_verse: आशंसे स्वाशिता सेना वत्स्यत्येनां विभावरीम्। अर्चितो विविधैः कामैः श्वः ससैन्यो गमिष्यसि॥
en: I pray that entertained in various ways and heartily partaking of meats and drinks, this army may spend the night here. Tomorrow morning, you will go along with your forces.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु भरतो निषादाधिपतिं गुहम्। प्रत्युवाच महाप्राज्ञो वाक्यं हेत्वर्थसंहितम्॥
en: Thus addressed, the exceedingly wise Bharata answered the lord of the Nişādas, in words fraught with sense and reason.

sa_verse: ऊर्जितः खलु ते कामः कृतो मम गुरो सखे। यो मे त्वमीदृशीं सेनामभ्यर्चयितुमिच्छसि॥
en: Your great desire, O friend of my superior, is surely as good as attained; since you of mighty energy have set your heart on entertaining my army.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा स महातेजा गुहं वचनमुत्तमम्। अब्रवीद् भरतः श्रीमान् पन्थानं दर्शयन् पुनः॥
en: Having said these fair words to Guha, the graceful and highly energetic Bharata again addressed the lord of Nişādas.

sa_verse: कतरेण गमिष्यामि भरद्वाजाश्रमं पथा। गहनोऽयं भृशं देशो गङ्गानूपो दुरत्ययः॥
en: By what way shall I go to Bharadvāja's hermitage? These lands watered by the Gangā are dense and hard to track.

sa_verse: तस्य तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा राजपुत्रस्य धीमतः। अब्रवीत् प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा गुहो गहनगोचरः॥
en: Hearing these words of the intelligent son of the king, Guha well acquainted with the forest, said with joined hands.

sa_verse: दाशास्त्वनुगमिष्यन्ति देशज्ञाः सुसमाहिताः। अहं चानुगमिष्यामि राजपुत्र महाबल॥
en: My servant well acquainted with the place shall attentively follow you; and, O prince possessed of mighty strength, I myself will also walk in your wake.

sa_verse: कच्चिन्न दुष्टो व्रजसि रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः। इयं ते महती सेना शङ्कां जनयतीव मे॥
en: But do you go after Rāma of energetic acts with some evil intention? This vast force of yours raise my apprehension/

sa_verse: तमेवमभिभाषन्तमाकाश इव निर्मलः। भरतः श्लक्ष्णया वाचा गुहं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: When Guha had asked this, Bharata with a presence unclouded like the sky, spoke to Guha these sweet words.

sa_verse: मा भूत् स कालो यत् कष्टं न मां शङ्कितुमर्हसि। राघवः स हि मे भ्राता ज्येष्ठः पितृसमो मतः॥
en: May a time never come when I shall do wrong to Rāghava! It behoves you not to fear me. Rāghava is my eldest brother dear to me even as my sire himself.

sa_verse: तं निवर्तयितुं यामि काकुत्स्थं वनवासिनम्। बुद्धिरन्या न मे कार्या गुह सत्यं ब्रवीमि ते॥
en: I go to make Kākutstha dwelling in the woods, turn back. Other intention cherish I none. O Guha, this I tell you truly.

sa_verse: स तु संहृष्टवदनः श्रुत्वा भरतभाषितम्। पुनरेवाब्रवीद् वाक्यं भरतं प्रति हर्षितः॥
en: Having heard Bharata's speech, Guha with a countenance lighted up with delight, again cheerfully addressed Bharata, saying.

sa_verse: धन्यस्त्वं न त्वया तुल्यं पश्यामि जगतीतले। अयत्नादागतं राज्यं यस्त्वं त्यक्तुमिहेच्छसि॥
en: Blessed are you! Your like find I none on earth, inasmuch as you wish to resign a kingdom that comes to you without search.

sa_verse: शाश्वती खलु ते कीर्तिर्लोकाननु चरिष्यति। यस्त्वं कृच्छ्रगतं रामं प्रत्यानयितुमिच्छसि॥
en: Your eternal fame will certainly range this world, since you wish to bring back Rāma passing through misfortune.

sa_verse: एवं सम्भाषमाणस्य गुहस्य भरतं तदा। बभौ नष्टप्रभः सूर्यो रजनी चाभ्यवर्तत ॥
en: As Guha was speaking thus to Bharata, the sun became shorn of his splendour and night fell.

sa_verse: संनिवेश्य स तां सेनां गुहेन परितोषितः। शत्रुघ्नेन समं क्षीमाञ्छयनं पुनरागमत्॥
en: Thereupon having disposed his troops, the auspicious Bharata gratified by Guha, went to bed along with Śatrughna.

sa_verse: रामचिन्तामयः शोको भरतस्य महात्मनः। उपस्थितो ह्यनर्हस्य धर्मप्रेक्षस्य तादृशः॥
en: Then arose thoughts of Rāma in the mind of the magnanimous Bharata ever having his gaze fixed on virtue and undeserving (of hardship).

sa_verse: अन्तर्दाहेन दहनः संतापयति राघवम्। वनदाहाग्निसंतप्त गूढोऽग्निरिव पादपम्॥
en: Then even as a tree already heated by a forestfire burns with a fire hidden in its cavity that descendant of Raghu began to burn with the fire of grief inflamed in his heart.

sa_verse: प्रसृतः सर्वगात्रेभ्यः स्वेदं शोकाग्निसम्भवम्। यथा सूर्यांशुसंतप्तो हिमवान् प्रसृतो हिमम्॥
en: Perspiration produced by the fire of sorrow issued out of all his limbs, as the Himavat heated by the solar warmth generates water.

sa_verse: ध्याननिर्दरशैलेन विनिःश्वासितधातुना। दैन्यपादपसंघेन शोकायासाधिशृङ्गिणा॥ प्रमोहानन्तसत्त्वेन संतापौषधिवेणुना। आक्रान्तो दुःखशैलेन महता कैकयीसुतः॥
en: And Kaikeyi's son was overpowered and drowned by the mountain of grief, having thoughts (of Rāma) for its entire crags, sighs for its mineral substance, disgust with the avocations of life, for its trees, mental feebleness through grief for its summits, stupor for the animals inhabiting it, and burning for its annual shrubs and bamboos.

sa_verse: विनिःश्वसन् वै भृशदुर्मनास्ततः प्रमूढसंज्ञः परमापदं गतः। शमं न लेभे हृदयज्वरार्दितो नरर्षभो यूथहतो यथर्षभः॥
en: And sighing heavily with a heart oppressed with sorrow, well nigh deprived of consciousness, and involved in high peril, that best of men, oppressed by the fever of his heart, like to a mighty leader of a herd, separated from it, did not attain peace of mind.

sa_verse: गुहेन सार्धं भरतः समागतो महानुभावः सजनः समाहितः। गुंहः समाश्वासयदग्रज प्रति॥
en: Meeting with Guha, the magnanimous Bharata accompanied by his people, engrossed with the thoughts of Rāma, became oppressed with grief. (Seeing this), Guha by and by encouraged Bharata concerning his elder brother.

sa_verse: आचचक्षेऽथ सद्भावं लक्ष्मणस्य महात्मनः। भरतायाप्रमेयाय गुहो गहनगोचरः॥
en: Guha, acquainted with the forest, described to Bharata of immeasurable prowess the regard the high-souled Laksmana bore to Rama.

sa_verse: तं जाग्रतं गुणैर्युक्तं वरचापेषु धारिणम्। भ्रातृगुप्त्यर्थमत्यन्तमहं लक्ष्मणमब्रुवम्॥
en: To Lakşmaņa crowned with every virtue, waking up, holding the bow with the arrow fixed on it for the purpose of guarding well his brother.

sa_verse: इयं तात सुखा शय्या त्वदर्थमुपकल्पिता। प्रत्याश्वसिहि शेष्वास्यां सुखं राघवनन्दन ॥ उचितोऽयं जनः सर्वो दुःखानां त्वं सुखोचितः। धर्मात्मस्तस्य गुप्त्यर्थं जागरिष्यामहे वयम्॥
en: I said, “This easeful bed has been prepared for you, my child. O son of Raghu's descendant, cheer up! Do you lie down at ease. All these people can bear hardship; but you are meant for comfort. For protecting him religiously, we shall wake.

sa_verse: नहि रामात् प्रियतरो ममास्ति भुवि कश्चन। मोत्सुको भूर्बवीम्येतदथ सत्यं तवाग्रतः॥ अस्य प्रसादादाशंसे लोकेऽस्मिन् सुमहद् यशः। धर्मावाप्तिं च विपुलामर्थकामौ च केवलौ॥
en: To me also there is none that is dearer on earth than Rāma. Do not be anxious. This I tell you truly, through his grace I expect high fame among men, and immense religious merit, and interest and desire in entirety.

sa_verse: सोऽहं प्रियसखं रामं शयानं सह सीतया। रक्षिष्यामि धनुष्पाणिः सर्वैः स्वैातिभिः सह॥
en: Bow in hand I shall along with my kin protect Rāma lying down with Sītā.

sa_verse: नहि मेऽविदितं किंचिद् वनेऽस्मिंश्चरतः सदा। चतुरङ्ग ह्यपि बलं प्रसहेम वयं युधि ॥
en: To me always ranging in this forest, nothing whatever is unknown. I can even cope in battle with an army of fourfold forces.

sa_verse: एवमस्माभिरुक्तेन लक्ष्मणेन महात्मना। अनुनीता वयं सर्वे धर्ममेवानुपश्यता ॥
en: Thus accosted by us, the magnanimous Laks maņa with his gaze ever fixed on virtue, humbly observed.

sa_verse: कथं दाशरथौ भूमौ शयाने सह सीतया। शक्या निद्रा मया लब्धं जीवितानि सुखानि वा॥
en: How, Dasaratha's son sleeping on the earth with Sītā, can I attain sleep, or life, or happiness?

sa_verse: यो न देवासुरैः सर्वैः शक्यः प्रसहितुं युधि। तं पश्य गुह संविष्टं तृणेषु सह सीतया॥
en: How see him who is capable of bearing in battle the onslaught of the gods and the Asuras combined, sleeping in a cave on grass?

sa_verse: महता तपसा लब्धो विविधैश्च परिश्रमैः। एको दशरथस्यैष पुत्रः सदृशलक्षणः॥ अस्मिन् प्रवाजिते राजा न चिरं वर्तयिष्यति। विधवा मेदिनी नूनं क्षिप्रमेव भविष्यति॥
en: It is by virtue of mighty austerities and uncommon exertions that Dasaratha has obtained this son of his crowned with every auspicious sign. He being banished, the king shall not live long; and the Earth shall certainly be widowed soon.

sa_verse: विनद्य सुमहानादं श्रमेणोपरताः स्त्रियः। निर्घोषो विरतो नूनमद्य राजनिवेशने॥
en: Having bewailed aloud, by this time have the women got exhausted; and surely the king's mansion is to-day still.

sa_verse: कौसल्या चैव राजा च तथैव जननी मम। नाशंसे यदि ते सर्वे जीवेयुः शर्वरीमिमाम्॥
en: I do not expect that either Kausalyā or the king or my mother is alive. If they live, it can be for this night only.

sa_verse: जीवेदपि च मे माता शत्रुघ्नस्यान्ववेक्षया। दुःखिता या हि कौसल्या वीरसूर्विनशिष्यति॥
en: Even if my mother live seeing Satrughna, that mother of a hero, the afflicted Kausalyā, will (surely) resign her existence.

sa_verse: अतिक्रान्तमतिक्रान्तमनवाप्य मनोरथम्। राज्ये राममनिक्षिप्य पिता मे विनशिष्यति॥
en: Saying, All is lost, All is lost, with his desire unattained, my father having failed to install Rāma in the kingdom, will resign his existence.

sa_verse: सिद्धार्थाः पितरं वृत्तं तस्मिन् काले घुपस्थिते। प्रेतकार्येषु सर्वेषु संस्करिष्यन्ति भूमिपम् ॥
en: Blessed are they that when the time shall come for the same, shall perform the funeral ceremonies of the king, my deceased sire.

sa_verse: रम्यचत्वरसंस्थानां सुविभक्तमहापथाम्। हर्म्यप्रासादसम्पन्नां सर्वरत्नविभूषिताम्॥ गजाश्वरथसम्बाधां तूर्यनादविनादिताम्। सर्वकल्याणसम्पूर्णां हृष्टपुष्टजनाकुलाम् ॥
en: Then shall they with happy hearts range the looking terraces, with the highways laid out orderly, crowned with lordly edifices, adorned with various gems, crowded by cars and elephants and horses, resounding with the notes of trumpets, abounding in auspicious things, filled with fat and contented people, having gardens and pleasure-houses, and possessed of divers classed of men indulging festal mirth.

sa_verse: आरामोद्यानसम्पूर्णां समाजोत्सवशालिनीम्। सुखिता विचरिष्यन्ति राजधानी पितुर्मम ॥ अपि सत्यप्रतिज्ञेन सार्धं कुशलिना वयम्। निवृत्ते समये ह्यस्मिन् सुखिताः प्रविशेमहि ॥
en: Shall we, on the occasion of Rāma's return, with glad hearts peacefully enter the city in company with that one firm in his promise?
