sa_verse: परिदेवयमानस्य तस्यैवं हि महात्मनः। तिष्ठतो राजपुत्रस्य शर्वरी सात्यवर्तत ॥
en: As the magnanimous son of the king was thus lamenting, the night passed away.

sa_verse: प्रभाते विमले सूर्ये कारयित्वा जटा उभौ। अस्मिन् भागीरथीतीरे सुखं संतारितौ मया॥
en: In the morning, with an unclouded sun, both, having made matted locks on the banks of the Bhāgīrathi, crossed the river along with me.

sa_verse: जटाधरौ तौ दुमचीरवाससौ महाबलौ कुञ्जरयूथपोपमौ। वरेषुधीचापधरौ परंतपौ व्यपेक्षमाणौ सह सीतया गतौ॥
en: Wearing matted locks and clad in barks of trees, those persons possessed of mighty strength, like to leaders of elephant-herds, equipped with excellent arrows and quivers and bows, those repressors of foes, expecting (their return form exile), departed with Sītā.

sa_verse: गुहस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा भरतो भृशमप्रियम्। ध्यानं जगाम तत्रैव यत्र तच्छ्रुतमप्रियम्॥
en: Hearing the words of Guha, exceedingly unpleasant, Bharata as soon as he heard them, became plunged in thought.

sa_verse: सुकुमारो महासत्त्वः सिंहस्कन्धो महाभुजः। पुण्डरीकविशालाक्षस्तरुणः प्रियदर्शनः॥ प्रत्याश्वस्य मुहूर्तं तु कालं परमदुर्मनाः। ससाद सहसा तोत्रैर्हदि विद्ध इव द्विपः॥
en: And then taking heart for a while, that tenderfamed one possessed of immense strength, gifted with leonine shoulders and length of arms, having expansive eyes resembling white lotuses, young in years, and endowed with a handsome presence, afflicted with great grief, was overpowered, like an elephant wounded in the heart with a goad.

sa_verse: भरतं मूर्च्छितं दृष्ट्वा विवर्णवदनो गुहः। बभूव व्यथितस्तत्र भूमिकम्पे यथा द्रुमः॥
en: Seeing Bharata deprived of his senses with his countenance covered with pallor, Guha became exceedingly agitated, like a tree during an earth quake.

sa_verse: तदवस्थं तु भरतं शत्रुघ्नोऽनन्तरस्थितः। परिष्वज्य रुरोदोच्चैर्विसंज्ञः शोककर्शितः॥
en: Seeing Bharata in that condition, ſatrughna who was near, taking the former on his lap, began to cry, almost deprived of his senses and oppressed with grief.

sa_verse: ततः सर्वाः समापेतुर्मातरो भरतस्य ताः। उपवासकृशा दीना भर्तृव्यसनकर्शिताः॥ ताश्च तं पतितं भूमौ रुदत्यः पर्यवारयन्। कौसल्या त्वनुसृत्यैनं दुर्मनाः परिषस्वजे॥ वत्सला स्वं यथा वत्समुपगुह्य तपस्विनी। परिपप्रच्छ भरतं रुदती शोकलालसा॥
en: Thereat, all the mothers of Bharata, fasting, undergoing distress, and afflicted with the calamity that had befallen their lord, came forward, and surrounding Bharata, began to lament him fallen on the ground. And the distressed Kausalyā drawing nigh embraced him like a cow approaching her calf; and weeping from excess of grief, spoke to Bharata, saying.

sa_verse: पुत्र व्याधिर्न ते कच्चिच्छरीरं प्रति बाधते। अस्य राजकुलस्याद्य त्वदधीनं हि जीवितम्॥
en: My son, does any malady afflict your body? Now the life of this royal race is, without doubt, in your hands.

sa_verse: त्वां दृष्ट्वा पुत्र जीवामि रामे सभ्रातृके गते। वृत्ते दशरथे राज्ञि नाथ एकस्त्वमद्य नः॥
en: Ráma having gone away along with his brother, I shall, O son, live, seeing you. King Dasaratha having departed this life, you alone are our lord.

sa_verse: कच्चिन्न लक्ष्मणे पुत्र श्रुतं ते किंचिदप्रियम्। पुत्रे वा ह्येकपुत्रायाः सहभार्ये वनं गते॥
en: Have you, my son, heard anything unpleasant concerning Lakṣmaṇa; or the son of that one having an only son,* who has gone to the forest along with his wife? *Meaning herself.

sa_verse: स मुहूर्तं समाश्वस्य रुदन्नेव महायशाः। कौसल्यां परिसान्त्व्येदं गुहं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having taken comfort for a while, that one of high fame weeping, and solacing Kausalya, spoke to Guha, saying.

sa_verse: अन्नमुच्चावचं भक्ष्याः फलानि विविधानि च। रामायाभ्यवहारार्थं बहुशोऽपहतं मया॥
en: I procured for Rāma's use rice and fruits and roots and various kinds of food.

sa_verse: तत् सर्वं प्रत्यनुज्ञासीद् रामः सत्यपराक्रमः। न हि तत् प्रत्यगह्नात् स क्षत्रधर्ममनुस्मरन्॥
en: All these Rāma having truth for his prowess accepted, but observing Ksatriya morality, he did not take them.

sa_verse: नह्यस्माभिः प्रतिग्राह्यं सखे देयं तु सर्वदा। इति तेन वयं सर्वे अनुनीता महात्मना॥
en: O friend, we ought not to take anything: ours is always to give.'

sa_verse: लक्ष्मणेन यदानीतं पीतं वारि महात्मना। औपवास्यं तदकार्षीद् राघवः सह सीतया॥
en: Thus did that magnanimous one beseech us. On the high-souled Lakşmaņa bringing water, Rāghava having drunk it, fasted along with Sītā.

sa_verse: ततस्तु जलशेषेण लक्ष्मणोऽप्यकरोत् तदा। वाग्यतास्ते त्रयः संध्यां समुपासन्त संहिताः॥
en: Then Lakşmaņa drank up the water that remained. Then the three with fixed minds silently performed their adorations to the Twilight.

sa_verse: सौमित्रिस्तु ततः पश्चादकरोत् स्वास्तरं शुभम्। स्वयमानीय बींषि क्षिप्रं राघवकारणात्॥
en: After that, Sumitrā's son prepared a goodly bed for Rāghava, himself bringing Kuća grass.

sa_verse: एतत् तदिगुदीमूलमिदमेव च तत् तृणम्। यस्मिन् रामश्च सीता च रात्रिं तां शयितावुभौ ॥
en: This is the foot of the Ingudī, and this that grass. On it both Rāma and Sītā lay down that night.

sa_verse: शरैः सुपूर्णाविषुधी परंतपः। महद्धनुः सज्जमुपोह्य लक्ष्मणो निशामतिष्ठत् परितोऽस्य केवलम्॥
en: Fastening on his back a pair of quivers filled with arrows furnished with finger-fences, and taking his mighty bow, Lakşmaņa all night kept watch around.

sa_verse: ततस्त्वहं चोत्तमबाणचापभृत् स्थितोऽभवं तत्र स यत्र लक्ष्मणः। महेन्द्रकल्पं परिपालयंस्तदा॥
en: I also taking an excellent bow, remained where remained Lakṣmaṇa, and surrounded by my kindred who stayed there vigilantly, equipped with bows, guarded him that resembled the mighty Indra.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा निपुणं सर्वं भरतः सह मन्त्रिभिः। इङ्गुदीमूलमागम्य रामशय्यामवैक्षत॥
en: Having heard everything, Bharata in company with the counsellors went to the foot of the Ingudi tree and saw the bed of Rāma.

sa_verse: अब्रवीज्जननीः सर्वा इह तस्य महात्मनः। शर्वरा शयिता भूमाविदमस्य विमर्दितम्॥
en: And he said to his mothers, That high-souled one lay down here on the ground during the night, and his limbs pressed this spot.

sa_verse: महाराजकुलीनेन महाभागेन धीमता। जातो दशरथेनोया न रामः स्वप्तुमर्हति॥
en: Begot of that foremost of monarchs, the exalted and intelligent Daſaratha, Rāma does not deserve to sleep on the earth.

sa_verse: अजिनोत्तरसंस्तीर्णे वरास्तरणसंचये। शयित्वा पुरुषव्याघ्रः कथं शेते महीतले॥
en: How can that chief of men having reposed in beds furnished with pillows made of deer-skins and having superb cloths, have lain down on the earth?

sa_verse: प्रासादाग्रविमानेषु वलभीषु च सर्वदा। हैमराजतभौमेषु वरास्तरणशालिषु॥ पुष्पसंचयचित्रेषु चन्दनागुरुगन्धिषु । पाण्डुराभ्रप्रकाशेषु शुकसंघरुतेषु च॥ प्रासादवरवर्येषु शीतवत्सु सुगन्धिषु। उषित्वा मेरुकल्पेषु कृतकाञ्चनभित्तिषु ॥ गीतवादित्रनिर्घोषैर्वराभरणनिःस्वनैः। मृदङ्गवरशब्दैश्च सततं प्रतिबोधितः॥ बन्धिभिर्वन्दितः काले बहुभिः सूतमागधैः। गाथाभिरनुरूपाभिः स्तुतिभिश्च परंतपः॥
en: Always reposing in mansions and in upper apartments paved with silver and gold and supplied with excellent bed-cloths, decked with heaps of flowers, perfumed with sandal and aguru, hued like to pale coulds, resonant with the notes of many parrots, in palaces going before the choicest of their class, ringing with music, and perfumed, like to Meru itself, with their bases composed of gold, Rāma used to be awakened with vocal and instrumental music, the tinklings of elegant ornaments and the peals of goodly mydargas, that subduer of foes being in due season hymned by the eulogists, and friends, bards and genealogists, with worthy ballads and panegyrics. (This assertion of Rāma's lying down on the ground) appears to me incredible:

sa_verse: अश्रद्धेयमिदं लोके न सत्यं प्रतिभाति मा। मुह्यते खलु मे भावः स्वप्नोऽयमिति मे गतिः॥
en: It does by no means look to me like truth. Forsooth, I am amazed. I take it, this is a vision.

sa_verse: न नूनं दैवतं किंचित् कालेन बलवत्तरम्। यत्र दाशरथी रामो भूमावेवमशेत सः॥ यस्मिन् विदेहराजस्य सुता च प्रियदर्शना। दयिता शयिता भूमौ स्नुषा दशरथस्य च॥
en: Verily, no destiny is superior to Time, since Dasaratha's son, Rāma himself, had to repose on the earth, and the beauteous beloved daughter of Videha's king, and the daughter-in-law of Dasaratha, had to lie down on the ground.

sa_verse: इयं शय्या मम भ्रातुरिदमावर्तितं शुभम्। स्थण्डिले कदिने सर्वं गात्रैर्विमृदितं तृणम्॥
en: This was the bed of my brother; on this hard spot did he turn his lovely limbs, and this grass was pressed by them.

sa_verse: मन्ये साभरणा सुप्ता सीतास्मिशयने शुभा। तत्र तत्र हि दृश्यन्ते सक्ताः कनकबिन्दवः॥
en: I think that the graceful Sītā adorned with ornaments slept in this bed, for here and there are scattered particles of gold.

sa_verse: उत्तरीयमिहासक्तं सुव्यक्तं सीतया तदा। तथा ह्येते प्रकाशन्ते सक्ताः कौशेयतन्तवः॥
en: It is clear that Sītā had spread her sheet at this spot, hence it is that fibres of silk are discoverable here.

sa_verse: मन्ये भर्तुः सुखा शय्या येन बाला तपस्विनी। सुकुमारी सती दुःखं न विजानाति मैथिली॥
en: I deem that the bed of her lord appears agreeable to a wife, since a girl tender and in affliction, the chaste daughter of Mithilā experienced no inconvenience (in sleeping in one such).

sa_verse: हा हतोऽस्मि नृशंसोऽस्मि यत् सभार्यः कृते मम । ईदशी राघवः शय्यामधिशेते ह्यनाथवत्॥
en: Ah, I am undone! baleful am I, for it is on my account that Rāghava along with his wife, lay down in such a bed, like one forlorn.

sa_verse: सार्वभौमकुले जातः सर्वलोकसुखावहः। सर्वप्रियकरस्त्यक्त्वा राज्यं प्रियमनुत्तमम्॥ कथमिन्दीवरश्यामो रक्ताक्षः प्रियदर्शनः। सुखभागी न दुःखार्हः शयितो भुवि राघवः॥
en: Born in the imperial race, and capable of conferring happiness on all, the bringer-about of all good, why did Rāghava of dark blue hue like that of a lotus, graceful, and crowned with red eyes, the inheritor of happiness and undeserving of misery, having left his dear consummate kingdom, lie down on the ground?

sa_verse: धन्वः खलु महाभागो लक्ष्मणः शुभलक्षणः। भ्रातरं विषमे काले यो राममनुवर्तते॥
en: Surely the mighty-armed Lakşmaņa graced with auspicious marks is blessed, he who in the time of dire adversity follows his brother Rāma.

sa_verse: सिद्धार्था खलु वैदेही पति यानुगता वनम्। वयं संशयिताः सर्वे हीनास्तेन महात्मना॥
en: And blessed is Videha's daughter who follows her husband into the woods. Bereft of that magnanimous one, we have all been brought into jeopardy.

sa_verse: अकर्णधारा पृथिवी शून्येव प्रतिभाति मे। गते दशरथे स्वर्गं रामे चारण्यमाश्रिते॥
en: The Earth without her helmsman seems to me quite empty, on Dasaratha having ascended the celestial regions and Rāma taken refuge in the wilderness.

sa_verse: न च प्रार्थयते कश्चिन्मनसापि वसुंधराम्। वने निवसतस्तस्य बाहुवीर्याभिरक्षिताम्॥
en: On Rãma having set up his dwelling in the forest, one (like me) does not even mentally covet this earth which had been protected by the immense prowess (of Rāghava).

sa_verse: शून्यसंवरणारक्षामयन्त्रितहयद्विपाम्। अनावृतपुरद्वारां राजधानीमरक्षिताम्॥ अप्रहृष्टबलां शून्यां विषमस्थामनावृताम्। शत्रवो नाभिमन्यन्ते भक्ष्यान् विषकृतानिव॥
en: With her walls undefined, her horses and elephants unrestrained, and her gates left open, the defenceless metropolis deprived of her power, placed in peril and without any protection, is surely not regarded by the enemies, like food mixed with poison.

sa_verse: अद्यप्रभृति भूमौ तु शयिष्येऽहं तृणेषु वा। फलमूलाशनो नित्यं जटाचीराणि धारयन्॥
en: From this day forth I will lie down on the ground, or on the grass, daily subsisting on fruits and roots, and bearing matted locks and a cloth of bark.

sa_verse: तस्याहमुत्तरं कालं निवत्स्यामि सुखं वने। तत् प्रतिश्रुतमार्यस्य नैव मिथ्या भविष्यति॥
en: And for his sake I will in future live happily in the woods. (By my doing so), the promise of that high-minded one shall not be rendered null.

sa_verse: वसन्तं भ्रातुराय शत्रुघ्नो मानुवत्स्यति। लक्ष्मणेन सहायोध्यामार्यो मे पालयिष्यति॥
en: Me residing in the forest in the interests of my brother, Satrughna shall bear company; while my noble one will rule Ayodhyā assisted by Laksmana.

sa_verse: अभिषेक्ष्यन्ति काकुत्स्थमयोध्यायां द्विजातयः। अपि मे देवताः कुर्युरिमं सत्यं मनोरथम्॥
en: The twice-born ones will sprinkle Kākutstha in Ayodhyā. May the deities realize this desire of mine!

sa_verse: प्रसाद्यमानः शिरसा मया स्वयं बहुप्रकारं यदि न प्रपत्स्यत। ततोऽनुवत्स्यामि चिराय राघवं वनेचरं नार्हति मामुपेक्षितुम्॥
en: Propitiated by me personally in various ways with bent head, if he do not consent, then shall I ever stay with Rāghava in the woods. Surely he cannot long persist in putting me off.

sa_verse: व्युष्य रात्रिं तु तत्रैव गङ्गाकूले स राघवः। काल्यमुत्थाय शत्रुघ्नमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having spent the night there on the banks of the Gangā, that descendant of Raghu rising early in the morning, said these words to Satrughna.

sa_verse: शत्रुघ्नोत्तिष्ठ किं शेषे निषादाधिपतिं गुहम्। शीघ्रमानय भद्रं ते तारयिष्यति वाहिनीम्॥
en: O Śatrughna, arise! Why sleepest you? Bring you at once that lord of the Nisādhas, Guha, Good betide you! he will take the army (over the stream.)

sa_verse: जागर्मि नाहं स्वपिमि तथैवार्यं विचिन्तयन्। इत्येवमब्रवीद् भ्राता शत्रुघ्नो विप्रचोदितः॥
en: Thus urged by his brother, Śatrughna said, "Thinking of that noble one (Rāma), I have not slept, but have remained awake in a like manner. *i.e., as you have done.

sa_verse: इति संवदतोरेवमन्योन्यं नरसिंहयोः। आगम्य प्राञ्जलिः काले गुहो वचनमब्रवीत्।।४।
en: As those chiefs of men were thus conversing with each other, Guha appearing in time with joined hands, remarked.

sa_verse: कच्चित् सुखं नदीतीरेऽवात्सी: काकुत्स्थ शर्वरीम्। कच्चिच्च सहसैन्यस्य तव नित्यमनामयम्॥
en: O Kākutstha, have you spent the night happily on the banks of the river? And is it continuous good fortune with you along with your forces?

sa_verse: गुहस्य तत् तु वचनं श्रुत्वा स्नेहादुदीरितम्। रामस्यानुवशो वाक्यं भरतोऽपीदमब्रवीत्॥ सुखा नः शर्वरी धीमन् पूजिताश्चापि ते वयम्। गङ्गां तु नौभिर्बह्वीभिर्दासाः संतारयन्तु नः॥
en: Hearing Guha's speech fraught with affection, Bharata ever obedient to Rāma, spoke on his pare, saying. "Happily have we spent the night; and we have also been well received by you. Now let your servants take us over by means of many boats."

sa_verse: ततो गुहः संत्वरितः श्रुत्वा भरतशासनम्। प्रतिप्रविश्य नगरं तं ज्ञातिजनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereat, hearing Bharata's mandate, Guha, bestirring himself, re-entered the city and addressed his kinsfolk, saying.

sa_verse: उत्तिष्ठत प्रबुध्यध्वं भद्रमस्तु हि वः सदा। नावः समुपकर्षध्वं तारयिष्यामि वाहिनीम्॥
en: Arise you! Awake! May good always attend you! Do you draw up the boats; I shall ferry the forces over.

sa_verse: ते तथोक्ताः समुत्थाय त्वरिता राजशासनात्। पञ्च नावां शतान्येव समानिन्युः समन्ततः॥
en: Thus asked, they arising and bestirring themselves in consequence of the king's command, brought up five hundred boats around.

sa_verse: अन्याः स्वस्तिकविज्ञेया महाघण्टाधराधराः। शोभमानाः पताकिन्यो युक्तवाहाः सुसंहताः॥
en: Others also known by the name of Swastika, bearing large bells on their prows, and banners, well decked out, furnished with oars, and manned by bargemen, with their joints firmly constructed, (were brought up).

sa_verse: ततः स्वस्तिकविज्ञेयां पाण्डुकम्बलसंवृताम्। सनन्दिघोषां कल्याणी गुहो नावमुपाहरत्॥
en: And Guha himself brought a graceful barge called Swastika, covered with pale woollen cloth, and resounding with music.

sa_verse: तामारुरोह भरतः शत्रुघ्नश्च महाबलः। कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च याश्चान्या राजयोषितः॥ पुरोहितश्च तत् पूर्वं गुरवो ब्राह्मणाश्च ये। अनन्तरं राजदारास्तथैव शकटापणाः॥
en: On this boat ascended Bharata, the mighty Śatrughna, Kausalyā, Sumitrā, and other wives of the king. The priests, and preceptors belonging to the Brāhmaṇa order, has already ascended. After (Bharata and others had got up), ascended the wives of auxiliary princes, and cars and provisions were got on board.

sa_verse: आवासमादीपयतां तीर्थं चाप्यवगाहताम्। भाण्डानि चाददानानां घोषस्तु दिवमस्पृशत्॥
en: And the uproar consequent of the troops burning down dwellings, pressing down descents to the river, and loading goods, spread on all sides.

sa_verse: पताकिन्यस्तु ता नावः स्वयं दाशैरधिष्ठिताः। वहन्त्यो जनमारूढं तदा सम्पेतुराशुगाः॥
en: Then those boats hung with pennons, managed by the kinsfolk (of Guha), set off at speed with the teeming folks that had got on board.

sa_verse: नारीणामभिपूर्णास्तु काश्चित् काश्चित्तु वाजिनाम्। काश्चित् तत्र वहन्ति स्म यानयुग्ये महाधनम्॥
en: And some of these were filled with women, and some with horses, and some conveyed cars and cattle of great value.

sa_verse: तास्तु गत्वा परं तीरमवरोप्य च तं जनम्। निवृत्ता काण्डचित्राणि कियन्ते दासबन्धुभिः॥
en: And going to the other bank and landing the crowds on the shore, the friends and slaves (of Guha) while returning, movements (of the boats).

sa_verse: सवैजयन्तास्तु गजा गजारोहैः प्रचोदिताः। तरन्तः स्म प्रकाशन्ते सपक्षा इव पर्वताः॥
en: And elephants graced with flags ing spurred on by their riders, began to cross the stream, appearing like (so many) winged hills.

sa_verse: नावश्चारुरुहुस्त्वन्ये प्लवैस्तेरुस्तथापरे। अन्ये कुम्भघटैस्तेरुरन्ये तेरुश्च बाहुभिः॥
en: Others ascended boats, and others crossed on rafts, others crossed by means of reversed pitchers, and others by their arms alone.

sa_verse: सा पुण्या ध्वजिनी गङ्गां दासैः संतारिता स्वयम्। मैत्रे मुहूर्ते प्रययौ प्रयागवनमुत्तमम्॥
en: Ferried over the Gangā by the servants (of Guha), that beautiful army graced with streamers, at the third Muhurta arrived at the romantic woods of Prayāga.

sa_verse: आश्वासयित्वा च चमूं महात्मा निवेशयित्वा च यथोपजोषम्। मृत्विक्सदस्यैर्भरतः प्रतस्थे॥ स ब्राह्मणस्याश्रममभ्युपेत्य महात्मनो देवपुरोहतस्य। ददर्श रम्योटजवृक्षदेशं महद्वनं विप्रवरस्य रम्यम्॥
en: Having made the army take rest at its ease, and encamped it (at the woods of Prayāga), that magnanimous one, Bharata, for the purpose of seeing the asylum of the ascetic Bharadvāja, went thither, accompanied by Rtvijas and Sadasyas (members).

sa_verse: भरद्वाजाश्रमं गत्वा क्रोशादेव नरर्षभः। जनं सर्वमवस्थाप्य जगाम सह मन्त्रिभिः॥ पद्भ्यामेव तु धर्मज्ञो न्यस्तशस्त्रपरिच्छदः। वसानो वाससी क्षौमे पुरोधाय पुरोहितम्॥
en: Having arrived at (the neighbourhood of) Bharadvāja's asylum, that foremost of men while it was a krosa (to the destination), left his forces behind and went thither, accompanied by his counsellors alone. And leaving his attire and arms, and clad in a silk cloth, that pious one placing the priest in front, went on foot.

sa_verse: ततः संदर्शने तस्य भरद्वाजस्य राघवः। मन्त्रिणस्तानवस्थाप्य जगामानुपुरोहितम्॥
en: Then with the view of seeing Bharadvāja, that descendent of Raghu leaving behind the counsellors also, went in the wake of the priest.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठमथ दृष्ट्वैव भरद्वाजो महातपाः। संचचालासनात् तूर्णं शिष्यानर्घ्यमिति ब्रुवन्॥
en: As soon as Bharadvāja of rigid austerities saw Vasiştha, he at once rose from his seat, saying to him disciples "Arghya!"

sa_verse: समागम्य वसिष्ठेन भरतेनाभिवादितः। अबुध्यत महातेजाः सुतं दशरथस्य तम्॥
en: On being called upon by Vasiştha, that highly energetic one understood that it was Daſaratha's son.

sa_verse: ताभ्यामर्थ्य च पाद्यं च दत्त्वा पश्चात् फलानि च। आनुपूर्व्याच्च धर्मज्ञः पप्रच्छ कुशलं कुले॥ अयोध्यायां बले कोशे मित्रेष्वपि च मन्त्रिषु। जानन् दशरथं वृत्तं न राजानमुदाहरत्॥
en: Having offered them (the guests) water to wash their feet and Arghya, as well as fruits subsequently, that virtuous one (Bharadvāja) successively enquired after the welfare of their (respective) homes; and after that, of the forces, exchequer, friends and counsellors in Ayodhyā. And knowing that Dasaratha had departed this life, he did not ask anything relating to the monarch.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठो भरतश्चैनं पप्रच्छतुरनामयम्। शरीरेऽग्निषु शिष्येषु वृक्षेषु मृगपक्षिषु॥
en: Then Vasiştha and Bharata questioned him as to his welfare, in relation to the body, the (sacrificial) fire, the trees, the beasts and the birds (of the hermitage).

sa_verse: तथेति तु प्रतिज्ञाय भरद्वाजो महायशाः। भरतं प्रत्युवाचेदं राघवस्नेहबन्धनात्॥
en: To all this returning "So it is,” the illustrious Bharadvāja from affection for Rāghava said to Bharata.

sa_verse: किमिहागमने कार्यं तव राज्यं प्रशासतः। एतदाचक्ष्व सर्वं मे न हि मे शुध्यते मनः॥
en: What is the use of your visit here, seeing that you are engaged in the task of governing the kingdom? Do you relate all this to me; my mind is ill at ease.

sa_verse: सुषुवे यममित्रघ्नं कौसल्याऽऽनन्दवर्धनम्। भ्रात्रा सह सभार्यो यश्चिरं प्रवाजितो वनम्॥ नियुक्तः स्त्रीनिमित्तेन पित्रा योऽसौ महायशाः। वनवासी भवेतीह समाः किल चतुर्दश॥ कच्चिन्न तस्यापापस्य पापं कर्तुमिहेच्छसि। अकण्टकं भोक्तुमना राज्यं तस्यानुजस्य च॥
en: That slayer of foes and perpetuator of his race who has been born by Kausalyā, and who along with his wife and brother has been banished to the woods for a long term, that illustrious one who enjoined by his sire in the interests of a woman, has become an inhabitant of the woods for fourteen years, do you, desirous of securely enjoying the kingdon belonging to him as well to his younger brother, intend to do any harm to that sinless one?

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो भरद्वाजं भरतः प्रत्युवाच ह। पर्यश्रुनयनो दुःखाद् वाचा संसज्जमानया॥
en: Thus accosted, Bharata replied to Bharadvāja with tears filling his eyes and his words choked with grief.

sa_verse: हतोऽस्मि यदि मामेवं भगवानपि मन्यते। मत्तो न दोषमाशङ्के मैवं मामनुशाधि हि॥
en: Undone am I if the reverend one also deems me so. Do not apprehend any wrong from me; and do you not reproach me thus.

sa_verse: न चैतदिष्टं माता मे यदवोचन्मदन्तरे। नाहमेतेन तुष्टश्च न तद्वचनमाददे॥
en: Whatever my mother has said in relation to me, does not express my wish, I am not satisfied with that, nor do I endorse her speech.

sa_verse: अहं तु तं नरव्याघ्रमुपयातः प्रसादकः। प्रतिनेतुमयोध्यायां पादौ चास्याभिवन्दितुम्॥
en: I, intending to pacify him, and going to that chief of men, with the view of taking him to Ayodhyā and of paying homage to his feet.

sa_verse: तं मामेवंगतं मत्वा प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि। शंस मे भगवन् रामः क्व सम्प्रति महीपतिः॥
en: Thinking me as already gone, you should show your favour to me. O reverend Sir, tell me where stay at present Rāma, lord of the earth.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठादिभित्विग्भिर्याचितो भगवांस्ततः। उवाच तं भरद्वाजः प्रसादाद् भरतं वचः॥
en: Besought by Vasiştha and the other Rtvijas, the venerable Bharadvāja well pleased, spoke to Bharata.

sa_verse: त्वय्येतत् पुरुषव्याघ्र युक्तं राघववंशजे। गुरुवृत्तिर्दमश्चैव साधूनां चानुयायिता॥
en: foremost of men, even this is worthy of you. Serving superiors, restraint of the senses, and following the pious, are ever found in one sprung in the Rāghava line.

sa_verse: जाने चैतन्मनःस्थं ते दृढीकरणमस्त्विति। अपृच्छं त्वां तवात्यर्थं कीर्तिं समभिवर्धयन्॥
en: I know that even this is your intention, but for the purpose of making your mind all the firmer, I had asked you in this wise; so that your fame might greatly increase.

sa_verse: जाने च रामं धर्मज्ञं ससीतं सहलक्ष्मणम्। अयं वसति ते भ्राता चित्रकूटे महागिरौ॥
en: I also know the righteous Rāma with Sītā and Lakşmaņa. This brother of yours at present stays at the mighty mountain Citrakuta.

sa_verse: श्वस्तु गन्तासि तं देशं वसाद्य सह मन्त्रिभिः। एतं मे कुरु सुप्राज्ञ कामं कामार्थकोविद॥
en: On the morrow you will set out for that region. Do you to-day sojourn here along with your counsellors. O wise one, do this at your pleasure, O you understanding interest and desire.

sa_verse: ततस्तथेत्येवमुदारदर्शनः प्रतीतरूपो भरतोऽब्रवीद् वचः। चकार बुद्धिं च तदाश्रमे तदा निशानिवासाय नराधिपात्मजः॥
en: Thereat the gently-looking and highly famous one said, “Be it so;" and the king's son made up his mind to spend the night in the great hermitage (of the saint).

sa_verse: कृतबुद्धिं निवासाय तत्रैव स मुनिस्तदा। भरतं केकयीपुत्रमातिथ्येन न्यमन्त्रयत्॥
en: Then the ascetic asked Bharata, the son of Kaikeyi, when he had decided for staying there, to receive his hospitality.

sa_verse: अब्रवीद् भरतस्त्वेनं नन्विदं भवता कृतम्। पाद्यमय॑मथातिथ्यं वने यदुपपद्यते॥
en: Thereat Bharata said, “You have for certain done this, (have offered me) water for washing my feet, Arghya, and hospitality with what can be procured in the forest.

sa_verse: अथोवाच भरद्वाजो भरतं प्रहसन्निव। जाने त्वां प्रीतिसंयुक्तं तुष्येस्त्वं येन केनचित्॥
en: Then Bharadvāja as if in jest, said to Bharata, "I know you to be of a complacent disposition; and that you are pleased with anything and everything."

sa_verse: सेनायास्तु तवैवास्याः कर्तुमिच्छामि भोजनम्। मम प्रीतियथारूपा त्वमर्हो मनुजर्षभ॥
en: But I wish to feast your forces. And, O best of men, it behoves you to act in harmony with my desire.

sa_verse: किमर्थं चापि निक्षिप्य दूरे बलमिहागतः। कस्मान्नेहोपयातोऽसि सबलः पुरुषर्षभ॥
en: Why did you coming higher, leave your army at a distance:? And, you man of men, why did you not come accompanied with your forces?

sa_verse: भरतः प्रत्युवाचेदं प्राञ्जलिस्तं तपोधनम्। न सैन्येनोपयातोऽस्मि भगवन् भगवद्भयात्॥
en: Bharata replied to him, saying, “I had not come accompanied with my forces, from fear of you, O reverend Sir.”

sa_verse: राज्ञा हि भगवन् नित्यं राजपुत्रेण वा तथा। यत्नतः परिहर्तव्या विषयेषु तपस्विनः॥
en: O worshipful one, kings and their sons should always carefully avoid the regions of ascetics.

sa_verse: वाजिमुख्या मनुष्याश्च मत्ताश्च वरवारणाः। प्रच्छाद्य भगवन् भूमिं महतीमनुयान्ति माम्॥
en: Choice steeds, men, and mad elephants of the best kind, covering a spacious tract of country, come in my train, respected Sir.

sa_verse: ते वृक्षानुदकं भूमिमाश्रमेषूटजांस्तथा। न हिंस्युरिति तेनाहमेक एवागतस्ततः॥
en: That these might not injure trees or water or the grounds of the cottages pertaining to the asylums, have I come hither alone.

sa_verse: आनीयतामितः सेनेत्याज्ञप्तः परमर्षिणा। तथानुचक्रे भरतः सेनायाः समुपागमम्॥
en: Bring the forces here,", thus desired by the mighty ascetic, Bharata accordingly caused the troops to be brought in.

sa_verse: अग्निशालां प्रविश्याथ पीत्वापः परिमृज्य च। आतिथ्यस्य क्रियाहेतोर्विश्वकर्माणमाह्वयत्॥
en: Then entering the chamber of the sacrificial fire, (Bharadvāja) having sipped water and rubbed his lips therewith, invoked Viśvakarmā for the purpose of entertaining his guest.

sa_verse: आह्वये विश्वकर्माणमहं त्वष्टारमेव च। आतिथ्यं कर्तुमिच्छामि तत्र मे संविधीयताम्॥
en: I invoke Visvakarmā; even Tvasta himself. I wish to entertain guests. Let him accomplish this for me.

sa_verse: आह्वयेलोकपालांस्त्रीन् देवाशक्रपुरोगमान्। आतिथ्यं कर्तुमिच्छामि तत्र मे संविधीयताम्॥
en: I invoke the three guardians of the worlds, gods headed by Sakra. I wish to entertain guests. Let them accomplish this for me,

sa_verse: प्राक्स्रोतसश्च या नद्यस्तिर्यक्स्रोतस एव च। पृथिव्यामन्तरिक्षे च समायान्त्वद्य सर्वशः॥
en: Let those rivers that flow westwards, and those that move tortuously on the earth and in the sky, come hither in a body.

sa_verse: अन्याः स्रवन्तु मैरेयं सुरामन्याः सुनिष्ठिताम्। अपराश्चोदकं शीतमिक्षुकाण्डरसोपमम्॥
en: And let others run Maireya, and others refined wine, and others again cool waters resembling the juice of the sugarcane.

sa_verse: आह्वये देवगन्धर्वान् विश्वावसुहहाहुहून्। तथैवाप्सरसो देवगन्धर्वैश्चापि सर्वशः॥
en: I invoke the celestials and the Gandharvas and Viśvāvasu and Hahā and Huhu and also the divine Apsaras and Gandharvis all.

sa_verse: घृताचीमथ विश्वाची मिश्रकेशीमलम्बुषाम्। नागदत्तां च हेमां च सोमामद्रिकृतस्थलीम्॥
en: And Ghịtâcī, Viśvācī, Misrakesi and Alambuşā; and Nāgadattā and Hemā and Somā residing in the mountain.

sa_verse: शक्रं याश्चोपतिष्ठन्ति ब्रह्माणं याश्च भामिनीः। सर्वास्तुम्बुरुणा सार्धमाह्वये सपरिच्छदाः॥
en: And those ladies that attend Sakra, and those that attend Brahmā. I invoke all these females well attired, in company with Tumuru.

sa_verse: वनं कुरुषु यद् दिव्यं वासोभूषणपत्रवत्। दिव्यनारीफलं शश्वत् तत्कौबेरमिहैव तु॥
en: And let that beautiful forest of Kubera in the north Kuru, having its foliage resembling attires and ornaments, and its fruits debonair damsels, exist even at this very spot.

sa_verse: इह भगवान् सोमो विधत्तामन्नमुत्तमम्। भक्ष्यं भोज्यं च चोष्यं च लेह्यं च विविधं बहु॥ विचित्राणि च माल्यानि पादपप्रच्युतानि च। सुरादीनि च पेयानि मांसानि विविधानि च।॥
en: And here let the worshipful Soma yield me excellent viands of diverse kinds in plenty; things; that may be eaten or enjoyed, sucked or licked; and variegated blossoms growing in the trees, and wines and (other) drinks, and meats of various kinds.

sa_verse: एवं समाधिना युक्तस्तेजसाप्रतिमेन च। शिक्षास्वरसमायुक्तं सुव्रतश्चाब्रवीन्मुनिः॥
en: Thus, furnished with unrivalled ascetic energy, did that anchorite observing excellent vows, express himself orthoepically in consonance with Śikṣā.

sa_verse: मनसा ध्यायतस्तस्य प्रामुखस्य कृताञ्जलेः। आजग्मुस्तानि सर्वाणि दैवतानि पृथक् पृथक् ॥
en: And as he sat with joined hands facing the west with a rapt mind, there came separately all those deities.

sa_verse: मलयं दर्दुरं चैव ततः स्वेदनुदोऽनिलः। उपस्पृश्य ववौ युक्त्या सुप्रियात्मा सुखं शिवः॥
en: And then touching Malaya and Dardura, and laden (with perfumes), a delicious and welcome wind began to blow delightfully, removing sweat.

sa_verse: ततोऽभ्यवर्षन्त घना दिव्याः कुसुमवृष्टयः। देवदुन्दुभिघोषश्च दिक्षु सर्वासु शुश्रुवे॥
en: And the clouds poured down a pleasant shower of blossoms; and from all sides were heard sounds of celestial kettle-drums.

sa_verse: प्रववुश्चोत्तमा वाता ननृतुश्चाप्सरोगणाः। प्रजगुर्देवगन्धर्वा वीणाः प्रमुमुचुः स्वरान् ॥
en: And a rare breeze set in, and the multitudes of Apsaras danced; and the celestials and the Gandharvas sang, and the Vinas let out their notes.

sa_verse: स शब्दो द्यां च भूमी च प्राणिनां श्रवणानि च। विवेशोच्चावचः श्लक्ष्णः समो लयगुणान्वितः॥
en: And the dulcet sounds high and low furnished with Sama and measure, entered the Earth and the firmament and the ears of all creatures.

sa_verse: तस्मिन्नेवंगते शब्दे दिव्ये श्रोत्रसुखे नृणाम्। ददर्श भारतं सैन्यं विधानं विश्वकर्मणः॥
en: When that celestial symphony delightful to the ears of men, had thus developed itself, Bharata's forces saw the workmanship of Visvakarman.

sa_verse: बभूव हि समा भूमिः समन्तात् पञ्चयोजनम्। शाद्वलैर्बहुभिश्छन्ना नीलवैदूर्यसंनिभैः॥
en: That spot widening into a level plain measuring five Yojanas was covered with thick grass resembling blue lapises.

sa_verse: तस्मिन् बिल्वाः कपित्थाश्च पनसा बीजपूरकाः। आमलक्यो बभूवुश्च चूताश्च फलभूषिताः॥
en: And on it stood Bilvas, and Kapitthas, Panasas, citrons, and Amalakas, and mangoes, embellished with fruits.

sa_verse: उत्तरेभ्यः कुरुभ्यश्च वनं दिव्योपभोगवत्। आजगाम नदी सौम्या तीरजैर्बहुभिर्वृता॥
en: From the north Kuru had spread a wood capable of conferring every enjoyment; and a beautiful river coursed through bordered by many a tree.

sa_verse: चतुःशालानि शुभ्राणि शालाश्च गजवाजिनाम्। हर्म्यप्रासादसंयुक्ततोरणानि शुभानि च॥ सितमेघनिभं चापि राजवेश्म सुतोरणम्। शुक्लमाल्यकृताकारं दिव्यगन्धसमुक्षितम्॥ चतुरस्रमसम्बाधं शयनासनयानवत्। दिव्यैः सर्वरसैर्युक्तं दिव्यभोजनवस्त्रवत्॥ उपकल्पितसर्वान्नं धौतनिर्मलभाजनम्। क्लृप्तसर्वासनं श्रीमत्स्वास्तीर्णशयनोत्तमम्॥
en: And there had arisen white edifices having four divisions; and stables for horses and elephants; and grand gateways belonging to places and mansions; and royal residences with graceful gates, resembling white clouds, bearing white garlands and washed with fragrant waters, having four corners, and spacious, fitted up with beds, seats, and vehicles, having every kind of excellent sapid food, stocked with excellent edibles and apparels, having every variety of food, possessing washed and bright vessels, with every description of seats, graceful, and accommodated with choice beds with coverlets.

sa_verse: प्रविवेश महाबाहुरनुज्ञातो महर्षिणा। वेश्म तद् रत्नसम्पूर्ण भरतः कैकयीसुतः॥ अनुजग्मुश्च ते सर्वे मन्त्रिणः सपुरोहिताः। बभूवुश्च मुदा युक्तास्तं दृष्ट्वा वेश्मसंविधिम्॥
en: Permitted by the Maharsi, Kaikeyi's son, Bharata, entered that mansion abounding in gems. And he was followed by the counsellors and the priests; and the latter beholding the arrangements of the palace, were filled with delight.

sa_verse: तत्र राजासनं दिव्यं व्यजनं छत्रमेव च। भरतो मन्त्रिभिः सार्धमभ्यवर्तत राजवत्॥
en: And Bharata in company with the counsellors there went round the august royal seat, the chowri, and the umbrella, worthy of a king. And having bowed down to Rāma, he worshipped that seat.

sa_verse: आसनं पूजयामास रामायाभिप्रणम्य च। वालव्यजनमादाय न्यषीदत् सचिवासने॥
en: And then holding the chowri of hair, he sat down on the seat of a minister.

sa_verse: आनुपूर्व्यानिषेदुश्च सर्वे मन्त्रिपुरोहिताः। ततः सेनापतिः पश्चात् प्रशास्ता च न्यषीदत॥
en: Then the counsellors and priests seated themselves according to rank. And thereafter the general and the protector of the encampment (got themselves seated).

sa_verse: ततस्तत्र मुहूर्तेन नद्यः पायसकर्दमाः। उपातिष्ठन्त भरतं भरद्वाजस्य शासनात्॥
en: Then at Bharadvāja's command, came into being near Bharata streams having Pāyasa for their slime.

sa_verse: आसामुभयतः कूलं पाण्डुमृत्तिकलेपनाः। रम्याश्चावसथा दिव्या ब्राह्मणस्य प्रसादजाः॥
en: And at the pleasure of the Brāhmaṇa, on both their banks arose charming dwellings, covered with pale clay.

sa_verse: तेनैव च मुहूर्तेन दिव्याभरणभूषिताः। आगुर्विंशतिसाहस्रा ब्राह्मणा प्रहिताः स्त्रियः।॥
en: And at that very moment there came twenty thousand women commissioned by Brahma, adorned with divine ornaments.

sa_verse: सुवर्णमणिमुक्तेन प्रवालेन च शोभिताः। आगुर्विंशतिसाहस्राः कुबेरप्रहिताः स्त्रियः॥ याभिर्गृहीतः पुरुषः सोन्माद इव लक्ष्यते।
en: And there came also twenty thousand females sent by Kuvera, decked out in gold and gems, pearls and corals. The sight of these was capable of filling men's mind with enchanting ravishment.

sa_verse: आगुर्विंशतिसाहस्रा नन्दनादप्सरोगणाः॥ नारदस्तुम्बुरुर्गोपः प्रभया सूर्यवर्चसः। एते गन्धर्वराजानो भरतस्याग्रतो जगुः॥
en: And there came from Nandana twenty thousand damsels; and Nārada and Tumburů and Gopa, resembling the sun in splendour. The Gandharba kings began to sing before Bharata.

sa_verse: अलम्बुषा मिश्रकेशी पुण्डरीकाथ वामना। उपानृत्यन्त भरतं भरद्वाजस्य शासनात्॥
en: And Alambusa, and Misrakesi, and Puņdarīka, and Vāmana danced before Bharata, at the command of Bharadvāja.

sa_verse: यानि माल्यानि देवेषु यानि चैत्ररथे वने। प्रयागे तान्यदृश्यन्त भरद्वाजस्य तेजसा॥
en: And those blossoms that are in the celestial regions, and that bloom in the forest of Caitraratha, became visible in Prayāga at the energy of Bharadvāja.

sa_verse: बिल्वा मार्दङ्गिका आसन् शम्याग्राहा बिभीतकाः। अश्वत्था नर्तकाश्चासन् भरद्वाजस्य तेजसा॥
en: And Vilwas did the office of players of the Mặdanga, and Bibhitakas, that of keepers of Soma, and Asvatthas became dancers, at the energy of Bharadvāja.

sa_verse: ततः सरलतालाश्च तिलकाः सतमालकाः। प्रहृष्टास्तत्र सम्पेतुः कुब्जा भूत्वाथ. वामनाः॥
en: And Saralas, Tālas, Tilakas, and Tamālas, being delighted, became hump-backed ones and dwarfs.

sa_verse: शिंशपामलकी जम्बूर्याश्चान्याः कानने लताः। मालती मल्लिका जातियश्चान्याः कानने लताः। प्रमदाविग्रहं कृत्वा भरद्वाजाश्रमेऽवसन्॥
en: And Sinsapās, Āmalakīs, Jambūs and other plants of the forest, wearing the forms of females, stood at the mansion of Bharadvāja.

sa_verse: सुरां सुरापाः पिबत पायसं च बुभुक्षिताः। मांसानि च सुमेध्यानि भक्ष्यन्तां यो यदिच्छति॥
en: Let wine-drinkers drink wine, the hungry eat Pāyasa, and those that are inclined to it, feed on clean meat.

sa_verse: उच्छोद्य स्नापयन्ति स्म नदीतीरेषु वल्गुषु । अप्येकमेकं पुरुषं प्रमदाः सप्त चाष्ट च॥
en: And every seven or eight females taking a man, bathed him on the lovely banks of the rivers.

sa_verse: संवाहन्त्यः समापेतुर्नार्यो विपुललोचनाः। परिमृज्य तदान्योन्यं पाययन्ति वराङ्गनाः॥
en: And damsels furnished with expansive eyes, having wiped the persons (of the bathers), pressed their legs, and those magnificent women made them drink (wine).

sa_verse: हयान् गजान् खरानुष्ट्रांस्तथैव सुरभेः सुतान्। अभोजयन् वाहनपास्तेषां भोज्यं यथाविधि॥
en: And the keepers duly fed excellent horses, elephants, camels and Surabhi's sons (oxen), with their (proper) food.

sa_verse: इखूश्च मधुलाजांश्च भोजयन्ति स्म वाहनान्। इक्ष्वाकुवरयोधानां चोदयन्तो महाबलाः॥
en: And some persons possessed of mighty strength, being directed thereto, fed the bearers of the foremost Ikşvāku warriors with sugercanes, honey, and fried paddy.

sa_verse: नाश्वबन्धोऽश्वमाजानान्न गजं कुञ्जरग्रहः। मत्तप्रमत्तमुदिता सा चमूस्तत्र सम्बभौ॥
en: And the groom forgot his horse, and the elephant-keeper his elephant: that army there became transported with wine and exhilaration.

sa_verse: तर्पिताः सर्वकामैश्च रक्तचन्दनरुषिताः। अप्सरोगणसंयुक्ताः सैन्या वाचमुदीरयन्॥
en: And sumptuously entertained with every enjoyment, with their bodies decked with red sandal, the soldiery in the company of bevies of Apsaras, exclaimed.

sa_verse: नैवायोध्यां गमिष्यामो न गमिष्याम दण्डकान्। कुशलं भरतस्यास्तु रामस्यास्तु तथा सुखम्॥
en: To Ayodhyā will we not go, nor yet to Dandaka. Peace be to Bharata, and may Rāma reap happiness!

sa_verse: इति पादातयोधाश्च हस्त्यश्वारोहबन्धकाः। अनाथास्तं विधिं लब्ध्वा वाचमेतामुदीरयन्॥
en: Thus did footmen and the riders and keepers of elephants and horses, as well as others, having experienced such a state, utter words.

sa_verse: सम्प्रहृष्टा विनेदुस्ते नरास्तत्र सहस्रशः। भरतस्यानुयातारः स्वर्गोऽयमिति चाब्रुवन्॥ नृत्यन्तश्च हसन्तश्च गायन्तश्चैव सैनिकाः। समन्तात् परिधावन्तो माल्योपेताः सहस्रशः॥
en: And men by thousand, exceedinly delighted, sent up shouts. And saying. “This is heaven,” the retiune of Bharata, the soldiers, began to dance and laugh and sing; and bearing garlands, they on all sides rushed by thousands.

sa_verse: ततो भुक्तवतां तेषां तदन्नममृतोपमम्। दिव्यानुद्वीक्ष्य भक्ष्यांस्तानभवद् भक्षणे मतिः॥
en: And beholding the inviting viands resembling ambrosia, they although already fed, became desirous of eating once again.

sa_verse: प्रेष्याश्चेट्यश्च वध्वश्च बलस्थाश्चापि सर्वशः। बभूवुस्ते भृशं प्रीताः सर्वे चाहतवाससः॥
en: And wearing new clothes, all the servants, and maids, and females of the household, became exceedingly well pleased.

sa_verse: कुञ्जराश्च खगोष्ट्राश्च गोऽश्वाश्च मृगपक्षिणः। बभूवुः सुभृतास्तत्र नातो ह्यन्यमकल्पयम्॥
en: And elephants, and asses, camels, kine and horses, and beats and birds, being fed their fill, did not hunger after anything else.

sa_verse: नाशुक्लवासास्तत्रासीत् क्षुधितो मलिनोऽपि वा। रजसा ध्वस्तकेशो वा नरः कश्चिददृश्यत॥
en: And there appeared no one who wore a soiled habit, or who was hungry, or melancholy, or whose hair was covered with dust.

sa_verse: आजैश्चापि,च वाराहैर्निष्ठानवरसंचयैः। फलनिषूहसंसिद्धैः सूपैर्गन्धरसान्वितैः॥ पुष्पध्वजवतीः पूर्णाः शुक्लस्यानस्य चाभितः। ददृशुर्विस्मितास्तत्र नरा लौहीः सहस्रशः॥
en: And the people with wonder beheld before them vessels of precious metals by thousands graceful with chaplets of flowers, filled with essences of fruits and fragrant soups and curries and the flesh of goats and bears, and white rice.

sa_verse: बभूवुर्वनपार्वेषु कूपाः पायसकर्दमाः। ताश्च कामदुधा गावो दुमाश्चासन् मधुच्युतः॥
en: And there were on the skirts of the wood wells having Pāyasa for their slime; and the kine yeilded whatever was asked and all the trees dropped honey.

sa_verse: वाप्यो मैरेयपूर्णाश्च मृष्टमांसचयैर्वृताः। प्रतप्तपिठरैश्चापि मार्गमायूरकौक्कुटैः॥
en: And the tanks were filled with Maireya as well as with clean hot meat of dear, peacocks, and cocks, dressed in pans.

sa_verse: पात्रीणां च सहस्राणि स्थालीनां नियुतानि च। न्यर्बुदानि च पात्राणि शातकुम्भमयानि च।७१।।
en: And there were rice-holders by thousands, and curry-pots by hundred thousands, and golden vessels by Arbudas.

sa_verse: स्थाल्यः कुम्भ्यः करम्भ्यश्च दधिपूर्णाः सुसंस्कृताः। यौवनस्थस्य गौरस्य कपित्थस्य सुगन्धिनः॥ ह्रदाः पूर्णा रसालस्य दनः श्वेतस्य चापरे। बभूवुः पायसस्यान्ये शर्कराणां च संचयाः॥
en: And there were pitchers and water-pots and cleaned vessels for churning curd, filled with the same. And there were tanks of savoury and yellow butter-milk, well-tempered. And there were tanks filled with Rasāla;* and others filled with milk, and with sugar. *A preparation of butter-milk.

sa_verse: कल्कांश्चूर्णकषायांश्च स्नानानि विविधानि च। ददृशुर्भाजनस्थानि तीर्थेषु सरितां नराः॥ शुक्लानंशुमतश्चापि दन्तधावनसंचयान्। शुक्लांश्चन्दनकल्कांश्च समुद्रेष्ववतिष्ठतः॥ दर्पणान् परिमृष्टांश्च वाससां चापि संचयान्। पादुकोपानहं चैव युग्मान्यत्र सहस्रशः॥ आञ्जनीः कङ्कतान् कूर्चाश्छत्राणि च धनूंषि च। मर्मत्राणानि चित्राणि शयनान्यासनानि च॥
en: And men saw sediments, acrid powders and various others things in vessels, for the purpose of bath, on the terraces of tanks; and toothcleaning sticks of Ansuman and other (trees); and white sandal paste lying before; and cleaned mirros; and lots of cloths; and sandals; and shoes in pairs by thousand; and collyrium-pots; and combs; and brushes; and bows at some places; and mail; and various kinds of seats and beds.

sa_verse: प्रतिपानह्वदान् पूर्णान् खरोष्ट्रगजवाजिनाम्। अवगाह्य सुतीर्थांश्च ह्रदान् सोत्पलपुष्करान्। आकाशवर्णप्रतिमान् स्वच्छतोयान् सुखाप्लवान्॥ नीलवैदूर्यवर्णाश्च मृदून् यवससंचयान्। निर्वापार्थं पशूनां ते ददृशुस्तत्र सर्वशः।७९ ।।
en:
And they saw reservoirs for asses, camels, elephants, and horses, with easy descents, filled with water to assist their digestion; and pools furnished with lotuses, of the hue of the firmament, with transparent water, comfortable for ablutions; and tender (plots of) grass all around coloured like blue lapises, to serve as pasture for beasts.
'

sa_verse: व्यस्मयन्त मनुष्यास्ते स्वप्नकल्पं तदद्भुतम्। दृष्ट्वाऽतिथ्यं कृतं तादृग् भरतस्य महर्षिणा॥
en: Witnessing the wonderful hospitality provided by the Maharşi Bharadvāja, like to a vision, the men marvelled.

sa_verse: इत्येवं रममाणानां देवानामिव नन्दने। भरद्वाजाश्रमे रम्ये सा रात्रिर्व्यत्यवर्तत ॥
en: Thus entertained like to celestials in Nandana, they passed the night at the hermitage of Bharadvāja.

sa_verse: प्रतिजग्मुश्च ता नद्यो गन्धर्वाश्च यथागतम्। भरद्वाजमनुज्ञाप्य ताश्च सर्वा वराङ्गनाः॥
en: Then taking the permission of Bharadvāja, all the Gandharvas as well as the superb damsels went away as they had come.

sa_verse: स्तथैव दिव्यागुरुचन्दनोक्षिताः। तथैव दिव्या विविधाः स्रगुत्तमाः पृथग्विकीर्णा मनुजैः प्रमर्दिताः॥
en: And the men remained intoxicated and highly inebriate with the liquor, their persons daubed with goodly aguru and sandal; and the various elegant garlands beautiful to behold, lay by themselves all around, crushed by the people.

sa_verse: ततस्तां रजनी व्युष्य भरतः सपरिच्छदः। कृतातिथ्यो भरद्वाजं कामादभिजगाम ह॥
en: Having passed that night, Bharata having been entertained along with his family, appeared before Bharadvāja, desirous (of seeing Rāma).

sa_verse: तमृषिः पुरुषव्याघ्र प्रेक्ष्य प्राञ्जलिमागतम्। हुताग्निहोत्रो भरतं भरद्वाजोऽभ्यभाषत॥
en: Seeing that foremost of men (standing) with joined hands, Bharadvāja, who had just finished his fire-sacrifice, said.

sa_verse: कच्चिदत्र सुखा रात्रिस्तवास्मद्विषये गता। समग्रस्ते जनः कच्चिदातिथ्ये शंस मेऽनघ॥
en: Have you passed the night pleasantly at our place? And have all your men been pleased with our hospitality? Do you tell me this O sinless one. we

sa_verse: तमुवाचाञ्जलिं कृत्वा भरतोऽभिप्रणम्य च। आश्रमादुपनिष्क्रान्तमृषिमुत्तमतेजसम्॥
en: Thereupon, Bharata bowing down, with joined hands said to that ascetic of excellent energy, as he had issued out of his hermitage.

sa_verse: सुखोषितोऽस्मि भगवन् समग्रबलवाहनः। बलवत्तर्पितश्चाहं बलवान् भगवंस्त्वया॥ अपेतक्लमसंतापाः सुभिक्षाः सुप्रतिश्रयाः। अपि प्रेष्यानुपादाय सर्वे स्म सुसुखोषिताः॥
en: O reverend Sire, I along with all my forces and vehicles have passed (the night) happily. I have been full well entertained by you, o worshipful one possessed of power. And with our languor and heat removed, all sumptuously feasted and comfortably quartered, have passed (the night) agreeably along with our servants.

sa_verse: आमन्त्रयेऽहं भगवन् कामं त्वामृषिसत्तम। समीपं प्रस्थितं भ्रातुमैत्रेणेक्षस्व चक्षुषा।७।।
en: Now, O best of ascetics, I beseech you to look with a propitious eye on me who am bound for my brother's place.

sa_verse: आश्रमं तस्य धर्मज्ञ धार्मिकस्य महात्मनः। आचक्ष्व कतमो मार्गः कियानिति च शंस मे॥
en: And tell me, O you cognizant of morality, how far is it to that high-souled righteous one's asylm, and by what way (shall I reach there)?

sa_verse: इति पृष्ठस्तु भरतं भ्रातुर्दर्शनलालसम्। प्रत्युवाच महातेजा भरद्वाजो महातपाः॥
en: When Bharata eager to see his brother had asked thus, the highly energetic Bharadvāja of rigid austerities answered.

sa_verse: भरतार्धतृतीयेषु योजनेष्वजने वने। चित्रकूटगिरिस्तत्र रम्यनिर्झरकाननः॥
en: O Bharata, two and a half Yojanas hence, embossed in a tenantless wood is the mountain Citrakuta, charming with rocks and woods.

sa_verse: उत्तरं पार्श्वमासाद्य तस्य मन्दाकिनी नदी। पुष्पितदुमसंछन्ना रम्यपुष्पितकानना॥ अनन्तरं तत्सरितश्चित्रकूटं च पर्वतम्। तयोः पर्णकुटीं तात तत्र तौ वसतो ध्रुवम्॥
en: On its northern border flows the river Mandākinſ, covered with flowering trees and with blossoming woods. Beyond the stream is the mountain Citrakuta. There is their thatched cottage, my child; there they abide for certain.

sa_verse: दक्षिणेन च मार्गेण सव्यदक्षिणमेव च। गजवाजिसमाकीर्णां वाहिनीं वाहिनीपते॥ वाहयस्व महाभाग ततो द्रक्ष्यसि राघवम्।
en: Proceeding by the southern way, do you with your forces composed of elephants and horses, O master of the army, turn to the left, O exalted one, and go southwards. By doing so, you will be able to see Rāghava.

sa_verse: प्रयाणमिति च श्रुत्वा राजराजस्य योषितः॥ हित्वा यानानि यानार्दा ब्राह्मणं पर्यवारयन्।
en: Hearing of their departure, the wives of that king of kings, leaving their cars, albeit worthy of them, gathered round the Brāhmaṇa.

sa_verse: वेपमाना कृशा दीना सह देव्या सुमित्रया॥ कौसल्या तत्र जग्राह कराभ्यां चरणौ मुनेः।
en: Lean and trembling and in woeful guise, Kausalyā along with the noble Sumitrā, with her hands took the feet of the ascetic.

sa_verse: असमृद्धेन कामेन सर्वलोकस्य गर्हिता॥ कैकेयी तत्र जग्राह चरणौ सव्यपत्रपा। तं प्रदक्षिणमागम्य भगवन्तं महामुनिम्॥ अदूराद् भरतस्यैव तस्थौ दीनमनास्तदा।
en: Depised universally because of her unrighteous desire, Kaikeyi also bashfully took hold of his feet and, having circumambulated the mighty and venerable anchoret, stood near Bharata in dejection of spirits.

sa_verse: तत्र पप्रच्छ भरतं भरद्वाजो महामुनिः॥ विशेषं ज्ञातुमिच्छामि मातॄणां तव राघव।
en: Then the mighty ascetic Bharadvāja asked Bharata, “O descendant of Raghu, I wish to know particularly about your mothers."

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु भरतो भरद्वाजेन धार्मिकः॥ उवाच प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा वाक्यं वचनकोविदः।
en: Thus accosted by Bharadvāja, the pious Bharata deft in speech said with joined hands.

sa_verse: यामिमां भगवन् दीनां शोकानशनकर्शिताम्॥ पितुर्हि महिषी देवी देवतामिव पश्यसि । एषा तं पुरुषव्याघ्र सिंहविक्रान्तगामिनम्॥ कौसल्या सुषुवे रामं धातारमदितिर्यथा।
en: O reverend sir, she whom you behold depressed and emaciated through grief and fasting, resembling a very goddess, is the noble Queen of my father. This one, Kausalyā, it is that gave birth to that chief of men, having the powerful gait of a lion, Rāma, even as Aditi gave birth to Dhātā.

sa_verse: अस्यावामभुजं श्लिष्टा या सा तिष्ठति दुर्मनाः॥ इयं सुमित्रा दुःखार्ता देवी राज्ञश्च मध्यमा। कर्णिकारस्य शाखेव शीर्णपुष्पा वनान्तरे॥ एतस्यास्तौ सुतौ देव्याः कुमारौ देववर्णिनौ। उभौ लक्ष्मणशत्रुघ्नौ वीरौ सत्यपराक्रमौ॥
en: She that stands at her left hand, in dejected guise, is the noble Sumitrā afflicted with sorrow, the second wife of the monarch, like a Karņikāra bough in a forest, with all its blossoms shrivelled up. The sons of this exalted lady are the youthful and heroic Lakşmaņa and ſatrughna, having truth for their prowess, and resembling celestials in shape.

sa_verse: यस्याः कृते नरव्याघ्रौ जीवनाशमितो गतौ। राजा पुत्रविहीनश्च स्वर्गं दशरथो गतः॥ क्रोधनामकृतप्रज्ञां दृप्तां सुभगमानिनीम्। ऐश्वर्यकामां कैकेयीमनार्यामायरूपिणीम्॥ ममैतां मातरं विद्धि नृशंसां पापनिश्चयाम्। यतोमूलं हि पश्यामि व्यसनं महदात्मनः॥
en: And her in consequence of whose act those foremost of men have come by crushing misfortune, and the king Dasaratha has gone to heaven, deprived of his son, wrathful and proud of her good fortune, setting her heart of wealth, Kaikeyi, dishonourable, although endowed with the semblance of honour, do you know this wicked one intent on sin as my mother. In her do I perceive the root of my mighty misfortune.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा नरशार्दूलो बाष्पगद्गदया गिरा। विनिःश्वस्य स ताम्राक्षः क्रुद्धो नाग इव श्वसन्॥
en: Having said this, with his words choked with emotion, that best of men with his eyes reddened, sighed like an enraged serpent.

sa_verse: भरद्वाजो महर्षिस्तं ब्रुवन्तं भरतं तदा। प्रत्युवाच महाबुद्धिरिदं वचनमर्थवित्॥
en: As Bharata was speaking thus, the great ascetic Bharadvāja gifted with high understanding and knowing interest, answered Bharata, saying.

sa_verse: न दोषेणावगन्तव्या कैकेयी भरत त्वया। रामप्रव्राजनं ह्येतत् सुखोदकं भविष्यति॥
en: O Bharata, you ought not to cast any blame on Kaikeyī. This banishment of the king (Rāma), shall be for the good (of all).

sa_verse: देवानां दानवानां च ऋषीणां भावितात्मनाम्। हितमेव भविष्यद्धि रामप्रव्राजनादिह॥
en: The banishment of Rāma shall surely be for the welfare of the gods and the Asuras and sages of concentrated souls.

sa_verse: अभिवाद्य तु संसिद्धः कृत्वा चैनं प्रदक्षिणम्। आमन्त्र्य भरतः सैन्यं युज्यतामिति चाब्रवीत्॥
en: Thus blest, Bharata saluted the ascetic and went round him, and then summoning the soldiery, said, “Yoke,"

sa_verse: ततो वाजिरथान् युक्त्वा दिव्यान् हेमविभूषितान्। अध्यारोहत् प्रयाणार्थं बहून् बहुविधो जनः॥
en: Thereupon, getting ready excellent steeds and cars decked with gold, many people mounted, with the intention of departing.

sa_verse: गजकन्या गजाश्चैव हेमकक्ष्याः पताकिनः। जीमूता इव धर्मान्ते सघोषाः सम्प्रतस्थिरे॥
en: And male and female elephants with golden chains round their necks, and furnished with banners, with the sounds (of bells), proceeded, like clouds at the end of the summer season.

sa_verse: विविधान्यपि यानानि महान्ति च लघूनि च। प्रययुः सुमहार्हाणि पादैरपि पदातयः॥
en: And then proceeded various kinds of cars great and light of movement and of high value; and the infantry went on foot.

sa_verse: अथ यानप्रवेकैस्तु कौसल्याप्रमुखाः स्त्रियः। रामदर्शनकाक्षिण्यः प्रययुर्मुदितास्तदा॥
en: And on a magnificent car went the ladies headed by Kausalyā, with delighted hearts, eager to see Rāghava.

sa_verse: चन्द्रार्कतरुणाभासां नियुक्तां शिबिकां शुभाम्। आस्थाय प्रययौ श्रीमान् भरतः सपरिच्छदः॥
en: And ascending an elegant car resembling the infant sun or moon, driven by charioteers, went the graceful Bharata well attired.

sa_verse: सा प्रयाता महासेना गजवाजिसमाकुला। दक्षिणां दिशमावृत्य महामेघ इवोत्थितः॥ वनानि च व्यतिक्रम्य जुष्टानि मृगपक्षिभिः। गङ्गायाः परवेलायां गिरिष्वथ नदीष्वपि॥
en: And that mighty host abounding with horses and elephants proceeded, blocking up the southern quarter, like a colossal cloud arisen (in the sky), leaving behind on the other bank of the Gangā woods inhabited by birds and beasts, and coursing by rivers mountains.

sa_verse: सा सम्प्रहृष्टद्विपवाजियूथा वित्रासयन्ती मृगपक्षिसंघान्। महद्वनं तत् प्रविगाहमाना रराज सेना भरतस्य तत्र।४०।।
en: And composed of numbers of elephants and horses in high spirits, that army of Bharata, frightening multitudes of beasts and birds, dived into that mighty forest.

sa_verse: तया महत्या यायिन्या ध्वजिन्या वनवासिनः। अर्दिता यूथपा मत्ताः सयूथाः सम्प्रदुद्रुवुः॥
en: Afflicted by the mighty force on its way with banners (displayed), those inhabitants of the woods, leaders of elephant-herds, took to their heels in company with the herds themselves.

sa_verse: ऋक्षाः पृषतमुख्याश्च रुरवश्च समन्ततः। दृश्यन्ते वनवाटेषु गिरिष्वपि नदीषु च॥
en: And bears and Prsatas and Rurus were on all sides seen in the forest-ways, and on hills and rivers.

sa_verse: स सम्प्रतस्थे धर्मात्मा प्रीतो दशरथात्मजः। वृतो महत्या नादिन्या सेनया चतुरङ्गया॥
en: And that virtuous son of Dasaratha with a glad heart held on this way, surrounded by that vast army consisting of fourfold forces, raising a tremendous uproar.

sa_verse: सागरौघनिभा सेना भरतस्य महात्मनः। महीं संछादयामास प्रावृषि द्यामिवाम्बुदः॥
en: And that army of the high-souled Bharata resembling the waves of the ocean, covered the earth quite, as clouds in the rainy season cover the welkin.

sa_verse: तुरंगौघैरवतता वारणैश्च महाबलैः। अनालक्ष्या चिरं कालं तस्मिन् काले बभूव सा॥
en: And filled with steeds and mighty elephants, the earth at that time for a long while remained invisible.

sa_verse: स गत्वा दूरमध्वानं सम्परिश्रान्तवाहनः। उवाच वचनं श्रीमान् वसिष्ठं मन्त्रिणां वरम्॥
en: And having proceeded a long way, the graceful Bharata, with his bearers extremely tired, said these words to that foremost of counsellors, Vasistha.

sa_verse: यादृशं लक्ष्यते रूपं यथा चैव मया श्रुतम्। व्यक्तं प्राप्ताः स्म तं देशं भरद्वाजो यमब्रवीत्॥
en: From appearances, and from what I had heard, it is evident that we have arrived at that region which Bharadvāja had told us of.

sa_verse: अयं गिरिश्चित्रकूटस्तथा मन्दाकिनी नदी। एतत् प्रकाशते दूरान्नीलमेघनिभं वनम्॥
en: This is the mountain Citrakuta and that the river Mandakinī. And from a distance this forest appears like dark clouds.

sa_verse: गिरेः सानूनि रम्याणि चित्रकूटस्य सम्प्रति। वारणैरवमृद्यन्ते मामकैः पर्वतोपमैः।।।।।
en: And now our elephants resembling hills afflict the romantic sides of Citrakuta.

sa_verse: मुञ्चन्ति कुसुमान्येते नगाः पर्वतसानुषु। नीला इवातपापाये तोयं तोयधरा घनाः॥
en: And the trees scatter blossoms over the sides of the mountain, even as after summer sable clouds pour down showers.

sa_verse: किंनराचरितं देशं पश्य शत्रुघ्न पर्वते। हयैः समन्तादाकीर्णं मकरैरिव सागरम्॥
en: O Śatrughna, behold the realms inhabited by Kinnaras, scattered with steeds, like the main with makaras.

sa_verse: एते मृगगणा भान्ति शीघ्रवेगाः प्रचोदिताः। वायुप्रविद्धाः शरदि मेघजाला इवाम्बरे॥
en: And these herds of deer furnished with celerity, being urged on, roam about like masses of clouds in the sky in autumn, propelled by the winds.

sa_verse: कुर्वन्ति कुसुमापीडाशिरःसु सुरभीनमी। मेघप्रकाशैः फलकैर्दाक्षिणात्या नरा यथा॥
en: And like the people of the south, these warriors bearing shields resembling clouds, are adorning their heads with ornaments of perfumed blossoms.

sa_verse: निष्कूजमिव भूत्वेदं वनं घोरप्रदर्शनम्। अयोध्येव जनाकीर्णा सम्प्रति प्रतिभाति मे॥
en: And this forest, although void of men and dreadful in appearance, at present appears to me like Ayodhyā teeming with people.

sa_verse: खुरैरुदीरितो रेणुर्दिवं प्रच्छाद्य तिष्ठति। तं वहत्यनिलः शीघ्रं कुर्वन्निव मम प्रियम्॥
en: The dust raised by the hoofs (of horses) stands covering the sky: anon the wind bearing it away, compasses my pleasure.

sa_verse: स्यन्दनांस्तुरगोपेतान् सूतमुख्यैरधिष्ठितान्। एतान् सम्पततः शीघ्रं पश्य शत्रुघ्न कानने॥
en: And, O Satrughna, see how fast these cars yoked with steeds and driven by skilful charioteers, are proceeding in the forest.

sa_verse: एतान् वित्रासितान् पश्य बर्हिणः प्रियदर्शनान्। एवमापततः शैलमधिवासं पतत्रिणः॥
en: And behold these beauteous peacocks, which, being frightened, take refuge in the mountain, the home of feathered tribes.

sa_verse: अतिमात्रमयं देशो मनोज्ञः प्रतिभाति मे। तापसानां निवासोऽयं व्यक्तं स्वर्गपथोऽनघ ॥
en: This country appears to me exceedingly lovely. This abode of the ascetics is like to the way to heaven itself.

sa_verse: मृगा मृगीभिः सहिता बहवः पृषता वने। मनोज्ञरूपा लक्ष्यन्ते कुसुमैरिव चित्रिताः॥
en: Male and female deer and Prşatas in the forest, beautiful to look at, appear as if variegated with flowers.

sa_verse: साधु सैन्याः प्रतिष्ठन्तां विचिन्वन्तु च काननम्। यथा तौ पुरुषव्याघ्रौ दृश्येते रामलक्ष्मणौ॥
en: Now let the soldiers go advisedly and search this forest, so that they light upon those chiefs of men, Rāma and Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: भरतस्य वचः श्रुत्वा पुरुषाः शस्त्रपाणयः। विविशुस्तद्वनं शूरा धूमाग्रं ददृशुस्ततः॥
en: Hearing Bharata's speech, persons with weapons in their hands, plunged into the forest, and those heroes presently discovered the top of a (column of) smoke.

sa_verse: ते समालोक्य धूमाग्रमूचुर्भरतमागताः। नामनुष्ये भवत्यग्निव्यक्तमत्रैव राघवौ॥
en: Having seen the top of the (column of) smoke, they came before Bharata and said, "Fire cannot exist where there is no man present. Therefore it is evident that even here are those descendants of Raghu.”

sa_verse: अथ नात्र नरव्याघ्रौ राजपुत्रौ परंतपौ। अन्ये रामोपमाः सन्ति व्यक्तमत्र तपस्विनः॥
en: But if those foremost of men, those subduers of their enemies, the princes, be not here, there are others, being ascetics, resembling Rāma.

sa_verse: तच्छुत्वा भरतस्तेषां वचनं साधुसम्मतम्। सैन्यानुवाच सर्वोस्तानमित्रबलमर्दनः॥
en: Hearing their words acceptable to the pious, that afflicter of hostile ranks, Bharata, said to the entire army.

sa_verse: यत्ता भवन्तस्तिष्ठन्तु नेतो गन्तव्यमग्रतः। अहमेव गमिष्यामि सुमन्त्रो धृतिरेव च ॥
en: Do you carefully stay here: do not proceed further. I myself will go, and Sumantra and Dhrti.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तास्ततः सैन्यास्तत्र तस्थुः समन्ततः। भरतो यत्र धूमाग्रं तत्र दृष्टिं समादधत्॥
en: Thus desired, the troops remained all about that place. Bharata went away, keeping his gaze fixed in the direction of the top of the column of) smoke.

sa_verse: निरीक्षमाणापि च भूमिमग्रतः। बभूव हृष्टा नचिरेण चानती प्रियस्य रामस्य समागमं तदा॥
en: Desired by Bharata to halt, that army, looking in the direction of the smoke, rejoiced soon on learning that the beloved Rāma had arrived (at that place).

sa_verse: बभूव हृष्टा नचिरेण चानती प्रियस्य रामस्य समागमं तदा॥
en: Desired by Bharata to halt, that army, looking in the direction of the smoke, rejoiced soon on learning that the beloved Rāma had arrived (at that place).

sa_verse: दीर्घकालोषितस्तस्मिन् गिरौ गिरिवरप्रियः। वैदेह्याः प्रियमाकाङ्क्षन् स्वं च चित्तं विलोभयन्॥ अथ दाशरथिश्चित्रं चित्रकूटमदर्शयत्। भार्याममरसंकाशः शचीमिव पुरंदरः॥
en: Having spent a long time in that mountain, that lover of hills and woods, Dasaratha's son resembling an immortal, anxious to pleasure Vaidehī as well as to please his own mind, showed the variegated Citrakuța to his wife, like Purandara to Sacī.

sa_verse: न राज्यभ्रंशनं भद्रे न सुहद्भिर्विनाभवः। मनो मे बाधते दृष्ट्वा रमणीयमिमं गिरिम्॥
en: O gentle one, neither deprivation of the kingdom nor separation from friends afflicts my mind on beholding this romantic mountain.

sa_verse: पश्येममचलं भद्रे नानाद्विजगणायुतम्। शिखरैः खमिवोद्विद्धैर्धातुमद्भिर्विभूषितम्॥
en: My gentle one, look but at the hill abounding with flocks of various birds, adorned with summits cleaving the welkin and teeming with mineral substances,

sa_verse: केचिद् रजतसंकाशाः केचित् क्षतजसंनिभाः। पीतमाञ्जिष्ठवर्णाश्च केचिन्मणिवरप्रभाः॥ पुष्पार्ककेतकाभाश्च केचिज्ज्योतीरसप्रभाः। विराजन्तेऽचलेन्द्रस्य देशा धातुविभूषिताः॥
en: And some pares of this monarch of mountains are like silver, and some are blood-red, and some yellow like the hue of Mañjiștha, and some lustrous like sapphires, and some shining like blossoms or crystal or Ketakas, and some blazing like stars or mercury, and some digit in mineral substances.

sa_verse: नानामृगगणैर्दीपितरक्ष्वृक्षगणैर्वृतः। अदुष्टैर्भात्ययं शैलो बहुपक्षिसमाकुलः।७।।
en: And the mountain shines, being filled with divers beasts and multitudes of innocuous tigers, hyenas and bears, and thronged with innumerable birds.

sa_verse: आम्रजम्ब्वसनैलॊधैः प्रियालैः पनसैर्धवैः। अङ्कोलैर्भव्यतिनिशैर्बिल्वतिन्दुकवेणुभिः।।।। काश्मर्यारिष्टवरणैर्मधूकैस्तिलकैरपि। बदर्यामलकैर्नीपैत्रधन्वनबीजकैः॥ पुष्पवद्भिः फलोपेतैश्छायावद्भिर्मनोरमैः। एवमादिभिराकीर्णः श्रियं पुष्पत्ययं गिरिः॥
en: And overspread with mangos, tose-apples, and Asanas, and Lodhras,* Piyālas, jacks, Ankolas, and Bhavyatinisas, and Bilvas, and Tindukas, and bamboos, Kasmaris, Aristas, and Varanas, and Madhukas, sesames, and jujubes, and Amalakas, Nipas, canes, Dhanvanas, and citrons, all in full flower, and bearing fruits, umbrageous and charming, the mountain attains an accession of loveliness. *Symplocos racemosa.

sa_verse: शैलप्रस्थेषु रम्येषु पश्येमान् कामहर्षणान्। किंनरान् द्वन्द्वशो भद्रे रममाणान् मनस्विनः॥ शाखावसक्तान् खङ्गांश्च प्रवराण्यम्बराणि च। पश्य विद्याधरस्त्रीणां क्रीडोद्देशान् मनोरमान्॥
en: And, you gentle one, on the picturesque plateou of the hill behold these intelligent couples of Kinnaras engaged in sport at spots yeilding every enjoyment and look at their swords hung up on the boughs. And see the gorgeous apparel of Vidyadharis, as well as the charming regions in which they sport.

sa_verse: जलप्रपातैरुद्भेदैनिष्पन्दैश्च क्वचित् क्वचित्। स्रवद्भिर्भात्ययं शैलः स्रवन्मद इव द्विपः॥
en: And like an elephant dropping the temporal juice, this hill appears beautiful with cascades, fountains and riles, flowing here and there.

sa_verse: गुहासमीरणो गन्धान् नानापुष्पभवान् बहून्। घ्राणतर्पणमभ्येत्य के नरं न प्रहर्षयेत्॥
en: Whom does not the breeze laden with the perfumes of many a flower, soothing the sense of smell, fill with delight?

sa_verse: यदीह शरदोऽनेकास्त्वया सार्धमनिन्दिते। लक्ष्मणेन च वत्स्यामि न मां शोकः प्रधर्षति॥
en: If, O blameless one, I dwell (here) for many years with you as well as Lakşmaņa, grief cannot overcome me.

sa_verse: बहुपुष्पफले रम्ये नानाद्विजगणायुते। विचित्रशिखरे ह्यस्मिन् रतवानस्मि भामिनि ॥
en: O damsel, I take delight in this picturesque peak abounding in flowers and fruits, and frequented by various birds.

sa_verse: अनेन वनवासेन मम प्राप्तं फलद्वयम्। पितुश्चानृण्यता धर्मे भरतस्य प्रियं तथा॥
en: By this banishment of mine, I have gained two things, my father has maintained his truth in religion, and Bharata has obtained his dear interest.

sa_verse: वैदेहि रमसे कच्चिच्चित्रकूटे मया सह। पश्यन्ती विविधान् भावान् मनोवाक्वायसंमतान्॥
en: O daughter of Videha, are you being pleased on viewing along with me on Citrakuța, various objects grateful to mind, speech and body?

sa_verse: इदमेवामृतं प्राहू राज्ञि राजर्षयः परे। वनवासं भवार्थाय प्रेत्य मे प्रपितामहाः॥
en: O queen, this abode in the forest like to ambrosia has been declared by those royal saints, my ancestors, as working out one's emancipation after death.

sa_verse: शिलाः शैलस्य शोभन्ते विशालाः शतशोऽभितः। बहुला बहुलैर्वर्णैनीलपीतसितारुणैः॥
en: The giant crags of the mountain grace the place all round by hundreds; many and varioushued, blue and yellow and pale and red.

sa_verse: निशि भान्त्यचलेन्द्रस्य हुताशनशिखा इव। ओषध्यः स्वप्रभालक्ष्म्या भ्राजमानाः सहस्रशः॥
en: In the night, the annual herbs by thousands growing on this foremost of hills, shine and become visible by their own lustre, like flames of fire.

sa_verse: केचित् क्षयनिभा देशाः केचिदुद्यानसंनिभाः। केचिदेकशिला भान्ति पर्वतस्यास्य भामिनि॥
en: And, O lady, some parts of the mountain appear like dwellings, and some like gardens, and some, again, consist of single rocks (capable of accommodating numbers of men).

sa_verse: भित्त्वेव वसुधा भाति चित्रकूटः समुत्थितः। चित्रकूटस्य कूटोऽयं दृश्यते सर्वतः शुभः॥
en: And Citrakuța looks as if it had arisen, riving the earth; and the fair front of Citrakuța can be perceived from every point.

sa_verse: कुष्ठस्थगरपुंनागभूर्जपत्रोत्तरच्छदान्। कामिनां स्वास्तरान् पश्य कुशेशयदलायुतान्॥
en: behold the beds of pleasure-seekers, consisting of the petals of lotuses, with Sthagaras, Punāgas and Bhūrjapatras for their coverlets.

sa_verse: मृदिताश्चापिविद्धाश्च दृश्यन्ते कमलस्रजः। कामिभिर्वनिते पश्य फलानि विविधानि च॥
en: And, my wife, behold these lotus-garlands have been crushed and scattered; and the various fruits have been partaken of.

sa_verse: वस्वौकसारां नलिनीमतीत्यैवोत्तरान् कुरून्। पर्वतश्चित्रकूटोऽसौ बहुमूलफलोदकः॥
en: The mountain Citrakuța abounding in fruits and roots and waters, surpasses Kuvera's capital Śakra's city or the north Kurus.

sa_verse: स्त्वया च सीते सह लक्ष्मणेन । रतिं प्रपत्स्ये कुलधर्मवर्धिनी सतां पथि स्वैर्नियमैः परैः स्थितः॥
en: My wife, O Sītā, if in consonance with my own excellent rules, I can, remaining in the path of the pious, pleasantly pass this time along with yourself and Lakşmaņa, then I shall attain the happiness resulting from observing the duties of one's race. or

sa_verse: अथ शैलाद् विनिष्क्रम्य मैथिली कोशलेश्वरः। अदर्शयच्छुभजलां रम्यां मन्दाकिनी नदीम्॥
en: Then going out of the mountain, the Lord of Kosala showed to Mithila's daughter the charming stream Mandākini of excellent waters.

sa_verse: अब्रवीच्च वरारोहां चन्द्रचारुनिभाननाम्। विदेहराजस्य सुतां रामो राजीवलोचनः॥
en: And Rāma, furnished with eyes resembling lotuses, addressed the daughter of king Videha, transcendentally beautiful, with a countenance like the fair moon, saying.

sa_verse: विचित्रपुलिनां रम्यां हंससारससेविताम्। कुसुमैरुपसम्पन्नां पश्य मन्दाकिनी नदीम्॥ नानाविधैस्तीररुहैर्वृतां पुष्पफलदुमैः। राजन्ती राजराजस्य नलिनीमिव सर्वतः॥
en: Behold the river Mandākini, having variegated islets; beautiful; frequented by ducks and cranes; and filled with flowers; covered with diverse trees bearing fruits and flowers; and looking graceful all round like Saughandhikā herself of Kuvera.

sa_verse: मृगयूथनिपीतानि कलुषाम्भांसि साम्प्रतम्। तीर्थानि रमणीयानि रतिं संजनयन्ति मे॥
en: And the waters rendered muddy in consequence of herds of deer drinking of them, as well as the graceful descents to the river, fill me with pleasure.

sa_verse: जटाजिनधराः काले वल्कलोत्तरवाससः। ऋषयस्त्ववगाहन्ते नदीं मन्दाकिनी प्रिये॥
en: And, my beloved sages wearing matted locks and deer-skins, with barks for their sheets, are in season performing their ablutions in the river Mandakini.

sa_verse: आदित्यमुपतिष्ठन्ते नियमादूर्ध्वबाहवः। एते परे विशालाक्षि मुनयः संशितव्रताः॥
en: And observing rules, persons raising up their arms, are worshipping the sun, and, O you of expansive eyes, after these appear ascetics following vows, (engaged if Tapa)

sa_verse: मारुतोद्धृतशिखरैः प्रनृत्त इव पर्वतः। पादपैः पुष्पपत्राणि सृजद्भिरभितो नदीम्॥
en: And the hill seems to dance on the wind swaying the tops of tree; and on both sides of the river, the trees are crowned with flowers and leaves.

sa_verse: क्वचिन्मणिनिकाशोदां क्वचित् पुलिनशालीनीम्। क्पाचेत् सिद्धजनाकीर्णां पश्य मन्दाकिनी नदीम्॥
en: And behold the river Mandakini, somewhere with its waters resembling pearls, and somewhere with islets, and somewhere filled with persons who have attained emancipation.

sa_verse: निर्धूतान् वायुना पश्य विततान् पुष्पसंचयान्। पोप्लूयमानानपरान् पश्य त्वं तनुमध्यमे॥
en: O you of slender waist, behold these hosts of flowers spreading along, and others dipping themselves (in the stream).

sa_verse: पश्यैतद्वल्गुवचसो रथाङ्गाह्वयना द्विजाः। अधिरोहन्ति कल्याणि निष्कूजन्तः शुभा गिरः॥
en: And, O auspicious one, behold these sweetthroated birds, the Chakravākas, getting upon the islets, uttering pleasant notes.

sa_verse: दर्शनं चित्रकूटस्य मन्दाकिन्याश्च शोभने। अधिकं पुरवासाच्च मन्ये तव च दर्शनात्॥
en: Methinks, O beauteous one, the sight of Citrakuta and of Mandakini is even more delightful than life in the metropolis, or the sight of your own self.

sa_verse: विधूतकल्मषैः सिद्धैस्तपोदमशमान्वितैः। नित्यविक्षोभितजलां विगाहस्व मया सह ॥
en: Do you like to her companion perform your bath with me in this stream, whose waters are perpetually stirred by emancipated ones, furnished with asceticism, self-restraint, and control over the senses, who have had their sins removed.

sa_verse: सखीवच्च विगाहस्व सीते मन्दाकिनी नदीम्। कमलान्यवमज्जन्ती पुष्कराणि च भामिनि॥
en: Do you, O Sītā, perform your ablutions in the Mandākinī, scattering at the sometime, O girl, red and white lotuses.

sa_verse: त्वं पौरजनवद् व्यालानयोध्यामिव पर्वतम्। मन्यस्व वनिते नित्यं सरयूवदिमां नदीम्॥
en: Do you, my wife, always consider the wild animals as citizens, the mountain as Ayodhyā, and this stream as the Sarayū.

sa_verse: लक्ष्मणश्चैव धर्मात्मा मनिदेशे व्यवस्थितः। त्वं चानुकूला वैदेहि प्रीतिं जनयती मम॥
en: The virtuous Lakşmaņa is ever obedient to my commands; and, O Videha's daughter, you also are favourable to me.

sa_verse: उपस्पृशंत्रिषवणं मधुमूलफलाशनः। नायोध्यायै न राज्याय स्पृहये च त्वया सह ॥
en: This causes delight in my heart. Bathing thrice (in this river), and living on tasteful fruits and roots, I in your company do not today wish either for Ayodhyā or royalty.

sa_verse: इमां हि रम्यां गजयूथलोडितां निपीततोयां गजसिंहवानरैः। सुपुष्पितां पुष्पभरैरलंकृतां न सोऽस्ति यः स्यान्न गतक्लमः सुखी॥
en: Bathing in this beauteous stream agitated by herds of elephants, whose waters are drunk by elephants, lions and monkeys, which is graced with flowers, and which is decked with multitudes of blossoms, there is not one who has not his fatigue removed, and who does not feel exhilarated.

sa_verse: इतीव रामो बहुसंगतं वचः प्रियासहायः सरितं प्रति ब्रुवन्। चचार रम्यं नयनाअनप्रभं स चित्रकूटं रघुवंशवर्धनः॥
en: Having thus along with his beloved one, spoken variously regarding the stream, that perpetuator of the Raghu race, began to range the charming Citrakuța, resembling the collyrium in hue.

sa_verse: तां तदा दर्शयित्वा तु मैथिली गिरिनिम्नगाम्। निषसाद गिरिप्रस्थे सीतां मांसेन छन्दयन्॥
en: Having showed to Mithila's daughter the river belonging to the mountain, Rāma sat down on its table-land and, gratifying Sita with meat, said to her.

sa_verse: इदं मेध्यमिदं स्वादु निष्टप्तमिदमग्निना। एवमास्ते स धर्मात्मा सीतया सह राघवः॥ तथा तत्रासतस्तस्य भरतस्योपयायिनः। सैन्यरेणुश्च शब्दश्च प्रादुरास्तां नभस्पृशौ ॥
en: This clean meat tastes sweet, having been roasted in fire. The righteous Rāghava was thus seated in company with Sītā, when Bharata's followers came there. And filling the heavens, there arose clouds of dust raised by the army as well as an uproar.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे त्रस्ताः शब्देन महता ततः। अर्दिता यूथपा मत्ताः सयुथाद् दुद्रुवुर्दिशः॥
en: And at this time mad leaders of elephant-herds accompanied by the latter, scared by the terrible tumult, scudded on all sides.

sa_verse: स तं सैन्यसमुद्भूतं शब्दं शुश्राव राघवः। तांश्च विप्रदुतान् सर्वान् यूथपानन्ववैक्षत ॥
en: And Rāghava heard the noise raised by the army; and also saw all those leaders of elephantherds scampering away.

sa_verse: तांश्च विप्रदुतान् दृष्ट्वा तं च श्रुत्वा महास्वनम्। उवाच रामः सौमित्रिं लक्ष्मणं दीप्ततेजसम्॥
en: And having seen them running away and heard that hubbub, Rāma spoke to Sumitrā's son, Lakşmaņa of flaming energy.

sa_verse: हन्त लक्ष्मण पश्येह सुमित्रा सुप्रजास्त्वया। भीमस्तनितगम्भीरं तुमुलः श्रूयते स्वनः॥ गजयूथानि वारण्ये महिषा वा महावने। वित्रासिता मृगाः सिंहै: सहसा प्रदुता दिशः।।।।। राजा वा राजपुत्रो वा मृगयामटते वने। अन्यद्वा श्वापदं किंचित् सौमित्रे ज्ञातुमर्हसि॥
en: Ha! Lakşmaņa, in whom Şumitrā has been blest with a worthy son, hark! A tremendous and dreadful uproar resembling the rumbling of clouds is being heard; and in the woods and mighty forest, deer and buffaloes and herds of elephants being accompanied by lions are suddenly scampering away in all directions. O Sumitrā's son, it behoves you to learn whether any king or prince is hunting in the forest, or any ferocious beast is (ravaging the woods).

sa_verse: सुदुश्चरो गिरिश्चायं पक्षिणामपि लक्ष्मण। सर्वमेतद् यथातत्त्वमभिज्ञातुमिहार्हसि॥
en: O Lakşmana, this mountain is even incapable of being frequented by fowls. Therefore it behoves you to learn all about it, as has actually been the case.

sa_verse: स लक्ष्मणः संत्वरितः सालमारुह्य पुष्पितम्। प्रेक्षमाणो दिशः सर्वाः पूर्वां दिशमवैक्षत॥
en: Thereat, hurriedly ascending a flowering Säla tree, Laksmana surveying all sides, fixed his gaze on the east. to

sa_verse: उदङ्मुखः प्रेक्षमाणो ददर्श महतीं चमुम्। गजाश्वरथसम्बाधां यत्तैर्युक्तां पदातिभिः॥
en: And viewing the east, he discovered a mighty army, abounding with elephants, horses and cars, and consisting of equipped infantry.

sa_verse: तामश्वरथसम्पूर्णां रथध्वजविभूषिताम्। शशंस सेनां रामाय वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereupon, Lakşmaņa communicated Rāma tidings concerning that army filled with elephants and steeds, and decked with cars and streamers; and spoke to Rāma, saying.

sa_verse: अग्नि संशमयत्वार्यः सीता च भजतां गुहाम्। सज्जं कुरुष्व चापं च शरांश्च कवचं तथा॥
en: O noble one, do you put out the fire; and let Sītā go into the cave. And do you string your bow and make ready the arrows and don on your mail.

sa_verse: तं रामः पुरुषव्याघ्रो लक्ष्मणं प्रत्युवाच ह। अङ्गावेक्षस्व सौमित्रे कस्येमां मन्यसे चमूम्॥
en: Thereat, Rāma-chief of men, answered Lakş mana, saying O son of Sumitra, do you (first) ascertain whom this host belongs to.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण लक्ष्मणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। दिधक्षन्निव तां सेनां रुषितः पावको यथा॥
en: Thus accosted by Rama, Laksmana, as if consuming that army by his wrath resembling fire, said.

sa_verse: सम्पन्नं राज्यमिच्छंस्तु व्यक्तं प्राप्याभिषेचनम्। आवां हन्तुं समभ्येति कैकेय्या भरतः सुतः॥
en: Having got himself installed, Kaikeyi's son, Bharata, anxious to render his royalty perfectly safe, is coming hither for the purpose of slaying us both.

sa_verse: एष वै सुमहाञ्छ्रीमान् विटपी सम्प्रकाशते। विराजत्युज्ज्वलस्कन्धः कोविदारध्वजो रथे।॥
en: Yonder is seen the graceful tree. By the same apr-ars on the car the Kovidāra standard, having a shining top.

sa_verse: भजन्त्येते यथाकाममश्वानारुह्य शीघ्रगान्। एते भ्राजन्ति संहृष्टा गजानारुह्य सादिनः॥
en: And men riding swift coursers are at their riders, riding on elephants, are also cheerfully directing their course hither.

sa_verse: गृहीतधनुषावाचां गिरिं वीर श्रयावहे। अथवेहैव तिष्ठावः संनद्धाबुद्यतायुधौ॥ अपि नौ वशमागच्छेत् कोविदारध्वजो रणे। अपि द्रक्ष्यामि भरतं यत्कृते व्यसनं महत्॥ त्वया राघव सम्प्राप्तं सीतया च मया तथा। यनिमित्तं भवान् राज्याच्च्युतो राघव शाश्वतात्॥
en: Let us, O hero, taking our bows, station ourselves on this hill. I will (to day) see Bharata, for whom we have come by this mighty misfortune. Or let us rather remain where we are accoutred in mail and with our arms ready. Shall he of the Kovidāra banner in conflict come under our sway? O hero, we have met with that foe of ours for whom, O Rāghava, you, Sītā and myself have (experienced such misery), for whom, O Rāghava, you have been cast off from the entire kingdom.

sa_verse: सम्प्राप्तोऽयमरिर्वीर भरतो वध्य एव हि। भरतस्य वधे दोषं नाहं पश्यामि राघव॥ पूर्वापकारिणं हत्वा न ह्यधर्मेण युज्यते। पूर्वापकारी भरतस्त्यागे धर्मश्च राघव॥
en: Surely, Bharata should be slain by me. O Rāghava, fault find I none in slaying Bharata; slaying a former wronger, one does not reap unrighteousness. O Raghava, there is religious merit to be reaped by slaying Bharata, who had formerly done us wrong.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन् निहते कृत्स्नामनुशाधि वसुंधराम्। अद्य पुत्रं हतं संख्ये कैकेयी राज्यकामुका॥ मया पश्येत् सुदुःखार्ता हस्तिभिन्नमिव द्रुमम्।
en: On this one being slain, you will rule the entire earth. To day shall Kaikeyi lusting after the kingdom, with grief behold her son slain in battle by me, like a tree riven by an elephants.

sa_verse: कैकेयीं च वधिष्यामि सानुबन्धां सबान्धवाम्॥ कलुषेणाद्य महता मेदिनी परिमुच्यताम्।
en: I will also kill Kaikeyi along (with the humpbacked one, and her friends. Let the earth today be freed from foul sins.

sa_verse: अद्येमं संयतं क्रोधमसत्कारं च मानद॥ मोक्ष्यामि शत्रुसैन्येषु कक्षेष्विव हुताशनम्।
en: To day will I, O bestower of honour, vent my restrained ire and bad blood upon the hostile hosts, like fire set to a heap of hay.

sa_verse: अद्यैव चित्रकूटस्य काननं निशितैः शरैः॥ छिन्दञ्छत्रुशरीराणि करिष्ये शोणितोक्षितम्।
en: To day with sharpened shafts will I cut the bodies of the hostile hosts and drench the woods of Citrakuta with their blood.

sa_verse: शरैर्निभिन्नहृदयान् कुञ्जरांस्तुरगांस्तथा॥ श्वापदाः परिकर्षन्तु नरांश्च निहतान् मया।
en: The ferocious beasts shall drag away elephants and steeds and men slaughtered by me with arrows penetrating into their hearts.

sa_verse: शराणां धनुषश्चाहमनृणोऽस्मिन् महावने। ससैन्यं भरतं हत्वा भविष्यामि न संशयः॥
en: I will, without doubt, in this forest pay the debt I owe to my bow and arrows, by slaying Bharata together with all his forces.

sa_verse: सुसंरब्धं तु भातं लक्ष्मणं क्रोधमूर्छितम्। रामस्तु परिसान्त्व्याथ वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Pacifying Sumitrā's son, Laksmana, transported with rage and eager for encounter, Rama addressed him, saying.

sa_verse: किमत्र धनुषा कार्यमसिना वा सचर्मणा। महाबले महोत्साहे भरते स्वयमागते ॥
en: When the mighty Bharata possessed of high spirits has himself come here, what is the use of the bow or the sword or the shield?

sa_verse: पितुः सत्यं प्रतिश्रुत्य हत्वा भरतमाहवे। किं करिष्यामि राज्येन सापवादेन लक्ष्मण॥
en: Having promised to maintain my father's truth, what, O Laksmana, shall I, having slain Bharata in battle, do with the kingdom with a stain attached to it?

sa_verse: यद् द्रव्यं बान्धवानां वा मित्राणां वा क्षये भवेत्। नाहं तत् प्रतिगृह्णीयां भक्ष्यान् विषकृतानिव॥
en: That thing which falls to my lot on the destruction of friends and adherents I never accept, even like food mixed with poison.

sa_verse: धर्ममर्थं च कामं च पृथिवीं चापि लक्ष्मण। इच्छामि भवतामर्थे एतत् प्रतिश्रृणोमि ते॥
en: I swear to you, I wish for the (possession of) righteousness, interest, desire and the earth, in your interests alone, O Laksmana.

sa_verse: भ्रातॄणां संग्रहार्थं च सुखार्थं चापि लक्ष्मण। राज्यमप्यहमिच्छामि सत्येनायुधमालभे॥
en: O Lakşmaņa, I swear by my weapon that it is for the maintenance and happiness of my brothers that I wish for the kingdom.

sa_verse: नेयं मम मही सौम्य दुर्लभा सागराम्बरा। नहीच्छेयमधर्मेण शक्रत्वमपि लक्ष्मण॥
en: O mild one, this Earth herself is not difficult of being attained by me; but, O Laksmana, I do not through unrighteousness wish to possess myself of Sakra's state.

sa_verse: यद् विना भरतं त्वां च शत्रुघ्नं वापि मानद। भवेन्मम सुखं किंचिद् भस्म तत् कुरुतां शिखी॥
en: May fire reduce to ashes any happiness of mine that, O bestower of honour, happens to be dissevered from Bharata, or yourself, or Satrughna.

sa_verse: मन्येऽहमागतोऽयोध्यां भरतो भ्रातृवत्सलः। मम प्राणैः प्रियतरः कुलधर्ममनुस्मरन्॥ श्रुत्वा प्रवाजितं मां हि जटावल्कलधारिणम्। जानक्या सहितं वीर त्वया च पुरुषोत्तम॥ स्नेहेनाक्रान्तहृदयः शोकेनाकुलितेन्द्रियः। द्रष्टुमभ्यागतो ह्येष भरतो नान्यथाऽऽगतः॥
en: I think Bharata attached to his brothers had come to Ayodhyā; and then, following the morality regulating the race, that one dearer to me than life, hearing of me banished, bearing matted locks and bark, together with Jānakī, O hero, and yourself, you foremost of men, has, with his heart surcharged with affection, and his senses overwhelmed by grief, come hither for seeing us. He cannot have come on any other account.

sa_verse: अम्बां च केकयीं रुष्य भरतश्चाप्रियं वदन्। प्रसाद्य पितरं श्रीमान् राज्यं मे दातुमागतः॥
en: And having got wroth with Kaikeyī, and given her rough speech, that auspicious one, having gratified my sire, has come hither to make the kingdom over to me.

sa_verse: प्राप्तकालं यथैषोऽस्मान् भरतो द्रष्टुमर्हति। अस्मासु मनसाप्येष नाहितं किंचिदाचरेत्॥
en: And the season being fit. meet it is that Bharata should see us. He does not even in thought act against us.

sa_verse: विप्रियं कृतपूर्वं ते भरतेन कदा नु किम्। ईदृशं वा भयं तेऽद्य भरतं यद् विशङ्कसे॥
en: Has ere this Bharata done you any bad turn? Or did he tell you any thing so alarming that today you stand in fear of him?

sa_verse: नहि ते निष्ठुरं वाच्यो भरतो नाप्रियं वचः। अहं ह्यप्रियमुक्तः स्यां भरतस्याप्रिये कृते॥
en: Certainly you ought not to say cruel or unpleasant words in relation to Bharata, if wrong be done to Bharata, I shall consider myself as wornged.

sa_verse: कथं नु पुत्राः पितरं हन्युः कस्यांचिदापदि। भ्राता वा भ्रातरं हन्यात् सौमित्रे प्राणमात्मनः॥
en: Do sons, in times of peril, ever slay their father, or brothers their brother like to their life, O son of Sumitrā?

sa_verse: यदि राज्यस्य हेतोस्त्वमिमां वाचं प्रभाषसे। वक्ष्यामि भरतं दृष्ट्वा राज्यमस्मै प्रदीयताम्॥
en: If you speak thus for the sake of the monarchy, on seeing Bharata, I will say to him, "Make over the kingdom to this one/"

sa_verse: उच्यमानो हि भरतो मया लक्ष्मण तद्वचः। ' राज्यमस्मै प्रयच्छेति बाढमित्येव मंस्यते॥
en: Earnestly exhorted by me, saying, “Do you place the kingdom in his hands', he will say, "Very well'."

sa_verse: तथोक्तो धर्मशीलेन भ्रात्रा तस्य हिते रतः। लक्ष्मणः प्रविवेशेव स्वानि गात्राणि लज्जया॥
en: Thus addressed by his brother of a virtuous disposition, Laksmana ever engaged in Rama's good, from shame seemed to enter into his body.

sa_verse: तद्वाक्यं लक्ष्मणः श्रुत्वा व्रीडितः प्रत्युवाच ह। त्वां मन्ये द्रष्टुमायातः पिता दशरथः स्वयम्॥
en: And hearing those words, Lakşmaņa affected 'by shame, answered, I conceive our father Daśaratha himself has come to see you.

sa_verse: व्रीडितं लक्ष्मणं दृष्ट्वा राघवः प्रत्युवाच ह। एष मन्ये महाबाहुरिहास्मान् द्रष्टुमागतः॥ अथवा नौ ध्रुवं मन्ये मन्यमानः सुखोचितौ। वनवासमनुध्याय गृहाय प्रतिनेष्यति॥
