en: And finding Lakşmaņa overcome with shame, Rāghava replied, I think that mighty-armed one has come hither to see us; or I take it for certain that, considering that we are fit for ease only, and taking our banishment to heart, he will take us home.

sa_verse: इमां चाप्येष वैदेहीमत्यन्तसुखसेविनीम्। पिता मे राघवः श्रीमान् वनादादाय यास्यति॥
en: Or it may be that graceful descendant of Raghu, my father, will go away, taking form the forest Videha's daughter brought up in the lap of luxury.

sa_verse: एतौ तौ सम्प्रकाशेते गोत्रवन्तौ मनोरमौ। वायुवेगसमौ वीरौ जवनौ तुरगोत्तमौ॥
en: There are seen these graceful and well-bred steeds, courageous, swift, and furnished with the speed of the wind, the best of horses.

sa_verse: स एष सुमहाकायः कम्पते वाहिनीमुखे। नागः शचुंजयो नाम वृद्धस्तातस्य धीमतः॥
en: And this huge elephant belonging to our aged sire, named Satrunjaya, proceeds in the van of the army.

sa_verse: न तु पश्यामि तच्छत्रं पाण्डुरं लोकविश्रुतम्। पितुर्दिव्यं महाभाग संशयो भवतीह मे॥
en: But, you exalted one, I do not see the splendid white umbrella of our father known among men. Therefore, doubts arise in my mind.

sa_verse: वृक्षाग्रादवरोह त्वं कुरु लक्ष्मण मद्वचः। इतीव रामो धर्मात्मा सौमित्रिं तमुवाच ह॥ अवतीर्य तु सालाग्रात् तस्मात् स समितिंजयः। लक्ष्मणः प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा तस्थौ रामस्य पार्श्वतः॥
en: Do you descend from the top of the tree, O Laksmana. Do my bidding. Thus did the righteous Rāma accosts Sumitra's Descending from the top of the Sāla tree, that conqueror in battles, Lakşmaņa, stood by Rāma with joined hands. son.

sa_verse: भरतेनाथ संदिष्टा सम्पर्दो न भवेदिति। समन्तात् तस्य शैलस्य सेना वासमकल्पयत्॥
en: Commanded by Bharata, Let not (Rāma's asylum) by trampled by the forces, the army took up its quarters at a distance from the hill.

sa_verse: अध्यर्धमिक्ष्वाकुचमूर्योजनं पर्वतस्य ह। पार्वे न्यविशदावृत्य गजवाजिनराकुला॥
en: And the Ikşvāku host filled with elephants and steeds covering half a Yojana, encamped at the side of the mountain.

sa_verse: सा चित्रकूटे भरतेन सेना धर्म पुरस्कृत्य विधूय दर्पम्। प्रसादनार्थं रघुनन्दनस्य विरोचते नीतिमता प्रणीता॥
en: And keeping morality in their fore-front, and renouncing pride, the disciplined forces schooled by Bharata in view of pleasing that descendant of Raghu (Rāma), stayed in Citrakuța.

sa_verse: निवेश्य सेनां तु विभुः पद्भ्यां पादवतां वरः। अभिगन्तुं स काकुत्स्थमियेष गुरुवर्तकम्॥ निविष्टमात्रे सैन्ये तु यथोद्देशं विनीतवत्। भरतो भ्रातरं वाक्यं शत्रुघ्नमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having stationed his troops, that best of men, the master, became anxious to go on foot to the Käkutstha honouring his superiors. And the forces having with humility took up the quarters assigned, Bharata addressed his brother, Satrughna, saying.

sa_verse: क्षिप्रं वनमिदं सौम्य नरसंधैः समन्ततः। लुब्धैश्च सहितैरेभिस्त्वमन्वेषितुमर्हसि॥
en: O mild one, it behoves you at once to search this forest all round in company with a large body of men as well as these Nişadas.

sa_verse: गुहो ज्ञातिसहस्रेण शरचापासिपाणिना। समन्वेषतु काकुत्स्थावस्मिन् परिवृतः स्वयम्॥
en: And let Guha himself accompanied by a thousand of his kindred bearing in their hands arrows and bows and scimitars, also search for the Käkutstha in this forest.

sa_verse: अमात्यैः सह पौरैश्च गुरुभिश्च द्विजातिभिः। सह सर्वं चरिष्यामि पद्भ्यां परिवृतः स्वयम्॥
en: Accompanied by by counsellors, citizens, preceptors and twice-born ones, I will on foot range every direction.

sa_verse: यावन्न रामं द्रक्ष्यामि लक्ष्मणं वा महाबलम्। वैदेहीं वा महाभागां न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति॥
en: So long as I do not see Răma, or the mighty Lakṣmaṇa, or the highly exalted daughter of Videha, I shall not attain peace of mind.

sa_verse: यावन्न चन्द्रसंकाशं तद् द्रक्ष्यामि शुभाननम्। भ्रातुः पद्मविशालाक्षं न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति॥
en: And so long as I do not see that face of his fair as the moon, with eyes expansive like lotuses, I shall not attain peace of mind.

sa_verse: सिद्धार्थः खलु सौमित्रिर्यश्चन्द्रविमलोपमम्। मुखं पश्यति रामस्य राजीवाक्षं महाद्युति॥
en: Surely, Sumitra's son, Laksmana, who beholds Rāma's countenance like the stainless moon, with eyes resembling lotuses, and beaming in effulgence, is blessed.

sa_verse: यावन्न चरणौ भ्रातुः पार्थिवव्यञ्जनान्वितौ। शिरसा प्रग्रहीष्यामि न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति॥
en: So long as I do not take on my head those feet of my brother bearing royal marks, I shall not attain peace of mind.

sa_verse: यावन्न राज्ये राज्याहः पितृपैतामहे स्थितः। अभिषिक्तो जलक्लिन्नो न मे शान्तिर्भविष्यति॥
en: So long as established in the kingdom of his father and grandfather, that one worthy of the monarchy is not sprinkled with the water on installation, I shall not attain peace of mind.

sa_verse: कृतकृत्या महाभागा वैदेही जनकात्मजा। भर्तारं सागरान्तायाः पृथिव्या यानुगच्छति॥
en: Blessed is Vaidehī, the eminently virtuous daughter of Janaka, who follows the lord of this Earth bounded by the seas.

sa_verse: सुशुभश्चित्रकूटोऽसौ गिरिराजसमो गिरिः। यस्मिन् वसति काकुत्स्थः कुबेर इव नन्दने॥
en: This Citrakuța is fortunate, this hill like to the monarch of mountains, in which resides Kākutstha, like Kuvera in Nandana.

sa_verse: कृतकार्यमिदं दुर्गवनं व्यालनिषेवितम्। यदध्यास्ते महाराजो रामः शस्त्रभृतां वरः॥
en: And blessed is this deep forest inhabited by ferocious animals, where abide the great king Rāma, the foremost of those bearing arms.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महाबाहुर्भरतः पुरुषर्षभः। पद्भ्यामेव महातेजा प्रविवेश महद् वनम् ॥
en: Having said this, that best of men, the mightyarmed and highly energetic Bharata, on foot entered the vast forest.

sa_verse: स तानि दुमजालानि जातानि गिरिसानुषु। पुष्पिताग्राणि मध्येन जगाम वदतां वरः॥
en: And that best of speakers went over the mountain-side through ranks of blossoming trees.

sa_verse: स गिरेश्चित्रकूटस्य सालमारुह्य सत्वरम्। रामाश्रमगतस्याग्नेर्ददर्श ध्वजमुच्छ्रितम्॥
en: Then swiftly ascending a Sāla on Citrakuta, he descried the high column of smoke belonging to Răma's asylum.

sa_verse: तं दृष्ट्वा भरतः श्रीमान् मुमोद सहबान्धवः। अत्र राम इति ज्ञात्वा गतः पारमिवाम्भसः॥
en: Having seen this, like one that has crossed over the ocean, the graceful Bharata, concluding that Rāma was there, rejoiced exceedingly along with his friends.

sa_verse: स चित्रकूटे तु गिरौ निशम्य रामाश्रमं पुण्यजनोपपत्रम्। गुहेन सार्धं त्वरितो जगाम पुनर्निवेश्यैव चमूं महात्मा।।
en: Having heard that Rama's asylum containing pious people lay in Citrakuta, that high-souled one again stationing his forces, speedily went (in that direction), accompanied by Guha.

sa_verse: निविष्टायां तु सेनायामुत्सुको भरतस्ततः। जगाम भ्रातरं द्रष्टुं शत्रुघ्नमनुदर्शयन्॥
en: Having quartered his forces, Bharata eager (to go to Rāma's place), went to see his brother, showing to Satrughna the signs of Rāma's abode being in the vicinity.

sa_verse: ऋषि वसिष्ठं संदिश्य मातृमें शीघ्रमानय। इति त्वरितमग्रे स जगाम गुरुवत्सलः॥
en: And having desired Vasistha, saying, “Bring my mothers without delay," that one attached to his superiors went before.

sa_verse: सुमन्त्रस्त्वपि शत्रुघ्नमदूरादन्वपद्यत। रामदर्शनजस्तों भरतस्येव तस्य च॥
en: And eager to see Rāma even like Bharata himself, Sumantra followed Bharata at a short distance.

sa_verse: गच्छन्नेवाथ भरतस्तापसालयसंस्थिताम्। भ्रातुः पर्णकुटी श्रीमानुटजं च ददर्श ह॥
en: As Bharata passed on, he observed a neat cottage of leaves stationed among the asylums of anchorets, furnished with a portion having a wall with a door.

sa_verse: शालायास्त्वग्रतस्तस्या ददर्श भरतस्तदा। काष्ठानि चावभग्नानि पुष्पाण्यपचितानि च॥ स लक्ष्मणस्य रामस्य ददर्शाश्रममीयुषः। कृतं वृक्षेष्वभिज्ञानं कुशचीरैः क्वचित् क्वचित्॥
en: Before the cottage, Bharata saw fuel broken up, and flowers gathered. And he saw at places signs of Kuśa and bark set up on trees when Rāma and Lakşmaņa (first) arrived at their asylum.

sa_verse: ददर्श भवने तस्मिन् महतः संचयान् कृतान्। मृगाणां महिषाणां च करीषैः शीतकारणात्।।
en: And in that habitation, Bharata saw great heaps of dry dung of deer and buffaloes, gathered for preventing cold.

sa_verse: गच्छन्नेव महाबाहुर्युतिमान् भरतस्तदा। शत्रुघ्नं चाब्रवीद्धृष्टस्तानमात्यांश्च सर्वशः॥
en: As he proceeded, the intelligent and mightyarmed Bharata with a cheerful heart remarked to Śatrughna and all the courtiers.

sa_verse: मन्ये प्राप्ताः स्म तं देशं भरद्वाजो यमब्रवीत्। नातिदूरे हि मन्येऽहं नदी मन्दाकिनीमितः॥
en: I conceive, we have reached the tract that was mentioned by Bharadvāja. Hard by this spot, I fancy, is the river Mandākini.

sa_verse: उच्चैर्बद्धानि चीराणि लक्ष्मणेन भवेदयम्। अभिज्ञानकृतः पन्था विकाले गन्तुमिच्छता॥
en: On high are barks set up by Lakşmaņa. Having to pass by the way at unusual hours, (Laksmana) has marked it with signs.

sa_verse: इतश्चोदात्तदन्तानां कुञ्जराणां तरस्विनाम्। शैलपार्श्वे परिक्रान्तमन्योन्यमभिगर्जताम्॥
en: On the side of the hill is the way by which long-tusked elephants pass to and fro with vehemence, roaring at each other.

sa_verse: यमेवाधातुमिच्छन्ति तापसाः सततं वने। तस्यासौ दृश्यते धूमः संकुलः कृष्णवर्त्मनः॥
en: Here is seen the dense and dark smoke of that which the anchorets are ever anxious to preserve in the forest, fire.

sa_verse: अत्राहं पुरुषव्याघ्रं गुरुसत्कारकारिणम्। आर्य द्रक्ष्यामि संहृष्टं महर्षिमिव राघवम्॥
en: Even here shall I with a delighted heart see that foremost of men, the noble Rāghava resembling a Maharși, ever engaged in serving his superiors.

sa_verse: अथ गत्वा मुहूर्त तु चित्रकूटं स राघवः। मन्दाकिनीमनु प्राप्तस्तं जनं चेदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then going to Citrakuta, that descendant of Raghu, coming to the Mandakini, said to the men.

sa_verse: जगत्यां पुरुषव्याघ्र आस्ते वीरासने रतः। जनेन्द्रो निर्जनं प्राप्य धिङ्मे जन्म सजीवितम्॥
en: That foremost of men in all the world, the lord of all, coming into seclusion, is in his yoga posture. O fie upon my birth and my life!

sa_verse: मत्कृते व्यसनं प्राप्तो लोकनाथो महाद्युतिः। सर्वान् कामान् परित्यज्य वने वसति राघवः॥
en: For me, having come by misfortune, and renounced every comfort, the effulgent lord of men, Rāghava, is dwelling in the woods.

sa_verse: इति लोकसमानुष्टः पादेष्वद्य प्रसादयन्। रामं तस्य पतिष्यामि सीताया लक्ष्मणस्य च ॥
en: I shall be taxed of men on the score. To-day (first) pacifying him, I will fall at the feet of Rāma as well as of Sitã and Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: एवं स विलपंस्तस्मिन् वने दशरथात्मजः। ददर्श महतीं पुण्यां पर्णशालां मनोरमाम्॥
en: Having thus bewailed, Dasaratha's son saw a splendid, charming, and holy dwelling in that forest, composed of leaves.

sa_verse: सालतालाश्वकर्णानां पर्णैर्बहुभिरावृताम्। विशालां मृदुभिस्तीर्णां कुशैर्वेदिमिवाध्वरे॥ शक्रायुधनिकाशैश्च कार्मुकै रसाधनैः। रुक्मपृष्ठैर्महासारैः शोभितां शत्रुबाधकैः॥ अर्करश्मिप्रतीकाशैोरैस्तूणगतैः शरैः। शोभितां दीप्तवदनैः सर्भोगवतीमिव॥ महारजतवासोभ्यामसिभ्यां च विराजिताम्। रुक्मबिन्दुविचित्राभ्यां चर्मभ्यां चापि शोभिताम्॥ गोधाङ्गलिौरासक्तैश्चित्रकाञ्चनभूषितैः। अरिसंधैरनाधृष्यां मृगैः सिंहगुहामिव ॥ प्रागुदकप्रवणां वेदिं विशालां दीप्तपावकाम्। ददर्श भरतस्तत्र पुण्यां रामनिवेशने॥
en: And Bharata beheld in Rāma's habitation a sacred structure made of leaves, covered with a profusion of Sāla, palm, and Aśvakarņa leaves; spread with soft Kuśa, like a dais in a sacrifice; adorned with bows resembling the iris, plaited on the back with gold, of mighty force, and capable of achieving arduous feets and destroying foes; and garnished with arrows in quivers, seeming like the rays of the sun, with flaming mouths, like to the Bhogavatī with serpents; and exceedingly beautified with golden sheaths and scimitars and shields spangled with gold and nice guana finger-fences decked with gold; inaccessible to foes like a lion's den to deer; and furnished with a spacious dais inclined on the north-east, with a fire flaming on it.

sa_verse: निरीक्ष्य स मुहूर्तं तु ददर्श भरतो गुरुम्। उटजे राममासीनं जटामण्डलधारिणम्॥ कृष्णाजिनधरं तं तु चीरवल्कलवाससम्। ददर्श राममासीनमभितः पावकोपमम्॥ सिंहस्कन्धं महाबाहुं पुण्डरीकनिभेक्षणम्। पृथिव्याः सागरान्ताया भर्तारं धर्मचारिणम्॥ उपविष्टं महाबाहुं ब्रह्माणमिव शाश्वतम्। स्थण्डिले दर्भसंस्तीर्णे सीतया लक्ष्मणेन च॥
en: And looking around, anon Bharata saw his superior Rāma seated in the cottage bearing a head of matted locks, clad in a black deer-skin, and having tattered cloth and bark for his garment. And he saw Råma seated like to a flame, with leonine shoulders, mighty arms, and eyes resembling lotuses, the righteous lord of this world bounded by the seas, saw the mightyarmed on like to the eternal Brahma, seated on a skin-seat on the ground along with Sītā and Lakş mana.

sa_verse: तं दृष्ट्वा भरतः श्रीमान्शोकमोहपरिप्लुतः। अभ्यधावत धर्मात्मा भरतः केकयीसुतः॥
en: And seeing him, overwhelmed with grief and affliction, the righteous and graceful son of Kaikeyi, Bharata, rushed (towards him).

sa_verse: दृष्ट्वैव विललापा” बाष्पसंदिग्धया गिरा। अशक्नुवन् वारयितुं धैर्याद् वचनमब्रुवन् ॥
en: And soon as Bharata saw Rāma, he, exceedingly distressed, broke out into lamentations in words choked with sorrow. And incapable of holding himself in patience, he said.

sa_verse: यः संसदि प्रकृतिभिर्भवेद् युक्त उपासितुम्। वन्यैर्मृगैरुपासीनः सोऽयमास्ते ममाग्रजः॥
en: That elder brother of mine, who (seated) in court should be surrounded by the subjects intent upon paying him homage, is now surrounded by wild deer.

sa_verse: वासोभिर्बहुसाहौर्यो महात्मा पुरोचितः। मृगाजिने सोऽयमिह प्रवस्ते धर्ममाचरन्॥
en: He that used to adorn his person with attire worth many thousands (of things), engaged in observing morality, is clad in deer-skin.

sa_verse: अधारयद् यो विविधाश्चित्राः सुमनसः सदा। सोऽयं जटाभारमिमं सहते राघपः कथम्॥
en: Why does he that always wore variegated blossoms, Rāghava, bears this burthen of matted locks?

sa_verse: यस्य यज्ञैर्यथादिष्टैर्युक्तो धर्मस्य संचयः। शरीरक्लेशसम्भूतं स धर्म परिमार्गते॥
en: He who is worthy of acquiring religious merit by celebrating sacrifices according to the ordinance, is now following morality by afflicting his person.

sa_verse: चन्दनेन महार्हेण यस्याङ्गमुपसेवितम्। मलेन तस्याङ्गमिदं कथमार्यस्य सेव्यते॥
en: How is the person of that one whose body used to be daubed with costly sandal, covered with dust.

sa_verse: मनिमित्तमिदं दुःखं प्राप्तो रामः सुखोचितः। धिग्जीवितं नृशंसस्य मम लोकविगर्हितम्॥
en: It is for me that Rama, although deserving of comfort, has come by this misfortune. Wicked that I am, fie upon my life despised of men!

sa_verse: इत्येवं विलपन दीनः प्रस्विनमुखपङ्कजः। पादावप्राप्य रामस्य पपात भरतो रुदन्॥
en: Thus lamenting in woeful guise, with the lotus of his countenance covered with sweat, Bharata coming at Rāma's feet, fell at them bewailing.

sa_verse: दुःखाभितप्तो भरतो राजपुत्रो महाबलः। उक्त्वाऽऽर्येति सकृद्दीनं पुनर्नोवाच किंचन॥
en: And inflamed with grief, the exceedingly powerful prince Bharata, having in distress of spirit uttered, O noble one, again said nothing.

sa_verse: बाष्पैः पिहितकण्ठश्च प्रेक्ष्य रामं यशस्विनम्। आर्येत्येवाभिसंक्रुश्य व्याहतु नाशकत् ततः॥
en: And beholding the illustrious Rāma, Bharata with his utterance choked with emotion, exclaimed, O noble one, and was unable to say anything further.

sa_verse: शत्रुघ्नश्चापि रामस्य ववन्दे चरणौ रुदन्। तावुभौ च समालिङ्गय रामोऽप्यश्रूण्यवर्तयत्॥ ततः सुमन्त्रेण गुहेन चैव समीयतू राजसुतावरण्ये। दिवाकरश्चैव निशाकरश्च यथाम्बरे शुक्रबृहस्पतिभ्याम्।।
en: Then Śatrughna also weeping paid homage unto the feet of Rāma. And shedding tears, Rāma embraced them both. Then as in the sky, the Sun and the Moon meet with śukra and Brhaspati, those two princes (Rāma and Laks mana) met with Sumantra and Guha in the forest.

sa_verse: तान् पार्थिवान् वारणयूथपार्हान् समागतांस्तत्र महत्यरण्ये। वनौकसस्तेऽभिसमीक्ष्य सर्वे त्वश्रूण्यमुञ्चन् प्रविहाय हर्षम्॥
en: And beholding those kings resembling leaders of elephant-herds met together in that mighty forest, the dwellers in the woods, resigning their cheerfulness, began to shed tears. forest, the dwellers in the woods, resigning their cheerfulness, began to shed tears.

sa_verse: जटिलं चीरवसनं प्राञ्जलिं पतितं भुवि। ददर्श रामो दुर्दर्श युगान्ते भास्करं यथा॥ कथंचिदभिविज्ञाय विवर्णवदनं कृशम्। भ्रातरं भरतं रामः परिजग्राह पाणिना॥ आघ्राय रामस्तं मूर्ध्नि परिष्वज्य च राघवम्। अङ्के भरतमारोप्य पर्यपृच्छत सादरम्॥
en: Then Rāma cast his eyes on (Bharata) as clad in bark and wearing matted locks he lay on the earth with joined hands, incapable of being gazed at, like the Sun at the time of the universal dissolution. Then recognizing him a little, he took by the hand his brother Bharata, lean, with a pallid countenance. And smelling the crown of his head, and embracing that descendant of Raghu, Rāma took Bharata on his lap and asked him affectionately.

sa_verse: क्व नु तेऽभूत् पिता तात यदरण्यं त्वमागतः। न हि त्वं जीवतस्तस्य वनमागन्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: Where was your father, child, that you have come to the forest? It certainly behoves you not to come to the forest while he is living.

sa_verse: चिरस्य बत पश्यामि दूराद् भरतमागतम्। दुष्प्रतीकमरण्येऽस्मिन् किं तात वनमागतः॥
en: Ah! I see you come from far after a long time. Why, my child, have you come to this gloomy forest?

sa_verse: कच्चिन्नु धरते तात राजा यत् त्वमिहागतः। क्वच्चिन्न दीनः सहसा राजा लोकान्तरं गतः॥
en: Is the king alive, my child, seeing that you have come hither; or, afflicted with grief, has he suddenly gone to the other world?

sa_verse: कच्चित् सौम्य न ते राज्यं भ्रष्टं बालस्य शाश्वतम्। कच्चिच्छुश्रूषसे तात पितुः सत्यपराक्रम।७।।
en: O mild one, child that you are, your kingdom ever yours has not been wrested from you? And, O you having truth for prowess, do you, my child, minister to our sire?

sa_verse: कच्चिद् दशरथो राजा कुशली सत्यसंगरः। राजसूयाश्वमेधानामाहर्ता धर्मनिश्चितः॥
en: Is that truthful one, that performer of Rajasüya and Aśvamedha, ever devoted to righteousness, king Dasaratha, well?

sa_verse: स कच्चिद् ब्राह्मणो विद्वान् धर्मनित्यो महाद्युतिः। इक्ष्वाकूणामुपाध्यायो यथावत् तात पूज्यते॥
en: My child, is that exceedingly effulgent and learned Brāhmaṇa ever steady in morality, the priest of the Iksvākus, duly honoured?

sa_verse: तात कच्चिच्च कौसल्या सुमित्रा च प्रजावती। सुखिनी कच्चिदार्या च देवी नन्दति कैकेयी॥
en: And, my child, are Kausalyā, and Sumitrā having a son, in happiness? And is the noble Kaikeyi in spirits?

sa_verse: कच्चिद् विनयसम्पन्नः कुलपुत्रो बहुश्रुतः। अनसूयरनुद्रष्टा सत्कृतस्ते पुरोहितः॥
en: And is that one, sprung from a mighty line, humble and versed in various lore, your priest, who performs every ceremony, who bears no ill will, and whose gaze is ever fixed upon our welfare, honoured?

sa_verse: कच्चिदग्निषु ते युक्तो विधिज्ञो मतिमानृजुः। हुतं च होष्यमाणं च काले वेदयते सदा॥
en: Do intelligent and sincere people cognizant of the rules, look after the sacrificial fire? And do they regularly inform you of the proper seasons for performing the fire-sacrifice?

sa_verse: कच्चिद् देवान् पितृन् भृत्यान् गुरून् पितृसमानपि। वृद्धांश्च तात वैद्यांश्च ब्राह्मणांश्चाभिमन्यसे॥
en: Do you regard the deities, the ancestral manes, the preceptors like to predecessors, the physicians, the Brahmanas, and the servants?

sa_verse: इष्वस्त्रवरसम्पन्नमर्थशास्त्रविशारदम्। सुधन्वानमुपाध्यायं कच्चित् त्वं तात मन्यसे ॥
en: And do not disregard the preceptor Sudhanvā versed in excellent arms whether inspired with mantras or not, and accomplished in the knowledge of polity?

sa_verse: कच्चिदात्मसमाः शूराः श्रुतवन्तो जितेन्द्रियाः। कुलीनाश्चेङ्गितज्ञाश्च कृतास्ते तात मन्त्रिणः॥
en: And, my child, have you employed as your controlled, well-born, and understanding signs, who are like your own self?

sa_verse: मन्त्री विजयमूलं हि राज्ञां भवति राघव। सुसंवृतो मन्त्रिधुरैरमात्यैः शास्त्रकोविदैः॥
en: O descendant of Raghu, counsel well kept by clever councillors versed in lore, is the root of victory with kings.

sa_verse: कच्चिन्निद्रावशं नैषि कच्चित् कालेऽवबुध्यसे। कच्चिच्चापररात्रेषु चिन्तयस्यर्थनैपुणम्॥
en: You have not come under the sway of sleep? and you awake at the proper hour? And do you during the short hours revolve the means of acquiring wealth?

sa_verse: कच्चिन्मन्त्रयसे नैकः कच्चिन्न बहुभिः सह। कच्चित् ते मन्त्रितो मन्त्रो राष्ट्रं न परिधावति॥
en: And you did not take counsel either with yourself alone, or (on the other hand) with a multiplicity of counsellors? and your counsel does not range the kingdom (i.e. does not take air)?

sa_verse: कच्चिदर्थं विनिश्चित्य लघुमूलं मदोदयम्। क्षिप्रमारभसे कर्म न दीर्घयसि राघव॥
en: O descendant of Raghu, having determined upon a course costing small effort but fraught with a mighty result, you set about it sharply and delay not?

sa_verse: कच्चिनु सुकृतान्येव कृतरूपाणि वा पुनः। विदुस्ते सर्वकार्याणि न कर्तव्यानि पार्थिवाः॥
en: Do the (auxiliary) kings know only those acts of yours that have been accomplished or those that are well nigh so, and not those that you intend to set yours hand to?

sa_verse: कच्चिन तर्कैर्युक्त्या वा ये चाप्यपरिकीर्तिताः। त्वया वा तव वामात्यैर्बुध्यते तात मन्त्रितम्॥
en: Do others through inference or appearances come at a knowledge of your counsels, although undivulged to others by yourself or your counsellors; and (do you and your ministers) attain to a knowledge of others' counsels?

sa_verse: कच्चित् सहस्रैर्मूर्खाणामेकमिच्छसि पण्डितम्। पण्डितो ह्यर्थकृच्छेषु कुर्यानिःश्रेयसं महत्॥
en: And passing by a thousand dunces, do you set your heart on having a single wise man? In times of pecuniary stress, a wise man stands in excellent stead.

sa_verse: सहस्राण्यपि मूर्खाणां यधुपास्ते महीपतिः। अथवाप्ययुतान्येव नास्ति तेषु सहायता॥
en: And although a king might be surrounded by a thousand or ten thousand fools, yet he can count upon no assistance (at their hands).

sa_verse: एकोऽप्यमात्यो मेधावी शूरो दक्षो विचक्षणः। राजानं राजपुत्रं वा प्रापयेन्महतीं श्रियम्॥
en: And a single able counsellor, intelligent, heroic, and sagacious, bring great prosperity upon a king or a prince.

sa_verse: कच्चिन्मुख्या महत्स्वेव मध्यमेषु च मध्यमा। जघन्याश्च जघन्येषु भृत्यास्ते तात योजिताः॥
en: And, my child, do you employ the best servants upon the best offices, the middling upon middling, and the worst upon the worst?

sa_verse: अमात्यानुपधातीतान् पितृपैतामहाशुचीन्। श्रेष्ठाञ्छेष्ठेषु कच्चित् त्वं नियोजयसि कर्मसु॥
en: And did you employ upon the most worthy offices counsellors who are above bribery, who have served your father and grand-father, and who are pure?

sa_verse: कच्चिन्नोग्रेण दण्डेन भृशमुद्वेजिताः प्रजाः। राष्ट्रे तवावजानन्ति मन्त्रिणः कैकयीसुत ॥
en: And do the subjects visited with condign, punishment, as well as the ministers, disregard you, O son of Kaikeyi?

sa_verse: कच्चित् त्वां नावजानन्ति याजकाः पतितं यथा। उग्राप्रतिग्रहीतारं कामयानमिव स्त्रियः॥
en: Do the priests scorn you like a fallen one, even as females do those lascivious folks who use force towards the former?

sa_verse: उपायकुशलं वैद्यं भृत्यसंदूषणे रतम्। शूरमैश्वर्यकामं च यो हन्ति न स हन्यते॥
en: He that does not slay a physician skilled in ways and means, a servant given to enlisting the sympathies of his fellow-servants against his master, or a hero that covets riches, is slain (by them).

sa_verse: कच्चिद् धृष्टश्च शूरश्च धृतिमान् मतिमाञ्छुचिः। कुलीनश्चानुरक्तश्च दक्षः सेनापतिः कृतः॥
en: And had you chosen for your general one that is confident, is endowed with intelligence and fortitude, sprung in a respectable race, and attached and able?

sa_verse: बलवन्तश्च कच्चित् ते मुख्या युद्धविशारदाः। दृष्टापदाना विक्रान्तास्त्वया सत्कृत्य मानिताः॥
en: And do you practically honour your foremost warriors possessed of prowess, who have already given evidence of their manliness?

sa_verse: कच्चिद् बलस्य भक्तं च वेतनं च यथोचितम्। सम्प्राप्तकालं दातव्यं ददासि न विलम्बसे॥
en: And do you at the proper time grant your soldiers what you should, provision and pay; and do not delay in doing this?

sa_verse: कालातिक्रमणे ह्येव भक्तवेतनयो ताः। भर्तुरप्यतिकुप्यन्ति सोऽनर्थः सुमहान् कृतः॥
en: If the proper time for granting provision and pay be passed, the servants get wroth with their master and tax him; and great is the evil that springs herefrom.

sa_verse: कच्चित् सर्वेऽनुरक्तास्त्वां कुलपुत्राः प्रधानतः। कच्चित् प्राणांस्तवार्थेषु संत्यजन्ति समाहिताः॥
en: And are the principal descendants of our race attached to you; and are they, when enlisted on your side with concentrated minds, ready to lay down their lives?

sa_verse: कच्चिज्जानपदो विद्वान् दक्षिणः प्रतिभानवान्। यथोक्तवादी दूतस्ते कृतो भरत पण्डितः॥
en: O Bharata, are your spies persons coming from the provinces, and learned, upright, endowed with presence of mind, representing the truth, and possessed of wisdon?

sa_verse: कच्चिदष्टादशान्येषु स्वपक्षे दश पञ्च च। त्रिभिस्त्रिभिरविज्ञातैत्सि तीर्थानि चारणैः॥
en: And do you acquire intelligence of the expedients, eighteen* in respect of others, and fifteen in respect of the own self, by means of every three spies appointed in connection with each of these expedients, men quite ignorant of each other's counsels? *1. Minister. 2. Priest. 3. Heir-apparent. 4. General. 5. Warder. 6. Gatekeeper of the inner-apartment. 7. Jailer. 8. Treasurer. 9. Conveyer of the royal orders. 10. Pleaders. 11. Judges. 12. Members of the council. 13. Distributor of pay and provision to the army. 14. Journeymen. 15. Justice of the peace. 16. Protector of the frontiers of the kingdom. 17. Magistrate. 18. Guards of rivers, hills, and fortresses.

sa_verse: कच्चिद् व्यपास्तानहितान् प्रतियातांश्च सर्वदा। दुर्बलाननवज्ञाय वर्तसे रिपुसूदन॥
en: And do you not contemn those. weak ones that, O destroyer of your. foes, having been expelled, have come again (to you)?

sa_verse: कच्चिन्न लोकायतिकान् ब्राह्मणांस्तात सेवसे। अनर्थकुशला ह्येते बालाः पण्डितमानिनः॥ धर्मशास्त्रेषु मुख्येषु विद्यमानेषु दुर्बुधाः। बुद्धिमान्वीक्षिकी प्राप्य निरर्थं प्रवदन्ति ते॥
en: And, my child, you did not minister to atheistical Brāhmaṇas? These childish persons proud of their learning are only fit for bringing evils upon others. While there are excellent scriptures, these people of subtle intellects, having acquired a knowledge of dialectics, speak vanities.

sa_verse: वीरैरध्युषितां पूर्वमस्माकं तात पूर्वकैः। सत्यनामां दृढद्वारा हस्त्यश्वरथसंकुलाम्॥ ब्राह्मणैः क्षत्रियैर्वैश्यैः स्वकर्मनिरतैः सदा। जितेन्द्रियैर्महोत्साहैर्वृतामार्यैः सहस्रशः॥ प्रासादैर्विविधाकारैर्वृतां वैद्यजनाकुलाम्। कच्चित् समुदितां स्फीतामयोध्यां परिरक्षसे।॥
en: My child, do you protect the prosperous and renowned Ayodhyā, inhabited formerly by our heroic predecessors; bearing a true name; having strong gates; filled with elephants, steeds, and cars; thronged by thousands; with noble Brāhmaṇas, Kștriyas and Vaisyas breathing high spirits, and with their senses controlled, each engaged in his own task; abounding in people learned in the Veda; and surrounded with palatial mansions of various shapes?

sa_verse: कच्चिच्चैत्यशतैर्जुष्टः सुनिविष्टजनाकुलः। देवस्थानैः प्रपाभिश्च तटाकैश्चोपशोभितः॥ प्रहृष्टनरनारीक: समाजोत्सवशोभितः। सुकृष्टसीमापशुमान् हिंसाभिरभिवर्जितः॥ अदेवमातृको रम्यः श्वापदैः परिवर्जितः। परित्यक्तो भयैः सर्वैः खनिभिश्चोपशोभितः।।४५ । विवर्जितो नरैः पापैर्मम पूर्वैः सुरक्षितः। कच्चिज्जनपदः स्फीतः सुखं वसति राघव॥
en: And, O descendant of Raghu, are the flourishing provinces marked with hundreds of Chaityas, filled with prosperous people, graced with abodes of deities, places for distributing water, and tanks, with man and women in happy mood, gay with meetings and festivities, having their outskirts well furrowed, provided with beasts, void of ill feelings, depending on tanks for their water supply, charming, renounced by fierce animals, free from all kinds of fear, decked with mines, left by unrighteous people, and well governed by my predecessors, having a good time of it?

sa_verse: कच्चित् ते दयिताः सर्वे कृषिगोरक्षजीविनः। वार्तायां संश्रितस्तात लोकोऽयं सुखमेधते॥
en: And do agriculturists and cow herds find favour in your sight? And remaining in their respective vocations, do they enjoy happiness?

sa_verse: तेषां गुप्तिपरीहारैः कच्चित् ते भरणं कृतम्। रक्ष्या हि राज्ञा धर्मेण सर्वे विषयवासिनः।४८।।
en: And do you maintain them by securing to them what they wish for and removing from them what they wish away? All the dwellers in his dominions should be protected by the king.

sa_verse: कच्चित् स्त्रियः सान्त्वयसे कच्चित् तास्ते सुरक्षिताः। कच्चिन्न श्रद्दधास्यासां कच्चिद् गुह्यं न भाषसे॥
en: And do you conciliate the females; and are they well protected by you? And do you not regard them; and do you not open to them your mind?

sa_verse: कच्चिन्नागवनं गुप्तं कच्चित् ते सन्ति धेनुकाः। कच्चिन्न गणिकाश्वानां कुञ्जराणां च तृप्यसि॥
en: And are the woods where elephants breed, kept by you; and have you kine? And do you not foster mares and female-elephants?

sa_verse: कच्चिद् दर्शयसे नित्यं मानुषाणां विभूषितम्। उत्थायोत्थाय पूर्वाह्ने राजपुत्र महापथे॥
en: And do you show yourself daily in the court, well robed? And rising in the morning, do you show yourself in the high-ways?

sa_verse: कच्चिन्न सर्वे कर्मान्ताः प्रत्यक्षास्तेऽविशङ्कया। सर्वे वा पुनरुत्सृष्टा मध्यमेवात्र कारणम्॥
en: And do your servants boldly present themselves before you; or do they all keep away? A middle course contributes to their good fortune.

sa_verse: कच्चिद् दुर्गाणि सर्वाणि धनधान्यायुधोदकैः। यन्त्रैश्च प्रतिपूर्णानि तथा शिल्पिधनुधरैः॥
en: Are all the forts furnished with wealth, corn, arms water, machines, artisans, and bowmen?

sa_verse: आयस्ते विपुलः कच्चित् कच्चिदल्पतरो व्ययः। अपात्रेषु न ते कच्चित् कोषो गच्छति राघव॥
en: And are your incomings great and outgoings slender? And, O Raghu's descendant, you do not give away your coffers to the undeserving?

sa_verse: देवतार्थे च पित्रर्थे ब्राह्मणाभ्यागतेषु च। योधेषु मित्रवर्गेषु कच्चिद् गच्छति ते व्ययः॥
en: And did you spend your wealth in the interests of the deities, or the pitris, or the Brāhmaṇas who have come to you, or warriors, or friends?

sa_verse: कच्चिदार्योऽपि शुद्धात्मा क्षारितश्चापकर्मणा। अदृष्टः शास्त्रकुशलैर्न लोभाद् बध्यते शुचिः॥
en: If any respectable, pure-spirited and clean person happen to be accused by some one of theft or other crimes, do you from covetousness punish him without first having him tried by persons versed in scripture?

sa_verse: गृहीतश्चैव पृष्ठश्च काले दृष्टः सकारण:। कच्चिन्न मुच्यते चोरो धनलोभानरर्षभ॥
en: And, O best of men, is a thief, that has been caught, interrogated (as to his guilt), and found with the stolen property on his person, set free (by your men) from motives of gain.

sa_verse: व्यसने कच्चिदाढयस्य दुर्बलस्य च राघव। अर्थं विरागाः पश्यन्ति तवामात्या बहुश्रुताः।५८।।
en: And do your counsellors, o descendant of Raghu, accomplished in various lore, uninfluenced by greed, consider the conduct of both the rich and the poor involved in peril?

sa_verse: यानि मिथ्याभिशस्तानां पतन्त्यश्रूणि राघव। तानि पुत्रपशून् घ्नन्ति प्रीत्यर्थमनुशासतः॥
en: O son of the Raghu race, the tears of those who have been falsely charged with any offence, (and who have failed to obtain justice), dropping, destroy the sons as well as the beasts of the ruler that minds his own comfort only.

sa_verse: कच्चिद् वृदांश्च बालांश्च वैद्यान् मुख्यांश्च राघव। दानेन मनसा वाचा त्रिभिरेतैर्बुभूषसे॥
en: And do you with these three, gifts, mind and word, try to win over aged people, boys, physicians, and the principal ones?

sa_verse: कच्चिद् गुरुंश्च वृद्धांश्च तापसान् देवतातिथीन्। चैत्यांश्च सर्वान् सिद्धार्थान् ब्राह्मणांश्च नमस्यसि॥
en: And do you salute spiritual preceptors, aged persons, ascetics, gods, guests, Caityas, emancipated one, and Brāhmaṇās?

sa_verse: कच्चिदर्थेन वा धर्ममर्थं धर्मेण वा पुनः। उभौ वा प्रीतिलोभेन कामेन न विबाधसे ॥
en: And you do not oppose righteousness by interest, or interest by virtue, or both by desire, intent on gratifying the senses?

sa_verse: कच्चिदर्थं च कामं च धर्मं च जयतां वर। विभज्य काले कालज्ञ सर्वान् वरद सेवसे॥
en: And, O foremost of conquerors, do you, O you cognisant of time, in season resorting to interest, desire, and virtue respectively, attain them, O bestower of boons?

sa_verse: कच्चित् ते ब्राह्मणः शर्म सर्वशास्त्रार्थकोविदाः। आशंसन्ते महाप्राज्ञ पौरजानपदैः सह॥
en: And do Brāhmaṇas versed in all religious lore and knowing interest, together with the citizens and the inhabitants of the provinces wish for your happiness, O highly wise one?

sa_verse: नास्तिक्यमनृतं क्रोधं प्रमादं दीर्घसूत्रताम्। अदर्शनं ज्ञानवतामालस्यं पञ्चवृत्तिताम्॥ एकचिन्तनमर्थानामनर्थज्ञैश्च मन्त्रणम्। निश्चितानामनारम्भं मन्त्रस्यापरिरक्षणम्॥ मङ्गलाद्यप्रयोगं च प्रत्युत्थानं च सर्वतः। कच्चित् त्वं वर्जयस्येतान् राजदोषांश्चतुर्दश॥
en: Atheism, untruthfulness, inattention, anger, procrastination, companionship with evil persons, indolence, gratification of the senses, consultation with a single person concerning the needs of a kingdom, taking counsel with those that are cognisant of evils alone, omission to take in hand a task that has been decided upon, divulgence of counsel, non-commencement of a course in the morning, and marching against all the foes at one and the same time, have you eschewed these ten and four faults?

sa_verse: दशपञ्चचतुर्वर्गान् सप्तवर्गं च तत्त्वतः। अष्टवर्गं त्रिवर्गं च विद्यास्तिस्रश्च राघव॥ इन्द्रियाणां जयं बुद्ध्वा षाड्गुण्यं दैवमानुषम्। कृत्य विंशतिवर्गं च तथा प्रकृतिमण्डलम्॥ यात्रादण्डविधानं च द्वियोनी संधिविग्रहो। कच्चिदेतान् महाप्राज्ञ यथावदनुमन्यसे॥
en: O descendant of Raghu, truly understanding the tenth, fifth, fourth and seventh classes as well as the eighth, and third ones, and the three kinds of learning, and victory over the senses, and the evils human and superhuman, six attributes, and the (peculiar) duties (of royalty), and the twenty classes, and the kinds of Prakritis and Mandala, and Yatra chastisement, and war and peace having each two sources; do you with due order observe all these?

sa_verse: मन्त्रिभिस्त्वं यथोद्दिष्टं चतुर्भिस्त्रिभिरेव वा। कच्चित् समस्तैर्व्यस्तैश्च मन्त्रं मन्त्रयसे बुध ॥
en: And, O wise one, do you, as laid down in the ordinance, take counsel, severally and in a body with three or four men?

sa_verse: कच्चित्ते सफला वेदाः कच्चित्ते सफलाः क्रियाः। कच्चित्ते सफला दाराः कच्चित्ते सफलं श्रुतम्॥
en: And do you observe the Vedas? And do you perceive the fruit of your acts? And have your wives borne children? And has your knowledge of scripture borne fruit?

sa_verse: कच्चिदेषैव ते बुद्धिर्यथोक्ता मम राघव। आयुष्या च यशस्या च धर्मकामार्थसंहिता॥
en: O descendant of Raghu, is your intellect going the way that I have indicated above? This course is conducive to long life, and fame; and virtue, desire and interest.

sa_verse: यां वृत्तिं वर्तते तातो यां च नः प्रपितामहः। तां वृत्तिं वर्तसे कच्चिद् या च सत्पथगा शुभा॥
en: O child, are you following the course that was followed by our ancestors? And are you maintaining the conduct that is excellent and passes along pious ways?

sa_verse: राजा तु धर्मेण हि पालयित्वा महीपतिर्दण्डधरः प्रजानाम्। दितश्च्युतः स्वर्गमुपैति विद्वान्।७६ ।।
en: The learned king ruling (all) righteously, that chastiser of the subjects, the monarch attaining duly the entire earth, going away from hence, acquires the celestial regions.

sa_verse: तं तु रामः समाज्ञाय भ्रातरं गुरुवत्सलम्। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा प्रष्टुं समुपचक्रमे॥
en: Knowing Bharata as devoted to his superior Răma along with his brother Lakşmaņa, asked him.

sa_verse: इत्युक्तः कैकेयीपुत्रः काकुत्स्थेन महात्मना। प्रगृह्य बलवद् भूयः प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।४।
en: Thus accosted by the high-souled Kākutstha, Kaikeyi's son, suppressing his grief by a strong effort, with joined hands said.

sa_verse: आर्य तातः परित्यज्य कृत्वा कर्म सुदुष्करम्। गतः स्वर्ग महाबाहुः पुत्रशोकाभिपीडितः॥ स्त्रिया नियुक्तः कैकेय्या मम मात्रा परंतप। चकार सा महत्पापमिदमात्मयशोहरम्॥
en: O noble one, forsaking us all, our father possessed of mighty arms, having performed this terrible task, in consequence of being urged by a woman, my mother Kaikeyi, has gone to heaven afflicted with grief on account of his son, O repressor of foes.

sa_verse: सा राज्यफलमप्राप्य विधवा शोककर्शिता। पतिष्यति महाघोरे नरके जननी मम ॥
en: And she has committed a signal sin capable of destroying her fame. And without obtaining the kingdom which she had coveted as the fruit of her action, a widow tried with grief, my mother will fall into a terrible hell.

sa_verse: तस्य मे दासभूतस्य प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि। अभिषिञ्चस्व चाचैव राज्येन मघवानिव॥
en: Now it behoves you to extend your favour to me who have become your slave. Do you this very day get yourself installed in the kingdom, like to Indra himself.

sa_verse: इमाः प्रकृतयः सर्वा विधवा मातरश्च याः। त्वत्सकाशमनुप्राप्ताः प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि।९।।
en: All these subjects and our widowed mothers have come to you. It behoves you to show your favour to them.

sa_verse: तथानुपूर्व्या युक्तश्च युक्तं चात्मनि मानद। राज्यं प्राप्नुहि धर्मेण सकामान् सुहृदः कुरु॥
en: You are the first-born; and meet it is that you should get yourself installed, O bestower of honour. Therefore do you receive the kingdom righteously and fulfil the desire of your friends.

sa_verse: भवत्वविधवा भूमिः समग्रा पतिना त्वया। शशिना विमलेनेव शारदी रजनी यथा॥ एभिश्च सचिवैः सार्धं शिरसा याचितो मया। भ्रातुः शिष्यस्य दासस्य प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि॥
en: And like the autumnal Night on having the unclouded moon, let the entire Earth cease to be a widow on having you counsellors. It behoves you to show your favour to your brother, disciple, and slave.

sa_verse: तदिदं शाश्वतं पित्र्यं सर्वं सचिवमण्डलम्। पूजितं पुरुषव्याघ्र नातिक्रमितुमर्हसि ॥
en: Therefore, O chief of men, you ought not to pass by this honoured band of ancestral ministers, who have always been serving this race.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महाबाहुः सबाष्पः कैकयीसुतः। रामस्य शिरसा पादौ जग्राह भरतः पुनः॥
en: Having said this, with tears flooding his eyes, the mighty-armed son of Kaikeyi, Bharata, again took Rāma's feet on this head.

sa_verse: तं मत्तमिव मातङ्ग निःश्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः। भ्रातरं भरतं रामः परिष्वज्येदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereupon Rāma embracing his brother Bharata resembling a mad elephant, and sighing again and again, said.

sa_verse: कुलीनः सत्त्वसम्पन्नस्तेजस्वी चरितव्रतः। राज्यहेतोः कथं पापमाचरेन्मद्विधो जनः॥
en: Of a high race, possessed of strength, endowed with energy, and vowed to sterling worth of character, how can one like me commit sin for the sake of dominion?

sa_verse: न दोषं त्वयि पश्यामि सूक्ष्ममप्यरिसूदन। न चापि जननी बाल्यात् त्वं विगर्हितुमर्हसि ॥
en: Fault find I none ever so small in you, you destroyer of foes. Nor does it become you from puerility to tax your mother.

sa_verse: कामकारो महाप्राज्ञ गुरूणां सर्वदानघ। उपपन्नेषु दारेषु पुत्रेषु च विधीयते॥
en: O exceedingly wise one, O you that are sinless, superiors may act as they list in relation to those wives and sons of theirs that are after theirs heart.

sa_verse: वयमस्य यथा लोके संख्याताः सौम्य साधुभिः। भार्याः पुत्राश्च शिष्याश्च त्वमपि ज्ञातुमर्हसि॥
en: And this also you should learn that wives, sons and disciples should always be obedient as has been held by the emancipated ones.

sa_verse: वने वा चीरवसनं सौम्य कृष्णाजिनाम्बरम्। राज्ये वापि महाराजो मां वासयितुमीश्वरः॥
en: O mild one, the monarch is competent to make me stay in the woods clad in black deerskin, as to establish me in the monarchy.

sa_verse: यावत् पितरि धर्मज्ञ गौरवं लोकसत्कृते। तावद् धर्मकृतां श्रेष्ठ जनन्यामपि गौरवम्॥
en: O you conversant with morality, O foremost of those observing righteousness, in respect of virtuous conduct a mother should be as much regarded (by a son) as a father is.

sa_verse: एताभ्यां धर्मशीलाभ्यां वनं गच्छेति राघव। मातापितृभ्यामुक्तोऽहं कथमन्यत् समाचरे॥
en: How can I, O descendant of Rāghava, having been told by my righteous father and mother, 'Go to the woods', act otherwise?

sa_verse: त्वया राज्यमयोध्यायां प्राप्तव्यं लोकसत्कृतम्। वस्तव्यं दण्डकारण्ये मया वल्कलवाससा॥
en: You ought to receive the kingdom, Ayodhyā, honoured of men; and I ought to dwell in Dandaka dressed in bark.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महाराजो विभागं लोकसंनिधौ। व्यादिश्य च महाराजो दिवं दशरथो गतः॥
en: Having made this division of duties in the presence of all, and also enjoined this, the mighty monarch, Dasaratha, has ascended heaven.

sa_verse: स च प्रमाणं धर्मात्मा राजा लोकगुरुस्तव। पित्रा दत्तं यथाभागमुपभोक्तं त्वमर्हसि ॥
en: That superior of all, even the virtuous king, is your evidence. It behoves you to enjoy that which has been assigned to you by your sire.

sa_verse: चतुर्दश समाः सौम्य दण्डकारण्यमाश्रितः। उपभोक्ष्ये त्वहं दत्तं भागं पित्रा महात्मना॥
en: And, O mild one, taking refuge in the forest of Dandaka for fourteen years, I will act the part that has been set apart for me by my magnanimous father.

sa_verse: यदब्रवीन्मां नरलोकसत्कृतः पिता महात्मा विबुधाधिपोपमः। तदेव मन्ये परमात्मनो हितं न सर्वलोकेश्वरभावमव्ययम्॥
en: What has been assigned to me by that one respected by all men, my high-souled father resembling the lord of celestials himself, is my prime good; the masterdom of all the worlds I would decline (should it be opposed to the will of my sire).

sa_verse: रामस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा भरतः प्रत्युवाच ह। किं मे धर्माद् विहीनस्य राजधर्मः करिष्यति॥
en: Hearing Rāma's words, Bharata answered, Deprived of the kingdom in consequence of my posteriority in point of birth, what does regard for morality avail me?

sa_verse: शाश्वतोऽयं सदा धर्मः स्थितोऽस्मासु नरर्षभ। ज्येष्ठ पुत्रे स्थिते राजा न कनीयान् भवेनृपः॥
en: O best of men, even this morality has ever been established with reference to us, viz., that the eldest son of the king existing, a younger one cannot be the king.

sa_verse: स समृद्धां मया सार्धमयोध्यां गच्छ राघव। अभिषेचय चात्मानं कुलस्यास्य भवाय नः॥
en: Do you, therefore, O Rāghava go along with me to the prosperous Ayodhya; and get yourself ir stalled there for the behoof of our race.

sa_verse: राजानं मानुषं प्राहुर्देवत्वे सम्मतो मम। यस्य धर्मार्थसहितं वृत्तमाहुरमानुषम्॥
en: Although a king observing interest and virtue, and who towers above average humanity, has been called a mortal, yet to me he is a very deity.

sa_verse: केकयस्थे च मयि तु त्वयि चारण्यमाश्रिते। धीमान् स्वर्गं गतो राजा यायजूकः सतां मतः॥
en: While I was in Kekaya and you was in the forest that intelligent monarch honoured of the good, given to celebrating sacrifices, ascended heaven.

sa_verse: निष्क्रान्तमात्रे भवति सहसीते सलक्ष्मणे। दुःखशोकाभिभूतस्तु राजा त्रिदिवमभ्यगात्॥
en: As soon as you had set out (for the forest) along with Sītā and Lakşmaņa, the king borne down by grief and chagrin, went to heaven.

sa_verse: उत्तिष्ठ पुरुषव्याघ्र क्रियतामुदकं पितुः। अहं चायं च शत्रुघ्नः पूर्वमेव कृतोदकौ॥
en: O foremost of men, do you arise, and offer water to the spirit of our sire. Śatrughna and I have ere this offered water to (the departed).

sa_verse: प्रियेण किल दत्तं हि पितृलोकेषु राघव। अक्षयं भवतीत्याहुर्भावांश्चैव पितुः प्रियः॥
en: O Rāghava, anything offered to the (manes of the) ancestors by a beloved descendant, conduces to their eternal behoof; and you the favourite of our father.

sa_verse: स्त्वय्येव सक्तामनिवर्त्य बुद्धिम्। स्त्वां संस्मरन्नेव गतः पिता ते॥
en: Mourning you and exceedingly desirous of seeing you, his mind being fastened on you and incapable of being turned away, deprived of you, and smitten with grief of your account, your father departed this life, remembering you. was

sa_verse: तां श्रुत्वा करुणां वाचं पितुर्मरणसंहिताम्। राघवो भरतेनोक्तां बभूव गतचेतनः॥
en: Hearing those piteous words uttered by Bharata in connection with the demise of their father, Rāghava was deprived of his senses.

sa_verse: तं तु वज्रमिवोत्सृष्टमाहवे दानवारिणा। वाग्वजं भरतेनोक्तममनोज्ञं परंतपः॥ प्रगृह्य रामो बाहु वै पुष्पिताङ्ग इव दुमः। वने परशुना कृत्तस्तथा भुवि पपात ह॥
en: On that thunder-bolt of a speech being uttered by Bharata, like to a (real) thunder-bolt hurled in battle by the enemy of the Dānavas (Indra), that subduer of foes, Rāma, stretching his arms, fell down to the earth, like a blossoming tree that has been hewn by an axe.

sa_verse: तथा हि पतितं रामं जगत्यां जगतीपतिम्। कूलघातपरिश्रान्तं प्रसुप्तमिव कुञ्जरम्॥ भ्रातरस्ते महेष्वासं सर्वतः शोककर्शितम्। रुदन्तः सह वैदेह्या सिषुचुः सलिलेन वै॥
en: Seeing that lord of the world and mighty bowman, Rāma, fallen, like a sleeping elephant fatigued with turning up earth with its tusks, his brothers exercised with sorrow, broke out into lamentations, and along with Videha's daughter began to sprinkle water (on his face).

sa_verse: स तु संज्ञा पुनर्लब्ध्वा नेत्राभ्यामश्रुमुत्सृजन्। उपाक्रामत काकुत्स्थः कृपणं बहु भाषितुम्॥
en: Then regaining his consciousness, Kākutstha shedding tears from his eyes, distressfully addressed himself to speech.

sa_verse: स रामः स्वर्गतं श्रुत्वा पितरं पृथिवीपतिम्। उवाच भरतं वाक्यं धर्मात्मा धर्मसंहितम्॥
en: And hearing that lord of earth, his sire, had gone to heaven, that righteous one said to Bharata words fraught with virtue and interest.

sa_verse: किं करिष्याम्ययोध्यायां ताते दिष्टां गतिं गते। कस्तां राजवराद्धीनामयोध्यां पालयिष्यति॥ किं नु तस्य मया कार्यं दुर्जातेन महात्मनः। यो मृतो मम शोकेन स मया न च संस्कृतः॥
en: What shall I do with Ayodhyā, my sire having gone the way ordained by the gods? And who will govern her, now that she has been deprived of that foremost of monarchs? Of fruitless birth that I am, what can I do for that magnanimous one? And of him that renounced his life from grief on my account I have not even performed the last rites.

sa_verse: अहो भरत सिद्धार्थो येन राजा त्वयानघ। शत्रुघ्नेन च सर्वेषु प्रेतकृत्येषु सत्कृतः॥
en: Ah! Bharata, you, O sinless one, are blessed, since by you as well as by Satrughna have been performed all the funeral rites of the king.

sa_verse: निष्प्रधानामनेकानां नरेन्द्रेण विना कृताम्। निवृत्तवनवासोऽपि नायोध्यां गन्तुमुत्सहे॥
en: To Ayodhyā, bereft of the monarch, having none to preside over her destinies, and many rulers, will I not return even when the term of my abode in the woods has been passed.

sa_verse: समाप्तवनवासं मामयोध्यायां परंतप। कोऽनुशासिष्यति पुनस्ताते लोकान्तरं गते॥
en: My father having gone to the other world, who, O subduer of enemies, will again counsel me when, my stay in the forest being over, I shall have returned to Ayodhyā?

sa_verse: पुरा प्रेक्ष्य सुवृत्तं मां पिता यान्याह सान्त्वयन्। वाक्यानि तानि श्रोष्यामि कुतः कर्णसुखान्यहम्॥
en: And from whom shall I hear those words grateful to the ear, which my father gratifying me used to speak to me when I happened to do something well?

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वाथ भरतं भार्यामभ्येत्य राघवः। उवाच शोकसंतप्तः पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम्॥
en: Having thus addressed Bharata, Rāghava burning in grief spoke to his wife, with her countenance resembling the full moon, saying.

sa_verse: सीते मृतस्ते श्वशुरः पितृहीनोऽसि लक्ष्मण। भरतो दुःखमाचष्टे स्वर्गतिं पृथिवीपतेः॥
en: O Sītā, your father-in-law is dead and, O Laks inana, you are fatherless. Bharata has communicated to me that sad intelligence that our father has ascended heaven.

sa_verse: ततो बहुगुणं तेषां बाष्पं नेत्रेष्वजायत। तथा ब्रुवति काकुत्स्थे कुमाराणां यशस्विनाम्॥
en: When Kakutstha had said this, tears began to shower forth from the eyes of the renowned princes.

sa_verse: ततस्ते भ्रातरः सर्वे भृशमाश्वास्य दुःखितम्। अब्रुवञ्जगतीभर्तुः क्रियतामुदकं पितुः॥
en: Then all those brothers pacifying as best they could Rāma stricken with sorrow, said to him, “Do you perform the watery rites of that lord of the earth, our sire."

sa_verse: सा सीता स्वर्गतं श्रुत्वा श्वशुरं तं महानृपम्। नेत्राभ्यामश्रुपूर्णाभ्यां न शशाकेक्षितं प्रियम्॥
en: Having heard that her father-in-law, the king, had gone to the celestial regions. Sītā with her eyes filled with tears, could not see her beloved.

sa_verse: सान्त्वयित्वा तु मां रामो रुदती जनकात्मजाम्। उवाच लक्ष्मणं तत्र दुःखितो दुःखितं वचः॥
en: Thereupon, pacifying the weeping daughter of Janaka, Rama moved with grief, spoke to the distressed Laksmana, saying.

sa_verse: आनयेगुदिपिण्याकं चीरमाहर चोत्तरम्। जलक्रियार्थं तातस्य गमिष्यामि महात्मनः॥
en: Do you bring Ingudi fruits as well as a piece of new bark. I will go to perform the watery rites of our high-souled sire.

sa_verse: सीता पुरस्ताद् व्रजतु त्वमेनामभितो व्रज। अहं पश्चाद् गमिष्यामि गतिथेषा सुदारुणा॥
en: Let Sītā go first. do you follow her. I shall go last. Even this is the course of those in mourning.

sa_verse: ततो नित्यानुगस्तेषां विदितात्मा महामतिः। मृदुर्दान्तश्च कान्तश्च रामे च दृढभक्तिमान्॥ सुमन्त्रस्तैर्नृपसुतैः सार्धमाश्वास्य राघवम्। अवतारयदालम्ब्य नदीं मन्दाकिनी शिवाम्॥
en: Then that magnanimous one, having a knowledge of the soul, mild, graceful, capable of controlling his senses, steady in his regard for Rāma, and ever following him, Sumantra, in company with those sons of the auspicious river, Mandakini.

sa_verse: ते सुतीर्थां ततः कृच्छ्रादुपगम्य यशस्विनः। नदी मन्दाकिनी रम्यां सदा पुष्पितकाननाम्॥ शीघ्रस्रोतसमासाद्य तीर्थं शिवमकर्दमम्। सिषिचुस्तूदकं राज्ञे तत एतद् भवत्विति ॥
en: Then those illustrious ones, having in distress arrived at the river Mandākini, Mandākini, having convenient descents, charming, ever furnished with blossomag woods, and of rapid currents; and approached it descents, goodly and void of mud, offered water to the monarch, uttering, “May this be so!"

sa_verse: प्रगृह्य तु महीपालो जलापूरितमञ्जलिम्। दिशं याम्यामभिमुखो रुदन् वचनमब्रवीत्॥ एतत्ते राजशार्दूल विमलं तोयमक्षयम्। पितृलोकगतस्याद्य मद्दत्तमुपतिष्ठतु॥
en: The protector of the earth (Rama), holding water with his joined hands, facing the south, said weeping, “O foremost of monarchs, may this clear water knowing no deterioration, reach you, who have gone to the world of the ancestral manes.!"

sa_verse: ततो मन्दाकिनीतीरं प्रत्युत्तीर्य स राघवः। पितुश्चकार तेजस्वी निर्वापं भ्रातृभिः सह॥
en: Then drawing nigh to the marge of the Mandākinī, the energetic Rāghava along with his brothers, offered the Pinda to his father.

sa_verse: ऐङ्गदं बदरैर्मिश्रं पिण्याकं दर्भसंस्तरे। न्यस्य रामः सुदुःखार्तो रुदन् वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: And placing the Ingudi Pinda mixed with jujube on a bed of darbha, Rāma crying in distress, said.

sa_verse: इदं भुक्ष्व महाराज प्रीतो यदशना वयम्। यदन्नः पुरुषो भवति तदत्रास्तस्य देवताः॥
en: O mighty monarch! do you well pleased feed on this, which we also live upon. That which is the fare of an individual, is also the fare of his divinities.

sa_verse: ततस्तेनैव मार्गेण प्रत्युत्तीर्य सरित्तटात्। आरुरोह नरव्याघ्रो रम्यसानुं महीधरम्॥ ततः पर्णकुटीद्वारमासाद्य जगतीपतिः। परिजग्राह पाणिभ्यामुभौ भरतलक्ष्मणौ॥
en: Then that forernost of men ascending the bank of the stream by the self-same way, got up on the charming side of the hill. And having arrived at the gate of the cottage of leaves, that lord of the earth held both Bharata and Laksmana with his hands.

sa_verse: तेषां तु रुदतां शब्दात् प्रतिशब्दोऽभवद् गिरौ। भ्रातृणां सह वैदेह्या सिंहानां नर्दतामिव ॥
en: And there the hill reverberated at the sounds raised by the brothers wailing along with Vaidehi; like to lions roaring.

sa_verse: महाबलानां रुदतां कुर्वतामुदकं पितुः। विज्ञाय तुमुलं शब्दं त्रस्ता भरतसैनिकाः॥ अब्रुवंश्चापि रामेण भरतः संगतो ध्रुवम्। तेषामेव महाशब्दः शोचतां पितरं मृतम्॥
en: And perceiving the loud uproar of those mighty ones engaged in offering water to their sire, indulging in lamentations, the army of Bharata became agitated. And they said, “For certain Bharata has met with Rāma; and this mighty noise proceeds from them, as they are bitterly mourning their deceased sire.”

sa_verse: अथ वाहान् परित्यज्य तं सर्वेऽभिमुखाः स्वनम्। अप्येकमनसो जग्मुर्यथास्थानं प्रधाविता॥
en: Thereat leaving aside their vehicles, they with one mind, rushed towards the spot wherefrom proceeded the uproar.

sa_verse: हयैरन्ये गजैरन्ये रथैरन्ये स्वलंकृतैः। सुकुमारास्तथैवान्ये पद्भिरेव नरा ययुः॥
en: And of those that were tender, some went on horses, and some on elephants, and some on ornamented chariots, while others went on foot.

sa_verse: अचिरप्रोषितं रामं चिरविप्रोषितं यथा। द्रष्टुकामो जनः सर्वो जगाम सहसाश्रमम्॥
en: And eager to behold Rāma staying away for a short time though seeming to do so for a long period, all the men at once went to the hermitage.

sa_verse: भ्रातृणां त्वरितास्ते तु द्रष्टुकामाः समागमम्। ययुर्बहुविधैर्यानैः खुरनेमिसमाकुलैः॥
en: And desirous of witnessing the meeting of the brothers (with Rāma), they with all despatch proceeded by means of various vehicles consisting of beasts and cars.

sa_verse: सा भूमिर्बहुभिर्यानै रथनेमिसमाहता। मुमोच तुमुलं शब्दं द्यौरिवाभ्रसमागमे॥
en: And the ground trodden by the wheels of innumerable cars, emitted loud sounds, like those emitted by the sky on clouds gathering.

sa_verse: तेन वित्रासिता नागाः करेणुपरिवारिताः। आवासयन्तो गन्धेन जग्मुरन्यद्वनं ततः॥
en: And frightened by the uproar, elephants accompanied by female ones, perfuming all sides (by the fragrance of temporal juice), went to another forest.

sa_verse: वराहवृकसिंहाश्च महिषाः सृमरास्तथा। व्याघ्रगोकर्णगवया वित्रेसुः पृषतैः सह।॥
en: And boars, and deer, and lions, and buffaloes, and Srmaras, and tigers, and Gokarnas, Gavayas and Prşatas were stricken with panic.

sa_verse: रथाह्वहंसानत्यूहाः पल्वाः कारण्डवाः परे। तथा पुंस्कोकिलाः क्रौञ्चा विसंज्ञा भेजिरे दिशः।॥
en: And wild with alarm, Chakravakas, and swans, and Natyuhas, and Plavas, and Karandavas, and male coels, and Kraunchas, fled away in all directions.

sa_verse: तेन शब्देन वित्रस्तैराकाशं पक्षिभिर्वृतम्। मनुष्यैरावृता भूमिरुभयं प्रबभौ तदा॥
en: And the welkin was enveloped by birds frightened by the noise, as the earth was covered by men, and both the sky and the land then gave out great effulgence.

sa_verse: ततस्तं पुरुषव्याघ्रं यशस्विनमकल्मषम्। आसीनं स्थण्डिले रामं ददर्श सहसा जनः॥ विगर्हमाणः कैकेयीं मन्थरा सहितामपि। अभिगम्य जनो रामं बाष्पपूर्णमुखोऽभवत्॥
en: As the people suddenly saw that foremost of men, the sinless and illustrious Rāma seated on the ground, accusing Kaikeyi as well as the vile Mantharā, the people apprached Rāma, with their countenances discovering tears.

sa_verse: तान् नरान् बाष्पपूर्णाक्षान् समीक्ष्याथ सुदुःखितान्। पर्यष्वजत धर्मज्ञः पितृवन्मातृवच्च सः॥
en: Seeing those men oppressed with grief with their eyes filled with tears, that one cognizant of virtue like fathers and mothers, embraced those that deserved it.

sa_verse: स तत्र कांश्चित् परिषस्वजे नरान् नराश्च केचित्तु तमभ्यवादयन्। चकार सर्वान् सवयस्यबान्धवान् यथार्हमासाद्य तदा नृपात्मजः॥
en: And he embraced some persons; and some offered him salutations. And the king's son, as each deserved, properly received them along with their friends and equals in age.

sa_verse: ततः स तेषां रुदतां महात्मनां भुवं च खं चानुविनादयन् स्वनः। गुहा गिरीणां च दिशश्च संततं मृदङ्गघोषप्रतिमो विशुश्रुवे॥
en: And the sounds, produced by those high souled persons lamenting, resounding the earth and the sky, and the mountain-caverns, and all the cardinal points, were heard like peals of Mrdangas. And the sounds, produced by those high souled persons lamenting, resounding the earth and the sky, and the mountain-caverns, and all the cardinal points, were heard like peals of Mrdangas.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठः पुरतः कृत्वा दारान् दशरथस्य च। अभिचक्राम तं देशं रामदर्शनतर्षितः॥
en: Desirous of seeing Rama, Vasistha, taking before him the wives of Dasaratha, proceeded towards the hermitage.

sa_verse: राजपत्ल्यश्च गच्छन्त्यो मन्दं मन्दाकिनी प्रति। ददृशुस्तत्र तत् तीर्थं रामलक्ष्मणसेवितम्॥
en: And as the wives of the king were going slowly by the Mandākinī, they discovered the landing-place which was used by Rāma and Laksmana.

sa_verse: कौसल्या बाष्पपूर्णेन मुखेन परिशुष्यता। सुमित्रामब्रवीद् दीनां याश्चान्या राजयोषितः॥ इदं तेषामनाथानां क्लिष्टमक्लिष्टकर्मणाम्। वने प्राक्कलनं तीर्थं ये ते निर्विषयीकृताः॥
en: Thereupon Kausalyä, with her eyes filled with tears and her countenance rendered pale, observed to the forlorn Sumitra as well as the other wives of the king, sacred like to a first wife, in this forest this is the landing-place of those unfortunate ones of untiring energy, who had been deprived of the kingdom.

sa_verse: इतः सुमित्रे पुत्रस्ते सदा जलमतन्द्रितः। स्वयं हरति सौमित्रिर्मम पुत्रस्य कारणात्॥
en: From here, O Sumitrā, does your son, Saumitri, ever vigilant, personally procure water for my son.

sa_verse: जघन्यमपि ते पुत्रः कृतवान् न तु गर्हितः। भ्रातुर्यदर्थरहितं सर्वं तद् गर्हितं गुणैः॥
en: Although your son performs a servile office, yet he is not to blame: (the performance of) that alone which serves no purpose of his brother possessed of many perfections, could bring blame upon him.

sa_verse: अद्यायमपि ते पुत्रः क्लेशानामतथोचितः। नीचानर्थसमाचारं सज्जं कर्म प्रमुञ्चतु॥
en: To day let your son, who does not deserve such toilsome work, cease to perform that office which is fraught with hardship fit only for the base.

sa_verse: दक्षिणाग्रेषु दर्भेषु सा ददर्श महीतले। पितुरिङ्गुदिपिण्याकं न्यस्तमायतलोचना ॥
en: That lady of expansive eyes happened to see on the earth the Ingudī piņņa, which had been placed by Rāma for his sire on the darbha with their tops pointing south-wards.

sa_verse: तं भूमौ पितुरातेन न्यस्तं रामेण वीक्ष्य सा। उवाच देवी कौसल्या सर्वा दशरथस्त्रियः॥
en: Seeing this, which had been placed on the ground by Rāma disconsolate for his sire, the noble Kausaly, addressed all the wives of Dasaratha.

sa_verse: इदमिक्ष्वाकुनाथस्य राघवस्य महात्मनः। राघवेण पितुर्दत्तं पश्यतैतद् यथाविधि॥
en: Do you behold this that has been duly offered to the high-souled descendant of Raghu, lord of the Ikşvāku race, by Rāghava.

sa_verse: तस्य देवसमानस्य पार्थिवस्य महात्मनः। नैतदौपयिकं मन्ये भुक्तभोगस्य भोजनम्॥
en: Do not deem this as fit fare for that magnanimous monarch resembling a celestial, who had enjoyed every luxury (in life).

sa_verse: चतुरन्तां महीं भुक्त्वा महेन्द्रसदृशो भुवि। कथमिङ्गुदिपिण्याकं स भुङ्क्ते वसुधाधिपः॥
en: Having enjoyed this earth bounded by the four seas, how can that. lord of the world, resembling on earth the mighty Indra, feed on this Ingudī pinda?

sa_verse: अतो दु:खतरं लोके न किञ्चित् प्रतिभाति मे। यत्र रामः पितुर्दद्यादिगुदीक्षोदमृद्धिमान्॥
en: Nothing appears to me more deplorable in this world than this that the auspicious Rāma has offered an Ingudi cake to his father.

sa_verse: रामेणेङ्गुदिपिण्याकं पितुर्दत्तं समीक्ष्य मे। कथं दुःखेन हृदयं न स्फोटति सहस्रधा॥
en: Seeing the Ingudi pinda offered by Rāma to his father, why does not my heart break into a thousand shivers?

sa_verse: श्रुतिस्तु खल्वियं सत्या लौकिकी प्रतिभाति मे। यदन्नः पुरुषो भवति तदन्नास्तस्य देवता॥
en: Now the tradition in vogue among men, appearth to be true, viz., that “the fare that is pareaken by a person, is also partaken by his deity."

sa_verse: एवमाः सपत्न्यस्ता जग्मुराश्वास्य तां तदा। ददृशुश्चाश्रमे रामं स्वर्गच्युतमिवामरम्॥
en: Then those that were co-wives with her, consoled the distressed Kausalyā; and, (entering the asylum), beheld Rāma like an immortal dropped from the celestial regions.

sa_verse: तं भोगैः सम्परित्यक्तं रामं सम्प्रेक्ष्य मातरः। आर्ता मुमुचुरश्रूणि सस्वरं शोककर्शिताः॥
en: Seeing Rāma, who had been deprived of every comfort, his mothers, overwhelmed with grief and distress, began to shed tears, lamenting.

sa_verse: तासां रामः समुत्थाय जग्राह चरणाम्बुजान्। मातृणां मनुजव्याघ्रः सर्वासां सत्यसंगरः॥
en: Raising his mothers, that foremost of men, Rāma, true to his promise, took hold of those lotus feet of theirs.

sa_verse: ताः पाणिभिः सुखस्पर्श द्वङ्गुलितलैः शुभैः। प्रममा रजः पृष्ठाद् रामस्यायतलोचनाः॥
en: And those ones furnished with expansive eyes, (on their turn) by means of their fair hands of delicious feed furnished with soft fingers and palms, fell to rubbing the dust off Rāma's back.

sa_verse: सौमित्रिरपि ताः सर्वा मातृः सम्प्रेक्ष्य दुःखितः। अभ्यवादयदासक्तं शनै रामादनन्तरम्॥
en: After Rāma had done, Sumitrā's son also, seeing all his mothers, with sorrow gently paid his reverence to them with affection.

sa_verse: यथा रामे तथा तस्मिन् सर्वा ववृतिरे स्त्रियः। वृत्तिं दशरथाज्जाते लक्ष्मणे शुभलक्षणे॥
en: Thereat, as they had treated Rāma, all the ladies treated that one sprung from Daśaratha, Laksmana, graced with auspicious marks.

sa_verse: सीतापि चरणांस्तासामुपसंगृह्य दुःखिता। श्वश्रूणामश्रुपूर्णाक्षी सम्बभूवाग्रतः स्थिता॥
en: Sītă also with her eyes filled with tears, law, stood before them in distressful guise.

sa_verse: तां परिष्वज्य दुःखार्ता माता दुहितरं यथा। वनवासकृतां दीनां कौसल्या वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Embracing that woeful one in banishment, even as a mother does her daughter, Kausalya, smitten with grief, said.

sa_verse: वैदेहराजन्यसुता स्नुषा दशरथस्य च। रामपत्नी कथं दुःखं सम्प्राप्ता विजने वने॥
en: The daughter of Videha's King, and the daughter-in-law of Dasaratha, and the wife of Rāma himself, why does such a lady undergo misery in the lone forest?

sa_verse: पद्ममातपसंतप्तं परिक्लिष्टमिवोत्पलम्। काञ्चनं रजसा ध्वस्तं क्लिष्टं चन्द्रमिवाम्बुदैः॥ मुखं ते प्रेक्ष्य मां शोको दहत्यग्निरिवाश्रयम्। भृशं मनसि वैदेहि व्यसनारणिसम्भवः॥
en: O Vaidehi, beholding your face like to a lotus heated under the sun, or a lily that has been crushed, or like to gold covered with dust, or the moon enveloped by clouds, grief begot of this vortex of disaster that is in my mind, fiercely burns me, as fire consume a structure.

sa_verse: ब्रुवन्त्यामेवमार्तायां जनन्यां भरताग्रजः। पादावासाद्य जग्राह वसिष्ठस्य च राघवः॥
en: As his wretched mother was thus speaking, Bharata's elder brother, Räghava, approaching, took the feet of Vasiştha.

sa_verse: पुरोहतस्याग्निसमस्य तस्य वै बृहस्पतेरिन्द्र इवामराधिपः। प्रगृह्य पादौ सुसमृद्धतेजसः सहैव तेनोपविवेश राघवः॥
en: Having taken hold of the feet of the priest resembling a flame, and of accumulated energy, like to that lord of the immortals, Indra, taking the feet of Bșhaspati, Rāghava sat down with him.

sa_verse: ततो जघन्यं सहितैः स्वमन्त्रिभिः पुरप्रधानैश्च तथैव सैनिकैः। नुपोपविष्टो भरतस्तदाग्रजम्॥
en: Then behind them (Rāma and Vasistha), along with his own counsellors, and principal citizens, and generals, and persons of eminent piety, sat the virtuous Bharata in the presence of his elder brother.

sa_verse: स्तपस्विवेषेण समीक्ष्य राघवम्। र्यथा महेन्द्रः प्रयतः प्रजापतिम्॥
en: Seeing Raghava in the guise of an ascetic, flaming in grace, the exceedingly powerful Bharata with joined palms sat down in company with his brother, like the great Indra of controlled faculties in presence of Prajāpati.

sa_verse: किमेष वाक्यं भरतोऽद्य राघवं प्रणम्य सत्कृत्य च साधु वक्ष्यति। इतीव तस्यार्यजनस्य तत्त्वतो बभूव कौतूहलमुत्तमं तदा॥
en: What will Bharata, having bowed to Rāghava and paid him homage, will say to him?", this intense curiosity arose in (the mind of) all the noble persons present there.

sa_verse: स राघवः सत्यधृतिश्च लक्ष्मणो महानुभावो भरतश्च धार्मिकः। वृताः सुहृद्भिश्च विरेचिरेऽध्वरे यथा सदस्यैः सहितात्रयोऽग्नयः॥
en: And Rāghava having truth and forbearance, and Lakşmaņa endowed with magnanimity, and Bharata possessed of righteousness, surrounded by their friends, appeared (there) like to the three fires surrounded by Sadasyas.

sa_verse: ततः पुरुषसिंहानां वृतानां तैः सुद्गणैः। शोचतामेव रजनी दुःखेन व्यत्यवर्तत॥ रजन्यां सुप्रभातायां भ्रातरस्ते सुहृद्वताः। मन्दाकिन्यां हुतं जप्यं कृत्वा राममुपागमन्॥
en: As those foremost of persons surrounded by their friends indulged in lamentations, the night passed away in grief. On the night being succeeded by an auspicious morning, those brothers surrounded by their friends, having performed Homa and Japa on the Mandākinī, returned to Rāma.

sa_verse: तूष्णीं ते समुपासीना न कश्चित् किंचिदब्रवीत्। भरतस्तु सुहृन्मध्ये रामं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: And sitting silent, no one said anything. Then Bharata addressed Rāma in the midst of those friends, saying.

sa_verse: सान्त्विता मामिका माता दत्तं राज्यमिदं मम। तद् ददामि तवैवाहं भुक्ष्व राज्यमकण्टकम्॥
en: My mother was (first) pacified (by grant to the kingdom.) The kingdom is (now) mine. I grant the same to you. Do you enjoy the kingdom rid of its thorns.

sa_verse: महतेवाम्बुवेगेन भिन्नः सेतुर्जलागमे। दुरावरं त्वदन्येन राज्यखण्डमिदं महत्॥
en: Like to a dyke forced by a torrent during the rains, this mighty monarchy is difficult of being protected save by you.

sa_verse: गतिं खर इवाश्वस्य तार्थ्यस्येव पतत्त्रिणः। अनुगन्तुं न शक्तिर्मे गतिं तव महीपते॥
en: As a mule is incapable of imitating the course of a horse, or as birds, that of Tarksya, I, O Lord of earth, lack the strength to imitate you.

sa_verse: सुजीवं नित्यशस्तस्य यः परैरुपजीव्यते। राम तेन तु दुर्जीवं यः परानुपजीवति॥
en: O Rāma, ever happy is the life of him that others depend upon for subsistence: unhappy is the life of the person that depends upon others for support.

sa_verse: यथा तु रोपितो वृक्षः पुरुषेण विवर्धितः। ह्रस्वकेन दुरारोहो रूढस्कन्धो महादुमः॥ स यदा पुष्पितो भूत्वा फलानि न विदर्शयेत्। स तां नानुभवेत् प्रीतिं यस्य हेतोः प्ररोपितः॥ एषोपमा महाबाहो तदर्थ वेत्तुमर्हसि। यत्र त्वमस्मान् वृषभो भर्ता भृत्यान् न शाधि हि॥
en: As a tree planted by a person, and by him made to increase, (until at last), sending out branches, a mighty tree, it is incapable of being got up by a dwarf; and then if, flowering, it show no fruits, it cannot contribute to the satisfaction of him for whom it has been planted. O mightyarmed one, this comparison is meant for you. This* it behoves you to apprehend, inasmuch as you are our excellent lord, and you do not teach us who depend upon you for support. *The import of the image.

sa_verse: श्रेणयस्त्वां महाराज पश्यन्त्वग्र्याश्च सर्वशः। प्रतपन्तमिवादित्यं राज्यस्थितमरिंदमम्॥
en: Let the principal orders, O monarch, behold you, represser of foes, established in the kingdom, like the powerful sun himself.

sa_verse: तथानुयाने काकुत्स्थ मत्ता नर्दन्तु कुञ्जराः। अन्तःपुरगता नार्यो नन्दन्तु सुसमाहिताः॥
en: O Kākutstha, let mad elephants roar, with the view of following you; and let the women of the inner apartments with concentrated rainds utter jubilation.

sa_verse: तस्य साध्वनुमन्यन्त नागरा विविधा जनाः। भरतस्य वचः श्रुत्वा रामं प्रत्यनुयाचतः॥
en: On hearing the words of Bharata, who was beseeching Rāma, many of the citizens expressed their approbation by exclaiming, “Excellent well!"

sa_verse: तमेवं दुःखितं प्रेक्ष्य विलपन्तं यशस्विनम्। रामः कृतात्मा भरतं समाश्वासयदात्मवान्॥
en: Seeing the illustrious Bharata aggrieved and engaged in lamentation, the calm and considerate Rāma consoled him, saying.

sa_verse: नात्मन: कामकारो हि पुरुषोऽयमनीश्वरः। इतश्चेतरतश्चैनं कृतान्तः परिकर्षति॥
en: No creature is endowed with the power of exercising any control over the course of events, man has no independent status (in nature).

sa_verse: सर्वे क्षयान्ता निचयाः पतनान्ताः समुच्छ्याः। संयोगा विप्रयोगान्ता मरणान्तं च जीवितम्॥
en: The Destroyer draw him both here and hereafter. Those that increase, are destined to deteriorate; those that go upward, ultimately fall, those that come together, separate in the end; and life at length meet with death.

sa_verse: यथा फलानां पक्वानां नान्यत्र पतनाद् भयम्। एवं नरस्य जातस्य नान्यत्र मरणाद् भयम्॥
en: As a ripe fruit has no other fear than fall, so man who is born, has no other fear than death.

sa_verse: यथाऽऽगारं दृढस्थूणं जीर्णं भूत्वोपसीदति। तथावसीदन्ति नरा जरामृत्युवशंगताः॥
en: Even as a stout-pillared edifice, getting dilapidated, waxes weak, so men coming under the sway of decrepitude and death, get enfeebled.

sa_verse: अत्येति रजनी या तु सा न प्रतिनिवर्तते। यात्येव यमुना पूर्ण समुद्रमुदकार्णवम्॥
en: A night that has gone by, does not return, as the full Yamunā, when she has entered the ocean, does not come back.

sa_verse: अहोरात्राणि गच्छन्ति सर्वेषां प्राणिनामिह। आयूंषि क्षपयन्त्याशु ग्रीष्मे जलमिवांशवः॥
en: In this world, days and nights pass away with creatures, and speedily impair their lives, even as in summer the rays (of the sun) (dry up) the waters.

sa_verse: आत्मानमनुशोच त्वं किमन्यमनुशोचसि। आयुस्तु हीयते यस्य स्थितस्यास्य गतस्य च॥
en: Do you therefore deplore yourself. Why do you lament any thing else? Every one's life is decreasing, whether he sits or moves.

sa_verse: सहैव मृत्युजति सह मृत्युनिषीदति। गत्वा सुदीर्घमध्वानं सह मृत्युर्निवर्तते ॥
en: Death goes with one, sits down with one, and after having gone a long way, returns with one.

sa_verse: गात्रेषु वलयः प्राप्ताः श्वेताश्चैव शिरोरुहाः। जरया पुरुषो जीर्णः किं हि कृत्वा प्रभावयेत्॥
en: The person is filled with folds in the skin, the hair has grown hoary, the individual is enfeebled because of age, by doing what, can be prevent this?

sa_verse: नन्दन्त्युदित आदित्ये नन्दन्त्यस्तमितेऽहनि। आत्मनो नावबुध्यन्ते मनुष्या जीवितक्षयम्॥
en: People rejoice on the rising of the sun; they feel delighted at the approach of night, but they do not understand that their lives have (meanwhile) been shortened.

sa_verse: हृष्यन्त्य॒तुमुखं दृष्ट्वा नवं नवमिवागतम्। ऋतूनां परिवर्तेन प्राणिनां प्राणसंक्षयः॥
en: People are exhilarated at the commencement of a new season of novel fashion; creatures get their lives shortened at the change of season.

sa_verse: यथा काष्ठं च काष्ठं च समेयातां महार्णवे। समेत्य तु व्यपेयातां कालमासाद्य कंचन॥ एवं भार्याश्च पुत्राश्च ज्ञातयश्च वसूनि च। समेत्य व्यवधावन्ति ध्रुवो ह्येषां विनाभवः॥
en: As on the mighty ocean, one piece of wood comes in contact with another; so, a person, having been in association with another, is separated from him in time. In this way, wives and sons and kindred and wealth, having been in association, go away; their separation is certain.

sa_verse: नात्र कश्चिद् यथाभावं प्राणी समतिवर्तते। तेन तस्मिन् न सामर्थ्य प्रेतस्यास्त्यनुशोचतः॥
en: There exists not one in this world that can change one's nature as received. A person lamenting a dead individual, has no power to prevent his own death.

sa_verse: यथा हि सार्थं गच्छन्तं ब्रूयात् कश्चित् पथि स्थितः। अहमप्यागमिष्यामि पृष्ठतो भवतामिति ॥ एवं पूर्वैर्गतो मार्गः पैतृपितामहैध्रुवः। तमापन्नः कथं शोचेद् यस्य नास्ति व्यतिक्रमः॥ वयसः पतमानस्य स्रोतसो वानिवर्तिनः। आत्मा सुखे नियोक्तव्यः सुखभाजः प्रजाः स्मृताः।।३१।
en: As while one is proceeding on a road, another stationed by the way, says, 'I too will go in your wake,' even so, the way that has been followed by our predecessors, (must be followed as well by us.) Why should people mourn for deceased relatives), when they are themselves subject to the fate that knows no turning? (Perceiving the destruction of) life declining, like to a current that never turns back, one should engage his soul in happiness; for all men are said to be born for the same.

sa_verse: धर्मात्मा सुशुभैः कृत्स्नैः क्रतुभिश्चाप्तदक्षिणैः। धूतपापो गतः स्वर्गं पिता नः पृथिवीपतिः॥
en: My child, our righteous sire, who, after having performed excellent and entire sacrifices, accompanied with Dakșiņās, has repaired to heaven, honoured of he good, should not be mourned.* *The N.W.P. text has seven additional lines here, wanting in the other texts:, “That lord of the earth, our father Dasaratha, has gone to heaven. On account of having adequately maintained his servants and governed his subjects, and virtuously given away wealth, our sire has gone to heaven. And by virtue of supremely excellent and desirable acts, and sacrifices accompanied with Dakşiņā, that master of this world, our father Dasaratha, has ascended heaven. And having celebrated many sacrifices and enjoyed luxuries, that lord of the earth, having attained a goodly age, has gone to heaven."

sa_verse: भृत्यानां भरणात् सम्यक् प्रजानां परिपालनात्। अर्थादानाच्च धर्मेण पिता नस्त्रिदिवं गतः॥
en: Having renounced his human frame wasted and worn out with age, our father has attained celestial state, which exists in the regions of Brahma.

sa_verse: कर्मभिस्तु शुभैरिष्टैः क्रतुभिश्चाप्तदक्षिणैः। स्वर्ग दशरथः प्राप्तः पिता नः पृथिवीपतिः॥ इष्ट्वा बहुविधैर्यज्ञैर्भोगांश्चावाप्य पुष्कलान्। उत्तमं चायुरासाद्य स्वर्गतः पृथिवीपतिः॥ आयुरुत्तममासाद्य भोगानपि च राघवः। न स शोच्यः पिता तात स्वर्गतः सत्कृतः सताम्।।३६। स जीर्णमानुषं देहं परित्यज्य पिता हि नः। दैवीमृद्धिमनुप्राप्तो ब्रह्मलोकविहारिणीम्॥ तं तु नैवंविधः कश्चित् प्राज्ञः शोचितुमर्हसि। त्वद्विधो मद्विधश्चापि श्रुतवान् बुद्धिमत्तर॥ एते बहुविधाः शोका विलापरुदिते तदा। वर्जनीया हि धारेण सर्वावस्थासु धीमता॥ स स्वस्थो भव मा शोको यात्वा चावस तां पुरीम्। तथा पित्रा नियुक्तोऽसि वशिना वदतां वर॥ यत्राहमपि तेनैव नियुक्तः पुण्यकर्मणा। तत्रैवाहं करिष्यामि पितुरार्यस्य शासनम्॥
en: Such an one should never be mourned by any wise person like you or myself, accomplished in learning and more than ordinarily intelligent. Such manifold grief and mourning and lamentation should be renounced by intelligent and firm persons in all conditions in life. Do you cast off this grief: let not sorrow overpower you. Going thither, stay in that city. And, O best of speakers, this was also enjoined by our sire of controlled sense. I also must do my noble father's will as to whatever that one of pious acts has laid upon me.

sa_verse: न मया शासनं तस्य त्यक्तुं न्याय्यमरिंदम। स त्वयापि सदामान्यः स बन्धुः स नः पिता॥
en: O subduer foes, it is not proper for me to pass by his orders. So they are also worthy of being honoured by you. He is our friend and father.

sa_verse: तद् वचः पितुरेवाहं सम्मतं धर्मचारिणाम्। कर्मणा पालयिष्यामि वनवासेन राघव॥
en: O descendant of Raghu, that mandate of our righteous father, acceptable to me, will I obey by abiding in the woods.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा वचनं महात्मा पितुर्निदेशप्रतिपालनार्थम्। यवीयसं भ्रातरमर्थवच्च प्रभुर्मुहूर्ताद् विरराम रामः॥
en: Having said these significant words to his younger brother, with the view of making him obey the injuctions of their father, that lord, the magnanimous Rāma, paused.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा तु विरते रामे वचनमर्थवत्। ततो मन्दाकिनीतीरे रामं प्रकृतिवत्सलम्॥ उवाच भरतश्चित्रं धार्मिको धार्मिकं वचः। को हि स्यादीदृशो लोके यादृशस्त्वमरिंदम॥
en: On Rāma having stopped after speaking these pregnant words, the virtuous Bharata addressed the righteous Rāma attached to him subjects in an excellent speech on the banks of the Mandākinī, saying, “O vanquisher of foes, who is there is this world like to you?"

sa_verse: न त्वां प्रव्यथयेद् दुःखं प्रीतिर्वा न प्रहर्षयेत्। सम्मतश्चापि वृद्धानां तांश्च पृच्छसि संशयान्॥
en: Pain does not afflict you, nor does pleasure exhilarate. Yourself the exemplar of even aged people, you refer to them on doubtful point (of morality).

sa_verse: यथा मृतस्तथा जीवन् यथासति तथा सति। यस्यैष बुद्धिलाभः स्यात् परितप्येत केन सः॥
en: Living like to dead and existing like to nonexisting', what shall make a person that has attained this intellectual state, grieve?

sa_verse: परावरज्ञो यश्च स्यात् यथा त्वं मनुजाधिप। स एव व्यसनं प्राप्य न विषीदितुमर्हति ॥
en: O lord of men, he that like to you understands the nature of the soul and its environment coming by any calamity, ought not to despond.

sa_verse: अमरोपमसत्त्वस्त्वं महात्मा सत्यसंगरः। सर्वज्ञः सर्वदर्शी च बुद्धिमांश्चासि राघव ॥ न त्वामेवंगुणैर्युक्तं प्रभवाभवकोविदम्। अविषह्यतमं दुःखमासादयितुमर्हति॥
en: You resemble, O Rāghava, the god in strength, and are magnanimous, and truthful in promise, and know every thing and are endued with intelligence, Calamity, however unbearable it may be, should not overpower a person like yourself furnished with such virtues and cognizant of life and death.

sa_verse: प्रोषिते मयि यत् पापं मात्रा मत्कारणात् कृतम्। क्षुद्रया तदनिष्टं मे प्रसीदतु भवान् मम ॥
en: The sin that in my absence from home has been perpetrated by my mean-minded mother does not find favour in my sight. Be you therefore propitious to me.

sa_verse: धर्मबन्धेन बद्धोऽस्मि तेनेमां नेह मातरम्। हन्मि तीव्रण दण्डेन दण्डाहाँ पापकारिणीम्॥
en: I am bound by the fetters of religion. For this it is that I do not by a severe penalty slay my wicked mother deserving of chastisement.

sa_verse: कथं दशरथाज्जातः शुभाभिजनकर्मणः। जानन् धर्ममधर्मं च कुर्यां कर्म जुगुप्सितम्॥
en: How having sprung from Dasaratha of righteous deeds and born of immaculate race, and knowing virtue and vice, can I commit such a reprehensible action?

sa_verse: गुरुः क्रियावान् वृद्धश्च राजा प्रेतः पितेति च। तातं न परिगर्हेऽहं दैवतं चेति संसदि॥
en: Dasaratha is our superior, of meritorious acts, aged our king, a departed spirit, and our father, it is on account of this that I do not censure our father who is a deity to us.

sa_verse: को हि धर्मार्थयो नमीदृशं कर्म किल्बिषम्। स्त्रियः प्रियचिकीर्षुः सन् कुर्याद् धर्मज्ञ धर्मवित्॥
en: O cognizant of virtue, what virtuous person conversant in morality, should, seeking the pleasure of his wife, commit such a sinful act devoid of both righteousness and interest?

sa_verse: अन्तकाले हि भूतानि मुह्यन्तीति पुरा श्रुतिः । राज्ञैवं कुर्वता लोके प्रत्यक्षा सा श्रुतिः कृता॥
en: Creatures, as their end approaches, lose their sense this ancient adage has been illustrated in the world by the course the king has taken.

sa_verse: साध्वर्थमभिसंधाय क्रोधान्मोहाच्च साहसात्। तातस्य यदतिक्रान्तं प्रत्याहरतु तद् भवान्॥
en: Do you, intent upon bringing about good, redeem the wrong that has been done by our sire through anger, ignorance and recklessness.

sa_verse: पितुर्हि समतिक्रान्तं पुत्रो यः साधु मन्यते। तदपत्यं मतं लोके विपरीतमतोऽन्यथा ॥
en: The son that repair the wrong done by his father by acting contrary to the latter is in this world considered really a son; but not he that acts otherwise.

sa_verse: तदपत्यं भवानस्तु मा भवान् दुष्कृतं पितुः। अति यत् तत् कृतं कर्म लोके धीरविगर्हितम्॥
en: Be you that (real) son (of the monarch). Do you not approve the action of your father, since what he has done is divorced from righteousness and is blameworthy.

sa_verse: कैकेयीं मां च तातं च सुहृदो बान्धवांश्च नः। पौरजानपदान् सर्वास्त्रातुं सर्वमिदं भवान्॥
en: Do you rescue all these, Kaikeyī, myself, my father, our friends and adherents, and the whole body of the citizens as well as the inhabitants of the provinces.

sa_verse: क्व चारण्यं क्व च क्षात्रं व जटाः क्व च पालनम्। ईदृशं व्याहतं कर्म न भवान् कर्तुमर्हति॥
en: Where is the forest? And where is Ksatriya morality? Where are matted locks? And where is your government of the country? It behoves you not to act in such an untoward way.

sa_verse: एष हि प्रथमो धर्मः क्षत्रियस्याभिषेचनम्। येन शक्यं महाप्राज्ञ प्रजानां परिपालनम्॥
en: Even this is the first duty of a Ksatriya, viz., getting oneself installed, by means of which, O highly wise one, he can compass the government of the people.

sa_verse: कश्च प्रत्यक्षमुत्सृज्य संशयस्थमलक्षणम्। आयतिस्थं चरेद् धर्मं क्षत्रबन्धुरनिश्चितम्॥
en: What base Ksatriya setting aside this indubitable morality, resorts to a dubious and inauspicious course, which should be followed by the old alone?

sa_verse: अथ क्लेशजमेव त्वं धर्म चरितुमिच्छसि। धर्मेण चतुरो वर्णान् पालयन् क्लेशमाप्नुहि ॥
en: But if you be bent upon practising this austere morality, do you undergo this trouble, after having righteously ruled the four orders.

sa_verse: चतुर्णामाश्रमाणां हि गार्हस्थ्यं श्रेष्ठमुत्तमम्। आहुर्धर्मज्ञ धर्मज्ञास्तं कथं त्यक्तुमिच्छसि॥
en: O you cognizant of morality, those versed in duty say that of the four modes of life, the life of the householder is the foremost. Why then do you wish to renounce the same?

sa_verse: श्रुतेन बालः स्थानेन जन्मना भवतो ह्यहम्। स कथं पालयिष्यामि भूमिं भवति तिष्ठति ॥
en: I am inferior to you in learning, in position, and in birth. How can I then govern the earth, you existing?

sa_verse: हीनबुद्धिगुणो बाला हीनस्थानेन चाप्यहम्। भवता च विनाभूतो न वर्तयितुमुत्सहे॥
en: Void of sense and quality, a body, and inferior to you in point of years, I, deprived of you, can not live.

sa_verse: इदं निखिलमप्यग्र्यं राज्यं पित्र्यमकण्टकम्। अनुशाधि स्वधर्मेण धर्मज्ञ सह बान्धवैः॥
en: O you cognizant of morality, do you, along with your friends, according to your proper morality rule this entire ancestral kingdom rid of its thorns and enjoying tranquillity.

sa_verse: इहैव त्वाभिषिञ्चन्तु सर्वाः प्रकृतयः सह। ऋत्विजः सवसिष्ठाश्च मन्त्रविन्मन्त्रकोविदाः॥
en: Even here, O you cognizant of the Mantras, let all the subjects and the Rtvijas with Vasiștha, versed in the Mantras, install you.

sa_verse: अभिषिक्तस्त्वमस्माभिरयोध्यां पालने व्रज। विजित्य तरसा लोकान् मरुद्भिरिव वासवः॥
en: Having been installed, go to Ayodhyā for the purpose of governing it, having with our assistance conquered your enemies by they strength, like Vāsava conquering (his foes) with the help of the Maruts.

sa_verse: ऋणानि त्रीण्यपाकुर्वन्दुहृदः साधु निर्दहन्। सुहृदस्तर्पयन् कामैस्त्वमेवात्रानुशाधि माम्॥
en: Having freed yourself from the threefold debts, do you govern me, repressing your foes, and propitiating your friends with every gratification.

sa_verse: अद्यापि मुदिताः सन्तु सुहृदस्तेऽभिषेचने। अद्य भीताः पलायन्तु दुष्प्रदास्ते दिशो दश॥
en: O noble one, to-day let your friends rejoice in consequence of your coronation. To day let those that intend to do you harm, being frightened, fly to the ten cardinal points.
