sa_verse: आक्रोशं मम मातुश्च प्रमृज्य पुरुषर्षभ। अद्य तत्रभवन्तं च पितरं रक्ष किल्बिषात्॥
en: O foremost of men, wiping out the disgrace of my mother, do you emancipate our sire from sin.

sa_verse: शिरसा त्वाभियाचेऽहं कुरुष्व करुणां मयि। बान्धवेषु च सर्वेषु भूतेष्विव महेश्वरः॥
en: I beseech you with bent head. Be you merciful to me, to all our friends, and, O great lord, to all creatures in general.

sa_verse: अथवा पृष्ठतः कृत्वा वनमेव भवानितः। गमिष्यति गमिष्यामि भवता सार्धमप्यहम्॥
en: But, if disregarding my solicitations, you wend from here to the forest, I shall go along with you.

sa_verse: तथाभिरामो भरतेन ताम्यता प्रसाद्यमानः शिरसा महीपतिः। न चैव चक्रे गमनाय सत्त्ववान् मतिं पितुस्तद् वचने प्रतिष्ठितः॥
en: Although thus besought and propitiated by Bharata with bent head, that lord of the earth, Rāma, possessed of strength, established in the words of his father, did not decide for going.

sa_verse: तदद्भुतं स्थैर्यमवेक्ष्य राघवे समं जनो हर्षमवाप दुःखितः। न यात्ययोध्यामिति दुःखितोऽभवत् स्थिरप्रतिज्ञत्वमवेक्ष्य हर्षितः॥
en: Witnessing that wonderful firmness in Rāghava, the people were at one and the same time delighted and depressed. They were aggrieved because he would not go to Ayodhyā; they rejoiced on seeing his firm resolution.

sa_verse: स्तथा विसंज्ञाश्रुकलाश्च मातरः। तथा ब्रुवाणं भरतं प्रतुष्टवुः प्रणम्य रामं च ययाचिरे सह॥
en: Then the Rtvijas, the citizens, and their leaders, and the mothers with their senses lost and with tears in their eyes, extolled Bharata as he was speaking thus; and, bowing down to Rāma, they directed their solicitations together.

sa_verse: पुनरेवं ब्रुवाणं तं भरतं लक्ष्मणाग्रजः। प्रत्युवाच ततः श्रीमाज्ञातिमध्ये सुसत्कृतः॥
en: As Bharata was again ng in this strain, his graceful elder brother, having been highly honoured, answered Bharata in the midst of his relatives, saying.

sa_verse: उपपन्नमिदं वाक्यं यस्त्वमेवमभाषथाः। जातः पुत्रो दशरथात् कैकेय्यां राजसत्तमात्॥
en: Having been born as a son to Daśaratha, foremost of monarchs, by Kaikeyſ, this speech of yours is worthy of you.

sa_verse: पुरा भ्रातः पिता नः स मातरं ते समुद्वहन्। मातामहे समाश्चौषीद् राज्यशुल्कमनुत्तमम्॥
en: O brother, formerly when our father espoused the hand of your mother, he promised her the kingdom as her marriage portion.

sa_verse: देवासुरे च संग्रामे जनन्यै तव पार्थिवः। सम्प्रहृष्टो ददौ राजा वरमाराधितः प्रभुः॥
en: Then on the occasion of the war between the gods and the Asuras, that master, the king, well pleased (with her), being besought, granted her a boon.

sa_verse: ततः सा सम्प्रतिश्राव्य तव माता यशस्विनी। अयाचत नरश्रेष्ठं द्वौ वरौ वरवर्णिनी॥ तव राज्यं नरव्याघ्र मम प्रव्राजनं तथा। तच्च राजा तथा तस्यै नियुक्तः प्रददौ वरम्॥
en: Having been thus promised, that virtuous lady, your illustrious mother, O foremost of men, asked for two boons (of the king), viz., your enthronement, O best of men, and my banishment. Thus besought by her, the king conferred on her the boon.

sa_verse: तेन पित्राहमप्यत्र नियुक्तः पुरुषर्षभ । चतुर्दश वने वासं वर्षाणि वरदानिकम्।७।।
en: And, you foremost of men, I have been enjoyed by my sire to stay in the woods for fourteen years, in consequence of his having granted her the boon.

sa_verse: सोऽयं वनमिदं प्राप्तो निर्जनं लक्ष्मणान्वितः। सीतया चाप्रतिद्वन्द्वः सत्यवादे स्थितः पितुः॥
en: And, having, in company with Laksmana and Sītā, come to the lone forest, I in humble guise am staying in the truthful speech of my father.

sa_verse: भवानपि तथेत्येव पितरं सत्यवादिनम्। कर्तुमर्हसि राजेन्द्र क्षिप्रमेवाभिषिञ्चनात्॥
en: You too, you foremost of kings, should in the same way speedily render our father truthful, by getting yourself installed.

sa_verse: ऋणान्मोचय राजानं मत्कृते भरत प्रभुम्। पितरं त्राहि धर्मज्ञ मातरं चाभिनन्दय॥
en: O Bharata, for my sake, do you free that lord, the king, from his debts. Do you, O you cognizant of morality, deliver our father and gladden your mother.

sa_verse: श्रूयते धीमता तात श्रुतिर्गीता यशस्विना। गयेन यजमानेन गयेष्वेव पितृन् प्रति॥
en: O child, we hear that in Gaya, formerly the famous Gaya, engaged in a sacrifice, chanted this Vedic hymn, for pleasing his departed ancestors.

sa_verse: पुन्नाम्नो नरकाद् यस्मात् पितरं त्रायते सुतः। तस्मात् पुत्र इति प्रोक्तः पितृन् यः पाति सर्वतः॥
en: Since a son delivers his sire form the hell named Put, a son goes by the appellation of putra, he protecting his (departed) ancestors in every way.

sa_verse: एष्टव्या बहवः पुत्रा गुणवन्तो बहुश्रुताः। तेषां वै समवेतानामपि कश्चिद् गयां व्रजेत्॥
en: One should wish for many sons crowned with qualities and versed in various lore, for the chance is that one at least of these may repair to Gayā.

sa_verse: एवं राजर्षयः सर्वे प्रतीता रघुनन्दन। तस्मात् त्राहि नरश्रेष्ठ पितरं नरकात् प्रभो॥
en: O son of the Raghu race, the Rājarşis have delivered their decision on the point. Therefore, you foremost of men, do you, O lord, rescue your sire from hell.

sa_verse: अयोध्यां गच्छ भरत प्रकृतीरुपरञ्जय। शत्रुघ्नसहितो वीर सह सर्वैद्विजातिभिः॥
en: O Bharata, go to Ayodhyā, and please the subjects, in company with Śatrughna, O hero, and all the regenerate ones.

sa_verse: प्रवेक्ष्ये दण्डकारण्यमहमप्यविलम्बयन्। आभ्यां तु सहितो वीर वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणेन च॥
en: I also, O hero, without delay shall have to enter the forest of Dandaka in company with Lakşmaņa and Sītā.

sa_verse: त्वं राजा भरत भव स्वयं नराणां वन्यानामहमपि राजराणमृगाणाम्। गच्छ त्वं पुरवरमद्य सम्प्रहृष्टः संहृष्टस्त्वहमपिदण्डकान् प्रवेक्ष्ये॥
en: O Bharata, be you yourself the monarch of men. I shall become the king of kings of deer. Go you to that foremost of cities with a glad heart: with a glad heart will I enter Dandaka.

sa_verse: छायां ते दिनकरभाः प्रबाधमानं वर्षत्रं भरत करोतु मूर्धि शीताम्। एतेषामहमपि काननदुमाणां छायां तामतिशयिनीं शनैः श्रयिष्ये॥
en: O Bharata, let the umbrella barring out the rays of the sun, afford cool shade to your head: I shall happily seek the dense shade of these forest trees.

sa_verse: शत्रुघ्नस्त्वतुलमतिस्तु ते सहायः सौमित्रिर्मम विदितः प्रधानमित्रम्। चत्वारस्तनयवरा वयं नरेन्द्र सत्यस्थं भरत चराम मा विषीद॥
en: Śatrughna endued with cleverness is your helper: Sumitrā's son is well known as my best friend. We four worthy sons of that foremost of monarchs will keep him established in truth, O Bharata. Let not your mind despond.

sa_verse: आश्वासयन्तं भरतं जाबालिब्राह्मणोत्तमः। उवाच रामं धर्मज्ञं धर्मापेतमिदं वचः॥
en: As Rāma cognizant of righteousness was thus encouraging Bharata, Jābāli, best of Brāhmaṇas, addressed him in words divorced from morality, saying.

sa_verse: साधु राघव मा भूत् ते बुद्धिरेवं निरर्थिका। प्राकृतस्य नरस्येव ह्यार्यबुद्धेस्तपस्विनः॥
en: O Rāghava, endowed with a noble understanding and leading a life of asceticism, do not suffer your intellect to entertain inanities, like any low person.

sa_verse: कः कस्य पुरुषो बन्धुः किमाप्यं कस्य केनचित्। एको हि जायते जन्तुरेक एव विनश्यति॥ तस्मान्माता पिता चेति राम सज्जेत यो नरः। उन्मत्त इव स ज्ञेयो नास्ति कश्चिद्धि कस्यचित्॥
en: Who is whose friend ? And to what is one entitled and by virtue of what relation? And who is such ? Since a creature is born alone and dies alone, a person that cherishes his father and mother with affection, must, O Rāma, be looked upon as a madman.

sa_verse: पित्र्यं राज्यं समुत्सृज्य स नार्हसि नरोत्तम। आस्थातुं कापथं दुःखं विषमं बहुकण्टकम्॥
en: Therefore, O best of men, leaving your ancestral kingdom, you ought not to abide in the disagreeable forest filled with dangers and difficulties.

sa_verse: समृद्धायामयोध्यायामात्मानमभिषेचय। एकवेणीधरा हित्वा नगरी सम्प्रतीक्षते॥
en: Do you get yourself sprinkled in the prosperous Ayodhyā. The city expects you wearing a single woven braid.

sa_verse: राजभोगाननुभवन् महार्हान् पार्थिवात्मज। विहर त्वमयोध्यायां यथा शक्रस्त्रिविष्टपे॥
en: O king's son, enjoying costly regal pleasures, do you sport in Ayodhyā, like Sakra in the celestial regions.

sa_verse: न ते कश्चिद् दशरथस्त्वं च तस्य न कश्चन। अन्यो राजा त्वमन्यस्तु तस्मात् कुरु यदुच्यते॥
en: Dasaratha is none to you, and you too are none's to Dasaratha. He is quite other than your sire; and you have no connection with him. Therefore, do you act as I tell you.

sa_verse: बीजमात्रं पिता जन्तोः शुक्रं शोणितमेव च। संयुक्तमृतुमन्मात्रा पुरुषस्येह जन्म तत्॥
en: A father is merely an instrumental cause (touching the generation of his child). A father's semen coming in contact with a mother's blood, at the time befitting conception, a person is brought into being.

sa_verse: गतः स नृपतिस्तत्र गन्तव्यं यत्र तेन वै। प्रवृत्तिरेषा भूतानां त्वं तु मिथ्या विहन्यसे॥
en: The king has gone the way he should. This is the nature of all creatures. But you for naught deny yourself (the manliness of monarchical power).

sa_verse: अर्थधर्मपरा ये ये तांस्ताशोचामि नेतरान्। ते हि दुःखमिह प्राप्य विनाशं प्रेत्य लेभिरे॥
en: Those that disregarding interest are devoted to virtue, do I mourn, and not others; for having suffered misery here, they in the end meet with extinction.

sa_verse: अष्टकापितृदेवत्यमित्ययं प्रसृतो जनः। अन्नस्योपद्रवं पश्य मृतो हि किमशिष्यति॥
en: People engage in Aştaka* in behalf of ancestors and deities. Behold the waste of edibles. Does any dead person feed? *The lunar days and months for propitiating the manes.

sa_verse: यदि भुक्तमिहान्येन देहमन्यस्य गच्छति। दद्यात् प्रवसतां श्राद्धं न तत् पथ्यशनं भवेत्॥
en: If food partaken by one is transferred to the body of another, offer Śrāddha to one going to a distant and, that shall serve for his provender on the way.

sa_verse: दानसंवनना ह्येते ग्रन्था मेधाविभिः कृताः। यजस्व देहि दीक्षस्व तपस्तप्यस्व संत्यज ॥
en: Works (on morality) enjoining, Worship,' 'Give away,' 'Be initiated,' 'Observe rites,' "Renounce', have been composed by intelligent persons, for inducing people to be charitable.

sa_verse: स नास्ति परमित्येतत् कुरु बुद्धिं महामते। प्रत्यक्षं यत् तदातिष्ठ परोक्षं पृष्ठतः कुरु॥
en: O magnanimous one, assure yourself there is no hereafter. Do you remain grounded in the evident, turning your back on what is beyond our ken.

sa_verse: सतां बुद्धिं पुरस्कृत्य सर्वलोकनिदर्शिनीम्। राज्यं स त्वं निगृह्णीष्व भरतेन प्रसादितः॥
en: Placing in front the intellect of the good, and approved by all, do you, propitiated by Bharata, accept the monarchy.

sa_verse: जाबालेस्तु वचः श्रुत्वा रामः सत्यपराक्रमः। उवाच परया सूक्त्या बुद्ध्याविप्रतिपत्रया।॥
en: Hearing Jāvāli's words, Rāma having truth for prowess, by help of an exceedingly subtle intellect uninfluenced (under the exhortations of that sage), said.

sa_verse: भवान् मे प्रियकामार्थं वचनं यदिहोक्तवान्। अकार्यं कार्यसंकाशमपथ्यं पथ्यसंनिभम् ॥
en: What you, wishing for my welfare, have dwelt upon, though wearing the guise of a good action, is really not such; and though appearing to be beneficent, is really calculated to entail misery.

sa_verse: निर्मर्यादस्तु पुरुषः पापाचारसमन्वितः। मानं न लभते सत्सु भिन्नचारित्रदर्शनः॥
en: The person that with his sinful acts sticking to him, walk astray, as well as he that hold up (to others) different patterns of character (from those recommended by scripture), does not win honour with the good.

sa_verse: कुलीनमकुलीनं वा वीरं पुरुषमानिनम्। चारित्रमेव व्याख्याति शुचिं वा यदि वाशुचिम्॥
en: One's character (fashioned according to scripture) shows whether one is high-born or base, heroic or vainly priding himself on his manliness, pure or impure.

sa_verse: अनार्यस्त्वार्यसंस्थानः शौचाद्धीनस्तथा शुचिः। लक्षण्यवदलक्षण्यो दुःशीलः शीलवानिव ॥
en: But by adopting the code of conduct inculcated by you), a mean character may appear as a noble one, one bereft of purity may appear pure, an inauspicious individual may seem auspicious, and one of vile ways may appear honest.

sa_verse: अधर्मं धर्मवेषेण यद्यहं लोकसंकरम्। अभिपत्स्ये शुभं हित्वा क्रियां विधिविवर्जिताम्॥ कश्चेतयानः पुरुषः कार्याकार्यविचक्षणः। बहु मन्येत मां लोके दुर्वृत्तं लोकदूषणम्।७।।
en: If I adopt this unrighteous course, calculated to produce confusion of castes, and do acts not recognized by scripture, I shall, renouncing good, have to reap only evil. Then what man possessed of consciousness and capable of discriminating between right and wrong, shall honour me, given to wicked ways and deserving of universal reprehension?

sa_verse: कस्य यास्याम्यहं वृत्तं केन वा स्वर्गमाप्नुयाम्। अनया वर्तमानोऽहं वृत्त्या हीनप्रतिज्ञया॥
en: Whose is this course (that you asked me to follow)? And by what way shall I attain heaven, by following the present course, which would make me give up my vow?

sa_verse: कामवृत्तोऽन्वयं लोकः कृत्स्नः समुपवर्तते। यद्वृत्ताः सन्ति राजानस्तद्वृत्ताः सन्ति हि प्रजाः॥
en: When I have (first) myself set up desire as my standard of action, the entire body of the people shall follow me: subjects take to the ways that have been adopted by their sovereigns.

sa_verse: सत्यमेवानृशंसं च राजवृत्तं सनातनम्। तस्मात् सत्यात्मकं राज्यं सत्ये लोकः प्रतिष्ठितः॥
en: This eternal regal morality founded in kindness towards the subjects, is verily true. Hence a kingdom is essentially based upon truth; and this world itself is established in truth.

sa_verse: ऋषयश्चैव देवाश्च सत्यमेव हि मेनिरे। सत्यवादी हि लोकेऽस्मिन् परं गच्छति चाक्षयम्॥
en: Saints and celestials for certain regard truth alone. In this world a truthful person attains the regions of Brahma.

sa_verse: उद्विजन्ते यथा सनिरादनृतवादिनः
धर्मः सत्यपरो लोके मूलं सर्वस्य चोच्यते॥
en: Untruthful persons harass people as much as serpents. In this world virtue, which is said to be the root of everything, is itself established in truth.

sa_verse: सत्यमेवेश्वरो लोके सत्ये धर्मः सदाश्रितः। सत्यमूलानि सर्वाणि सत्यानास्ति परं पदम्॥
en: In this world, truth is the Lord; in truth is established righteousness. Everything has truth for its basis. No condition is superior to truth.

sa_verse: दत्तमिष्टं हुतं चैव तप्तानि च तपांसि च। वेदाः सत्यप्रतिष्ठानास्तस्मात् सत्यपरो भवेत्॥
en: The Veda, which inculcates gift, sacrifice homa, and asceticism, is based on truth. I never or

sa_verse: एकः पालयते लोकमेकः पालयते कुलम्। मज्जत्येको हि निरय एकः स्वर्गे महीयते॥
en: One protects men, another his family; one is plunged in hell, another is honoured in heaven.

sa_verse: सोऽहं पितुर्निदेशं तु किमर्थं नानुपालये। सत्यप्रतिश्रवः सत्यं सत्येन समयीकृतम्॥
en: Why should I not then obey the mandate of my father? My father was of truthful promise and of righteous ways and with the object of observing his promise, he laid on me this (for faithful performance).

sa_verse: नैव लोभान्न मोहाद् वा न चाज्ञानात् तमोऽस्वितः। सेतुं सत्यस्य भेत्स्यामि गुरोः सत्यप्रतिश्रवः॥
en: Having promised to my superior with an oath, shall through covetousness forgetfulness or pride rive the bridge of truth.

sa_verse: असत्यसंधस्य सतश्चलस्यास्थिरचेतसः। नैव देवा न पितरः प्रतीच्छन्तीति नः श्रुतम्॥
en: We have heard that the gods and the Pitrs do not accept offerings from one inclined to untruth, or who is unsteady and of volatile faculties.

sa_verse: प्रत्यगात्ममिमं धर्मं सत्यं पश्याम्यहं ध्रुवम्। भारः सत्पुरुषैश्चीर्णस्तदर्थमभिनन्द्यते॥
en: This duty of maintaining truth, whose influence radiates all over one's soul, I certainly find to be the prime one; and this burthen has (ere this) been borne by worthy people. And it is for this that I reverence the same.

sa_verse: क्षात्रं धर्ममहं त्यक्ष्ये ह्यधर्मं धर्मसंहितम्। क्षुदैर्नृशंसैलुब्धैश्च सेवितं पापकर्मभिः॥
en: I abjure that Ksatriya morality which, wearing the garb of righteousness, is in fact impiety, and which is pursued by the low-minded, or the wicked, or the covetous, or the sinful.

sa_verse: कायेन करते पापं मनसा सम्प्रधार्य तत्। अनृतं जिह्वया चाहं त्रिविधं कर्म पातकम्॥
en: A person commits a sin by means of his physique, but after he has accurately cast it in his mind; and he has also spoken an untruth with his tongue. Thus a sin is threefold.

sa_verse: भूमिः कीर्तिर्यशो लक्ष्मीः परुषं प्रार्थयन्ति हि। सत्यं समनुवर्तन्ते सत्यमेव भजेत् ततः॥
en: The earth and fame and renown and auspiciousness pay court to the truthful person. The good follow truth, therefore even truth is to be sought by all).

sa_verse: श्रेष्ठं ह्यनार्यमेव स्याद् यद् भवानवधार्य माम्। आह युक्तिकरैर्वाक्यैरिदं भद्रं कुरुष्व ह॥
en: Therefore the seemingly excellent thing that you, after having well ascertained it, have said to me in words informed with reason, 'You had rather do this', appears to me wanting in nobility.

sa_verse: कथं ह्यहं प्रतिज्ञाय वनवासमिमं गुरोः। भरतस्य करिष्यामि वचो हित्वा गुरोर्वचः॥
en: How, after having promised to my superior this exile of mine, shall I act up to Bharata's words, setting aside those of my superior?

sa_verse: स्थिरा मया प्रतिज्ञाता प्रतिज्ञा गुरुसंनिधौ। प्रहृष्टमानसा देवी कैकेयी चाभवत् तदा॥
en: I having remained firm in the promise I had made to my superior, that noble lady, Kaikeyi, became exceedingly delighted.

sa_verse: वनवासं वसनेव शुचिनियतभोजनः। मूलपुष्पफलैः पुण्यैः पितॄन् देवांश्च तर्पयन्॥
en: Living in the forest, pure, with regulated fare, I shall, propitiating the gods and the Pits with fruits, flowers and roots, (fulfil my vow).

sa_verse: संतुष्टपञ्चवर्गोऽहं लोकयात्रां प्रवाहये। अकुहः श्रद्दधानः सन्कार्याकार्यविचक्षणः॥
en: Satisfying the five classes,* I shall, retaining my simplicity and my religious faith and being able to distinguish between right and wrong, pass away the term (of banishment). * Prakrāmam, The commentator says that the word means, Cāndrāyaṇa and other rites.

sa_verse: कर्मभूमिमिमां प्राप्य कर्तव्यं कर्म यच्छुभम्। अग्निर्वायुश्च स्मश्च कर्मणां फलभागिनः॥
en: Having come to this scene of action, one should do that which is proper. Even Agni and Vāyu and Soma reap the fruits of their own acts.

sa_verse: शतं क्रतूनामाहृत्य देवराट् त्रिदिवं गतः। तपांस्युग्राणि चास्थाय दिवं प्राप्ता महर्षयः॥
en: Having acquired the appellation of Satakratu, the sovereign of the celestials has gone to heaven; and, having performed rigid austerities, the Maharșis have attained the celestial regions.'

sa_verse: अमृष्यमाणः पुनरुग्रतेजा निशम्य तन्नास्तिकवाक्यहेतुम्। अथाब्रवीत् तं नृपतेस्तनूजो विगर्हमाणो वचनानि तस्य॥
en: Having heard of the reasons couched in that atheristical speech which he could not allow to pass without stricture, that one of fierce energy, the king's son, censuring what Jāvāli had said, again spoke.

sa_verse: सत्यं च धर्मं च पराक्रमं च भूतानुकम्पां प्रियवादितां च। द्विजातिदेवातिथिपूजनं च पन्थानमाहुत्रिदिवस्य सन्तः॥
en: Truth, righteousness, prowess, kindness to creatures, fair spokeness, and worship of the twice-born ones, gods and guests, these have by the pious been styled the ways to heaven.

sa_verse: तेनैवमाज्ञाय यथावदर्थमेकोदयं सम्प्रतिपद्य विप्राः। धर्मं चरन्तः सकलं यथावत् कानन्ति लोकागममप्रमत्ताः॥
en: Having duly heard that these are capable of conferring the summum bonum, and also come to the same conclusion by reasoning, Vipras, adequately and competely observing morality with the utmost care, are eagerly desirous of attaining those regions.

sa_verse: निन्दाम्यहं कर्म कृतं पितुस्तद् यस्त्वामगृह्णाद् विषमस्थबुद्धिम्। बुद्ध्यानयैवंविधया चरन्तं सुनास्तिकं धर्मपथादपेतम्॥
en: I blame this act of my father's, viz., that he took (for his (for his priest) one of perverse understanding, who range by help of such an intellect, who is frightfully atheistic, and who has swerved from the path of righteousness.

sa_verse: यथा हि चोरः स तथा हि बुद्धस्तथागतं नास्तिकमत्र विद्धि। तस्माद्धि यः शक्यतमः प्रजानां स नास्तिके नाभिमुखो बुधः स्यात्॥
en: As a thief is, so is a Buddha, and know that in this matter, an atheist is in a like predicament. Therefore, such an one, when capable of being punished like a thief for the good of the people, should be punished like a thief; and let no Brāhamaņa ever speak with an atheist.

sa_verse: धर्मे रताः सत्पुरुषैः समेतास्तेजस्विनो दानगुणप्रधानाः। अहिंसका वीतमलाश्च लोके भवन्ति पूज्या मुनयः प्रधानाः॥
en: Therefore those Brahmanas, a men, who have spared life and in other ways practised morality, and have also given away in charity, performed austerities and served others, (perform sacrifices in consonance with Vedas). And principal ascetics engaged in religion, surrounded by the good, possessed of energy, having charity for their foremost attribute, void of envy, and their hearts free from all stain, are honoured in this world.

sa_verse: इति ब्रुवन्तं वचनं सरोषं रामं महात्मानमदीनसत्त्वम्। उवाच पथ्यं पुनरास्तिकं च सत्यं वचः सानुनयं च विप्रः॥
en: When the magnanimous Rama of undiminished strength had wrathfully said this, that Vipra with supplications again spoke words fraught with morality and high spiritual truth.

sa_verse: स चापि कालोऽयमुपागतः शनैर्यथा मया नास्तिकवागुदीरिता। निवर्तनार्थं तव राम कारणात् प्रसादनार्थं च मयैतदीरितम्॥
en: O Rāma, the time has gradually drawn nigh when for the purpose of making you turn back, it is necessary that I should speak the language of atheism. But pacifying you, I have (again) spoken this (i.e. I am a believer).

sa_verse: निवर्तयितुकामस्त त्वामेतद् वाक्यमब्रवीत्। इमां लोकसमुत्पत्तिं लोकनाथ निबोध मे॥
en: Anxious of making the turn back, he had spoken thus. Do you now, O lord of the world, learn the genesis of creatures from me.

sa_verse: सर्वं सलिलमेवासीत् पृथिवी तत्र निर्मिता। ततः समभवद् ब्रह्मा स्वयंभूर्दैवतैः सह॥
en: Water was everywhere. The earth was constructed therein. Then sprang the self-create Brahmā along with all the celestials.

sa_verse: स वराहस्ततो भूत्वा प्रोज्जहार वसंधराम्। असृजच्च जगत् सर्वं सह पुत्रैः कृतात्मभिः॥
en: Having become a boar, he raised up the earth, and along with his sons of subdued souls created everything.

sa_verse: आकाशप्रभवो ब्रह्मा शाश्वतो नित्य अव्ययः। तस्मान्मरीचिः संजज्ञे मरीचेः कश्यपः सुतः॥
en: Brahma eternal, existing through all time and incapable of decay, sprang from the sky. From him Marici came into being, and Kaśyapa is Marici's son.

sa_verse: विवस्वान् कश्यपाज्जज्ञे मनुर्वैवस्वतः स्वयम्। स तु प्रजापतिः पूर्वमिक्ष्वाकुस्तु मनो सुतः॥ यस्येयं प्रथमं दत्ता समृद्धा मनुना मही। तमिक्ष्वाकुमयोध्यायां राजानं विद्धि पूर्वकम्॥
en: Vivasvat drew his birth from Kaśyapa, and Manu from Vivasvat. Manu was formerly known as Prajāpati. Ikṣvāku is son to Manu; and this world was first conferred upon Ikşvāku by Manu. Do you know Ikşvāku as the first kind in Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकोस्तु सुतः श्रीमान् कुक्षिरित्येव विश्रुतः। कुक्षेरथात्मजो वीर विकुक्षिरुदपद्यत ॥
en: The graceful Kukşi is known as the son of Iksvāku. And, O hero, Kuksi's son was Vikuksi.

sa_verse: विकुक्षेस्तु महातेजा बाणः पुत्रः प्रतापवान्। बाणस्य च महाबाहुरनरण्यो महातपाः॥
en: Vikukși had the powerful Bāņa possessed of exceeding energy for his son. Bāna had the mighty-armed Anaranya of high austerities for his son.

sa_verse: नानावृष्टिर्बभूवास्मिन् न दुर्भिक्षः सतां वरे। अनरण्ये महाराजे तस्करो वापि कश्चन॥
en: During the time of that foremost of righteous person, the monarch Anaranya, drought or famine did not occur (in Ayodhyā); nor were there any thieves at that time.

sa_verse: अनरण्यान्महाराज पृथू राजा बभूव ह। तस्मात् पृथोर्महातेजास्त्रिशङ्कुरुदपद्यत॥
en: O mighty king, from Anaranya sprang king Pșthu. From Pệthu sprang Trišanku of mighty energy.

sa_verse: स सत्यवचनाद् वीरः सशरीरो दिवं गतः। त्रिशङ्कोरभवत् सूनुर्धन्धुमारो महायशाः॥ धुन्धुमारान्महातेजा युवनाश्वो व्यजायत। युवनाश्वसुतः श्रीमान् मान्धाता समपद्यत॥ मान्धातुस्तु महान् नाः सुसंधिरुदपद्यत। सुसंधेरपि पुत्रौ द्वौ ध्रुवसंधिः प्रसेनजित्॥
en: This hero by virtue of his truth telling went to heaven in person. Trisanku had the famous Dhundhumāra for his son; and from Dhundhumāra Yuvanāśva born. Yuvanāśva's son was the handsome Māndhătā; was and from Māndhātā Susandhi came into being. Susandhi had two sons; Dhruvasandhi and Prasenajit.

sa_verse: यशस्वी ध्रुवसंधेस्तु भरतो रिपुसूदनः। भरतात् तु महाबाहोरसितो नाम जायत॥ यस्यैते प्रतिराजान उदपद्यन्त शत्रवः। हैहयास्तालजङ्घाश्च शूराश्च शशबिन्दवः॥
en: Dhruvasandhi had the illustrious Bharata, destroyer of foes (for his son) From Bharata sprang Asita, to whom sprang these hostile kings as foes, Haihayas, Tālajanghas and Sasabindus, heroes all.

sa_verse: तांस्तु सर्वान् प्रतिव्यूह्य युद्धे राजा प्रवासितः। स च शैलवरे रम्ये बभूवाभिरतो मुनिः॥
en: Having engaged with them in battle, the king was excited (by them). And on the romantic Himavat, he become engaged in asceticism.

sa_verse: द्वे चास्य भार्ये गर्भिण्यौ बभूवतुरिति श्रुतिः। तत्र चैका महाभागा भार्गवं देववर्चसम्॥ ववन्दे पद्मपत्राक्षी काङ्गिणी पुत्रमुत्तमम्। एका गर्भविनाशाय सपत्न्यै गरलं ददौ॥
en: It is said that at the time, his two wives were gone with child. There one of the exalted ladies having eyes resembling lotus petals saluted Bhrgu's son furnished with the splendour of a celestial, desirous of having an excellent son. Another administered poison to the other for destroying her foetus.

sa_verse: भार्गवश्च्यवनो नाम हिमवन्तमुपाश्रितः। तमृषि साभ्युपागम्य कालिन्दी त्वभ्यवादयत्॥
en: Bhrgu's son named Cyavana was at that time staying in Himavat. Appearing before the sage, Kalindi saluted him.

sa_verse: स तामभ्यवदत् प्रीतो वरेप्सुं पुत्रजन्मनि । पुत्रस्ते भविता देवि महात्मा लोकविश्रुतः॥ धार्मिकश्च सुभीमश्च वंशकर्तारिसूदनः।
en: Thereupon he returned the greeting that had been made by that lady anxious to secure a boon for the birth of a son. You, shall, O lady get a son of mighty soul, who shall be celebrated among men; and who shall be pious and powerful, the perpetuator of his race and destroyer of enemies

sa_verse: श्रुत्वा प्रदक्षिणं कृत्वा मुनिं तमनुमान्य च ॥ पद्मपत्रसमानाक्षं पद्मगर्भसमप्रभम्। ततः सा गृहमागम्य पत्नी पुत्रमजायत ॥
en: Hearing this, that noble lady having gone round him and paid in respect to the ascetic went to her home and gave birth to a son furnished with eyes resembling lotus petals, and of sheen like that of the inside of a lotus.

sa_verse: सपन्या तु गरस्तस्यै दत्तो गर्भजिघांसया। गरेण सह तेनैव तस्मात् स सगरोऽभवत्॥
en: And because her co-wife, had given her poison for destroying her foetus, and as in consequence of this, he had come in contact with poison, he came to be allied Sagara.

sa_verse: स राजा सगरो नाम यः समुद्रमखानयत्। इष्टा पर्वणि वेगेन त्रासयान इमाः प्रजाः॥
en: The name of that king is Sagara, who having been initiated in a sacrifice excavated the ocean, frightening the people with the vehemence of his operations.

sa_verse: असमञ्जस्तु पुत्रोऽभूत् सगरस्येति नः श्रुतम्। जीवनेव स पित्रा तु निरस्तः पापकर्मकृत्॥
en: Asamañja is known to have been the son of Sagara. This wicked man while yet in life, was banished by his father.

sa_verse: अंशुमानपि पुत्रोऽभूदसमञ्जस्य वीर्यवान्। दिलीपोंऽशुमतः पुत्रो दिलीपस्य भगीरथः॥
en: Asamanja's son was the puissant Ansumat. Dilipa is the son of Ansumat and Dilipa's son is Bhagiratha.

sa_verse: भगीरथात् ककुत्स्थश्च काकुत्स्था येन तु स्मृताः। ककुत्स्थस्य तु पुत्रोऽभूद् रघुर्येन तु राघवाः॥
en: From Bhagiratha sprang Kakutstha; from whom the Kākutsthas came to be well known. Kakutstha had Raghu for his son; after whom have been named the Rāghavas.

sa_verse: रघोस्तु पुत्रस्तेजस्वी प्रवृद्धः पुरुषादकः। कल्माषपादः सौदास इत्येवं प्रथितो भुवि॥
en: Raghu's son is the energetic Pravědha, who are human beings. He is known on earth under such names as Kalmāşapāda, Saudāsa.

sa_verse: कल्माषपादपुत्रोऽभूच्छङ्खणस्त्विति नः श्रुतम्। सहसैन्यो व्यनीनशत्॥
en: We have heard that Kalmāșapāda's son was Śarkhaņa, who having been endowed with prowess, found destruction along with his forces.

sa_verse: शङ्खणस्य तु पुत्रोऽभूच्छूरः श्रीमान् सुदर्शनः। सुदर्शनस्याग्निवर्ण अग्निवर्णस्य शीघ्रगः॥
en: Sarkhana's son was the beautiful and heroic Sudarśana. Sudarśana's son was Agnivarṇa and Agnivarna's, Sighraga.

sa_verse: शीघ्रगस्य मरुः पुत्रो मरोः पुत्रः प्रशुश्रुवः। प्रशुश्रुवस्य पुत्रोऽभूदम्बरीषो महामतिः॥
en: Śighraga's son was Maru, and Maru's Praśuśruva. Praśuśruva's son the magnanimous Ambarisa.

sa_verse: अम्बरीषस्य पुत्रोऽभूनहुषः सत्यविक्रमः। नहुषस्य च नाभागः पुत्रः परमधार्मिकः॥
en: Ambarīşa's son was Nahuşa having truth for his prowess. Nahușa's son was the exceedingly virtuous Nābhaga.

sa_verse: अजश्च सुव्रतश्चैव नाभागस्य सुताबुभौ। अजस्य चैव धर्मात्मा राजा दशरथः सुतः॥
en: Nābhāga had two sons, Aja and Suvrata. Aja's son was the virtuous king Dasaratha.

sa_verse: तस्य ज्येष्ठोऽसि दायादो राम इत्यभिविश्रुतः। तद् गृहाण स्वकं राज्यमवेक्षस्व जगन्नृप ॥
en: His eldest son are you celebrated under the name of Rāma. Do you receive your own kingdom and look after the world.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकूणां हि सर्वेषां राजा भवति पूर्वजः। पूर्वजे नावरः पुत्रो ज्येष्ठो राजाऽभिषिच्यते॥
en: Among the Ikșwākus, the first born becomes the sovereign. The first born existing, an inferior son cannot be installed in the kingdom.

sa_verse: स राघवाणां कुलधर्ममात्मनः सनातनं नाद्य विहन्तुमर्हसि। प्रभूतरत्नामनुशाधि मेदिनी प्रभूतराष्ट्रां पितृवन्महायशः॥
en: It therefore does not today behove you to depart from the ever existing morality of the descendants of Raghu. Do you rule this earth filled with gems, and furnished with high fame, do you like to a father govern her containing many kingdoms.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठः स तदा राममुक्त्वा राजपुरोहितः। अब्रवीद् धर्मसंयुक्तं पुनरेवापरं वचः॥
en: Having thus addressed Rāma, the royal priest Vasiștha again spoke to him words fraught with righteousness, saying.

sa_verse: पुरुषस्येह जातस्य भवन्ति गुरवः सदा। आचार्यश्चैव काकुत्स्थ पिता माता च राघव॥
en: To a man born there are three superiors, viz, the preceptor, O Kakutstha, and father and mother, O Raghava.

sa_verse: पिता ह्येनं जनयति पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ। प्रज्ञां तदाति चाचार्यस्तस्मात् स गुरुरुच्यते॥
en: Oforemost of men, the father begets a person, the preceptor imparts wisdom and therefore is he called a superior.

sa_verse: स तेऽहं पितुराचार्यस्तव चैव परंतप। मम त्वं वचनं कुर्वन् नातिवर्तेः सतां गतिम्॥
en: I, O subduer of foes, have been the preceptor of both yourself and your father. By doing what I say, you will not lose the state of the righteous.

sa_verse: इमा हि ते परिषदो ज्ञातयश्च नृपास्तथा। एषु तात चरन् धर्मं नातिवर्तेः सतां गतिम् ॥ वृद्धाया धर्मशीलाया मातुर्हिस्यवर्तितुम्। अस्या हि वचनं कुर्वन् नातिवर्तेः सतां गतिम्॥
en: These courtiers of yours, these relatives, and these kings;, by protecting them virtuously you will not lose the state of the righteous. It does not behove you to disregard the words of your aged and virtuous mother. By doing her bidding you will not lose the state of the righteous.

sa_verse: भरतस्य वचः कुर्वन् याचमानस्य राघव। आत्मानं नातिवर्तेस्त्वं सत्यधर्मपराक्रम॥
en: O Rāghava, by acting as Bharata, who is soliciting you, says, you having the morality of truth for your prowess, will not be frustrated of the state of the pious.

sa_verse: एवं मधुरमुक्तः स गुरुणा राघवः स्वयम्। प्रत्युवाच समासीनं वसिष्ठं पुरुषर्षभः॥
en: Thus sweetly addressed by his preceptor personally, that foremost of men, Rāghava, replied to Vasistha, seated with him, saying.

sa_verse: यन्मातापितरौ वृत्तं तनये कुरुतः सदा। न सुप्रतिकरं तत् तु मात्रा पित्रा च यत्कृतम्॥ यथाशक्तिप्रदानेन स्वापनोच्छादनेन च। नित्यं च प्रियवादेन तथा संवर्धनेन च॥
en: What his father and mother always do in behalf of their son, and what they effect by way of provision and sleep and clothing and constant sweet speech, and sport, is difficult of being repaid.

sa_verse: स हि राजा दशरथः पिता जनयिता मम। आज्ञापयन्मां यत् तस्य न तन्मिथ्या भविष्यति॥
en: Therefore, what my father, king Dasaratha has commanded me to do, must not be falsified.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण भरतः प्रत्यनन्तरम्। उवाच विपुलोरस्कः सूतं परमदुमनाः॥
en: Rāma having said this, the broad-breasted Bharata, extremely depressed spoke to the charioteer who was by, saying.

sa_verse: इह तु स्थण्डिले शीघ्रं कुशानास्तर सारथे। आर्य प्रत्युपवेक्ष्यामि यावन्मे सम्प्रसीदति॥ निराहारो निरालोको धनहीनो यथा द्विजः। शये पुरस्ताच्छालायां यावन्मां प्रतियास्यति ॥
en: O charioteer, do you speedily spread Kuša on the ground here. I will remain near the noble one until he be kindly disposed (towards me). Even as a twice born one deprived of his wealth Leith down (at the door of an unrighteous person), eschewing food and in darkness, will I lie down in front of this cottage, until he betakes himself back (to Ayodhyā)

sa_verse: स तु राममवेक्षन्तं सुमन्त्रं प्रेक्ष्य दुर्मनाः। कुशोत्तरमुपस्थाप्य भूमावेवास्थितः स्वयम्॥
en: Then with a dispirited heart finding Sumantra looking up to Rāma, Bharata himself spread Kuśa and lay down upon it.

sa_verse: तमुवाच महातेजा रामो राजर्षिसत्तमः। किं मां भरत कुर्वाणं तात प्रत्युपवेक्ष्यसे॥
en: To him spoke that foremost of Rājarşis, the highly energetic Rāma, O Bharata, my dear brother, what have I done that you have down by me?

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणो ह्येकपा
en: ā

sa_verse: न नरान् रोद्भुमिहार्हति। न तु मूर्धाभिषिक्तानां विधिः प्रत्युपवेशने॥
en: It is a Brāhmaṇa who alone can obstruct a person by lying down beside him; but there is no rule by which a member of the military class* can lie down (in this wise). *Murdhabhisikta, Murdha, the head; Abhisikta, sprinkled; kings being consecrated by having poured on their heads, while seated on a throne, prepared for the purpose, water from some sacred stream, mixed with honey, clarified butter, and spirituous liquor, as well as two sorts of grass and sprouts of corn; the term applies to the Kșetriya as identified with the king, the duties of royalty belonging specially to the military caste.

sa_verse: उत्तिष्ठ नरशार्दूल हित्वैतद् दारुणं व्रतम्। पुरवयमितः क्षिप्रमयोध्यां याहि राघव॥
en: O best of men, arise, renouncing this difficult vow; and, O descendant of Raghu, do you from hence take yourself to that best of cities, Ayodhyā,

sa_verse: आसीनस्त्वेव भरतः पौरजानपदं जनम्। उवाच सर्वतः प्रेक्ष्य किमार्य नानुशासथ॥
en: Being seated, Bharata, eyeing the citizens and the inhabitants of the provinces, remarked, What for do you not solicit the noble one?

sa_verse: ते तदोचुर्महात्मानं पौरजानपदा जनाः। काकुत्स्थमभिजानीमः सम्यग् वदति राघवः॥ एषोऽपि हि महाभागः पितुर्वचसि तिष्ठति। एत एव न शक्ताः स्म व्यावर्तयितुमञ्जसा ॥
en: Thereupon the citizens and the dwellers of the provinces answered that high-souled one, saying, We perceive that the descendant of Raghu says to Kākutstha what is fit; and this exceedingly virtuous one stay by the words of his sire. Therefore we are incapable of suddenly saying anything.

sa_verse: तेषामाज्ञाय वचनं रामो वचनमब्रवीत्। एवं निबोध वचनं सुहृदां धर्मचक्षुषाम्॥
en: Hearing their speech, Rāma said, “Listen to the words of these friends having morality for their vision."

sa_verse: एतच्चैवोभयं श्रुत्वा सम्यक् सम्पश्य राघव। उत्तिष्ठ त्वं महाबाहो मां च स्पृश तथोदकम्॥
en: And, O scion of the Raghu race, hearing their utterances as regards both yourself and me, do you decide. Arise, you mighty-armed one. Touch me and then water.

sa_verse: अथोत्थाय जलं स्पृष्ट्वा भरतो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। शृण्वन्तु मे परिषदो मन्त्रिणः शृणुयुस्तथा॥ न याचे पितरं राज्यं नानुशासामि मातरम्। एवं परमधर्मज्ञं नानुजानामि राघवम्॥
en: Thereat arising, Bharata, having touched water, said, You courtiers, and you ministers, listen to me! I did not wish for my paternal kingdom; nor did I sway my mother (towards obtaining the same); nor did I know that the exceedingly righteous Rāghava was to seek the woods.

sa_verse: यदि त्ववश्यं वस्तव्यं कर्तव्यं च पितुर्वचः। अहमेव निवत्स्यामि चतुर्दश वने समाः॥
en: But if he must dwell here, and thus act out the mandate of our father, I also will abide in the forest for fourteen years.

sa_verse: धर्मात्मा तस्य सत्येन भ्रातुर्वाक्येन विस्मितः। उवाच रामः सम्प्रेक्ष्य पौरजानपदं जनम्॥
en: Astonished at the determination of Bharata, the righteous Rāma eyeing the citizens and the inhabitants of the provinces, said.

sa_verse: विक्रीतमाहितं क्रीतं यत् पित्रा जीवता मम। न तल्लोपयितुं शक्यं मया वा भरतेन वा॥
en: Neither Bharata nor I can annual any sale or pledge or purchase effected by our father during his life.

sa_verse: उपाधिर्न मया कार्यो वनवासे जुगुप्सितः। युक्तमुक्तं च कैकेय्या पित्रा मे सुकृतं कृतम्॥
en: Therefore I ought not to accept any substitute for living in the woods, who can but contribute to my censure. Kaikeyi has spoken what is proper; and my father has acted well.

sa_verse: जानामि भरतं क्षान्तं गुरुसत्कारकारिणम्। सर्वमेवात्र कल्याणं सत्यसंधे महात्मनि॥
en: I know Bharata to be forgiving, and given to serving superiors. Everything in that high-souled one intent on truth appear beneficial.

sa_verse: अनेन धर्मशीलेन वनात् प्रत्यागतः पुनः भ्रात्रा सह भविष्यामि पृथिव्याः पतिरुत्तमः॥
en: Having returned from the forest, I will in company with this brother of virtuous character become the worthy master of this earth.

sa_verse: वृतो राजा हि कैकेय्या मया तद्वचनं कृतम्। अनृतान्मोचयानेन पितरं तं महीपतिम्॥
en: The king having been made by Kaikeyi to consent (to her proposal, I have done as she told me. Do you by acting in the way calculated to free that lord of the earth, our father (from the debt of promise), do so. company with this brother of virtuous character become the worthy master of this earth.

sa_verse: वृतो राजा हि कैकेय्या मया तद्वचनं कृतम्। अनृतान्मोचयानेन पितरं तं महीपतिम्॥
en: The king having been made by Kaikeyi to consent (to her proposal, I have done as she told me. Do you by acting in the way calculated to free that lord of the earth, our father (from the debt of promise), do so.

sa_verse: तमप्रतिमतेजोभ्यां भ्रातृभ्यां रोमहर्षणम्। विस्मिताः संगमं प्रेक्ष्य समुपेता महर्षयः॥
en: Observing the meeting of the two brothers of unparalleled energy, capable of making one's hair stand on end, the Maharsis struck with surprise came to the scene.

sa_verse: अन्तर्हिता मुनिगणाः स्थिताश्च परमर्षयः। तौ भ्रातरौ महाभागौ काकुत्स्थौ प्रशशंसिरे॥
en: And remaining invisible, the ascetics, Siddhas and prime saints admired those brothers, the virtuous Käkutsthas.

sa_verse: सदा
राजपुत्रौ द्वौ धर्मज्ञौ धर्मविक्रमौ। श्रुत्वा वयं हि सम्भाषामुभयोः स्पृहयामहे ॥
en: Blessed is he whose sons these, two cognizant of virtue and having truth for prowess are. Having heard their converse, we are delighted.

sa_verse: ततस्त्वृषिगणाः क्षिप्रं दशग्रीववधैषिणः। भरतं राजशार्दूलमित्यूचुः संगता वचः॥
en: Then the saints, desirous of the destruction of the ten-headed one, having become unanimous, speedily addressed that foremost of monarchs, Bharata, saying.

sa_verse: कुले जात महाप्राज्ञः महावृत्त महायशः। ग्राह्यं रामस्य वाक्यं ते पितरं यद्यवेक्षसे॥
en: Born in an illustrious line, endued with high wisdom, of high character and high fame, you should accept Rāma's words, in you have a care for the happiness of your sire.

sa_verse: सदानृणमिमं रामं वयमिच्छामहे पितुः। अनृणत्वाच्च कैकेय्याः स्वर्गं दशरथो गतः॥
en: We wish that Rāma may always act truthfully in respect of his father. By virtue of this truth connected with Kaikeyi, Dasaratha has gone to heaven.

sa_verse: एतावदुक्त्वा वचनं गन्धर्वाः समहर्षयः। राजर्षयश्चैव तथा सर्वे स्वां स्वां गतिं गताः॥
en: Having said this, the Gandharvas, Maharsis, and Rājarșis each went to his proper quarter.

sa_verse: ह्लादितस्तेन वाक्येन शुशुभे शुभदर्शनः। रामः संहृष्टवदनस्तानृषीनभ्यपूजयत्॥
en: Having paid homage to those saints, Rāma possessed of a gracious presence, hearing their words, was filled with joy, and looked lovely with his delightful countenance.

sa_verse: त्रस्तगावस्तु भरतः स वाचा सज्जमानया। कृताञ्जलिरिदं वाक्यं राघवं पुनरब्रवीत्॥
en: But with his frame agitated (with emotion), Bharata with joined hands again addressed Rāghava in words faltering (with feeling).

sa_verse: राम धर्ममिमं प्रेक्ष्य कुलधर्मानुसंततम्। कर्तुमर्हसि काकुत्स्थ मम मातुश्च याचनाम्॥
en: O Rāma, taking into account the morality of this race, it behoves you to act in accordance with the solicitations of my mother (and yours).

sa_verse: रक्षितं सुमहद् राज्यमहमेकस्तु नोत्सहे। पौरजानपदांश्चापि रक्तान् रञ्जयितुं तदा॥
en: I do not venture to govern this vast kingdom alone, or please the citizens and the inhabitants of the provinces attached to you.

sa_verse: ज्ञातयश्चापि योधाश्च मित्राणि सुहृदश्च नः। त्वामेव हि प्रतीक्षन्ते पर्जन्यमिव कर्षकाः॥
en: And as husbandmen remain expecting rain, our kindred, and warriors and friends and adherents wait in expectation of you.

sa_verse: इदं राज्यं महाप्राज्ञ स्थापय प्रतिपद्य हि। शक्तिमान् स हि काकुत्स्थ लोकस्य परिपालने॥
en: O exceedingly wise one, accepting the kingdom, do you place it in the hands of some person. O Kākutstha, whomsoever you will chose for performing this office, will surely prove strong enough to govern the people.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वापतद् भ्रातुः पादयोर्भरतस्तदा। भृशं सम्प्रार्थयामास राघवेऽतिप्रियं वदन्॥
en: Having said this, Bharata fell at his brother's feet; and sweetly addressing him, O Rāghava, solicited him once again.

sa_verse: तमः भ्रातरं कृत्वा रामो वचनमब्रवीत्। श्यामं नलिनपत्राक्षं मत्तहंसस्वरः स्वयम्॥
en: Taking on his lap that sable-hued on furnished with eyes resembling lotus-leaves, Rāma with a voice like that of a mad swan, spoke to (Bharata).

sa_verse: आगता त्वामियं बुद्धिः स्वजा वैनयिकी च या। भृशमुत्सहसे तात रक्षितं पृथिवीमपि॥
en: You have at length attained the mental attitude that is natural and that also comes of the training one receives at the hands of his preceptor. And brother you venture greatly to protect the earth.

sa_verse: आमात्यैश्च सुहृद्भिश्च बुद्धिमद्भिश्च मन्त्रिभिः। सर्वकार्याणि सम्मन्त्र्य महान्त्यपि हि कारय ॥
en: Taking counsel with courtiers, adherents and intelligent ministers, do you achieve grand tasks.

sa_verse: लक्ष्मीश्चन्द्रादपेयाद् वा हिमवान् वा हिमं त्यजेत्। अतीयात् सागरो वेलां न प्रतिज्ञामहं पितुः॥
en: Even if beauty forsake the moon, or Himavat his snow, or the ocean overleaps its shores, I shall not forsake the promise of my sire.

sa_verse: कामाद् वा तात लोभाद् वा मात्रा तुभ्यमिदं कृतम्। न तन्मनसि कर्तव्यं वर्तितव्यं च मातृवत्॥
en: My brother, you ought not to cherish in your mind what your mother incited by covetousness or affection for you has done; and you should act by her as one should by one's mother.

sa_verse: एवं ब्रुवाणं भरतः कौसल्यासुतमब्रवीत्। तेजसाऽऽदित्यसंकाशं प्रतिपच्चन्द्रदर्शनम्॥
en: When Rāma resembling the sun in energy and like the moon of Pratipat in appearance, had said this, Bharata said to the son of Kausalya.

sa_verse: अधिरोहार्यपादाभ्यां पादुके हेमभूषिते। एते हि सर्वलोकस्य योगक्षेमं विधास्यतः॥
en: O noble one, do you take off your feet your sandals adorned with gold. These shall protect what the people have got and secure to you what they lack.

sa_verse: सोऽधिरुह्य नरव्याघ्रः पादुके व्यवमुच्य च। प्रायच्छत् सुमहातेजा भरताय महात्मने॥
en: Thereupon taking off his sandals, that highly energetic one gave them to the magnanimous Bharata.

sa_verse: स पादुके सम्प्रणम्य रामं वचनमब्रवीत्। चतुर्दश हि वर्षाणि जटाचीरधरो ह्यहम्॥ फलमूलाशनो वीर भवेयं रघुनन्दन। तवागमनमाकाङ्क्षन् वसन् वै नगराद् बहिः॥ तव पादुकयोय॑स्य राज्यतन्त्रं परंतप।
en: Bharata, bowing down to the sandals, said, O hero, for, four and ten years shall I, wearing matted locks and bark, and subsisting upon fruits and roots, O scion of the Raghu race, expecting your arrival, remain outside the city, having, O subduer of foes, made over the task of government to your sandals.

sa_verse: चतुर्दशे हि सम्पूर्णे वर्षेऽहनि रघूत्तम॥ न द्रक्ष्यामि यदि त्वां तु प्रवेक्ष्यामि हुताशनम्।
en: O foremost of Raghus, if after the completion of the fourteen year, I do not see you, I shall enter into fire.

sa_verse: तथेति च प्रतिज्ञाय तं परिष्वज्य सादरम्॥ शत्रुघ्नं च परिष्वज्य वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्। मातरं रक्ष कैकेयीं मा रोषं कुरु तां प्रति ॥ मया च सीतया चैव शप्तोऽसि रघुनन्दन। इत्युक्त्वाश्रुपरीताक्षो भ्रातरं विससर्ज ह॥
en: Promising, “So be it,” and affectionately embracing Bharata, and also embracing Śatrughna, Rāma said, Do you protect your mother Kaikeyi. Do not be angry with her. Both Jānaki and I conjure you to do this, O descendant of Raghu. Having said this, he forsook his brother, with tears in his eyes.

sa_verse: स पादुके ते भरतः स्वलंकृते महोज्ज्वले सम्परिगृह्य धर्मवित्। प्रदक्षिणं चैव चकार राघवं चकार चैवोत्तमनागमूर्धनि॥
en: Then accepting those burnished and garnished sandals, Bharata versed in morality circumabulated Rāghava, and placed the sandals on the head of an excellent elephant.

sa_verse: अथानुपूर्व्या प्रतिपूज्य तं जनं गुरुंश्च मन्त्रीन् प्रकृतीस्तथानुजौ। । व्यसर्जयद् राघववंशवर्धनः स्थितः स्वधर्मे हिमवानिवाचलः॥
en: Then having one by one honoured that assembly, and his preceptor and the counsellors, and the subjects and his younger brothers, perpetuator of the Raghu race, steady in his own duty like. Himavat itself, took leave of them.

sa_verse: तं मातरो बाष्पगृहीतकण्ठ्यो दुःखेन नामन्त्रयितुं हि शेकुः। स चैव मातृरभिवाद्य सर्वा रुदन् कुटी स्वां प्रविवेश रामः॥
en: His mothers with their throats invaded by the vapour of grief could not from distress of heart say ought to him. And Rāma also having paid homage to all of them, weeping entered his own cottage.

sa_verse: ततः शिरसि कृत्वा तु पादुके भरतस्तदा। आरुरोह रथं हृष्टः शत्रुघ्नसहितस्तदा॥
en: Then taking the pair of sandals on his head, Bharata in company with Śatrughna well pleased, ascended the car.

sa_verse: वसिष्ठो वामदेवश्च जाबालिश्च दृढव्रतः। अग्रतः प्रययुः सर्वे मन्त्रिणो मन्त्रपूजिताः॥
en: And Vasiştha, and Vāmadeva and Jāvāli steady his vow as well as the minister, honoured on account of their counsels, went before.

sa_verse: मन्दाकिनी नदी रम्यां प्राङ्मुखास्ते ययुस्तदा। प्रदक्षिणं च कुर्वाणाश्चित्रकूटं महागिरिम्॥
en: And going round the charming Mandākinī, they went eastwards, circumambulating (at the same time) the mighty hill, Citrakuta.

sa_verse: पश्यन् धातुसहस्राणि रम्याणि विविधानि च। प्रययौ तस्य पाइँन ससैन्यो भरतस्तदा॥
en: And beholding various kinds of beautiful minerals by thousand. Bharata accompanied by his army went by the side (of the hill).

sa_verse: अदूराच्चित्रकूटस्य ददर्श भरतस्तदा। आश्रमं यत्र स मुनिर्भरद्वाजः कृतालयः॥
en: At a short distance from Citrakuța, Bharata beheld the spot where the ascetic Bharadvāja had built his habitation.

sa_verse: स तमाश्रममागम्य भरद्वाजस्य वीर्यवान्। अवतीर्य रथात् पादौ ववन्दे कुलनन्दनः॥
en: Approaching the asylum, the puissant Bharata descended from the car, and the son of Raghu then paid homage to the saint's feet.

sa_verse: ततो हृष्टो भरद्वाजो भरतं वाक्यमब्रवीत्। अपि कृत्यं कृतं तात रामेण च समागतम्॥
en: Thereat Bharadvāja well pleased said, Have you, my child, on meeting with Rama, done what it behoved you to do?

sa_verse: एवमुक्तः स तु ततो भरद्वाजेन धीमता। प्रत्युवाच भरद्वाजं भरतो धर्मवत्सलः॥
en: Thus addressed by the intelligent Bharadvāja, Bharata attached to righteousness answered the former, saying.

sa_verse: स याच्यमानो गुरुणा मया च दृढविक्रमः। राघवः परमप्रीतो वसिष्ठं वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Solicited by our preceptor as well as by myself, Rāghava of steady prowess highly pleased spoke to Vasiștha.

sa_verse: पितुः प्रतिज्ञां तामेव पालयिष्यामि तत्त्वतः। चतुर्दश हि वर्षाणि या प्रतिज्ञा पितुर्मम ॥
en: I would faithfully perform my father's promise for fourteen years; for even this is the promise of my father.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो महाप्राज्ञो वसिष्ठः प्रत्युवाच ह। वाक्यज्ञो वाक्यकुशलं राघवं वचनं महत्॥
en: Thus accosted, the eminently wise Vasiștha skilled in speech replied to Rāghava in these pregnant words.

sa_verse: एते प्रयच्छ संहृष्टः पादुके हेमभूषिते। अयोध्यायां महाप्राज्ञ योगक्षेमकरो भव॥
en: Do you well pleased confer (on Bharata) your sandals decked in gold. Thereby, exceedingly wise one, you will be able to protect what the people have and secure to them what they have not.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो वसिष्ठेन राघवः प्राङ्मुखः स्थितः। पादुके हेमविकृते मम राज्याय ते ददौ ॥
en: Thus addressed by Vasiştha, Rāghava facing the east, conferred on me his sandals decked in gold, in order that I mighty carry on the government of the kingdom.

sa_verse: निवृत्तोऽहमनुज्ञातो रामेण सुमहात्मना। अयोध्यामेव गच्छामि गृहीत्वा पादुके शुभे॥
en: Commanded by the magnanimous Rāma, I retrace my steps. I will go to Ayodhyā, taking the sandals along with me.

sa_verse: एतच्छ्रुत्वा शुभं वाक्यं भरतस्य महात्मनः। भरद्वाजः शुभतरं मुनिर्वाक्यमुदाहरत्॥
en: Hearing these auspicious words of the highsouled Bharata, the ascetic Bharadvāja said to Bharata.

sa_verse: नैतच्चित्रं नरव्याघ्र शीलवृत्तविदां वरे। यदार्यं त्वयि तिष्ठेत्तु निम्नोत्सृष्टमिवोदकम्॥
en: It is no wonder that Rāma has acted nobly by you, foremost among men and possessed of an (excellent) character and disposition, even as water poured out, flow downwards.

sa_verse: अनृणः स महाबाहुः पिता दशरथस्तव। यस्य त्वमीदृशः पुत्रो धर्मात्मा धर्मवत्सलः॥
en: Your father Dasaratha has become freed from his debts, since he has a son like to you, righteous and attached to virtue.

sa_verse: तमृषिं तु महाप्राज्ञमुक्तवाक्यं कृताञ्जलिः। आमन्त्रयितुमारेभे चरणावुपगृह्य च ॥
en: When the ascetic had said this, Bharata with joined palms paid his respects to that highly wise one, by taking hold of his feet.

sa_verse: ततः प्रदक्षिणं कृत्वा भरद्वाजं पुनः पुनः। भरतस्तु ययौ श्रीमानयोध्यां सह मन्त्रिभिः॥
en: And after having again and again gone round Bharadvāja, the graceful Bharata went to Ayodhyā accompanied by his counsellors.

sa_verse: यानैश्च शकटैश्चैव हयैर्नागैश्च सा चमूः। पुनर्निवृत्ता विस्तीर्णा भरतस्यानुयायिनी॥
en: And returning by means of cars and carts, horses and elephants, that army marching in the wake of Bharata, spread wide.

sa_verse: ततस्ते यमुनां दिव्यां नदीं तीोर्मिमालिनीम्। ददृशुस्तां पुनः सर्वे गङ्गां शिवजलां नदीम्॥
en: And after having crossed the beautiful river Yamunā flowing in waves, they again beheld the river Gangā of auspicious waters.

sa_verse: तां रम्यजलसम्पूर्णां संतीर्य सहबान्धवः। शृङ्गवेरपुरं रम्यं प्रविवेश ससैनिकः॥
en: And having along with his friend crossed over that river filled with beautiful waters, Bharata together with his army entered the charming city of Srigavera.

sa_verse: शृङ्गवेरपुराद् भूय अयोध्यां संददर्श ह। अयोध्यां तु तदा दृष्ट्वा पित्रा भ्रात्रा विवर्जिताम्॥ भरतो दुःखसंतप्तः सारथिं चेदमब्रवीत्।
en: And from the city of Śộngava he went to Ayodhyā and again beheld it. And seeing Ayodhyā bereft of his father and mother, Bharata burning in grief, said to the charioteer.

sa_verse: सारथे पश्य विध्वस्ता अयोध्या न प्रकाशते॥ निराकारा निरानन्दा दीना पतिहतस्वना ॥
en: O charioteer, behold that Ayodhyā, which shorn of its splendour and decorations, and plunged in grief, forlorn and silent, does not appear delightful.

sa_verse: स्निग्धगम्भीरघोषेण स्यन्दनेनोपयान् प्रभुः। अयोध्यां भरतः क्षिप्रं प्रविवेश महायशाः॥
en: Arriving by means of a car emitting a low and solemn sound, that lord, the highly famous Bharata speedily entered Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: विडालोलूकचरितामालीननरवारणाम्। तिमिराभ्याहतां कालीमप्रकाशां निशामिव ॥ राहुशत्रोः प्रियां पत्नीं श्रिया प्रज्वलितप्रभाम्। ग्रहेणाभ्युदितेनैकां रोहिणीमिव पीडिताम्॥
en: Ranged by cats and owls, with the doors of the people's dwellings remaining closed; like a night enveloped in darkness gloomy; and invisible; resembling the beloved wife of Rāhu's enemy, Rohini, brilliant with the lustre of the latter, when she is forlorn in consequence of her lover being afflicted by that planet.

sa_verse: अल्पोष्णक्षुब्धसलिलां धर्मतप्तविहंगमाम्। लीनमीनझषग्राहां कृशां गिरिनदीमिव॥
en: Like to a mountain stream shrunk up, having its waters turbid and slightly heated, and its fowls burning in the heat; and with its fishes, alligators and other aquatic animals rendered lean.

sa_verse: विध्वस्तकवचां रुग्णगजवाजिरथध्वजाम्। हतप्रवीरामापन्नां चमूमिव महाहवे॥
en: With armour scattered all round, and sick elephants and horses and cars and standards, and heroes lying dead, in distress.

sa_verse: सफेनां सस्वनां भूत्वा सागरस्य समुत्थिताम्। प्रशान्तमारुतोद्भूतां जलोमिमिव निःस्वनाम्।७।।
en: Like to an army in a mighty encounter; resembling the silent ripples of the ocean raised by the gentle breeze which ere now (in the shapes of surges) were heaving, brimming over with foam and sending forth roars.

sa_verse: त्यक्तां यज्ञायुधैः सर्वैरभिरूपैश्च याजकैः। सुत्याकाले सुनिर्वृत्ते वेदिं गतरवामिव॥
en: Like a silent dais after the sacrifice is over, without the sacrificial appurtenances, without worthy priests.

sa_verse: गोष्ठमध्ये स्थितामार्तामवरन्तीं नवं तृणम्। गोवृषेण परित्यक्तां गवां पत्नीमिवोत्सुकाम्॥
en: Like to the wife of a youthful ox, exercised with anxiety on having been forsaken by her favourite ox, staying in distress in a pen, abstaining from fresh grass.

sa_verse: प्रभाकराद्यैः सुस्निग्धैः प्रज्वलद्भिरिवोत्तमैः। वियुक्तां मणिभिर्जात्यैर्नवां मुक्ताबलीमिव॥
en: Like to a string of new pearls divorced from noble, mild-gleaming rubies and other excellent gems.

sa_verse: सहसाचरितां स्थानान्महीं पुण्यक्षयाद् गताम्। संहृतद्युतिविस्तारां तारामिव दिवश्च्युताम्॥
en: Like a star on the expiration of its virtue, moving from its place and dislodged from heaven, dropping to the earth with its brightness contracted.

sa_verse: पुष्पनद्धां वसन्तान्ते मत्तभ्रमरशालिनीम्। द्रुतदावाग्निविप्लुष्टां क्लान्तां वनलतामिव ॥
en: Like a blossoming creeper at the end of spring with maddened Bhramaras* suddenly rendered nerveless in consequence of being caught by a forest fire. *Black bees.

sa_verse: सम्मूढनिगमां सर्वां संक्षिप्तविपणापणाम्। प्रच्छन्नशशिनक्षत्रां द्यामिवाम्बुधरैर्युताम्॥
en: With her merchants plunged in grief, and her shops and stalls closed; like the welkin covered with clouds, and having its moon and stars hid.

sa_verse: क्षीणपानोत्तमैर्भग्नैः शरावैरभिसंवृताम्। हतशौण्डामिव ध्वस्तां पानभूमिमसंस्कृताम्॥
en: Like an unclean, uncovered spot used for drinking, with all the wine drained, and scattered with broken bowls, and with the tipplers lying dead.

sa_verse: वृक्णभूमितलां निम्नां वृकणपात्रैः समावृताम्। उपयुक्तोदकां भग्नां प्रपां निपतितामिव ॥ विपुलां विततां चैव युक्तपाशां तरस्विनाम्। भूमौ बाणैर्विनिष्कृत्तां पतितां ज्यामिवायुधात्॥
en: Like to a reservoir with its terrace riven, filled with broken pots, and lying with its pillars crushed; like a long strong bow-string fixed on a bow furnished with nooses, falling off the bow to the ground, being snapped in consequence of the pressure of the arrow.

sa_verse: सहसा युद्धशोण्डेन हयारोहेण वाहिताम्। निहतां प्रतिसैन्येन वडवामिव पातिताम्॥
en: Like a mare, suddenly urged on by a rider skilled in battle, lying (on the ground), having been slain by the hostile forces.

sa_verse: भरतस्तु रथस्थः सञ्श्रीमान् दशरथात्मजः। वाहयन्तं रथश्रेष्ठं सारथिं वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: That son of Dasaratha, the beauteous Bharata, while proceeding on his car, addressed the charioteer as he drove that best of cars, saying.

sa_verse: किं नु खल्वद्य गम्भीरो मूर्च्छितो न निशाम्यते। यथापुरमयोध्यायां गीतवादित्रनिःस्वनः॥
en: Why do I not hear as formerly the solemn sounds of song and instrumental music spreading on all sides in Ayodhyā?

sa_verse: वारुणीमदगन्धश्च माल्यगन्धश्च मूर्च्छितः। चन्दनागुरुगन्धश्च न प्रवाति समन्ततः॥
en: And there does not breathe around the aroma of liquor, or the perfume of garlands, or the incense of aguru and sandal.

sa_verse: यानप्रवरघोषश्च सुस्रिग्धहयनिःस्वनः। प्रमत्तगजनादश्च महांश्च रथनिःस्वनः॥
en: And on Rāma being banished, in this city is not heard the mild neighing of steeds, the rattle of vehicles, the roar of mad elephants, or the mighty clatter of arms.

sa_verse: नेदानीं श्रूयते पुर्यामस्यां रामे विवासिते। चन्दनागुरुगन्धांश्च महाश्चि वनस्रजः॥ गते रामे हि तरुणाः संतप्ता नोपभुञ्जते। बहिर्यात्रां न गच्छन्ति चित्रमाल्यधरा नराः॥
en: And on Rāma having repaired to the forest, youthful folk stricken with sorrow do not enjoy the incense of aguru and sandal, or costly fresh chaplets. And persons bearing variegated garlands do not walk abroad; and no festivities are celebrated in the city stricken with grief on account of Rāma.

sa_verse: नोत्सवाः सम्प्रवर्तन्ते रामशोकार्दिते पुरे। सा हि नूनं मम भ्रात्रा पुरस्यास्य द्युतिर्गता॥ नहि राजत्ययोध्येयं सासारेवार्जुनी क्षपा। कदा नु खलु मे भ्राता महोत्सव इवागतः॥ जनयिष्यत्ययोध्यायां हर्षं ग्रीष्म इवाम्बुदः।
en: Surely, the grace of the city has gone away with my brother. Surely, that Ayodhyā does not look beautiful like an autumnal night with showers pouring down. When shall my brother, coming hither like a carnival, gladden all hearts in Ayodhyā like rain in summer?

sa_verse: तरुणैश्चारुवेषैश्च नरैरुन्नतगामिभिः॥ सम्पतद्भिरयोध्यायां नाभिभान्ति महापथा।
en: And the highways shall be graced with young people elegantly attired and bearing themselves bravely in Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: इति ब्रुवन् सारथिना दुःखितो भरतस्तदा॥ अयोध्यां सम्प्रविश्यैव विवेश वसतिं पितुः। तेन हीनां नरेन्द्रेण सिंहहीनां गुहामिव ॥
en: Having said this in grief, Bharata in company with the charioteer entered Ayodhyā and immediately went to the residence of his father, bereft of that foremost of men, like a cavern bereft of its lion.

sa_verse: तदा तदन्तः पुरमुज्झितप्रभं सुरैरिवोत्कृष्टमभास्करं दिनम्। निरीक्ष्य सर्वत्र विभक्तमात्मवान् मुमोच बाष्पं भरतः सुदुःखितः॥
en: Seeing the inner apartment shorn of its splendour, like a day deprived of the sun, mouisted by the deities, and everywhere unclean, the self-possessed Bharata, moved by grief, began to drop tears. to his

sa_verse: ततो निक्षिप्य मातृस्ता अयोध्यायां दृढव्रतः। भरतः शोकसंतप्तो गुरूनिदमथाब्रवीत्॥
en: Having placed his mothers in Ayodhyā, Bharata steady in his vow, kindling in grief, said preceptors.

sa_verse: नन्दिग्रामं गमिष्यामि सर्वानामन्त्रयेऽत्र वः। तत्र दुःखमिदं सर्वं सहिष्ये राघवं विना॥
en: I shall go to Nandigrāma, and therefore greet you all. There I shall suffer all this grief on account of Rāma's absence.

sa_verse: गतश्चाहो दिवं राजा वनस्थः स गुरुर्मम। रामं प्रतीक्षे राज्याय स हि राजा महायशाः॥
en: The king has gone to heaven, and my superior is staying in the woods. I shall remain in expectation of Rāma, assuming the reins of government. Verily that illustrious one is the king.

sa_verse: एतच्छ्रुत्वा शुभं वाक्यं भरतस्य महात्मनः। अब्रुवन् मन्त्रिणः सर्वे वसिष्ठश्च पुरोहितः॥
en: Hearing this excellent speech of the highsouled Bharata the counsellors as well as the priest Vasiştha said.

sa_verse: सुभृशं श्लाघनीयं च यदुक्तं भरत त्वया। वचनं भ्रातृवात्सल्यादनुरूपं तवैव तत्॥
en: What, О Bharata, influenced by affection for your brother you say, is worthy of you and is highly commendable.

sa_verse: नित्यं ते बन्धुलुब्धस्य तिष्ठतो भ्रातृसौहृदे। मार्गमार्यं प्रपन्नस्य नानुमन्येत कः पुमान्॥
en: Who will not endorse what has fallen from you, ever engaged in serving your friends, established in fraternal love, and who have entered upon a noble course?

sa_verse: मन्त्रिणां वचनं श्रुत्वा यथाभिलषितं प्रियम्। अब्रवीत् सारथिं वाक्यं रथो मे युज्यतामिति॥
en: Having heard the words of the counsellors acceptable and welcome, Bharata said to the charioteer, Do you yoke my car.

sa_verse: प्रहृष्टवदनः सर्वा मातृः समभिभाष्य च। आरुरोह रथं श्रीमाशत्रुघ्नेन समन्तितः॥
en: Then with a cheerful countenance having spoken to his mothers, that graceful one ascended the car in company with Satrughna.

sa_verse: आरुह्य तु रथं क्षिप्रं शत्रुघ्नभरताबुभौ। ययतुः परमप्रीतौ वृतौ मन्त्रिपुरोहितैः॥
en: Having swiftly ascended the car, both Satrughna and Bharata, right glad at heart, set out surrounded by counsellors and priests.

sa_verse: अग्रतो गुरवः सर्वे वसिष्ठप्रमुखा द्विजाः। प्रययुः प्राङ्मुखाः सर्वे नन्दिग्रामो यतो भवेत्॥
en: And going ahead, all the preceptors headed by Vasiştha proceeded eastwards, in which direction lay Nandigrāma.

sa_verse: बलं च तदनाहूतं गजाश्वरथसंकुलम्। प्रययौ भरते याते सर्वे च पुरवासिनः॥
en: And the forces, filled with elephants and horses and cars, although not called, set out on Bharata proceeding, as well as all the citizens.

sa_verse: रथस्थः स तु धर्मात्मा भरतो भ्रातृवत्सलः। नन्दिग्रामं ययौ तूर्णं शिरस्यादाय पादुके॥
en: And ascending the car, the virtuous Bharata attached to his brother, speedily went to Nandigrāma, holding the sandals (of Rāma) on his head.

sa_verse: भरतस्तु ततः क्षिप्रं नन्दिग्रामं प्रविश्य सः। अवतीर्य रथात् तूर्णं गुरुनिदमभाषत ॥
en: Then entering Nandigrāma in a short time, Bharata swiftly alighted from the car and addressed his preceptors, saying.

sa_verse: एतद् राज्यं मम भ्रात्रा दत्तं संन्यासमुत्तमम्। योगक्षेमवहे चेमे पादुके हेमभूषिते॥
en: This monarchy has been consigned to me as a trust; and these gold-decked sandals shall carry on the work of government, protecting what the people have and securing to them what they have not.

sa_verse: भरतः शिरसा कृत्वा संन्यासं पादुके ततः। अब्रवीद् दुःखसंतप्तः सर्वं प्रकृतिमण्डलम्॥
en: Then bowing to the sandals which represented the trust. Bharata burning in grief addressed the entire body of the subjects, saying.

sa_verse: छत्रं धारयत क्षिप्रमार्यपादाविमौ मतौ। आभ्यां राज्ये स्थितो धर्मः पादुकाभ्यां गुरोर्मम॥
en: Do you speedily bold the umbrella (over these sandals). These represent the dear feet. of the exalted one. Through these sandals of my superior, will be established the regal morality (of the kingdom).

sa_verse: भ्रात्रा तु मयि संन्यासो निक्षिप्तः सौहृदादयम्। तमिमं पालयिष्यामि राघवागमनं प्रति॥
en: This kingdom from love has been conferred on me as a trust; therefore I shall govern it until Rāghava comes.

sa_verse: क्षिप्रं संयोजयित्वा तु राघवस्य पुनः स्वयम्। चरणौ तौ तु रामस्य द्रक्ष्यामि सहपादुकौ॥
en: Again speedily putting these sandals on Raghava's feet, I shall behold them with the sandals on.

sa_verse: ततो निक्षिप्तभारोऽहं राघवेण समागतः। निवेद्य गुरवे राज्यं भजिष्ये गुरुवर्तिताम्॥
en: On Rāghava coming (back), I shall, my burden being cast upon him, making over the kingdom to him, engage myself in serving my superior.

sa_verse: राघवाय च संन्यासं दत्त्वेमे वरपादुके। राज्यं चेदमयोध्यां च धूतपापो भवाम्यहम्॥
en: And having rendered to Rāghava the trust in the shape of these sandals, this kingdom, and Ayodhyā, I shall be washed form my sin.

sa_verse: स वल्कलजटाधारी मुनिवेषधरः प्रभुः। नन्दिग्रामेऽवसद्धीरः ससैन्यो भरतस्तदा॥ सवालव्यजनं छत्रं धारयामास स स्वयम्। भरतः शासनं सर्वं पादुकाभ्यां निवेदयन्॥ ततस्तु भरतः श्रीमानभिषिच्यार्यपादुके। तदधीनस्तदा राज्यं कारयामास सर्वदा॥
en: Henceforth wearing bark and matted locks, and in the guise of an ascetic, the heroic Bharata dwelt in Nandigrāma along with his forces. Having consigned all rule to the sandals, Bharata himself held the umbrella and the chowri furnished with hair..

sa_verse: दुपायनं चोपहृतं महार्हम्। स पादुकाभ्यां प्रथमं निवेद्य चकार पश्चाद्भरतो यथावत्॥
en: And the graceful Bharata, Having installed the sandals of the noble one, always carried on the government in subordinations to them. Having consigned all rule to the sandals, Bharata himself held the umbrella and the chowri furnished with hair..

sa_verse: दुपायनं चोपहृतं महार्हम्। स पादुकाभ्यां प्रथमं निवेद्य चकार पश्चाद्भरतो यथावत्॥
en: And the graceful Bharata, Having installed the sandals of the noble one, always carried on the government in subordinations to them.

sa_verse: प्रतियाते तु भरते वसन् रामस्तदा वने। लक्षयामास सोद्वेगमथौत्सुक्यं तपस्विनाम् ॥
en: On Bharata having returned, while Rāma was dwelling in the woods, he noticed affright among the ascetics, added to a desire to remove to another place.

sa_verse: ये तत्र चित्रकूटस्य पुरस्तात् तापसाश्रमे। राममाश्रित्य निरतास्तानलक्षयदुत्सुकान्॥
en: And those ascetic that were living happily in the asylum in Citrakuța, relying upon Rāma (for their protection), he found to be wrought up with anxiety.

sa_verse: नयनैर्धकुटीभिश्च रामं निर्दिश्य शङ्किताः। अन्योन्यमुपजल्पन्तः शनैश्चक्रुर्मिथः कथाः॥
en: And by means of signs by the eye and contractions of the brow, they, afflicted with fear, were, alluding to Răma, slowly speaking to each other.

sa_verse: तेषामौत्सुक्यमालक्ष्य रामस्त्वात्मनि शङ्कितः। कृताञ्जलिरुवाचेदमृषि कुलपतिं ततः॥
en: Perceiving their anxiety, Rāma apprehensive of himself, with joined hands addressed the patriarchal ascetic, saying.

sa_verse: न कश्चिद् भगवन् किंचित् पूर्ववृत्तमिदं मयि। दृश्यते विकृतं येन विक्रियन्ते तपस्विनः॥
en: O reverend one, do you find me deviating from the path of former sovereigns, or acting contrary to their practice, which has perplexed (the minds of) these ascetics?

sa_verse: प्रमादाच्चरितं किंचित् कच्चिन्नावरजस्य मे। लक्ष्मणस्यर्षिभिर्दष्टं नानुरूपं महात्मनः॥
en: Have the sages seen my younger (brother), the high-souled Lakṣmaṇa, do anything from inadvertence that in unworthy of him?

sa_verse: कच्चिच्छुश्रूषमाणा वः शुश्रूषणपरा मयि। प्रमदाभ्युचितां वृत्तिं सीता युक्तां न वर्तते।७।।
en: Does not Sītā engaged in serving you, having to serve me, now minister to you as a woman should?

sa_verse: अथर्षिर्जरया वृद्धस्तपसा च जरां गतः। वेपमान इवोवाच रामं भूतदयापरम्॥
en: Thereat a certain aged and decrepit ascetic, with his frame trembling said to Rāma ever kind towards creatures.

sa_verse: कुतः कल्याणसत्त्वायाः कल्याणाभिरते: सदा। चलनं तात वैदेह्यास्तपस्विषु विशेषतः॥
en: Where is the falling off of that one of excellent character, engaged in offices of beneficence,-more specially with reference to ascetics?

sa_verse: त्वनिमित्तमिदं तावत् तापसान् प्रति वर्तते। रक्षोभ्यस्तेन संविग्नाः कथयन्ति मिथः कथाः॥
en: This fear arising from Rākşasas afflicts the ascetics because of you; and they, extremely agitated, converse with each other (on the subject).

sa_verse: रावणावरजः कश्चित् खरो नामेह राक्षसः। उत्पाट्य तापसान् सर्वाञ्जनस्थाननिवासिनः॥ धृष्टश्च जितकाशी च नृशंसः पुरुषादकः। अवलिप्तश्च पापश्च त्वां च तात न मृष्यते ॥ त्वं यदाप्रभृति ह्यस्मिन्नाश्रमे तात वर्तसे। तदाप्रभृति रक्षांसि विप्रकुर्वन्ति तापसान्॥
en: A Rākşasa named Khara, a younger brother of Rāvana, has been harassing the ascetics dwelling in Janasthāna. And Dhrsta also a fearless and wicked Rākṣasa feeding on human beings, and the unrighteous Avalipta, cannot bear you, my child. As long, my child, as you have been residing in this asylum, the Rākşasas have been plaguing the ascetics.

sa_verse: दर्शयन्ति हि बीभत्सैः क्रूरैर्भीषणकैरपि। नानारूपैर्विरूपैश्च रूपैरसुखदर्शनैः॥ अप्रशस्तैरशुचिभिः : सम्प्रयुज्य च तापसान्। प्रतिघ्नन्त्यपरान् क्षिप्रमनार्याः पुरतः स्थितान्॥
en: And they show themselves sometimes in disgusting shapes, sometimes in horrible and dreadful ones, in various frightful forms capable of frightening folks. And they throw execrable and unclean things among the anchorets, and torment those that happen to come before them.

sa_verse: तेषु तेष्वाश्रमस्थानेष्वबुद्धमवलीय च। रमन्ते तापसांस्तत्र नाशयन्तोऽल्पचेतसः॥
en: And they take delight in approaching asylums unperceived, and slaughtering ascetics, after having bound them by means of their arms.

sa_verse: अवक्षिपन्ति स्रुग्भाण्डानग्नीन् सिञ्चन्ति वारिणा। कलशांश्च प्रमर्दन्ति हवने समुपस्थिते॥
en: And on the occasion of sacrifices, they throw away the vessels containing sacrificial articles, pour water on the sacrificial fire and break vessels containing water.

sa_verse: तैर्दुरात्मभिराविष्टानाश्रमान् प्रजिहासवः। गमनायान्यदेशस्य चोदयन्त्यूषयोऽद्य माम्॥
en: Eager to renounce this asylum infested by those wicked ones, and removing to another place, the sages today have been exhorting me (on the subject).

sa_verse: तत् पुरा राम शारीरीमुपहिंसां तपस्विषु। दर्शयन्ति हि दुष्टास्ते त्यक्ष्याम इममाश्रमम्॥
en: And, O Rama, ere this, those wicked ones have slain ascetics. Therefore would renounce this asylum.

sa_verse: बहुमूलफलं चित्रमविदूरादितो वनम्। अश्वस्याश्रममेवाहं श्रयिष्ये सगणः पुनः॥
en: Not far from here is a picturesque wood abounding in fruits and roots. Thither shall I betake myself together with my own folks.

sa_verse: खरस्त्वय्यपि चायुक्तं पुरा राम प्रवर्तते। सहास्माभिरितो गच्छ यदि बुद्धिः प्रवर्तते॥
en: Khara shall also annoy you. If you think it proper, go hence in company with us. we

sa_verse: सकलत्रस्य संदेहो नित्यं युक्तस्य राघव। समर्थस्यापि सहितो वासो दुःखमिहाद्य ते॥
en: O Rāghava, although you are vigilant, able, and in all ways capable, it would be unpleasant for you to dwell here now in doubt.

sa_verse: इत्युक्तवन्तं रामस्तं राजपुत्रस्तपस्विनम्। न शशाकोत्तरैर्वाक्यैरवबद्धं समुत्सुकम्॥
en: When the anxious ascetic had said this, Rāma could not console him by his words.

sa_verse: अभिनन्द्य समापृच्छय समाधाय च राघवम्। स जगामाश्रमं त्यक्त्वा कुलैः कुलपतिः सह ॥
en: Then having paid homage to Rāma, and spoken to him and encouraged Rāghava, the patriach ascetic renouncing the asylum, went away along with his own people.

sa_verse: रामः संसाध्य ऋषिगणमनुगमनाद् देशात् तस्मात् कुलपतिमभिवाद्य ऋषिम्। सम्यक्प्रीतैस्तैरनुमत उपदिष्टार्थः पुण्यं वासाय स्वनिलयमुपसम्पेदे॥
en: And Rāma having (for a time) followed the sage who was departing from that place, and having paid him respects, being permitted by them, well-pleased, and advised (as to what it behoved him to do), came to his quarters reposing there.

sa_verse: आश्रममृषिविरहितं प्रभुः क्षणमपि न जहौ स राघवः। स्तापसाश्चार्षचरिते धृतगुणाः॥
en: Thenceforth) Rāghava never for a moment left that asylum forsaken by the sages., Rāghava having such qualities in his character as were likely to stand the sages in stead, the ascetics (that remained behind) ever followed Rāghava.

sa_verse: राघवस्त्वपयातेषु सर्वेष्वनुविचिन्तयन्। न तत्रारोचयद् वासं कारणैर्बहुभिस्तदा ॥
en: When the ascetics had gone away, Rāghava reflected (as to his stay at that place); and from diverse reasons, he did not relish remaining there any further.

sa_verse: इह मे भरतो दृष्टो मातरश्च सनागराः। सा च मे स्मृतिरन्वेति तान् नित्यमनुशोचतः॥
en: Here have I seen Bharata and my mother in company with the citizens. Overwhelmed with grief on my account, they ever recur to my memory.

sa_verse: स्कन्धावारनिवेशेन तेन तस्य महात्मनः। हयहस्तिकरीषैश्च उपमर्दः कृतो भृशम्॥
en: And in consequence of the troops of that highsouled one having quartered here as well as the dung discharged by his elephants and horses, this place has got exceedingly dirty.

sa_verse: तस्मादन्यत्र गच्छाम इति संचिन्त्य राघवः। प्रातिष्ठत स वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणेन च संगतः॥
en: Therefore will I go to another quarter. Having settled this in his mind, Rāghava departed along with Videha's daughter and Laksmana.

sa_verse: सोऽत्रेराश्रममासाद्य तं ववन्दे महायशाः। तं चापि भगवानत्रिः पुत्रवत् प्रत्यपद्यत॥
en: And having arrived at Atri's asylum, that renowned one paid obeisance (to the sage); and the reverend Atri also on his pare received Rāma asason.

sa_verse: स्वयमातिथ्यमादिश्य सर्वमस्य सुसत्कृतम्। सौमित्रिं च महाभागं सीतां च समसान्त्वयत्॥
en: And having personally given Rāma respectful reception and entertainment, the ascetic regarded the exalted Lakşmaņa and Sītā with a gracious eye.

sa_verse: पत्नीं च तमनुप्राप्तां वृद्धामामन्त्र्य सत्कृताम्। सान्त्वयामास धर्मज्ञः सर्वभूतहिते रतः।७।। अनसूयां महाभागां तापसी धर्मचारिणीम्। प्रतिगृह्णीष्व वैदेहीमब्रवीदृषिसत्तमः॥
en: And his aged wife having come there, Atri greeted her; and after she had been paid homage, the sage cognizant of virtue ever engaged in the good of all creatures, gladdened her (by presenting Sītā to her.) And that best of sages said to the pious female ascetic, Anasüyā, engaged in offices of virtue, “Do you accept Videha's daughter."

sa_verse: तामिमां सर्वभूतानां नमस्कार्यां तपस्विनीम्। अभिगच्छतु वैदेही वृद्धामक्रोधनां सदा॥
en: Let Vaidehī always resort to this aged ascetic devoid of anger, who is worthy of being bowed down to by all creatures.

sa_verse: एवं ब्रुवाणं तमृषि तथेत्युक्त्वा स राघवः। सीतामालोक्य धर्मज्ञामिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: When the saint had spoken thus, Rāghava saying, “So be it," turned his eyes to Sītā, and thus spoke to that one knowing morality.

sa_verse: राजपुत्रि श्रुतं त्वेतन्मुनेरस्य समीरितम्। श्रेयोऽर्थमात्मनः शीघ्रमभिगच्छ तपस्विनीम्॥ अनसूयेति या लोके कर्मभिः ख्यातिमागता। तां शीघ्रमभिगच्छ त्वमभिगम्यां तपस्विनीम्॥
en: O princess, you have heard what has been uttered by the ascetic. For the sake of your own welfare, do you speedily resort to the female anchoret. Do you without delay resort to the ascetic Anasuya, who is worthy of being associated with, and who has by virtue of her acts attained renown among men.f

sa_verse: सीता त्वेतद् वचः श्रुत्वा राघवस्य यशस्विनी। तामत्रिपत्नी धर्मज्ञामभिचक्राम मैथिली।॥
en: Hearing these words of Rāghava Mithila's daughter, the illustrious Sītā, drew near the righteous wife of Atri.

sa_verse: शिथिलां वलितां वृद्धां जरापाण्डुरमूर्धजाम्। सततं वेपमानाङ्गी प्रवाते कदलीमिव ॥ तां तु सीता महाभागामनसूयां पतिव्रताम्। अभ्यवादयदव्यग्रा स्वं नाम समुदाहरत्॥
en: Thereupon Sītā, announcing her name, saluted the pious and chaste Anasuyā old with slackened joints, with folds all over her skin, and her hair hoary from age, whose frame shook perpetually, like a plantain-leaf in the wind.

sa_verse: अभिवाद्य च वैदेही तापसी तां दमान्विताम्। बद्धाञ्जलिपुटा हृष्टा पर्यपृच्छदनामयम्॥
en: Having paid homage to that ascetic with her senses subdued, Vaidehi glad at heart, with joined hands, enquired after her welfare.

sa_verse: ततः सीतां महाभागां दृष्ट्वा तां धर्मचारिणीम्। सान्त्वयन्त्यब्रवीद् वृद्धा दिष्ट्या धर्ममवेक्षसे॥
en: Seeing the virtuous Sītā engaged in righteous acts, the old lady consoling her, said "By luck it is that you regard righteousness."

sa_verse: त्यक्त्वा ज्ञातिजनं सीते मानवृद्धिं च मानिनि। अवरुद्धं वने रामं दिष्ट्या त्वमनुगच्छसि॥
en: O Sita, having left your kindred, and banishing pride, you have o honoured lady, followed Rāma staying in the woods.

sa_verse: नगरस्थो वसन्थो वा शुभो वा यदि वाशुभः। यासां स्त्रीणां प्रियो भर्ता तासां लोका महोदयाः।।२३।
en: They that love their husbands, whether living in the city or the forest, whether well or ill disposed towards them, attain great state.

sa_verse: दुःशीलः कामवृत्तो वा धनैर्वा परिवर्जितः। स्त्रीणामार्यस्वभावानां परमं दैवतं पतिः॥
en: Wicked, ur libidinous, or indigent, a husband is a supreme deity to a wife of noble character.

sa_verse: न त्वेवमनुगच्छन्ति गुणदोषमसत्स्त्रियः। कामवक्तव्यहृदया भर्तृनाथाश्चरन्ति याः॥
en: But bad women whose hearts hunger after carnality, and who lord over their husbands, do not get acquainted with the virtues and demerits (of their husbands); and range at their will.

sa_verse: प्राप्नुवन्त्ययशश्चैव धर्मभ्रंशं च मैथिलि। अकार्यवशमापन्नाः स्त्रियो याः खलु तद्विधाः॥
en: O Mithilā's daughter, surely women of this sort who are given to doing evil acts, reap infamy and fall off from righteousness.

sa_verse: त्वद्विधास्तु गुणैर्युक्ता दृष्टलोकपरावराः। स्त्रियः स्वर्गे चरिष्यन्ति यथा पुण्यकृतस्तथा॥
en: But worthy women like you furnished with excellence, see a superior and better world, and range the celestial regions, like pious people.

sa_verse: तदेवमेतं त्वमनुव्रता सती पतिप्रधाना समयानुवर्तिनी। भव स्वभर्तुः सहधर्मचारिणी यशश्च धर्म च ततः समाप्स्यसि।२९।।
en: Therefore following this one, and adopting the course of chaste women, do you prove the associate in virtue of your husband, and then shall you attain both fame and religious merit.

sa_verse: सा त्वेवमुक्ता वेदेही त्वनसूयानसूयया। प्रतिपूज्य वचो मन्दं प्रवक्तुमुपचक्रमे॥
en: Thus addressed by Anasūyā, Vaidehī devoid of malice, honouring her words, began, “That you should instruct me is no wonder in you."

sa_verse: नैतदाश्चर्यमार्यायां यन्मां त्वमनुभाषसे। विदितं तु ममाप्येतद् यथा नार्याः पतिर्गुरुः॥ यद्यप्येष भवेद् भर्ता अनार्यो वृत्तिवर्जितः। अद्वैधमत्र वर्तव्यं तथाप्येष मया भवेत्॥
en: I know that a woman's spiritual guide is her husband. Even if a husband should be poor and of a disreputable character, he should be ungiudgingly obeyed by the like of me. one

sa_verse: किं पुनर्यो गुणश्लाघ्यः सानुक्रोशो जितेन्द्रियः। स्थिरानुरागो धर्मात्मा मातृवत्पितृवत्प्रियः॥
en: And to be said of one that is crowned with qualities, kind, self-controlled, of steady affection, righteous-souled, and who is dear as a father or a mother?

sa_verse: यां वृत्तिं वर्तते रामः कौसल्यायां महाबलः। तामेव नृपनारीणामन्यासामपि वर्तते॥
en: The exceedingly strong Rāma bear himself towards the other wives of the monarch as he does towards Kausalyā herself.

sa_verse: सकृद् दृष्टास्वपि स्त्रीषु नृपेण नृपवत्सलः। मातृवद् वर्तते वीरो मानमुत्सृज्य धर्मवित्॥
en: Renouncing sense of self-importance that heroic conversant with righteousness, devoted to his king, regards as his mothers those on whom the monarch once cast his eyes.

sa_verse: आगच्छन्त्याश्च विजनं वनमेवं भयावहम्। समाहितं हि मे श्वश्वा हृदये यत् स्थिरं मम॥
en: What my mother-in-law instructed me at the time that I was leaving for the lonely and fearful forest, is constantly present in my mind.

sa_verse: पाणिप्रदानकाले च यत् पुरा त्वग्निसंनिधौ। अनुशिष्टं जनन्या मे वाक्यं तदपि मे धृतम्॥
en: And what also my mother taught me in presence of fire on the occasion of the bestowal of my hand, is also remembered by me.

sa_verse: न विस्मृतं तु मे सर्वं वाक्यैः स्वैर्धर्मचारिणि । पतिशुश्रूषणानार्यास्तपो नान्यद् विधीयते॥
en: And, O you engaged in acts of righteousness, I have not forgotten the words that my relatives said to me, viz, the asceticism of a woman is ministering to her husband. They did not teach any thing else.

sa_verse: सावित्री पतिशुश्रूषां कृत्वा स्वर्गे महीयते। तथावृत्तिश्च याता त्वं पतिशुश्रूषया दिवम्॥
en: Having served her lord. Sāvitri is highly honoured in heaven; and you also, following the same course, by virtue of having served your husband, has secured heaven.

sa_verse: वरिष्ठा सर्वनारीणामेषा च दिवि देवता। रोहिणी न विना चन्द्रं मुहूर्तमपि दृश्यते॥
en: And this foremost of females, this goddess of heaven, Rohiņi, is not seen for a moment without the moon.

sa_verse: एवंविधाश्च प्रवराः स्त्रियो भर्तृदृढव्रताः। देवलोके महीयन्ते पुण्येन स्वेन कर्मणा॥
en: And prime women of this sort, firm in their husbands, are highly respected in the celestial regions by virtue of their pious acts.

sa_verse: ततोऽनसूया संहृष्टा श्रुत्वोक्तं सीतया वचः। शिरसाऽघ्राय चोवाच मैथिली हर्षयन्त्युत॥
en: Hearing Sītā's words, Anasuyā, exceedingly delighted, smelling her head, spoke thus, pleasing Maithili.

sa_verse: नियमैर्विविधैराप्तं तपो हि महदस्ति मे। तत् संश्रित्य बलं सीते छन्दये त्वां शुचिव्रते॥
en: By observing restrictions, I have earned great asceticism. By resorting to that energy, I would, O Sīta, confer a boon upon you, O you of pure vows.

sa_verse: उपपन्नं च युक्तं च वचनं तव मैथिलि। प्रीता चास्म्युचितां सीते करवाणि प्रियं च किम्॥
en: O Maithili, your words are just and proper. I am well pleased (with you). Tell me, O Sītā, what good shall I do you?

sa_verse: तस्यास्तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा विस्मिता मन्दविस्मया। कृतमित्यब्रवीत् सीता तपोबलसमन्विताम्॥
en: Hearing her words, Sītā, surprised, said with a smile, to that lady equipped with ascetic strength, “All this has been done by you."

sa_verse: सा त्वेवमुक्ता धर्मज्ञा तया प्रीततराऽभवत्। सफलं च प्रहर्षं ते हन्त सीते करोम्यहम्॥
en: Thus accosted, that one cognizant of virtue, was still more pleased, and said, “I am exceedingly pleased (with your words). I will attain a desire of mine."

sa_verse: इदं दिव्यं वरं माल्यं वस्त्रमाभरणानि च। अङ्गरागं च वैदेहि महार्हमनुलेपनम् ॥ मया दत्तमिदं सीते तव गात्राणि शोभयेत्। अनुरूपमसंक्लिष्टं नित्यमेव भविष्यति॥
en: This noble and grand garland, this apparel, these ornaments, and this precious paste for adorning the person, presented by me, let these, O Sītā, grace your person. These, worthy of you will never be tarnished.

sa_verse: अङ्गरागेण दिव्येन लिप्ताङ्गी जनकात्मजे। शोभयिष्यसि भर्तारं यथा श्रीविष्णुमव्ययम्॥
en: O daughter of Janaka, daubing your person with this excellent paint, you will grace your husband even as Śrī does the undeteriorating Visnu.

sa_verse: सा वस्त्रमङ्गरागं च भूषणानि स्रजस्तथा। मैथिली प्रतिजग्राह प्रीतिदानमनुत्तमम्॥ प्रतिगृह्य च तत् सीता प्रीतिदानं यशस्विनी। श्लिष्टाञ्जलिपुटा धीरा समुपास्त तपोधनाम्॥
en: Thereupon Mithila's daughter accepted those things presented to her out of love, viz., the apparel, the paint, the ornaments and the garland. Having accepted those accepted those presents conferred on her from love, that illustrious one, Sītā, quietly sat beside the female ascetic with joined hands.

sa_verse: तथा सीतामुपासीनामनसूया दृढव्रता। वचनं प्रष्टुमारेभे कथां काञ्चिदनुप्रियाम्॥
en: Then as Sītā was sitting, Anasūyā firm in vow asked her concerning a thing that was near her heart, saying.

sa_verse: स्वयंवरे किल प्राप्ता त्वमनेन यशस्विना। राघवेणेति मे सीते कथा श्रुतिमुपागता॥
en: I have heard, O Sītā, that you were won by the renowned Rāghava on the occasion of a selfchoice.

sa_verse: एवमुक्ता तु सा सीता तापसी धर्मचारिणीम्। श्रूयतामिति चोक्त्वा वै कथयामास तां कथाम्॥
en: Thus address, Sītā, saying to the ascetic engaged in pious acts, "Listen!" began to tell the story.

sa_verse: मिथिलाधिपतिर्वीरो जनको नाम धर्मवित्। क्षत्रकर्मण्यभिरतो न्यायतः शास्ति मेदिनीम्॥
en: Mithila's lord, the heroic Janaka, justly rule the earth, engaged in observing the duties of Ks atriyas.

sa_verse: तस्य लाङ्गलहस्तस्य कृषतः क्षेत्रमण्डलम्। अहं किलोत्थिता भित्त्वा जगतीं नृपतेः सुता॥
en: As he was ploughing a plain intended for a sacrifice, I rose from under the earth; and (in this sense) I am the daughter of that king.
