sa_verse: स मां दृष्ट्वा नरपतिर्मुष्टिविक्षेपतत्परः। पांसुगुण्ठितसर्वाङ्गी विस्मितो जनकोऽभवत्॥
en: Tending me, with my body covered with dust, Janaka, engaged in throwing handfuls of dust (to level hollow spots), was struck with amazement.

sa_verse: अनपत्येन च स्नेहादङ्कमारोप्य च स्वयम्। ममेयं तनयेत्युक्त्वा स्नेहो मयि निपातितः॥
en: Being childless, he took me on his lap from affection, and saying, 'This is my daughter', conceived affection for me.

sa_verse: अन्तरिक्षे च वागुक्ता प्रतिमामानुषि किल। एवमेतन्नरपते धर्मेण तनया तव॥
en: Then there were uttered words in the welkin, resembling those of a human being,! 'O king, in all righteousness, this is your daughter'.

sa_verse: ततः प्रहृष्टो धर्मात्मा पिता मे मिथिलाधिपः। अवाप्तो विपुलामृद्धिं मामवाप्य नराधिपः॥
en: Then well-pleased, my righteous father, the king, lord of Mithilā, receiving me, attained mighty good fortune.

sa_verse: दत्ता चास्मीष्टवद्देव्यै ज्येष्ठायै पुण्यकर्मणे। तया सम्भाविता चास्मि स्निग्धया मातृसौहृदात्॥
en: Consigned to the pious eldest noble one (queen), desirous of having offspring, I was brought up by that mild lady, with the tenderness of a mother.

sa_verse: पतिसंयोगसुलभं वयो दृष्ट्वा तु मे पिता। चिन्तामभ्यगमद् दीनो वित्तनाशादिवाधनः॥
en: In time) seeing me fit for the company of a husband, my father in distress was plunged in thought, like a poor man that has lost his wealth.

sa_verse: सदृशाच्चापकृष्टाच्च लोके कन्यापिता जनात्। प्रधर्षणमवाप्नोति शक्रेणापि समो भुवि॥
en: Even if the father of a girl be like to ſakra himself on earth he reaps odium at the hands of his equals and inferiors.

sa_verse: तां धर्षणामदूरस्थां संदृश्यात्मनि पार्थिवः। चिन्तार्णवगतः पारं नाससादाप्लवो यथा॥
en: Perceiving this obloquy at a short distance, the king was plunged in a sea of anxiety, but could not cross it, like one that has no raft.

sa_verse: अयोनिजां हि मां ज्ञात्वा नाध्यगच्छत् स चिन्तयन्। सदृशं चाभिरूपं च महीपालः पति मम ॥
en: Knowing me as unborn from any female vessel, the lord of earth reflecting upon it, could not come upon a suitable and fit husband for me.

sa_verse: तस्य बुद्धिरियं जाता चिन्तयानस्य संततम्। स्वयंवरं तनूजायाः करिष्यामीति धर्मतः॥
en: Then as he reflected, this thought occurred to him 'I will righteously celebrate the self-choice of my daughters'.

sa_verse: महायज्ञे तदा तस्य वरुणेन महात्मना। दत्तं धनुर्वरं प्रीत्या तूणी चाक्षय्यसायकौ॥
en: In the great sacrifice of Daksa, the highsouled Varuna had well-pleased conferred on Devarata an excellent bow with inexhaustible arrows and a couple of quivers.

sa_verse: असंचाल्यं मनुष्यैश्च यत्नेनापि च गौरवात्। तन्न शक्ता नमयितं स्वप्नेष्वपि नराधिपाः॥
en: Incapable of being moved on account of its weight, the kings could not even dream of bending the bow.

sa_verse: तद्धनुः प्राप्य मे पित्रा व्याहृतं सत्यवादिना। समवाये नरेन्द्राणां पूर्वमामन्त्र्य पार्थिवान्॥
en: Having obtained the bow, my truthful father said, 'No fear!' inviting at the same time the kings to an assembly of sovereigns.

sa_verse: इदं च धनुरुद्यम्य सज्यं यः कुरुते नरः। तस्य मे दुहिता भार्या भविष्यति न संशयः।॥
en: He that, raising the bow, shall string it, shall doubtless receive my daughter for his wife.

sa_verse: तच्च दृष्ट्वा धनुःश्रेष्ठं गौरवाद् गिरिसंनिभम्। अभिवाद्य नृपा जग्मुरशक्तास्तस्य तोलने॥
en: Seeing that best of bows in weight resembling a hill, the kings saluting it, go away, unable to move it.

sa_verse: सुदीर्घस्य तु कालस्य राधवोऽयं महाद्युतिः। विश्वामित्रेण सहितो यज्ञं द्रष्टुं समागतः॥ लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा रामः सत्यपराक्रमः। विश्वामित्रस्तु धर्मात्मा मम पित्रा सुपूजितः॥
en: It came to pass that after a long time, this highly effulgent Rāghava came to witness the sacrifice in company with Viśvāmitra. And Rāma having truth for his prowess, and the righteous Viśvāmitra were respectfully received by my sire.

sa_verse: प्रोवाच पितरं तत्र राघवौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। सुतौ दशरथस्येमौ धनुर्दर्शनकाक्षिणौ॥
en: Then Viśvāmitra said to my father, 'The descendants of Raghu, sons of Daśaratha, Rāma and Lakşmaņa are anxious to behold the bow.'

sa_verse: इत्युक्तस्तेन विप्रेण तद् धनुः समुपानयत्। तद् धनुर्दर्शयामास राजपुत्राय दैविकम्॥
en: Thus accosted by the Vipra, my father brought the bow, and showed that celestial bow to the princes.

sa_verse: निमेषान्तरमात्रेण तदानम्य महाबलः। ज्यां समारोप्य झटिति पूरयामास वीर्यवान्॥
en: Thereupon, in the twinkling of an eye, bending it, the powerful (Raghava) stringed the bow and drew it.

sa_verse: तेनापूरयता वेगान्मध्ये भग्न द्विधा धनुः। तस्य शब्दोऽभवद् भीमः पतितस्याशनेर्यथा॥
en: And as he drew it vehemently, it broke in the middle in twain. And the sound of it was dreadful like to the bursting of a thunder-bolt.

sa_verse: ततोऽहं तत्र रामाय पित्रा सत्याभिसंधिना। उद्यता दातुमुद्यम्य जलभाजनमुत्तमम्॥
en: Then raising an excellent vessel of water, my truthful father prepared to bestow me on Rāghava.

sa_verse: दीयमानां न तु तदा प्रतिजग्राह राघवः। अविज्ञाय पितुश्छन्दमयोध्याधिपतेः प्रभोः॥
en: And bestowed on Rāghava, I was accepted of him, after he had consulted the wish of his father, that lord, the master of Ayodhyā.

sa_verse: ततः श्वशुरमामन्त्र्य वृद्धं दशरथं नृपम्। मम पित्रा त्वहं दत्ता रामाय विदितात्मने॥
en: And inviting my father-in-law, the old king Dasaratha, my father bestowed me on Rāma having knowledge of self.

sa_verse: मम चैवानुजा साध्वी ऊर्मिला शुभदर्शना। भार्यार्थे लक्ष्मणस्यापि दत्ता पित्रा मम स्वयम्॥
en: And my father bestowed on Lakşmaņa for his wife, my younger sister, the beauteous and chaste Urmila.

sa_verse: एवं दत्तास्मि रामाय तथा तस्मिन् स्वयंवरे। अनुरक्तास्मि धर्मेण पतिं वीर्यवतां वरम्॥
en: Thus was I bestowed on Rāma on the occasion of that self-choice. And ever since I have been devoted to my husband, the foremost of those possessing prowess.

sa_verse: मम चैवानुजा साध्वी ऊर्मिला शुभदर्शना। भार्यार्थे लक्ष्मणस्यापि दत्ता पित्रा मम स्वयम्॥
en: And my father bestowed on Lakşmaņa for his wife, my younger sister, the beauteous and chaste Urmila.

sa_verse: एवं दत्तास्मि रामाय तथा तस्मिन् स्वयंवरे। अनुरक्तास्मि धर्मेण पतिं वीर्यवतां वरम्॥
en: Thus was I bestowed on Rāma on the occasion of that self-choice. And ever since I have been devoted to my husband, the foremost of those possessing prowess.

sa_verse: अनसूया तु धर्मज्ञा श्रुत्वा तां महती कथाम्। पर्यष्वजत् बाहुभ्यां शिरस्याघ्राय मैथिलीम्॥
en: Having heard this grand story, Anasāyā cognizant of righteousness, smelling the head of Mithilā's daughter, embraced Sītā with her arms.

sa_verse: व्यक्ताक्षरपदं चित्रं भाषितुं मधुरं त्वया। यथा स्वयंवरं वृत्तं तत् सर्वं च श्रुतं मया ॥
en: I have heard all that you, describing the selfchoice, have spoken sweetly and beautifully in articulated letters and feet.

sa_verse: रमेयं कथया ते तु दृढं मधुरभाषिणि । रविरस्तं गतः श्रीमानुपोह्य रजनी शुभाम्॥ दिवसं परिकीर्णानामाहारार्थं पतत्त्रिणाम्। संध्याकाले निलीनानां निद्रार्थं श्रूयते ध्वनिः॥
en: O sweet-speeched one, I have been delighted with your narration. But ushering in the auspicious night, the graceful Sun has set. And there are heard the notes of feathered ones, which, after having gone about in search of food, are resting now for the purpose of sleeping.

sa_verse: एते चाप्यभिषेकाः मुनयः कलशोद्यताः। सहिता उपवर्तन्ते सलिलाप्लुतवल्कलाः॥
en: And these ascetics having bathed, are wending their way in a body, carrying waterpitchers, their barks drenched with water.

sa_verse: अग्निहेत्रे च ऋषिणा हुते च विधिपूर्वकम्। कपोताङ्गारुणो धूमो दृश्यते पवनोद्धतः॥
en: The smoke arising from the fire-sacrifice duly performed by the ascetics, reddish like the hue of the pigeon's neck, is seen, wafted by the wind.

sa_verse: अल्पपर्णा हि तरवो घनीभूताः समन्ततः। विप्रकृष्टेन्द्रिये देशे न प्रकाशन्ति वै दिशः॥
en: And trees of spare foliage appear dense (on all sides); and distance is no longer perceptible.

sa_verse: रजनीचरसत्त्वानि प्रचरन्ति समन्ततः। तपोवनमृगा ह्येते वेदितीर्थेषु शेरते॥
en: And animals ranging in the night are going about all round; and those deer of the asylum are reposing on the daises.

sa_verse: सम्प्रवृत्ता निशा सीते नक्षत्रसमलंकृता। ज्योत्स्नाप्रावरणश्चन्द्रो दृश्यतेऽभ्युदितोऽम्बरे॥
en: O Sītā, the night crested by the stars has arrived; and, veiled in moonlight, appears the moon in the heavens.

sa_verse: गम्यतामनुजानामि रामस्यानुचरी भव। कथयन्त्या हि मधुरं त्वयाहमपि तोषिता ॥
en: Go you; I permit you. Do you seek the society of Rāma. I have been pleased with your sweet converse.

sa_verse: अलंकुरु च तावत् त्वं प्रत्यक्षं मम मैथिलि। प्रीतिं जनय मे वत्से दिव्यालंकारशोभिनी॥
en: O daughter of Mithilā, do you deck yourself before me. Do you thus please me, my child, you that look beautiful with excellent ornaments.

sa_verse: सा तदा समलंकृत्य सीता सुरसुतोपमा। प्रणम्य शिरसा पादौ रामं त्वभिमुखी ययौ॥
en: Thereat, having adorned herself, Sītā, resembling the daughter of a celestial, bowing down to the feet (of the female ascetic), directed her steps towards Rāma.

sa_verse: तथा तु भूषितां सीतां ददर्श वदतां वरः। राघवः प्रीतिदानेन तपस्विन्या जहर्ष च॥
en: And that best of speakers, Raghava, saw Sita, decked with the ornaments affectionately presented by the ascetic; and he rejoiced thereat.

sa_verse: प्रहृष्टस्त्वभवद् रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महारथः। मैथिल्याः सत्क्रियां दृष्ट्वा मानुषेषु सुदुर्लभाम्॥
en: Thereupon, witnessing the honour that had been accorded to Maithili, rare among men, Rāma became well pleased, as also that mighty car-warrior, Laksmana.

sa_verse: ततः स शर्वरी प्रीतः पुण्यां शशिनिभाननाम्। अर्चितस्तापसैः सर्वैरुवास रघुनन्दनः॥
en: Then Raghu's son, ministered to by the ascetic, happily spent the delightful night there, with her face resembling the moon.

sa_verse: तस्यां रात्र्यां व्यतीतायामभिषिच्य हुताग्निकान्। आपृच्छेतां नरव्याघ्रौ तापसान् वनगोचरान्॥
en: On the night having passed away, those foremost of men, after performing their ablutions, enquired of the ascetics performing fire sacrifices, who lived in the forest.

sa_verse: तावूचुस्ते वनचरास्तापसा धर्मचारिणः। वनस्य तस्य संचारं राक्षसैः समभिप्लुतम् ॥ रक्षांसि पुरुषादानि नानारूपाणि राघव। वसन्त्यस्मिन् महारण्ये व्यालाश्च रुधिराशनाः॥
en: Thereat the righteous ascetics ranging the forest said that all the sides of the forest were infested by Rākşasas. “Ferocious beasts feasting on blood, and Rākşasas, O Rāghava, living on human beings, wearing various shapes, abide in this mighty forest."

sa_verse: उच्छिष्टं वा प्रमत्तं वा तापसं ब्रह्मचारिणम्। अदन्त्यस्मिन् महारण्ये तान् निवारय राघव ॥
en: These eat up ascetics leading the Brahmacarya mode of life, who happen to be unclean or heedless. Do you, therefore, O Raghava, let them.

sa_verse: एष पन्था महर्षीणां फलान्याहरतां वने। अनेन तु वनं दुर्गं गन्तुं राघव ते क्षमम्।।
en: This is the path of the Maharșis, who procure fruits in the forest. By this way you will be able to enter the impracticable wild, O Rāghava.

sa_verse: इतीरितः प्राञ्जलिभिस्तपस्विभिर्द्विजैः कृतस्वस्त्ययनः यनः परंतपः। वनं सभार्यः प्रविवेश राघवः सलक्ष्मणः सूर्य इवाभ्रमण्डलम्॥
en: Thus addressed by the ascetic twice-born ones, and having been blessed by them, that repressor of foes, Rāghava, entered the forest in company with his wife and Lakşmaņa, like the sun entering a mass of clouds.

sa_verse: प्रविश्य तु महारण्यं दण्डकारण्यमात्मवान्। रामो ददर्श दुर्धर्षस्तापसाश्रममण्डलम्॥ कुशचीरपरिक्षिप्तं ब्राह्मलक्ष्म्या समावृतम्। यथा प्रदीप्तं दुर्दर्श गगने सूर्यमण्डलम्॥ शरण्यं सर्वभूतानां सुसंमृष्टाजिरं सदा। मृगैर्बहुभिराकीर्णं पक्षिसंधैः समावृतम्॥ पूजितं चोपनृत्तं च नित्यमप्सरस गणैः। विशालैरग्निशरणैः स्रग्भाण्डैरजिनैः कुशैः॥ समिद्भिस्तोयकलशैः फलमूलैश्च शोभितम्। आरण्यैश्च महावृक्षैः पुण्यैः स्वादुफलैर्वृतम्॥ बलिहोमार्चितं पुण्यं ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम्। पुष्पैश्चान्यैः परिक्षिप्तं पद्मिन्या च सपाया॥ फलमूलाशनैर्दान्तैश्चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरैः। सूर्यवैश्वानराभैश्च पुराणैर्मुनिभिर्युतम्।७।।
en: Entering the extensive forest of Dandaka, the irrepressible and self-composed Rāma saw the collection of asylums belonging to the ascetics, strewn with Kusa and bark, and environed by spiritual energy; incapable of being beheld; like the solar disc in the heavens, the refuge of all creatures with their ornamented yards; filled with a great many deer, and abounding in multitudes of birds; in which Apsaras always danced and which they held in respect; (asylums) beauteous with spacious rooms for fire sacrifice, with sacrificial necessaries, deer-skins, Kusa, faggots, water-pitchers, fruits and roots; surrounded by mighty and sacred fcrest-trees; crowned with lucious fruits; honoured with Bali and homa*; holy; resounding with the sounds of Vedic recitations; scattered with divers blossoms; and containing tanks filled with lotuses; with ancient ascetics living on fruits and roots, having their senses under control, wearing bark and black deer-skins, and possessing the splendour of the sun or fire; and adorued by great and holy sages living upon regulated fare.

sa_verse: पुण्यैश्च नियताहारैः शोभितं परमर्षिभिः। तद्ब्रह्मभवनप्रख्यं ब्रह्मघोषनिनादितम्॥ ब्रह्मविद्भिर्महाभागैाह्मणैरुपशोभितम्। तद् दृष्ट्वा राघवः श्रीमांस्तापसाश्रममण्डलम्॥ अभ्यगच्छन्महातेजा विज्यं कृत्वा महद् धनुः।
en: Beholding that collection of asylums belonging to the ascetics, resembling the regions of Brahmă, resonant with the voices of Vedic recitations; and grateful; with highly pious Brāhmaṇas versed in the Vedas, the exceedingly energetic and graceful Rāghava entered the same, having first unstrung his mighty bow.

sa_verse: दिव्यज्ञानोपपन्नास्ते रामं दृष्ट्वा महर्षयः॥ अभिजग्मुस्तदा प्रीता वैदेहीं च यशस्विनीम्। ते तु सोममिवोद्यन्तं दृष्ट्वा वै धर्मचारिणम्॥ लक्ष्मणं चैव दृष्ट्वा तु वैदेहीं च यशस्विनीम्। मङ्गलानि प्रयुञ्जानाः प्रत्यगृह्णन्दृढव्रताः॥
en: Thereupon, seeing the righteous Rāma resembling the moon risen, as well as Lakşmaņa and the illustrious Vaidehī, those . Maharsis endued with spiritual intuition, came forward (to meet the incomers); and, having uttered benedictions, those persons of rigid VOWS, received them.

sa_verse: रूपसंहननं लक्ष्मी सौकुमार्यं सुवेषताम्। ददृशुर्विस्मिताकारा रामस्य वनवासिनः॥
en: And those dwellers of the wood, struck with wonder, beheld Rāma's tender grace and loveliness and elegance of dress.

sa_verse: वैदेही लक्ष्मणं रामं नेत्रैरनिमिषैरिव। आश्चर्यभूतान्ददृशुः सर्वे ते वनवासिनः॥
en: And struck with astonishment, those inhabitants of the woods of pre-eminent piety saw Vaidehī, Lakşmaņa and Rāma, with winkless eyes.

sa_verse: अत्रैनं हि महाभागाः सर्वभूतहिते रताः। अतिथिं पर्णशालायां राघवं संन्यवेशयन्॥
en: And those persons of exalted virtue, engaged in the welfare of all creatures, made Rāghava, their guest, sit down in their thatched cottage.

sa_verse: ततो रामस्य सत्कृत्य विधिना पावकोपमाः। आजहुस्ते महाभागाः सललं धर्मचारिणः॥
en: Then, having received Rāma respectfully according to scriptural prescription, those virtuous ones of eminent piety, resembling fire, procured water (for Rāma).

sa_verse: मङ्गलानि प्रयुञ्जाना मुदा परमया युताः। मूलं पुष्पं फलं सर्वमाश्रमं च महात्मनः॥ निवेदयित्वा धर्मज्ञास्ते तु प्राञ्जलयोऽब्रुवन्। धर्मपालो जनस्यास्य शरण्यश्च महायशाः॥ पूजनीयश्च मान्यश्च राजा दण्डधरो गुरुः। इन्द्रस्यैव चतुर्भागः प्रजा रक्षति राघवः॥ राजा तस्माद् वरान् भोगान् रम्यान् भुङ्क्ते नमस्कृतः।
en: And, experiencing great delight, those highsouled ones, uttering benedictions, procured wild fruits, flowers and roots; and, having assigned an asylum (to Rāma), those persons cognizani of righteousness, said with joined hands, “Possessed of high fame, you, the protector of righteousness, are the refuge of these people. you should be honoured and worshipped, being their king, holding the rod, and their superior. O Rāghava, it is because he that governs his subjects, is a fourth part of Indra himself, that the king, being bowed down to by all, enjoys the choices things.

sa_verse: ते वयं भवता रक्ष्या भवद्विषयवासिनः। नगरस्थो वनस्थो वा त्वं नो राजा जनेश्वरः॥
en: And we, being in your dominions, ought to be protected by you. Whether living in the city or in the woods, you, lord of men, are our sovereign.

sa_verse: न्यस्तदण्डा वयं राजञ्जितक्रोधा जितेन्द्रियाः। रक्षणीयास्त्वया शश्वद् गर्भभूतास्तपोधनाः॥
en: We have renounced chastising others; and, O monarch, we have conquered our anger, and subdued our passions. Therefore, even as a child in its mother's womb (should be protected by her), should we be protected by you."

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा फलैर्मूलैः पुष्पैरन्यैश्च राघवम्। वन्यैश्च विविधाहारैः सलक्ष्मणमपूजयन्॥
en: Having said this, they entertained Rāghava, along with Laksmana, with fruits and roots and flowers and diverse other edibles procurable in the woods.

sa_verse: तथान्ये तापसाः सिद्धा रामं वैश्वानरोपमाः। न्यावृता यथान्यायं तर्पयामासुरीश्वरम् ॥
en: In the same way, other ascetics of accomplished purposes, living lives of integrity, duly pleased that lord, Rāma, resembling Vaisvānara.

sa_verse: कृतातिथ्योऽथ रामस्तु सूर्यस्योदयनं प्रति। आमन्त्र्य स मुनीन् सर्वान् वनमेवान्वगाहत॥
en: Having received the homage of the ascetics, Rāma about sun-rise, having greeted them all, entered into the forest.

sa_verse: नानामृगगणाकीर्णमृक्षशार्दूलसेवितम्। ध्वस्तवृक्षलतागुल्मं दुर्दर्शसलिलाशयम्॥ निष्कूजमानशकुनि झिल्लिकागणनादितम्। लक्ष्मणानुचरो रामो वनमध्यं ददर्श ह॥
en: And Rāma, followed by Lakṣmaṇa, saw the heart of the forest abounding in various kinds of beasts, ranged by bears and tigers, with its trees and shrubs torn and trampled, its pools turbid, and its birds crying.

sa_verse: सीतया सह काकुत्स्थस्तस्मिन् घोरमृगायुते। ददर्श गिरिशृङ्गाभं पुरुषादं महास्वनम् ॥ गभीराक्षं महावक्त्रं विकटं विकटोदरम्। बीभत्सं विषमं दीर्घ विकृतं घोरदर्शनम्॥ वसानं चर्म वैयाघ्रं वसाट्टै रुधिरोक्षितम्। त्रासनं सर्वभूतानां व्यादितास्यमिदान्तकम्॥ त्रीन् सिंहाश्चतुरो व्याघ्रान् द्वौ वृकौ पृषतान् दश। सविषाणं वसादिग्धं गजस्य च शिरो महत्॥ अवसज्यायते शूले विनदन्तं महास्वनम्।
en: Having in company with Sītā arrived at that forest abounding in terrible beasts, Kākutstha saw a man-eater, resembling a mountainsummit, emitting tremendous roars, with hollow eyes, a huge face, frightful, having a deformed belly, disgusting, dreadful, cyclopean, misshapen, of a horrible sight, clad in a tigerskin, besmeared with fat, covered with blood, capable of frightening all creatures, with his mouth widely extended, like to the Destroyer himself, and uttering loud shouts,-who stood piercing with his iron dart three lions, four tigers, two leopards, ten Prşatas, and the huge tusked head of an elephant dripping fat.

sa_verse: स राम लक्ष्मणं चैव सीतां दृष्ट्वा च मैथिलीम्॥ अभ्यधावत् सुसंक्रुद्धः प्रजाः काल इवान्तकः। स कृत्वा भैरवं नादं चालयन्निव मेदिनीम्॥
en: Having seen Rāma and Laksmana as well as Sītā the daughter of Mithilā, he growing angry, rushed (against them), like the Destroyer himself rushing against creatures at the universal dissolution.

sa_verse: अङ्केनादाय वैदेहीमपक्रम्य तदाब्रवीत्। युवां जटाचीरधरौ सभायौं क्षीणजीवितौ॥ प्रविष्टौ दण्डकारण्यं शरचापासिपाणिनौ।
en: Uttering a dreadful yell, and, as if making the earth tremble, he took Vaidehi on his waist, and, going a little distance, said, “O you wearing bark and matted locks, O you of feeble strength, that accompanied by your (common) wife, have entered the forest of Dandaka, bearing bows and arrows and scimitars, why, being ascetics, do you wish to associate with a (single) woman?

sa_verse: कथं तापसयोर्वां च वासः प्रमदया सह॥ अधर्मचारिणौ पापौ कौ युवां मुनिदूषको। अहं वनमिदं दुर्गं विराधो नाम राक्षसः॥ चरामि सायुधो नित्यमृषिमांसानि भक्षयन्। इयं नारी वरारोहा मम भार्या भविष्यति॥ युवयोः पापयोश्चाहं पास्यामि रुधिरं मृधे।
en: You wicked wretches, you imf.ous wights, who are you that bring disgrace upon ascetics? I am a Rākṣasa, Virādha by name. This forest is my fastness. Accoutred in arms, I range (here), feeding on the flesh of ascetics. This transcendentally beauteous one shall be my wife. And in battle I shall drink your blood, wretches that you are.

sa_verse: तस्यैवं ब्रुवतो दुष्टं विराधस्य दुरात्मनः॥ श्रुत्वा सगर्वितं वाक्यं सम्भ्रान्ता जनकात्मजा। सीता प्रवेपितोद्वेगात् प्रवाते कदली यथा॥
en: Hearing the wicked and vaunting speech of the impious Virādha, as he said this, Janaka's daughter, Sītā, began to tremble from fear, like a plantain tree shaken by the wind.

sa_verse: तां दृष्ट्वा राघवः सीतां विराधाङ्कगतां शुभाम्। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणं वाक्यं मुखेन परिशुष्यता ॥
en: Seeing the graceful Sītā on the waist of Virādha, Rāghava with a blank countenance said to Laksmana,

sa_verse: पश्य सौम्य नरेन्द्रस्य जनकस्यात्मसम्भवाम्। मम भार्यां शुभाचारां विराधाङ्के प्रवेशिताम् ॥
en: O amiable one, behold the daughter of king Janaka, my wife of pure ways, an illustrious princess brought up in luxury, on Virādha's waist.

sa_verse: अत्यन्तसुखसंवृद्धां राजपुत्रीं यशस्विनीम्। यदभिप्रेतमस्मासु प्रियं वरवृतं च यत्॥ कैकेयास्तु सुसंवृत्तं क्षिप्रमद्यैव लक्ष्मण। या न तुष्यति राज्येन पुत्रार्थं दीर्घदर्शिनी॥ ययाहं सर्वभूतानां प्रियः प्रस्थापितो वनम्। अद्येदानीं सकामा सा या माता मध्यमा मम॥
en: Lakşmaņa, what had been wished for by Kaikeyi with reference to us, (has taken place), and the dear boon (that she had asked) has born fruit. That far-sighted lady, my second mother, who had not been satisfied with having secured the kingdom in the interests of her son, and by whom I that was dear to her above all other creatures, had been banished to the woods, has to-day her wishes crowned with success.

sa_verse: परस्पर्शात् तु वैदेह्या न दुःखतरमस्ति मे। पितुर्विनाशात् सौमित्रे स्वराज्यहरणात् तथा॥
en: O Sumitra's son, nothing can impart to me greater anguish than the touching of Vaidehi by another, which surpasses my father's demise and my having been deprived of the kingdom.”'

sa_verse: इति ब्रुवति काकुत्स्थे बाष्पशोकपरिप्लुतः। अब्रवील्लक्ष्मणः क्रुद्धो रुद्धो नाग इव श्वसन्॥
en: As Kākutstha with his eyes flooded with tears was speaking thus, Lakşmaņa flying into a rage, spoke like to a confined elephant:

sa_verse: अनाथ इव भूतानां नाथस्त्वं वासवोपमः। मया प्रेष्येण काकुत्स्थ किमर्थं परितप्यसे ॥
en: Why do you, O Kākutstha, being the lord of all creatures and resembling Väsava himself, and also backed by myself, your servant, grieve like one forlorn ?

sa_verse: शरेण निहतस्याद्य मया क्रुद्धेन रक्षसः। विराधस्य गतासोर्हि मही पास्यति शोणितम्॥
en: The earth shall drink the gore of this Rākşasa, Virādha, when deprived of life, after he has been slain with a shaft by my angry self.

sa_verse: राज्यकामो मम क्रोधो भरते यो बभूव ह। तं विराधे विमोक्ष्यामि वज्री व्रजमिवाचले॥
en: That ire of mine which I had conceived against Bharata hankering after the kingdom, will I discharge on Virādha; even as the wielder of the thunder-bolt hurls his weapon at a hi!l.

sa_verse: मम भुजबलवेगवेगितः पततु शरोऽस्य महान् महोरसि। व्यपनयतु तनोश्च जीवितं पततु ततश्च महीं विघूर्णितः।।
en: Let my mighty arrow acquiring velocity from the speed imparted to it through the energy of my arms, light on his huge breast, and force his life from forth his body; and let him whirling (at length) fall down to the earth.

sa_verse: अथोवाच पुनर्वाक्यं विराधः पूरयन् वनम्। पृच्छतो मम हि ब्रूतं कौ युवां क्व गमिष्यथः॥
en: Then Virādha again spoke filling the forest, Do you tell me who ask you, who are you and whither are you going? The North-West Provinces text has additional Sloka : Having said this, the graceful Lakşmaņa spoke to Virādha as if jestingly, Who are you that coming to the forest, are ranging it pleasantly?-

sa_verse: तमुवाच ततो रामो राक्षसं ज्वलिताननम्। पृच्छन्तं सुमहातेजा इक्ष्वाकुकुलमात्मनः॥ क्षत्रियौ वृत्तसम्पन्नौ विद्धि नौ वनगोचरौ। त्वां तु वेदितुमिच्छावः कस्त्वं चरसि दण्डकान्॥
en: Thereupon, the highly energetic Rāma answered the Rākşasa with a flaming face, when he had thus asked (Rāma) that he belonged to the Iksvaku race. Know us that are in the forest, for Kșatriyas of respectable character. I also am anxious of knowing who you are, wandering in the Dandakas.

sa_verse: तमुवाच विराधस्तु रामं सत्यपराक्रमम्। हन्त वक्ष्यामि ते राजन् निबोध मम राघव॥
en: Virădha answered Råma having truth for his prowess, Ah! I will tell you, O king. Do you,O Răghava understand!

sa_verse: पुत्रः किल जवस्याहं माता मम शतहदा। विराध इति मामाहुः पृथिव्यां सर्वराक्षसाः॥
en: I am son of Java, and my mother is Satahradā. All the Rāksasas of the earth have called me Virādha.

sa_verse: तपसा चाभिसंप्राप्ता ब्रह्मणो हि प्रसादजा। शस्त्रेणावध्यता लोकेऽच्छेद्याभेद्यत्वमेव च ॥
en: Having gratified Brahmă by my asceticism, IⓇreceived (this boon) that none in the world would be able to slay me by mangling my body with weapons.

sa_verse: उत्सृज्य प्रमदामेनामनपेक्षौ यथागतम्। त्वरमाणौ पलायेथां न वां जीवितमाददे॥ तं रामः प्रत्युवाचेदं कोपसंरक्तलोचनः।
en: Forsaking this fair one, do you, renouncing all hope (of either recovering her or vanquishing me), speedily flee away to that place whence you had come; else I shall deprive you of your lives. Thereat, Rāma with his eyes reddened through wrath replied to that Rākşasa of a deformed shape, the wicked Viradha.

sa_verse: क्षुद्र धिक् त्वां तु हीनार्थं मृत्युमन्वेषसे ध्रुवम्। रणे प्राप्स्यसि संतिष्ठ न मे जीवन् विमोक्ष्यसे॥
en: Little! shame on you of base purposes! You surely seek your own death; and (death) you shall get in battle. Stay! You escapist me not with life.

sa_verse: ततः सज्यं धनुः कृत्वा रामः सुनिशिताशरान्। सुशीघ्रमभिसंधाय राक्षसं निजघान ह॥ धनुषा ज्यागुणवता सप्त बाणान् मुमोच ह। रुक्मपुङ्खान् महावेगान् सुपर्णानिलतुल्यगान्॥
en: Then stringing his bow, Rāma, speedily aiming at the Rākşasa, pierced him with sharpened shafts; and (then) from his bow-string let go seven shafts, having their feathered parts plaited with good, furnished with great velocity, and coursing like Suparna or the wind.

sa_verse: ते शरीरं विराधस्य भित्त्वा बर्हिणवाससः। निपेतुः शोणितादिग्धा धरण्यां पावकोपमाः॥
en: Thereat, resembling flames, those arrows furnished with peacocks, feathers, pierced. Viradha's body, and fell to the earth, covered with blood.

sa_verse: स विद्धो न्यस्य वैदेहीं शूलमुद्यम्य राक्षसः। अभ्यद्रवत् सुसंक्रुद्धस्तदा रामं सलक्ष्मणम्॥
en: On being thus pierced, the Rākṣasa set Vaidehi down, and, upraising a dart, rushed in wrath towards Rāma and Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: स विनद्य महानादं शूलं शक्रध्वजोपमम्। प्रगृह्याशोभत तदा व्यात्तानन इवान्तकः॥
en: And sending forth a mighty roar, he took up a dart resembling the banner of Sakra and he then appeared like the Destroyer* with opening mouth.

sa_verse: अथ तौ भ्रातरौ दीप्तं शरवर्षं ववर्षतुः। विराधे राक्षसे तस्मिन् कालान्तकयमोपमे॥
en: Then the brothers showered a blazing volley of shafts on that Rākşasa, resembling the Destroyer himself. The text has Kalāntakayamopame-abl..~~ resembling Kala, Antaka, or Yama. The commentator cites a sloka, which assigns three several functions to the three, Kāla execute his office on the occasion of the separation of life from the body; Antaka compasses the end; and Yama finally sits in judgement upon the departed soul. But this strikes me as misplaced erudition. I, however, give the commonsense rendering,

sa_verse: स प्रहस्य महारौद्रः स्थित्वाजृम्भत राक्षसः। जृम्भमाणस्य ते वाणाः कायान्निष्पेतुराशुगाः॥
en: Stationing himself at a spot, the Rākşasa, laughing terribly, yawned, and as he yawned the flest-coursing shafts fell off from his person, a

sa_verse: स्पर्शात्तु वरदानेन प्राणान् संरोध्य राक्षसः। विराधः शूलमुद्यम्य राघवावभ्यधावत॥
en: And by virtue of the bestowal of the boon (by Brahmā), the Rākşasa Virādha drew up his vital energies, and, lifting up a pike, darted at the descendants of Raghu.

sa_verse: तच्छूलं वज्रसंकाशं गगने ज्वलनोपमम्। द्वाभ्यां शराभ्यां चिच्छेद रामः शस्त्रभृतां वरः॥
en: That best of those bearing arms cut off with two arrows that dart resembling the thunder-bolt and flaming in the sky.

sa_verse: तद् रामविशिखैश्छिन्नं शूलं तस्यापतद् भुवि। पपाताशनिना छिन्नं मेरोरिव शिलातलम्॥
en: And the dart severed by the shafts of Rāma, fell to the earth, as falls a crag severed by the thunderbolt.

sa_verse: तौ खङ्गौ क्षिप्रमुद्यम्य कृष्णसर्पाविवोद्यतौ। तूर्णमापेततुस्तस्य तदा प्रहरतां बलात्॥
en: Thereat, swiftly raising up sword, resembling a black snake, each (of the Rāghavas) approached his antagonist, and began to assail him hastily.

sa_verse: स वध्यमानः सुभृशं भुजाभ्यां परिगृह्य तौ। अप्रकम्प्यौ नरव्याघ्रौ रौद्रः प्रस्थातुमैच्छत ॥
en: Thus hard beset, that terrible one, seizing them both with his arms, attempted to make away with those foremost of men, who, however, retained their calmness.

sa_verse: तस्याभिप्रायमाज्ञाय रामो लक्ष्मणब्रवीत्। वहत्वयमलं तावत् पथानेन तु राक्षसः॥ यथा चेच्छति सौमित्रे तथा वहतु राक्षसः। अयमेव हि नः पन्था येन याति निशाचरः॥
en: Reading his purpose, Rāma spoke to Lakşmana, Let the Rākşasa bear us by this way. Not use of letting him. O Sumitra's son let the Rākşasa bear us wherever he likes. Even this, by which the ranger of the night is proceeding, is our way.

sa_verse: स तु स्वबलवीर्येण समुत्क्षिप्य निशाचरः। बालाविव स्कन्धगतौ चकारातिर्बलोद्धतः॥
en: And lifting up (Rāma and Lakşmana) by his might and. prowess, as if they were striplings, that haughty ranger of the night laid them on his shoulders.

sa_verse: तावारोप्य ततः स्कन्धं राघवौ रजनीचरः। विराधो विनदन् घोरं जगामाभिमुखो वनम्॥
en: And having deposited the Rāghavas on his shoulders, Virādha-ranger* of night-sending up dreadful shouts, directed his course towards the forest. Another reading is:-That ranger of the night laid on his shoulders, those ones, who were puffed up with their exceeding prowess.

sa_verse: वनं महामेघनिभं प्रविष्टो द्रुमैर्महाद्भिर्विविधैरुपेतम्। नानाविधैः पक्षिकुलैर्विचित्रं शिवायुतं व्यालमृगैर्विकीर्णम्॥
en: And he at length entered the forest resembling a mighty mass of clouds, furnished with various kinds of trees, picturesque with diverse species of birds, and abounding in jackals, serpents and beasts.

sa_verse: ह्रियमाणौ तु काकुत्स्थौ दृष्ट्वा सीता रघूत्तमौ । उच्चैः स्वरेण चुक्रोश प्रगृह्य सुमहाभुजौ॥
en: Seeing those foremost of Raghus, the Kākutsthas, carried away, Sītā carried in a loud voice, raising up her arms. * *Another reading is Subhujābhujau-That one of elegant arms (raising) them up.

sa_verse: एष दाशरथी रामः सत्यवाञ्छीलवाञ्शुचिः। रक्षसा रौद्ररूपेण ह्रियते सहलक्ष्मणः॥
en: Rāma, the son of Dasaratha, the strong, the truthful and the pure, is, along with Laksmana, being carried away by you, Rākşasa of a terrible appearance.

sa_verse: मामृक्षा भक्षयिष्यन्ति सार्दुलद्वीपिनस्तथा। मां हरोत्सृज काकुत्स्थौ नमस्ते राक्षसोत्तम॥
en: Me bears will devour and tigers and panthers. Renouncing the Kākutsthas, do you carry me away. I bow to you, O best of Raksasas.

sa_verse: तस्यास्तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा वैदेह्या रामलक्ष्मणौ। वेगं प्रचक्रतुर्वीरौ वधे तस्य दुरात्मनः॥
en: Hearing these words of Vaidehi, Rāma and Lakşmaņa bestirred themselves for compassing the death of that wicked one.

sa_verse: तस्य रौद्रस्य सौमित्रिः सव्यं बाहुं बभञ्ज ह। रामस्तु दक्षिणं बाहुं तरसा तस्य रक्षसः॥
en: And Sumitra's son broke his left arm; while Rāma at once broke the Rākşasa's right one.

sa_verse: स भग्नबाहुः संविग्नः पपाताशु विमूछितः। धरण्यां मेघसंकाशो वज्रभिन्न इवाचलः॥
en: On his arms being broken, the Raksasa resembling a mass of clouds, growing weak, sank down on the ground in a swoon, like a hill riven by the thunderbolt.

sa_verse: मुष्टिभिर्वाहुभिः पद्भिः सूदयन्तौ तु राक्षसम्। उद्यम्योद्यम्य चाप्येनं स्थण्डिले निष्पिपेषतुः॥
en: Thereupon, They assailed the Rākṣasa with their fists, arms and feet; and lifting him up once and again, and pressing him, they trod on him over the ground.

sa_verse: स विद्धो बहुभिर्बाणैः खङ्गाभ्यां च परिक्षतः। निष्पिष्टो बहुधा भूमौ न ममार स राक्षसः॥
en: Although he was sore pierced by full many an arrow and cut shortly by scimitars, and pressed down on the earth in various ways, yet the Rākşasa expired not.

sa_verse: तं प्रेक्ष्य रामः सुभृशमवध्यम च लोपमम्। भयेष्वभयदः श्रीमानिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥ तपसा पुरुषव्याघ्र राक्षसोऽयं न शक्यते। शस्त्रेण युधि निर्जेतुं राक्षसं निखनावहे॥
en: Seeing him utterly incapable of being killed, and resembling a hill, that inspirer of hope in times of peril, the graceful (Rama), said, O foreinost of men, in consequence of his austerities, yon Rākşasa cannot be vanquished with weapons in conflict. Therefore, let us cast him into a pit.

sa_verse: कुञ्जरस्येव रौद्रस्य राक्षसस्यास्य लक्ष्मण। वनेऽस्मिन् सुमहच्छ्वभ्रं खन्यतां रौद्रवर्चसः॥
en: O Lakşmaņa, dig a capacious pit in this forest* for this Rākşhasa resembling an elephant, terrible, and of a grim presence. *The reading slightly varies in some texts.-T

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा लक्ष्मणं रामः प्रदरः खन्यतामिति । तस्थौ विराधमाक्रम्य कण्ठे पादेन वीर्यवान्॥
en: Having said this to Lakşmaņa,-Do you dig a pit, the powerful Rāma remained planting his foot on Virādha's throat.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा राघवेणोक्तं राक्षस: प्रश्रितं वचः। इदं प्रोवाच काकुत्स्थं विराधः पुरुषर्षभम्॥
en: Having heard Rāghava's words, the Rākşasa Virādha, spoke gently to the descendant of Kākutstha, the best among the men.

sa_verse: हतोऽहं पुरुषव्याघ्र शक्रतुल्यबलेन वै। मया तु पूर्वं त्वं मोहान ज्ञातः पुरुषर्षभ॥
en: Slain am I, O chief of men, by you possessed of strength equal to that of Śakra. Through ignorance, O foremost of men, I could not before know you.

sa_verse: कौसल्या सुप्रजास्तात रामस्त्वं विदितो मया। वैदेही च महाभागा लक्ष्मणश्च महायसाः॥
en: Now I know you that are Rāma, the worthy Son of Kausalyā, my child; as well as the highly virtuous Vaidehi and the renowned Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: अभिशापादहं घोरां प्रविष्टो राक्षसी तनुम्। तुम्बुरुर्नाम गन्धर्वः शप्तो वैश्रवणेन हि ॥
en: By virtue of a curse, I entered this dreadful Räkşasi form, I a Gandharva, having been cursed by Vaisravaņa.

sa_verse: प्रसाद्यमानश्च मया सोऽब्रवीन्मां महायशाः। यदा दाशरथी रामस्त्वां वधिष्यति संयुगे॥ तदा प्रकृतिमापन्नो भवान् स्वर्गं गमिष्यति।
en: Being propitiated by me, that greatly famous one said, 'When Rāma the son of Dasaratha, shall slay you in encounter, you, attaining your natural condition, shall repair to the celestial regions.'

sa_verse: अनुपस्थीयमानो मां स क्रुद्धो व्याजहार ह॥ इति वैश्रवणो राजा रम्भासक्तमुवाच ह।
en: Getting wroth, he cursed me, who had been absent; and thus did king Vaiśravaņa address me, who had conceived love for Rambhā.

sa_verse: तव प्रसादान्मुक्तोऽहमभिशापात् सुदारुणात्॥ भुवनं स्वं गमिष्यामि स्वस्ति वोऽस्तु परंतप।
en: Through your grace have I been freed from this fearful curse, I shall (now) repair to heaven. Hail, O repressor of foes!

sa_verse: इतो वसति धर्मात्मा सरभङ्गः प्रतापवान्॥ अध्यर्धयोजने तात महर्षिः सूर्यसंनिभः। तं क्षिप्रमभिगच्छ त्वं स ते श्रेयोऽभिधास्यति ॥ अवटे चापि मां राम निक्षिप्य कुशली व्रज। रक्षसां गतसत्वानामेष धर्मः सनातनः॥
en: Half a Yojana hence, my child, dwells the righteous and potent Maharși, Śarabharga, resembling the Sun. Seek him speedily: he will bring about your good. Casting me (into this pit) in the wood, do you, O Rama, peacefully go your way. Even this is the eternal usage of the Raksasas.

sa_verse: अवटे ये निधीयन्ते तेषां लोकाः सनातनाः। एवमुक्त्वा तु काकुत्स्थं विराधः शरपीडितः॥ बभूव स्वर्गसम्प्राप्तो न्यस्तदेहो महाबलः।
en: Those that die in the forest, attain eternal regions. Having said this to Kākutstha, the mighty Viradha, afflicted with arrows, having his body deposited (in the pit), attained heaven.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा राघवो वाक्यं लक्ष्मणं व्यादिदेश ह॥ कुञ्जरस्येव रौद्रस्य राक्षसस्यास्य लक्ष्मण। वनेऽस्मिन्सुमहाश्वभ्रः खन्यतां रौद्रकर्मणः॥
en: Having heard that speech of Virādha, Rāghava ordered Laksmana, saying, O Laksmana, do you in this forest dig a capacious pit for this Rākṣasa resembling an elephant, dreadful, and of terrible acts.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा लक्ष्मणं रामः प्रदरः खन्यतामिति। तस्थौ विराधमाक्रम्य कण्ठे पादेन वीर्यवान् ॥
en: Having said to Laksmana, “Dig a pit," Rāma possessed of prowess remained fixing his foot on Virādha's throat.

sa_verse: ततः खनित्रमादाय लक्ष्मणः श्वभ्रमुत्तमम्। अखनत् पार्श्वतस्तस्य विराधस्य महात्मनः॥
en: Then taking a hoe, Lakşmaņa dug a spacious pit by the side of the huge-bodied* Virādha. *Mahātmānam-lit.-high-souled. The poet may have applied the epithet in this sense in view of Virādha being really a Gandharva. I have, however, preferred the other sense, as being more in harmony with the character of Virādha as a Rākşasa; and have accordingly rendered the word, huge bodied.

sa_verse: तं मुक्तकण्ठमुत्क्षिप्य शङ्कुकर्णं महास्वनम्। विराधं प्राक्षिपच्छ्वभ्रे नदन्तं भैरवस्वनम्॥ तमाहवे दारुणमाशुविक्रमौ स्थिरावुभौ संयति रामलक्ष्मणौ। मुदान्वितौ चिक्षिपतुर्भयावह नदन्तमुत्क्षिप्य बलेन राक्षसम्॥
en: Then when his throat had been freed (Laks maņa) cast into the pit Virādha having ears resembling javelins, sending loud and dreadful sounds.

sa_verse: अवध्यतां प्रेक्ष्य महासुरस्य तौ शितेन शस्त्रेण तदा नरर्षभौ। समर्थ्य चात्यर्थविशारदावुभौ बिले विराधस्य वधं प्रचक्रतुः॥
en: Having vanquished him in fight, Rāma and Lakşmana possessed of activity and steady in fight, being exceedingly delighted, raising up the dreadful Räksasa by main force, cast him howling (into the pit). Reflecting that he was incapable of being slain (by sharpened weapons), those foremost of men, exceedingly well versed (in arms), compassed the death of that mighty Asura, Virādha, in the pit, after they had dug it.

sa_verse: स्वयं विराधेन हि मृत्युमात्मनः प्रसह्य रामेण यथार्थमीप्सितः। निवेदितः काननचारिणा स्वयं न मे वधः शस्त्रकृतो भवेदिति ॥
en: Virādha had himself sincerely wished for his death at the hands of Räma; and that ranger of the woods had accordingly told (Rāma),: My death cannot be effected by means of weapons.

sa_verse: तदेव रामेण निशम्य भाषितं कृता मतिस्तस्य बिलप्रवेशने। बिलं च तेनातिबलेन रक्षसा प्रवेश्यमानेन वनं विनादितम्॥
en: Having heard these words, Rāma decided upon casting him into a pit. And when the excessively strong Rākṣasa entered the cavity, he made the forest resound with his cries,

sa_verse: प्रहृष्टरूपाविव रामलक्ष्मणौ विराधमुर्त्यां प्रदरे निपात्य तम्। ननन्दतुर्वीतभयौ महावने शिलाभिरन्तर्दधतुश्च राक्षसम्॥ ततस्तु तौ काञ्चनचित्रकार्मुको निहत्य रक्षः परिगृह्य मैथिलीम्। विजह्रतुस्तौ मुदितौ महावने दिवि स्थितौ चन्द्रदिवाकराविव॥
en: Having thrown him into the hole in the earth, Rāma and Lakşmaņa, their fear being removed, and covered the Rākṣasa with rocks. And having slain the Rākşasa and taken Mithila's daughter, those ones having bows decked in gold, being delighted, rejoiced in that mighty forest, like the sun and the moon seated in the sky.

sa_verse: हत्वा तु तं भीमबलं विराधं राक्षसं वने। ततः सीतां परिष्वज्य समाश्वास्य च वीर्यवान्॥ अब्रवीद् भ्रातरं रामो लक्ष्मणं दीप्ततेजसम्। कष्टं वनमिदं दुर्गं न च स्मो वनगोचराः॥ अभिगच्छामहे शीघ्रं शरभङ्गं तपोधनम्। आश्रमं शरभङ्गस्य राघवोऽभिजगाम ह॥
en: Having in the forest slain the Rākṣasa Virādha possessed of tremendous strength, Rāma endued with prowess, embracing and cheering Sītā, spoke to his brother Lakşınaņa of flaming energy, saying, This dense forest is hard to live in; nor are reacquainted with the was of the wood. Therefore will we speedily repair to the ascetic Sarabhanga. Thereupon Rāghava set out for the hermitage of Sarabhanga.

sa_verse: तस्य देवप्रभावस्य तपसा भावितात्मनः। समीपे शरभङ्गस्य ददर्श महदद्भुतम्॥
en: And Rāma beheld a mighty wonder near that one of celestial virtue, who had purified his soul through asceticism.

sa_verse: विभ्राजमानं वपुषा सूर्यवैश्वानरप्रभम्। रथप्रवरमारूढमाकाशे विबुधानुगम्॥ असंस्पृशन्तं वसुधां ददर्श विबुधेश्वरम्। सम्प्रभाभरणं देवं विरजोऽम्बरधारिणम्॥
en: He saw the lord of the celestials, possessed of a resplendent person, resembling in effulgence the sun or Vaisvanara-mounted on a superb car (saw) him that is followed by all the celestials, stationed in the sky, not touching the earth, adorned in luminous ornaments-the god dressed in a clean appear!

sa_verse: तद्विधैरेव बहुभिः पूज्यमानं महात्मभिः। हरितैर्वाजिभिर्युक्तमन्तरिक्षगतं रथम्॥ ददर्शादूरतस्तस्य तरुणादित्यसंनिभम्।
en: Worshipped by many a high-should personage dressed in the same way. And from a distance, (Rāma) saw the car stationed in the sky yoked with green steeds-resembling the infant sun;

sa_verse: पाण्डुराभ्रघनप्रख्यं चन्द्रमण्डलसंनिभम्॥ अपश्यद् विमलं छत्रं चित्रमाल्योपशोभितम्।
en: And he saw the spotless umbrella possessing the beauty of a mass of white clouds, resembling the lunar disc, and graced with variegated wreaths.

sa_verse: चामरव्यजने चाग्ये रुक्मदण्डे महाधने॥ गृहीते वरनारीभ्यां धूयमाने च मूर्धनि।
en: And his (Indira's) head was being fanned by two chowris, costly and furnished with golden handles, held by two magnificent damsels; and innumerable Gandharvas and Siddhas and prime sages were hymning the celestial stationed in the welkin. And seeing Sarabhanga engaged in converse with Satakratu, Rāma said to Laksmana (the following words). And pointing out the car, Rāma showed the wonder to his brother.

sa_verse: गन्धर्वामरसिद्धाश्च बहवः परमर्षयः॥ अन्तरिक्षगतं देवं गीर्भिरग्र्याभिरैडयन्। सह सम्भाषमाणे तु शरभङ्गेन वासवे॥ दृष्ट्वा शतक्रतुं तत्र रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्। रामोऽथ रथमुद्दिश्य भ्रातुर्दर्शयताद्भुतम्॥ अर्चिष्मन्तं श्रिया जुष्टमद्भुतं पश्य लक्ष्मण । प्रतपन्तमिवादित्यमन्तरिक्षगतं रथम्॥
en: Behold, O Laksmana, yonder car stationed in the sky, splendid, furnished with exceeding grace, wonderful, and seeming as if the sun had fallen from the heavens.

sa_verse: ये हयाः पुरुहूतस्य पुरा शक्रस्य नः श्रुताः। अन्तरिक्षगता दिव्यास्त इमे हरयो ध्रुवम्॥
en: The steeds standing in the sky are for certain those of which we had formerly heard as beionging to Sakra of many sacrifices.

sa_verse: इमे च पुरुषव्याघ्र ये तिष्ठन्त्यभितो दिशम्। शतं शतं कुण्डलिनो युवानः खङ्गपाणयः॥ विस्तीर्णविपुलोरस्काः परिघायतबाहवः।। शोणांशुवसनाः सर्वे व्याघ्रा इव दुरासदाः॥
en: And, you foremost of men, these youths envisioning (the celestial) by hundreds, wearing earrings, and bearing swords in their hands, are broad of chest, have arms resembling bludgeons in size, and are clad in crimson vesture, like to tigers incapable of being approached.

sa_verse: उरोदेशेषु सर्वेषां हारा ज्वलनसंनिभाः। रूपं विभ्रति सौमित्रे पञ्चविंशतिवार्षिकम्॥
en: Over the breasts of all appear chains of blazing sheen; and, O son of Sumitrā, their beauty appears that of five and twenty.

sa_verse: एतद्धि किल देवानां वयो भवति नित्यदा। यथेमे पुरुषव्याघ्रा दृश्यन्ते प्रियदर्शनाः॥
en: And even this is the ever-enduring age of the celestials, as these foremost of men of sweet looks appear.

sa_verse: इहैव सह वैदेह्या मुहूर्तं तिष्ठ लक्ष्मण। यावज्जानाम्यहं व्यक्तं क्व एष द्युतिमान् रथे॥
en: O Laksmana, stay here awhile with Vaidehi, so long as I do not gather who it is that is in the effulgent car.

sa_verse: तमेवमुक्त्वा सौमित्रिमिहैव स्थीयतामिति। अभिचक्राम काकुत्स्थः शरभङ्गाश्रमं प्रति॥
en: Saying to Sumitrās son, Stay here, Kākutstha bent his steps towards Sarabhaiga's asylum.

sa_verse: ततः समभिगच्छन्तं प्रेक्ष्य रामं शचीपतिः। शरभङ्गमनुज्ञाप्य विबुधानिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: And seeing Rāma approach, Saci's lord, taking Sarabhanga's permission, addressed the celestials.

sa_verse: इहोपयात्यसौ रामो यावन्मां नाभिभाषते। निष्ठां नयत तावत् तु ततो माद्रष्टुमर्हति॥
en: Rāma is coming hither. Do you set out for another place, ere he yet addresses me. He is not fit to behold me (now.)

sa_verse: जितवन्तं कृतार्थं हि तदाहमचिरादिमम्। कर्म ह्यनेन कर्तव्यं महदन्यैः सुदुष्करम्॥
en: When he shall have vanquished (his foe in fight) and won success, shall I readily show myself to him. He shall have to achieve an act incapable of being achieved by others.

sa_verse: अथ वज्री तमामन्त्र्य मानयित्वा च तापसम्। रथेन हययुक्तेन ययौ दिवमरिंदमः॥
en: Then rendering homage to that anchorite and conversing with him, the wielder of the thunderbolt-subduer of enemies-repaired to heaven in a car yoked with steeds.

sa_verse: प्रयाते तु सहस्राक्षे राघवः सपरिच्छदः। अग्निहोत्रमुपासीनं शरभङ्गमुपागमत्॥
en: When the thousand-eyed one had gone away, Rāghava accompanied by his companions appeared before Sarabhanga in the (Agni-hotra) chamber.

sa_verse: तस्य पादौ च संगृह्य रामः सीता च लक्ष्मणः। निषेदुस्तदनुज्ञाता लब्धवासा निमन्त्रिताः॥
en: Taking his feet, Rāma, Sītā and Lakşmaņa sat down with the permission of the ascetic, who received them and set apart quarters for them.

sa_verse: मामेष वरदो राम ब्रह्मलोकं निनीषति। जितमुग्रेण तपसा दुष्प्रापमकृतात्मभिः॥
en: O Rāma, this bestower of boons wishes to take me to the regions of Brahmā. I have conquered them, incapable of being attained by persons that have not schooled themselves, by virtue of fierce austerities.

sa_verse: अहं ज्ञात्वा नरव्याघ्र वर्तमानमदूरतः। ब्रह्मलोकं न गच्छामि त्वामदृष्ट्वा प्रियातिथिम्॥
en: O chief of men, knowing that you were near, I became anxious to go to the minor celestial regions and the supreme heaven (of Brahmā), after I had seen you, righteous and high-souled, who are my dear guest now.

sa_verse: त्वयाहं पुरुषव्याघ्र धार्मिकेण महात्मना। समागम्य गमिष्यामि त्रिदिवं चावरं परम्॥ अक्षया नरशार्दूल जिता लोका मया शुभाः। ब्राह्मयाश्च नाकपृष्ठ्याश्च प्रतिगृह्णीष्व मामकान्॥
en: O foremost of men, having had a meeting with you, a high-souled prince of righteous nature, I have for aye attained the excellent regions of Brahmã and Nākaprştha. Do you receive them as pertaining to me.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो नरव्याघ्रः सर्वशास्त्रविशारदः। ऋषिणा शरभङ्गेन राघवो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥
en: Thus addressed by the sage, Sarabhanga, that best of men, Rāghava, versed in every branch of learning, said.

sa_verse: अहमेवाहरिष्यामि सर्वांल्लोकान् महामुने। आवासं त्वहमिच्छामि प्रदिष्टमिह कानने॥
en: O mighty anchorite, I shall acquire all those regions myself. I now wish that you may direct me as to my abode in this forest.

sa_verse: राघवेणैवमुक्तस्तु शक्रतुल्यबलेन वै। शरभङ्गो महाप्राज्ञः पुनरेवाब्रवीद् वचः॥
en: Thus accosted by Rāghava resembling Sakra in strength, the eminently wise Sarabhanga again said,

sa_verse: इह राम महातेजाः : सुतीक्ष्णो नाम धार्मिकः। वसत्यरण्ये नियतः स ते श्रेयो विधास्यति॥
en: O Rāma, here in this forest lives a pious and self-restrained ascetic of mighty energy, named Sutiksna. He will bring about your good.

sa_verse: सुतीक्ष्णमभिगच्छ त्वं शुचौ देशे तपस्विनम्। रमणीये वनोद्देशे स ते वासं विधाास्यति॥ इमां मन्दाकिनी राम प्रतिस्रोतामनुव्रज । नदी पुषपोडुपवहां ततस्तत्र गमिष्यसि ॥
en: O Rāma, follow this Mandākini flowing among flowers, which (here runs in an opposite direction to her usual course; and then will you reach there.

sa_verse: एष पन्था नरव्याघ्र मुहर्तं पश्य तात माम्। यावज्जहामि गात्राणि जीर्णां त्वचमिवोरगः॥
en: This is your course, you best of men. Do you now, my child, for a space look at me; while I leave of my limbs, even as a serpent renounces its slough.

sa_verse: ततोऽग्नि स समाधाय हुत्वा चाज्येन मन्त्रवत्। शरभङ्गो महातेजाः प्रविवेश हुताशनम्॥
en: Then preparing a .fire and with Mantras a offering obiations into it, the exceedingly energetic Sarabhanga entered into the fire.

sa_verse: तस्य रोमाणि केशांश्च तदा वह्निर्महात्मनः। जीर्णां त्वचं तदस्थीनि यच्च मांसं च शोणितम्॥
en: The fire consumed the down, hair, old skin, bones, flesh and blood of that high-souled one.

sa_verse: स च पावकसंकाशः कुमारः समपद्यत। उत्थायाग्निचयात् तस्माच्छरभङ्गो व्यरोचत ॥
en: Anon) Śarabharga became youth resembling fire; and rising from the flame, he appeared splendid.

sa_verse: स लोकानाहिताग्नीनामृषीणां च महात्मनाम्। देवानां च व्यतिक्रम्य ब्रह्मलोकं व्यरोहत॥
en: And proceeding past the regions of highsouled sages sacrificing with fire, as well as of the celestials, he ascended those of Brahmā.

sa_verse: स पुण्यकर्मा भुवने द्विजर्षभः पितामहं सानुचरं ददर्श ह। पितामहश्चापि समीक्ष्य तं द्विजं ननन्द सुस्वागतमित्युवाच ह ॥
en: That one of pious acts done on earth-that best of the twice-born the grandfather, in company with his followers. And the grand-father also, beholding that Brahmana, rejoiced greatly, and said-Hail! race-saw

sa_verse: शरभङ्गे दिवं प्राप्ते मुनिसङ्घाः समागताः। अभ्यगच्छन्त काकुत्स्थं रामं ज्वलिततेजसम्॥
en: After Sarabhanga had ascended heaven, ascetics in bodies, assembled together, presented themselves before Kākutstha, Rāma of flaming energy.

sa_verse: अभिगम्य च धर्मज्ञा रामं धर्मभृतां वरम्। ऊचुः परमधर्मज्ञमृषिसङ्घाः समागताः॥
en: And coming before Rāma, the foremost of those practising righteousness, those sages conversant with morality, met in a body, said to that pre-eminently virtuous one,

sa_verse: त्वमिक्ष्वाकुकुलस्यास्य पृथिव्याश्च महारथः। प्रधानश्चापि नाथश्च देवानां मघवानिव॥
en: A mighty car-warrior, you are the foremost person of the lkshvāku race and the world; as well as their lord, even as Maghavān is the lord of the celestials.

sa_verse: विश्रुतस्रिषु लोकेषु यशसा विक्रमेण च। पितृव्रतत्वं सत्यं च त्वयि धर्मश्च पुष्कलः॥
en: Famous over the three worlds in high worth and prowess, in you are found truth and virtue in profusion and obedience to the mandate of your sire.

sa_verse: त्वामासाद्य महात्मानं धर्मज्ञं धर्मवत्सलम्। अर्थित्वान्नाथ वक्ष्यामस्तच्च नः क्षन्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: O lord, it behoves you cognizant of virtue and attached to it, to forgive us for what we as suiters say to you.

sa_verse: अधर्मः सुमहान् नाथ भवेत् तस्य तु भूपतेः। यो हरेद् बलिषड्भागं न च रक्षति पुत्रवत्॥
en: The sin, O lord, of that monarch is mighty that take a sixth part of the subjects' incomes, but dose not protect them as sons.

sa_verse: युञ्जानः स्वानिव प्राणान् प्राणैरिष्टान् सुतानिव। नित्ययुक्तः सदा रक्षन् सर्वान् विषयवासिनः॥ प्राप्नोति साश्वती राम कीर्तिं स बहुवार्षिकीम्। ब्रह्मणः स्थानमासाद्य तत्र चापि महीयते॥
en: But he that, setting his heart on protecting the people, dose ever carefully protect all the inhabitants of his dominions, like his own life or like his son dearer to him than life itself, repeat, O Rāma, enduring renown extending over many years, and (at length) attaining the regions of Brahmā, is glorified there.

sa_verse: यत् करोति परं धर्मं मुनिर्मूलफलाशनः। तत्र राज्ञश्चतुर्भागः प्रजा धर्मेण रक्षतः॥
en: The king that protect his subjects righteously, is entitled to a fourth part of the great religious merit that is reaped by an ascetic subsisting on fruits and roots.

sa_verse: सोऽयं ब्राह्मणभूयिष्ठो वानप्रस्थगणो महान्। त्वंन्नाथोऽनाथवद् राम राक्षसैर्हन्यते भृशम्॥
en: O Rāma, this many Brahmanas-this great body of men that have assumed the Vānaprastha mode of life, although having you for their lord, are being sorely troubled by Rákşasas, as if they had none.

sa_verse: एहि पश्य शरीराणि मुनीनां भावितात्मनाम्। हतानां राक्षसैघोरैर्बहूनां बहुधा वने॥
en: Come and behold the bodies of innumerable ascetics of pure hearts that have in various ways been slaughtered by Rākşasas in the forest.

sa_verse: पम्पानदीनिवासानामनुमन्दाकिनीमपि। चित्रकूटालयानां च क्रियते कदनं महत्॥
en: And great is the worry that is experienced by the dwellers on the river Pampā and the Mandakini as well as those that reside in Citrakūta.

sa_verse: एवं वयं न मृष्यामो विप्रकारं तपस्विनाम्। क्रियमाणं वने घोरं रक्षोभिर्भीमकर्मभिः॥
en: We cannot bear the terrible affliction of the ascetics in the forest at the hands of Räkşasas of dreadful deeds.

sa_verse: ततस्त्वां शरणार्थं च शरण्यं समुपस्थिताः। परिपालयं नो राम वध्यमानान् निशाचरैः॥
en: Therefore, for obtaining refuge, have we come before you who are the refuge (of all). Do you, O Rāma, deliver us all, who are being exceedingly afflicted by the rangers of the night.

sa_verse: परा त्वत्तो गतिर्वीर पृथिव्यां नोपपद्यते। परिपालय नः सर्वान् राक्षसेभ्यो नृपात्मज ॥
en: O hero, we have no greater refuge on earth than yourself. Do you, O king's son, rescues us all from Raksasas.

sa_verse: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु काकुत्स्थस्तापसानां तपस्विनाम्। इदं प्रोवाच धर्मात्मा सर्रानेव तपस्विनः॥
en: Hearing these words of the sages and ascetics, that righteous-souled one addressed them.

sa_verse: नैवमर्हथ मां वक्तुमाज्ञाप्योऽहं तपस्विनाम्। केवलेन स्वकार्येण प्रवेष्टव्यं वनं मया॥
en: It dose not behove you to speak thus. I should be commanded by the anchorets. I have entered the forest solely with a view to my own purpose.

sa_verse: विप्रकारमपाक्रष्टुं राक्षसैर्भवतामिमम्। पितुस्तु निर्देशकरः प्रविष्टोऽहमिदं वनम् ॥
en: I have entered this forest in obedience to the mandate of my father, with the object (at the same time) of putting an end to these ravages of the Rākşasas on you.

sa_verse: भवतामर्थसिद्ध्यर्थमागतोऽहं यदृच्छया। तस्य मेऽयं वने वासो भविष्यति महाफलः॥
en: I have at my own will come hither for securing your interest. Then shall my stay in the woods be crowned with mighty fruit.

sa_verse: तपस्विनां रणे शत्रून् हन्तुमिच्छामि राक्षसान्। पश्यन्तु वीर्यमृषयः सभ्रातुर्मे तपोधनाः॥
en: I wish to slay in battle the enemies of the ascetics. Let the sages and ascetics behold my prowess as well as that of my brother.

sa_verse: दत्वा वरं चापि तपोधतानानां धर्मे धृतात्मा सह लक्ष्मणेन। तपोधनैश्चापि सहार्यदत्त : सुतीक्ष्णमेवाभिजगाम वीरः॥
en: Having been conferred a boon by the ascetics, that hero entered upon a noble undertaking, and accompanied by the ascetics, in company with Laksmana directed his course to the hermitage of) Sutiksna.

sa_verse: रामस्तु सहितो भ्रात्रा सीतया च परंतपः। सुतीक्ष्णस्याश्रमपदं जगाम सह तैर्द्विजैः॥
en: And Rāma accompanied by his brother as well as Sītā went to the asylum of Sutīksna in company with the twice-born ones.

sa_verse: सं गत्वा दूरमध्वानं नदीस्तीत्वा॑ बहूदकाः। ददर्श विमलं शैलं महामेरुमिवोत्रतम्॥
en: And having proceeded far, and crossed many a stream of copious waters, Rāma saw a holy peak towering high like the mighty Meru.

sa_verse: ततस्तदिक्ष्वाकुवरौ सततं विविधैर्दुमैः। काननं तौ विविशतुः सीतया सह राघवौ॥
en: Then those worthy scions of the Ikşvāku race-descendants of Raghu—with Sītā entered the forest ever furnished with various kinds of trees.

sa_verse: प्रविष्टस्तु वनं घोरं बहुपुष्पफलद्रुमम्। ददर्शाश्रममेकान्ते चीरमालापरिष्कृतम्॥
en: And having entered the dense forest abounding in blossoms and fruits and trees, Rāma saw in a recess an asylum glittering with bark and garlands.

sa_verse: तत्र तापसमासीनं मलपङ्कजधारिणम्। रामः सुतीक्ष्णं विधिवत् तपोधनमभाषत॥
en: There Rāma duly addressed an ascetic seated in the lotus-attitude* for warding off evil, even Sutīksna, *The yoya system has many positions for concentrating thought.

sa_verse: रामोऽहमस्मि भगवन् भवन्तं द्रष्टुमागतः। तन्माभिवद धर्मज्ञ महर्षे सत्यविक्रम॥
en: O you conversant with righteousness, speak to me, O Maharsi, You that have truth for your prowess.

sa_verse: स निरीक्ष्य ततो धीरो रामं धर्मभृतां वरम्। समाश्लिष्य च बाहुभ्यामिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereupon eyeing Rāma keenly, that calm (ascetic)-the foremost of those practising righteousness, embracing him with his arms, said,

sa_verse: स्वागतं ते रघुश्रेष्ठ राम सत्यभृतां वर। आश्रमोऽयं त्वयाऽऽक्रान्तः सनाथ इव साम्प्रतम॥
en: Welcome, you foremost of the Raghus, O Rāma, you the best of those practising righteousness. This asylum now has been furnished with its master in consequence of your visit.

sa_verse: प्रतीक्षमाणस्त्वामेव नारोहेऽहं महायशः। देवलोकमितो वीर देहं त्यक्त्वा महीतले॥
en: O illustrious one, O hero, expecting you, I have not yet ascended heaven, renouncing on earth this body of mine.

sa_verse: चित्रकूटमुपादाय राज्यभ्रष्टोऽसि मे श्रुतः। इहोपयातः काकुत्स्थ देवराजः शतक्रतुः॥
en: I had already) heard that, coming to Citrakūta, (you had been staying there), having been deprived of the kingdom. Here came, O Kākutstha, the Sovereign of the celestials of an hundred sacrifices.

sa_verse: उपागम्य च मे देवो महादेवः सुरेश्वरः। सर्वांल्लोकाञ्जितानाह मम पुण्येन कर्मणा॥
en: Coming to me, that mighty deity, the Sovereign of the celestials, said that I had acquired all the worlds by my pious acts.

sa_verse: तेषु देवर्षिजुष्टेषु जितेषु तपसा मया। मत्प्रसादात् सभार्यस्त्वं विहरस्व सलक्ष्मणः॥
en: Do you, by my grace, in company with your wife and Lakşmaņa, dwell delightfully in those regions won by my asceticism, containing Devarsis.

sa_verse: तमुग्रतपसं दीप्तं महर्षि सत्यवादिनम्। प्रत्युवाचात्मवान रामो ब्रह्माणमिव वासवः॥
en: Thereupon the self-possessed Rāma answered that blazing and truth-telling Brāhmana of fiery austerities, like Vasava answering Brahma.

sa_verse: अहमेवाहरिष्यामि स्वयं लोकान् महामुने। आवासं त्वहमिच्छामि प्रदिष्टमिह कानने॥
en: O mighty ascetic, I myself will win those regions. I wish to be directed to a dwelling in this forest.

sa_verse: भवान् सर्वत्र कुशलः सर्वभूतहिते रतः। आख्यातं शरभङ्गेन गौतमेन महात्मना ॥
en: You are possessed of ability in respect of everything, and are (at the same time) engaged in the welfare of all beings, this has been told to me by the high-soulcd Sarabhanga of the Gautama race.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण महर्षिलोकविश्रुतः। अब्रबीन्मधुरं वाक्यं हर्षेण महता युतः॥
en: Thus addressed by Rāma, that Maharşi known over all the worlds, Spoke sweet words in great joy,

sa_verse: अयमेवाश्रमोः राम गुणवान् रम्यतामिति। ऋषिसङ्घांनुचरितः सदा मूलफलैर्युतः॥
en: O meritorious Rāma, even this is the asylum (for you). Do you live here pleasantly. It is inhabited by numbers of saints and is always provided with fruits and roots.

sa_verse: इममाश्रममागम्य मृगसङ्घा महीयसः। अहत्वा प्रतिगच्छन्ति लोभयित्वाकुतोभयाः॥
en: This forest is haunted by herds of deer that range here without doing harm to any one, although they possess great energy; and go away, having bewitched people (by their beauty).

sa_verse: नान्यो दोषो भवेदत्र मृगेभ्योऽन्यत्र विद्धि वै। तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य महर्षेर्लक्ष्मणाग्रजः॥ उवाच वचनं धीरो विगृह्य सशरं धनुः।
en: Save what comes, from these deer, there is no other evil to be encountered here. Hearing those words of the Maharși, the placid elder brother of Lakşınaņa, taking up his bow with the arrow set, said

sa_verse: तानहं सुमहाभाग मृगसंघान् समागतान्॥ हन्यां निशितधारेण शरेणानतपर्वणा। भवांस्तत्राभिषज्येत किं स्यात् कृच्छ्रतरं ततः॥
en: O exalted one, if by means of sharpened shafts blazing like lightning, I slay those herds of deer when they come, it shall impart pain to you, and what can be more unfortunate than this?

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नाश्रमे वासं चिरं तु न समर्थये। तमेवमुक्त्वोपरमं रामः संध्यामुपागमत् ॥ अन्वास्य पश्चिमां संध्यां तत्र वासमकल्पयत्। सुतीक्ष्णस्याश्रमे रम्ये सीतया लक्ष्मणेन व॥
en: Therefore I shall not be able to dwell long in this asylum. Having said this, Rāma ceased and became engaged in his evening adorations, and, having finished them, along with Sīta and Lakşmaņa fixed his quarters in the asylum of Sutiksna.

sa_verse: ततः शुभं तापसयोग्यमन्नं स्वयं सुतीक्ष्णः पुरुषर्षभाभ्याम्। ताभ्यां सुसत्कृत्य ददौ महात्मा संध्यानिवृत्तौ रजनीं समीक्ष्य॥
en: Then, when the evening had passed away and night fell, Sutiksna, having done homage to those chiefs of men, offered them excellent fare, suitable to ascetics.

sa_verse: रामस्तु सहसौमित्रिः सुतीक्ष्णेनाभिपूजितः। परिणाम्य निशां तत्र प्रभाते प्रत्यबुध्यत ॥
en: Having been well entertained by Sutīksna, Rāma in company with Sumitrā's son, having passed away the night there, awoke in the morning.

sa_verse: उत्थाय च यथाकालं राघवः सह सीतया। उपस्पृश्य सुशीतेन तोयेनोत्पलगन्धिना॥ अथ तेऽग्नि सुरांश्चैव वैदेही रामलक्ष्मणौ। काल्यं विधिवदभ्यर्च्य तपस्विशरणे वने॥ उदयन्तं दिनकरं दृष्ट्वा विगतकल्मषाः। सुतीक्ष्णमभिगम्येदं श्लक्ष्णं वचनमब्रुवन्॥
en: And arising in due time, Rāma along with Sītā, bathed in cool waters odorous with the aroma of lotuses; and having in proper time duly worshipped Fire as well as the deities, in that forest containing abodes of ascetics, Rāma, Lakṣmaṇa and Videha's daughter, their sins purged off, seeing the sun risen, approached Sutīksna and spoke to him these mild words.

sa_verse: सुखोषिताः स्म भगवंस्त्वया पूज्येन पूजिताः। आपृच्छामः प्रयास्यामो मुनयस्त्वरयन्ति नः॥
en: O Reverend sir, we we have stayed here pleasantly, being excellently ministered to by you. We greet you, and go away. The ascetics urge speed upon us.

sa_verse: त्वरामहे वयं द्रष्टुं कृत्स्नमाश्रममण्डलम्। ऋषीणां पुण्यशीलानां दण्डकारण्यवासिनाम्॥
en: We hasten to view the collections of asylums that belong to the holy sages inhabiting the forest of Dandaka.

sa_verse: अभ्यनुज्ञातुमिच्छामः सहैभिर्मुनिपुंगवैः। धर्मनित्यैस्तपोदान्तैर्विशिखैरिव पावकैः॥
en: Now we crave your permission along with that of these foremost of anchorites, steady in virtue, crowned with asceticism and selfcontrolled, resembling smokeless flames.

sa_verse: अविषह्यातपो यावत् सूर्यो नातिविराजते। अमार्गेणागतां लक्ष्मी प्राप्येवान्वयवर्जितः॥ तावदिच्छामहे गन्तुमित्युक्त्वा चरणौ मुनेः। ववन्दे सहसौमित्रिः सीतया सह राघवः॥
en: And we intend to set out ere the sun like to a low-sprung one that has attained to auspicious fortune through evil ways, with rays incapable of being borne, shines too fiercely.

sa_verse: तौ सोस्पृशन्तौ चरणावुत्थाप्य मुनिपुंगवः। गाढमाश्लिष्य सस्नेहमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having said this, Rāghava along with Sītā and Sumitrā's son bowed down to the ascetic's feet.

sa_verse: अरिष्टं गच्छ पन्थानं राम सौमित्रिणा सह। सीतया चानया सार्धं छाययेवानुवृत्तया॥
en: And as they touched his feet, that best of ascetics, raising Rāma and Lakşmaņa up, embraced them closely and said, O Rāma, go your way safely, in company with Sumitrā's son and this Sītā that follows you like a shadow.

sa_verse: पश्याश्रमपदं रम्यं दण्डकारण्यवासिनाम्। एषां तपस्विनां वीर तपसा भावितात्मनाम्॥
en: Behold the beauteous asylums, O hero. of these pure-spirited ascetics inhabiting the forest of Dandaka.

sa_verse: सुप्राज्यफलमूलानि पुष्पितानि वनानि च। प्रशस्तमृगयूथानि शान्तपक्षिगणानि च॥ फुल्लपङ्कजखण्डानि प्रसन्नसलिलानि च। कारण्डवविकीर्णानि तटाकानि सरांसि च॥ द्रक्ष्यसे दृष्टिरम्याणि गिरिप्रस्रवणानि च । रमणीयान्यरण्यानि मयूराभिरुतानि च॥
en: You will see blossoming woods garnished with fruits and roots, containing goodly deer, and mild feathered tribes; tanks and pools laughing with blown lotuses, containing pleasant waters, and abounding in Kārandavas; charming mountain-springs; and romantic forests picturesque with peacocks.

sa_verse: गम्यतां वत्स सौमित्रे भवानपि च गच्छतु। आगन्तव्यं च ते दृष्ट्वा पुनरेवाश्रमं प्रति ॥
en: Go, O child; and go you also, O Sumitra's son. And come again to my asylum after having seen these.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तथेत्युक्त्वा काकुत्स्थः सहलक्ष्मणः। प्रदक्षिणं मुनिं कृत्वा प्रस्थातुमुपचक्रमे॥
en: Thus addressed, Kākutstha along with Laks maņa, having gone round the ascetic, prepared for departing.

sa_verse: ततः शुभतरे तूणी धनुषी चायतेक्षणाः। ददौ सीता तयोर्धात्रोः खङ्गौ च विमलौ ततः॥
en: Then Sīlā of expansive eyes handed to the brothers excellent quivers bows and shining swords.

sa_verse: आबध्य च शुभे तूणी चापे चादाय सस्वने। निष्क्रान्तावाश्रमाद् गन्तुमुभौ तौ रामलक्ष्मणौ॥
en: Then fastening the graceful quivers, and taking the sweet-sounding bows, both Rāma and Lakşmaņa issued from the hermitage.

sa_verse: शीघ्रं तौ रूपसम्पन्नावनुज्ञातौ महर्षिणा। प्रस्थितौ धृतचापासी सीतया सह राघवौ॥
en: And permitted by the Maharşi, the Rāghavas furnished with grace, equipped with bows and scimitars, swiftly set out along with Sītā.

sa_verse: सुतीक्ष्णेनाभ्यनुज्ञातं प्रस्थितं रघुनन्दनम्। हृद्यया स्निग्धया वाचा भर्तारमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: When the son of Raghu had set out with Sutiksna's permission, SItā addressed him in affectionate words and convincing speech.

sa_verse: अधर्मं तु सुसूक्ष्मेण विधिना प्राप्यते महान्। निवृत्तेन च सक्योऽयं व्यसनात् कामजादिह॥
en: Although you are great and follow the narrow way (of righteousness), yet you are on the eve of entering into unrighteousness. But you could by refraining from action, eschew this unrighteousness that springs from an evil begot of desire.

sa_verse: त्रीण्येव व्यसनान्यद्य कामजानि भवन्त्युत। मिथ्यावाक्यं तु परमं तस्माद् गुरुतरावुभौ॥ परदाराभिगमनं विना वैरं च रौद्रता। मिथ्यावाक्यं न ते भूतं न भविष्यति राघव ॥
en: This evil begot of desire is threefold. One prominent evil is falsehood, and both the others' are of weightier significance, association with others; wives, and vindictiveness without any (basis of) hostility. Falsehood, O Rāghava, has never been yours nor can it ever be yours (in the future).

sa_verse: कुतोऽभिलषणं स्त्रीणां परेषां धर्मनाशनम्। तव नास्ति मनुष्येन्द्र न चाभूत् ते कदाचन ॥ मनस्यपि तथा राम न चैतद् विद्यते क्वचित्। स्वदारनिरतश्चैव नित्यमेव नृपात्मज ॥ धर्मिष्ठः सत्यसंधश्च पितुर्निर्देशकारकः। त्वयि धर्मश्च सत्यं च त्वयि सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्॥
en: Nor yet, O foremost of men, can you ever even in fancy be (guilty of) going after others' wives, which destroy all religious merit. These, O Rāma, are by no means in you. O King's son, you ever and a day direct your attentions to own wife. And you are Righteous and truthful and do the will of your şire. In you are established virtue and truth, and every thing; and by help of your conquered senses, you, O mighty armed one, are capable of bearing everything.

sa_verse: तच्च सर्वं महाबाहो शक्यं वोढुं जितेन्द्रियैः। तव वश्येन्द्रियत्वं च जानामि शुभदर्शन॥ तृतीयं यदिदं रौद्रं परप्राणाभिहिंसनम्। निर्वैरं क्रियते मोहात् तच्च ते समुपस्थितम्॥
en: O you of a gracious presence, yours is control over sense. The third evil that leads men through ignorance to bear hostility towards others without any (cause of) hostility, is now present (to you).

sa_verse: प्रतिज्ञातस्त्वया वीर दण्डकारण्यवासिनाम्। ऋषीणां रक्षणार्थाय वधः संयति रक्षसाम् ॥
en: You have, O hero, for the protection of the saints dwelling in the forest of Dandaka, Promised the slaughter of Rākṣasas in battle.

sa_verse: एतन्निमित्तं वचनं दण्डका इति विश्रुतम्। प्रस्थितस्त्वं सह भ्रात्रा धृतबाणशरासनः॥
en: And it is for this reason that equipped with bows and arrows, you have along with your brother set out for the forest known as Dandaka.

sa_verse: नहि मे रोचते वीर गमनं दण्डकान् प्रति । कारणं तत्र वक्ष्यामि वदन्त्याः शृयतां मम ॥
en: O hero, I do not relish this journey to Dandaka. Thereof I will tell you the reason. Do you listen to me as I tell you.

sa_verse: त्वं हि बाणधनुष्पाणिर्धात्रा सह वनं गतः। दृष्ट्वानवचरान् सर्वान् कच्चित् कुर्याः शरव्ययम्॥
en: Bearing bows and arrows in your hands, you have come to the wood along with your brother; and it may well happen) that seeing grim rangers of the forest, you may discharge your shafts.

sa_verse: क्षत्रियाणामिह धनुर्हताशस्येन्धनानि च। समीपतः स्थितं तेजोबलमुच्छुयते भृशम्॥
en: Even as the vicinity of faggots increases the energy of fire, the proximity of (the bow) enhances the strength and energy of the Ksatriya.

sa_verse: पुरा किल महाबाहो तपस्वी सत्यवाञ्छुचिः। कस्मिश्चिंदभवत् पुण्ये वने रतमृगद्विजे॥
en: Formerly, O long-armed one, in a sacred wood haunted by beasts and birds, there lived a truthful ascetic of a pure person.

sa_verse: तस्यैव तपसो विघ्नं कर्तुमिन्द्रः शचीपतिः। खङ्गपाणिरथागच्छदाश्रमं भटरूपधृक् ॥
en: Intending to disturb his austerities, Saci's lord, Indra, bearing a sword in his hand, came to the asylum in the guise of a warrior.

sa_verse: तस्मिंस्तदाश्रमपदे निहितः खङ्ग उत्तमः। स न्यासविधिना दत्तः पुण्ये तपसि तिष्ठतः॥
en: And in that asylum, that excellent scimitar was deposited as a trust with that righteous person practising asceticisin.

sa_verse: स तच्छस्त्रमनुप्राप्य न्यासरक्षणतत्परः। वने तु विचरत्येव रक्षन् प्रत्ययमात्मनः॥
en: Receiving that weapon, that ascetic intent upon preserving his trust, range the forest, maintaining his faith.

sa_verse: यत्र गच्छत्युपादातुं मूलानि च फलानि च। न विना याति तं खङ्ग न्यासरक्षणतत्परः॥
en: And intent upon preserving his trust, he goes nowhere for procuring fruits and roots without that sword deposited with him as a trust.

sa_verse: नित्यं शस्त्रं परिवहन् क्रमेण स तपोधनः। चकार रौद्रीं स्वां बुद्धिं त्यक्त्वा तपसि निश्चयम्॥
en: Constantly carrying the sword, by degrees, the ascetic, foregoing all thoughts about asceticism, had his mind involved in fierce sentiments.

sa_verse: ततः स रौद्राभिरतः प्रमत्तोऽधर्मकर्षितः। तस्य शस्त्रस्य संवासाज्जगाम नरकं मुनिः॥
en: Thus in consequence of bearing that weapon, that ascetic taken up with fierce thoughts, losing his sobriety and led astray from righteousness, went to hell.

sa_verse: एवमेतत् पुरावृत्तं शस्त्रसंयोगकारणम्। अग्निसंयोगवद्धेतुः शस्त्रसंयोग उच्यते॥
en: This ancient story anent the carrying of arms, asserts that even as fire works change in a piece of wood, the presence of arms works alteration in the mind of him bearing them.

sa_verse: स्नेहाच्च बहुमानाच्च स्मारये त्वां तु शिक्षये। न कथंचन सा कार्या गृहीतधनुषा त्वया॥ बुद्धिर्वैरं विना हन्तुं राक्षसान् दण्डकाश्रितान्। अपराधं विना हन्तुं लोको वीर न मंस्यते॥
en: From affection and the high honour in which I hold you, I merely remind you of this matter. I do not teach you. Equipped with bows as you are, you should renounce all thoughts of slaying without hostility the Rākṣasas residing in Dandaka. Without offence none should be slain.

sa_verse: क्षत्रियाणां तु वीराणां वनेषु नियतात्मनाम्। धनुषा कार्यमेतावदार्तानामभिरक्षणम्॥
en: It is the duty of Kșatriya heroes by means of their bows to protect persons of subdued souls, come by any calamity.

sa_verse: क्व च शस्त्रं क्व च वनं क्व च क्षात्रं तपः क्व च। व्याविद्धमिदमस्माभिर्देशधर्मस्तु पूज्यताम्॥
en: Where are arms? And where is the forest? Where is Kaştriya virtue? And where is asceticism? These are opposed to each other, let us, therefore, honour the morality that pertains to this place.

sa_verse: कदर्यकलुषा बुद्धिर्जायते शस्त्रसेवनात्। पुनर्गत्वा त्वयोध्यायां क्षत्रधर्मं चरिष्यसि ॥
en: From following arms, one's sense gets befouled and deformed. Again going to Ayodhyā, You will observe the duties of Kastriyas.

sa_verse: अक्षया तु भवेत् प्रीतिः श्वश्रूश्वशुरयोर्मम । यदि राज्यं हि संन्यस्य भवेस्त्वं निरतो मुनिः॥
en: Then my mother-in-law and father-in-law shall experience enduring delight, if having renounced the kingdom, you lead the life of an ascetic.

sa_verse: धर्मादर्थः प्रभवति धर्मात् प्रभवते सुखम्। धमेण लभते सर्वं धर्मसारमिदं जगत्॥
en: Interest springs from righteousness; and happiness also results therefrom. One attains everything through righteousness, in this world the only substantial thing.

sa_verse: आत्मानं नियमैस्तैस्तैः कर्षयित्वा प्रयत्नतः। प्राप्यते निपुणैर्धर्मो न सुखाल्लभते सुखम्॥
en: Repressing self by diverse restrictions, intelligent people attain righteousness; but virtue crowned with felicity, is incapable of being attained by following pleasure.

sa_verse: नित्यं शुचिमतिः सौम्य चर धर्मं तपोवने। सर्वं तु विदितं तुभ्यं त्रैलोक्यमपि तत्त्वतः॥
en: O mild one! ever cherishing your heart in purity, do you practise piety in the wood of asceticism Everything the three worlds are truly known to you.

sa_verse: स्त्रीचा पलादेतदुपाहृतं मे धर्मं च वक्तुं तव कः समर्थः। विचार्य बुद्ध्या तु सहानुजेन यद् रोचते तत् कुरु माचिरेण ॥
en: I have spoken this through feminine fickleness. Who can speak of righteousness to you? Reflecting on and understanding things, do you along with your younger brother speedily do what you like.

sa_verse: वाक्यमेतत् तु वैदेह्या व्याहृतं भर्तृभक्तया। श्रुत्वा धर्मे स्थितो रामः प्रत्युवाचाथ जानकीम्॥
en: Hearing Vaidehi's words spoken through high regard (for her husband), Rāma with his energy enhanced, answered Janaka's daughter.

sa_verse: हितमुक्तं त्वया देवि स्निग्धया सदृशं वचः। कुलं व्यपदिशन्त्या च धर्मज्ञे जनकात्मजे॥
en: O noble lady, you have spoken wild words fraught with worth and profit; and, O Janaka's daughter versed in virtue, you have expounded the duties of Kșatriyas.

sa_verse: किं नु वक्ष्याम्यहं देवि त्वयैवोक्तमिदं वचः। क्षत्रियैर्धार्यते चापो नार्तशब्दो भवेदिति॥
en: What, O exalted one, shall I say? You have yourself by your words (furnished an answer to what you had said). Kşatriyas wear bows in order that the word 'distressed' may not exist (on earth).

sa_verse: ते चार्ता दण्डकारण्ये मुनयः संशितव्रताः। मां सीते स्वयमागम्य शरण्यं शरणं गताः॥ वसन्त कालकाले वने मूलफलाशनाः। न लभन्ते सुखं भीरु राक्षसैः क्रूरकर्मभिः॥
en: O Sītā, those ascetics of severe vows that are beset with perils in Dandaka, having personally come to me, who am their refuge, have sought protection at my hands.

sa_verse: भक्ष्यन्ते राक्षसैर्भीमैनरमांसोपजीविभिः। ते भक्ष्यमाणा मुनयो दण्डकारण्यवासिनः॥ अस्मानभ्यवपद्यैते मामूचुर्द्विजसत्तमाः।
en: Always dwelling in the forest, subsisting on fruits and roots, they on account of Rākṣasas of cruel deeds do not, O timid attain ease. These ascetics are devoured by terrible Rākṣasas living on human flesh. Being eaten up (by the Rākşasas), the anchorites living in the forest of Dandaka, those best of the twice-born ones, said to us-Be you gracious to us.'

sa_verse: मया तु वचनं श्रुत्वा तेषामेवं मुखाच्च्युतम्।।७ कृत्वा वचनशुश्रूषां वाक्यमेतदुदाहृतम्।
en: Hearing those words of theirs which fell from their lips, I, resolving to act in accordance with their request, said.

sa_verse: प्रसीदन्तु भवन्तो मेऽहीरेषा तु ममातुला॥ यदीदृशैरहं विप्रैरुपस्थेयैरुपस्थितः। किं करोमीति च मया व्याहृतं द्विजसंनिधौ॥
en: Be you propitious.' This is surpassing shame to me that such Vipras who themselves are worthy of being sought, seek me. What shall I do? I said this in the presence of those twiceborn ones.

sa_verse: सर्वैरेव समागम्य वागियं समुदाहृता। राक्षसैर्दण्डकारण्ये बहुभिः कामरूपिभिः॥
en: Thereat all those that had come, said, We have been, O Rāma, immensely harassed in the forest of Dandaka by Räkşasas wearing shapes at will. Do you deliver us.

sa_verse: अर्दिताः स्म भृशं राम भवान् नस्तत्र रक्षतु। होमकाले तु सम्प्राप्ते पर्वकालेषु चानघ॥ धर्षयन्ति स्म दुर्धर्षा राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः।
en: O sinless one! These irrepressible Rākṣasas living on human flesh vanquish us at the time of Homa, and on the occasions of Parvas.

sa_verse: राक्षसैर्धर्षितानां च तापसानां तपस्विनाम्॥ गतिं मृगयमाणानां भवान् नः परमा गतिः।
en: Of the saints and ascetics afflicted by the Rākşasas, who are on the search for their refuge, you are our best refuge. sore

sa_verse: कामं तपःप्रभावेण शक्ता हन्तुं निशाचरान्॥ चिरार्जितं न चेच्छामस्तपः खण्डयितुं वयम्। बहुविघ्नं तपो नित्यं दुश्चरं चैव राघव॥
en: We can by virtue of the energy of our asceticism easily destroy the rangers of the night; but we are loath to lose our asceticism earned in a long time. O Rāghava our austerities are constantly disturbed, and we can hardly perform thein.

sa_verse: तेन शापं न मुञ्चामो भक्ष्यमाणाश्च राक्षसैः। तदर्घमानान् रक्षोभिर्दण्डकारण्यवासिभिः ॥ रक्षकस्त्वं सह भ्रात्रा त्वन्नाथा हि वयं वने।
en: Therefore, although afflicted and devoured by the Rākşasas dwelling in the forest of Dandaka, we do not cast our curse on them. You along with your brother are our protector: you are our master in this forest.

sa_verse: मया चैतद्वचः श्रुत्वा कात्स्न्येन परिपालनम्॥ ऋषीणां दण्डकारण्ये संश्रुतं जनकात्मजे।
en: Having heard these words, I promised my perfect protection to the saints in the forest of Dandaka, O daughter of Janaka.

sa_verse: संश्रुत्य न च शक्ष्यामि जीवमानः प्रतिश्रवम्॥ मुनीनामन्यथा कर्तुं सत्यमिष्टं हि मे सदा।
en: Having promised, I living cannot violate my vow concerning the ascetics; verily truth is ever dear to me.

sa_verse: अप्यहं जीवितं जह्यां त्वां वा सीते सलक्ष्मणाम्॥ न तु प्रतिज्ञां संश्रुत्य ब्राह्मणेभ्यो विशेषतः।
en: I had rather renounce my life, or you, O Sītā, along with Lakşmaņa-but by no means my promise made, especially to Brahmanas.

sa_verse: तदवश्यं मया कार्यमृषीणां परिपालनम्॥ अनुक्तेनापि वैदेहि प्रतिज्ञाय कथं पुनः।
en: O Videha's daughter, the protection of the saints is incumbent on me even without any representation, and what (shall I say) when I have promised the same?

sa_verse: मम स्नेहाच्च सौहार्दादिदमुक्तं त्वा वचः॥ परितुष्टोऽस्म्यहं सीते न ह्यनिष्टोऽनुशास्यते।
en: You have spoken this to me through affection and friendship. I have been well pleased with you, O Sītā. One did not instruct another that one did not bear affection to.

sa_verse: सदृशं चानुरूपं च कुलस्य तव शोभने। सधर्मचारिणी मे त्वं प्राणेभ्योऽपि गरीयसी॥
en: O beauteous damsel, (wat you have said) is worthy of both your love and your race. You are dearer to me even than life, being a companion in my religious acts.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा वचनं महात्मा सीतां प्रियां मैथिलराजपुत्रीम्। रामो धनुष्मान् सह लक्ष्मणेन जगाम रम्याणि तपोवनानि ॥
en: Having said these words to Mithila's princess, the, beloved Sītā, the high-souled Răma, equipped with bows, along with Laksmana, directed his steps towards the inviting woods of asceticism.

sa_verse: अग्रतः प्रययौ रामः सीता मध्ये सुशोभना। पृष्ठतस्तु धनुष्पाणिर्लक्ष्मणोऽनुजगाम ह॥
en: Rāma went first; in the middle, Sītā; and bearing a bow in his hand, Laksmana went in their wake.

sa_verse: तौ पश्यमानौ विविधाशैलप्रस्थान बनानि च। नदीश्च विविधा रम्या जग्मतुः सह सीतया॥ सारसांश्चक्रवाकांश्च नदीपुलिनचारिणः। सरांसि च सपद्मानि युतानि जलजैः खगैः॥ यूथबन्धाश्च पृषतान् मदोन्मत्तान् विषाणिनः। महिषांश्च वराहांश्च गजांश्च द्रुमवैरिणः।४।।
en: And they went with Sītā, seeing many imountain-peaks, and forests, and delightful streams, and Cakravākas haunting river islets, and tanks with lotuses containing aquatic birds, and herds of deer, and horned buffalo's maddened with juices boars, and elephants, foes to trees.

sa_verse: ते गत्वा दूरमध्वानं लम्बमाने दिवाकरे। ददृशुः सहिता रम्यं तटाकं योजनायुतम्॥ पद्मपुष्करसम्बाधं गजयूथैरलंकृतन्। सारसैहँसकादम्बैः संकुलं जलजातिभिः॥
en: Having proceeded a long way, when the sun was aslant, they together saw a delightful tank measuring a Yojana, filled with red and pale Swans lotuses, graced with herds of elephants, and abounding in aquatic cranes, and Kadambas.

sa_verse: प्रसन्नसलिले रम्ये तस्मिन् सरसि शुश्रुवे।। गीतवादिनिर्घोषो न तु कश्चन दृश्यते॥
en: And in that tank containing charming and pleasant waters, they heard sounds of song and instrumental music; but no one was seen there.

sa_verse: ततः कौतूहलाद् रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महारथः। मुनिं धर्मभृतं नाम प्रष्टुं समुपचक्रमे ॥
en: There at, from curiosity, Rāma and Lakşmana, mighty car warrior, asked an ascetic named Dharmabhrt.

sa_verse: इदमत्यद्भुतं श्रुत्वा सर्वेषां नो महामुने। कौतूहलं महज्जातं किमिदं साधु कथ्यताम्॥
en: Hearing this mighty wonder, we have been worked up with intense curiosity. Do you tell us what this is.

sa_verse: तेनैवमुक्तो धर्मात्मा राघवेण मुनिस्तदा। प्रभावं सरसः क्षिप्रमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे॥
en: Thus accosted by Rāghava, the ascetic then at once began to expatiate over the potency of the pool.

sa_verse: इदं पञ्चाप्सरो नाम तटाकं सार्वकालिकम्। निर्मितं तपसा राम मुनिना माण्डकर्णिना॥
en: This tank goes by the name of Pancāpsara, and is always filled with water. It was made, O Rāma, by the ascetic Măndakarni, by virtue of his asceticism.

sa_verse: स हि तेपे तपस्तीव्र माण्डकर्णिमहामुनिः। दशवर्षसहस्राणि वायुभक्षो जलाशये॥
en: In yonder tank, the mighty ascetic, living on air, performed signal austerities for ten thousand years.

sa_verse: ततः प्रव्यथिताः सर्वे देवाः साग्निपुरोगमाः। अब्रटन् वचनं सर्वे परस्परसमागताः॥ अस्माकं कस्यचित् स्थानमेष प्रार्थयते मुनिः। इति संविग्नमनसः सर्वे तत्र दिवौकसः॥
en: Thereat, exceedingly agitated, all the deities with Agni at their head assembled together, said, This ascetic wishes to have the position of one of us.' Thus all the deities present there were filled with anxiety.

sa_verse: ततः कर्तुं तपोविघ्नं सर्वदेवैर्नियोजिताः। प्रधानाप्सरसः पञ्च विद्युच्चलितवर्चसः॥
en: Then with the view of disturbing his austerities, the deities ordered five principal Apsarās, possessed of the splendour of live lighting.

sa_verse: अप्सरोभिस्ततस्ताभिर्मुनिर्दृष्टपरावरः। नीतो मदनवश्यत्वं देवानां कार्यसिद्धये॥
en: And for compassing the end of the celestials, that ascetic conversant with the morality and otherwise as well of this life as that to come, was brought by those Apsaras (nymphs) under the sway of Madana.

sa_verse: ताश्चैवाप्सरसः पञ्च मुनेः पत्नीत्वमागताः। तटाके निर्मितं तासां तस्मिन्नन्तर्हितं गृहम् ॥
en: And those five nymphs (ultimately) became the wives of the ascetic; and their hidden residence was reared in the pool.

sa_verse: तत्रैवाप्सरसः पञ्च निवसन्त्यो यथासुखम्। रमयन्ति तपोयोगान्मुनिं यौवनमास्थितम्॥
en: And there the five nymphs living happily, pleased the anchorite, established in youth through asceticism and yoga.

sa_verse: तासां संक्रीडमानानामेष वादिनि:स्वनः। श्रूयते भूषणोन्मिश्रो गीतशब्दो मनोहरः॥
en: As your sport, we hear the sounds of their musical instruments, and the sweet voice of their song mixed with the tinkling of their instruments.

sa_verse: आश्चर्यमिति तस्यैतद् वचनं भावितात्मनः। राघवः प्रतिजग्राह सह भ्रात्रा महायशाः॥
en: Hearing this), the illustrious Rāghava along with his brother declared the story narrated by that one of a pure heart to be wonderful.

sa_verse: एवं कथयमानः स ददर्शाश्रममण्डलम्। कुशचीरपरिक्षिप्तं ब्राह्मया लक्ष्म्या समावृतम्॥
en: Thus conversing, Rāma saw the collection of asylums, strewn with Kusa and bark, and be-girt with energy derived from Brahmā lore.

sa_verse: प्रविश्य सह वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणेन च राघवः। तदा तस्मिन् स काकुत्स्थः श्रीमत्याश्रममण्डले॥ । उषित्वा स सुखं तत्र पूज्यमानो महर्षिभिः।
en: Entering (the place) along with Vaidehi and Lakşmaņa, the highly famous Rāghava dwelt there respected by all the ascetics.
