sa_verse: जगाम चाश्रमांस्तेषां पर्यायेण तपस्विनाम्॥ येषामुषितवान् पूर्वं सकाशे स महास्त्रवित्।
en: Having happily dwelt in those collections of graceful asylums, honoured of the Maharsis, Kākutstha by turns went to the hermitages of those ascetics with whom that one well versed in arms had dwelt before.

sa_verse: क्वचित् परिदशान् मासानेकसंवत्सरं क्वचित्॥ क्वचित चतुरो मासान् पञ्च षट् च परान् क्वचित। अपरत्राधिकान् मासानध्यर्धमधिकं क्वचित्॥ त्रीन्मासानष्टमासांश्च राघवो न्यवसत् सुखम्।
en: And Rāghava happily passed his days somewhere for ten months, somewhere for one year, somewhere for four months somewhere for five or six months, somewhere for many months, somewhere for a month and a half, somewhere for more, somewhere for three months, and somewhere for eight.

sa_verse: तत्र संवसतस्तस्य मुनीनामाश्रमेषु वै॥ रमतश्चानुकूल्येन ययुः संवत्सरा दश।
en: And as Rāma lived in the asylums of the ascetics and amused himself through their good graces, ten years were passed away (in this way).

sa_verse: परिसृत्य च धर्मज्ञो राघवः सह सीतया॥ सुतीक्ष्णस्याश्रमपदं पुनरेवाजगाम ह।
en: Having gone round the asylums of all the ascetics, Rāghava cognizant of righteousness returned to the hermitage of Sutīkşna.

sa_verse: स तमाश्रममागम्य मुनिभिः परिपूजितः॥ तत्रापि न्यवसद् रामः किंचित् कालमरिन्दमः।
en: Coming to this asylum, respected by the ascetics, that subduer of enemies, Rāma, stayed there for a time.

sa_verse: अथाश्रमस्थो विनयात् कदाचित् तं महामुनिम्॥ उपासीनः स काकुत्स्थः सुतीक्ष्णमिदमब्रवीत्।
en: Once upon a time, as Kākutstha dwelling in that asylum was seated, he humbly observed to that great ascetic.

sa_verse: अस्मिन्नरण्ये भगवन्नगस्त्यो मुनिसत्तमः॥ वसतीति मया नित्यं कथाः कथयतां श्रुतम्। न तु जानामि तं देशं वनस्यास्य महत्तया॥
en: I have always heard from men speaking on the subject that that foremost of ascetics, the reverend Agastya, lives in this forest. On account of the vastness of this forest, I do not know that place.

sa_verse: कुत्राश्रमपदं रम्यं महर्षेस्तस्य धीमतः। प्रसदार्थं भगवतः सानुजः सह सीतया॥ अगस्त्यमधिगच्छेयमभिवादयितुं मुनिम्। मनोरथो महानेष हृदि संपरिवर्तते ॥
en: Where is the hermitage of that intelligent Maharsi? For propitiating that revered one, I, accompanied by my brother and Sītā, will go to Agastya for paying our respects to the ascetic.

sa_verse: यदहं तं मुनिवरं सुश्रूषेयमपि स्वयम्। इति रामस्य स मुनिः श्रुत्वा धर्मात्मनो वचः॥ सुतीक्ष्णः प्रत्युवाचेदं प्रीतो दशरथात्मजम्।
en: This great desire is burning in my heart, that I should myself minister to that best of anchorets. Hearing these words of the righteous-souled Rama, Sutiksna, well pleased, answered Dasaratha's son.

sa_verse: अहमप्येतदेव त्वां वक्तुकामः सलक्ष्मणम्॥ अगस्त्यमभिगच्छेति सीतया सह राघव। दिष्टया त्विदानीमर्थेऽस्मिन् स्वयमेव ब्रवीषि माम्।।३६ ।
en: I also am desirous of telling you this along with Lakşmaņa. Repair to Agastya in company with Sītā, O Rāghava. By luck you have yourself said this to me as to your purpose.

sa_verse: अयमाख्यामि ते राम यत्रागस्त्यो महामुनिः। योजनान्याश्रमात् तात याहि चत्वारि वै ततः। दक्षिणेन महाञ्छ्रीमानगस्त्यभ्रातुराश्रमः॥ स्थलीप्रायवनोद्देशे पिप्पलीवनशोभिते। बहुपुष्पफले रम्ये नानाविहगनादिते॥ पद्मिन्यो विविधास्तत्र प्रसन्नसलिलाशयाः। हंसकारण्डवाकीर्णाश्चक्रवाकोपशोभिताः॥
en: I will, O Rāma, tell you where that mighty ascetic, Agastya, is. My child, go four miles from the hermitage in a southerly direction; and you will come to the hermitage of the brother of the auspicious Agastya situated on a land covered with trees, graced with Pippali woods, abounding in fruits and flowers, charming, and resonant with the notes of various birds. There are many tanks containing delightful waters, swarming with swans and Kārandavas, and beauteous with Cakravākas.

sa_verse: तत्रैकां रजनी व्युष्य प्रभाते राम गम्यताम्। दक्षिणां दिशमास्थाय वनखण्डस्य पार्श्वतः॥ तत्रागस्त्याश्रमपदं गत्वा योजनमन्तरम्। रमणीये वनोद्देशे बहुपादपशोभिते॥
en: Passing a night there, do you, O Rāma, in the morning, taking a southerly course, go by the skirts of the forest tract. Passing a Yojana, you
shall come upon Agastya's asylum, located at a charming woodland graced with many a tree.

sa_verse: रंस्यते तत्र वैदेही लक्ष्मणश्च त्वया सह। स हि रम्यो वनोद्देशो बहुपादपसंयुतः॥
en: There Videha's daughter and Lakşınana shall experience delight in your company.

sa_verse: यदि बुद्धिः कृता द्रष्टुमगस्त्यं तं महामुनिम्। अद्यैव गमने बुद्धिं रोचयस्व महामते।॥
en: O magnanimous one, if you intend to see the mighty ascetic, Agastya, in that charming woodland, containing a great many trees, then do you make up your mind to set out this very day.

sa_verse: इति रामो मुनेः श्रुत्वा सह भ्रात्राऽभिवाद्य च। प्रतस्थेऽगस्त्यमुद्दिश्य सानुगः सह सीतया॥
en: Hearing these words of the anchorite, Rāma, saluting him, along with his brother, set off for Agastya's (place) with his younger brother and Sītā.

sa_verse: पश्यन् वनानि चित्राणि पर्वतांश्चाभ्रसंनिभान्। सरांसि सरितश्चैव पथि मार्गवशानुगान्॥ . सुतीक्ष्णेनोपदिष्टेन गत्वा तेन पथा सुखम्। इदं परमसंहष्टो वाक्यं लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥
en: And, pleasantly proceeding by the way dire ted by Sutiksna, seeing picturesque woods, hills resembling clouds, watery expanses and streams flowing by their path; Rāma filled with delight said these words to Lakşmana,

sa_verse: एतदेवाश्रमपदं नूनं तस्य महात्मनः। अगस्त्यस्य मुनेतुर्दृश्यते पुण्यकर्मणः॥
en: Surely we see the asylumn of that high-souled one, the ascetic, Agastya's brother of pious acts.

sa_verse: यथा हीमे वनस्यास्य ज्ञाताः पथि सहस्रशः। संनताः फलभारेण पुष्पभारेण च द्रुमाः॥
en: These trees standing by thousands on the way bending beneath the weight of fruits and flowers, bear the signs that had been mentioned to me as belonging to this wood.

sa_verse: पिप्पलीनां च पक्वानां वनादम्मादुपागतः। गन्धोऽयं पवनोत्क्षिप्तः सहसा कटुकोदयः॥
en: And coming from the wood this scent is wafted by the wind the pungent odour of ripe Pippalis.

sa_verse: तत्र तत्र च दृस्यन्ते संक्षिप्ता: काष्ठसंचयाः। लूनाश्च परिदृश्यन्ते दर्भा वैडूर्यवर्चसः॥
en: And here and there are found heaps of firewood, and torn Darbha are seen, of the lustre of lapses.

sa_verse: एतञ्च वनमध्यस्थं कृष्णाभ्रशिखरोपमम्। पावकस्याश्रमस्थस्य धूमाग्रं संप्रदृश्यते॥
en: And the top of the column of smoke belonging to the fire lit in the asylum in this wood, appears like the peak of a dark mountain.

sa_verse: विविक्तेषु च तीर्थेषु कृतस्नाना द्विजातयः। पुष्योपहारं कुर्वन्ति कुसुमैः स्वयमर्जितैः॥
en: And twice-born ones, having performed their ablutions in sacred and retired bathing places, are offering flowers gathered by themselves.

sa_verse: ततः सुतीक्ष्णवचनं यथा सौम्य मया श्रुतम्। अगस्त्याश्रमो भ्रातुनमेष भविष्यति॥
en: O placid one, from what I had heard from Sutiksna, this would appear to be the asylum of Agastya's brother.

sa_verse: निगृह्य तरसा मृत्युं लोकानां हितकाम्यया। यस्य भ्रात्रा कृतेयं दिक्शरण्या पुण्यकर्मणा॥
en: The righteous Agastya it is who, wishing for the welfare of the worlds, destroying by virtue of his austerities a Daitya resembling Death, has rendered this quarter habitable.

sa_verse: इहैकदा किल कुरो वातापिरपि चेल्वलः। भ्रातरौ सहितावास्तां ब्राह्मणघ्नौ महासुरौ॥
en: Once on a time here dwelt together two mighty Asuras, brothers, given to slaughtering Brāhmanas, the wily Vātāpi and Ilvala.

sa_verse: धारयन् ब्राह्मणं रूपमिल्वलः संस्कृतं वदन्। आमन्त्रयति विप्रान् स भाद्धमुद्दिश्य निघृणः॥ भ्रातरं संस्कृतं कृत्वा ततस्तं मेषरूपिणम्। तान् द्विजान् भोजयामास श्राद्धदृष्टेन कर्मणा ॥
en: Wearing the form of a Brāhmana, and speaking Sanskrit, the cruel one used to invite Vipras to a Śrāddha. And, cooking his brother wearing the shape of a sheep, he used to feed the twice-born ones according to the rites prescribed for Śrāddhas.

sa_verse: ततो भुक्तवतां तेषां विप्राणामिल्वलोऽब्रवीत्। वातापे निष्क्रमस्वेति स्वरेण महता वदन्॥
en: Then when the Vipras had fed, Ilvala said'uttering a loud sound, O Vātāpi; come out.'

sa_verse: ततो भ्रातुर्वचः श्रुत्वा वातापिर्मेषवन्नदन्। भित्त्वा भित्त्वा शरीराणि ब्राह्मणानां विनिष्पतत्।
en: Hearing his brother's words, Vātāpi, bleating like a sheep, came out, riving their bodies.

sa_verse: ब्राह्मणानां सहस्राणि तैरेवं कामरूपिभिः। विनाशितानि संहत्य नित्यशः पिशिताशनैः॥
en: In this way, thousands of Brāhmaṇas gathered together, were destroyed by flesh eating ones wearing shapes at will.

sa_verse: अगस्त्येन तदा देवैः प्रार्थितेन महर्षिणा। अनुभूय किल श्राद्धे भक्षितः स महासुरः॥
en: And it came to pass that once upon a time) the Maharși Agastya, having been invited to a Sraddha, fed on the mighty Asura.

sa_verse: ततः सम्पन्नमित्युक्त्वा दत्त्वा हस्तेऽवनेजनम्। भ्रातरं निष्क्रमस्वेति इल्वलः समभाषत ॥
en: Thereupon uttering Finished' and offering water to wash hands with, Ilvala said to his brother, 'Come out'!

sa_verse: स तदा भाषमाणं तु भ्रातरं विप्रघातिनम्। अब्रवीत् प्रहसन् धीमानगस्त्यो मुनिसत्तमः॥
en: And, as that brother of Vātãpi, given to slaughtering Vipras was speaking thus, that foremost of ascetics, the intelligent Agastya, said with a laugh,

sa_verse: कुतो निष्क्रमितुं शक्तिर्मया जीर्णस्य रक्षसः। भ्रातुस्तु मेषरूपस्य गतस्य यमसादनम्॥
en: Where is the power of coming out, of the Rākṣasa, your brother wearing the shape of a sheep, who has gone to Yama's abode?'

sa_verse: अथ तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा भ्रातुर्निधनसंश्रितम्। प्रधर्षयितुमारेभे मुनि क्रोधान्निशाचरः॥
en: Hearing his words, from wrath the ranger of the night prepared to assail the ascetic, and he rushed against that foremost of the twice-born ones.

sa_verse: सोऽभ्यद्रवद् द्विजेन्द्रं तं मुनिना दीप्ततेजसा। चक्षुषाऽनलकल्पेन निर्दग्धो निधनं गतः॥
en: And, being consumed by that ascetic of flaming energy with his eyes resembling fire, the Rākşasa met his end.

sa_verse: तस्यायमाश्रमो भ्रातुस्तटाकवनशोभितः। विप्रानुकम्पया येन कर्मेदं दुष्करं कृतम्॥
en: This asylum graced with pools and groves belongs to the brother of him who has performed this arduous feat from compassion for the Vipras'.

sa_verse: एवं कथयमानस्य तस्य सौमित्रिणा सह। रामस्यास्तं गतः सूर्यः संध्याकालोऽभ्यवर्तत॥
en: As Rāma was thus conversing with Sumitra's son, the sun set and evening approached.

sa_verse: उपास्य पश्चिमां संध्यां सह भ्रात्रा यथाविधि। प्रविवेशाश्रमपदं तमृषिं चाभ्यवादयत्॥
en: Then, duly performing his afternoon adorations along with his brother, Rāma entered the hermitage, and saluted the ascetic.

sa_verse: सम्यकप्रतिगृहीतस्तु मुनिना तेन राघवः। न्यवसत् तां निशामेकां प्रास्य मूलफलानि च॥
en: Well received by the ascetic, Rāghava spent there a night, eating fruits and roots.

sa_verse: तस्यां रात्र्यां व्यतीतायामुदिते रविमण्डले। भ्रातरं तमगस्त्यस्य आमन्त्रयत राघवः। ७१ ।।
en: When the night had passed away, and solar disc arose, Rāghava greeted Agastya's brother.

sa_verse: अभिवादये त्वां भगवन् सुखमस्म्युषितो निशाम्। आमन्त्रये त्वां गच्छामि गुरुं ते द्रष्टुमग्रजम् ॥
en: O reverend Sir, I salute you. I have pleasantly passed the night. I greet you; I shall go to behold my preceptor, your elder brother.

sa_verse: गम्यतामिति तेनोक्तो जगाम रघुनन्दनः। यथोद्दिष्टेन मार्गेण वनं तच्चावलोकयन्॥
en: Thereat on the ascetic's saying, 'Go you,' the descendant of Raghu went away by the prescribed route.

sa_verse: नीवारान् पनसान् सालान् वञ्जुलांस्तिनिशांस्तथा। चिरिबिल्वान् मधूकांश्च बिल्वानथ च तिन्दुकान्॥ पुष्पितान् पुष्पिताग्राभिलताभिरुपशोभितान्। ददर्श रामः शतशस्तत्र कान्तारपादपान्॥ हस्तिहस्तैर्विमृदितान् वानरैरूपसोभितान्। मत्तैः शकुनिसबैश्च शतशः प्रतिनादितान्॥
en: And Rāma viewed the forest, and Nivāra, and Panasa, and Sal, Vanjula and Tinisa, and Ciribilva, and Madhuka. And Bilva, and Tinduka, all in full flower, and graced with blossoming creepers, and trees in the wood by hundreds, roughly handled by elephants with their trunks, and graced by monkeys, and resounding with the voices of an hundred maddened warblers.

sa_verse: ततोऽब्रवीत् समीपस्थं रामो राजीवलोचनः। पृष्ठतोऽनुगतं वीरं लक्ष्मणं लक्ष्मिवर्धनम्॥
en: Then the lotus-eyed Rāma said to that enhancer of auspiciousness, the heroic Laks maņa, who was by him, and was following him at his back,

sa_verse: स्निग्धपत्रा यथा वृक्षा यथा क्षान्ता मृगद्विजाः। आश्रमो नातिदूरस्थो महर्षेर्भावितात्मनः॥
en: The leaves of these trees are glossy, and the beasts and birds are mild, even as (we had been told). This asylum of the pure-hearted Maharsi must not be far.

sa_verse: अगस्त्य इति विख्यातो लोके स्वेनैव कर्मणा। आश्रमो दृश्यते तस्य परिश्रान्तश्रमापहः॥ प्राज्यधूमाकुलवनश्चीरमालापरिष्कृतः। प्रशान्तमृगयूथश्च नानाशकुनिनादितः॥
en: This asylum capable of removing the fatigue of the weary, belonging to him that is known among men as Agastya by his own acts, is seen, with the (neighbouring woods) filled with smoke, and itself decorated with bark and wreaths, containing herds of mild deer, and ringing with the notes of various birds.

sa_verse: निगृह्य तरसा मृत्युं लोकानां हितकाम्यया। दक्षिणा दिक् कृता येन शरण्या पुण्यकर्मणा ॥ तस्येदमाश्रमपदं प्रभावाद् यस्य राक्षसैः। दिगियं दक्षिणा त्रासाद् दृश्यते नोपभुज्यते॥
en: This is the asylum of that pious one, who destroying (the Asura resembling) Death, has, desirous of the welfare of mankind, rendered the Southern quarter habitable, and through whose potency the Rākşasas from fear barely cast their eyes in this direction, but do not approach.

sa_verse: यदाप्रभृति चाक्रान्ता दिगियं पुण्यकर्मणा। तदाप्रभृति निर्वैराः प्रशान्ता रजनीचराः॥
en: Ever since that one of pious ways possessed himself of this quarter, the rangers of the night have foregone their hostility, and assumed a peaceful attitude.

sa_verse: नाम्ना चेयं भगवतो दक्षिणा दिक्प्रदक्षिणा। प्रथिता त्रिषु लोकेषु दुर्धर्षा क्रुरकर्मभिः॥
en: This Southern quarter rendered safe (by Agastya), and incapable of being harassed by those ones of tortuous ways, is created over the three worlds in connection with the name of the reverend ascetic.

sa_verse: मार्ग निरोद्धं सततं भास्करस्याचलोत्तमः। संदेसं पालयंस्तस्य विन्ध्यशैलो नि वर्धते ॥ अयं दीर्घायुषस्तस्य लोके विश्रुतकर्मणः। अगस्त्यस्याश्रमः श्रीमान् विनीतमृगसेवितः॥
en: This graceful asylum ranged by mild beasts belongs to that long-lived one of renowned achievements, Agastya, in obedience to whose command, the Vindhya mountain-foremost of its kind, which had always obstructed the way of the Sun, does not increase.

sa_verse: एष लोकार्चितः साधुर्हिते नित्यं रतः सताम्। अस्मानधिगतानेष श्रेयसा योजयिष्यति॥
en: This pious one honoured of men, ever engaged in the welfare of the righteous, shall do good to us, who have come to him.

sa_verse: आराधयिष्याम्यत्राहमगस्त्यं तं महामुनिम्। शेषं च वनवासस्य सौम्य वत्स्याम्यहं प्रभो।।८८।
en: I shall adore the mighty ascetic, Agastya, and, O mild one, O master, here pass away the remainder of the term of my banishment.

sa_verse: अत्र देवाः सगन्धर्वाः सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः। अगस्त्यं नियताहाराः सततं पर्युपासते ॥
en: Here celestials with the Gandharvas, and Siddhas and eminent saints, observing restrictions in respect of food, aye adore Agastya.

sa_verse: नात्र जीवेन्मृषावादी क्रुरो वा यदि वा शठः। नृशंसः पापवृत्तो वा मुनिरेष तथाविधः॥
en: And the ascetic is such that a liar cannot live here, nor a cunning or a crafty person, nor a wicked wight, nor one that is given to unrighteousness.

sa_verse: अत्र देवाश्च यक्षाश्च नागाश्च पतगैः सह। वसन्ति नियताहारा धर्ममाराधयिष्णवः॥
en: And adoring righteousness, celestials, and Yaksas, and Nagas, and birds, live here restricting their fare.

sa_verse: अत्र सिद्धा महात्मानो विमानैः सूर्यसंनिभैः। त्यक्त्वा देहान् नवैर्देहै: स्वर्याताः परमर्षयः॥
en: And high-souled Siddhas and eminent saints, renouncing their bodies, repair to the celestial regions in cars resembling the sun.

sa_verse: यक्षत्वममरत्वं च राज्यानि विविधानि च। अत्र देवाः प्रयच्छन्ति भूतैराराधिताः शुभैः॥
en: And adored by auspicious individuals, the deities here confer on them the states of Yakşas and celestials, and divers kingdoms.

sa_verse: आगताः स्माश्रमपदं सौमित्रे प्रविशाग्रतः। निवेदयेह मां. प्राप्तमृषये सह सीतया॥
en: Sumitra's son, entering the asylum before us, do you announce to the saints that I along with Sītā, have arrived here.

sa_verse: स प्रविश्याश्रमपदं लक्ष्मणो राघवानुजः। अगस्त्यशिष्यमासाद्य वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह॥
en: Having entered the asylum, Räghava's younger brother, Lakşmana, coming to a disciple of Agastya, spoke to him.

sa_verse: राजा दशरथो नाम ज्येष्ठस्तस्य सुतो बली। रामः प्राप्तो मुनिं द्रष्टुं भार्यया सह सीतया ॥ लक्ष्मणो नाम तस्याहं भ्राता त्ववरजो हितः। अनुकूलश्च भक्तश्च यदि ते श्रोत्रमागतः॥
en: There was a king, named Dasaratha. His eldest son, the strong Rāma, has come (to this asylum) along with his wife, Sītā, for seeing the ascetic. Named Lakşmaņa, I am his younger brother, obedient and devoted to him. you may have heard of it.

sa_verse: ते वयं वनमत्युग्रं प्रविष्टाः पिदृशासनात्। द्रष्टुमिच्छामहे सर्वे भगवन्तं निवेद्यताम्॥
en: Having entered this horrid forest, in consonance with the mandate of our sire, we desire to see the reverend one. Tell this to him.

sa_verse: तस्य तद् वचनं श्रुत्वा लक्ष्मणस्य तपोधनः। तथेत्युक्त्वाऽग्निशरणं प्रविवेश निवेदितुम्॥
en: Hearing Laksmana's words, that ascetic, saying, “So be it," entered the chamber of the sacrificial fire, for the purpose of communicating (the news to Agastya).

sa_verse: स प्रविश्य मुनिश्रेष्ठं तमसा दुषप्रधर्षणम्। कृताञ्जलिरुवाचेदं रामागमनमञ्जसा॥ यथोक्तं लक्ष्मणेनैव शिष्योऽगस्त्यस्य सम्मतः।
en: Entering in, Agastya's beloved disciple, with joined hands communicated to that foremost of ascetics, incapable of being repressed*, exactly what Laksmana had told him, *Some texts:-Effulgent.

sa_verse: ततः शिष्यादुपश्रुत्य प्राप्तं रामं सलक्ष्मणम्॥ वैदेहीं च महाभागामिदं वचनमब्रवीत्।
en: Hearing from his disciple that Rāma had come along with Lakşmaņa and the highly virtuous Vaidehi, Agastya said,

sa_verse: दिष्ट्या रामश्चिरस्याद्य द्रष्टुं मां समुपागतः॥ मनसा काङ्कितं ह्यस्य मयाप्यागमनं प्रति। गम्यतां सत्कृतो रामः सभार्यः सहलक्ष्मणः॥
en: By luck it is that after a long time, Rāma has come to see me. I had mentally wished for his arrival. Go you; and let Rāma, having been respectfully received, come before me. Why have you not brought himself?

sa_verse: प्रवेस्यतां समीपं मे किमसौ न प्रवेशितः। एवमुक्तस्तु मुनिना धर्मज्ञेन महात्मना॥ अभिवाद्याब्रवीच्छिष्यस्तथेति नियताञ्जलिः।
en: Thus addressed by the high-souled and righteous ascetic, the disciple saluting him, with joined hands, said, So be it.

sa_verse: तदा निष्क्रम्य सम्भ्रान्तः शिष्यो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥ कोऽसौ रामो मुनिं द्रष्टुमेतु प्रविशतु स्वयम्।
en: Then issuing out, the disciple said to Laks mana, Where is Rama? Let him come and enter in.

sa_verse: ततो गत्वाऽऽश्रमपदं शिष्येण सह लक्ष्मणः ॥ दर्शयामासकाकुत्स्थं सीतां च जनकात्मजाम् ।
en: Threat, going to the asylum in company with the disciple (of Agastya), Laksmana showed to him Kākutstha and the daughter of Janaka, Sītā.

sa_verse: तं शिष्यः प्रश्रितं वाक्यमगस्त्यवचनं ब्रुवन्॥ प्रावेशयद् यथान्यायं सत्काराहँ सुसत्कृतम्।
en: Then joyfully communicating to Rāma the words of the reverend one, the disciple (of Agastya) duly took in that one worthy of being honoured.

sa_verse: प्रविवेश ततो राम: सीतया सह लक्ष्मणः॥ प्रशान्तहरिणाकीर्णमाश्रमं ावलोकयन्। स तत्र ब्रह्मणः स्थानमग्नेः स्थानं तथैव च॥
en: And seeing the asylum teeming with mild deer, Rama entered in with Laksmana and Sita. And there Rāma beheld the place of Brahmä, and that of Agni,

sa_verse: विष्णो: स्थानं महेन्द्रस्य स्थानं चैव विवस्वतः। सोमस्थानं भगस्थानं स्थानं कौबेरमेव च॥ धातुर्विधातुः स्थानं च वायोः स्थानं तथैव च। स्थानं च पाशहस्तस्य वरुणस्य महात्मनः॥ स्थानं तथैव गायत्र्या वसूनां स्थानमेव च। स्थानं च नागराजस्य गरुडस्थानमेव च॥ कार्तिकेयस्य च स्थानं धर्मस्थानं च पश्यति।
en: That of Vişnu, and that of the great Indra, the place of Vivasvat, and that of Soma, and that of Bhaga, and that of Dhātā and Vidhătā, and that of Vayu, and that of the high-souled Varuna having the noose in his hand, and that of Gāyatri and that of the Vasus, and that of the monarch of the Nāgas, and that of Garuda, and that of Kārtikeya, and that of Dharma.

sa_verse: ततः शिष्यैः परिवृतौ मुनिरष्यभिनिषपतत्॥ तं ददर्शाग्रतो रामो मुनीनां दीप्ततेजसम्। अब्रवीद् वचनं वीरो लक्ष्मणं लक्ष्मिवर्धनम्॥
en: And it came to pass that, surrounded by his disciples, the ascetic came (before Rāma). And Rāma saw that one of flaming energy at the head of the ascetics, and the hero said to Lakşınana, enhancer of auspiciousness.

sa_verse: बहिर्लक्ष्मण निष्क्रामत्यगस्त्यो भगवानृषिः। औदार्येणावगच्छामि निधानं तपसामिमम्॥
en: O Laksmana, the revered saint, Agastya, is coming out. I recognize that mass of asceticism by a certain kind of majesty (that characterises him).

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महाबाहुरगस्त्यं सूर्यवर्चसम्। जग्राहापततस्तस्य पादौ च रघुनन्दनः॥
en: Having said this touching Agastya of the splendour of the sun, that son of Raghu took hold of his feet.

sa_verse: अभिवाद्य तु धर्मात्मा तस्थौ रामः कृताञ्जलिः। सीतया सह वैदेहा तदा रामः सलक्ष्मणः॥
en: Then, having paid him homage, Rama with joined hands stood there in company with Videha's daughter, Sītā, and Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: प्रतिगृह्य च काकुत्स्थमर्चयित्वाऽऽसनोदकैः। कुशलप्रश्नमुक्त्वा च आसयतामिति सोऽब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereat, embracing Rāma and honouring him with water and a seat, and asking him questions anent his welfare, the saint said, Welcome!

sa_verse: राजा सर्वस्य लोकस्य धर्मचारी महारथः। पूजनीयश्च मान्यश्च भवान् प्राप्तः प्रियातिथिः॥
en: The sovereign of all the worlds, righteous, a mighty car warrior, worthy of being honoured and worshipped, you have become our beloved guest.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा फलैर्मूलैः पुष्पैश्चान्यैश्च राघवम्। पूजयित्वा यथाकामं ततोऽगस्त्यस्तमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having said this, Agastya, according to his desire, worshipping Rāghava with fruits, roots and flowers, said to him,

sa_verse: इदं दिव्यं महच्चापं हेमवज्रविभूषितम्। वैष्णवं पुरुषव्याघ्र निर्मितं विश्वकर्मणा॥ अमोघः सूर्यसंकाशो ब्रह्मदत्तः शरोत्तमः। दत्तौ मम महेन्द्रेण तूणी चाक्षय्यसायकौ॥ सम्पूर्णौ निशितैर्बाणैवलद्भिरिव पावकैः। महाराजतकोशोऽयमसिहेमविभूषितः॥
en: O foremost of men, this mighty, celestial bow belonging to Vişnu, and constructed by Visvakarma, and this best of arrows (named) Brahmadatta, infallible and resembling the sun and this inexhaustible couple of quivers filled with sharpened shafts, like to flaming fire, were granted to me by the mighly Indra. And here is this mighty golden scabbard, and this sword decked in gold.

sa_verse: अनेन धनुषा राम हत्वा संख्ये महासुरान्। आजहार श्रियं दीप्तां पुरा विष्णुर्दिवौकसाम् ॥ तद्धनुस्तौ च तूणी च शरं खङ्गं च मानद। जयाय प्रतिगृह्णष्व वज्रं वज्रधरो यथा॥
en: Having, O Rama, slain the mighty Asuras with this bow, Vişņu in days of yorc in battle secured the effulgent Fortune of the celestials. O bestower of honour, do you for securing victory, take this bow, these quivers, this arrow, and this scimitar, like the holder of the thunderbolt, taking the same.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महातेजाः समस्तं तद्वरायुधम्। दत्त्वा रामाय भगवानगस्त्यः पुनरब्रवीत् ॥
en: Having said this, that highly energetic one, the reverend Agastya, consigning to Rāma all those weapon, again said. SCCTION 13

sa_verse: राम प्रीतोऽस्मि भद्रं ते परितुष्टोऽस्मि लक्ष्मण। अभिवादयितुं यन्मां प्राप्तौ स्थः सह सीतया॥ अध्वश्रमेण वां खेदो बाधते प्रचुरश्रमः। व्यक्तमुत्कण्ठते वापि मैथिली जनकात्मजा ॥
en: O Rāma, I am pleased with you; good betide you! And, O Laksmana, I am gratified by you. You are in trouble in consequence of the great fatigue that you have undergone on the way. And Janaka's daughter, the noble Maithili, is evidently cager (for rest).

sa_verse: एषा च सुकुमारी च खेदैश्च न विमानिता। प्राज्यदोषं वनं प्राप्ता भर्तृस्नेहप्रचोदिता॥
en: Of tender years and unkn wn to hardship, she has come to the forest rife with troubles, being urged by the love she bears to her lord.

sa_verse: यथैषा रमते राम इह सीता तथा कुरु। दुष्करं कृतवत्येषा वने त्वामभिगच्छती॥
en: Do you, O Rāma, conduct yourself so, that Sītā may find a pleasant time of it. By fcllowing you to the forest, it is a hard task that she is performing.

sa_verse: एषा हि प्रकृतिः स्त्रीणामा सृष्टे रघुनन्दन । समस्थमनुरज्यन्ते विषमस्थं त्य नन्ति च ॥
en: O son of Raghu, this has been the nature of the fair sex from the commencement of creation, that they gladden him that is well off, and forsake a person in adversity.

sa_verse: शतहदानां लोलत्वं शस्त्राणां तीक्ष्णतां तथा। गरुडानिलयोः शैघ्यमनुगच्छन्ति योषितः॥
en: And women imitate the instability of lightning, the sharpness of weapons, and the celerity of Garuda and the wind.

sa_verse: इयं तु भवतो भार्या दोषैरेतैर्विवर्जिता। श्लाघ्या च व्यपदेश्या च यथा देवेष्वरुन्धती॥
en: But this wife of yours is absolutely free from all these defects; she is worthy of being extolled and the foremost of those devoted to their lord, like Arundhati among the gods.

sa_verse: अलंकृतोऽयं देशश्च यत्र सौमित्रिणा सह। वैदेह्या चानया राम वत्स्यसि त्वमरिंदम॥
en: This region, O Rāma, will be adorned, since, O subduer of enemies, you along with Vaidehī and Sumitra's son, will dwell here.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु मुनिना राघवः संयताञ्जलिः। उवाच प्रश्रितं वाक्यमृषि दीप्तमिवानलम्॥
en: Thus addressed by the ascetic, Rāghava, joining his hands, humbly observed to that saint resembling flaming fire,

sa_verse: धन्योऽस्म्यनुगृहीतोऽस्मि यस्य मे मुनिपुंगवः। गुणैः सभ्रातृभार्यस्य गुरुर्नः परितुष्यति॥
en: Blessed and beholden am I, since the foremost of ascetics is gratified with my merits as well as with those of my brother and wife.

sa_verse: किं तु व्यादिश मे देशं सोदकं बहुकाननम्। यत्राश्रमपदं कृत्वा वसेयं निरतः सुखम्॥
en: Do you now direct me to a country well watered, and abounding in woods, where rearing an asylum, 1 may dwell delightfully and pleasantly.

sa_verse: ततोऽब्रवीन्मुनिश्रेष्ठः श्रुत्वा रामस्य भाषितम्। ध्यात्वा मुहुर्तं धर्मात्मा ततोवाच वचः शुभम्॥
en: Hearing Rāma's words, that best of ascetics, reflecting for a while, spoke these excellent words.

sa_verse: इतो द्वियोजने तात बहुमूलफलोदकः। देशो बहुमृगः श्रीमान् पञ्चवट्यभिविश्रुतः॥
en: Two Yojanas hence, my child, is a region abounding in fruits and roots, containing countless deer, and beautiful-known by the name of Pancavați.

sa_verse: तत्र गत्वाऽऽश्रमपदं कृत्वा सौमित्रिणा सह। रमस्व त्वं पितुर्वाक्यं यथोक्तमनुपालयन्॥
en: Repairing thither, do you, rearing an asylum, pass your time pleasantly in company with Sumitrā's son, duly doing the mandate of your sire.

sa_verse: विदितो ह्येष वृत्तान्तो मम सर्वस्तवानघ। तपसश्च प्रभावेण स्नेहाद् दशरथस्य च।१५।।
en: O sinless one, all this news relating to yourself, has from affection been known to me through the potency of my asceticism, as also that relating to Dasaratha.

sa_verse: हृदयस्थं च ते च्छन्दो विज्ञातं तपसा मया। इह वासं प्रतिज्ञाय मया सह तपोवने ॥
en: Although you have promised to dwell with me in this ascetic grove, yet by virtue of my asceticism I know the desire that is in your heart.

sa_verse: अतश्च त्वामहं ब्रूमि गच्छ पञ्चवटीमिति। स हि रम्यो वनोद्देशो मैथिली तत्र रंस्यते॥
en: I therefore tell you, repair to Pancavati. That is a charining woodland, and there Mithila's daughter shall dwell with delight.

sa_verse: स देशः श्लाघनीयश्च नातिदूरे च राघव । गोदावर्याः समीपे च मैथिली तत्र रंस्यते॥
en: And that tract is worthy of all praise, and, O Rāghava, it is not distant from here. It is in the vicinity of the Godāvarī. Mithilā's daughter shall live there agreeably.

sa_verse: प्राज्यमूलफलैश्चैव नानाद्विजगणैर्युतः। विविक्तश्च महाबाहो पुण्यो रम्यस्तथैव च॥
en: And that spot abounds in fruits and roots, is frequented by various fowls, is retired, O mighty-armed one, and is sacred and beautiful.

sa_verse: भवानपि सदाचारः शक्तश्च परिरक्षणे। अपि चात्र वसन् राम तापसान् पालयिष्यसि॥
en: And you of pure ways, and competent to protect the ascetics, shall, O Rāma protect them.

sa_verse: एतदालक्ष्यते वीर मधूकानां महावनम्। उत्तरेणास्य गन्तव्यं न्यग्रोधमपि गच्छता॥ ततः स्थलमुपारुह्य पर्वतस्याविदूरतः। ख्यातः पञ्चवटीत्येव नित्यपुष्पितकाननः॥
en: O hero, yonder is the mighty wood of Madhūkas. Directing your course to the asylum of Nyagrodha trees, go by the north of this Madhūka wood. Then arriving at a spot hard by a hill, (you will) come upon the celebrated Pañcavați, crowned with blossoming woods.

sa_verse: अगस्त्येनैवमुक्तस्तु रामः सौमित्रिणा सह। सत्कृत्यामन्त्रयामास तमृषि सत्यवादिनम्॥
en: Thus accosted by Agastya, Rāma along with Sumitra's son, honouring the truth-speaking saint, greeted him.

sa_verse: तौ तु तेनाभ्यनुज्ञातौ कृतपादाभिवन्दनौ। तमाश्रमं पञ्चवटी जग्मतुः सह सीतया॥
en: Then, having saluted his feet, they, taking the saint's permission, set out along with Sītā for the hermitage of Pañcavați.

sa_verse: गृहीतचापौ तु नराधिपात्मजौ विषक्ततूणी समरेष्वकातरौ। यथोपदिष्टेन पथा महर्षिणा प्रजग्मतुः पञ्चवटीं समाहितौ॥
en: And, taking their bows and quivers those sons of the king, of undiminished martial virtue, with intent minds bent their course to Pañcavați by the way laid down by the Maharși.

sa_verse: अथ पञ्चवटीं गच्छन्नन्तरा रघुनन्दनः। आससाद महाकायं गृधं भीमपराक्रमम्॥
en: As he was proceeding to Pancavati, Raghu's son saw a huge-bodied vulture of terrible prowess.

sa_verse: तं दृष्ट्वा तौ महाभागौ वनस्थं रामलक्ष्मणौ। मेनाते राक्षसं पक्षिं ब्रुवाणौ को भवानिति॥
en: And seeing him in the forest, the exalted Rāma and Lakşmaņa, as they were conversing with each other, knowing the bird to be (in reality) a Raksasa, asked him, saying, Who are you?

sa_verse: ततो मधुरया वाचा सौम्यया प्रीणयन्निव। उवाच वत्स मां विद्धि वयस्यं पितुरात्मनः॥
en: There-upon, in soft and sweet words, he, pleasing them, said, My child, know me for a friend of your father.'

sa_verse: स तं पितृसखं मत्वा पूजयामास राघवः। स तस्य कुलमव्यग्रमथ पप्रच्छ नाम च॥
en: Knowing him to be a friend of his sire, Rāghava paid him homage, and enquired for his name and lineage.

sa_verse: पूर्वकाले महाबाहो ये प्रजापतयोऽभवन्। तान् मे निगदतः सर्वानादितः शृणु राघव ॥
en: O mighty-armed one, I shall describe (to you) from the very beginning the (history of) those that were Prajāpatis in days of yore.

sa_verse: कर्दमः प्रथमस्तेषां विकृतस्तदनन्तरम्। शेषश्च संश्रयश्चैव बहुपुत्रश्च वीर्यवान्॥ स्थाणुर्मरीचिरत्रिश्च क्रतुश्चैव महाबलः। पुलस्त्यश्चाङ्गिराश्चैव प्रचेताः पुलहस्तथा ॥ दक्षो विवस्वानपरोऽरिष्टनेमिश्च राघव। कश्यपश्च महातेजास्तेषामासीच्च पश्चिमः॥
en: Listen, O Raghava. Of these, the first is Kardama, then Vikrta, and then Sesa, Sansraya, the powerful Bahuputra, Sthanu, Marici, Atri, the mighty Kratu, Pulastya, Aigiri, Praceta, Pulaha, Daksa, Vivasvat, Aristanemi, O Rāghava, and the exceedingly energetic Kasyapa. These had the west.

sa_verse: प्रजापतेस्तु दक्षस्य बभूवुरिति विश्रुताः। षष्टिर्दुहितरो राम यशस्विन्यो महायशः॥
en: The Prajāpati Dakşa, it has been heard by us, O Rāma, had sixty famous and illustrious daughters.

sa_verse: कश्यपः प्रतिजग्राह तासामष्टौ सुमध्यमाः। अदितिं च दितिं चैव दनूमपि च कालकाम्॥ ताम्रां क्रोधवशां चैव मर्नु चाप्यनलामपि।
en: Of these Kaśyapa wed eight, with elegant waists; Aditi, Diti, Danu, Kālikā, Tamră, Krodhavāśa, Manu and Analā.

sa_verse: तास्तु कन्यास्ततः प्रीतः कश्यपः पुनरब्रवीत्॥ पुत्रांस्त्रैलोक्यभतृन् वै जनयिष्यथ मत्समान्।
en: Then, well pleased Kaśyapa again said to those damsels, Do you bring forth sons like to myself, who shall be lords of the three worlds.

sa_verse: अदितिस्तन्मना राम दितिश्च दनुरेव च ॥ कालका च महाबाहो शेषास्त्वमनसोऽभवन्।
en: Thereat, Aditi, O Raina, Diti, Danu, and Kalika*, O mighty-armed one, consented, the rest were of another mind. *Some text:-Kalaka.

sa_verse: अदित्यं जज्ञिरे देवास्त्रयस्त्रिंशदरिंदम॥ आदित्या वसवो रुद्रा अश्विनौ च परंतप ।
en: And of Aditi were born thirty-three deities, O repressor of your foes, viz., the Adityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, and the Asvinas, O subduer of enemies.

sa_verse: दितिस्त्वजनयत् पुत्रान् दैत्यांस्तात यशस्विनः॥ तेषामियं वसुमती पुराऽऽसीत् सवनार्णवा।
en: And, my child, Diti gave birth to those sons, the renowned Daityas. Formerly, this earth girt with seas was in the possession of these.

sa_verse: दनुस्त्वजनयत् पुत्रमश्वग्रीवमरिंदम ॥ नरकं कालकं चैव कालकापि व्यजायत।
en: And, O vanquisher of your foes, Danu gave birth to a son, named, Ašvagriva. And Kālikā brought forth Naraka and Kālaka.

sa_verse: क्रौञ्ची भासीं तथा श्येनी धृतराष्ट्री तथा शुकीम्॥ ताम्रा तु सुषुरे कन्याः पञ्चैता लोकविश्रुताः।
en: And Tāmrā gave birth to these five daughters celebrated in the world, Kraunci, Bhāsi, Syeni, Dhrtarastri, and Suki.

sa_verse: उलूकाज्जनयत् क्रौञ्ची भासी भासान् व्यजायत॥ श्येनी श्येनांश्च गृधांश्च व्यजायत सुतेजसः। धृतराष्ट्री तु हंसांश्च कलहंसांश्च सर्वशः॥ चक्रवाकांश्च भद्रं ते विजज्ञे सापि भामिनी। शुकी नतां विजज्ञे तु नतायां विनता सुता॥
en: And Krauncī gave birth to the Ulūkas, and Bhāsī, to Bhāsas, and Śyeni to hawks and vultures possessed of energy; and Dhịtarāştri to swans, all kinds of Kalahansas, and Cakravākas, and that damsel, Suki, to Natā And Vinatā was Natā's daughter.

sa_verse: दश क्रोधवशा राम विजज्ञेऽप्यात्मसम्भवाः। मृगी च मृगमन्दां च हरिं भद्रमदामपि॥ मातङ्गीमथ शार्दूलीं श्वेतां च सुरभी तथा। सर्वलक्षणसम्पन्नां सुरसां कद्रुकामपि ॥
en: And Krodhavasā, O Rāma, brought forth ten daughters viz., Mrgi, Mrgamanda, Hari, Bhadramadā, Mātangi, Śårdūlī, Śvetā, Surabhi crowned with every (auspicious mark), Surasā and Kadrukā.

sa_verse: अपत्यं तु मृगाः सर्वे मृग्या नरवरोत्तम। ऋक्षाश्च मृगमन्दायाः सृमराश्चमरास्तथा॥
en: You foremost of the best of men, Mrgi's sons were all the deer, and those of Mſgamandã were bears, Srmaras and Camaras.

sa_verse: ततस्त्विरावतीं नाम जज्ञे भद्रमदा सुताम्। तस्यास्त्वैरावतः पुत्रो लोकनाथो महागजः॥
en: And Bhadramadā bore one daughter, Irāvati. And Irāvati's son is the mighty elephant; who is the lord of the worlds.

sa_verse: हर्याश्च हरयोऽपत्यं वानराश्च तपस्विनः। गोलाङ्गलाश्च शार्दूली व्याघ्रांश्चाजनयत् सुतान् ॥ मातङ्गयोस्त्वथ मातङ्गा अपत्यं मनुजर्षभ। दिशागजं तु काकुत्स्थ श्वेता व्यजनयत् सुतम्॥
en: And Hari's sons are lions and the nimble monkeys. And Śārdúlí brought forth as her sons, Golāngūlas, and tigers; and the offspring of Matangi were mad elephants, O best of men. And Śvetā, O Kākutstha, gave birth to the elephants of the cardinal points.

sa_verse: ततो दुहितरौ राम सुरभिट्टै व्यजायत। रोहिणी नाम भद्रं ते गन्धर्वी च यशस्विनीम्॥
en: O Rāma, then, Surabhi gave birth to two daughters-the famous Rohini, and Gandharvi.

sa_verse: रोहिण्यजनयद् गावो गन्धर्वी वाजिनः सुतान्। सुरसाऽजनयन्त्रागान् राम कद्रूश्च पन्नगान्॥
en: Rohini produced kine, and the sons of Gandharvī are horses. And Surasā, O Rāma, gave birth to Nāgas, and Kadru to Pannagas.

sa_verse: मनुर्मनुष्याञ्जनयत् कश्यपस्य महात्मनः। ब्राह्मणान् क्षत्रियान् वैश्याशूद्रांश्च मनुजर्षभ॥
en: Kāśyapa's other wife Manu begot mankind-Brahmanas, Ksatriyas, Vaisyas, and Sudras.

sa_verse: मुखतो ब्राह्मणो जाता उरसः क्षत्रियास्तथा। ऊरुभ्यां जज्ञिरे वैश्याः पद्भयां शूद्रा इति श्रुतिः॥
en: From the mouth, it is known sprang Brahmanas, from the breast, Ksatriya, from the thighs, Vaisyas, and from the feet, Sudras.

sa_verse: सर्वान् पुण्यफलान् वृक्षाननलापि व्यजायत। विनता च शुकीपौत्री कद्रूश्च सुरसास्वसा ॥
en: And Anală produced all trees bearing sacred fruits. Vinatā (was) Śuki' grand-daughter, and Kadru, Surasā's aunt.

sa_verse: कर्नागसहस्रं तु विजज्ञे धरणीधरान्। द्वौ पुत्रौ विनतायास्तु गरुडोऽरुण एव च॥
en: And Kadru gave birth to a thousand Nāgas with the holder of the Earth. And Vinatā brought forth two sons-Garuda and Aruna.

sa_verse: तस्माज्जातोऽहमरुणात् सम्पतिश्च ममाग्रजः। जटायुरिति मां विद्धि श्येनीपुत्रमरिंदम॥
en: An from Aruņa sprang myself and my elder brother Sampāti subduer of enemies, know me for Jatayu, the son of Syeni.

sa_verse: सोऽहं वाससहायस्ते भविष्यामि यदीच्छसि। सीतां च तात रक्षिष्ये त्वयि याते सलक्ष्मणे॥
en: If you will, I shall become your help during your abode (in the woods). And, O child, I shall protect Sītā when you shall go out along with Laksimana.

sa_verse: स तत्र सीतां परिदाय मैथिली सहैव तेनातिबलेन पक्षिणा। जगाम तां पञ्चवटीं सलक्ष्मणो रिपून् दिधक्षस वनानि पालयन्॥
en: Then consigning to that bird of exceeding strength, Mithila's daughter, Sita, Rama accompanied by Lakşmana went to Pañcavatī, destroying his foes and protecting the worlds.

sa_verse: ततः पञ्चवटीं गत्वा नानाव्यालमृगायुताम्। उवाच लक्ष्मणं रामो भ्रातरं दीप्ततेजसम्॥
en: Then repairing to Pañcavațī filled with various animals and beasts of prey, Rāma remarked to his brother Laksmana of flaming energy,

sa_verse: आगताः स्म यथोद्दिष्टं यं देशं मुनिरब्रवीत्। अयं पञ्चवटीदेशः सौम्य पुष्पितकाननः॥
en: Now) we have come to the place to which we had been directed by the ascetic This, O amiable one, is Pañcavați furnished with blossoming woods.

sa_verse: सर्वतश्चार्यतां दृष्टिः कानने निपुणो ह्यसि । आश्रमः कतरस्मिन् नो देशे भवति सम्मतः॥
en: Do you cast your eyes around this forest, and (ascertain) what spot shall suit our asylum.

sa_verse: रमते यत्र वैदेही त्वमहं चैव लक्ष्मणः। तादृशो दृश्यतां देशः संनिकृष्टजलाशयः॥ वनरामण्यकं यत्र जलरामण्यकं तथा। संनिकृष्टं च यस्मिस्तु समित्पुष्पकृशोदकम्॥
en: Do you find out such a place in the vicinity of a tank where yourself Sītā and I may dwell happily, which is graced with the garniture of woods and delightful with liquid lapses, and whose neighbourhood yields fuel, flowers, Kuća and water.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण लक्ष्मणः संयताञ्जलिः। सीतासमक्षं काकुत्स्थमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thus addressed by Rāma. Lakşmaņa with joined hands, said to Kākutstha in the presence of Sītā,

sa_verse: परवानस्मि काकुत्स्थ त्वयि वर्षशतं स्थिते। स्वयं तु रुचिरे देशे क्रियतामिति मां वद॥
en: O Kákutstha, even if I were to stay with you for an hundred years, I shall remain your servant. Thyself selecting some beauteous spot do you tell me-'Construct (an asylum)'.

sa_verse: सुप्रीतस्तेन वाक्येन लक्ष्मणस्य महाद्युतिः। विमृशन् रोचयामास देशं सर्वगुणान्वितम्॥ स तं रुचिरमाक्रम्य देशमाश्रमकर्मणि। हस्ते गृहीत्वा हस्तेन रामः सौमित्रिमब्रवीत्॥
en: Well pleased with the words of Lakşınana, that highly effulgent one, after dure reflection, selected a site having every recommendation. Going to that romantic spot for rearing an asylum, Rama, taking the hand of Sumitra's son in his, said to him.

sa_verse: अयं देशः समः श्रीमान् पुष्पितैस्तरुभिर्वृतः। इहाश्रमपदं रम्यं यथावत् कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: This place is level, graceful and surrounded with blossoming trees. Do you duly construct an asylum at this spot.

sa_verse: इयमादित्यसंकाशैः पद्मः सुरभिगन्धिभिः। अदूरे दृश्यते रम्या पद्मिनी पद्मशोभिता ॥
en: Hard by is seen a beautiful pool, embellished with lotuses, resembling the sun, and breathing balmy perfume.

sa_verse: यथाख्यातमगस्त्येन मुनिना भावितात्मना। १ इयं गोदावरी रम्या पुष्पितैस्तरुभिर्वृता॥ हंसकारण्डवाकीर्णा चक्रवाकोपशोभिता। नातिदूरे स चासन्ने मृगयूथनिपीडिता॥
en: And, as told by that pure-hearted ascetic, Agastya, this is the graceful Godåvarī, bordered by flowering trees; swarming with swans and Kārandavas, delighted with Cakravākas, thronged with herds of deer,* not far, yet not so very near.

sa_verse: मयूरनादिता रम्याः प्रांशवो बहुकन्दराः। दृश्यन्ते गिरयः सौम्य फुल्लैस्तरुभिरावृताः॥ सौवर्णं राजतैस्तारैर्देशे देशे तथा शुभैः। गवाक्षिता इवाभान्ति गजाः परमभक्तिभिः॥ सालैस्तालैस्तमालैश्च खजूरैः पनसैर्दुमैः। नीवारैस्तिनिशैश्चैव पुन्नागैश्चौपशोभिताः॥ चूतैरशोकैस्तिलकैः केतकैरपि चम्पकैः। पुष्पगुल्मलतोपेतैस्तैस्तैस्तरुभिरावृताः॥ स्यन्दनैश्चन्दनैपैः पनसैलकुचैरपि। धवाश्वकर्णखदिरैः शमीकिंशुकपाटलैः॥
en: And resounding with the cries of peacocks; charming; elevated; containing full many a cave; do you, O amiable one, behold these hills, covered with trees in full flower; and they are shining like elephants painted with diverse colours by persons with the utmost care; adorned with Salas, palmyras, Tamālas, dates, Panasas, Nīvāras, Tinišas, and Punnāgas. And covered with mangoes, Asokas, Tilakas, Ketakas, and Campakas, and trees entwined by flowers, herbs and plants; and containing Syandanas, sandals, Nīpas, Paņāsas, Lakucas, Dhavas, Ašvakarņas, Khadiras, Samls, Kinsukas and Patalas.

sa_verse: इदं पुण्यमिदं रम्यमिदं बहुमृगद्विजम्। इह वत्स्याम सौमित्रे सार्धमेतेन पक्षिणा॥
en: This spot is sacred-this spot is charmingthis spot abounds in beasts and birds. Here will I dwell, O Sumitra's son, in company with this birds.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण लक्ष्मणः परवीरहा। अचिरेणाश्रमं भ्रातुश्चकार सुमहाबलः॥
en: Thus addressed by Rāma, the exceedingly powerful Lakşmaņa, slayer of hostile heroes, in a short time raised as asylum for his brother.

sa_verse: पर्णशालां सुविपुलां तत्र संघातमृत्तिकाम्। सुस्तम्भां मस्करैदीर्धेः कृतवंशां सुशोभनाम्॥ शमीशाखाभिरास्तीर्य दृढपाशावपाशिताम्। कुशकाशशरैः पर्णैः सुपरिच्छादितां तथा॥ समीकृततलां रम्यां चकार सुमहाबलः। निवासं राघवस्यार्थे प्रेक्षणीयमनुत्तमम्॥
en: And the exceedingly stout Lakşmaņa created there for Rāghava a spacious hut thatched with leaves made of clay, furnished with pillars, constructed with long bomboos, graceful-spread with Samī boughs; tightly fastened with strong cords; covered with Kusa reeds, and leaves; with its floor well levelled; and charming; beautiful to look at, and exceedingly excellent.

sa_verse: स गत्वा लक्ष्मणः श्रीमान् नदी गोदावरी तदा। स्नात्वा पद्मानि चादाय सफलः पुनरागतः॥
en: And then going to the river Godāvarī, the lovely Lakşmana, performing his bath, and securing lotuses and fruits, came back (to the asylum).

sa_verse: ततः पुष्पबलिं कृत्वा शान्तिं च स यथाविधि। दर्शयामास रामाय तदाश्रमपदं कृतम्॥
en: Then offering flowers, and duly performing rites for the peace (of the habitation), Lakşmaņa showed the asylum which he had made to Rāma.

sa_verse: स तं दृष्ट्वा कृतं सौम्यमाश्रमं सह सीतया। राघवः पर्णशालायां हर्षमाहारयत् परम्॥
en: Seeing the beautiful asylum along with Sitā, Rāghava experienced high raptures.

sa_verse: सुसंहृष्टः परिष्वज्य बाहुभ्यां लक्ष्मणं तदा। अतिस्निग्धं च गाढं च वचनं चेदमब्रवीत्॥
en: And joyfully embracing Lakṣmaṇa with his arms, Rāma said these exceedingly calm and solemn words.

sa_verse: प्रीतोऽस्मि ते महत् कर्म त्वया कृतमिदं प्रभो। प्रदेयो यन्निमित्तं ते परिष्वङ्गो मया कृतः॥
en: Pleased am I with you. You have done a great deed, my brother; for which I have granted you my embrace by way of reward.

sa_verse: भावज्ञेन कृतज्ञेन धर्मज्ञेन च लक्ष्मण। त्वया पुत्रेण धर्मात्मा स संवृतः पिता मम ॥
en: While you, his son skilled in reading thought, grateful, and cognizant of righteousness are alive, O Laksmana, my father is not dead.

sa_verse: एवं लक्ष्मणमुक्त्वा तु राघवो लक्ष्मिवर्धनः। तस्मिन् देशे बहुफले न्यवसत् स सुखं सुखी॥
en: Having said this to Lakşmana, Rāghava— the enhancer of auspiciousness experiencing felicity, began to dwell happily in that region filled with fruits.

sa_verse: कंचित् कालं स धर्मात्मा सीतया लक्ष्मणेन च। अन्वास्यमानो न्यवसत् स्वर्गलोके यथामरः॥
en: And ministered to by Sītā and Lakşmaņa that righteous one lived there, like the immortals in heaven.

sa_verse: वसतस्तस्य तु सुखं राघवस्य महात्मनः। ''शरद्व्यपाये हेमन्तऋतुरिष्टः प्रवर्तत॥
en: As the high-souled Rāghava was dwelling there peacefully, after autumn had departed, the welcome winter commenced.

sa_verse: स कदाचित् प्रभातायां शर्वर्यां रघुनन्दनः। प्रययावभिषेकार्थं रम्यां गोदावरी नदीम्॥
en: And it came to pass that once on a time, when the night had passed away and day broke, that son of Raghu went to the romantic river Godāvarī for performing his bath.

sa_verse: प्रह्वः कलशहस्तस्तु सीतया सह वीर्यवान्। पृष्ठतोऽनुव्रजन् भ्राता सौमित्रिरिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: And as the humble son of Sumitrā Rāma's puissant brother bearing a water pitcher in his hand was following Rāma along with Sītā at his back, the former said to Rāma,

sa_verse: अयं स कालः सम्प्राप्तः प्रियो यस्ते प्रियंवद। अलंकृत इवाभाति येन संवत्सरः शुभः॥
en: O sweet-speeched one, now is come that season which you have ever held dear; and as if decorated by which comes on the entire auspicious year.

sa_verse: नीहारपरुषो लोकः पृथिवी सस्यमालिनी। जलान्यनुपभोग्यानि सुभगो हव्यवाहनः ॥
en: Now people have their persons rendered rough from dew; the earth is replenished with com; water is difficult of being used; and fire becomes enjoyable.

sa_verse: नवाग्रयणपूजाभिरभ्यर्च्य पितृदेवताः। कृताग्रयणकाः काले सन्तो विगतकल्मषाः॥
en: And men having worshipped the gods and the Pitſis by celebrating Āgrayana on the occasion of partaking new rice, have at this season, their sins purged off.

sa_verse: प्राज्यकामा जनपदा: सम्पन्नतरगोरसाः। विचरन्ति महीपाला यात्रार्थं विजिगीषवः॥
en: The provinces overflow with food, and abound in milk and articles prepared therefrom; and kings bent on conquest, range about for surveying thein.

sa_verse: सेवमाने दृढं सूर्ये दिशमन्तकसेविताम्। विहीनतिलकेव स्त्री नोत्तरा दिक् प्रकाशते॥
en: And on the sun having veered steadily to the quarter presided over by the Destroyer*, the north appears like a female without her tilaka. ** *i.e.-The South, **A mark made with coloured earth's or unguents upon the forehead and between the eye-brows either by way of ornament or a sectarial distinction.

sa_verse: प्रकृत्या हिमकोशाढ्यो दूरसूर्यश्च साम्प्रतम्। यथार्थनामा सुव्यक्तं हिमवान् हिमवान् गिरिः॥
en: Naturally abounding in snow, and now having the sun at a great distance, the mountain Himavān+ justly bear that name. Himavan means having snow.-T

sa_verse: अत्यन्तसुखसंचारा मध्याह्ने स्पर्शतः सुखाः। दिवसाः सुभगादित्याश्छायासलिलदुर्भगाः॥
en: At midday the days are delightful to a degree to range in, feel highly agreeable, and have pleasant suns; while shade and water are uncomfortable.

sa_verse: मृदुसूर्याः सुनीहाराः पटुशीताः समाहिताः। शून्यारण्या हिमध्वस्ता दिवसा भान्ति साम्प्रतम्॥
en: The days now have mild suns, are covered with dew severely cold, with the forests remaining idle, and the lotuses destroyed by frost.

sa_verse: निवृत्ताकाशशयनाः पुष्यनीता हिमारुणाः। शीतवृद्धतरायामास्त्रियामा यान्ति साम्प्रतम्॥ रविसंक्रांन्तसौभाग्यस्तुषारारुणमण्डलः। निःश्वासान्ध इवादर्शश्चन्द्रमा न प्रकाशते॥ ज्योत्स्ना तुषारमलिना पौर्णमास्यां न राजते। सीतेव चातपश्यामा लक्ष्यते न च शोभते॥
en: And now at night people cannot lie down in unsheltered places; and the nights are inferred from the presence of Pusya And at night the atmosphere (being covered with vapour) looks brown; and it is bitter cold then; and the hours are long And the good fortune of the Moon has fallen to the Sun; and the disc of the former is reddish with vapour; and like to a glass breathed upon, it dose not shine clearly. And the moonlight furnished by vapour dose not appear pleasant; and like Sītā pallid because of exposure to) sunshine, is perceptible, but dose not look beauteous,

sa_verse: प्रकृत्या शीतलस्पर्शो हिमविद्धश्च साम्प्रतम्। प्रवाति पश्चिमो वायुः काले द्विगुणशीतलः॥
en: And at this season the western wind naturally of gelid feel, being permeated at present with cold, Flows with ouble coldness.

sa_verse: वाष्पच्छन्नान्यरण्यानि यवगोधूमवन्ति च। शोभन्तेऽभ्युदिते सूर्ये नदद्भिः कौञ्चसारसैः॥
en: And the forests enveloped in mist, and stocked with wheat and barley, look brilliant at sunrise, with Krauñcas and cranes crying (in chorus).

sa_verse: खजूरपुष्पाकृतिभिः शिरोभिः पूर्णतण्डुलैः। शोभन्ते किंचिदालम्बाः शालय: कनकप्रभाः॥
en: Golden-hued paddy slightly inclined, appear graceful, with their heads like to date-flowers filled with rice.

sa_verse: मयूखैरुपसर्पद्भिर्हिमनीहारसंवृतैः। दूरमभ्युदितः सूर्यः शशाङ्क इव लक्ष्यते॥
en: And the sun although high advanced. yet having its rays covered with thick mist, appear like the moon,

sa_verse: आग्राह्यवीर्यः पूर्वाह्ने मध्याह्ने स्पर्शतः सुखः। संरक्तः किंचिदापाण्डुरातपः शोभते क्षितौ॥
en: And feeble at the first part of the day, and of agreeable touch at mid-day, the sunshine, being surcharged with mist, appear polish over the face of the earth.

sa_verse: अवस्यायनिपातेन किंचित्प्रक्लिन्नशाद्वला। वनानां शोभते भूमिर्निविष्टतरुणातपा॥
en: And in the woods, swards covered with green grass, and with dew-drops on it, look handsome on the morning sun brightening it up.

sa_verse: स्पृशन् सुविपुलं शीतमुदकं द्विरदः सुखम्। अत्यन्ततृषितो वन्यः प्रतिसंहरते करम्॥
en: And wild elephants waxing exceedingly thirsty, draw away their trunks, just as they touch beautifully clear and cold water.

sa_verse: एते हि समुपासीना विहगा जलचारिणः। नावगाहन्ति सलिलमप्रगल्भा इवाहवम्॥
en: And those aquatic fowls seated by, do not dip themselves in the water, like to cravens shunning fight.

sa_verse: अवस्यायतमोनद्धा नीहारतमसावृताः। प्रसुप्ता इव लक्ष्यन्ते विपुष्पा वनराजयः॥
en: And the rows of trees, shorn of their blossoms, on being enveloped with dew at night and mist at morn, look as if they were fast asleep.

sa_verse: वाष्पसंछन्त्रसलिला रुतविज्ञेयसारसाः। हिमावालुकैस्तीरैः सरितो भान्ति साम्प्रतम्॥
en: And the streams appear with their waters enveloped in vapour and the cranes, perceived through their cries, and their banks having their sands wet.

sa_verse: तुषारपतनाच्चैव मृदुत्वाद् भास्करस्य च। शैत्यादगाग्रस्थमपि प्रायेण रसवज्जलम्॥
en: What on account of the snow-fall, what of the mildness of the sun, and what through the cold, water even when it is on a mountain top, tastes sweet.

sa_verse: जराजर्जरितैः पत्रैः शीर्णकेसरकर्णिकैः। नालशेषा 'हिमध्वस्ता न भान्ति कमलाकराः॥
en: And lotuses afflicted by the cold, with the stalks only left, and with their petals dropping down and their pericarps and filaments shrivelled up, do not appear beautiful.

sa_verse: अस्मिंस्तु पुरुषव्याघ्र काले दुःखसमन्वितः। तपश्चरित धर्मात्मा त्वद्भक्त्या भरतः पुरे॥
en: O foremost of men, at this season, influenced by regard for you, Bharata undergoing affliction of spirit, is performing mortification's in the city.

sa_verse: त्यक्त्वा राज्यं च मानं च भोगांश्च विविधान् बहून्। तपस्वी नियताहारः शेते शीते महीतले॥
en: And forsaking kingdom, and dignity, and the many and various enjoyments, Bharata practising asceticism and restraining himself in respect of food, in this winter lie down on the bare earth.

sa_verse: सोऽपि वेलामिमां नूनमभिषेकार्थमुद्यतः। वृतः प्रकृतिभिर्नित्यं प्रयाति सरयूं नदीम्॥
en: He also for certain, at this hour of the day, surrounded by this subjects, daily wend to the river Sarayū for performing his ablutions.

sa_verse: अत्यन्तसुखसंवृद्धः सुकुमारो हिमादितः। कथं त्वपररात्रेषु सरयूमवगाहते॥
en: Brought up in luxury, and exceedingly tender, how can he, afflicted with cold, perforim his ablutions in the Sarayū during the latter part of the night?

sa_verse: पद्मपत्रेक्षणः श्यामः श्रीमान् निरुदरो महान्। धर्मशः सत्यवादी च ह्रीनिषेधो जितेन्द्रियः॥ प्रियाभिभाषी मधुरो दीर्घबाहुररिंदमः। संत्यज्य विविधान् सौख्यानार्यं सर्वात्मना श्रितः॥
en: Of eyes resembling lotus petals, grey-hued, endued with grace, with a navel depressed, and mighty, Bharata understand righteousness, is truth-speaking, of restrained senses (in respect of other' wives) and also of subdued senses. And he speak fair, and is sweet-tempered, and longarmed and the vanquisher of foes. And renouncing various pleasures, he has devoted himself to the noble one.

sa_verse: जितः स्वर्गस्तव भ्रात्रा भरतेन महात्मना। वनस्थमपि तापस्ये यस्त्वामनुविधीयते॥
en: Your high-souled brother has secured heaven, since he, resorting to asceticism, is iinitating you that resident in the woods.

sa_verse: न पित्र्यमनुवर्तन्ते मातृकं द्विपदा इति। ख्यातो लोकप्रवादोऽयं भरतेनान्यथा कृतः॥
en: The saying that people follow their mother and not their father, is falsified in the case of Bharata.

sa_verse: भर्ता दशरथो यस्याः साधुश्च भरतः सुतः। कथं नु साम्बा कैकेयी तादृशी क्रुरदशिनी॥
en: How can mother Kaikeyī, whose husband is Daśaratha and son the righteous Bharata, have such crooked way?

sa_verse: इत्येवं लक्ष्मणे वाक्यं स्नेहाद् वदति धार्मिके। परिवादं जनन्यास्तमसहन् राघवोऽब्रवीत्॥ न तेऽम्बा मध्यमा तात गर्हितव्या कदाचन। तामेवेक्ष्वाकुनाथस्य भरतस्य कथां कुरु॥
en: As the virtuous Lakṣmaṇa was speaking thus from affection, Rāghava, not bearing the blame cast upon his mother (Kaikeyi), said, My brother, you should by no means, tax our second mother. Do you talk of that lord of the Ikşvāku race, Bharata.

sa_verse: निश्चितैव हि मे बुद्धिर्वनवासे दृढव्रता। भरतस्नेहसंतप्ता बालिशीक्रियते पुनः॥
en: Albeit my mind is firm as regards my sojourn in the woods, yet is my mind unsteady, being inflained with affection for Bharata.

sa_verse: संस्मराम्यस्य वाक्यानि प्रियाणि मधुराणि च। हृद्यान्यमृतकल्पानि मन:प्रह्लादनानि च॥
en: I remember his dear sweet words, taking the heart, resembling ambrosia, filling the soul with delight.

sa_verse: कदा ह्यहं समेष्यामि भरतेन महात्मना। शत्रुघ्नेन च वीरेण त्वया च रघुनन्दन ॥
en: When shall I; O son of Raghu, meet with the high-souled Bharata, and the heroic Satrughna, and yourself*. *The meaning is evident when shall we all meet together.-T

sa_verse: इत्येवं विलपंस्तत्र प्राप्य गोदावरी नदीम्। चक्रेऽभिषेकं काकुत्स्थः सानुजः सह सीतया॥
en: Having bewailed thus, Kākutstha, coming to the river Godāvarī, there performed his ablutions in company with his younger brother and Sītā.

sa_verse: तर्पयित्वाथ सलिलैस्तैः पितॄन् दैवतानपि। स्तुवन्ति स्मोदितं सूर्य देवताश्च तथानघाः।॥
en: Then having offered his adorations with water to the gods and the pitrs, those sinless ones hymned the risen sun as well as the celestials.

sa_verse: कृताभिषेकः स रराज रामः सीताद्वितीयः सह लक्ष्मणेन। कृताभिषेकस्त्वगराजपुत्र्या रुद्रः सनन्दिर्भगवानिवेशः॥
en: Having with Sītā as the second and Laksmana, performed his bath. Rama looked beautiful like that lord the reverend Rudra in company with Nandi and the daughter of the king of mountains.

sa_verse: कृताभिषेको रामस्तु सीता सौमित्रिरेव च। तस्माद् गोदावरीतीरात् ततो जग्मुः स्वमाश्रमम्॥
en: Having bathed, Rāma Sītā and Sumitrā's son went to their own asylum from the banks of the Godāvarī.

sa_verse: आश्रमं तमुपागम्य राघवः सहलक्ष्मणः। कृत्वा पौर्वाह्निकं कर्म पर्णशालामुपागमत् ॥
en: Arriving at the asylum, Rāghava along with Lakşmana, performing his morning devotions, entered the cottage.

sa_verse: उवास सुखितस्तत्र पूज्यमानो महर्षिभिः। स रामः पर्णशालायामासीनः सह सीतया॥ विरराज महाबाहुश्चित्रया चन्द्रमा इव। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्राता चकार विविधाः कथाः॥
en: And honoured of the Maharsis, Rāma happily dwelt in that cottage; and seated with Sītā that mighty-armed one looked like the moon in conjunction with Citrā. And he carried on various converse with his brother Lakşınana.

sa_verse: तदासीनस्य रामस्य कथासंसक्तचेतसः। तं देशं राक्षसी काचिदाजगाम यदृच्छया॥ सा तु शूर्पणखा नाम दशग्रीवस्य रक्षसः। भगिनी राममासाद्य ददर्श त्रिदशोपमम्॥ दीप्तास्यं च महाबाहुं पद्भपत्रायतेक्षणम्। गजविक्रान्तगमनं जटामण्डलधारिणम्।७।।
en: As Räma was thus seated with his mind engrossed in that talk, a Rākṣasi came to that region at her will. And that one named Surpanakhā, sister to the ten-necked Raksasa, coming upon Răma, saw him like a celestial, having a leonine chest, mighty arms, and expansive eyes resembling lotus leaves, with the powerful gait of the elephant, wearing a head of matted locks, with a resplendent countenance, bearing regal marks.

sa_verse: सुकुमारं महासत्त्वं पार्थिवव्यञ्जनान्वितम्। राममिन्दीवरश्यामं कंदर्पसदृशप्रभम्॥ वभूवेन्द्रोपमं दृष्ट्वा राक्षसी काममोहिता।
en: And beholding Rāma dark-blue like the lotus, and becoming like Kandarpa himself, and resembling Indra, the Rākşasi was maddened with desire.

sa_verse: सुमुखं दुर्मुखी रामं वृत्तमध्यं महोदरी॥ विशालाक्षं विरूपाक्षी सुकेशं ताम्रमूर्धजा। प्रियरूपं विरूपा सा सुस्वरं भैरवस्वना ॥
en: Rāma graced with beauteous continence, that Raksasi had a hideous face; Ráma had a slender waist, she had a huge abdomen; he had expansive eyes, she had terrible eyes; he was gifted with an elegant head of hair, she had coppery hair; Rāma was of a dear presence, she was of an unsightly presence; Rāma had a sonorous voice, she had a hideous voice.

sa_verse: तरुणं दारुणा वृद्धा दक्षिणं वामभाषिणी। न्यायवृत्तं सुदुर्वृता प्रियमप्रियदर्शना ॥
en: Rāma was youthful, the Rākşasi was an old hag; Rāma had mellifluous accents, she had was a harsh accents; Rāma was ever abiding by justice, she was unruly; Rāma was handsome, she was ugly.

sa_verse: शरीरजसमाविष्टा राक्षसी राममब्रवीत्। जटी तापसवेषेण सभार्यः शरचापधृक् ॥ आगतस्त्वमिमं देशं कथं राक्षससेवितम्। किमागमनकृत्यं ते तत्त्वमाख्यातुमर्हसि॥
en: That Rākşasī being wrought up with passion addressed Rāma saying Wearing matted locks, and equipped with bows and arrows, why hast you along with your wife come to this region haunted by Rākșasas? It behove you to say "what the object of your visit is."

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु राक्षस्या शूर्पनख्या परंतपः। ऋजुबुद्धितया सर्वमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे॥
en: Thus accosted by the Rākṣasī, Śūrpanakhă, that subduer of his foes from sincerity of disposition began to relate everything;

sa_verse: आसीद् दशरथो नाम राजा त्रिदशविक्रमः। तस्याहमग्रजः पुत्रो रामो नाम जनैः श्रुतः॥
en: There was a king named Dasaratha endued with the prowess of a celestial. I am his eldest son known among men by the name of Rāma.

sa_verse: भ्रातायं लक्ष्मणो नाम यवीयान् मामनुव्रतः। इयं भार्या च वैदेही मम सीतेति विश्रुता॥
en: This is my younger brother (named) Laks inana, (ever)serving me. This is my wife, known by the name of Sītā.

sa_verse: नियोगात् तु नरेन्द्रस्य पितुर्मातुश्च यन्त्रितः। धर्मार्थं धर्मकाण्क्षी च वनं वस्तुमिहागतः॥
en: Commanded by my father and mother, I in obedience their mandate, desirous of acquiring religious merits, have for the purpose of securing righteousness, come to the forest for dwelling here.

sa_verse: त्वां तु वेदितुमिच्छामि कस्य त्वं कासि कस्य वा। त्वं हि तावन्मनोज्ञाङ्गी राक्षसी प्रतिभासि मे ॥ इह वा किंनिमित्तं त्वमागता ब्रूहि तत्त्वतः।
en: I also am anxious to know you. Whose daughter are you? And what is your name, and what your lineage? Possessed of a captivating form, you appear to me as a Rākşasī. Tell me truly why you have come here. to

sa_verse: साब्रवीद् वचनं श्रुत्वा राक्षसी मदनार्दिता॥ श्रूयतां राम तत्त्वार्थं वक्ष्यामि वचनं मम। अहं शूर्पणखा नाम राक्षसी कामरूपिणी॥
en: Hearing these words the Rákşasi, afflicted with lust said, Listen, O Rāma. I will relate everything truly. I am a Räkșasī, capable of wearing shapes at will. My name in Śūrpanakhā.

sa_verse: अरण्यं विचरामीदमेका सर्वभयंकरा। रावणो नाम मे भ्राता यदि ते श्रोत्रमागतः॥
en: Striking terror into the hearts of all, I range this forest alone. My brother is named Ravana* You may have heard of him.

sa_verse: वीरो विश्रवसः पुत्रो यदि ते श्रोत्रमागतः। प्रवृद्धनिद्रश्च सदा कुम्भकर्णो महाबलः॥ विभीषणस्तु धर्मात्मा स तु राक्षसचेष्टितः। प्रख्यातवीर्यौ च रणे भ्रातरौ खरदूषणौ॥
en: My brother Råvana is a valiant son of Viśravā if you have heard of him. And he that is given to long sleep-the mighty Kumbhakarna, the righteous Vibhīşaņa, who never serves the Rākşasas, and the brothers Khara and Düşaņa renowned in conflict (are also my brothers).

sa_verse: तानहं समतिक्रांता राम त्वापूर्वदर्शनात्। समुपेतास्मि भावेन भर्तारं पुरुषोत्तमम्॥
en: O Rāma, I have surpassed them all (in prowess). At first sight of you, I approach you, you best of men, as iny husband, with (feelings of) love.

sa_verse: अहं प्रभावसम्पन्ना स्वच्छन्दबलगामिनी। चिराय भव भर्ता मे सीतया किं करिष्यसि ॥
en: I am endued with power, and range about at will by virtue of my strength. Become my husband for ever and a day. What will you do with Sita?

sa_verse: विकृता च विरूपा च न सेयं सदृशी तव। अहमेवानुरूपा ते भार्यारूपेण पश्य माम्।।
en: Deformed and unsightly, she is not worthy of you. I am fit for you. Do you look upon me as a wife.

sa_verse: इमां विरूपामसती कराला निर्णतोदरीम्। अनेन सह ते भ्रात्रा भक्षयिष्यामि मानुषीम्॥
en: This unsightly, grim unchaste and human one with me, with a lean abdomen, will I devour up, along with this brother of yours.

sa_verse: ततः पर्वतशृङ्गाणि वनानि विविधानि च। पश्यन् सह मया कामी दण्डकान् विचरिष्यसि॥
en: Then having your wish, in company you will range the Daņdakas, surveying the various mountain summits and forests.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त: काकुत्स्थः प्रहस्य मदिरेक्षणाम्। इदं वचनमारेभे वक्तुं वाक्यविशारदः॥
en: Thus addressed, Kākutstha having charming eyes, well skilled in speech, with a smile, began to speak thus. with me, with a lean abdomen, will I devour up, along with this brother of yours.

sa_verse: ततः पर्वतशृङ्गाणि वनानि विविधानि च। पश्यन् सह मया कामी दण्डकान् विचरिष्यसि॥
en: Then having your wish, in company you will range the Daņdakas, surveying the various mountain summits and forests.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त: काकुत्स्थः प्रहस्य मदिरेक्षणाम्। इदं वचनमारेभे वक्तुं वाक्यविशारदः॥
en: Thus addressed, Kākutstha having charming eyes, well skilled in speech, with a smile, began to speak thus.

sa_verse: तां तु शूर्पणखां रामः कामपाशावपाशिताम्। स्वेच्छया श्लक्ष्णया वाचा स्मितपूर्वमथाब्रवीत्॥
en: With a smile, Rāma jestingly addressed Śūrpanakhā who had been ensnared in the noose of love, in soft words.

sa_verse: कृतदारोऽसि भवती भार्येयं दयिता मम। त्वद्विधानां तु नारीणां सुदु:खा ससपत्नता॥
en: Gentle one, I am already wedded; this is my beloved wife. To females like to you co-wifehood is exceedingly miserable.

sa_verse: अनुजस्त्वेष मे भ्राता शीलवान् प्रियदर्शनः। श्रीमानकृतदारश्च लक्ष्मणो नाम वीर्यवान्॥ अपूर्वी भार्यया चार्थी तरुणः प्रियदर्शनः। अनुरूपश्च ते भर्ता रूपस्यास्य भविष्यति॥
en: This young brother of mine is good-looking, and is of an excellent character; he is graceful, and is still unwived. The powerful one is named Laksmana. He has not yet tested the pleasures of a wife's company, and is desirous of having a spouse. And he is youthful and of an inviting presence. He will become a fit husband for you, considering this your beauty.

sa_verse: एनं भज विशालाक्षि भर्तारं भ्रातरं मम। असपत्ना वरारोहे मेरुमर्कप्रभा यथा॥
en: O you of expansive eyes, seek this brother of mine as your husband, even as the solar beam seeks Meru. (By doing so), You O supremely beautiful damsel, will not have to fear a co-wife.

sa_verse: इति रामेण सा प्रोक्ता राक्षसी काममोहिता। विसृज्य रामं सहसा ततो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having been thus addressed by Rāma, the Rākṣasī intoxicated with lust, at once leaving Rāma, spoke to Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: अस्य रूपस्य ते युक्ता भार्याहं वरवर्णिनी। मया सह सुखं सर्वान् दण्डकान् विचरिष्यसि।७।।
en: I am fit to be your wife possessed of transcendental grace, having regard to this beauty of yours. And with me you will happily range these Dandakas.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु सौमित्री राक्षस्या वाक्यकोविदः। ततः शूपनखीं स्मित्वा लक्ष्मणो युक्तमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thus accosted by the Räkşasi, Sumitrā's son, Lakşmana versed in speech, with a smile appropriately observed to Śūrpanakhā,

sa_verse: कथं दासस्य मेदासी भार्या भवितुमिच्छसि। सोऽहमार्येण परवान् भ्रात्रा कमलवर्णिनी॥
en: Why wish you to be the servant of me who am a servant myself*? O lotus-hued one, I am dependant on my noble brother. The text is difficult to render literally:—Why wish you to be the female servant of me who am myself a male servant.

sa_verse: समृद्धार्थस्य सिद्धार्था मुदितामलवर्णिनि। आर्यस्य त्वं विशालाक्षि भार्या भव यवीयसी॥
en: O you of expansive eyes, be you securing the sumimum bonum, with a joyful heart, become, O superbly beautiful wench, the younger wife of the noble one of great good fortune.

sa_verse: एतां विरूपामसती कराला निर्णतोदरीम्। भार्यां वृद्धां परित्यज्य त्वामेवैष भजिष्यति॥
en: Renouncing this frightful, unchaste, hideous, old wife possessed of a lean abdomen; he will devote himself to you.

sa_verse: को हि रूपमिदं श्रेष्ठं संत्यज्य वरवर्णिनि। मानुषीषु वरारोहे कुर्याद् भावं विचक्षणः॥
en: O paragon among damsels, O supremely charming lady, what man possessed of discernment, passing by such grace, would bear affliction to a human female?

sa_verse: इति सा लक्ष्मणेनोक्ता कराला निर्णतोदरी। मन्यते तद्वचः सत्यं परिहासाविचक्षणा॥
en: Thus accosted by Lakşmaņa, that hideous one having a lean abdomen being incapable of understanding jest, took his words as true.

sa_verse: सा रामं पर्णशालायामुपविष्टं परंतपम्। सीतया सह दुर्धर्षमब्रवीत् काममोहिता॥
en: Thereupon as that subduer of enemies, the irrepressible Rāma, was seated in the cottage in company with Sītā, the Rākşasī, transported with lust, said to him.

sa_verse: इमां विरूपामसती कराला निर्णतोदरीम्। वृद्धां भार्यामवष्टभ्य न मां त्वं बहु मन्यसे॥
en: Forsaking this deformed, unchaste, horrible old wife of yours possessed of a lean abdomen, you do not regard me.

sa_verse: अद्येमां भक्षयिष्यामि पश्यतस्तव मानुषीम्। त्वया सह चरिष्यामि निःसपत्ना यथा सुखम्॥
en: To-day, you looking on, I shall eat up this human female. And I shall, rid of a co-wife, happily range with you.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा मृगशावाक्षीमलातसदृशेक्षणा। अभ्यगच्छत् सुसंक्रुद्धा महोल्का रोहिणीमिव ॥
en: Having said this, even as a mighty meteor strike Rohiņī, that one having eyes resembling live coals, waxing exceedingly enraged, rushed towards that (damsel) with the eyes of a doe.

sa_verse: तां मृत्युपाशप्रतिमामापतन्ती महाबलः। विगृह्य रामः कुपितस्ततो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereupon, hindering the Rākṣasi resembling the noose of death, as she was rushing on, the mighty Rāma, growing wroth said to Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: कुरैरनार्यैः सौमित्रे परिहासः कथंचन। न कार्यः पश्य वैदेहीं कथंचित् सौम्य जीवतीम्॥
en: O Saumitri (Sumitrā's son), what is the good of jesting with a base and wicked one? No use of doing it. Look, I amiable one, Vaidehi (Videha's daughter) is well nigh dead.

sa_verse: इमां विरूपामसतीमतिमत्तां महोदरीम्। राक्षसी पुरुषव्याघ्र विरूपयितुमर्हसि॥
en: It behoves you, O foremost of men, to deform this frightful, unchaste Rākşasī of a huge abdomen, transported with lust.

sa_verse: इत्युक्तो लक्ष्मणस्तस्या क्रुद्धो रामस्य पश्यतः। उद्धृत्य खङ्ग चिच्छेद कर्णनासे महाबलः॥
en: Thus desired the exceedingly strong Laksmana, fired with wrath, taking out his sword, in the sight of Rāma, cut off her nose and ears.

sa_verse: निकृत्तकर्णनासा तु विस्वरं सा विनद्य च। यथागतं प्रदुद्राव घोरा शूर्पणखा वनम्॥
en: Her ears and nose cut off, the terrible Śūrpanakhā, uttering frightful cries, fled a main into the forest whence she had come.

sa_verse: सा विरुपा महाघोरा राक्षसी शोणितोक्षिता। ननाद विविधान् नादान् यथा प्रावृषि तोयदः॥
en: And being deformed, and covered with gore, the dreadful Rākşasī sent up many a roar, like clouds rumbling in the rains.

sa_verse: सा विक्षरन्ती रुधिरं बहुधा घोरदर्शना। प्रगृह्य बाहू गर्जन्ती प्रविवेश महावनम्॥
en: And bleeding profusely, the grimvisaged Rākṣasī, lifting up her arms, and roaring, entered the mighty forest.

sa_verse: ततस्तु सा राक्षससङ्घसंवृतं खरं जनस्थानगतं विरूपिता। उपेत्या तं भ्रातरमुग्रतेजसं पपात भूमौ गगनाद् यथाशनिः॥
en: Then having been deformed, (the Rākşasī) approaching, her brother of fierce energy, Khara, come to Janasthāna, (seated surrounded by numbers of Raksasas), fell down to the earth, even as the thunderbolt bursts from the sky.

sa_verse: तां तथा पतितां दृष्ट्वा विरूपां शोणितोक्षिताम्। भगिनीं क्रोधसंतप्तः शरः पप्रच्छ राक्षसः॥
en: Seeing his sister deformed, and covered with blood, fallen on the ground, the Raksasa, Khara, fired with wrath asked her.

sa_verse: उत्तिष्ठ तावदाख्याहि प्रमोहं जहि सम्भ्रमम्। व्यक्तमाख्याहि केन त्वमेवंरूपा विरूपिता॥
en: Arise! Leave off fear and amazement. Tell me plainly by whom you have been thus deformed.

sa_verse: कः कृष्णसर्पमासीनमाशीविषमनागसम्। तुदत्यभिसमापनमङ्गुल्यग्रेण लीलया॥
en: Who is it that with his finger-end has by way of sport hart a black venomous snake that was innocent?

sa_verse: कालपाशं समासज्य कण्ठे मोहान बुध्यते। यस्त्वामद्य समासाद्य पीतवान् विषमुत्तमम्॥
en: He that coming in contact with you has to-day drunk virulent poison, winding a fatal noose round about his neck, know it not through ignorance.

sa_verse: बलविक्रमसम्पन्ना कामगा कामरूपिणी। इमामवस्थां नीता त्वं केनान्तकसमागता॥
en: Endowed with strength and prowess, ranging about at will, wearing shapes at pleasure, and resembling the destroyer himself, going to whom hast you come by this condition?

sa_verse: देवगन्धर्वभूतानामृषीणां च महात्मनाम्। कोऽयमेवं महावीर्यस्त्वां विरूपां चकार ह॥
en: Who among the gods, Gandharvas or creatures or the light souled saints is of such mighty energy as to have deformed you?

sa_verse: नहि पश्याम्यहं लोके यः कुर्यान्मम विप्रियम्। अमरेषु सहस्राक्षं महेन्द्रं पाकशासनम्।७।।
en: In this world I do not see him that would do me : wrong, not even the mighty Indra, the thousand eyed tamer of the demon Pāka.

sa_verse: अद्याहं मार्गणैः प्राणानादास्ये जीवितान्तगैः। सलिले क्षीरमासक्तं निष्पिबन्निव सारसः॥ निहतस्य मया संख्ये शरसंकृत्तमर्मणः। सफेनं रुधिरं कस्य मेदिनी पातुमिच्छति॥
en: a crane Even as afflicted with thirst appropriate milk mixed in water (leaving the latter out), so by means of fleet an lifedestroying shafts I will among the immortals take the life of that chastiser. The frothy blood of whom, having his marrow pierced (by me) and slain by me in battle, dose the Earth wish to drink?

sa_verse: कस्य पत्ररथाः कायान्मांसमुत्कृत्य संगताः। प्रहृष्टा भक्षयिष्यन्ति निहतस्य मया रणे॥
en: Tearing off flesh from the corpse of whom slain by me in conflict, shall the birds so joyfully feed on it?

sa_verse: तं न देवा न गन्धर्वा न पिशाचा न राक्षसाः। मयापकृष्टं कृपणं शक्तास्त्रातुं महाहवे॥
en: Him, whom I shall wrong mightily, neither the gods, nor the Gandharvas, nor the Piśācas, nor the Rākşasas, will be able to rescue in fierce encounter.

sa_verse: उपलभ्य शनैः संज्ञां तं मे शंसितुमर्हसि। येन त्वं दुर्विनीतेन वने विक्रम्य निर्जिता॥
en: Recovering by degrees your sense, it behoves you to tell me what individual it was of execrable character, who by his prowess, half humbled you in the forest.

sa_verse: इति भ्रातुर्वचः श्रुत्वा कुद्धस्य च विशेषतः। ततः शूर्पणखा वाक्यं सवाष्पमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Hearing these words of his brother, who was under the influence of extreme rage, Sūrpanakhā with tears in her eyes, said.

sa_verse: तरुणौ रूपसम्पनौ सुकुमारौ महाबलौ। पुण्डरीकविशालाक्षौ चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरौ॥ फलमूलाशनौ दान्तौ तापसौ ब्रह्मचारिणौ। पुत्रौ दशरथस्यास्तां भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ॥ गन्धर्वराजप्रतिमौ पार्थिवव्यञ्जनान्वितौ। देवौ वा दानवावेतौ न तर्कयितुमुत्सहे ॥
en: Tender, and endowed with beauty, possessed of youth and gifted with great strength, with expansive eyes resembling white lotuses; clad in bark and dark dear-skins subsisting on fruits and roots, having their senses under control; leading an ascetic life and a Brahmacarya mode of existence; there are two sons of Dasaratha the brothers Räma and Lakşmaņa resembling the Sovereign of the Gandharvas, and marked with signs betokening royalty. I cannot ascertain whether they be celestial, or human beings.

sa_verse: तरुणी रूपसम्पन्ना सर्वाभरणभूषिता। दृष्ट्वा तत्र मया नारी तयोर्मध्ये सुमध्यमा॥
en: And between them I saw there a youthful female furnished with grace, and having a dainty waist; adorned with every kind of ornament.

sa_verse: ताभ्यामुभाभ्यां सम्भूय प्रमदामधिकृत्य ताम्। इमामवस्थां नीताहं यथाऽनाथाऽसती तथा॥
en: And it is on account of this feinale that I have like one unchaste and uncared for, have come by this condition at their hands.

sa_verse: तस्याश्चानृजुवृत्तायास्तयोश्च हतयोरहम्। सफेनं पातुमिच्छामि रुधिरं रणमूर्धनि॥
en: Nor I wish in the midst of encounter to drink the frothy blood of that crooked one as well as those two slain (in battle).

sa_verse: एव मे प्रथमः कामः कृतस्तत्र त्वया भवेत्। तस्यास्तयोश्च रुधिरं पिबेयमहमाहवे॥
en: Let this my prime wish be crowned there with fruition. her and their blood will I drink in dreadful conflict.

sa_verse: इति तस्यां ब्रुवाणायां चतुर्दश महाबलान्। व्यादिदेश खरः क्रुद्धो राक्षसानन्तकोपमान्॥ मानुषौ शस्त्रसम्पन्नौ चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरौ। प्रविष्टौ दण्डकारण्यं घोरं प्रमदया सह ॥ तौ हत्वा तां च दुर्वृत्तामुपावर्तितुमर्हथ। इयं च भगिनी तेषां रुधिरं मम पास्यति ॥
en: As she said this, Khara wrought up with boundless passion, said, Two human beings accoutred in arins, and wearing bark and dark deer-skins have in company with a female entered this deep Dandaka forest. Do you returni, after slaying them, as well as that wicked one. And this sister of mine will drink their blood.

sa_verse: मनोरथोऽयमिष्टोऽस्या भगिन्या मम राक्षसाः। शीघ्रं सम्पाद्यतां गत्वा तौ प्रमथ्य स्वतेजसा ॥
en: You Rākşasas, even this is my sister's dear desire. Repairing thither, specdily accomplish this, bearing them down by your native might.

sa_verse: युष्माभिनिहतौ दृष्ट्वा ताबुभौ भ्रातरौ रणे। इयं प्रहृष्टा मुदिता रुधिरं युधि पास्यति ॥
en: Seeing those two brothers slain by you this one exceedingly rejoiced, will drink their blood in the field.

sa_verse: इति प्रतिसमादिष्टा राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। तत्र जग्मुस्तया सार्धे घना वातेरिता इव ॥
en: Thus commissioned, those fourteen Räksasas set out there like clouds driven by the winds, accompanied by Śürpanakhā.

sa_verse: ततः शूर्पणखा घोरा राघवाश्रममागता। राक्षसानाचचक्षे तौ भ्रातरौ सह सीतया॥
en: Then the grim Śūrpanakhā, coming to Rāghava's asylum pointed out to the Rākşasas those brother in company with Sītā.

sa_verse: ते रामं पर्णशालायामुपविष्टं महाबलम्। ददृशुः सीतया सार्धं लक्ष्मणेनापि सेवितम्॥
en: And they saw the mighty Rāma seated in the cottage in company with Sītā, and ministered to by Laksmana.

sa_verse: तां दृष्ट्वा राघवः श्रीमानागतांस्तांश्च राक्षसान्। अब्रवीद् भ्रातरं रामो लक्ष्मणं दीप्ततेजसम्॥
en: And seeing her, as well as the Rākşasas who had come, Rāma remarked to his brother, Lakşınaņa of flaming energy.

sa_verse: मुहूर्ते भव सौमित्रे सीतायाः प्रत्यनन्तरः। इमानस्या वधिष्यामि पदवीमागतानिह॥
en: O Sumitrā's son, do you for a while guard Sītā. I shall slay these that have come to this asylum here.

sa_verse: वाक्यमेतत् ततः श्रुत्वा रामस्य विदितात्मनः। तथेति लक्ष्मणो वाक्यं राघवस्य प्रपूजयन्।।
en: Hearing these words of Rāma versed in the knowledge of self, Lakşmaņa honoured his speech by saying, So be it.

sa_verse: राघवोऽपि महच्चापं चामीकरविभूषितम्। चकार सज्यं धर्मात्मा तानि रक्षांसि चाब्रवीत्॥
en: And the righteous Rāghava strung his mighty bow decked with gold; and addressed the Raksasas.

sa_verse: पुत्रौ दशरथस्यावां भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ। प्रविष्टौ सीतया सार्धं दुश्चरं दण्डकावनम्॥ फलमूलाशनौ दान्तौ तापसौ ब्रह्मचारिणौ। वसन्तौ दण्डकारण्ये किमर्थमुपहिंसथ॥
en: We that have entered into this untraceable forest of Dandaka in company with Sītā are the sons of Dasaratha, the brothers, Rama and Lakşmana. Why do you intend to do injury to us. Subsisting on fruits and roots, having our senses under control, carrying on asceticism and leading a Brahmacarya life, we are passing our days in the forest of Dandaka.

sa_verse: युष्मान् पापात्मकान् हन्तुं विप्रकारान् महाहवे। ऋषीणां तु नियोगेन सम्प्राप्तः सशरासनः॥
en: It is do destroy you, wicked, and troublesome that I equipped with the bow have come here at the desire of the saints.

sa_verse: तिष्ठतैवात्र संतुष्टा नोपवर्तितुमर्हथ। यदि प्राणैरिहार्थो वो निवर्तघ्नाः॥
en: Stay there content you should not move further. If you have a care for your lives, desist, you rangers of the night.

sa_verse: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। ऊचुर्वाचं सुसंक्रुद्धा ब्रह्मघ्नाः शूलपाणयः॥ संरक्तनयना घोरा रामं संरक्तलोचनम्। परुषा मधुराभाषं हृष्टा दृष्टपराक्रमम्॥
en: Hearing those words of his, those fourteen grim Rākşasas bearing darts in their hands, exceedingly enraged, with their eyes reddened, breathing high spirits, harshly said to the sweetspeeched Rāma, having his eyes reddened whose prowess they had not seen before.

sa_verse: क्रोधमुत्पाद्य नो भर्तुः खरस्य सुमहात्मनः। त्वमेव हास्यसे प्राणान् सद्योऽस्माभिर्हतो युधि ॥
en: Having excited there our lord. the high-souled Khara, it is you that shal: lose your life no later than this day, being slain by us in battle.

sa_verse: का हि ते शक्तिरेकस्य बहूनां रणमूर्धनि । अस्माकमग्रतः स्थातुं किं पुनर्यो माहवे॥
en: What power hast you, being one to stay before us many in the field, what shall I say of your coping with us in conflict?

sa_verse: एभिर्बाहुप्रयुक्तैश्च परिघैः शूलपट्टिशैः। प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यसि वीर्यं च धनुश्च करपीडितम्॥
en: In consequence of the parighas (iron-clubs), darts, and pattisas (a kind of sharp-edged sword) discharged by our arms, you will surely lose your life, along with your prowess, and this bow which you press with your hand.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा संरब्धा राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। उद्यतायुधनिस्त्रिंशा राममेवाभिदुद्रुवुः॥
en: Having said this in wrath, those fourteen Rākşasas, uplifting their weapons and daggers rushed towards Rāma.

sa_verse: चिक्षिपुस्तानि शूलानि राघवं प्रति दुर्जयम्। तानि शूलानि काकुत्स्थः समस्तानि चतुर्दश॥
en: And discharged darts at the invincible Rāghava. Thereupon Kākutstha by means of shafts decked with gold, cut off those fourteen darts of theirs.

sa_verse: तावद्भिरेव चिच्छेद शरैः काञ्चनभूषितैः। ततः पश्यान्महातेजा नाराचान् सूर्यसंनिभान्॥ जग्राह परमकुद्धश्चतुर्दश शिलाशितान्। गृहीत्वा धनुरायम्य लक्ष्यानुद्दिश्य राक्षसान्॥ मुमोच राघवो बाणान् वज्रानिव शतक्रतुः।
en: Seeing this, that exceedingly cnergetic one, waxing highly enraged, look out fourteen darts with many golden arrows, whetted on stone, resembling the sun. And even as Satakratu hurls the thunder bolt, Rāghava drawing his bow and taking his aim at the Rākşasas, let go those shafts.

sa_verse: ते भित्वा रक्षसां वेगाद् वक्षांसि रुधिरप्लुताः॥ विनिष्पेतुस्तदा भूमौ वल्मीकादिव पन्नगाः।
en: And those Rākşasas, their breasts pierced with vehemence, and themselves bathed in blood, fell down to the carth, like snakes dropping down from an ant hill.

sa_verse: तैर्भग्नहदया भूमौ छिन्नमूला इव द्रुमाः॥ निपेतुः शोणितस्नाता विकृता विगतासवः।
en: And with their breasts pierced, they, dripping with blood, deformed and deprived of their lives, lay on the ground like trees whose roots have been severed.

sa_verse: तान् भूमौ पतितान् दृष्ट्वा राक्षसी क्रोधमूछिताः॥ उपगम्य खरं सा तु किंचित्संशुष्कशोणिता। पपात पुनरेवार्ता सनिर्यासेव वल्लरी॥
en: Seeing them fallen-on the ground, the Rākşasī, beyond herself in wrath, approaching Khara, with her blood a little dried up, again in distressful guise threw herself on the earth, like a plant exuding gum.

sa_verse: भ्रातुः समीपे शोकार्ता ससर्ज निनदं महत्। सस्वरं मुमुचे वाष्पं विवर्णवदना तदा॥
en: And in presence of her brother, she set up a mighty roar; and then her face grown pallid, she dropped tears, emitting cries.

sa_verse: स पुनः पतितां दृष्ट्वा क्रोधाच्छूर्पणखां पुनः। उवाच व्यक्तया वाचा तामनर्थार्थमागताम्॥
en: Seeing Sūrpanakhă again lying on the ground, Khara in anger spoke out to that one, who had come to bring on evil.

sa_verse: मया त्विदानी शूरास्ते राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः। त्वत्प्रियार्थं विनिर्दिष्टाः किमर्थं रुद्यते पुनः॥
en: I had but recently commissioned for your behoof those heroic Rākşasas living on flesh. Why then do you again weep?

sa_verse: भक्ताश्चैवानुरक्ताश्च हिताश्च मम नित्यशः। हन्यमाना न हन्यन्ते न न कुर्युर्वचो मम॥
en: Bearing regard and attached to me, and ever engaged in my welfare, they, assailed by others, are incapable of being slain; nor do they not obey my orders.

sa_verse: किमेतच्छ्रोतुमिच्छामि कारणं यत्कृते पुनः। हा नाथेति विनर्दन्ती सर्पवच्चेष्टसे क्षितौ॥
en: What can it be? I would hear of the occasion owing to which again, crying ‘Ah lord,' you roll on the earth like a serpent.

sa_verse: अनाथवद् विलपसि किं नु नाथे मयि स्थिते। उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ मा मैवं वैल्कव्यं त्यज्यतामिति ॥
en: Why do you be weep like one forlorn, while I your lord, am living? Arise! Arise! Do not fear, cast off your stupor.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्ता दुर्धर्षा खरेण परिसान्त्विता। विमृज्य नयने सारे खरं भ्रातरमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thus addressed, and consoled by Khara, that irrepressible one, wiping her eyes, spoke to Khara.

sa_verse: अस्मीदानीमहं प्राप्ता हतश्रवणनासिका। शोणितौघपरिक्लिन्ना त्वया च परिसान्त्विता।७।।
en: Having had my nose and ears cut off, I had come hither, covered with blood; and you had consoled me.

sa_verse: प्रेषिताश्च त्वया शूरा राक्षसास्ते चतुर्दश। निहन्तुं राघवं घोरं मत्प्रियार्थं सलक्ष्मणम्॥ ते तु रामेण सामर्षाः शूलपट्टिशपाणयः। समरे निहताः सर्वे सायकैमर्मभेदिभिः॥
en: And you also had despatched fourteen heroic Rākṣasas, for my hehoof, for slaying the terrific Rāghava along with Lakşmaņa. But all those, bearing ill will (towards Rāma), bearing darts and pattiśas in their hands, have been slain in conflict by means of weapons penetrating into the vitals.

sa_verse: तान् भूमौ पतितान् दृष्ट्वा क्षणेनैव महाजवान्। रामस्य च महत्कर्म महांस्त्रासोऽभवन्मम॥
en: Seeing those possessed of great speed, in a moment laid low on the ground, as well as (witnessing) Rāma's mighty deed, great fear has taken possession of me.

sa_verse: सास्मि भीता समुद्विग्ना विषण्णा च निशाचर। शरणं त्वं पुनः प्राप्ता सर्वतो भयदर्शिनी॥ विषादनक्राध्युषिते परित्रासोर्मिमालिनि। किं मां न त्रायसे मग्नां विपुले शोकसागरे॥
en: O ranger of the night, I am afraid, and agitated, and cast down. I have (at length) found in you a refuge, seeing fear on all sides. Will you not rescue me who am sinking in this ocean of grief, having sorrow for its alligators and affright for billows?

sa_verse: एते च निहता भूमौ रामेण निशितैः शरैः। ये च मे पदवी प्राप्ता राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः॥
en: And these Rākşasas, living on flesh who had arrived at that place, have been slain by Rāma with sharpened shafts.
