sa_verse: मयि ते यद्यनुक्रोशो यदि रक्षःसु तेषु च। रामेण यदि शक्तिस्ते तेजो वास्ति निशाचर॥ दण्डकारण्यनिलयं जहि राक्षसकण्टकम्।
en: If you have any kindness for me as well as those sons of Rākşasas* and, O ranger of night, if you have strength and energy to cope with Rāma, do you kill this thorn of the Rákşasas, who has set up his dwelling in the forest of Dandaka. Another reading is that enemy of mine.

sa_verse: यदि रामममित्रघ्नं न त्वमद्य वधिष्यसि ॥ तव चैवाग्रतः प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यामि निरपत्रपा।
en: If you do not to-day slay that destroyer of foes, Rāma, I shall banishing shame, give up my life in your very presence.

sa_verse: बुद्ध्याहमनुपश्यामि न त्वं रामस्य संयुगे॥ स्थातुं प्रतिमुखे शक्तः सबलोऽपि महारणे।
en: Meseems, you backed by your forecast are incapable of staying in battle before Rāma in high encounter. Some texts-(Rāma) equipped with the bow.

sa_verse: शूरमानीं न शूरस्त्वं मिथ्यारोपितविक्रमः॥ अपयाहि जनस्थानात् त्वरितः सहबान्धवः। जहि त्वं समरे मूढान्यथा तु कुलपांसन॥ मानुषौ तौ न शक्नोसि हन्तुं वै रामलक्ष्मणौ। निःसत्त्वस्याल्पवीर्यस्य वासस्ते कीदृशस्त्विह॥
en: You plume yourself on being a hero; but are really none such. Prowess has been falsely attributed to you. Go off from Janasthāna without delay, along with your friends. O stainer of your line, do you in battle slay those foolhardy ones. But if you fail in slaying those human beings, Rama and Laksmana, then how can you void of strength and of slender prowess, stay here?

sa_verse: रामतेजोऽभिभूतो हि त्वं क्षिप्रं विनशिष्यसि। स हि तेजः समायुक्तो रामो दशरथात्मजः॥ भ्राता चास्य महावीर्यो येन चास्मि विरूपिता।
en: Overcome by the energy of Rāma, you shall speedily meet with destruction Dasaratha's son, Rāma, you shall speedily meet with destruction. Dasaratha's son, is endued with energy. His brother is possessed of high vigour by whom I have come to be deformed.

sa_verse: एवं विलप्य बहुशो राक्षसी प्रदरोदरी॥ भ्रातुः समीपे शोकार्ता नष्टसंज्ञा बभूव ह। कराभ्यामुदरं हत्वा रुरोद भृशदुःखिता॥
en: Having thus bewailed long, that Raksasi, possessed of a spacious abdomen, through sorrow, became bereft of her senses before her brother, and exercised with great grief cried, striking her abdomen with her hands.

sa_verse: एवमाधर्षितः शूरः शूर्पनख्या खरस्ततः। उवाच रक्षसां मध्ये खरः खरतरं वचः॥
en: On being thus taunted by Śūrpanakha, Khara spoke these sharp words in the midst of the Raksasas.

sa_verse: तवापमानप्रभवः क्रोधोऽयमतुलो मम। न शक्यते धारयितुं लवणाम्भ इवोल्बणम्॥
en: Arising from this your humiliating censure, my wrath is beyond compare. I can not bear it, like salt-water cast on a sore.

sa_verse: न रामं गणये वीर्यान्मानुषं क्षीणजीवितम्। आत्मदुश्चरितैः प्राणान् हतो योऽद्य विमोक्ष्यते॥
en: From my prowess I count not Rāma who is a human being possessed of a frail life him who slain by me shall to-day in consequence of his misdeed give up existence.

sa_verse: वाष्पः संधार्यतामेष सम्भ्रमश्च विमुच्यताम्। अहं रामं सह भ्रात्रा नयामि यमसादनम्॥
en: Restrain your tears, and remove your fear. I shall despatch a Rāma along with his brother to the abode of Yama.

sa_verse: परश्वधहतस्याद्य मन्दप्राणस्य भूतले। रामस्य रुधिरं रक्तमुष्णं पास्यसि राक्षसि ॥
en: You shall O Rākşasī, quaff on the ground the hot blood of Rāma of evil life, slain by my axe.

sa_verse: सम्प्रहृष्टा वचः श्रुत्वा खरस्य वदनाच्च्युतम्। प्रशशंस पुनर्मोाद् भ्रातरं रक्षसां वरम्॥
en: Overjoyed on hearing the words that dropped from Khara's mouth, she again through fickleness extolled that foremost of Rākşasas, her brother.

sa_verse: तया परुषितः पूर्वं पुनरेव प्रशंसितः। अब्रवीद् दूषणं नाम खरः सेनापति तदा॥
en: At first reprimanded by her and then praised, Khara spoke to is general, named Dusana.

sa_verse: चतुर्दश सहस्राणि मम चित्तानुवर्तिनाम्। रक्षसां भीमवेगानां समरेष्वनिवर्तिनाम्॥ नीलजीमूतवर्णानां लोकहिंसाविहारिणाम्। सर्वोद्योगमुदीर्णानां रक्षसां सौम्य कारय॥
en: Do you, O gentle one, array fourteen thousand of those those Rākṣasas, gifted with furious vehemence, ever following my wish, who never turn away froin the field; who are hued like to purple clouds, who revel in cruelty*, and who are elated (in consequence of their prowess). *The North-West Province's text has some additional sloka:-Dreadful, wearing forms at will, possessing the pride of the lion, having large mouth, high-souled, revelling in cruelty, endued with strength, of fierce energy.

sa_verse: उपस्थापय मे क्षिप्रं रथं सौम्य धनूंषि च। शरांश्च चित्रान् खङ्गांश्च शक्तीश्च विविधाः शिताः॥
en: And you gentle one, do you at once bring my car as well as my bows, and pasty coloured arrows and scimitars, and darts, and various whetted javelins.

sa_verse: अग्रे निर्यातुमिच्छामि पौलस्त्यानां महात्मनाम्। वधार्थं दुर्विनीतस्य रामस्य रणकोविद ॥
en: O you versed in warfare, for slaying this haughty Rāma, I go in the very fore-front of the high-souled Paulastyas. Lit.-Pulastya's The Rākşasas commonly descended from Pulastya.

sa_verse: इति तस्य ब्रुवाणस्य सूर्यवर्णं महारथम्। सदश्वैः शबलैर्युक्तमाचचक्षेऽथ दूषणः॥
en: As he said this, Dūşaņa brought his great car hued like the sun, yoked with excellent steeds of various colours. sons. were

sa_verse: तं मेरुशिखराकारं तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम्। हेमंचक्रमसम्बाधं वैदूर्यमयकूवरम्॥ मत्स्यैः पुष्पैर्दुमैः शैलैश्चन्द्रकान्तैश्च काञ्चनैः। माङ्गल्यैः पक्षिसङ्घश्च ताराभिश्च समावृतम्॥ ध्वजनिस्त्रिशसम्पन्न किङ्किणीवरभूषितम्। सदश्वयुक्तं सोऽमर्षादारुरोह खरस्तदा॥
en: Thereupon in a passion Khara ascended that car resembling a peak of Meru, embellished with burnished-gold, furnished with golden wheels, open; having its pole studded with lapises; surrounded with fishes and flowers and trees and stones, and the sun and the moon and gold, and auspicious articles; and swarms of birds, and stars; having streamers and swords; garnished with bells; and yoked with superb coursers.

sa_verse: खरस्तु तन्महत्सैन्यं रथचर्मायुधध्वजम्। निर्यातेत्यब्रवीत् प्रेक्ष्य दूषणः सर्वराक्षसान्॥
en: Beholding that mighty army consisting of cars and arms and pennons, Khara as well as Dūşana, said to that multitude of Rākşasas, March forth!

sa_verse: ततस्तद् राक्षसं सैन्यं घोरचर्मायुधध्वजम्। निर्जगाम जनस्थानान्माहानादं महाजवम्॥ मुद्वरैः पट्टिशैः शूलैः सुतीक्ष्णैश्च परश्वधैः। खङ्गैश्चक्रैश्च हस्तस्थैभ्राजमानैः सतोमरैः॥ शक्तिभिः परिधैोरैरतिमात्रैश्च कार्मुकैः। गदासिमुसलैर्वज्रगृहीतैर्भीमदर्शनैः॥ राक्षसानां सुघोराणां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश । निर्यातानि जनस्थानात् खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम्॥
en: Then uttering tremendous roars, rushed out with great vehemence four and ten thousand of those terrible Rākşasas equipped with clubs and parțiśas and darts and sharpened axes, and scimitars and discuses, shining beautifully in their hands and saktis and dreadful parighas and innumerable bows, and maces, and musalas and vajras fearful to behold, griped fast. And those following the will of Khara issued out of Janasthāna.

sa_verse: सांस्तु निर्धावतो दृष्ट्वा राक्षसान् भीमदर्शनान्। खरस्याथ रथः किंचिज्जगाम तदनन्तरम्॥
en: Seeing those Rākṣasas of dreadful forms rushing out, Khara's car remaining a little away (from the press), proceeded after a short space.

sa_verse: ततस्ताञ्छबलानश्वांस्तप्तकाञ्चनभूषितान्। खरस्य मतमाज्ञाय सारथिः पर्यचोदयत्॥
en: Then taking the perinission of Khara, the charioteer drove at speed those powerful steeds decked in shining gold.

sa_verse: संचोदितो रथः शीघ्रं खरस्य रिपुघातिनः। शब्देनापूरयामास दिशः सप्रदिशस्तथा॥
en: And driven with speed, the car of Khara. slayer of foes, filled all directions with its rattle.

sa_verse: प्रवृद्धमन्युस्तु खरः खरस्वरो रिपोर्वधार्थ त्वरितो यथान्तकः। महाबलो मेघ इवाश्मवर्षवान्॥
en: Khara, fired with wrath, and having piercing voice, hastening to destroy his enemy like the destroyer himself, again urged his charioteer with roars, like a mighty cloud showering down stones.

sa_verse: तत्प्रयातं बलं घोरमशिवं शोणितोदकम्। अभ्यवर्षन्महागोरस्तुमुलो गर्दवारुणः॥
en: As those terrible and inauspicious forces were marching on, a mighty sable cloud with dreadful sounds began to shower down blood.

sa_verse: निपेतुस्तुरगास्तस्य रथयुक्ता महाजवाः। समे पुष्पचिते देशे राजमार्गे यदृच्छया॥
en: The steeds yoked to his (Khara's) car, gifted with great celerity, dropped down all of a sudden on the level high ways strewn with blossoms.

sa_verse: श्यामं रुधिरपर्यन्तं बभूव परिवेषणम्। अलातचक्रप्रतिम प्रतिगृह्य दिवाकरम् ॥
en: A dark circle bordered with red, resembling that of charcoal, appeared in the vicinity of the sun.

sa_verse: ततो ध्वजमुपागम्य हेमदण्डं समुच्छ्रितम्। समाक्रम्य महाकायस्तस्थौ गृध्रः सुदारुणः॥
en: And a frightful vulture, having a huge body, approaching the banner, sat upon the upraised golden flag-staff.

sa_verse: जनस्थानसमीपे च समाक्रम्य खरस्वनाः। विस्वरान् विविधान् नादान् मांसादा मृगपक्षिणः।।।। व्याजहुरभिदीप्तायां दिशि वै भैरवस्वनम्। अशिवं यातुधानानां शिवा घोरा महास्वनाः॥
en: And beasts and birds having shrill voices, living on flesh, remaining near Janasthāna, emitted various dissonant notes. And in the quarter lighted up by the sun, fierce jackals having mighty voices, in dreadful cries presaged evil to the Rākşasas.

sa_verse: प्रभिन्नगजसंकाशास्तोयशोणितधारिणः। आकाशं तदनाकाशं चक्रुभीमाम्बुवाहकाः॥
en: And terrific clouds surcharged with water and blood, and resembling elephants with rent temples, enveloped the sky.

sa_verse: बभूव तिमिरं घोरमुद्धतं रोमहर्षणम्। दिशो वा प्रादिशो वापि सुव्यक्तं न चकाशिरे॥
en: And a dense darkness appeared, capable of making one's hair stand on end, and no quarter was distinctly visible.

sa_verse: क्षतजासवर्णाभा संध्या कालं बिना बभौ। खरं चाभिमुखं नेदुस्तदा घोरा मृगाः खगाः॥
en: And out of season, there appeared evening hued like a cloth soaked in blood. And facing Khara, terrible beasts and birds set up cries.

sa_verse: कङ्कगोमायुगृध्राश्च चुक्रुशुर्भयशंसिनः। नित्याशिवकरा युद्धे शिवा घोरनिदर्शनाः॥ नेदुर्बलस्याभिमुखं ज्वालोद्गारिभिराननैः।
en: Kankas, Gomayas and vultures, portending fear, began to cry. Ever inauspicious in war, jackals presaging evil, with their mouths belching forth flame, howled in the face of the forces.

sa_verse: कबन्धः परिघाभासो दृश्यते भास्करान्तिके॥ जग्राह सूर्यं स्वर्भानुरपर्वणि महाग्रहः। प्रवाति मारुतः शीघ्रं निष्प्रभौऽभूद् दिवाकरः॥
en: And comets, resembling parighas appeared beside the sun. That mighty planet Svarbhānu seized the sun when there was no parva. And the winds blew violently; and the sun was without lustre.

sa_verse: उपेतुश्च विना रात्रिं ताराः खद्योतसप्रभाः। संलीनमीनविहगा नलिन्यः शुष्कपङ्कजाः॥
en: And when there was no night, stars wearing the sheen of fireflies, began to fall. And in the tanks fishes and fowls were inert, and the lotuses were withered.

sa_verse: तस्मिन् क्षणे बभूवुश्च विना पुष्पफलैर्दुमाः। उद्धृतश्च विना वातं रेणुर्जलधरारुणः॥
en: And at that hour the trees were shorn of fruits and flowers; and without wind there arose dust dusky like clouds.

sa_verse: चीचीकूचीति वास्यन्त्यो बभूवुस्तत्र सारिकाः। उल्काश्चापि सनिर्घोषा निपेतुर्पोरदर्शनाः॥
en: And the parrots uttered wild notes-cīcīkūci. And meteors of terrific aspects fell with loud noises.

sa_verse: प्रचचाल मही चापि सशैलवनकानना। खरस्य च रथस्थस्य नर्दमानस्य धीमतः॥
en: And the earth with her mountains, woods, and forests, shook. And as the intelligent Khara was roaring from his car, his left arm shook, and his voice fell, and as he surveyed around, his eyes moistened, and his head ached; yet through ignorance, he did not desist.

sa_verse: प्राकम्पत भुजः सव्यः स्वरश्चास्यावसज्जत। सास्रा सम्पद्यते दृष्टिः पश्यमानस्य सर्वतः॥ ललाटे च रुजो जाता न च मोहान्न्यवर्तत। तान् समीक्ष्य महोत्पातानुत्थितान् रोमहर्षणान्॥ अब्रवीद् राक्षसान् सर्वान् प्रहसन् स खरस्तदा।
en: Witnessing these mighty portents capable of making one's hair stand on end, Khara with a laugh, addressed the whole body of Rākşasas.

sa_verse: महोत्पातानिमान् सर्वानुत्थितान् घोरदर्शनान् ॥ न चिन्तयाम्यहं वीर्याद् बलवान् दुर्बलानिव। तारा अपि शरैस्तीक्ष्णैः पातयेयं नभस्तलात्॥ मृत्यु मरणधर्मेण संक्रुद्धो योजयाम्यहम्। राघवं तं बलोत्सिक्तं भ्रातरं चापि लक्ष्मणम्॥ अहत्वा सायकैस्तीक्ष्णैर्नोपावर्तितुमुत्सहे।
en: Even as a strong person from prowess counts not a weak one, I do not dwell upon all these mighty portents, dreadful to behold, that have appeared. Even the stars will I bring down from heaven with my sharp shafts; and even Death will I engaged, bring to mortality. Without slaying Rāghava puffed up with pride as well as Laksmana, by means of sharp weapons, I return not (from the conflict).

sa_verse: यन्निमित्तं तु रामस्य लक्ष्मणस्य विपर्ययः॥ सकामा भगिनी मेऽस्तु पीत्वा तु रुधिरं तयोः।
en: Let that sister of mine, for whom Rāma and Lakṣmaṇa have had sense perverted, attain her desire, by drinking their blood..

sa_verse: न क्वचित् प्राप्तपूर्वो मे संयुगेषु पराजयः॥
en: I had never before met with defeat in conflict. You have witnessed it. I do not speak a falsehood.

sa_verse: देवराजमपि क्रुद्धो मत्तैरावतगामिनम्॥ वज्रहस्तं रणे हन्यां किं पुनस्तौ च मानवौ।
en: Wrought up with rage, I shall slay in fight the sovereign of the celestials himself, going on his mad Airāvata, with the thunderbolt in his hand, what shall I say of those two, who are human beings?

sa_verse: सा तस्य गर्जितं श्रुत्वा राक्षसानां महाचमूः॥ प्रहर्षमतुलं लेभे मृत्युपाशावपाशिता।
en: Entangled in the noose of death, that mighty army of the Rākşasas hearing his challenge, experienced boundless enthusiasm.

sa_verse: समेयुश्च महात्मानो युद्धदर्शनकाक्षिणः॥ ऋषयो देवगन्धर्वाः सिद्धाश्च सह चारणैः। समेत्य चोचुः सहितास्तेऽन्योन्यं पुण्यकर्मणः॥
en: And anxious to see the encounter, there came high-souled saints, and celestials, and Gandharvas, and Siddhas, with the Cāranas. And these pious ones assembled, spoke to one another.

sa_verse: स्वस्ति गोब्राह्मणेभ्यस्तु लोकानां ये च सम्मताः। जयतां राघवो युद्धे पौलस्त्यान् रजनीचरान्॥ चक्रहस्ते यथा विष्णुः सर्वानसुरसत्तमान् ।
en: Welfare to those cows, and Brāhmanas, and those that are prized by the worlds! As the discus-handed Vişņu vanquished the foremost Asuras, may Rāghava rout in battle those rangers of the night, the progeny of Pulastya!

sa_verse: एतश्चान्यच्च बहुशो ब्रुवाणाः परमर्षय: 1॥ जातकौतूहलास्तत्र विमानस्ताश्च देवताः। ददृशुर्वाहिनीं तेषां राक्षसानां गतायुषाम्॥
en: And saying this as well as various other things, the supreme saints and the celestials stationed in the sky conceiving curiosity (as to the issue of the conflict), beheld the host of those Rākşasas, whose days had been numbered.

sa_verse: महाकपालः स्थूलाक्षः प्रमाथस्त्रिशिरास्तथा। चत्वार एते सेनाने दूषणं पृष्ठतोऽन्वयुः॥
en: And Mahākapāla, Sthūlākṣa, Pramātha and Trisirā—These four going before the forces, went at the back of Dūşaņa.

sa_verse: सा भीमवेगा समराभिकाङ्गिणी सुदारुणा राक्षसवीरसेना। तौ राजपुत्रौ सहसाभ्युपेता माला ग्रहाणामिव चन्द्रसूर्यो।।
en: Then as the planets dart towards the sun and moon, that heroic and dreadful army of Rākşasas, eager for victory suddenly rushed towards the princes with great vehemence.

sa_verse: आश्रमं प्रतियाते तु खरे खरपराक्रमे। तानेवौत्पातिकान् रामः सह भ्रात्रा ददर्श ह॥
en: When Khara of fierce prowess had come to the asylum Rāma in company with his brother saw all those evil prognostics.

sa_verse: तानुत्पातान् महाघोरान् रामो दृष्ट्वात्यमर्षणः। प्रजानामहितान् दृष्ट्वा वाक्यं लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥
en: And beholding those dreadful portents, Rāma exceedingly distressed, apprehending some calamity to the Rākşasas, observed to Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: इमान् पश्य महाबाहो सर्वभूतापहारिणः। समुत्थितान् महोत्पातान् संहर्तुं सर्वराक्षसान्॥
en: O mighty armed one, behold these great presages that have taken place, capable of annihilating all beings, and which have taken place, capable of annihilating all beings, and which have for their object the utter extermination of the Raksasas!

sa_verse: अमी रुधिरधारास्तु विसृजन्ते खरस्वनाः। व्योम्नि मेघा निवर्तन्ते पुरुषा गर्दभारुणाः॥
en: Yonder threatening clouds of asinine sable, showering down blood and uttering loud sounds are ranging the welkin.

sa_verse: सधूमाश्च शराः सर्वे मम युद्धाभिनन्दिताः। रुक्मपृष्ठानि चापानि विचेष्टन्ते विचक्षण ॥
en: And, O discerning one, rejoicing at the prospect of my fight, all these arrows emit smoke, and my bows plaited on the back with gold, are restless.

sa_verse: यादृशा इह कूजन्ति पक्षिणो वनचारिणः। अग्रतो नोऽभयं प्राप्त संशयो जीवितस्य च ॥
en: Meseems from the noise of the wild birds that impending on us is danger and uncertainty to life.

sa_verse: सम्प्रहारस्तु सुमहान् भविष्यति न संशयः। अयमाख्याति मे बाहुः स्फुरमाणो मुहुर्मुहुः॥ संनिकर्षे तु नः शूर जयं शत्रोः पराजयम्। सुप्रभं च प्रसन्नं च तव वक्त्रं हि लक्ष्यते॥
en: Without doubt, there shall take place a mighty conflict. At this critical time, my arin shaking momentarily, betokens, O hero, victory to us, and defeat to the enemy. And your face appear pleasant with a delightful lustre.

sa_verse: उद्यतानां हि युद्धार्थं येषां भवति लक्ष्मण। निष्प्रभं वदनं तेषां भवत्यायुः परिक्षयः॥
en: O Lakşmaņa, the pallid face of those that prepare for conflict, auger shortening of life.

sa_verse: रक्षसां नर्दतां घोरः श्रूयतेऽयं महाध्वनिः। आहतानां च भेरीणां राक्षसैः क्रूरकर्मभिः॥
en: We can hear the shouts of the Rākşasas as they roar, as also the blasts of the trumpets of those doomed to be wounded blown by Rākṣasas of remorseless deeds.

sa_verse: अनागतविधानं तु कर्तव्यं शुभमिच्छता। आपदं शङ्कमानेन पुरुषेण विपश्चिता ॥
en: A considerate person that wishes for his welfare, apprehending peril, should prevent disaster ere it arrives.

sa_verse: तस्माद् गृहीत्वा वैदेहीं शरपाणिर्धनुर्धरः। गुहामाश्रय शैलस्य दुर्गो पादपसंकुलाम्॥
en: Therefore bearing arrows in your hand, and equipped with your bow, do you, taking Videha's daughter, take refuge in the mountain cavern, covered with trees and difficult of access.

sa_verse: प्रतिकूलितुमिच्छामि न हि वाक्यमिदं त्वया। शापितो मम पादाभ्यां गम्यतां वत्स मा चिरम्॥
en: That you should act contrary to my words, is what I do not wish. Swearing by my feet, go you without delay, my brother.

sa_verse: त्वं हि शूरश्च बलवान् हन्या एतान् न संशयः। स्वयं निहन्तुमिच्छामि सर्वानेव निशाचरान्॥
en: You are both strong and a hero: You can, without doubt, slay these Rākşasas. But I wish to slay all these rangers of the night myself.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण लक्ष्मणः सह सीतया। शरानादाय चापं च गुहां दुर्गा समाश्रयत्॥
en: Thus accosted by Rama, Laksmana, taking arrows and a bow, took refuge in an inaccessible cave along with Sītā.

sa_verse: तस्मिन् प्रविष्टें तु गुहां लक्ष्मणे सह सीतया। हन्त निर्युक्तमित्युक्त्वा रामः कवचमाविशत्॥ स तेनाग्निनिकाशेन कवचेन विभूषितः। बभूव रामस्तिमिरे महानग्निरिवोत्थितः॥
en: Thereupon, saying, Ah! we have spoken it sharp, Rāma put on his mail. And adorned with that mail resembling fire Rāma appeared in the dark like a mighty flame streaming up.

sa_verse: स चापमुद्यम्य महच्छरानादाय वीर्यवान्। सम्बभूवास्थितस्तत्र ज्यास्वनैः पूरयन् दिशः॥
en: And uplifting his bow, and taking his arrows, that powerful one stood there, feeling all directions with the twangs of his bow-string.

sa_verse: ततो देवाः सगन्धर्वाः सिद्धाश्च सह चारणैः। समेयुश्च महात्मानो युद्धदर्शनकाझ्या॥
en: Then the high-souled gods and Gandharvas, Siddhas and Cāranas came there, with the intention of witnessing the fight.

sa_verse: ऋषयश्च महात्मानो लोके ब्रह्मर्षिसत्तमाः। समेत्य चोचुः सहितास्तेऽन्योन्यं पुण्यकर्मणः॥ स्वस्ति गोब्राह्मणानां च लोकानां चेति संस्थिता। जयतां राघवो युद्धे पौलस्त्यान् रजनीचरान्॥ चक्रहस्तो यथा युद्धे सर्वानसुरपुंगवान्।
en: And high-souled saints of the world, and the foremost Brahmarșis of pious acts, coming together, spoke to one another, saying, Hail to cows and Brāhmaṇas, and all those in whom are established the worlds! may Rāghava vanquish in fight those rangers of the night, the progeny of Pulastya, even as the discus-handed Vişņu routed in battle the foremost Asuras!

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा पुनः प्रोचुरालोक्य च परस्परम्॥ चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। एकश्च रामो धर्मात्मा कथं युद्धं भविष्यति॥
en: Having said this, they again spoke, eyeing one another, There are fourteen thousand of the Rākşasas of dreadful deed while the righteous Rāma is single. How can fight take place (between two such parties)?

sa_verse: इति राजर्षयः सिद्धाः सगणाश्च द्विजर्षभाः। जातकौतूहलास्तस्थुर्विमानस्थाश्च देवताः॥
en: Having said this, the Rājarșis, Siddhas, multitudes of the best of the twice-born ones, and celestial stationed in the sky were moved with curiosity (as to the issue of the conflict).

sa_verse: आविष्टं तेजसा रामं संग्रामशिरसि स्थितम्। दृष्ट्वा सर्वाणि भूतानि भयाद् विव्यधिरे तदा॥
en: Then seeing Rāma filled with energy, remaining in the field, all beings from fear experienced great pain.

sa_verse: रूपमप्रतिमं तस्य रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः। बभूव रूपं क्रुद्धस्य रुद्रस्येव महात्मनः॥
en: And the peerless grace of Rāma of energetic deeds became like to that of the high souled infuriated Rudra.* There is a variation in reading here; the sense, however, is the same.

sa_verse: इति सम्भाष्यमाणे तु देवगन्धर्वचारणैः। ततो गम्भीरनिदिं घोरचर्मायुधध्वजम्॥ अनीकं यातुधानानां समन्तात् प्रत्यपद्यत। वीरालापान् विसृजतामन्योन्यमभिगच्छताम्॥ चापानि विस्फारयतां जृम्भतां चाप्यभीक्ष्णशः। विप्रघुष्टस्वनानां च दुन्दुभींश्चापि निघ्नताम्॥
en: While the gods, Gandharvas and Cāraņas were thus conversing, the forces of the Rākṣasas sending up solemn sounds, furnished with horrible armour, arms and flags, conversing in heroic parlance, roaring at each other, stretching bows, momentarily yawning, sending forth shouts, and blowing trumpets. The commentator says the yawning was in consequence of the soldiers having during the conflict indulged in liquor.

sa_verse: तेषां सुविपुलः शब्दः पूरयामास तद् वनम् । तेन शब्देन विवस्ताः स्त्रासिता वनचारिणः॥ दुद्रुवुर्यत्र नि:शब्दं पृष्ठतो नावलोकयन्।
en: The universal uproar filled that (entire) forest. Scared and terrified at the hubbub the rangers of the wood fle: to quarters free from noise; nor did they cast their eyes backward.

sa_verse: तच्चानीकं महावेगं रामं समनुवर्तत ॥ धृतनानाप्रहरणं गम्भीरं सागरोपमम्।
en: And that army resembling the ocean, and rife with sounds, equipped with various weapons, with furious speed came towards Rāma.

sa_verse: रामोऽपि चारयंश्चक्षुः सर्वतो रणपण्डितः॥ ददर्श खरसैन्यं तद् युद्धायाभिमुखो गतः।
en: And Rāma also versed in warfare, casting his eyes around, found the forces of Khara ready for fight. Another text:-The holder of Pinaka.

sa_verse: तितत्य च धनु मि तूण्याश्चोद्धृत्य सायकान् ॥ क्रोधमाहारयत् तीव्र वधार्थं सर्वरक्षसाम्। दुष्प्रेक्ष्यश्चाभवत् क्रुद्धो युगान्ताग्निरिव ज्वलन्॥
en: Then stretching his dreadful bow, and swiftly taking out shafts, (Rāma) for compassing the destruction of the entire body of the Rākşasas, waxed furiously enraged. And like to the flaming fire at the universal dissolution, he, growing wroth, was incapable of being looked i at.

sa_verse: तं दृष्ट्वा तेजसाऽऽविष्टं प्राव्यथन् वनदेवताः। तस्य रुष्टस्य रूपं तु रामस्य ददृशे तदा। दक्षस्येव क्रतुं हन्तुमुद्यतस्य पिनाकिनः॥
en: And seeing him filled with energy, the sylvan deities were extremely pained*. And the aspect of the enraged Rāma appeared like that of the holder of Pināka, intent upon destroying Dakşa's sacrifice. Another reading is:-The sylvan deities fled away.

sa_verse: तत्कार्मुकैराभरणै रथैश्च तद्वर्मभिश्चाग्निसमानवर्णैः। बभूव सैन्यं पिशिताशनानां सूर्योदये नीलमिवाभ्रजालम्।
en: Furnished with bows and ornaments and cars and mail hued like fire, that army of those subsisting on flesh, appeared like masses of blue clouds at sunrise.

sa_verse: अवष्टब्धधनु रामं क्रुद्धं तं रिपुघातिनम्। ददर्शाश्रममागम्य खरः सह पुरःसरैः॥ तं दृष्ट्वा सगुणं चापमुद्यम्य खरनिःस्वनम्। रामस्याभिमुखं सूतं चोद्यतामित्यचोदयत्॥
en: Coming to the asylum, Khara in company with those that went before him, saw that slayer of foes, the enraged Rāma, holding his bow. And seeing him, Khara possessing a shrill voice, raising his bow, commanded to the charioteer to drive towards Rāma, Drive on!

sa_verse: स खरस्याज्ञया सूतस्तुरगान् समचोदयत्। यत्र रामो महाबाहुरेको धुन्वन् धनुः स्थितः॥
en: At Khara's command, the charioteer drove the steeds to where the mighty—arined Rāma stood alone, holding his bow.

sa_verse: तं तु निष्पतितं दृष्ट्वा सर्वतो रजनीचराः। मुञ्चमाना महानादं सचिवाः पर्यवारयन्॥
en: And seeing him (Khara) entered the field, all those rangers of the night-the counsellers-uttering mighty shouts, cnvironed him round.

sa_verse: स तेषां यातुधानानां मध्ये रथगतः खरः। बभूव मध्ये ताराणां लोहिताङ्ग इवोद्धतः॥
en: Khara stationed on his car in the midst of Rākşasas appeared like red bodied one risen in the midst of the stars.

sa_verse: ततः शरसहस्रेण राममप्रतिमौजसम्। अर्दयित्वा महानादं ननाद समरे खरः॥
en: Then in battle Khara, afflicting Rāma of incomparable energy with a thousand shafts, uttered a tremendous roar.

sa_verse: ततस्सं भीमधन्वानं क्रुद्धाः सर्वे निशाचराः। रामं नानाविधैः शस्त्रैरभ्यवर्षन्त दुर्जयम्॥
en: Then all the rangers of the night, waxing exceeding wroth, showered various weapons on that terrible bowman, the invincible Rāma.

sa_verse: मुद्गरैरायसैः शूलैः प्रासैः खङ्गैः परश्वधैः। राक्षसाः समरे शूरं निजघ्नू रोषतत्पराः॥
en: And wrought up with rage, the Rākṣasas in battle assailed him with iron clubs, and darts, and prāsas, and swords, and axes.

sa_verse: ते बलाहकसंकाशा महाकाया महाबलाः। अभ्यधावन्त काकुत्स्थं रथैर्वाजिभिरेव च॥ गजैः पर्वतकूटाभै रामं युद्धे जिघांसवः।
en: And resembling clouds (in hue), the exceedingly strong Rākṣasas, having huge bodies, darted towards Kākutstha by means of steeds and cars.

sa_verse: ते रामे शरवर्षाणि व्यसृजन् रक्षसां गणाः ॥ शेलेन्द्रमिक धाराभिर्वर्षमाणा महाघनाः।
en: And mounted on elephants resembling mountain-peaks, numbers of Rākṣasas, intent upon staying Rāma in battle, showered arrows on him, as mighty clouds pour down showers on the monarch of mountains.

sa_verse: सर्वैः परिवृतो रामो राक्षसैः क्रूरदर्शनैः॥ तिथिष्विव महादेवो वृतः पारिषदां गणैः।
en: Rāma was hemmed in by all those fiercelooking Raksasas, even as in the evening Mahādeva is surrounded by his courtiers.

sa_verse: तानि मुक्तानि शस्त्राणि यातुधानैः स राघवः॥ प्रतिजग्राह विशिखैनद्यौघानिव सागरः।
en: And as the ocean resisted the tide of a river, Rāghava by means of arrow resist the weapons discharged by the Yātudhānas.

sa_verse: स तैः प्रहरणैोरैर्भिन्नगात्रो न विव्यथे॥ रामः प्रदीप्तैर्बहुभिर्वश्रेरिव महाचलः।
en: As a mighty mountain, assailed by the thunderbolt, dose not feel pain, Rāma, having his person pierced by terrible flaming weapons, did not feel pain.

sa_verse: स विद्धः क्षतजादिग्धः सर्वगात्रेषु राघवः॥ बभूव रामः संध्याभैर्दिवाकर इवावृतः।
en: And pierced, and with his person covered with blood, Rāma, the descendant of Raghu, resembled the sun enveloped in evening clouds.

sa_verse: विषेदुर्देवगन्धर्वाः सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः॥ एकं सहस्रैर्बहुभिस्तदा दृष्ट्वा समावृतम्।
en: And seeing him single, surrounded by many thousand, the Gods, Gandharvas, Siddhas, and supreme saints became sorrowful.

sa_verse: ततो रामस्तु संक्रुद्धो मण्डलीकृतकार्मुकः॥ ससर्ज निशितान् बाणाञ्छतशोऽथ सहस्रशः। दुरावारान् दुर्विषहान् कालपाशोपमान् रणे॥
en: Then Rama getting enraged, bringing his bow to a circle, discharged sharpened shafts by hundreds and by thousands. And as if in sport, Rāma in the conflict shot irresistible (shafts) furnished with Kanka feathers, and decked with gold, irresistible, capable of inflicting extreme pain, and resembling the noose of Death.

sa_verse: मुमोच लीलया कङ्कपत्रान् काञ्चनभूषणान्। ते शराः शत्रुसैन्येषु मुक्ता रामेण लीलया।।१८। आददू रक्षसां प्राणान् पाशाः कालकृता इव।
en: And sportively discharged by Rāma, those arrows deprived the Rākṣasas of their lives, like the noose forged by death.

sa_verse: भित्वा राक्षसदेहांस्तांस्ते शरा रुधिराप्लुताः॥ अन्तरिक्षगता रेजुर्दीप्ताग्निसमतेजसः।
en: And piercing the persons of the Rākşasas, those arrows, soaked in blood, going up to the sky, appeared with the splendour of flaming fire.

sa_verse: असंख्येयास्तु रामस्य सायकाश्चापमण्डलात्॥ विनिष्पेतुरतीवोग्रा रक्ष:प्राणापहारिणः।
en: And innumerable shafts, exceedingly fierce, capable of depriving the Rāksasas of their lives, were let go from the circle of his bow.

sa_verse: ते तैर्धनूंषि ध्वजाग्राणि चर्माणि कवचानि च ॥ बाहून् सहस्ताभरणानूरून् करिकरोपमान्। चिच्छेद रामः समरे शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः॥
en: And with those Rāma severed bows, in baitle by hundreds and by thousands and flag ends, and shields, and mail, and many arms with embellished hands, resembling the trunks of elephants.

sa_verse: हयान् काञ्चनसंनाहान् रथयुक्तान् ससारथीन्। गजांश्च सगजारोहान् सहयान् सादिनस्तदा॥ चिच्छिदुर्बिभिदुश्चैव रामबाणा गुणच्युताः। पदातीन् समरे हत्वा अनयद् यमसादनम्॥
en: And the arrows of Rāma discharged from the string pierced and cut off steeds mailed in gold, yoked to cars, together with the charioteer; and elephants with their riders; and horsemen with horses. And slaying foot-soldiers, he despatched them to the abode of Yama.

sa_verse: ततो नालीकनाराचैस्तीक्ष्णाग्रैश्च बिकर्णिभिः। भीममार्तस्वरं चक्रुश्छिद्यमाना निशाचराः॥
en: And cut off with nālīkas (steel-pointed arrows) and, nārācas (arrows wholly made of steel), and sharp-pointed vikarnas (barbed arrows), the rangers of the night uttered dreadful howls of distress.

sa_verse: तत्सैन्यं विविधैर्बाणैरर्दितं मर्मभेदिभिः। न रामेण सुखं लेभे शुष्कं वनमिवाग्निना॥
en: And like a withered wood afflicted by fire, that host harassed by the various marrowpiercing* shafts shot by Rāma, did not attain ease. Another reading is, sharpened.

sa_verse: केचिद् भीमबलाः शूरा प्रासाशूलान् परश्वधान्। चिक्षिपुः परमनुद्धा रामाय रमनीचराः॥
en: And some heroic rangers of the night possessed of great strength, waxing furious, threw at Rāma rrāsas, and darts and axes. Another reading: remaining before Rāma, threw powerful weapons.

sa_verse: तेषां बाणैर्महाबाहुः शस्त्राण्यावार्य वीर्यवान्। जहार समरे प्राणांश्चिच्छेद च शिरोधरान्॥
en: Thereupon resisting by means of shafts those weapons of theirs, the mighty-armed Rāma endued with prowess, took their lives in the conflict, and cut off the heads (of warriors).

sa_verse: ते छिन्नशिरसः पेतुश्छिन्नचर्मशरासनाः। सुपर्णवातविक्षिप्ता जगत्यां पादपा यथा ॥ अवशिष्टाश्च ये तत्र विषण्णास्ते निशाचराः। खरमेवाभ्यधावन्त शरणार्थं शराहताः॥
en: And having their heads, and shields and bowstrings, severed, they fell as fall on the earth trees thrown down by blasts froin the wings of Suparņa*. Those rangers of the night that remained there, wounded by arrows, and losing heart, fled with speed to Khara, to seek his protection. *Lit.-Of fair feather. A name of Garuda.

sa_verse: तान् सर्वान् धनुरादाय समाश्वास्य च दूषणः। अभ्यधावत् सुसंक्रुद्धः क्रुद्धं क्रुद्ध इवान्तकः॥
en: Thereat, encouraging them, Dāsana, taking his bow, ran furiously in high rage against Răma, like the enraged Destroyer himself,

sa_verse: निवृत्तास्तु पुनः सर्वे दूषणाश्रयनिर्भयाः। राममेवाभ्यधावन्त सालतालशिलायुधाः॥
en: And rallied again (by Dāsana) and, their fear dispelled through their having founded refuge with him, they armed with sālas, tālas, and crags, darted against Rāma.

sa_verse: शूलमुद्गरहस्ताश्च पाशहस्ता महाबलाः। सृजन्तः शरवर्षाणि शस्त्रवर्षाणि संयुगे॥
en: And bearing in their hands darts, and clubs, and nooses, those exceedingly strong ones showered in battle shafts and weapons.

sa_verse: द्रुमवर्षाणि मुञ्चन्त शिलावर्षाणि राक्षसाः। तद् बभूवाद्भुतं युद्धं तुमुलं रोमहर्षणम्॥ रामस्यास्य महाघोरं पुनस्तेषां च रक्षसाम्।
en: And the Räkşasas discharged vollies of trees and crags. And capable of making one's hair stand on end, that battle was dreadful and furious and now on the side of Rāma and now again on : that of the Raksasas.

sa_verse: ते समन्तादभिक्रुद्धा राघवं पुनरार्दयन् ॥ ततः सर्वा दिशो दृष्ट्वा प्रदिशश्च समावृताः। राक्षसैः सर्वतः प्राप्तैः शरवर्षाभिरावृतः॥
en: And waxing exceedingly wroth, they bore on him hard from all sides. Then finding all directions entirely covered with Rākṣasas, and showers of shafts, that one gifted with mighty strength, sending up a terrific shout, fixed (on the bow-string) the exceedingly effulgent Gandharva weapon (for discharging it) among the Raksasas.

sa_verse: स कृत्वा भैरवं नादमस्त्रं परमभास्वरम्। समयोजयद् गान्धर्वं राक्षसेषु महाबलः॥ ततः शरसहस्राणि निर्ययुश्चापमण्डलात्। सर्वा दश दिशो बाणैरापूर्यन्त समागतैः॥
en: Then thousands of shafts went forth from the circle of his bow; and all directions were covered with thronging arrows.

sa_verse: नाददानं शरान् घोरान् विमुञ्चन्तं शरोत्तमान्। विकर्षमाणं पश्यन्ति राक्षसास्ते शरार्दिताः॥
en: And those Rākṣasas, afflicted with arrows could not see Rāma how he took out his dread shafts, nor how he discharged those excellent shafts; they only saw him drawing his bow.

sa_verse: शरान्धकारमाकाशमावृणोत् सदिवाकरम्। बभूवावस्थितो रामः प्रक्षिपन्निव ताञ्छरान्॥
en: And the darkness spread by the arrows envelope the sky with the sun. And Rāma stationed there continued pouring in shafts.

sa_verse: युगपत्पतमानैश्च युगपच्च हतै शम्। युगपत्पतितैश्चैव विकीर्णा वसुधाऽभवत्॥ निहताः पतिताः क्षीणाश्छिन्ना भिन्ना विदारिताः। तत्र तत्र स्म दृश्यन्ते राक्षसास्ते सहस्रशः।॥
en: And the earth was covered with shafts shot, and alighting, and alighted simultaneously. And at places were seen Rākṣasas by thousands slain, falling, enfeebled, torn and riven.

sa_verse: सोष्णीषैरुत्तमाङ्गैश्च साङ्गदैर्बाहुभिस्तथा। ऊरुभिर्बाहुभिश्छिनैर्नानारूपैर्विभूषणैः।॥ हयैश्च द्विपमुख्यैश्च रथैर्भिन्नैरनेकशः। चामरव्यजनैश्छत्रैर्ध्वजैर्नानाविधैरपि॥ रामेण बाणाभिहतैर्विच्छिन्नैः शूलपट्टिशैः। विच्छिन्नैः समरे भूमिर्विस्तीर्णाऽभूद् भयंकरा ॥
en: And cut off by Rāma with arrows, darts and Pattiśas in that battle the fearful field was scattered, heads with turbans, arms with fingerfences; torn thighs and arms, and various ornaments, horses, excellent elephants and cars, shattered in numbers, chouris, fans, and umbrellas, and pennons of various descriptions.

sa_verse: तान् दृष्ट्वा निहतान् सर्वे राक्षसाः परमातुराः। न तत्र चलितुं शक्ता रामं परपुरंजयम्॥
en: Beholding all those stain, the (remaining) Raksasas, sore distressed, could not (again) advance before that captor of hostile capitals Rāma.

sa_verse: दूषणस्तु स्वकं सैन्यं हन्यमानं विलोक्य च। संदिदेश महाबाहुर्भीमवेगान् दुरासदान्॥ राक्षसान् पञ्चसाहस्रान् समरेष्वनिवर्तिनः।
en: Finding his own forces slaughtered, the mighty-armed Düşaņa speedily ordered five thousand Rākşasas, gifted with tremendous velocity, difficult of being approached, who never turned from the field.

sa_verse: ते शूलैः पट्टिशैः खङ्गैः शिलावषैर्दुमैरपि॥ शरवर्षैरविच्छिन्नं ववर्षुस्तं समन्ततः।
en: And from all sides they incessantly showered darts and pațțiśas, and scimitars, and stones, and trees, and shafts.

sa_verse: तद् द्रुमाणां शिलानां च वर्षं प्राणहरं महत्॥ प्रतिजग्राह धर्मात्मा राघवस्तीक्ष्णसायकैः।
en: Thereupon by means of shafts the righteous Rāghava resisted that mighty destructive shower of trees and stones.

sa_verse: प्रतिगृह्य च तद् वर्षं निमीलित इवर्षभः॥ रामः क्रोधं परं लेभे वधार्थं सर्वरक्षसाम्।
en: Resisting that shower, Rāma with his eyes staring and resembling a bull, flew into a great rage, for the purpose of slaying the whole body of Raksasas.

sa_verse: ततः क्रोधसमाविष्टः प्रदीप्त इव तेजसा ॥ शरैरभ्यकिरत् सैन्यं सर्वतः सहदूषणम्।
en: Then influenced by wrath, and flaming in energy, he on all sides covered the army along with Düşana with arrows.

sa_verse: तत: सेनापतिः क्रुद्धौ दूषणः शत्रुदूषणः॥ शरैरशनिकल्पैस्तं राघवं समवारयत्।
en: Then the general, Dusana, destroyer of enemies, getting wroth, opposed Rāghava with arrows resembling thunderbolts.

sa_verse: ततो रामः सुसंक्रुद्धः क्षुरेणास्य महद् धनुः॥ चिच्छेद समरे वीरश्चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान् । हत्वा चाश्वाशरैस्तीक्ष्णैरर्धचन्द्रेण सारथेः॥ शिरो जहार तद्रक्षस्त्रिभिर्विव्याध वक्षसि।
en: Then heroic Răma highly angered, severed his (Dusana's) mighty bow with shafts sharp as razors, and slew his four horses by ineans of as many shafts. And having slain the steeds, he (Rāma) cut off the head of the charioteer by means of a crescent-shaped weapon, and pierced the (Rakşasa Dūşaņa) in the breast with a brace of shafts.

sa_verse: स च्छिन्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः॥ जग्राह गिरिशृङ्गाभं परिघं रोमहर्षणम्। वेष्टितं काञ्चनैः पट्टैर्देवसैन्याभिमर्दनम्॥ आयसैः शङ्कुभिस्तीक्ष्णैः कीर्णं परवसोक्षितम्। वज्राशनिसमस्पर्श परगोपुरदारणम्॥
en: His bow cut off, his steeds together with the charioteer slain, and himself deprived of his car, he (Dūşana) took a parigha resembling a mountain peak, able to me one's down stand on end plated with gold, capable of afflicting celestial hosts, studded with sharp iron sankus, and graced with the fal of foes;-of the touch of a thunder-bolt, able to pierce the persons of enemies*. The text may also mean able to rend the cities of foes.

sa_verse: तं महोरगसंकाशं प्रगृह्य परिघं रणे। दूषणोऽभ्यपतद् रामं क्रूरकर्मा निशाचरः॥
en: And taking up in that encounter the parigha (an iron club) resembling a mighty snake, that ranger of the night of cruel deeds Duşaņa, rushed towards Rāina.

sa_verse: तस्याभिपतमानस्य दूषणस्य च राघवः। द्वाभ्यां शराभ्यां चिच्छेद सहस्ताभरणौ भुजौ॥
en: And as Dūsuņa was rushing forward, Rāghava by means of a couple of shafts cut off his two arms with the ornaments.

sa_verse: भ्रष्टस्तस्य महाकायः पपात रणमूर्धनि। परिघश्छिन्नहस्तस्य शक्रध्वज इवाग्रतः॥
en: And the huge parigha (an iron club) escaping from (Dusana's grasp) fell forward on the field like the banner of Sakra.

sa_verse: कराभ्यां च विकीर्णाभ्यां पपात भुवि दूषणः। विषाणाभ्यां विशीर्णाभ्यां मनस्वीव महागजः॥
en: And like a nighty elephant whose husks have fallen off, Dūşana, on his arms having been severed, fell down to the earth.

sa_verse: दृष्ट्वा तं पतितं भूमौ दूषणं निहतं रणे। साधु साध्विति काकुत्स्थं सर्वभूतान्यपूजयन्॥
en: Seeing Dūşaņa down on the ground, and slain in battle, all creatures, saying, ''well done! well done!" paid homage to Kākutstha.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे क्रुद्धास्त्रयः सेनाग्रयायिनः। संहत्याभ्यद्रवन् रामं मृत्युपाशावपाशिताः॥ महाकपालः स्थूलाक्षः प्रमाथी च महाबलः। महाकपालो विपुलं शूलमुद्यम्य राक्षसः॥ स्थूलाक्षः पट्टिशं गृह्य प्रमाथी च परश्वधम्।
en: In the meantime, three generals, getting wioth, being entrapped in the noose of death, rushed against Rāma in a body, viz; Mahakapāla, Stūlākșa, and the mighty Pramāthī the Rāksasa, Mahākapäla, upraising a large dart, and Sthulaksa, taking a pattisa, and Pramāthi all axe.

sa_verse: दृष्ट्वैवापततस्तांस्तु राघवः सायकैः शितैः॥ तीक्ष्णाग्रैः प्रतिजग्राह सम्प्राप्तानतिथीनिव।
en: And as Rāghava beheld them advance, he resisted them by means of sharp and keen-edged shafts, even as one receives guests that have come.

sa_verse: महाकपालस्य शिरश्चिच्छेद रघुनन्दनः॥ असंख्येयैस्तु बाणौधैः प्रममाथ प्रमाथिनम्। स्थूलाक्षस्याक्षिणी स्थूले पूरयामास सायकैः ॥
en: And Raghu's son split Mahākapāla's head, afflicted Pramăthſ with countless shafts, and lodged the eyes of Sthūlāksya with sharp shafts.

sa_verse: स पपात हतो भूमौ विटपीव महाद्रुमः। दूषणस्यानुगान् पञ्चसाहस्रान्कुपितः क्षणात्॥ हत्वा तु पञ्चसाहौरनयद् यमसादनम्।
en: as soon And they fell down to the earth like mighty trees of many bough. Thereat instantly inflained with wrath, Räma by means of five thousand shafts, sent as many thousands of Dusana's followers to Yama's abode.

sa_verse: दूषणं निहतं श्रुत्वा तस्य चैव पदानुगान् ॥ व्यादिदेश खरः क्रुद्धः सेनाध्यक्षान् महाबलाम्। अयं विनिहतः संख्ये दूषणः सपदानुगः॥ महत्या सेनया सार्धं युद्ध्वा रामं कुमानुषम्। शस्त्रैर्नानाविधाकारैर्हनध्वं सर्वराक्षसाः।।२५ ।
en: Hearing that Dusana had been slain, Khara, waxing wroth, commanded his mighty generals, saying, Fighting with that vile man, Rāma, along with his mighty forces Düşana has been slain in battle, together with his followers. Let all the Rākşasas slay him with weapons of various shapes.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा खरः क्रुद्धो राममेवाभिदुद्रुवे। श्येनगामी पृथुग्रीवो यज्ञशत्रुर्विहंगमः॥ दुर्जयः करवीराक्षः परुषः कालकार्मुकः। हेममाली महामाली सास्यो रुधिराशनः॥ द्वादशैते महावीर्या बलाध्यक्षाः ससैनिकाः। राममेवाभ्यधावन्त विसृजन्तः शरोत्तमान्॥
en: Having said this in wrath, Khara darted towards Rama. And discharging choice shafts, Syenagāmi Prthugrīva, Yajñaśatru, Vihangama, Durjaya, Karaviraksa, Parusa, Kalakarmuka, Hemamali, Mahämāli, Sarpāsya, and Rudhirāśana these twelve generals endued with mighty prowess accompanied with their forces, proceeded vehemently against Räina, discharging excellent shafts.

sa_verse: ततः पावकसंकाशैमवज्रविभूषितैः। जघान शेषं तेजस्वी तस्य सैन्यस्य सायकैः॥
en: Threat with shafts resembling fire, and decked with diamonds and with gold, (Rāma) possessed of energy destroyed the rest of his (Khara's) forces.

sa_verse: ते रुक्मपुङ्खा विशिखाः सधूमा इव पावकाः। निजध्नुस्तानि रक्षासिं वज्रा इव महाद्रुमान्॥
en: And as the thunderbolt slay the mighty, Asuras, those shafts studded with gold, and like to smoking fire, slew those Rākşasas.

sa_verse: रक्षसां तु शतं रामः शतेनैकेन कर्णिना। सहस्रं तु सहस्रेण जघान रणमूर्धनी॥
en: And in the field Rāma slew an hundred Rāks asa with an hundred Karnis, and a thousand (again) with a thousand.

sa_verse: तैर्भिन्नवर्माभरणाश्छिन्नभिन्नशरासनाः। निपेतुः शोणितादिग्धा धरण्यां रजनीचराः॥
en: And, their armour and ornaments severed, and their bows broken in shivers, those rangers of the night fell down on the earth, bathed in blood.

sa_verse: तैर्मुक्तकेशैः समरे पतितैः शोणितोक्षितैः । विस्तीर्णा वसुधा कृत्स्ना महावेदिः कुशैरिव ॥
en: And as a spacious dais is covered with Kusa, the entire field was scattered with the Rākṣasas fallen in battle with hair dishevelled, and covered with blood.

sa_verse: तत्क्षणै तु महाघोरं वनं निहतराक्षसम्। बभूव निरयप्रख्यं मांसशोणितकर्दमम्॥
en: And at that time that fearful forest, with the Rākṣasas slaughtered, and with its clay mired with flesh and blood, resembled hell itself.

sa_verse: चतुर्दशसहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। हतान्येकेन रामेण मानुषेण पदातिनः॥
en: Fourteen thousand Rākṣasas of dreadful deeds were slain by Rāma single, a human being, fighting, (moreover) on foot.

sa_verse: तस्य सैन्यस्य सर्वस्य खरः शेषो महारथः। राक्षसस्त्रिशिराश्चैव रामश्च रिपुसूदनः॥
en: And the remnant of his (Khara's) forces were that mighty car-warrior, Khara himself, and the Raksasa, Trisirā; and (on the other side) was that destroyer of foes-Rāma.

sa_verse: शेषा हता महावीर्या राक्षसा रणमूर्धनी। घोरा दुर्विषहाः सर्वे लक्ष्मणस्याग्रजेन ते॥
en: The rest of the Rākşasas, gifted with great prowess, terrible, and difficult of being withstood, were all slain in battle by the elder brother of Laksmana.

sa_verse: ततस्तु तद्भीमबलं महाहवे समीक्ष्य धर्मेण हतं बलीयसा। रथेन रामं महता खरस्ततः समाससादेन्द्र इवोद्यताशनिः॥
en: Then seeing that dreadful army destroyed in terrible conflict by the mighty Rāma, Khara ascending a great car, advanced before Rāma, like Indra with the upraised thunderbolt. Then seeing that dreadful army destroyed in terrible conflict by the mighty Rāma, Khara ascending a great car, advanced before Rāma, like Indra with the upraised thunderbolt.

sa_verse: खरं तु रामाभिमुखं प्रयान्तं वीहिनीपतिः। राक्षसस्त्रिशिरा नाम संनिपत्येदमब्रवीत्॥
en: As Khara was advancing before Rāma, that leader of the army named Triſirā, approaching him, said.

sa_verse: मां नीयोजय विक्रान्तं त्वं निवर्तस्व साहसात्। पश्य रामं महाबाहुं संयुगे विनीपातितम्॥
en: Do you employ me, who am possessed of prowess, and yourself desist from this rashness. Behold the mighty-armed Rāma brought down in battle.

sa_verse: प्रतिजानामि ते सत्यमायुधं चाहमालभे। यथा रामं वधिप्यामि वधार्ह सर्वरक्षसाम्॥
en: I swear (to you) truly; I touch this weapon, (to say) that I will stay Rāma, who deserves to be slain by all the Rākşasas.

sa_verse: अहं वास्य रणे मृत्युरेष वा समरे मम। विनिवर्त्य रणोत्साहं मुहूर्तं प्राश्निको भव॥
en: Either I shall prove his death in battle, or he shall prove mine. Restraining your martial ardour, do you for a while become a witness.

sa_verse: प्रहृष्टो वा हते रामे जनस्थानं प्रयास्यसि। मयि वा निहते रामं संयुगाय प्रयास्यसि ॥
en: Either, joyed in consequence of Rāma being slain, you shall repair to Janasthāna; or I being slain you shall enter the field (against him).

sa_verse: खरस्त्रिशिरसा तेन मृत्युलोभात् प्रसादितः। गच्छ युध्येत्यनुज्ञातो राघवाभिमुखो ययौ॥
en: Thus satisfied by Trisirā, from his desire to meet with death, the latter, on being permitted with Go, proceeded towards Rāma.

sa_verse: त्रिशिरास्तु रथेनैव वाजियुक्तेन भास्वता। अभ्यद्रवद् रणे रामं त्रिशृङ्ग इव पर्वतः॥
en: And like a hill with three sumınits, Trisirā rushed towards Rāma on an effulgent car yoked with steeds.

sa_verse: शरधारासमूहान् स महामेघ इवोत्सृजन्। व्यसृजत् सदृशं नादं जलार्द्रस्येव दुन्दुभेः॥
en: And as a mighty cloud pours down shower, (Trisirā) discharging vollies of shafts, uttered a roar resembling the sound of a wet kettledrum.

sa_verse: आगच्छन्तं त्रिशिरसं राक्षसं प्रेक्ष्य राघवः। धनुषा प्रतिजग्राह विधुन्वन् सायकाशितान्॥
en: And seeing that the Rākşasa Trisirã was advancing, Raghava resisted (his attack) by discharging sharpened shafts.

sa_verse: स सम्प्रहारस्तुमुलो रामत्रिशिरसोस्तदा। सम्बभूवातिबलिनोः सिंहकुञ्जरयोरिव॥
en: And that encounter of those exceedingly powerful ones, Rāma and Triſiră was fierce, like to that between a lion and an elephant.

sa_verse: ततस्त्रिशिरसा बाणैर्ललाटे ताडितस्त्रिभिः। अमर्षी कुपितो रामः संरब्ध इदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then struck on the forehead by a brace of shafts shot by Trisirā, the wrathful Rāma enraged, and inflamed with anger, said.

sa_verse: अहो विक्रमशूरस्य राक्षसस्येदृशं बलम्। पुष्पैरिव शरैर्योऽहं ललाटेऽस्मि परिक्षतः॥ ममापि प्रतिगृह्णीष्व शरांश्चापगुणाच्च्युतान्।
en: Ah! such is the strength of the heroic Rākşasas! I have been wounded in the forehead with shafts resembling flowers. Do you also take the arrows shot from my bow.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु संरब्धः शरानाशीविषोपमान्॥ त्रिशोरोवक्षसि क्रुद्धो निजघान चतुर्दश।
en: Saying this, (Rāma) enraged, and influenced by wrath, wounded Trisiră in the breast with fourteen arrows.

sa_verse: चतुर्भिस्तुरगानस्य शरैः संनतपर्वभिः॥ न्यपातयत तेजस्वी चतुरस्तस्य वाजिनः। अष्टभिः सायकैः सूतं रथोपस्थे नयपातयत्॥
en: And that energetic one by means of four shafts having their joints bent, brought down his four steeds. And by means of eight arrows (Rāma) laid low the charioteer from the front of the car.

sa_verse: रामश्चिच्छेद बाणेन ध्वजं चास्य समुच्छ्रितम्। ततो हतरथात् तस्मादुत्पतन्तं निशाचरम्॥ चिच्छेद रामस्तं बाणैर्हदये सोऽभवज्जडः।
en: And Rāma with a shaft severed his upraised standard. Then as that ranger of the night was descending from his broken car, Rāma pierced his breast with arrows, and thereat he was stupified.

sa_verse: सायकैश्चाप्रमेयात्मा सामर्षात्तस्य रक्षसः॥ शिरांस्यपातयत् त्रीणि वेगवद्भिस्त्रिभिः शरैः।
en: Thereupon, that one of immeasurable prowess, out of anger by means of three shafts possessed of celerity, brought down Trisira's three heads.

sa_verse: सधूमशोणितोद्गारी रामबाणाभिपीडितः॥ न्यपतत् पतितैः पूर्वं समरस्थो निशाचरः।
en: And that ranger of the night present in the field, afflicted by the shafts of Rāma, after his heads had fallen first, fell, vomiting smoking gore.

sa_verse: हतशेषास्ततो भग्ना राक्षसाः खरसंश्रयाः॥ द्रवन्ति स्म न तिष्ठन्ति व्याधत्रस्ता मृगा इव। तान् खरो द्रवतो दृष्ट्वा निवर्त्य रुषितस्त्वरन्।
en: And the Räksasas remaining after the rest had been slain, belonging to Khara's original forces losing heart, began to flee like deer terrified at a hunter.

sa_verse: राममेवाभिदुद्राव राहुश्चन्द्रमसं यथा॥
en: And seeing them fly, Khara waxing wroth, swiftly making them desist, darted towards Ráma, like Rāhu darting towards the Moon.

sa_verse: नेहतं दूषणं दृष्ट्वा रणे त्रिशिरसा सह। खरस्याप्यभवत् त्रासो दृष्ट्वा रामस्य विक्रमम्॥
en: Seeing Düşaņa slain in fight along with Trisiră, Khara, witnessing Rāma's prowess, was filled with fear.

sa_verse: स दृष्ट्वा राक्षसं सैन्यमविषह्यं महाबलम्। हतमेकेन रामेण दूषणस्त्रिशिरा अपि॥ तबलं हतभूयिष्ठं विमनाः प्रेक्ष्य राक्षसः। आससाद खरो रामं नमुचिर्वासवं यथा ॥
en: Seeing that irresistible Raksasa host-even Düşaņa and Triſirā-slain by the mighty Rāma alone, and seeing the great courage that was made in the army, that Raksasa, Khara, was seized with despondency. Then as Namuci advances against Väsava, Khara stretching his powerful bow, advanced against Răma.

sa_verse: विकृष्य बलवच्चापं नाराचान् रक्तभोजनान्। खरश्चिक्षेप रामाय क्रुद्धानाशीविषानिव॥
en: Khara hurled at Rāma nārācas revelling in blood, resembling infuriated venomous snakes.

sa_verse: ज्यां विधुन्वन् सुबहुशः शिक्षयास्त्राणि दर्शयन्। चचार समरे मार्गाशरैरथ गतः खरः॥
en: Repeatedly twanging his twanging his bow, Khara, mounted on his car, began to range the field, displaying his weapons through his acquired skill.

sa_verse: स सर्वाश्च दिशो बाणैः प्रदिशश्च महारथः। पूरयामास तं दृष्ट्वा रामोऽपि सुमहद् धनुः॥ स सायकैर्दुर्विषहैर्विस्फुलिङ्गैरिवाग्निभिः। नभश्चकाराविवरं पर्जन्य इव वृष्टिभिः॥
en: And that mighty car-warrior covered all sides with his shafts. And secing this, Rāma of a tremendous bow with shafts incapable of being borne, and resembling tongues of flaming fire, entirely enveloped the welkin, even as a cloud pour down showers.

sa_verse: तद् बभूव शितैर्बाणैः खररामविसर्जितैः। पर्याकाशमनाकाशं सर्वतः शरसंकुलम्॥
en: With the sharpened shafts shot by Khara and Rāma, the entire firmament on all sides was thronged.

sa_verse: शरजालावृतः सूर्यो न तदा स्म प्रकाशते। अन्योन्यवधसंरम्भादुभयोः सम्प्रयुध्यतोः॥
en: And as each enraged was engaged in coping with the other, the sun, enveloped in a net-work of shafts, did not appear.

sa_verse: ततो नालीकनाराचैस्तीक्ष्णाग्रैश्च विकर्णिभिः। आजघान रणे रामं तोत्रैरिव महाद्विपम्॥
en: As a mighty elephant is struck with the goad, Rāma in the conflict attacked (his opponent) with nalikas and nārācas and sharp-pointed vikarna.

sa_verse: तं रथस्थं धनुष्पाणिं राक्षसं पर्यवस्थितम्। ददृशुः सर्वभूतानि पाशहस्तमिवान्तकम्॥
en: And as that Rākşasa sat on his car, bow in hand, all creatures saw him, as if he were the very destroyer with the noose in his hand.

sa_verse: हन्तारं सर्वसैन्यस्य पौरुषे पर्यवस्थितम्। परिश्चान्तं महासत्त्वं मेने रामं खरस्तदा॥
en: At this time Khara thought that Destroyer of all his forces, established in his manliness, the exceedingly powerful Rāma to be overcome with fatigue.

sa_verse: तं सिंहमिव विक्रान्तं सिंहविक्रान्तगामिनम्। दृष्ट्वा नोद्विजते रामः सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगं यथा॥
en: Seeing that one powerful like the lion, and gifted with the vigorous gait of the lion, Rāma was not moved, as a lion seeing a puny deer (is not moved).

sa_verse: ततः सूर्यनिकाशेन रथेन महता खरः। आससादाथ तं रामं पतङ्ग इव पापकम्॥
en: And then as an insect falls into a flame, Khara mounting a mighty car, resembling the sun, approached Rāma.

sa_verse: ततोऽस्य सशरं चापं मुष्टिदेशे महात्मनः। खरश्चिच्छेद रामस्य दर्शयन् हस्तलाघवम्॥
en: And, displaying his lightness of hand, Khara severed the bow of the magnanimous Rāma, with the arrow (fixed on it) at the place where it is grasped.

sa_verse: स पुनस्त्वपरान् सप्त शरानादाय मर्मणि। निजघान रणे कुद्धः शक्राशनिसमप्रभान्॥ ततः शरसहस्रेण राममप्रतिमौजसम्। अर्दयित्वा महानादं ननाद समरे खरः॥
en: Then taking up seven other shafts, resplendent like the thunderbolt of Sakra, Khara, enraged, sent them into (Rāma's) main-joints, and then afflicting Rāma of unparalleled energy with a thousand shafts, Khara sent up in that conflict a loud shout.

sa_verse: ततस्तत्प्रहतं बाणैः खरमुक्तैः सुपर्वभिः। पपात कवचं भूमौ रामस्यादित्यवर्चसम्॥
en: And riven by the shafts discharged by Khara, Rāma's mail resembling the sun fell to the ground.

sa_verse: स शरैरर्पितः क्रुद्धः सर्वगात्रेषु राघवः। रराज समरे रामो विधूमोऽग्रिरिव ज्वलन्॥
en: And pierced with those arrow, all over his body, and inflamed with rage, Rāghava appeared in the field, like a smokeless flaming fire.

sa_verse: ततो गम्भीरनिर्हादं रामः शत्रुनिबर्हणः। चकारान्ताय स रिपोः सज्यमन्यन्महद्धनुः॥ सुमहद् वैष्णवं यत् तदतिसृष्टं महर्षिणा। वरं तद् धनुरुद्यम्य खरं समभिधावत ॥
en: Then that destroyer of foes, Rāma, for compassing the end of his enemy, stringed another mighty bow, sending forth solemn sounds, the redoubtable Vaisnava bow that had been conferred on him by the Maharşi.

sa_verse: ततः कनकपुखैस्तु शरैः संनतपर्वभिः। चिच्छेद रामः संक्रुद्धः खरस्य समरे ध्वजम्॥
en: And uplifting that superior bow, Rāma rushed against Khara. Then with shafts having bent knots and golden feathers, Rāma, wrought up with rage, severed in battle Khara's standard.

sa_verse: स दर्शनीयो बहुधा विच्छिन्नः काञ्चनो ध्वजः। जगाम धरणीं सूर्यो देवतानामिवाज्ञया॥
en: And on that exceedingly graceful golden standard being hewn down it seemed as if the sun dropped to the earth at the behest of the celestials.

sa_verse: तं चतुर्भि खरः क्रुद्धो रामं गात्रेषु मार्गणैः। विव्याध हृदि मर्मज्ञौ मातङ्गमिव तोयरैः॥
en: And thereat Khara, understanding the import of things, fired with wrath, pierced Rāma's breast with five arrows, like one striking an elephant with a goad.

sa_verse: स रामो बहुभिर्बाणैः खरकार्मुकनिः सृतैः। विद्धो रुधिरनिक्ताङ्गो बभूव रुषितो भृशम्॥
en: Rāma on being pierced with a good many shafts discharged from Khara's bow, and having his body bathed in blood, was highly wroth.

sa_verse: स धनुर्धन्विनां श्रेष्ठः संगृह्य परमाहवे। मुमोच परमेष्वासः षट् शरानभिलक्षितान्॥
en: Thereupon that foremost of bowmen, and wielder of a mighty bow, taking six shafts, let them go, after aiming at them.

sa_verse: शिरस्येकेन बाणेन द्वाभ्यां बाह्वोरथार्पयत्। त्रिभिश्चन्द्रार्धवक्त्रैश्च वक्षस्यभिजघान ह॥
en: And with one shaft he pierced Khara's head, with two his arms, and with three arrows headed like half-moons, Rāma wounded Khara in the chest.

sa_verse: ततः पश्चान्महोतेजा नाराचान् भास्करोपमान्। जघान राक्षसं क्रुद्धस्त्रयोदश शिलाशितान्॥ रथस्य युगमेकेन चतुर्भिः शबलान् हयान्। षष्ठेन च शिरः संख्ये चिच्छेद खरसारथेः ॥ त्रिभिस्त्रिवेणून् बलवान् द्वाभ्यामक्षं महाबलः। द्वादशेन तु बाणेन खरस्य सकरं धनुः॥ छित्त्वा वज्रनिकाशेन राघवः प्रहसन्निव। त्रयोदशेनेन्द्रसमो बिभेद समरे खरम्॥
en: Then that highly energetic one, influenced by anger, assailed the Räkşasa with thirteen närācas whetted on stone and with one that exceedingly powerful one, cut the yoke of the car, with four the four steeds, with the sixth the head of Khara's charioteer, with three the stout trivenu of the car, with two the wheel, and with the twelfth, severing as if in sport Khara's bow with his hand*, with the thirteenth, resembling the thunder-bolt pierced Khara in the encounter. Another text reads: with the arrow set.

sa_verse: प्रभग्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः। गदापाणिरवप्लुत्य तस्थौ भूमौ खरस्तदा॥
en: Then with his bow shattered, deprived of his car, (Khara) having his horses slain as well as his charioteer killed, taking a mace in his hand leaped to the ground, and stood there.

sa_verse: तत् कर्म रामस्य महारथस्य समेत्य देवश्च महर्षयश्च। स्तदा विमानाग्रगताः समेताः॥
en: And the celestials and Maharsis exceedingly rejoiced, assembled in the welkin in a body, and with joined hands extolled that feat of that mighty car-warrior Rāma.

sa_verse: खरं तु विरथं रामो गदापाणिमवस्थितम्। मृदुपूर्वं महातेजाः परुषं वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: And to Khara deprived of his car standing with a mace in his hand, that exceedingly energetic one, Rāma, precluding his speech with mildness, spoke.

sa_verse: गजाश्वरथसम्बाधे बले महति तिष्ठता। कृतं ते दारुणं कर्म सर्वलोकजुगुप्सितम्॥ उद्वेजनीयो भूतानां नृशंसः पापकर्मकृत्। त्रयाणामपि लोकानामीश्वरोऽपि स तिष्ठति ॥ कर्म लोकविरुद्धं तु कुर्वाणं क्षणदाचर। तीक्ष्णं सर्वजनो हन्ति सष दुष्टमिवागतम्॥
en: Backed by this mighty host abounding in elephants and horses and cars, you have done an exceedingly wicked deed, execrated by all the worlds. Even if one happen to be the lord of the three worlds, one given to troubling creatures, and who is cruel and engaged in wicked acts, can not exist. All persons destroy, like a snake that has intruded itself, him that dose cruel deeds, hostile to the interests of every one.

sa_verse: लोभात् पापानि कुर्वाणः कामाद् वा यो न बुध्यते। हृष्टः पश्यति तस्यान्तं ब्राह्मणी करकादिव॥
en: People delightedly behold the end of him that doing an action either from covetousness or desire, dose not like a Brāhmaṇi swallowing a Karakā, see the consequence thereof.

sa_verse: वसतो दण्डकारण्ये तापसान् धर्मचारिणः। किं नु हत्वा महाभागान् फलं प्राप्स्यसि राक्षस॥
en: What, O Rākṣasa, do you gain by slaughtering exceedingly pious ascetics engaged in righteous acts, living in the forest of Dandaka?

sa_verse: न चिरं पापकर्माणः क्रूरा लोकजुगुप्सिताः। ऐश्वर्यं प्राप्य तिष्ठन्ति शीर्णमूला इव द्रुमाः।७।।
en: Like to trees whose roots have been reduced, cruel persons, execrated of men, who perpetrate iniquitous acts, do not exist long.

sa_verse: अवश्यं लभते कर्ता फलं पापस्य कर्मणः। घोरं पर्यागते काले द्रुमः पुष्पमिवार्तवम् ॥
en: As a tree puts forth blossoms in season, the doer of sinful deeds, at the hour (of repentance) inevitably reaps their fruit in the shape of dreadful anguish (of the spirit).

sa_verse: न चिरात् प्राप्यते लोके पापानां कर्मणां फलम्। सविषाणामिवान्नानां भुक्तानां क्षणदाचर॥
en: O ranger of night, as the effect of having taken rice mixed with poison, appears without delay, even so also people readily reap the fruit of their own acts.

sa_verse: पापमाचरतां घोरं लोकस्याप्रियमिच्छताम्। अहमासादितो राज्ञा प्राणान् हन्तुं निशाचर ॥
en: O ranger of the night, it is to take the lives of the perpetrators of dreadful sins, who wish ill to men, that I the king have come.

sa_verse: अद्य भित्वा मया मुक्ताः शराः काञ्चनभूषणाः। विदार्यातिपतिष्यन्ति वल्मीकमिव पन्नगाः॥
en: Today the gold-decked arrows discharged by ine, piercing (your body), shall enter into the carth, cleaving it, like serpents falling into an ant-hill.

sa_verse: ये त्वया दण्डकारण्ये भक्षिता धर्मचारिणः। तानद्य निहतः संख्ये ससैन्योऽनुगमिष्यसि ॥
en: Slain in battle, you shall in company with your army, follow those people practising piety, whom you have devoured in the Dandaka forest.

sa_verse: अद्य त्वां निहतं बाणैः पश्यन्तु परमर्षयः। निरयस्थं विमानस्था ये त्वया निहताः पुरा॥
en: Today let those great saints, who had formerly been slain by you, stationed in the sky, behold you slain (in turn) with my arrows, and inhabiting hell.

sa_verse: प्रहरस्व यथाकामं कुरु यत्नं कुलाधम। अद्य ते पातयिष्यामि शिरस्तालफलं यथा॥
en: Do you strike as you like. And youl that are of an odious race, do you put forth your energy. Today I will bring down your head, even as a palm falls to the ground.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण क्रुद्धः संरक्तलोचनः। प्रत्युवाच ततो रामं प्रहसन् क्रोधमूच्छितः॥
en: Thus addressed by Rāma, Khara enraged and beyond himself with passion, with eyes reddened, replied.

sa_verse: प्राकृतान् राक्षसान् हत्वा युद्धे दशरथात्मज। आत्मना कथमात्मानमप्रशस्यं प्रशंससि॥
en: O son of Dasaratha, why having slain inferior Rākṣasas in battle, do you praise yourself without reason?

sa_verse: विक्रान्ता बलवन्तो वा ये भवन्ति नरर्षभाः। कथयन्ति न ते किंचित् तेजसा चातिगर्विताः॥
en: Those foremost of men that are puissant and powerful, do not, inflated with their energy, mouth (their own consequence).

sa_verse: प्राकृतास्त्वकृतात्मानो लोके क्षत्रियपांसनाः। निरर्थकं विकत्थन्ते यथा राम विकत्थसे ॥
en: It is the mean-minded Kșatriyas of impure hearts that magnify themselves among men, even as you, O Rama,do.

sa_verse: कुलं वपदिशन् वीरः समरे कोऽभिधास्यति। मृत्युकाले तु सम्प्राप्ते स्वयमप्रस्तवे स्तवम्॥
en: What hero, when the hour of his death has approached, publishes in the field his own lofty lineage and sings his own hymn.

sa_verse: सर्वथा तु लघुत्वं ते कत्थनेन विदर्शितम्। सुवर्णप्रतिरूपेण तप्तेनेव कुशाग्निना॥
en: As brass wearing the semblance of gold, displays its own defect on being heated*, with a fire lit with Kusa, so you have betrayed your own lightness by this speech of yours. i.c. becomes dusky, (Ramanuja).

sa_verse: न तु मामिह तिष्ठन्तं पश्यसि त्वं गदाधरम्। धराधरमिवाकम्प्यं पर्वतं धातुभिश्चितम्॥
en: You do not see me staying here mace in hand, like a moveless mountain dyed in metals, bearing mobile and immobile things.

sa_verse: पर्याप्तोऽहं गदापाणिर्हन्तुं प्राणान् रणे तव। त्रयाणामपि लोकानां पाशहस्त इवान्तकः॥
en: I can, mace in hand, deprive you and the three worlds to boot clean of your lives, like the very Destroyer with the noose in his hand.

sa_verse: कामं बह्वपि वक्तव्यं त्वयि वक्ष्यामि न त्वहम्। अस्तं प्राप्नोति सविता युद्धविघ्नस्ततो भवेत्॥
en: But I will not parley much with you as much as I could wish: the sun is going to set, and our fight shall be interrupted.

sa_verse: चतुर्दश सहस्राणि राक्षसानां हतानि ते। त्वद्विनाशात् करोम्यद्य तेषामथुप्रमार्जनम्॥
en: Fourteen thousand Räksasas have been slaughtered by you. I will for their deaths wipe their tears to-day.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा परमक्रुद्धः स गदां परमाङ्गदाम्। खरश्चिक्षेप रामाय प्रदीप्तामशनि यथा ॥
en: Saying this Khara, highly enraged, hurled his mace+ provided with golden rings at Rāma, like to the blazing thunderbolt. Another reading is, plaited with gold.

sa_verse: खरबाहुप्रमुक्ता सा प्रदीप्ता महती गदा। भस्मवृक्षांश्च गुल्मांश्च कृत्वागात् तत्समीपतः॥
en: Thereat, reducing to ashes trees and shrubs, that mighty flaming mace, discharged by Khara's arm, fell before Rama.

sa_verse: तामापतन्ती महती मृत्युपाशोपमां गदाम्। अन्तरिक्षगती रामश्चिच्छेद बहुधा शरैः॥
en: And Rāma severed in many fragments that mighty mace, resembling the noose of Death, as ascending the welkin, it was coming down.

sa_verse: सा विशीर्णा शरैर्भिन्ना पपात धरणीतले। गदा मन्त्रौषधिबलैालीव विनिपातिता॥
en: Thereupon, like a she-serpent brought down by force of incantations, the mace fell to the earth shattered and riven.

sa_verse: भित्वा तु तां गदां बाणे राघवो धर्मवत्सलः। स्मयमान इदं वाक्यं संरब्धमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: Cutting off the mace with his shafts, Rāghava attached to righteousness with a smile said to Khara these angry words.

sa_verse: एतत् ते बलसर्वस्वं दर्शितं राक्षसाधम। शक्तिहीनतरो मत्तो वृथा त्वमुपगर्जसि॥
en: You vile of Rākșasas, this is the utmost of your might, which you have displayed.

sa_verse: एषा बाणविनिर्भिन्ना गदा भूमितलं गता। अभिधानप्रगल्भस्य तव प्रत्ययघातिनी॥
en: Rendered more nerveless at my hands, in vain do you storm. Riven by my shafts, your mace, belonging to you who are prolix in the matter of vocabulary, destroying your confidence, has sought the earth.

sa_verse: यत् त्वयोक्तं विनष्टानामिदमश्रुप्रमार्जनम्। राक्षसानां करोमीति मिथ्या तदपि ते वचः॥
en: And what you had said, will wipe the tears of the Rākşasas that have been slain, has also proved false.

sa_verse: नीचस्य क्षुद्रशीलस्य मिथ्यावृत्तस्य रक्षसः। प्राणानपहरिष्यामि गरुत्मानमृतं यथा॥
en: As Garuda stole ambrosia, will I deprive you, O Rāksasa, who are base, of a mean disposition and a false character, of your life.

sa_verse: अद्य ते भिन्नकण्ठस्य फेनबुबुदभूषितम्। विदारितस्य मद्बाणैर्मही पास्यति शोणितम्॥
en: To day the earth shall drink the blood vitiated with foamy bubbles, of you, having your throat severed, and riven by my shafts.

sa_verse: पांसुरूषितसर्वाङ्गः स्रस्तन्यस्तभुजद्वयः। स्वप्स्यसे गां समाश्लिष्य दुर्लभां प्रमदामिव॥
en: Having all your body covered with dust, and your two arms lopped off, you shall, difficult to win, take your nap, embracing the earth, like a damsel difficult to win.

sa_verse: प्रवृद्धनिद्रे शयिते त्वयि राक्षसपांसने। भविष्यन्ति शरण्यानां शरण्या दण्डका इमे॥
en: On you, disgrace of Raksasas, lying down, and being fast asleep, this Dandaka shall be refuge of those that shall resort to it for shelter.

sa_verse: जनस्थाने हतस्थाने तव राक्षस मच्छरैः। निर्भया विचरिष्यन्ति सर्वतो मुनयो वने॥
en: O Rākşasa, in your Janasthāna, with its (Rāks asas) slain by my shafts, ascetics shall fearlessly go about in the wood.

sa_verse: अद्य विप्रसरिष्यन्ति राक्षस्यो हतबान्धवाः। वाष्पार्द्रवदना दीना भयादन्यभयावहाः॥
en: Today Rākṣasas, capable of exciting fear in others, rendered forlorn and with their friends slain, shall from fear, with their faces wet with tears, fly (this place).

sa_verse: अद्य शोकरसज्ञास्ता भविष्यन्ति निरर्थिकाः। अनुरूपकुलाः पत्न्यो यासां त्वं पतिरीदृशः॥
en: To day your wives whose husband are you of such a nature, and who are of a like lineage (with yourself), shall experience the sentiment of sorrow, and be deprived of their all.

sa_verse: नृशंसशील क्षुद्रात्मन् नित्यं ब्राह्मणकण्टक। त्वत्कृते शङ्कितैरग्नौ मुनिभिः पात्यते हविः॥
en: You of a cruel disposition, you of ignoble soul, you that are aye a thorn (in the side) of Brāhmanas, it is for you that ascetics, frightened and dispirited, have so long been pouring the clarified butter.

sa_verse: तमेवभिसंरब्धं ब्रुवाणं राघवं वने। खरो निर्भर्त्सयामास रोषात् खरतरस्वरः॥
en: As Rāghava, influenced by anger, said this in the field, Khara f. wrath, with accents rendered harsher, fell to censuring (Rāma),

sa_verse: दृढं खल्ववलिप्तोऽसि भयेष्वपि च निर्भयः। वाच्यावाच्यं ततो हि त्वं मृत्योर्वश्यो न बुध्यसे ॥
en: You are wondrous proud; and you are fearless albeit fear is present to you. And come under the sway of Death, you do not understand what should be said and what left unspoken.

sa_verse: कालपाशपरिक्षिप्ता भवन्ति पुरुषा हि ये। कार्याकार्यं न जानन्ति ते निरस्तषडिन्द्रियाः॥
en: Those persons that have been fast bound by the noose of death, do not in consequence of their senses having ceased to perform their functions, discern what is proper and what improper.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा ततो रामं संरुध्य भृकुटिं ततः। स ददर्श महासालमविदूरे निशाचरः॥ रणे प्रहरणस्यार्थे सर्वतो ह्यवलोकयन्। स तमुत्पाटयामास संदष्टदशनच्छदम्॥
en: Saying this to Rāma, that ranger of the night (Khara), pursing his brows, espied a mighty Sāla hard by. And looking about him on all sides in the field for a weapon, he uprooted it, biting his Nether lip.

sa_verse: तं समुत्क्षिप्य बाहुभ्यां विनर्दित्वा महाबलः। राममुद्दिश्य चिक्षेप हतस्त्वमिति चाब्रवीत्॥
en: And raising up the tree with his arms, and uttering a roar, that exceedingly powerful one aiming at Rāma discharged it, exclaiming, Dead you are.

sa_verse: तमापतन्तं बाणौघैश्छित्वा रामः प्रतापवान्। रोषमाहारयत् तीव्र निहन्तुं समरे खरम्॥
en: And as it descended, the puissant Rāma cut it off by means of a multitude of shafts, got into a mighty rage for the purpose of slaying Khara is battle.

sa_verse: जातस्वेदस्ततो रामो रोषरक्तान्तलोचनः। निर्बिभेद सहस्रेण बाणानां समरे खरम्॥
en: Then Rāma perspiring, with eyes reddened in wrath, pierced Khara in battle with a thousand shafts.

sa_verse: तस्य बाणान्तराद् रक्तं बहु सुस्राव फेनिलम् । गिरेः प्रस्रवणस्येव धारणां च परिस्रवः॥ विकलः स कृतो बाणैः खरो रामेण संयुगे। मत्तो रुधिरगन्धेन तमेवाभ्यद्रवद् द्रुतम्॥
en: And blood mixed with froth gushed by the sides of the shafts, like torrents flowing from fountain in a hill* stupefied in battle by the shafts shot by Rāina, and maddened by the smell of blood Khara furiously inade for Rāma. The text varies slightly in other texts.

sa_verse: तमापतन्तं संक्रुद्धं कृतास्त्रो रुधिराप्लुतम्। अपासर्पद् द्वित्रिपदं किंचित्वरित विक्रमः॥
en: And as he (Khara) was rushing on, bathed with blood, Rāma cquipped with arms, suddenly summoning his strength walked backward two or three paces.

sa_verse: ततः पावकसंकाशं वधाय समरे शरम्। खरस्य रामो जग्राह ब्रह्मदण्डमिवापरम् ॥
en: Then with the view of bringing about (Khara's end) Rāma took up in the conflict an arrow resembling fire or another weapon of Brahmã himself.

sa_verse: स तद् दत्तं मघवता सुरराजेन धीमता। संदधे च स धर्मात्मा मुमोच च खरं प्रति ॥
en: And that righteous one shot at Khara, that (arrow), which had been conferred on him by the intelligent Maghavān.

sa_verse: स विमुक्तो महाबाणो निर्घातसमनि:स्वनः। रामेण धनुरायम्य खरस्योरसि चापतत्॥
en: And discharged by Rāma from his bent bow, that mighty arrow with the roaring of the thunderbolt fell at Khara's breast.

sa_verse: स वृत्र इव वज्रेण फेनेन नमुचिर्यथा। बलो वेन्द्राशनिहतो निपपात हतः खरः॥
en: And threat Khara slain fell down like Vritra slain by the thunder-bolt, or Namuci by foam, or Bala by Indra's Ašani. Namuci was slain by a thunder-bolt laid over with foam.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे देवाश्चारणैः सह संगताः। दुन्दुभीश्चाभिनिघ्नन्तः पुष्पवर्षं समन्ततः॥ रामस्योपरि संहृष्टा ववर्षुर्विस्मितास्तदा। अधिकमुहूर्तेन रामेण निशितैः शरैः॥ चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां कामरूपिणाम्। खरदूषणमुख्यानां निहतानि महामृधे॥
en: After this, the celestials, assembled with the Cāraņas, struck with wonder joyfully sounded kettledrums and showered blossoms on Rāma. In over half a moment Rāma by means of sharpened shafts have slain in mighty encounter fourteen thousand shafts have slain in mighty encounter fourteen thousand Rākṣasas, wearing shapes at will, headed by Khara and Dūşaņa.

sa_verse: अहो बत महत्कर्म रामस्य विदितात्मनः। अहो वीर्यमहो दाढयं विष्णोरिव हि दृश्यते॥
en: Ah! mighty is the feat achieved by Rāma knowing self. Ah! this mighty prowess, this mighty firmness, show like to those of Vişnu himself.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा ते सर्वे ययुर्देवा यथागतम्। ततो राजर्षयः सर्वे संगताः परमर्षयः॥ सभाज्य मुद्रिता रामं सागस्त्या इदमब्रुवन्।
en: Saying this all the deities went to from where they had come. Then the Rājarşis in company with supreme saints, with Agastya (at their head), gladly paying homage to Rāma, said the following words.

sa_verse: एतदर्थं महातेजा महेन्द्रः पाकशासनः॥ शरभङ्गाश्रमं पुण्यमाजगाम पुरंदरः। एतदर्थं महातेजा महेन्द्रः पाकशासनः॥
en: It is for this that the chastiser of Pāka, the great Indra, Purandra, had paid a visit to the sacred asylum of Sarabhanga. And the Maharşis had dextrously brought you to this place, for compassing the destruction of those foes-the wicked Rākşasas.

sa_verse: एषां वधार्थं शत्रूणां रक्षसां पापकर्मणाम्। तदिदं नः कृतं कार्यं त्वया दशरथात्मज ॥ स्वधर्म प्रचरिष्यन्ति दण्डकेषु महर्षयः।
en: And it is owing to this, that, O son of Dasaratha, you have performed this mighty deed. (Now) the Maharsis will carry on their proper pious offices in the Dandaka.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे वीरो लक्ष्मणः सह सीतया॥ गिरिदुर्गाद् विनिष्क्रम्य संविवेशाश्रमे सुखी।
en: After this, that hero, Laksmana, accompanied with Sītā came out of the mountain cavern, and joyfully entered the asylum.

sa_verse: ततो रामस्तु विजयी पूज्यमानो महर्षिभिः॥ प्रविवेशाश्रमं वीरो लक्ष्मणेनाभिपूजितः।
en: Then the victorious and heroic, Rāma, honoured by the Maharsis, entered the asylum, worshipped by Lakṣmaṇa.

sa_verse: तं दृष्ट्वा शत्रुहन्तारं महर्षीणां सुखावहम्॥ बभूव हृष्टा वैदेही भर्ताहं परिषस्वजे। मुदा परमया युक्ता दृष्ट्वा रक्षोगणान् हतान्। रामं चैवाव्ययं दृष्ट्वा तुतोष जनकात्मजा॥
en: And seeing that destroyer of foes, and bringer of coinfort to the Maharsis, her husband, Vaidehi embraced him. And seeing the multitudes of Raksasas siain, Janaka's daughter, beholding the undeteriorating Rāma, ministered to him with supreme joy.

sa_verse: ततस्तु तं राक्षससङ्घमर्दनं सम्पूज्यमानं मुदितैर्ममात्मभिः। पुनः परिष्वज्य मुदान्वितानना बभूव दृष्ट्वा जनकात्मजा तदा। ४१।।
en: And with a delightful countenance again embracing that destroyer of foes, who had been honoured by the delighted Maharşis, Janaka's daughter became exceedingly happy. honoured by the delighted Maharşis, Janaka's daughter became exceedingly happy.

sa_verse: त्वरमाणस्ततो गत्वा जनस्थानादकम्पनः। प्रविश्य लङ्कां वेगेन रावणं वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then Akampana bestirring himself, speedily issuing out of Janasthāna, spoke to Rāvana,

sa_verse: जनस्थानस्थिता राजन् राक्षसा बहवो हताः
खरश्च निहतः संख्ये कथंचिदहमागतः॥
en: O king, a great many Rākşasa living in Janasthāna, have been slain, and Khara also had been slain in battle. I alone have with much difficulty managed to come here.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो दशग्रीवः क्रुद्धः संरक्तलोचनः। अकम्पनमुवाचेदं निर्दहन्निव तेजसा॥
en: Thus addressed, the ten-necked one, flaming up in energy, with his eyes reddened in wrath, said this to Akampana,

sa_verse: केन भीमं जनस्थानं हतं मम परासुना। को हि सर्वेषु लोकेषु गति नाधिगमिष्यति॥
en: Who, having his days numbered, had ravaged the dreadful Janasthāna? Who shall no more wend the way of all beings?

sa_verse: स हि मे विप्रियं कृत्वा शक्यं मघवता सुखम्। प्राप्तुं वैश्रवणेनापि न यमेन च विष्णुना ॥
en: Doing me a bad turn, Maghavan himself, or Vaiśravana, or Yama, or Vişnu, cannot attain happiness.

sa_verse: कालस्य चाप्यहं कालो दहेयमपि पावकम्। मृत्यु मरणधर्मेण संयोजयितुमुत्सहे॥
en: I am the destroyer of the Destroyer himself; and I burn even very Fire. And I can bring Death itself to mortality.

sa_verse: वातस्य तरसा वेगं निहन्तुमपि चोत्सहे। दहेयमपि संक्रुद्धस्तेजसाऽऽदित्यपावकौ॥
en: I can by my impetus resist the force of the wind. And when enraged, I can by my energy consume the Sun and Fire.

sa_verse: तथा क्रुद्धं दशग्रीवं कृताञ्जलिरकम्पनः। भयात् संदिग्धया वाचा रावणं याचतेऽभयम्॥
en: Thereat, Akampana, with joined hands, from fear replied to the ten-necked Rāvana, in faltering words, beseeching courage.

sa_verse: दशग्रीवोऽभयं तस्मै प्रददौ रक्षसां वरः। स विस्रब्धोऽब्रवीद् वाक्यमसंदिग्धमकम्पनः॥
en: Thereat that foremost of Raksasas, the tennecked one, granted him courage. Then inspired with confidence, Akampana without fear spoke,

sa_verse: पुत्रो दशरथस्यास्ते सिंहसंहननो युवा। रामो नाम महास्कन्धो वृत्तायतमहाभुजः॥ श्यामः पृथुयशाः श्रीमानतुल्यबलविक्रमः। हतस्तेन जनस्थाने खरश्च सहदूषणः॥
en: There is a son of Dasaratha, youthful, resembling a lion*, named Rāma. of broad shoulders, and possessed of excellent beauty of long and mighty-arms. (he) is sable-hued, of high fame, and of matchless prowess and vigour. It is he that in Janasthāna has slain Khara with Dusana. Another reading is Virunga: i upopetu: possessed of handsome person.-

sa_verse: अकम्पनवचः श्रुत्वा रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। नागेन्द्र इव निःश्वस्य इदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Hearing Akampana's words, that lord of the Rākşasas, Rāvana, breathing like a mighty serpent, said these words,

sa_verse: स सुरेन्द्रेण संयुक्तो रामः सर्वामरैः सह। उपयातो जनस्थानं ब्रूहि कच्चिदकम्पन ॥
en: Tell me, O Akampana, has Rāma come to Janasthāna, accompanied with the sovereign of the celestials and the body of the immortals?

sa_verse: रावणस्य पुनर्वाक्यं निशम्य तदकम्पनः। आचचक्षे बलं तस्य विक्रमं च महात्मनः॥
en: Again hearing Rāvana's words, Akampana described the strength and energy of that highsouled one.

sa_verse: रामो नाम महातेजाः श्रेष्ठः सर्वधनुष्मताम्। दिव्यास्त्रगुणसम्पन्नः परं धर्मं गतो युधि ॥
en: He) is named Rama, and is exceedingly energetic; the foremost of al bowmenfurnished with celestial panoply; and is possessed of pre-eminent prowess in warfare.

sa_verse: तस्यानुरूपो बलवान् रक्ताक्षो दुन्दुभिस्वनः। कनीयाँलक्ष्मणो भ्राता राकाशशिनिभाननः॥
en: Like to him in strength, of red eyes, and gifted with a voice like the sound of a kettledrum, his younger brother, Laksmana has a countenance resembling the full-moon.

sa_verse: स तेन सह संयुक्तः पावकेनानिलो यथा। श्रीमान् राजवरस्तेन जनस्थानं निपातितम्॥
en: He has met with him (Rāma) as the wind meeteth with a flame. he is endeued with grace, and is the foremost of monarchs. It is he who has ravaged Janasthāna.

sa_verse: नैव देवा महात्मानो नात्र कार्या विचारणा। शरा रामेण तूत्सृष्टा रुक्मपुङ्खाः पतत्त्रिणः॥ सर्पाः पञ्चानना भूत्वा भक्षयन्ति स्म राक्षसान्।
en: The magnanimous gods did not come there, No doubts need be entertained on this head. The feathered shafts, plated with gold near the plumed part, becoming five-mouthed serpents at up the Raksasas.

sa_verse: येन येन च गच्छन्ति राक्षसा भयकर्षिताः॥ तेन तेन स्म पश्यन्ति राममेवाग्रतः स्थितम्। इत्थं विनाशितं तेन जनस्थानं तवानघ ॥
en: Wherever oppressed with fear the Rākṣasas go, they see Rāma stationed before them. In this way, O sinless one, has Janasthāna been exterminated by him.

sa_verse: अकम्पनवचः श्रुत्वा रावणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। गमिष्यामि जनस्थानं रामं हन्तुं सलक्ष्मणम्॥
en: Hearing Akmpana's words, Ravana said, “I will go to Janasthāna for slaying Rāma with Laksmana.

sa_verse: अथैवमुक्ते वचने प्रोवाचेदमकम्पनः। शृणु राजन् यथावृत्तं रामस्य बलपौरुषम्॥
en: When he had said this, Akampana said, He ar, O king, the true report of Rāma's prowess and manliness.

sa_verse: असाध्यः कुपितो रामो विक्रमेण महायशाः। आपगायास्तु पूर्णाया वेगं परिहरेच्छरैः॥ सताराग्रहनक्षत्रं नभश्चाप्यवसादयेत् ।
en: Enraged, the highly famous Rāma cannot by putting forth vigour be checked. And by means of his shafts, he can me river in full flood turn its course. And he can bring down from the sky its stars and planets, and that graceful one can recover the depressed Earth.

sa_verse: असौरामस्तु सीदन्ती श्रीमानभ्युद्धरेन्महीम्॥ भित्वा वेलां समुद्रस्य लोकानाप्लावयेद् विभुः। वेगं वापि समुद्रस्य वायुं वा विधमेच्छरैः॥ संहत्य वा पुनर्लोकान् विक्रमेण महायशाः। शक्तः श्रेष्ठः स पुरुषः स्रष्टुं पुनरपि प्रजाः॥
en: That lord can sub-merge all creatures by riving the continents of the sea, and with his shafts can resist the onset of the occan, and the wind; and that illustrious one that foremost of persons by virtue of his vigour, destroying the worlds, can again create all creatures.

sa_verse: नहि रामो दशग्रीव शक्यो जेतुं रणे त्वया। रक्षसां वापि लोकेन स्वर्ग: पापजनैरिव ॥
en: O ten necked one, forsooth, Rāma cannot be subdued in conflict, either by you or the world of Rākşasas, as heaven is incapable of being attained by a sinner.

sa_verse: न तं वध्यमहं मन्ये सर्वैर्देवासुरैरपि। अयं तस्य वधोपायस्तन्ममैकमनाः शृणु॥
en: I deem him incapable of being slain by all the Gods and Asuras together. This alone is the means of slaying. Do you heedfully listen to it!

sa_verse: भार्या तस्योत्तमा लोके सीता नाम सुमध्यमा। श्यामा समविभक्ताङ्गी स्त्रीरत्नं रत्नभूषिता॥
en: He has a wife of sterling worth in the world, and that slender-waisted one is known by the name of Sītā. She is in the full bloom of youth, and has a symmetrical person-a jewel among womankind embellished with jewels.

sa_verse: नैव देवी न गन्धर्वी नाप्सरा न च पन्नगी। तुल्या सीमन्तिनी तस्य मानुषी तु कुतो भवेत्॥
en: And neither a goddess, nor a Gandharvī, nor yet an Apsarī, nor a. Pannagī is equal to her; and what is a human female?

sa_verse: तस्यापहर भार्यां त्वं तं प्रमथ्य महावने। सीतया रहितो रामो न चैव हि भविष्यति ॥
en: Thrashing him, do you in the mighty forest, carry away his wife. Without Sītā, Rama shall cease to exist.

sa_verse: अरोचयत तद्वाक्यं रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। चिन्तयित्वा महाबाहुरकम्पनमुवाच ह॥
en: Thereupon, the lord of the Rākṣasas, Rāvaņa, happened to relish those words; and reflecting (a while), that mighty-armed one addressed Akampana.

sa_verse: बाढं कल्यं गमिष्यामि ह्येकः सारथिना सह। आनेष्यामि च वेदेहीमिमां दृष्टो महापुरीम्॥
en: Excellent well. I will go there alone, accompanied by my charioteer only. I will this very morning with a glad heart bring Vaidehi to this spacious palace.

sa_verse: तदेवमुक्त्वा प्रययौ खरयुक्तेन रावणः। रथेनादित्यवर्णेन दिशः सर्वाः प्रकाशयन्॥
en: Saying this, Ravana departed, lighting up all sides, on a sun-shiny car, yoked with mules.

sa_verse: स रथो राक्षसेन्द्रस्य नक्षत्रपथगो महान्। चञ्चूर्यमाणः शुशुभे जलदे चन्द्रमा इव॥ स दूरे चाश्रमं गत्वा ताटकेयमुपागमत्। मारीचेनार्चितो राजा भक्ष्यभोज्यैरमानुषैः ॥
en: And coursing the firmament, that mighty car of that foremost of Rākşasas looked like the Moon among clouds. And proceeding far, he, approaching the asylum (of Taraka's son), presented himself before him. And Mārīca entertained the king with meats and drinks passing human.

sa_verse: तं स्वयं पूजयित्वा तु आसनेनोदकेन च। अर्थोपहितया वाचा मारीचो वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having entertained him personally with a seat and water (to wash the feet), Marica spoke these pregnant words.

sa_verse: कच्चित् सुकुशलं राजैल्लोकानां राक्षसाधिप। आशङ्के नाधिजाने त्वं यतस्तूर्णमुपागतः॥
en: O king, O lord of the Rākşasas, is it well with the worlds? I am filled with fear: I apprehend that all is not right, since you have come hither (alone) in such post-haste speed.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो महातेजा मारीचेन स रावणः। ततः पश्चादिदं वाक्यमब्रवीद् वाक्यकोविदः॥
en: Thus addressed by Mārica, the highly energetic Ravana, versed in speech, said.

sa_verse: आरक्षो मे हतस्तात रामेणाक्लिष्टकारिणा। जनस्थानमवध्यं तत् सर्वं युधि निपातितम्॥
en: My child, the guards (of Janasthāna) have been slain by Rāma of untiring energy; and all What Janasthāna, incapable of being slain, has (by him) been brought down in battle.

sa_verse: तस्य मे कुरु साचिव्यं तस्य भार्यापहारणे। राक्षसेन्द्रवचः : श्रुत्वा मारीचो वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Do you, therefore, assist me in carrying away his wife. Hearing these words of the lord of Rākş asas, Mārīca said.

sa_verse: आख्याता केन वा सीता मित्ररूपेण शत्रुणा। त्वया राक्षसशार्दूल को न नन्दति नन्दितः॥
en: What enemy of yours in the guise of a friend, has spoken of Sītā to you? And, O foremost of monarchs, who, having been, entertained by you, dose not bear you good will?

sa_verse: सीतामिहानयस्वेति को ब्रवीति ब्रवीहि मे। रक्षोलोकस्य सर्वस्य कः शृङ्ग छेत्तुमिच्छति॥
en: Tell me, who is it that has told you, 'Bring Sītā hither?' Who is it that has set his heart on severing the summit of the entire Räksasa world?

sa_verse: प्रोत्साहयति यश्च त्वां स च शत्रुरसंशयम्। आशीविषमुखात् दंष्ट्रमुद्धर्तुं चेच्छति त्वया॥
