en: He must be your enemy that excites you to this. Of this there is not the least doubt. he wishes to extract through your agency the fangs of a venomous snake.

sa_verse: कर्मणानेन केनासि कापथं प्रतिपादितः। सुखसुप्तस्य ते राजन् प्रहृतं केन मूर्धनि॥
en: Who (intends) to lead you astray by imposing on you such a deed? Who, O king, has struck in the head, you that was slumbering in peace?

sa_verse: तेजोमदः संस्थितदोर्विषाणः। उदीक्षितुं रावण नेह युक्तः स संयुगे राघवगन्धहस्ती॥
en: Rāghava in war is like a mad elephant, having unblemished ancestry for his trunk, perspiration for his temporal exudation; and arms resting well beside him for his tusks. O Rāvaņa, you are not competent even to look at an him.

sa_verse: असौ रणान्तः स्थितिसंधिवालो विदग्धरक्षोमृगहा नृसिंहः। सुप्तस्त्वया बोधयितुं न शक्यः शराङ्गपूर्णो निशितासिदंष्ट्रः॥
en: You ought not to rouse up the slecping manlion, that slayer of skilful Rākșasas resembling deer, with his sport in the field, for his joints and down; arrows for his body and sharp scimitar for his teeth.

sa_verse: चापापहारे भुजवेगपके शरोमिमाले सुमहाहवौघे। न रामपातालमुखेऽतिघोरे प्रस्कन्दितुं राक्षसराज युक्तम्।। ।
en: O Sovereign of the Räkşasas, you ought not to plunge yourself into this dreadful, and abyssless ocean, having the bow for its alligators, activity of arms for its shine, arrows for its billows, and engagement for its waters.

sa_verse: प्रसीद लङ्केश्वर राक्षसेन्द्र लङ्कां प्रसन्नो भव साधु गच्छ। त्वं स्वेषु दारेषु रमस्व नित्यं रामः सभार्यो रमतां वनेषु ॥
en: Be propitious, O lord of Lankā! O foremost of Rākşasas, with a contented heart, you had better go your way to Lankā. Do you ever sport with your own wives: let Rāma in company with his wife, sport in the woods.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो दशग्रीवो मारीचेन स रावणः। न्यवर्तत पुरी लङ्कां विवेश च गृहोत्तमम्॥
en: Thus addressed by Mārīca, the ten-throated Rāvana desisted, and entered Lankā the best of capitals.

sa_verse: ततः शूर्पणखा दृष्ट्वा सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। हतान्येकेन रामेण रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्॥ दूषणं च खरं चैव हतं त्रिशिरसं रणे। दृष्ट्वा पुनर्महानादान् ननाद जलदोपमा॥
en: Seeing fourteen thousands of Rākşasas of dreadful deeds, together with Duşaņa, and Khara, and Trisirā, slain in battle by Rāma single-handed, that one resembling clouds, Śūrpanakhā, again fell to send up mighty sounds.

sa_verse: सा दृष्ट्वा कर्म रामस्य कृतमन्यैः सुदुष्करम्। जगाम परमोद्विग्ना लङ्कां रावणपालिताम्॥
en: And witnessing Rāma's deeds, incapable of being performed by others, she, extremely agitated, went to Lankā, ruled by Rāvana.

sa_verse: सा ददर्श विमानाग्रे रावणं दीप्ततेजसम्। उपोपविष्टं सचिवैर्मरुद्भिरिव वासवम्॥ आसीनं सूर्यसंकाशे काञ्चने परमासने। रुक्मवेदिगतं प्राज्यं ज्वलन्तमिव पावकम्॥
en: And she saw the effulgent Rāvaņa in front of his palace, surrounded by his counsellors, like Vāsava surrounded by the Maruts; scated on a supreme golden seat resembling the sun, and like to a flaming fire on a golden dais kept alive by sacrificial offerings.

sa_verse: देवगन्धर्वभूतानामृषीणां च महात्मनाम्। अजेयं समरे घोरं व्यात्ताननमिवान्तकम्।६।। देवासुरविमर्देषु वज्राशनिकृतव्रणम्। ऐरावतविषाणाग्रैरुत्कृष्टकिणवक्षसम्॥
en: Unconquerable by high-souled saints, celestials, Gandharvas and all creatures; terrible like the Destroyer with his mouth wide open; his persons containing scars* of wounds inflicted by the thunder bolt and the lightings, in the war between the gods and Asuras; his breast bearing marks of attacks made by Airāvata with the ends of his tusks. *This reminds one of Milton: of Belzebub, he says, His person Deep scars of thunder had intrenched-Par, Lost-Book I.

sa_verse: विंशद्भुजं दशग्रीवं दर्शनीयपरिच्छदम्। विशालवक्षसं वीरं राजलक्षणलक्षितम्॥ नद्धवैदूर्यसंकाशं तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम्। सुभुजं शुक्लदशनं महास्यं पर्वतोपमम्॥
en: Having twenty hands, and ten heads, wearing elegant attire; broad breasted; heroic; marked with royal signs; (in hue) resembling cool lapises; embellished. in ornaments of burnished gold; having goodly hands, white teeth, and a huge face resembling a hill.

sa_verse: विष्णुचक्रनिपातैश्च शतशो देवसंयुगे। अन्यैः शस्त्रैः प्रहारैश्च महायद्धेषु ताडितम्॥ अहताङ्गैः समस्तैस्तं देवप्रहरणैस्तदा। अक्षोभ्याणां समुद्राणां क्षोभणं क्षिप्रकारिणम्॥ क्षेप्तारं पर्वताग्राणं सुराणां च प्रमर्दनम्। उच्छेत्तारं च धर्माणां परदाराभिमर्शनम्॥
en: Even him who in the war of the gods had been assailed an hundred ways with the descent of Viş ņu's discus; whose body had been cut with all the weapons of the celestials; (him). who furiously disturbs the deep incapable of being disturbed; uproots mountain summits, and tramples over gods, the destroyer of righteousness, and the violator of other's wives.

sa_verse: सर्वदिव्यास्त्रयोक्तारं यज्ञविघ्नकरं सदा। पुरीं भोगवतीं गत्वा पराजित्य च वासुकिम्॥ तक्षकस्य प्रियां भार्यां पराजित्य जहार यः।
en: The employer of all celestial arms, and the disturber of sacrifics-who going to the city of Bhogavatī, and vanquishing Vāsuki, had carried off Taksa's beloved wife.

sa_verse: कैलासं पर्वतं गत्वा विजित्य नरवाहनम्॥ विमानं पुष्पकं तस्य कामगं वै जहार यः।
en: Who, going to Kailasa, and defeating him having for his vehicle a human being, had carried of the car Puspaka coursing at every where at will;

sa_verse: वनं चैत्ररथं दिव्यं नलिनी नन्दनं वनम्॥ विनाशयति यः क्रोधाद् देवोद्यानानि वीर्यवान्।
en: Who endued with prowess had devastated the divine Caitraratha grove, the tank (situated there) and the Nandana wood, as well as the gardens of the gods;

sa_verse: चन्द्रसूर्यौ महाभागावुत्तिष्ठन्तौ परंतपौ॥ निवारयति बाहुभ्यां यः शैलशिखरोपमः।
en: And, who, himself resembling a mountain summit, had by means of his upraised arms, obstructed the rising of these repressor of foes the exalted Sun and Moon;

sa_verse: दशवर्षसहस्राणि तपस्तप्त्वा महावने॥ पुरा स्वयम्भुवे धीरः शिरांस्युपजहार यः।
en: Who, possessed of calmness, having formerly for ten thousand years carried on asceticism in the mighty forest, offered his own heads to the self create one;

sa_verse: देवदानवगन्धर्वपिशाचपतगोरगैः॥ अभयं यस्य संग्रामे मृत्युतो मानुषादृते।
en: Who in conflict fears, not death from either gods or Dānavas or Gandharvas, or Piśācas or birds or serpents, from none save human beings;

sa_verse: मन्त्रैरभिष्टुतं पुण्यमध्वरेषु द्विजातिभिः॥ हविर्धानेषु यः सोममुपहन्ति महाबलः।
en: Who, possessed of prodigious strength, forcibly takes away the clarified butter sanctified with mantras from the sacrificial ground;

sa_verse: प्राप्तयज्ञहरं दुष्टं ब्रह्मघ्नं क्रूरकारिणम्॥ कर्कशं निरनुक्रोशं प्रजानामहिते रतम्।
en: The destroyer of sacrifices about to be completed; of villainous nature; the slaughterer of Brahmanas; of cruel deeds; harsh and kindles, and ever bent on doing evil to all creatures;

sa_verse: रावणं सर्वभूतानां सर्वलोकभयावहम्॥ राक्षसी भ्रातरं क्रूरं सा ददर्श महाबलम्। तं दिव्यवस्त्राभरणं दिव्यमाल्योपशोभितम्॥ आसने सूपविष्टं तं काले कालमिवोद्यतम्।. राक्षसेन्द्रं महाभागं पौलस्त्यकुलनन्दनम्॥ उपगम्याब्रवीद् वाक्यं राक्षसी भयविह्वला। रावणं शत्रुहन्तारं मन्त्रिभिः परिवारितम्॥
en: And railing furiously at all creatures; the inspirer of fear in all beings. And the Räkşasas beheld her exceedingly powerful and cruel brother, wearing gorgeous apparel and ornaments, and decked in a glorious garland, seated, like the Destroyer at the time (of dissolution) ready (to destroy); the exalted chief of Rākşasas; the delight of the race of Paulastya. Stupefied with fear, the Rākşasī, approaching that destroyer of foes, Rāvana, surrounded by his counsellors, said these words.

sa_verse: तमब्रवीद् दीप्तविशाललोचनं प्रदर्शयित्वा भयलोभमोहिता। सुदारुणं वाक्यमभीतचारिणी महात्मना शूर्पणखा विरूपिता॥
en: And transported with fear and desire, Śūrpanakhā, given to fearlessly ranging every where, who had been deformed by that highsouled one, showing (her mutilation), addressed these harsh words to Rāvana of flaming and expansive eyes.

sa_verse: ततः शूर्पणखा दीना रावणं लोकरावणम्। अमात्यमध्ये संक्रुद्धा परुषं वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: Then the woe began Śūrpanakhā, in high wrath spoke harshly in the midst of the courtiers, to Rāvana, given to railing loudly against all creatures.

sa_verse: प्रमत्तश्च कामभोगेषु स्वैरवृत्तो निरङ्कुशः । समुत्पन्नं भयं घोरं बोद्धव्यं भवबुध्यसे ॥
en: Intoxicated with enjoyments, acting as you will, and without any control whatever, you do not see that a dreadful disaster is impending.

sa_verse: सक्तं ग्राम्येषु भोगेषु कामवृत्तं महीपतिम्। लुब्धं न बहु मन्यन्ते श्मशानाग्निमिव प्रजाः॥
en: The subjects do not esteem a monarch that is given to sensual enjoyments, in intent upon satisfying his lust and is covetous like the fire in a cemetery.

sa_verse: स्वयं कार्याणि यः काले नानुतिष्ठति पार्थिवः। स तु वै सह राज्येन तैश्च कार्यैर्विनश्यति॥
en: The king that dose not act at the proper time, finds destruction along with his kingdom and acts.

sa_verse: अयुक्तचारं दुर्दर्शमस्वाधीनं नराधिपम्। वर्जयन्ति नरा दूरानदीपङ्कमिव द्विपाः॥
en: Even as elephants shun the muddy river, do people shun from a distance, the ruler that dose not send cut spies, who show now himself (to his subjects), and who had lost his independence.

sa_verse: ये न रक्षन्ति विषयमस्वाधीनं नराधिपाः। ते न वृद्ध्या प्रकाशन्ते गिरयः सागरे यथा॥
en: Like to rocks in the sea, those monarchs that do not administer their dominions, that are not dependent, do not prosper.

sa_verse: आत्मवद्भिर्विगृह्य त्वं देवगन्धर्वदानवैः। अयुक्तचारश्चपलः कथं राजा भविष्यसि॥
en: Having incurred the hostility of the gods, the Dānavas and the Gandharvas of subdued souls and senses, how can you, who are fickle, and hast not employed spies, become the king?

sa_verse: त्वं तु बालस्वभावश्च बुद्धिहीनश्च राक्षस । ज्ञातव्यं तन्न जानीषे कथं राजा भविष्यसि॥
en: And. O Raksasa, you are childish and foolish; and dose not know what you should. How can you then become the king?

sa_verse: येषां चाराश्च कोशश्च लयश्च जयतां वर। अस्वाधीना नरेन्द्राणां प्राकृतैस्ते जनैः समाः॥
en: O you best of conquerors, those kings whose spies, exchequer and morality are not free, are like the common head.

sa_verse: यस्मात् पश्यन्ति दूरस्थान् सर्वानर्थान् नराधिपाः। चारेण तस्मादुच्यन्ते राजानो दीर्घचक्षुषः॥
en: It is because kings know distant dangers through spies, therefore they are styled farsighted.

sa_verse: अयुक्तचारं मन्ये त्वां प्राकृतैः सचिवैर्युतः। स्वजनं च जनस्थानं निहतं नावबुध्यसे॥
en: I believe you have no spies, and that your counsellors are common folks, since although Janasthāna with your kinsman is destroyed, yet you take it not to heart.

sa_verse: चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। हतान्येकेन रामेण खरश्च सहदूषणः॥ ऋषीणामभयं दत्तं कृतक्षेमाश्च दण्डकाः। धर्षितं च जनस्थानं रामेणाक्लिष्टकारिणा॥
en: Fourteen thousands of Raksasas of dreadful deeds, with Khara and Dūşana, have all been slain by Rāma single-handed; Rāma of untiring energy has inspired the saints with courage; the Dandakas have been benefited; and Janasthāna had been harassed.

sa_verse: त्वं तु लुब्धः प्रमत्तश्च पराधीनश्च राक्षस। विषये स्वे समुत्पन्नं यद् भयं नावबुध्यसे॥
en: But you, covetous and intoxicated and in the power of others, do not understand that a great danger is over-hanging (you).

sa_verse: तीक्ष्णमल्पप्रदातारं प्रमत्तं गर्वितं शठम्। व्यसने सर्वभूतानि नाभिधावन्ति पार्थिवम्॥
en: People do not in times of peril assist a sovereign that is wrathful, stingy, intoxicated, haughty and deceitful.

sa_verse: अतिमानिनमग्राह्यमात्मसम्भावितं नरम्। क्रोधनं व्यसने हन्ति स्वजनोऽपि नराधिपम्॥ नानुतिष्ठति कार्याणि भयेषु न विभेति च। क्षिप्रं राज्याच्च्युतोदीनस्तृणैस्तुल्यो भवेदिह ॥
en: Even his own kindred slay a sovereign that sets immense store by his own self, is of light worth, regards himself highly, and irascible. They do not serve him; nor do they fear when he intimidates them. Such an one is speedily dethroned; and reduced to poverty and becomes like a straw.

sa_verse: शुष्ककाष्ठैर्भवेत् कार्यं लोष्ठैरपि च पांसुभिः। न तु स्थानात् परिभ्रष्टैः कार्यं स्याद् वसुधाधिपैः।।१८।
en: Even dry wood may serve a purpose; or stone, or dust; but no purpose is capable of being served by a sovereign that has been cast off his place.

sa_verse: उपभुक्तं यथा वासः स्रजो वा मृदिता यथा। एवं राज्यात् परिभ्रष्टः समर्थोऽपि निरर्थकः॥
en: Like a cloth that has been worn, like a garland that has been trodden, a king that had been dethroned, although able, is of no consequence.

sa_verse: अप्रमत्तश्च यो राजा सर्वज्ञो विजितेन्द्रियः। कृतज्ञो धर्मशीलश्च स राजा तिष्ठते चिरम्॥
en: But a king that keeps his wits about him, understands everything, is of controlled senses, and grateful, and of virtuous character, endure for ever.

sa_verse: नयनाभ्यां प्रसुप्तो वा जागर्ति नयचक्षुषा। व्यक्तक्रोधप्रसादश्च स राजा पूज्यते जनैः॥
en: That king is honoured by men; that sleeping with his eyes, is awake as respects his eye of duty, and (the effects of) whose anger and favour, are seen (by all).

sa_verse: त्वं तु रावण दुर्बुद्धिर्गुणैरेतैर्विवर्जितः। यस्य तेऽविदितश्चारै रक्षसां सुमहान् वधः॥
en: But, you Rāvana, who hast not by means of spies; acquainted yourself with the slaughter of Rākşasas, are of evil understanding and bereft of all these virtues.

sa_verse: परावमन्ता विषयेषु सङ्गवान् न देशकालप्रविभागतत्ववित्। अयुक्तबुद्धिर्गुणदोष निश्चये विपन्नराज्यो नचिराद् विपत्स्यते॥
en: Given to disgracing others, ignorant of the proper distribution of time and place*, and never taking care to distinguish merits and defects, : you, your kingdom being in danger, will speedily meet with disaster. The commentator, is silent here. the meaning evidently is, you do not discern the where and when of things.

sa_verse: इति स्वदोषान् परिकीर्तितांस्तया समीक्ष्य बुद्ध्या क्षणदाचरेश्वरः। धनेन दर्पण बलेन चान्वितो विचिन्तयामास चिरं स रावणः॥
en: On his vices having been thus celebrated by her (Surpanakhā), that lord of the rangers of night, Råvaņa, musing awhile, was long plunged in thought.

sa_verse: ततः शूर्पणखां दृष्ट्वा ब्रुवन्ती परुषं वचः। अमात्यमध्ये संक्रुद्धः परिपप्रच्छ रावणः॥
en: Seeing Śūrpanakhā speak harsh words in the midst of the courtiers, Ravana, being enraged, asked her.

sa_verse: कश्च रामः कथंवीर्यः किंरूपः किंपराक्रमः। किमर्थे दण्डकारण्यं प्रविष्टश्च सुदुस्तरम्॥
en: Who is Rāma? And what is his prowess? And what his form? And what his power? And why had he entered the forest of Dandaka, difficult to range?

sa_verse: आयुघं किं च रामस्य येन ते राक्षसा हताः। खरश्च निहतः संख्ये दूषणस्त्रिशिरास्तथा॥
en: And what Rāma's weapons, by means of which he had slain the Rākşasas?. And Khara has been slain in battle, and Dūşaņa and Trisirā.

sa_verse: तत्त्वं ब्रूहि मनोज्ञाङ्गि केन त्वं च विरूपिता । इत्युक्ता राक्षसेन्द्रेण राक्षसी क्रोधमूर्छिता ॥
en: Do you, O you of a pleasing person, tell me the truth. And who had deformed you?

sa_verse: ततो रामं यथान्यायमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे। दीर्घबाहुर्विशालाक्षश्चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरः॥ कन्दर्पसमरूपश्च रामो दशरथात्मजः।
en: Thus addressed by the lord of the Rākṣasas, the Raksasi, transported with rage, commenced to duly narrate all about Rāma. Rāma the son of Dasaratha is long-armed, of expansive eyes, clad in bark and dark deer-skin, and like Kandarpa in grace.

sa_verse: शक्रचापनिभं चापं विकृष्य कनकाङ्गदम्॥ दीप्तान् त्रिपति नाराचान् सनिव महाविषान् ।
en: And drawing a bow resembling that of Sakra, decked with golden rings, he discharges blazing nāracas, like to serpents of virulent poison.

sa_verse: नाददानं शरान् घोरान् विमुञ्चन्तं महाबलम्।७।। न कार्मुकं विकर्षन्तं रामं पश्यामि संयुगे। हन्यमानं तु तत्सैन्यं पश्यामि शरवृष्टिभिः॥ इन्द्रेणेवोत्तमं सस्यमाहतं त्वश्मवृष्टिभिः। रक्षसां भीमवीर्याणां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश॥ निहतानि शरैस्तीक्ष्णैस्तेनैकेन पदातिना। अर्धाधिकमुहूर्तेन खरश्च सहदूषणः॥ ऋषीणामभयं दत्तं कृतक्षेमाश्च दण्डकाः॥ एका कथंचित्मुक्ताहं परिभूय महात्मना । स्त्रीवधं शङ्कमानेन रामेण विदितात्मना॥ भ्राता चास्य महातेजा गुणतस्तुल्यविक्रमः।
en: I do not see in the field, Rāma drawing his bow: I only see the host being slaughtered by a shower of shafts. And as Indra destroys (a field of) goodly crops, by pouring down hail stones, fourteen thousand Räksasas of dreadful prowess, as well as Khara and Duşaņa were in a little over a moment slain with sharp shafts by Rāma alone fighting on foot. And he had reassured the saints, and after having been deformed, I alone from fear of slaying a woman, have been let off by the high-souled Rāma knowing self.

sa_verse: अनुरक्तश्च भक्तश्च लक्ष्मणो नाम वीर्यवान्॥ अमर्षी दुर्जयो जेता विक्रान्तो बुद्धिमान् बली। रामस्य दक्षिणो बाहुनित्यं प्राणो बहिश्चरः॥
en: His brother is endued with mighty energy, and in merit, is of equal prowess, and he is devoted to his brother, and bear him regard; the puissant one is named Laksmana. And wrathful and invincible and victorious, and powerful, and intelligent and mighty, (he) is Rāma's right-arı-his life ranging externally.

sa_verse: रामस्य तु विशालाक्षी पूर्णेन्दुसदृशानना। धर्मपत्नी प्रिया नित्यं भर्तुः प्रियहिते रता॥
en: And Rāma's virtuously wedded beloved wife having expansive eyes, and a face resembling the full-moon, is ever to the welfare of her lord.

sa_verse: सा सुकेशी सुनासोरू: सुरूपा च यशस्विनी। देवतेव वनस्यास्य राजते श्रीरिवापरा॥
en: And that fair-haired, fair-nosed, and fairthighed illustrious one possessed of beauty, grace the forest like a goddess, as if a goddess of wealth herself.

sa_verse: तप्तकाञ्चनवर्णाभा रक्ततुङ्गनखी शुभा। सीता नाम वरारोहा वैदेही तनुमध्यमा॥
en: Of the lustre of burnished gold, with her finger nails reddish and projecting, and graceful, that surpassingly lovely wench is named Sītā—the slender-waisted daughter of Videha.

sa_verse: नैव देवी न गन्धर्वी न यक्षी न च किंनरी। तथारूपा मया नारी दृष्टपूर्वा महीतले॥
en: And neither a goddess, nor a Gandharvi, nor a Yaksi, nor a Kinnari, had I seen before on earth, possessed of such beauty.

sa_verse: यस्य सीता भवेद् भार्या यं च हृष्टा परिष्वजेत्। अभिजीवेत् स सर्वेषु लोकेष्वपि पुरंदरात्॥
en: He that shall have Sītā for his spouse, and who shall be warmly embraced by her, shall live longer in the world than the Lord of celestials himself.

sa_verse: सा सुशीला वपुःश्लाध्या रूपेणाप्रतिमा भुवि। तवानुरूपा भार्या सा त्वं च तस्याः पतिर्वरः॥
en: That good-natured girl, unparalleled on earth in loveliness, who can well pride herself on her person, is a worthy wife for you, and you too are a fit husband for her.

sa_verse: तां तु विस्तीर्णजघनां पीनोत्तुङ्गपयोधराम्। भार्यार्थे तु तवानेतुमुद्यताहं वराननाम्॥ विरूपितास्मि क्रूरेण लक्ष्मणेन महाभुज।
en: It is to bring over for you that one of spacious hips, and a high and well-developed bust, that I had put forth my endeavours. But, O mightyarmed one I have been disfigured by the wicked Laksmana.

sa_verse: तां तु दृष्ट्वाद्य वैदेही पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम्॥ मन्मथस्य शराणां च त्वं विधेयो भविष्यसि।
en: As soon as you have seen Vaidehi having a countenance resembling the full moon, you shall be afflicted with the shafts of Cupid.

sa_verse: यदि तस्यामभिप्रायो भार्यात्वे तव जायते। शीघ्रमुद्भियतां पादो जयार्थमिह दक्षिणः॥
en: If it is your purpose to have her for your wife, at once stretch forth your right leg. for attaining success.

sa_verse: रोचते यति ते वाक्यं ममैतद् राक्षसेश्वर। क्रियतां निर्विशङ्केन वचनं मम रावण ॥
en: If, O lord of Raksasas, you relish my speech, do you then, O Ravana, without fear, do as I tell you.

sa_verse: विज्ञायैषामशक्तिं च क्रियतां च महाबल। सीता तवानवद्याङ्गी भार्यात्वे राक्षसेश्वर॥
en: Understanding their incapacity, do you, O lord of Rākṣasas, for making her your wife, by force carry away the frail Sītā of a blameless person.

sa_verse: हताञ्जनस्थानगतान् निशाचरान्। खरं च दृष्ट्वा निहतं च दूषणं त्वमद्य कृत्यं प्रतिपत्तुमर्हसि॥
en: Hearing that Rāma by means of straight coursing shafts, has slain the Rākşasas that had gone to Janasthāna, and seeing Khara and Düşaņa, killed, do you ascertaining your course, adopt it.

sa_verse: ततः शूर्पणखावाक्यं तच्छ्रुत्वा रोमहर्षणाम्। सचिवानभ्यनुज्ञाय कार्यं बुद्ध्वा जगाम ह॥
en: Hearing those words of Śūrpanakhā, able to make one's hair stand on end, (Ravana) ascertaining his course after issuing his orders to this counsellors, departed.

sa_verse: तत् कार्यमनुगम्यान्तर्यथावदुपलभ्य च। दोषाणां च गुणानां च सम्प्रधार्य बलाबलम्॥ इति कर्तव्यमित्येव कृत्वा निश्चयमात्मनः। स्थिरबुद्धिस्ततो रम्यां यानशालां जगाम ह॥
en: And proposing to himself that act, weighing its good and evil, considering his capacity or Otherwise, and (finally) determining his purpose, Rāvana with a fixed mird went to the handsome stable.

sa_verse: यानशालां ततो गत्वा प्रच्छन्नं राक्षसाधिपः। सूतं संचोदयामास रथः संयुज्यताममिति ॥
en: And going to the stable in disguise, the lord of the Rākṣasasas commanded the charioteer, saying, Yoke the car.:

sa_verse: एवमुक्तः क्षणेनैव सारथिलघुविक्रमः। रथं संयोजयामास तस्याभिमतमुत्तमम्॥
en: Thus asked, the charioteer possessed of fleet vigour, in a trice, yoked an excellent car after his heart.

sa_verse: कामगं रथमास्थाय काञ्चनं रत्नभूषितम्। पिशाचवदनैर्युक्तं खरैः कनकभूषणैः॥ मेघप्रतिमनादेन स तेन धनदानुजः। राक्षसाधिपतिः श्रीमान् ययौ नदनदीपतिम्॥
en: And ascending the car coursing at will, made of gold and garnished with spectral faces as well as arrow decked with gold, resembling a mass of clouds, the graceful ruler of the Rākşasasyounger brother to the Lord of wealth proceeded in that noiseless (vehicle), past the lord of rivers and streams.

sa_verse: स श्वेतवालव्यजनः श्वेतच्छत्रो दशाननः। स्निग्धवैदूर्यसंकाशस्तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणः॥ दशग्रीवो विंशतिभुजो दर्शनीयपरिच्छदः। त्रिदशारिर्मुनीन्द्रघ्नो दशशीर्ष इवाद्रिराट्॥ कामगं रथमास्थाय शुशुभे राक्षसाधिपः। विद्युन्मण्डलवान् मेघः सबलाक इवाम्बरे॥
en: And with chouris of white hair, and a white umbrella, having ten-faces, resembling (in hue) cool lapises, wearing ornaments of polished gold, possessed of ten mouths, and twenty arms, clad in elegant apparel, the foe of the celestials, and slayer of the foremost ascetics-having huge heads like to the monarch of mountains, the lord of the Rākşasas, mounted on that chariot coursing at will, appeared beautiful like as mass of clouds in the sky, with cranes, in the midst of lighting.

sa_verse: सशैलसागरानूपं वीर्यवानवलोकयन्। नानापुष्पफलैर्वृक्षैरनुकीर्णं सहस्रशः॥ शीतमङ्गलतोयाभिः पद्मिनीभिः समन्ततः। विशालैराश्रमपदैर्वेदिमद्भिरलंकृतम्। सालैस्तालैस्तमालैश्च तरुभिश्च सुपुष्पितैः॥ अत्यन्तनियताहारैः शोभितं परमर्षिभिः। नागैः सुपर्णैर्गन्धर्वैः किंनरैश्च सहस्रशः॥ जितकामैश्च सिद्धैश्च चारणैश्चोपशोभितम्। आजैवैखानसैषैिर्वालखिल्यैर्मरीचिपैः॥
en: And that one possessed of prowess beheld the shore of the sea, containing crag, scattered with trees bearing fruits and flowers of various kinds by thousands, bordered all around with pools furnished with cool and delightful waters, ornamented with spacious asylums having daises, graced with woods of plantain, beauteous with coconuts, and blossoming Sāla and palmyra, and tamāla, trees, grateful with supreme saints rigidly restraining their fear, and with serpents and fowls of fair feathers and Gandharvas and Kinnaras by thousands; and pleasant with Siddhas and Caraņas, that have controlled their lust; with those descendants of Brahma-the Vaikhanas, the Māsas, the Vālakhilyas, and the Marīcipas;

sa_verse: दिव्याभरणमाल्याभिर्दिव्यरूपाभिरावृतम्। क्रीडारतविधिज्ञाभिरप्सरोभिः सहस्रशः॥ सेवितं देवपत्नीभिः श्रीमतीभिरुपासितम्। देवदानवसङ्घश्च चरितं त्वमृताशिभिः॥ हंसक्रौञ्चप्लवाकीर्णं सारसैः सम्प्रसादितम्। वैदूर्यप्रस्तरं स्निग्धं सान्द्रं सागरतेजसा ॥
en: Swarming with females, divinely beautiful, delight with gorgeous ornaments and garlands, and Apsarās skilled in sport by thousands; frequented by the wives of the celestials, and honoured by graceful girls; ranged by multitudes of deities and Dänavas, feeding on ambrosia; abounding with swans, Krauncas, and frogs, echoing to (the cries of) cranes; containing stones resembling the lapis, and mild and cool by the influence of the ocean.

sa_verse: पाण्डुराणि विशालानि दिव्यमाल्ययुतानि च। तूर्यगीताभिजुष्टानि विमानानि समन्ततः॥ तपसा जितलोकानां कामगान्यभिसम्पतन्। गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव ददर्श धनदानुजः॥
en: And as he proceeded, the younger brother of the Lord of wealth, saw on all sides spacious cars capable of coursing at will, sable, furnished with fair garlands, and resounding with trumpet notes, and Gandharvas and Apsaras.

sa_verse: निर्यासरसमूलानां चन्दनानां सहस्रशः। वनानि पश्यन् सौम्यानि घ्राणतृप्तिकराणि च ॥ अगुरूणां च मुख्यानां वनान्युपवनानि च। तक्कोलानां च जात्यानां फलानां च सुगन्धिनाम्।।२२। पुष्पाणि च तमालस्य गुल्मानि मरिचस्य च। मुक्तानां च समूहानि शुष्यमाणानि तीरतः॥ शैलानि प्रवरांश्चैव प्रवालनिचयांस्तथा। काञ्चनानि च शृङ्गाणि राजतानि तथैव च॥ प्रस्रवाणि मनोज्ञानि प्रसन्नान्यद्भुतानि च। धनधान्योपपन्नानि स्त्रीरत्नैरावृतानि च॥ हस्त्यश्वरथगाढानि नगराणि विलोकयन्। तं समं सर्वतः स्निग्धं मृदुसंस्पर्शमारुतम्॥ अनूपे सिन्धुराजस्य ददर्श त्रिदिवोपमम्।
en: And surveying by thousands woods of sandal exuding gum at their roots*, and of choice aguru, grateful to the sense of smell; and woods and groves of excellent and odorous takkola fruits; blossoms of the Tamāla, and thickets of black pepper; heaps of pearls drying on the shore; rocks; the best corals in masses, summits of gold and silver;+ charming pellucid rills at places; and cities filled with corn and wealth, abounding in gems of women, and thronged with horses, elephants and cars—Rāvana on the shore of the ocean beheld around a level and soft scene, resembling heaven itself—where breezes of delicate feel kept breathing. *Niryāsarasamūlānām, gen, (sandal), which forms the principal ingredient in perfumes, containing odorous gums. Another reading is: heaps of conch. Some text read Sailam-hills.

sa_verse: तत्रापश्यत् स मेघाभं न्यग्रोधं मुनिभिर्वृतम्॥ समन्ताद् यस्य ताः शाखा: शतयोजनमायताः। यस्य हस्तिनमादाय महाकायं च कच्छपम्॥ भक्षार्थं गरुडः शाखामाजगाम महाबलः।
en: And there he saw a fig tree, hued like clouds, surrounded by ascetics; its branches stretched around an hundred Yojanas, and the exceedingly powerful Garuda had ascended one of its boughs, taking an elephant and a huge tortoise, for the purpose of devouring them.

sa_verse: तस्य तां सहसा शाखां भारेण पतगोत्तमः॥ सुपर्णः पर्णबहुला बभञ्जाथ महाबलः।
en: And that best of birds, the powerful Suparņa by his weight suddenly broke a branch containing full many leaves.

sa_verse: तत्र वैखानसा माषा वालखिल्या मरीचिपाः॥ आजा बभूवुधूम्राश्च संगताः परमर्षयः।
en: And it came to pass that Vaikhānasas, Māşas, Valakhilyas, Maricipas, Ayas, and Dhumras, saints of the highest order-had assembled there.

sa_verse: तेषां दयार्थं गरुडस्तां शाखां शतयोजनाम्॥ भग्नामादाय वेगेन तौ चोभौ गजकच्छपौ। एकपादेन धर्मात्मा भक्षयित्वा तदामिषम्॥ निषादविषयं हत्वा शाखया पतगोत्तमः। प्रहर्षमतुलं लेभे मोक्षयित्वा महामुनीन् ॥
en: Thereat, moved by commiseration (for the ascetics), the virtuous Garuda, taking with proinptitude the broken bough measuring an hundred Yojanas as well as the elephant and the tortoise, by one leg, at length eating up the animals, and by means of the bough, exterminating the country of the Nişādas-that best of birds attained unequalled delight in consequence of having rescued the mighty ascetics.

sa_verse: स तु तेन प्रहर्षेण द्विगुणीकृतविक्रमः। अमृतानयनार्थं वै चकार मतिमान् मतिम्॥
en: Thereat, attaining double energy by virtue of that delight, that intelligent one set his heart on bringing ambrosia.

sa_verse: अयोजालानि निर्मथ्य भित्वा रत्नगृहं वरम्। महेन्द्रभवनाद् गुप्तमाजहारामृतं ततः॥
en: And tearing off the iron links of the network, and bursting into the repository of gem, he carried away secreted ambrosia from the residence of the great Indra.

sa_verse: तं महर्षिगणैर्जुष्टं सुपर्णकृतलक्षणम्। नाम्ना सुभद्रं न्यग्रोधं ददर्श धनदानुजः॥
en: Kubera's younger brother beheld this fig tree, named Suparna had left his mark.

sa_verse: तं तु गत्वा परं पारं समुद्रस्य नदीपतेः। ददर्शाश्रममेकान्ते पुण्ये रम्ये वनान्तरे॥
en: Then repairing to the other shore of that lord of rivers, the ocean, Rāvana saw an asylum lying in the forest in a charming and sacred recess.

sa_verse: तत्र कृष्णाजिनधरं जटामण्डलधारिणम्। ददर्श नियताहारं मारीचं नाम राक्षसम्॥
en: And there he saw a Rākṣasa, named Mārīca, clad in a dark deer-skin, bearing a head of matted locks, subsisting on restricted fare.

sa_verse: स रावणः समागम्य विधिवत् तेन रक्षसा। मारीचेनार्चितो राजा सर्वकामैरमानुषैः॥
en: And approaching Ravana duly, the Raksasa, Mārīca, received the king with every hospitality passing human.

sa_verse: तं स्वयं पूजयित्वा च भोजनेनोदकेन च। अर्थोपहितया वाचा मारीचो वाक्यमब्रवीत्॥
en: And having entertained him personally with meats and drinks, Mārica addressed him in weighty words.

sa_verse: कच्चित्ते कुशलं राजल्लङ्कायां राक्षसेश्वर। केनार्थेन पुनस्त्वं वै तूर्णमेव इहागतः॥
en: O lord of the Rākṣasas, O king, is it well with you in Lankā? And what for hast you again come hither so speedily?

sa_verse: एवमुक्तो महातेजा मारीचेन स रावणः। ततः पश्चादिदं वाक्यमब्रवीद् वाक्यकोविदः।॥
en: Thus addressed by Mārīca, that highly powerful one, skilled in speech, Ravana said.

sa_verse: मारीच श्रूयतां तात वचनं मम भाषतः। आर्तोऽस्मि मम चार्तस्य भवान् हि परमा गतिः॥
en: O Mărîca, hearken to me, my child, as I relate (everything to you). I am distressed and in this distress of mine, you are certainly my great refuge.

sa_verse: जानीषे त्वं जनस्थानं भ्राता यत्र खरो मम। दूषणश्च महाबाहु स्वसा शूर्पणखा च मे॥ त्रिशिराश्च महाबाहू राक्षसः पिशिताशनः। अन्ये च बहवः शूरा लब्धलक्षा निशाचराः॥ वसन्ति मनियोगेन अधिवासं च राक्षसाः। बाधमाना महारण्ये मुनीन् ये धर्मचारिणः॥
en: You know Janasthāna, where lives my brother Khara, and the mighty-armed Dusana, and my sister, sūrpanakhă; as also that Räksasa subsisting on (human) flesh, the long armed Trisirā, and many other heroic night rangers besides, of high enthusiasm in battlesRākşasas, who had been living there at my command, troubling in the mighty forest the ascetics carrying on their pious offices.

sa_verse: चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसां भीमकर्मणाम्। शूराणां लब्धलक्षाणां खरचित्तानुवर्तिनाम् ॥ ते त्विदानीं जनस्थाने वसमाना महाबलाः। सङ्गताः परमायत्ता रामेण सह संयुगे॥
en: And fourteen thousands of Rākşasas of dreadful deeds, heroic, high-spirited, Raksasas possessed of exceeding prowess; while residing in Janasthåna of late, clad in mail and equipped with various weapons, headed, by Khara, met with Rāma in the field.

sa_verse: नानाशस्त्र प्रहरणाः खरप्रमुखराक्षसाः। तेन संजातरोषेण रामेण रणमूर्धनि ॥ अनुक्त्वा परुषं किंचिच्छरैर्व्यापारितं धनुः। चतुर्दश सहस्राणि रक्षसामुग्रतेजसाम्॥ निहतानि शरैर्दीप्तैर्मानुषेण पदातिना। खरश्च निहतः संख्ये दूषणश्च निपातितः॥ हत्वा त्रिशिरसं चापि निर्भया दण्डकाः कृताः।
en: Getting enraged, Rāma in conflict without returning any harsh speech, by means of his shafts shot from his bow, has, a human being, and fighting on foot, with his flaming arrows slaughtered fourteen thousand Rākṣasas of fierce energy. And Khara has been slain in battle, and Dūşaņa also has been brought down. And having slain Triſirā too, (Rāma) has rid the Dandaka of all fear.

sa_verse: पित्रा निरस्तः कुद्धेन सभार्यः क्षीणजीवितः॥ स हन्ता तस्य सैन्यस्य रामः क्षत्रियपांसनः।
en: Having been expelled by his enraged sire, that disgrace of Kșatriyas, the weak Rāma, living in company with his wife has effected the destruction of this army.

sa_verse: अशीलः कर्कशस्तीक्ष्णो मूल् लुब्धोऽजितेन्द्रियः॥ त्यक्तधर्मा त्वधर्मात्मा भूतानामहिते रतः। येन वैरं विनारण्ये सत्वमास्थाय केवलम्॥ कर्णनासापहारेण भगिनी मे विरूपिता। अस्य भार्यां जनस्थानात् सीतां सुरसुतोपमाम्॥ आनयिष्यामि विक्रम्य सहायस्तत्र मे भव।
en: He is of a vile character, crabbed, foolish, covetous, of uncontrolled senses, bereft of morality, sinful, and ever engaged in the evil of all creatures, by whom, violently without hostility my sister has been deformed in the forest by having her nose and ears cut off. Of him will I carry off by force from Janasthāna, his wife, Sītā, resembling the daughter of a celestial.

sa_verse: त्वया ह्यहं सहायेन पावस्थेन महाबल॥ भ्रातृभिश्च सुरान् सर्वान् नाहमत्राभिचिन्तये। तत्सहायो भव त्वं मे समर्थो ह्यसि राक्षस॥
en: Be you my help in it. For certain, O exceedingly strong one, if you help me at my side, if my brothers also back me, I do not think much of all the celestials. Therefore, be you my help; for, you are capable, O Raksasa.

sa_verse: वीर्ये युद्धे च दर्प च न ह्यस्ति सदृशस्तव। उपायतो महाशूरो महामायाविशारदः॥
en: In prowess in battle, and in indomitableness, there is none like you. You are a mighty hero commanding resources, and conversant with potent illusory displays.

sa_verse: एतदर्थमहं प्राप्तस्त्वत्समीपं निशाचर। शृणु तत् कर्म साहाय्ये यत् कार्यं वचनान्मम॥
en: Taking this to my heart, I have come to you, O ranger of the night. Do you listen as to the business in which by my command you are to help ime.

sa_verse: सौवर्णस्त्वं मृगो भूत्वा चित्रो रजतबिन्दुभिः। आश्रमे तस्य रामस्य सीतायाः प्रमुखे चर॥
en: Becoming a golden deer, marked with silver spots, do you range about in Rāma's asylum in presence of Sītā.

sa_verse: त्वां तु नि:संशयं सीता दृष्ट्वा तु मृगरूपिणम्। गृह्यतामिति भर्तारं लक्ष्मणं चाभिधास्यति ॥
en: Seeing you helpless, in the shape of a deer, Sītā will say to her lord and Lakşmaņa also, Do you catch it.

sa_verse: ततस्तयोरपाये तु शून्ये सीतां यथासुखम्। निराबाधो हनिष्यामि राहुश्चन्द्रप्रभामिव ॥
en: When they shall have departed, I shall carry off from the empty asylum, Sītã without let, like Rāhu depriving the Moon of his splendour.

sa_verse: ततः पश्चात् सुखं रामे भार्याहरणकर्शिते। विस्रब्धं प्रहरिष्यामि कृतार्थेनान्तरात्मना॥
en: Then when Rāma shall be sore afflicted because of the carrying off of his wife, I shall easily, crowning my soul with success, safely bear away Sita.

sa_verse: तस्य रामकथां क्षुत्वा मारीचस्य महात्मनः। शुष्कं समभवद् वक्त्रं परित्रस्तो बभूव च॥
en: Hearing Rāma's words, the face of the highsouled Mārīca became blank and he was seized with apprehension.

sa_verse: ओष्ठौ परिलिहशुष्कौ नेत्रैरनिमिषैरिव। मृतभूत इवार्तस्तु रावणं समुदैक्षत ॥
en: And licking up his dried lips, with winkless eyes, (Marica) as if dead, and exceedingly distressed, eyed Ravana, steadily.

sa_verse: स रावणं त्रस्तविषण्णचेता महावने रामपराक्रमः। कृताञ्जलिस्तत्त्वमुवाच वाक्यं हितं च तस्मै हितमात्मनश्च ॥
en: And alarmed because of Rāvana, with his mind depressed, Mārīca who well knew Rāma's prowess in the forest, with joined hands in agitation spoke words lending to his own as well as Rāvana's welfare.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा राक्षसेन्द्रस्य वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः। प्रत्युवाच महातेजा मारीचो राक्षसेश्वरम्॥
en: Hearing the words of that sovereign of the Rākşasas, the highly energetic Mārica, skilled in speech, answered the Raksasa chief.

sa_verse: सुलभाः पुरुषा राजन् सततं प्रियवादिनः। अप्रियस्य च पथ्यस्य वक्ता श्रोता च दुर्लभः॥
en: O king, the speaker of soft words is common, but the speaker and the listener of unwelcome though beneficial words are rarities.

sa_verse: न नूनं बुध्यसे रामं महावीर्यगुणोन्नतम्। अयुक्तचारश्चपलो महेन्द्रवरुणोपमम्॥
en: Volatile, and employing no spies, you surely do not understand the exceedingly powerful Rāma towering high in virtues, and resembling the illustrious Indra or Varuņa himself.

sa_verse: अपि स्वस्ति भवेत् तात सर्वेषामपि रक्षसाम्। अपि रामो न संक्रुद्धः कुर्याल्लोकानराक्षसान्॥
en: I shall be well, me child, if Rāma fired with rage, do not render all the world bereft of Rākşasas;

sa_verse: अपि ते जीवितान्ताय नोत्पन्ना जनकात्मजा। अपि सीतानिमित्तं च स भवेद् व्यसनं महत्॥
en: If Janaka's daughter has not sprung to compass your destruction, if a dreadful disaster do not befall you because of Sītā,

sa_verse: अपि त्वामीश्वरं प्राप्य कामवृत्तं निरङ्कशम्। नि विनश्येत् पुरी लङ्का त्वया सह सराक्षसा॥
en: And if having obtained for her lord you that are wilful and wicked, the city of Lankā with you and the Rākṣasas do not meet with utter extermination.

sa_verse: त्वद्विधः कामवृत्तो हि दुःशीलः पापमन्त्रितः। आत्मानं स्वजनं राष्ट्रं स राजा हन्ति दुर्मतिः॥
en: The sovereign who is wicked, whimsical and of evil intent like you, bring about his own destruction as well as that of his kingdom and relatives.

sa_verse: न च पित्रा परित्यक्तो नामर्यादः कथंचन। न लुब्धो न च दुःशीलो न च क्षत्रियपांसनः॥
en: Rāma, the enhancer of Kausalyā's delight had not been abandoned by his father, nor is he devoid of propriety of conduct; he is not avaricious, wicked nor the destroyer of Kaşatriya race.

sa_verse: न च धर्मगुणैर्हीनः कौसल्यानन्दवर्धनः। स च तीक्ष्णो हि भूतानां सर्वभूतहिते रतः॥
en: He does not lack religious merits or accomplishments, nor is he of a harsh temperament and intent on causing misery to creatures.

sa_verse: वञ्चितं पितरं दृष्ट्वा कैकेय्या सत्यवादिनम्। करिष्यामीति धर्मात्मा ततः प्रव्रजितो वनम्॥ कैकेय्याः प्रियकामार्थं पितुर्दशरथस्य च। हित्वा राज्यं च भोगांश्च प्रविष्टो दण्डकावनम्॥
en: Finding his truthful sire imposed on by Kaikeyi he has sojourned to woods. For compassing the welfare of his father Dasaratha and Kaikeyi he has entered the forest of Dandaka.

sa_verse: न रामः कर्कशस्तात नाविद्वान् नाजितेन्द्रियः। अनृतं न श्रुतं चैव नैव त्वं वक्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: Rāma, O my child is not harsh or foolish; nor has he not control over his senses. Far from speaking untruth he does not know false stories even.

sa_verse: रामो विग्रहवान् धर्म: साधुः सत्यपराक्रमः। राजा सर्वस्य लोकस्य देवानामिव वासवः॥
en: It did not behove you therefore to use such improper language towards him. he is an incarnation of virtue, pious and truthful, and lord of all men as Vāsava of all celestials.

sa_verse: कथं नु तस्य वैदेहीं रक्षितां स्वेन तेजसा। इच्छसे प्रसभं हर्तुं प्रभामिव विवस्वतः॥
en: How does you then wish to carry away by force his Vaidehī, protected by virtue of her own chastity, like to the rays of Sun?

sa_verse: शरार्चिषमनाधृष्यं चापखङ्गेन्धनं रणे। रामाग्निं सहसा दीप्तं न प्रवेष्टुं त्वमर्हसि॥
en: It did not behove you to enter that fire of Rāma who has arrows for rays and bow and scimitar for fuel. It did not behove you,

sa_verse: धनुर्व्यादितदीप्तास्यं शरार्चिषममर्षणम्। चापबाणधरं तीक्ष्णं शत्रुसेनापहारिणम्॥ राज्यं सुखं च संत्यज्य जीवितं चेष्टमात्मनः। नात्यासादयितुं तात रामान्तकमिहार्हसि ॥
en: O Rāvana, to approach Räma renouncing your kingdom, happiness and love of life, who is like to death itself and has bow for his widened and flaming mouth, and arrows for his rays and who is irrepressible, of mighty prowess, holding bows and arrows and repulsing the forces of the enemy.

sa_verse: अप्रमेयं हि तत्तेजो यस्य सा जनकात्मजा। न त्वं समर्थस्तां हर्तुं रामचापाश्रयां वने॥
en: Incomparable is his power; daughter of Janaka is his wife and lives in the forest confiding in the mighty strength of his bow; you shall not be able to carry her away.

sa_verse: तस्य वै नरसिंहस्य सिंहोरस्कस्य भामिनी। प्राणेभ्योऽपि प्रियतरा भार्या नित्यमनुव्रता॥
en: She is the beloved wife of that best of men having a leonine chest—and he holds his wife dearer than his life and is ever attached to her.

sa_verse: न सा धर्षयितुं शक्या मैथिल्योजस्विनः प्रिया। दीप्तस्येव हुताशस्य शिखा सीता सुमध्यमा॥
en: And young Sītā beloved of the mighty Rāma, and like to the rays of flaming fire is ever incapable of being carried away by you.

sa_verse: किमुद्यमं व्यर्थमिमं कृत्वा ते राक्षसाधिप। दृष्टश्चेत् त्वं रणे तेन तदन्तमुपजीवितम्॥
en: Of what avail is this vain attempt O Rākşasa chief? No sooner Rāma shall see you in the battle you shall meet with your end.

sa_verse: जीवितं च सुखं चैव राज्यं चैव सुदुर्लभम्। यदीच्छसि चिरं भोक्तुं मा कृथा रामविप्रियम्॥ स सर्वैः सचिवैः सार्धं विभीषणपुरस्कृतैः। मन्त्रयित्वा स धर्मिष्ठः : कृत्वा निश्चयमात्मनः। दोषाणां च गुणानां च सम्प्रधार्य बलाबलम्॥ आत्मनश्च बलं ज्ञात्वा राघवस्य च तत्त्वतः। हितं हि तव निश्चित्य क्षमं त्वं कर्तुमर्हसि॥
en: Hard it is to attain life, happiness and kingdom, so it behoved you to act properly, after consulting with your ministers headed by Vibhīşaņa, judging Rāma's merits and demerits and ascertaining his and your own strength and as well as your welfare.

sa_verse: अहं तु मन्ये तव न क्षमं रणे समागमं कोसलराजसूनुना। इदं हि भूयः शृणु वाक्यमुत्तमं क्षमं च युक्तं च निशाचराधिप॥
en: Methinks your approaching conflict with the son of the Kosala chief forebodes no good to you; hear again therefore O prince of the night rangers, words sensible and lending to your welfare.

sa_verse: कदाचिदप्यहं वीर्यात् पर्यटन् पृथिवीमिमाम्। बलं नागसहस्रस्य धारयन् पर्वतोपमः॥
en: Once on a time I was engaged in travelling all over the earth. I had in my body, resembling a huge mountain, the strength of a thousand Nagas.

sa_verse: नीलजीमूतसंकाशस्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः। भयं लोकस्य जनयन् किरीटी परिघायुधः॥ व्यचरन् दण्डकारण्यमृषिमांसानि भक्षयन्।
en: I had parigha in my hands, crown on my head and golden ear-rings on my ears and my body was of a dark blue colour like that of a cloud. Causing fright to the people I used to wander through the forest of Dandaka and live upon the flesh of the Rsis.

sa_verse: विश्वामित्रोऽथ धर्मात्मा मद्वित्रस्तो महामुनिः॥ स्वयं गत्वा दशरथं नरेन्द्रमिदमब्रवीत्।
en: The pious ascetic Viśvāmitra being afraid of me went in person to the king Dasaratha and said,

sa_verse: अयं रक्षतु मां रामः पर्वकाले समाहितः॥ मारीचान्मे भयं घोरं समुत्पन्नं नरेश्वर।
en: When I shall remain absorbed in meditation on the occasion of Parva, let Rāma protect me O king, Truly am I afraid of this Mārīca.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्तो धर्मात्मा राजा दशरथस्तदा॥ प्रत्युवाच महाभागं विश्वामित्रं महामुनिम्।
en: Being thus addressed by the ascetic the virtuous-souled king Dasaratha replied.

sa_verse: ऊनद्वादशवर्णोऽयमकृतास्त्रश्च राघवः॥ कामं तु मम तत् सैन्यं मया सह गमिष्यति। बलेन चतुरङ्गेण स्वयमेत्य निशाचरम्॥ वधिष्यामि मुनिश्रेष्ठ शत्रु तव यथेप्सितम्।
en: Rāghava is still under twelve years of age and had not been well disciplined in military arts. But I have soldiers enough, and if permitted by you, O you best of ascetics, I shall with my fourfold forces kill your enemies, the rangers of the night.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तः स तु मुनी राजानमिदमब्रवीत्॥ रामानान्यद् बलं लोके पर्याप्तं तस्य रक्षसः। देवतानामपि भवान् समरेष्वभिपालकः॥ आसीत् तव कृतं कर्म त्रिलोकविदितं नृप। काममस्ति महत् सैन्यं तिष्ठत्विह परंतप॥ बालोऽप्येष महातेजाः समर्थस्तस्य निग्रहे। गमिष्ये राममादाय स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु परंतप॥
en: Being thus addressed that ascetic spoke to the .monarch, saying. True it is that you were the protector of the celestials in the War and your exploits are well known to the world, but no one shall be able to withstand the Rākṣasas but Rāma. The highly energetic Rāma, though a boy is sufficiently qualified to defeat the enemies; therefore, O Destroyer of foes, let your soldiery remain here and let me proceed along with Rāma. May God bless you.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा स मुनिस्तमादाय नृपात्मजम्। जगाम परमप्रीतो विश्वामित्रः स्वमाश्रमम्॥
en: Saying this the ascetic Viśvāmitra being pleased went to his asylum along with Rāma.

sa_verse: तं तथा दण्डकारण्ये यज्ञमुद्दिश्य दिक्षितम्। बभूवोपस्थितो रामश्चित्रं विस्फारयन् धनुः॥
en: After-words having been initiated for the sacrifice in the forest of Dandaka Rāma having unstrung his mighty bow came to the ascetic to protect the sacrifice.

sa_verse: अजातव्यञ्जनः क्षीमान् बालः श्यामः शुभेक्षणः। एकवस्त्रधरो धन्वी शिखी कनकमालया॥
en: He had a gold chain round his neck, a crest on his head and a bow in his hands; he had a pair of beautiful eyes, and only one piece of cloth; his countenance was of green hue and exquisite beauty and even then moustaches or other signs of manhood did not appear on his face.

sa_verse: शोभयन् दण्डकारण्यं दीप्तेन स्वेन तेजसा। अदृश्यत तदा रामो बालचन्द्र इवोदितः॥
en: Beautifying the entire forest of Dandaka with his own splendour Rāma appeared like the newly risen moon.

sa_verse: ततोऽहं मेघसंकाशस्तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलः। बली दत्तवरो दादाजगामाश्रमान्तरम् ॥
en: Thereupon I resembling a cloud and having golden ear locks entered the asylum being proud of my power on account of the boons offered to me by Brahma.

sa_verse: तेन दृष्टः प्रविष्टोऽहं सहसैवोद्यतायुधः। मां तु दृष्ट्वा धनुः सज्यमसम्भ्रान्तश्चकार ह॥
en: Seeing me enter he took up his dart and attached string to his bow with proper care.

sa_verse: अवजानन्नह मोहाद् बालोऽयमिति राघवम्। विश्वामित्रस्य तां वेदिमभ्यधावं कृतत्वरः॥
en: Being under the influence of sheer foolishness I passed by him as a child and darted towards the sacrificial alter of Viśvāmitra.

sa_verse: तेन मुक्तस्ततो बाणः शितः शत्रुनिबर्हणः। तेनाहं ताडितः क्षिप्तः समुद्रे शतयोजने॥
en: Thereupon he wounded me with a sharpened sword capable of doing away with the enemies and threw me away into the ocean situated at a distance of hundred Yojanas.

sa_verse: नेच्छता तात मां हन्तुं तदा वीरेण रक्षितः। रामस्य शरवेगेन निरस्तो भ्रान्तचेतनः॥ पातितोऽहं तदा तेन गम्भीरे सागराम्भसि। प्राप्य संज्ञां चिरात् तात लङ्कां प्रति गतः पुरीम्॥
en: He had no mind of killing me then and for this he saved my life. I was thrown however into the deep ocean being hindered by the velocity of his arrows and having lost my consciousness. Regaining my sense after a long while I returned to the city of Lankā.

sa_verse: एवमस्मि तदा मुक्तः सहायास्ते निपातिताः। अकृतास्त्रेण रामेण बालेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ॥
en: Myself saved thus, my followers however all killed by Rāma of unwearied activity though a mere child and a novice in the are of warfare.

sa_verse: तन्मया वार्यमाणस्तु यदि रामेण विग्रहम्। करिष्यस्यापदं घोरां क्षिप्रं प्राप्य न शिष्यसि॥
en: It is for this that I do prevent you; you shall be overwhelmed with calamities and meet with destruction if do you engage yourself in battle with him. In vain shall you bring about the affliction of the mirthful and sportive Rākşasas ever witnessing social festivities.

sa_verse: क्रीडारतिविधिज्ञानां समाजोत्सवदर्शिनाम्। द्रक्ष्यसि त्वं पुरीं लङ्कां विनष्टां मैथिलीकृते॥
en: And in vain shall you for Sītā compass the destruction of the city of Lankā, adorned with diverse jewels and filled with golden edifices.

sa_verse: अकुर्वन्तोऽपि पापानि शुचयः पापसंश्रयात्। परपापैर्विनश्यन्ति मत्स्या नागह्नदे यथा॥
en: Pious men living with a vicious man, 'meet with destruction for his sins, though they themselves do not commit any misdeed, like to fish (devoured by Garuda) living in a lake where snakes dwell.

sa_verse: दिव्यचन्दनदिग्धाङ्गान् दिव्याभरणभूषितान्। द्रक्ष्यस्यभिहतान् भूमौ तव दोषात्तु तु राक्षसान्॥
en: Thus shall you witness that for your own folly the rangers of the night, adorned with celestial ornaments and having their body pasted with sandal, have been killed and brought down to the earth.

sa_verse: हृतदारान् सदारांश्च दश विद्रवतो दिशः। हतशेषानशरणान् द्रक्ष्यसि त्वं निशाचरान्॥
en: And they deprived of shelter have fled away to different directions, some with their wives and some alone, having their wives carried away (by the enemies).

sa_verse: शरजालपरिक्षिप्तामग्निज्वालासमावृताम्। प्रदग्धभवनां लङ्कां द्रक्ष्यसि त्वमसंशयम्॥
en: You shall further observe that all edifices of Lankā being enveloped with arrows and flaming fire have been burnt down to ashes.

sa_verse: परदाराभिमर्शात् तु नान्यत् पापतरं महत्। प्रमदानां सहस्राणि तव राजन् परिग्रहे॥ भव स्वदारनिरतः स्वकुलं रक्ष राक्षसान्। मानं वृद्धिं च राज्यं च जीवितं चेष्टमात्मनः॥
en: There is no greater sin on earth than carrying away another's wife. There are thousand ladies in your seraglio O king. Being attached to your wives do you preserve the race of the Rākşasas, your own line, your wished for life, kingdom, wealth and dignity.

sa_verse: कलत्राणि च सौम्यानि मित्रवर्गं तथैव च। यदीच्छसि चिरं भोक्तुं मा कृथा रामविप्रियम्॥
en: Do not bring about Rāma's mischief if do you wish to live happily with your wives and friends.

sa_verse: निवार्यमाणः सुहृदा मया भृशं प्रसह्य सीतां यति धर्षयिष्यसि। गमिष्यसि क्षीणबल: सबान्धवो यमक्षयं रामशरास्तजीवितः॥
en: I am your friend and do ask you again and again to desist (from your evil intention); if do you encroach upon Sītā surely shall you along with your kinsmen go to the abode of Yama being enfeebled by the arrows of Rāma.

sa_verse: निवार्यमाणः सुहृदा मया भृशं प्रसह्य सीतां यति धर्षयिष्यसि। गमिष्यसि क्षीणबल: सबान्धवो यमक्षयं रामशरास्तजीवितः॥
en: I am your friend and do ask you again and again to desist (from your evil intention); if do you encroach upon Sītā surely shall you along with your kinsmen go to the abode of Yama being enfeebled by the arrows of Rāma.

sa_verse: एवमस्मि तदा मुक्तः कथंचित् तेन संयुगे। इदानीमपि यद् वृत्तं तच्छृणुष्व यदुत्तरम्॥
en: I Was however somehow saved by Rāma in that conflict. Listen what happened afterwards.

sa_verse: राक्षसाभ्यामहं द्वाभ्यामनिर्विण्णस्तथाकृतः। सहितो मृगरूपाभ्यां प्रविष्टो दण्डकावने॥
en: I was not humbled even by the danger of my life. Once again I entered the forest of Dandaka accompanied by two Rākşasas assuming the shape of deer.

sa_verse: दीप्तजिह्वो महादंष्ट्रस्तीक्ष्णशृङ्गो महाबलः। व्यचरन् दण्डकारण्यं मांसभक्षो महामृगः॥ अग्निहोत्रेषु तीर्थेषु चैत्यवृक्षेषु रावण। अत्यन्तघोरो व्यचरंस्तापसांस्तान् प्रधर्षयन्॥ निहत्य दण्डकारण्ये तापमान् धर्मचारिणः। रुधिराणि पिबंस्तेषां तन्मासानि च भक्षयन्॥
en: I had a flaming tongue, huge teeth, sharpened horns and lived on the flesh of ascetics. Assuming such a terrible appearance I began to traverse with great vehemence the Tīrtha, Agnihotri and the place of worship; eating the flesh and drinking the blood of the ascetics after killing them I began to hinder all religious services.

sa_verse: ऋषिमांसाशनः क्रूरस्त्रासयन् वनगोचरान्। तदा रुधिरमत्तोऽहं वचरं दण्डकावनम्॥
en: I had a most ugly figure and was maddened with drinking blood, and all the animals of the forest were exceedingly frightened at me.

sa_verse: तदाहं दण्डकारण्ये विचरन् धर्मदूषकः। आसादयं तदा रामं तापसं धर्ममाश्रितम्॥ वैदेहीं च महाभागां लक्ष्मणं च महारथम्। तापसं नियताहारं सर्वभूतहिते रतम्॥
en: While I was thus traversing the forest of Dandaka throwing obstacles in the way of religious services I saw the pious ascetic Răma living on a restricted fare, the honoured Sītā and the mighty Lakşmaņa, taking recourse to asceticism, moderate in eating and ever engaged in the welfare of created beings.

sa_verse: सोऽहं वनगतं रामं परिभूय महाबलम्। तापसोऽयमिति ज्ञात्वा पूर्ववैरमनुस्मरन्॥ अभ्यधावं सुसंक्रुद्धस्तीक्ष्णशृङ्गो मृगाकृतिः। जिघांसुरकृतप्रज्ञस्तं प्रहारमनुस्मरन्॥
en: Remembering his former enmity and aggrandisement, I, being extremely wrathful, in my deer shape and with my sharpened horns, darted towards him with a view to kill him, considering through my ignorance the effulgent Rāma as none other but an ascetic living in the forest.

sa_verse: तेन त्यक्ताश्रयो बाणाः शिताः शत्रुनिवर्हणाः। विकृष्य सुमहच्चापं सुपर्णानिलतुल्यगाः॥ ते बाणा वज्रसंकाशाः सुघोरा रक्तभोजनाः। आजग्मुः सहिताः सर्वे त्रयः संनतपर्वणः॥
en: Then stringing his huge bow he darted three sharpened arrows at me. Those three terrible arrows with bent knots resembling thunderbolts, killing enemies and drinking blood and having the motion of Garuda and air, coalescing with each other came before us.

sa_verse: पराक्रमज्ञो रामस्य शठो दृष्टभयः पुरा। समुत्क्रान्तस्ततो मुक्तस्तावुभौ राक्षसौ हतौ॥
en: Wicked as I am, I was fully aware of his prowess having been frightened by him on a former occasion, and so I saved myself by escaping, but those two Raksasas were killed.

sa_verse: शरेण मुक्तो रामस्य कथंचित् प्राप्य जीवितम्। इह प्रव्राजितो युक्तस्तापसोऽहं समाहितः॥
en: O Rāvana, any how saved from the arrows of Rāma and having got my life back I have resorted to the life of an ascetic and have been carrying on asceticism here being absorbed in Yoga.

sa_verse: वृक्षे वृक्षे हि पश्यामि चीरकृष्णाजिनाम्बरम्। गृहीतधनुषं रामं पाशहस्तमिवान्तकम्॥
en: From that very day I see Rāma wearing bark and deerskin even in the very trees before me like to Death with the noose in his hands.

sa_verse: अपि रामसहस्राणि भीतः पश्यामि रावण। रामभूतमिदं सर्वमरण्यं प्रतिभाति मे॥
en: Terrified as I am I always see thousands of Rāma around me. Me seems the entire forest is inhabited by Rāma only.

sa_verse: रोममेव हि पश्यामि रहिते राक्षसेश्वर। दृष्ट्वा स्वप्नगतं राममुद्धमामीव चेतनः॥
en: O king of Räksasas, I see Rāma even in the quarter where there is no Rāma. Seeing him in my dreams even I look around like one awaken.

sa_verse: रकारादीनि नामानि रामत्रस्तस्य रावण। रत्नानि च रथाश्चैव वित्रासं जनयन्ति मे॥
en: O Rāvana what more shall I speak to you, I am so much afraid of Rāma that such words as Ratna (jewel), Ratha (chariot) which begin with the letter Ra administer consternation to me.

sa_verse: अहं तस्य प्रभावज्ञो न युद्धं तेन ते क्षमम्। बलिं वा नमुचिं वापि हन्याद्धि रघुनन्दनः॥
en: I am fully apprised of the prowess of that descendant of Raghu; it dose not behove you to enter into conflict with him; he can destroy Vāli and Namuci.

sa_verse: रणे रामेण युध्यस्व क्षमा वा कुरु रावण। न ते रामकथा कार्या यति मां द्रष्टुमिच्छसि ॥
en: O Rāvana, whether do you fight with Rāma or not, do not mention him to me if do you wish to see me.

sa_verse: बहवः साधवो लोके युक्ता धर्ममनुष्ठिताः। परेषामपराधेन विनष्टाः सपरिच्छदाः ॥
en: Many persons pious and devoted to asceticism on this earth have met with destruction for another's misdeeds.

sa_verse: सोऽहं परापराधेम विनशेयं निशाचर। कुरु यत् ते क्षमं तत्त्वमहं त्वां नानुयामि वै॥
en: And I shall meet with a similar fate for your sins. O you the ranger of night do whatever you like, I shall not follow you.

sa_verse: रामश्च हि महातेजा महासत्त्वो महाबलः। अपि राक्षसलोकस्य भवेदन्तकरोऽपि हि॥
en: Truely is that highly intelligent and effulgent Rāma of mighty prowess, like death itself to the rangers of night.

sa_verse: यदि शूर्पणखाहेतोर्जनस्थानगतः खरः। अतिवृत्तो हतः पूर्वं रामेणक्लिष्टकर्मणा। अत्र ब्रूहि यथातत्वं को रामस्य वयतिक्रमः॥
en: Though the wicked Khara of Janasthāna has been slain by him on Sūrpanakhā's account, yet how is he to blame for it do you truly speak.

sa_verse: इदं वचो बन्धुहितार्थिना मया यथोच्यमानं यदि नाभिपत्स्यसे। सबान्धवस्त्यक्ष्यसि जीवितं रणे हतोऽद्य रामेण शरैरजिह्मगैः॥
en: You are my friend; it is for this and for your welfare that I speak this truth to you. If do you not follow my words, yourself with all your kinsmen, shall be slain in battle by Rāma.

sa_verse: मारीचस्य तु तद् वाक्यं क्षमं युक्तं च रावणः। उक्तो न प्रतिजग्राह मर्तुकाम इवौषधम्॥
en: As a person desirous of meeting with his end dose not take medicine, Ravana, the king of night-rangers paid no heed to the appropriate and sound words of Márica skilled in speech, foreboding good to him.

sa_verse: तं पथ्यहितवक्तारं मारीचं राक्षसाधिन। अब्रवीत् परुषं वाक्यमयुक्तं कालचोदितः॥
en: Moreover he addressed him with the following harsh words.

sa_verse: दुष्कुलैतदयुक्तार्थं मारीच मयि कथ्यते। वाक्यं निष्फलमत्यर्थं बीजमुप्तमिवोखरे॥
en: O, Marica, what you have spoken to me, shall bear no fruit like to seeds sown into a desertland.

sa_verse: त्वद्वाक्यैर्न तु मां शक्यं भेत्तुं रामस्य संयुगे। मूर्खस्य पापशीलस्य मानुषस्य विशेषतः॥
en: I shall not be frightened thereby to fight with that foolish Rāma—a human being of vicious deeds.

sa_verse: यस्त्यक्त्वा सुहदो राज्यं मातरं पितरं तथा। स्त्रीवाक्यं प्राकृतं श्रुत्वा वनमेकपदे गतः॥ अवश्यं तु मया तस्य संयुगे खरघातिनः। प्राणैः प्रियतरा सीता हर्तव्या तव संनिधौ॥
en: Surely shall I before you take away from that Rāma the destroyer of Khara his favourite wife, at whose words he has sojourned into woods renouncing his father mother, kingdom and friends.

sa_verse: एवं मे निश्चिता बुद्धिर्हदि मारीच विद्यते। न व्यावर्तयितुं शक्या सेन्ट्रैरपि सुरासुरैः॥
en: I have made this firm resolution in my mind O, Marica; even Indra, with the celestials and Asuras shall not be able to shake it.

sa_verse: देषं गुणं वा सम्पृष्टस्त्वमेवं वक्तुमर्हसि। अपायं वा उपायं वा कार्यस्यास्य विनिश्चये॥
en: It would have been proper for you to give vent to these expressions had I but asked you for advice to ascertain my duty, the propriety or impropriety of my action its way or losses,

sa_verse: सम्पृष्टेन तु वक्तव्यं सचिवेन विपश्चिता। उद्यताञ्जलिना राज्ञा य इच्छेद् भूतिमात्मनः॥
en: A wise counsellor who wishes prosperity to himself should always communicate to his master his desire with joined hands whenever asked to do so.

sa_verse: वाक्यमप्रतिकूलं तु मृदुपूर्वं शुभं हितम्। उपचारेण वक्तव्यो युक्तं च वसुधाधिपः॥
en: It is always proper to speak before monarchs such pleasant and favourable words as are intended for the welfare of the master and are sanctioned by the royal etiquette.

sa_verse: सावमदं तु यद्वाक्यमथवा हितमुच्यते। नाभिनन्देत तद् राजा मानार्थी मानवर्जितम्॥
en: A respectable king dose not receive with good grace friendly words even when they are expressed disre .pectfully.

sa_verse: पञ्च रूपाणि राजानो धारयन्त्यमितौजसः। अग्नेरिन्द्रस्य सोमस्य यमस्य वरुणस्य च ॥ औष्ण्यं तथा विक्रमं च सौम्यं दण्डं प्रसन्नताम्। धारयन्ति महात्मानो राजानः क्षणदाचर॥
en: Sovereigns of superb prowess assume the semblance of five deities–Agni, Indra, Moon, Yama, and Varuna. Powerful kings, O Ranger of the night, assume haughtiness, power, a placid look and contentment and administer punishment to the wicked.

sa_verse: तस्मात् सर्वास्ववस्थासु मान्याः पूज्याश्च नित्यदा। त्वं तु धर्ममविज्ञाय केवलं मोहमाश्रितः॥ अभ्यागतं तु दौरात्म्यात् परुषं वदसीदृशम्। गुणदोषौ न पृच्छामि क्षेमं चात्मनि राक्षस॥
en: They are to be adored and honoured therefore on all occasions. You are ignorant of kingly morality and fully absorbed in illusion. It is for this that you do out of your wickedness of heart address me your guest with these harsh words. I do not ask you, O, Raksasa about the propriety (of my conduct) or about my welfare.

sa_verse: मयोक्तमपि चैतावत् त्वां प्रत्यमितविक्रम। अस्मिंस्तु स भवान् कृत्ये साहाय्यं कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: I did only ask you for your help.

sa_verse: शृणु तत्कर्म साहाय्ये यत्कार्यं वचनान्मम। सौवर्णस्त्वं मृगो भूत्वा चित्रो रजतबिन्दुभिः॥ आश्रमे तस्य रामस्य सीतायाः प्रमुखे चर। प्रलोभयित्वा वैदेही यथेष्टं गन्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: Listen what you shall have to do at my request in my aid; assuming the shape of a deer having golden skin painted with silvery drops do you proceed to the asylum of Rāma, and ranging at large before Vaidehī do you after captivating her take your wished-for way.

sa_verse: त्वां हि मायामयं दृष्ट्वा काञ्चनं जातविस्मया। आनयैनमिति क्षिप्रं रामं वक्ष्यति मैथिली॥
en: Vaidehi, being struck with wonder seeing you a golden deer capable of illusions, shall request Rāma to get you soon for her.

sa_verse: अपक्रान्ते च काकुत्स्थे दूरं गत्वाप्युदाहर। हा सीते लक्ष्मणेत्येवं रामवाक्यानुरूपकम्॥
en: Kākutstha issuing out of the asylum, you shall, at a great distance utter such cries as O Sītā, O Lakşmaņa imitating his (Rāma's) voice.

sa_verse: तच्छ्रुत्वा रामपदवीं सीतया च प्रचोदितः। अनुगच्छति सम्भ्रान्तं सौमित्रिरपि सौहदात्॥
en: Hearing those cries Lakşmaņa also, at the behest of Sītā, shall proceed to Rāma,

sa_verse: अपक्रान्ते च काकुत्स्थे लक्ष्मणे च यथासुखम्। आहरिष्यामि वैदेही सहस्राक्षः शचीमिव ॥
en: When both Rāma and Laksmana shall go elsewhere in this way, I shall to my felicity carry away Vaidehī like to the thousand-eyed deity carrying away Sací.

sa_verse: एवं कृत्वा त्विदं कार्यं यथेष्टं गच्छ राक्षस। राज्यस्यार्धं प्रदास्यामि मारीच तव सुव्रत॥
en: O Rākşasa, do you go wherever you like after doing this. I shall confer on you, O Mārīca, half of my kingdom.

sa_verse: गच्छ सौम्य शिवं मार्ग कार्यस्यास्य विवृद्धये। अहं त्वानुगमिष्यामि सरथो दण्डकावनम्॥
en: O you of auspicious look, do you proceed towards the forest of Dandaka to compass this end of mine; I shall follow you in my chariot,

sa_verse: प्राप्य सीतामयुद्धेन वञ्चयित्वा तु राघवम्। लङ्कां प्रति गमिष्यामि कृतकार्यः सह त्वया॥
en: Acquiring Sītā without any conflict, after imposing upon Rāma I shall return to Lankā successful along with you.

sa_verse: नो चेत् करोषि मारीच हन्मि त्वामहमद्य वै। एतद् कार्यमवश्यं मे बलादपि करिष्यसि। राज्ञो विप्रतिकूलस्थो न जातु सुखमेधते॥
en: I shall kill you, O Mārīca, if do you not obey my behest. I shall compel you to do this. No body can attain to prosperity and happiness, acting against his sovereign's will.

sa_verse: आसाद्य तं जीवितसंशयस्ते मृत्युभ्रुवो ह्यद्य मया विरुध्यतः। एतद् यथावत् परिगण्य बुद्ध्या यदत्र पथ्यं कुरु तत्तथा त्वम्॥
en: Truly shall you risk your life if you approaches Rāma, but you shall meet with sure destruction if do you act against my will. Consider about it, and do what you think proper.

sa_verse: आज्ञप्तो रावणेनेत्थं प्रतिकूलं च राजवत्। अब्रवीत् परुषं वाक्यं निःशङ्को राक्षसाधिपम् ॥
en: Being thus commended by Rāvana like a king to act against his will Mārica fearlessly addressed the lord of the Rākṣasas with the following bold words.

sa_verse: केनायमुपदिष्टस्ते विनाशः पापकर्मणा। सपुत्रस्य सराज्यस्य सामात्यस्य निशाचर॥
en: What person of impious deeds has advised you ( ranger of the night thus in order to exterminate you with your kingdom, counsellors and children?

sa_verse: कस्त्वया सुखिना राजन् नाभिनन्दति पापकृत्। केनेदमुपदिष्टं ते मृत्युद्वारमुपायतः॥
en: Who of a vicious heart, O King, dose not feel himself happy at your welfare? Who has pointed out to you the way of your death under the cover of your means?

sa_verse: शत्रवस्तव सुव्यक्तं हीनवीर्या निशाचर। इच्छन्ति त्वं विनस्यन्तमुपरुद्धं बलीयसा॥
en: O Ranger of night surely do your weak enemies wish to bring about your destruction by making you enter into a conflict with a powerful man.

sa_verse: केनेदमुपदिष्टं ते क्षुद्रेणाहितबुद्धिना। यस्त्वामिच्छति नश्यन्तं स्वकृतेन निशाचर॥
en: What little-minded man of evil intent has counselled you thus? O Ranger of night, really do they desire that you do bring destruction upon yourself by your own actions.

sa_verse: वध्याः खलु न वध्यन्ते सचिवास्तव रावण। ये त्वामुत्पथमारूढं न निगृह्णन्ति सर्वशः॥
en: You do not exterminate your counsellors O Rāvana, albeit they are worthy of being destroyed. For they do not prevent these entirely from your evil course.

sa_verse: अमात्यैः कामवृत्तो हि राजा कापथमाश्रितः। निग्राह्यः सर्वथा सद्भिः स निग्राह्यो न गृह्यते॥
en: Wise counsellors do always restrain a king who wends a vicious track being guided by his impulses and passions; but about you they are quite unmindful, though you should by every means be brought under restraint.

sa_verse: धर्ममर्थं च कामं च यशश्च जयतां वर। स्वामिप्रसादात् सचिवाः प्राप्नुवन्ति निशाचर॥
en: O you the best of conquerors, O you the ranger of high, ministers by the grace of their sovereign attain virtue, wealth, desires and fame.

sa_verse: विपर्यये तु तत्सर्वं व्यर्थं भवति रावण। व्यसनं स्वामिवैगुण्यात् प्राप्नुवन्तीतरे जनाः॥
en: But those objects are never attained O Rāvana when they are not in good odour with their sovereign; moreover the subjects meet with dangers when he is devoid of all qualities.

sa_verse: राजमूलो हि धर्मश्च यशश्च जयतां वर। तस्मात् सर्वास्ववस्थासु रक्षितव्या नराधिपाः॥
en: O you the best of conquerors, the king is the root of virtue and fame to the subjects; he should therefore be always protected by them.

sa_verse: राज्यं पालयितुं शक्यं न तीक्ष्णेन निशाचर। न चातिप्रतिकूलेन नाविनीतेन राक्षस ॥
en: O ranger of night, proud kings of cruel temperament, acting against the subjects can never administer their kingdoms.

sa_verse: ये तीक्ष्णमन्त्राः सचिवा भुज्यन्ते सह तेन वै। विषमेषु रथाः शीघ्रं मन्दसारथयो यथा॥
en: So do the ministers of cruel counsel meet with their end like to a chariot driven into a wild track by an unskilled charioteer.

sa_verse: बहवः साधवो लोके युक्तधर्ममनुष्टिताः। परेषामपराधेन विनष्टाः सपरिच्छदाः॥
en: Many a pious person, on this earth, ever engaged in the performance of religious services, have, for the folly of other persons, met with destruction along with their relatives.

sa_verse: स्वामिना प्रतिकूलेन प्रजास्तीक्ष्णेन रावण। रक्ष्यमाणा न वर्धन्ते मृगा गोमायुना यथा॥
en: Verily the subjects, O Ravana, can never expect to prosper under the regime of hotnatured sovereigns acting against their subjects like to deer under the protection of a jackal.

sa_verse: अवश्यं विनशिष्यन्ति सर्वे रावण राक्षसाः। रेगां त्वं कर्क राजा दुर्बुद्धिरजितेन्द्रियः॥
en: All the Rākşasas shall surely die, O Rāvana whose lord are you, foolish, cruel and under the control of your passions.

sa_verse: तदिदं काकतालीयं घोरमासादितं मया। अत्र त्वं शोचनीयोऽसि ससैन्यो विनशिष्यसि ॥
en: I shall not be the least sorry if I do meet with a sudden accident—what I do deplore most is that you shall meet in no time with destruction along with your soldiery.

sa_verse: मां निहत्य तु रामोऽसावचिरात् त्वां वधिष्यति। अनेन कृतकृत्योऽस्मि म्रिये चाप्यरिणा हतः॥
en: Compassing my end Rāma shall exterminate you in no time and blessed I shall be being killed by an enemy in the conflict.

sa_verse: दर्शनादेव रामस्य हतं मामवधारय। आत्मानं च हतं विद्धि हृत्वा सीतां सबान्धवम्॥
en: Surely do you know that no sooner shall I see Rāma I shall be done away with; you shall carrying away Sītā meet with your end along with your kinsmen.

sa_verse: आनयिष्यसि चेत् सीतामाश्रमात् सहितो मया। नैव त्वमपि नाहं वै नैव लङ्का न राक्षसाः॥
en: And if do you bring Şītā with me from the asylum, none shall be saved, me, yourself, the city of Lankā and the Rākşasas.

sa_verse: निवार्यमाणस्तु मया हितैषिणा न मृष्यसे वाक्यमिदं निशाचर। परेतकल्पा हि गतायुषो नरा हितं न गृह्णन्ति सुहृद्भिरीरितम्॥
en: You do not relish my words O ranger of the night though prevented by me, who wishes you welfare; those men who range on the verge of death, well-nigh finishing the term of their existence do not receive with good grace the counsels of friends.

sa_verse: एवमुक्ता तु परुषं मारीचो रावणं ततः। गच्छावेत्यब्रवीत् दीनो भयाद् रात्रिंचरप्रभोः॥
en: Speaking these harsh words to Rāvana, Mārīca being afraid of him said, We shall both go.

sa_verse: दृष्टश्चाहं पुनस्तेन शरचापासिधारिणा। मद्धोद्यतशस्त्रेण निहतं जीवितं च मे॥
en: Surely shall I be deprived of my life b, (Rāma) taking up his scimitar and having a bow and arrows in his hands, if I go before hiin again.

sa_verse: नहि रामं पराक्रम्य जीवन् प्रतिनिवर्तते। वर्तते प्रतिरूपोऽसौ यमदण्डहतस्य ते॥
en: Meeting with his prowess you shall never return with your life; he is like the noose of Death to you though you had baffled its power.

sa_verse: किं नु कर्तुं मया शक्यमेवं त्वयि दुरात्मनि। एव गच्छाम्यहं तात स्वस्ति तेऽस्तु निशाचर॥
en: You are vicious-souled, what can I do for you? be you crowned with success O ranger of the night, I shall go.

sa_verse: प्रहृष्टस्त्वभवत्तेन वचनेन स राक्षसः। परिष्वज्य सुसंश्लिष्टमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: The Lord of Rākşasas being pleased with Mārīca's words embraced him warmly and said,

sa_verse: एतच्छौटीर्ययुक्तं ते मच्छन्दवशवर्तिनः। इदानीमसि मारीचः पूर्वमन्यो हि राक्षसः॥
en: This is worthy of your heroism that you have addressed yourself to act after my desire. You were another Raksasa before and have now become true Mārīca.

sa_verse: आरुह्यतामयं शीघ्रं खगो रत्नविभूषितः। मया सह रथो युक्त : पिशाचवदनैः खरैः॥
en: Do you now with me ascend this chariot coursing in the air, crested with jewels and driven by asses having faces of a demon.

sa_verse: प्रलोभयित्वा वैदेहीं यथेष्टं गन्तुमर्हसि । तां शून्ये प्रसभं सीतामानयिष्यामि मैथिलीम्॥
en: Enchanting Vaidehī you shall wend your way and away shall I carry the daughter of the king of Mithilā by force (when there shall be neither Rāma nor Laksmana by her.)

sa_verse: ततस्तथेत्युवाचैनं रावणं ताटकासुतः। ततो रावणमारीचौ विमानमिव तं रथम्॥ आरुह्याययतुः शीघ्रं तस्मादाश्रममण्डलात्।
en: Thereupon Tārakā's son assented to Rāvana's words, and both of them ascending the chariot like to a heavenly car set out for the hermitage.

sa_verse: तथैव तत्र पश्यन्तौ पत्तनानि वनानि च॥ गिरीश्च सरितः सर्वा राष्ट्राणि नगराणि च। समेत्य दण्डकारण्यं राघवस्याश्रमं ततः॥ ददर्श सहमारीचो रावणो राक्षसाधिपः।
en: And beholding diverse towns, forests, mountains, rivers, kingdoms and cities they reached the forest of Dandaka and the asylum of Rāma.

sa_verse: अवतीर्य रथात् तस्मात् ततः काञ्चनभूषणात्॥ हस्ते गृहीत्वा मारीचं रावणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।।
en: And descending now from the golden car the lord of Rākşasas together with Mārīca beheld Răma's asylum. Taking him (Mārīca) by the hand Ravana spoke.

sa_verse: एतद् रामाश्रमपदं दृश्यते कदलीवृतम्॥ क्रियतां तत् सखे शीघ्रं यदर्थे वयमागताः।
en: There stands the asylum of Rāma surrounded by palm trees. Do you do that for which we have come here.

sa_verse: स रावणवचः श्रुत्वा मारीचो राक्षसस्तदा॥ मृगो भूत्वाऽऽश्रमद्वारि रामस्य विचचार ह।
en: Hearing the words of Rāvaņa, the Rāksasa Mārīca assuming the wonderful shape of a (golden) deer began to range at large before Rama's hermitage.

sa_verse: स तु रूपं समास्थाय महदद्भुतदर्शनम्॥ मणिप्रवरशृङ्गाग्रः सितासितमुखाकृतिः। रक्तपद्मोत्पलमुख इन्द्रनीलोत्पलश्रवाः॥ किंचिदत्युनतग्रीव इन्द्रनीलनिभोदरः। मधूकनिभपार्श्वश्च कञ्जकिञ्जल्कसंनिभः॥ वैदूर्यसंकाशखुरस्तनुजङ्घः सुसंहतः। इन्द्रायुधसवर्णेन पुच्छेनोर्ध्वं विराजितः॥ मनोहरस्निग्धवर्णो रत्नैर्नानाविधैर्वृतः। क्षणेन राक्षसो जातो मृगः परमशोभनः॥
en: The points of its horns were like to (two) excellent jewels, its countenance was diversified with white and black colour, its face was like a red lotus, its cars were like to two blue lotuses, its neck was little raised, its bclly was like a sapphire, its sides were like to Madhūka flowers, its colour was like that of a filament of a lotus, its hoops were like to Vaidurayas (a gem of a dark colour); of lean thighs; of firm joints; its tail having the diversified colour of a rainbow was upraised. It was of a pleasant and cool hue and crested with various jewels.

sa_verse: वनं प्रज्वलयन् रम्यं रामाश्रमपदं च तत्। मनोहरं दर्शनीयं रूपं कृत्वा स राक्षसः॥ प्रलोभनार्थं वैदेह्या नानाधातुविचित्रितम्। विचरन् गच्छते सम्यक् शाद्वलानि समन्ततः॥
en: And in no time the Rākşasa assumed the shape of a beautiful deer. To tempt Vaidehī that ranger of the night, assuming a beautiful countenance painted with diverse metals, illumining the beautiful forest and Rāma's asylum with its beauty, and ranging at large on the green field and living on grass, began to

sa_verse: रौप्यैर्बिन्दुशतैश्चित्रं भूत्वा च प्रियदर्शनः। विटपीनां किसलयान् भक्षयन् विचचार ह॥
en: That one of lively presence having its body painted with hundreds of silver drops and living on twigs of trees began to range in the forest.

sa_verse: कदलीगृहकं गत्वा कर्णिकारानितस्ततः। तमाश्रमं मन्दगतिं सीतासंदर्शनं ततः॥
en: Sometimes walking into the plantain house, sometimes walking around the forest of Karņikā, sometimes coming within the compass of Sītā's vision, that best of deer having its back painted with gold began to range slowly around the hermitage.

sa_verse: विक्रीडंश्च क्वचिद् भूमौ पुनरेव निषीदति। आश्रमद्वारमागम्य मृगयूथानि गच्छति॥ मृगयूथैरनुगतः पुनरेव निवर्तते। सीतादर्शनमाकाङ्क्षन् राक्षसो मृगतां गतः॥
en: Sometimes playing around, sometimes lying on earth and sometimes following the deer-herd having come within the thresh hold of the asylum and then followed again by them that Rākṣasa assuming the form of a deer came back to see Sītā.

sa_verse: समुद्वीक्ष्य च सर्वे तं मृगा येऽन्ये वोचराः॥
en: He then began to range at large in the beautiful forest (extending far and wide). Seeing him other forest-deer came (by hiin) and smelling him fled away into different quarters.

sa_verse: उपगम्य समाघ्राय विद्रवन्ति दिशो दश। राक्षसः सोऽपि तान् वन्यान् मृगान् मृगवधे रतः॥
en: That Raksasa, though expert in killing deer, did not eat them up, though touching, in order to hide his real self.

sa_verse: प्रच्छादनार्थं भावस्य न भक्षयति संस्पृशन्। तस्मिन्नेव ततः काले वैदेही शुभलोचना॥ कुसुमापचये व्यग्रा पादपानत्यवर्तत। कर्णिकारानशोकांश्च चूतांश्च मदिरेक्षणा॥
en: In the meantime Vaidehī, of auspicious looks whose eyes inebriate like wine, being engaged in plucking flowers, was going sometimes to the Karņikā grove and sometimes to the mango grove.

sa_verse: कुसुमान्यपचिन्वन्ती चचार रुचिरानना। अनर्हा वनवासस्य सा तं रत्नमयं मृगम्॥ मुक्तामणिविचित्राङ्गं ददर्श परमाङ्गना।
en: That best of women, ever inured to living in the forest and possessed of a graceful countenance, walking and plucking flowers, saw that jewelled deer, having its body diversified with pearls and diamonds,

sa_verse: तं वै रुचिरदन्तोष्ठं रूप्यधातुतनूरुहम्॥ विस्मयोत्फुल्लनयना सस्नेहं समुदैक्षत।
en: It had beautiful teeth and lips and had its down resembling silver. She began to behold with affection and with her eyes expanded with surprise.

sa_verse: स च तां रामदयितां पश्यन् मायामयो मृगः॥ विचचार ततस्तत्र दीपयन्निव तद् वनम्।
en: That illusive deer seeing Rāma's wife began to inove around as if lighting up that forest (with the fire of its beauty).

sa_verse: अदृष्टपूर्वं दृष्ट्वा तं नानारत्नमयं मृगम्। विस्मयं परमं सीता जगाम जनकात्मजा॥
en: Beholding that deer ornamented with diverse jewels, and the like of which she had never seen before, the daughter of Janaka was struck with immense wonder. Beholding that deer ornamented with diverse jewels, and the like of which she had never seen before, the daughter of Janaka was struck with immense wonder.

sa_verse: सा तं सम्प्रेक्ष्य सुश्रोणी कुसुमानि विचिन्वती। नेमराजतवर्णाभ्यां पार्वाभ्यामुपशोभितम्॥ प्रहृष्टा चानवद्याङ्गी मृष्टहाटकवर्णिनी। भर्तारमपि चक्रन्द लक्ष्मणं चैव सायुधम्॥
en: Beholding that deer with its sides painted with gold and silver, while collecting flowers, Sītā, having blameless limbs and beautiful hips and having the colour of her body like that of pure gold became immensely pleased and called aloud her husband and Lakşmaņa with scimitars in their hands.

sa_verse: आहूयाहूय च पुनस्तं मृगं साधु वीक्षते। आगच्छागच्छ शीघ्रं वै आर्यपुत्र सहानुज ॥
en: Do you come soon, do you come soon, O son of the worshipful Sire, along with your younger brother

sa_verse: तावाहूतौ नरव्याघ्रौ वैदेह्या रामलक्ष्मणौ। वीक्षमाणौ तु तं देशं तदा ददृशतुर्मंगम्॥
en: Having called (her husband) again and again in this strain she continued beholding that deer. Being thus called by the daughter of the king of Videha those two best of men Rāma and Laks maņa, casting their looks around beheld that deer.

sa_verse: शङ्कमानस्तु तं दृष्ट्वा लक्ष्मणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। तमेवैनमहं मन्ये मारीचं राक्षसं मृगम्॥
en: Seeing him Lakşmana, struck with fear, said Me-thinks, this deer is the Raksasa Marica.

sa_verse: चरन्तो मृगधां हृष्टाः पापेनोपाधिना वने। अनेन निहता राम राजानः कामरूपिणा॥
en: This vicious Mārīca, O Rāma, assuming the shape of a deer oftentimes kills at pleasure the kings who come here a-hunting.

sa_verse: अस्य मायाविदो माया मृगरूपमिदं कृतम्। भानुमत् पुरुषव्याघ्र गन्धर्वपुरसंनिभम्॥ मृगो ह्येवंविधो रत्नविचित्रो नास्ति राघव। जगत्यां जगतीनाथ मायैषा हि न संशयः॥
en: He is cognizant of illusions, by virtue of which he has assumed this shape, O best of men; its countenance is perfectly fine like the abode of Gandharvas and resplendent like the Sun; there is no such jewelled deer on earth. O Rāghava, O Lord of earth, there is no doubt that this is nothing but illusion.

sa_verse: एवं ब्रुवाणं काकुत्स्थं प्रतिवार्य शुचिस्मिता। उवाच सीता संहृष्टा छद्मना हृतचेतना॥
en: When Lakşınaņa spoke thus Sītā with a pure smile being under the influence of enchantment contradicted him, and being pleased said

sa_verse: आर्यपुत्राभिरामोऽसौ मृगो हरति मे मनः। आनयैनं महाबाहो क्रीडार्थं नो भविष्यति ॥
en: O son of the worshipful Sire, this deer has attracted my mind, get it for me, pray, O you of mighty arms: it shall be an object of sport for us.

sa_verse: इहाश्रमपदेऽस्माकं बहवः पुण्यदर्शनाः। मृगाश्चरन्ति सहिताश्चमराः सृमरास्तथा॥ ऋक्षाः पृषतसङ्घाश्च वानराकिन्नरास्तथा। विहरन्ति महाबाहो रूपश्रेष्ठा महाबलाः॥ न चान्यः सदृशो राजन् दृष्टः पूर्वं मृगो मया। तेजसा क्षमया दीप्या यथायं मृगसत्तमः॥
en: Many a beautiful deer range at large in the vicinity of our hermitage such as Camaras, Srmaras, Rksas, Prsatas, Vanaras and Kinnaras. They are all very beautiful and of mighty strength; but O Prince, I have never seen before the like of this deer. In energy, strength and beauty it stands above all deer.

sa_verse: नानावर्णविचित्राङ्गो रत्नभूतो ममाग्रतः। द्योतयन् वनमव्यग्रं द्योतते शशिसंनिभः॥
en: Its cntire body is diversified with various colours, nay, it is a jewel itself. It moves before me beautifying the forest with its resplendence, like to Moon himself.

sa_verse: अहो रूपमहो लक्ष्मीः स्वरसम्पद्य शोभना। मृगोऽद्भुतो विचित्राङ्गो हृदय हरतीव मे॥
en: Ah! what beauty, what beautiful a lustre, how musical a voice, this wonderful deer of variegated countenance, has indeed captivated my heart!

sa_verse: यदि ग्रहणमभ्येति जीवन्नेव मृगस्तव। आश्चर्यभूतं भवति विस्मयं जनयिष्यति॥
en: If can you get by this deer alive, O what wonder, what surprise!

sa_verse: समाप्तवनवासानां राज्यस्थानां च नः पुनः। अन्तःपुरे विभूषार्थो मृग एष भविष्यति॥
en: When shall we regain our kingdom after the expire of the term of our banishment, this deer shall beautify the interior of our palace.

sa_verse: भरतस्यार्यपुत्रस्य श्वश्रूणां मम च प्रभो। मृगरूपमिदं दिव्यं विस्मयं जनयिष्यति॥
en: O Lord, truly shall this celestial deer create surprise in Bharata, yourself, me and my mothers-in-law.

sa_verse: जीवन यदि तेऽभ्येति ग्रहणं मृगसत्तमः। अजिनं नरशार्दूल रुचिरं तु भविष्यति ॥
en: O best of men, if can you not get hold of it alive its skin shall also look very beautiful (to us).

sa_verse: निहतस्यास्य सत्त्वस्य जाम्बूनदमयत्वचि। शष्पबृस्यां विनीतायामिच्छाम्यहमुपासितुम्॥
en: Spreading on Kuća the skin of this golden deer when killed do I wish to invoke the Almighty.

sa_verse: कामवृत्तमिदं रौद्रं स्त्रीणामसदृशं मतम्। वपुषा त्वस्य सत्त्वस्य विस्मयो जनितो मम॥
en: It looks (no doubt) unseemly and terrible and smacks of wilfulness, on the part of a wife to command her husband in this way, but I am sunk in surprise seeing the countenance of the deer.

sa_verse: तेन काञ्चनरोम्णा तु मणिप्रवरशृङ्गिणा। तरुणादित्यवर्णेन नक्षत्रपथवर्चसा॥ बभूव राघवस्यापि मनो विस्मयमागतम्। इति सीतावचः श्रुत्वा दृष्ट्वा च मृगमद्भुतम्॥ लोभितस्तेन रूपेण सीतया च प्रचोदितः। उवाच राघवो हृष्टो भ्रातरं लक्ष्मणं वचः॥
en: Rāma's heart was struck with surprise beholding that wonderful deer having its down resembling gold, horns resembling exquisitely finc diamonds, colour like that of the newly risen Sun, and resplendence like that of the orbit of the planets. Being enchanted by its beauty and spurred on by Sītā's words, Raghava pleased, spoke to his younger brother.

sa_verse: पश्य लक्ष्मण वैदेह्याः स्पृहामुल्लसितामिमाम्। रूपश्रेष्ठतया ह्येष मृगोऽद्य न भविष्यति॥
en: Behold, O Laksmana, it has immensely excited Vaidehi's desire. On account of its exquisite beauty it shall loose its life to-day.

sa_verse: न वने नन्दनोद्देशे न चैत्ररथसंश्रये। कुतः पृथिव्यां सौमित्रे योऽस्य कश्चिन्समो मृगः॥
en: It has its equal no where on the earth-neither in the celestial garden nor in the Caitraratha forest.

sa_verse: प्रतिलोमानुलोमाश्च रुचिरा रोमराजयः। शोभन्ते मृगमाश्रित्य चित्राः कनकबिन्दुभिः॥
en: The hairs of its body being arranged in regular and contrary courses and being painted with golden drops do indeed look very beautiful.

sa_verse: पश्यास्य जृम्भमाणस्य दीप्तामग्निशिखोपमाम्। जिह्वां मुखानिःसरन्तीं मेघादिव शतहदाम्॥
en: Do you see, while expanding its inouth, its tongue comes out resembling flaming fire like to a thunder-bolt reaving the mass of clouds.

sa_verse: मसारगल्वर्कमुखः शङ्खमुक्तानिभोदरः। कस्य नामानिरूप्योऽसौ न मनो लोभयेन्मृगः॥
en: Its face is like a glass made of a best blue sapphire, its belly resembling a conch and pearl, and it is very difficult to ascertain its shapes. Beholding it who is there (on earth) whose heart is not enamoured?

sa_verse: कस्य रूपमिदं दृष्ट्वा जाम्बूनदमयप्रभम्। नानारत्नमयं दिव्यं न मनो विस्मयं व्रजेत्॥
en: Its beauty resembles the rays of gold and is variegated with diverse hues of jewels. Whose heart is not enveloped with surprise beholding such a celestial beauty, O Laksmana?

sa_verse: मांसहेतोरपि मृगान् विहारार्थं च धन्विनः। घ्नन्ति लक्ष्मण राजानो मृगयायां महावने॥
en: Princes, with bows in their hands, while-a hunting in a deep forest destroy deer either for flesh or for sport.

sa_verse: धनानि व्यवसायेन विचीयन्ते महावने। धातवो विविधाश्चापि मणिरत्नसुवर्णिनः॥
en: Moreover while engaged in sporting they collect many a jewel and various metals as gold and silver, in the woods.

sa_verse: तत् सारमखिलं नृणां धनं निचयवर्धनम्। मनसा चिन्तितं सर्वं यथा शुक्रस्य लक्ष्मण ॥
en: There is not the least doubt that these wild riches filling up their coffers are comparatively far better (than the wealth acquired by other sources) like to the objects of enjoyment, enjoyed at their fancy by the people inhabiting the celestial region.

sa_verse: अर्थी येनार्थकृत्येन संव्रजत्यविचारयन्। तमर्थमर्थशास्त्रज्ञाः प्राहुरर्थ्याः सुलक्ष्मण ॥
en: The object which persons desirous of acquiring wealth follow without any discretion in their actions, is the true definition of wealth given by the political economists.

sa_verse: एतस्य मृगरत्नस्य परायें काञ्चनत्वचि। उपवेक्ष्यति वैदेही मया सह सुमध्यमा॥ न कादली न प्रियकी न प्रवेणी न चाविकी। भवेदेतस्य सदृशी स्पर्शेऽनेनेति मे मतिः॥
