en: And the sylvan deities, looking up now and again with eyes betokening fear, had their persons all in a tremble.

sa_verse: तां तु लक्ष्मण रामेति क्रोशन्तीं मधुरस्वराम्।॥ अवेक्षमाणां बहुशो वैदहीं धरणीतलम्। स तामाकुलकेशान्तां विप्रमृष्टविशेषकाम्। जहारात्मविनाशाय दशग्रीरो मनस्विनीम्॥
en: headed one carried away the intelligent Vaidehī, bewailing bitterly, Sītā, who had come by such misfortune, sweet-voiced, crying, O Lakşmaņa O Rāma, and casting glances on the ground many time and oft, the ends of her hair waving and her tilika wiped out.

sa_verse: ततस्तु सा चारुदती शुचिस्मिता विनाकृता बन्धुजनेन मैथिली। अपश्यती राधवलक्ष्मणाबुभौ विवर्षक्त्रा फयभारपीडिता॥
en: Then oppressed with the load of fear, Sītā of luminous smiles, Mithila's daughter, bereft of her friends-not beholding either Rāma or Lakşmaņa, became pale of countenance.

sa_verse: खमुत्पतन्तं तं दृष्ट्वा मैथिली जनकात्मजा। दुःखिता परमोद्विग्ना भये महति वर्तिनी॥
en: Seeing him fly up into the air, Janaka's daughter, Maithili, became aggrieved and exceedingly agitated, and great was the fear that possessed her.

sa_verse: रोषरोदनताम्राक्षी भीमाक्षं राक्षसाधिपम्। रुदती करुणं सीता ह्रियमाणा तमब्रवीत्॥
en: Her eyes expressing rage, weeping and fright, Sītā, as she was being carried away, weeping piteously, spoke to the grim-eyed lord of the Raksasas.

sa_verse: न व्यपत्रपसे नीच कर्मणानेन रावण। ज्ञात्वा विरहितां यो मां चोरयित्वा पलायसे॥
en: Do you not, O base wretch, O Ravana, feel shame on account of this act-you, who, knowing that I was alone fly away, carrying me?

sa_verse: त्वयैव नूनं दुष्टात्मन् भीरूणा हर्तुमिच्छता। ममापवाहितो भर्ता मृगरूपेण मायया॥
en: Coward that you are, you it was, who, desirous of carrying me off, by the shape of a deer, had, by your (powers of] illusion taken away my lord.

sa_verse: यो हि मामुद्यतस्त्रातुं सोऽप्ययं विनिपातितः। गृध्रराजः पुराणोऽसौ श्वशुरस्य सखा मम ॥
en: And he also that endeavoured to rescue me, has been slain by you—the ancient king of vultures, who was the friend of my father-in-law.

sa_verse: परमं खलु ते वीर्यं दृश्यते राक्षसाधम। विश्राव्य नामधेयं हि युद्धे नास्मि जिता त्वया॥ ईदृशं गर्हितं कर्म कथं कृत्वा न लज्जसे। स्त्रियाश्चाहरणं नीच रहिते च परस्य च ॥
en: Great, forsooth, is found to be your might, you vilest of Rākşasas, in that you have carried me off; by simply declaring your name, but have not won me in war. Why do you not, O execrable one, take shame to yourself, having perpetrated such a heinous act-having carried off another's wife in the absence of her husband?

sa_verse: कथयिष्यन्ति लोकेषु पुरुषाः कर्म कुत्सितम्। कुनृशंसमधर्मिष्ठं तव शौटीर्यमानिनः॥
en: This fell and foul ict of yours fraught with unrighteousness, heroic persons shall bruit about the world.

sa_verse: धिक् ते शौर्यं च सत्त्वं च यत्वया कथितं तदा। कुलाक्रोशकरं लोके धिक् ते चारित्रमीदृशम्॥
en: Fie on your heroism and your truth, of which you did speak at that time; and fie also on this character of yours, calculated to sully your line in this world.

sa_verse: किं शक्यं कर्तुमेवं हि यज्जवेनैव धावसि। मुहूर्तमपि तिष्ठ त्वं न जीवन् प्रतियास्यसि ॥
en: What can I do (to you), as you proceed with speed? But stay you for a moment; and you shall not return with life.

sa_verse: न हि चक्षुःपथं प्राप्य तयोः पार्थिवपुत्रयोः। ससैन्योऽपि समर्थस्त्वं मुहूर्तमपि जीवितुम् ॥
en: Should you come within the range of the vision of those sons of the king, you could not, although you should happen to be accompanied with your army, live for a moment.

sa_verse: न त्वं तयोः शरस्पर्श सोढुं शक्तः कथंचन। वने प्रज्वलितस्येव स्पर्शमग्नेविहंगमः॥
en: Even as a bird cannot bear the touch of a flaming fire in a forest, you can never bear the touch of their arrows. Effecting your own welfare, do you, O Ravana, leave me.

sa_verse: साधु कृत्वात्मनः पथ्यं साधु मां मुञ्च रावण। मत्प्रधर्षणसंक्रुद्धो भ्रात्रा सह पतिर्मम ॥ विधास्यति विनाशाय त्वं मां यदि न मुञ्चसि। येन त्वं व्यवसायेन बलान्मां हर्तुमिच्छसि॥ व्यवसायस्तु ते नीच भविष्यति निरर्थकः।
en: If you do not let me go, iny husband along with his brother would strive for your destruction. As intent upon sensual enjoyment, you endeavour to ravish me, this very endeavour of yours O mean wight, shall come to naught.

sa_verse: नह्यहं तमपश्यन्ती भर्तारं विबुधोपमम्॥ उत्सहे शत्रुवशगा प्राणान् धारयितुं चिरम्।
en: Not beholding my lord resembling a celestial, I cannot, come under the sway of my enemy, bear to live long.

sa_verse: न नूनं चात्मनः श्रेयः पथ्यं वा समवेक्षसे॥ मृत्युकाले यथा मर्यो विपरितानि सेवते। मुमूर्तृणां तु सर्वेषां यत् पथ्यं तन्न रोचते॥
en: As one dwelling on earth perceives objects in their reversed relations at the time of one's death SO you do not perceive what is for your good or profit. Those moribund do not relish what would do them good.

sa_verse: पश्यामीहि हि कण्ठे त्वां कालपाशावपाशितम्। यथा चास्मिन् भयस्थाने न बिभेषि निशाचर॥ व्यक्तं हिरण्यमयांस्त्वं हि सम्पश्यसि महीरूहान्। नदी वैतरणी घोरां रुधिरौघविवाहिनीम्॥ खङ्गपत्रवनं चैव भीमं पश्यसि रावण। तप्तकाञ्चनपुष्पां च वैदूर्यप्रवरच्छदाम्॥
en: I see with the noose of Death wound round your neck. As, O ranger of night, you are not affected with fear, albeit the situation is one calculated to raise one's apprehensions it is clear that you will see the golden treus, the dreadful river Vaitaraṇī flowing with blood, the terrible wood, O Rāvana, rife with leaves in the shape of swords, and a sharp Šalmali containing blossoms of shining gold, having lapises for its leaves, and bearing iron thorns.

sa_verse: द्रक्ष्यसे शाल्मली तीक्ष्णामायसैः कण्टकैश्रिताम्। नहि त्वमीदृशं कृत्वा तस्यालीकं महात्मनः॥ धारितुं शक्ष्यसि चिरं विषं पीत्वे निघृण। बद्धस्त्वं कालपाशेन दुर्निवारेण रावण ॥
en: But, O shameless one, like a person that has drunk poison, having done this wrong to that high-souled one, you with not be able to save yourself. O Rāvana, you are fast fettered in the noose of Death.

sa_verse: क्व गतो लप्स्यसे शर्म मम भर्तुर्महात्मनः। निमेषान्तरमात्रेण बिना भ्रातरमाहवे॥ राक्षसा निहता येन सहस्राणि चतुर्दश। कथं स राघवो वीरः सर्वास्त्रकुशलो बली॥
en: Having done this foul turn to that high-souled one, repairing whither, shall you obtain respite? Shall not that strong and heroic Rāghava, skilled in all weapons, who without his brother, in the twinkling of an eye, in battle slew fourteen thousand Rākṣasas, slay you, who carries away his beloved wife?

sa_verse: न त्वां हन्याच्छरैस्तीक्ष्णैरिष्टभार्यापहारिणम्। एतच्चान्यच्च परुषं वैदेही रावणाङ्कगा। भयशोकसमाविष्टा करुणं विललाप ह॥
en: Thus and in other ways, Videha's daughter, lying on the lap of Rāvana, overwhelmed with fear and grief, indulged in piteous lamentations.

sa_verse: तदा भृशात् बहु चैव भाषिणी विलापपूर्वं करुणं च भामिनीम्। जहार पापस्तरुणी विचेष्टती नृपात्मजामागतगात्रदे'थुः॥
en: And the wicked (Rävaņa) with a shaking frame carried away the daughter of the king extremely distressed, speaking much, and speaking piteously, uttering lamentations, and putting forth endeavours (to free herself.) saw

sa_verse: ह्रियमाणा तु वैदेही कंचिन्नाथमपश्यती। ददर्श गिरिशृङ्गस्थान् पञ्च वानरपुङ्गवान्॥
en: Carried away (by Ravana), Vaidehi, not finding any defender, five principal monkeys stationed on the top of a hill.

sa_verse: तेषां मध्ये विशालाक्षी कौशेयं कनकप्रभम्। उत्तरीयं वरारोहा शुभान्याभरणानि च॥ मुमोच यदि रामाय शंसेयुरिति भामिनी। वस्रमुत्सृज्य तन्मध्ये निक्षिप्तं सहभूषणम्॥
en: Thereat, that lady of expansive eyes and surpassing charms, in the hope that they might convey the intelligence to Rāma, flung off in their midst her gold-gleaming silken sheet, and elegant ornaments.

sa_verse: सम्भ्रमात् तु दशग्रीवस्तत्कर्म च न बुद्धवान्। पिङ्गाक्षास्तां विशालाक्षी नेत्रैरनिमिषैरिव ॥ विक्रोशन्तीं तदा सीतां दद्दशुर्वानरोत्तमाः।
en: But the Ten headed one owing to hurry did not observe the throwing of the cloth along with the ornaments. Those foremost of monkeys having tawny eyes observes with winkless eyes the large-eyed Sītā as she was giving way to grief.

sa_verse: स च पम्पामतिक्रम्य लङ्कामभिमुखः पुरीम्॥ जगाम मैथिली गृह्य रुदतीं राक्षसेश्वरः।
en: The lord of Rākşasas, passing beyond Pampā, directed his course towards the city of Lankā, taking Mithilā's daughter along with him, indulging lamentations.

sa_verse: तां जहार सुसंहृष्टो रावणो मृत्युमात्मनः॥ उत्सङ्गेनैव भुजगी तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रां महाविषाम्।
en: Experiencing the height of delight, Rāvana ravished her, taking her on his lap like a sharp-toothed serpent of virulent poison.

sa_verse: वनानि सरितः शैलान् सरांसि च विहायसा॥ स क्षिप्रं समतीयाय शरश्चापादिव च्युतः।
en: And speedily, like an arrow shot from a bow, he, coursing the welkin, left behind woods and streams and mountains and pieces of water.

sa_verse: तिमिनक्रनिकेतं तु वरुणालयमक्षयम्॥ सरितां शरणं गत्वा समतीयाय सागरम्।
en: Coming to the abode of Varuņa, that refuge of rivers, the exhaustless ocean—the home of whales and alligators, he crossed over it.

sa_verse: सम्भ्रमान् परिवृत्तोर्मी रुद्धमीनमहोरगः॥ वैदेह्यां ह्रियमाणायां बभूव वरुणालयः।
en: In consequence of the carrying away of Vaidehi, Varuna's abode, from grief, had all its waves stilled and its fishes and mighty snakes inert.

sa_verse: अन्तरिक्षगता वाचः ससृजुश्चारणास्तदा॥ एतदन्तो दशग्रीव इति सिद्धास्तथाब्रुवन्।
en: And the Cāranas uttered in the heavens these words, O Ten-headed one, this is your end. Thus did the Siddhas then say.

sa_verse: स तु सीतां विचेष्टन्तीमङ्केनादाय रावणः॥ प्रविवेश पुरीं लङ्कां रूपिणीं मृत्युमात्मनः।
en: And taking Sītā on his lap, representing his own Death, who endeavoured (to liberate herself). Rāvana entered the city of Lankā.

sa_verse: सोऽभिगम्य पुरीं लङ्कां सुविभक्तमहापथाम्॥ संरूढकक्ष्यां बहुलां स्वमन्तःपुरमाविशत्।
en: And entering the city of Lankā, vast, with all its highways well-arranged, and with people thronging its gates, he entered his own inner apartment.

sa_verse: तत्र तामसितापाङ्गी शोकमोहसमन्विताम्॥ निदधे रावणः सीतां मयो मायामिवासुरीम्।
en: Then Rāvana set Sītā there, having eyes with dark outer corners, exercised with grief and dole; as if Máyā had set his own Asura Illusion (in his own palace).

sa_verse: अब्रवीच्च दशग्रीवः पिशाची
रदर्शनाः॥ यथा नैनां पुमान् स्त्री वा सीतां पश्यत्यसम्मतः।
en: Then the Ten-headed one spoke to some female friends of terrible visages, Let no man or woman behold Sītā without my permission.

sa_verse: मुक्तामणि सुवर्णानि वस्त्राण्याभरणानि च॥ यद् यदिच्छेत् तदैवास्या देयं मच्छन्दतो यथा।
en: And I command that, should she ask for pearls, or rubies, or gold, or apparel, or ornaments, the same should be rendered to her.

sa_verse: या च वक्ष्यति वैदेही वचनं किंचिदप्रियम्॥ अज्ञानाद् वा ज्ञानान तस्या जीवितं प्रियम्।
en: She that, whether knowingly or unknowingly should say anything unpleasant to Vaidehī, would hold her life cheap.

sa_verse: स तान् दृष्ट्वा महावीर्यो वरदानेन मोहितः। उवाच तानिदं वाक्यं प्रशस्य बलवीर्यतः॥
en: And seeing them, the exceedingly powerful. (Ravana), blinded by the bestowal of the boon, after extolling their strength and heroism, addressed them.

sa_verse: नानाप्रहरणाः क्षिप्रमितो गच्छत सत्वराः। जनस्थानं हतस्थानं भूतं पूर्वं खरालयम्॥ तत्रास्यतां जनस्थाने शून्ये निहतराक्षसे। पौरुषं बलमाश्रित्य त्रासमुत्सृज्य दूरतः॥
en: Equipped with various weapons, do you speedily take yourself to Janasthāna-that field of carnage-which ere this contained the abode of Khara; and casting off fear at a distance, do you sojourn in vacant Janasthāna with all its Raksasas slain (by Rama).

sa_verse: ततः क्रोधो ममापूर्वो धैर्यस्योपरि वर्धते। वैरं च सुमहज्जातं रामं प्रति सुदारुणम् ॥
en: Hence unprecedented is my wrath, towering above my patience; and great and fierce also is the hostility I have conceived against Rāma.

sa_verse: निर्यातयितुमिच्छामि तच्च वैरं महारिपोः। नहि लप्स्याम्यहं निद्रामहत्वा संयुगे रिपुम्॥ तं त्विदानीमहं हत्वा खरदूषणघातिनम्। राम शर्मोपलप्स्यामि धनं लब्ध्वेव निर्धनः॥
en: I wish to avenge myself on my mighty enemy. Sleep find I none without slaying my foe in fight. Slaying that slayer of Khara and Dūşaņa, Rāma, I shall attain delight like to that attained by a pauper on gaining riches.

sa_verse: जनस्थाने वसद्भिस्तु भवद्भि राममाश्रिता। प्रवृत्तिरूपनेतव्या किं करोतीति तत्वतः॥ अप्रमादाच्च गन्तव्यं सर्वैरेव निशाचरैः। कर्तव्यश्च सदा यत्नो राघवस्य वधं प्रति॥
en: Staying in Janasthāna, you shall gather true information touching Rāma as to what he is about. Repair all you rangers of the night carefully, and strive yourselves always for slaying Rāma.

sa_verse: युष्माकं तु बलं ज्ञातं बहुशो रणमूर्धने। अतस्तस्मिञ्जनस्थाने मया यूयं निवेशिताः॥
en: I have been well acquainted with your strength in many a field, and it is for this that I set you in Janasthāna.

sa_verse: ततः प्रियं वाक्यमुपेत्य राक्षसा महार्थमष्टावभिवाद्य रावणम्। विहाय लङ्कां सहिताः प्रतस्थिरे यतो जनस्थानमलक्ष्यदर्शनाः॥
en: Hearing these agreeable and weighty words of Ravana, those Raksasas, bowing down to Rāvana, left Lankā, and in a body invisible proceeded in the direction of Janasthāna.

sa_verse: ततस्तु सीतामुपलभ्य रावणः सुसम्प्रहृष्टः परिगृह्य मैथिलीम्। प्रसज्य रामेण च वैरमुत्तमं बभूव मोहान्मुदितः स रावणः॥
en: Having obtained Mithilā's daughter, Rāvaņa experienced great joy in establishing her (in his own house); and having created high hostility with Rāma, Rāvana through blindness rejoiced greatly.

sa_verse: संदिश्य राक्षसान् घोरान् रावणोऽष्टौ महाबलान्। आत्मानं बुद्धिवैक्लव्यात् कृतकृत्यममन्यत॥
en: Having commissioned those eight terrible and mighty Rākşasas, Rāvana, in consequence of perversion of sense, considered himself as crowned with success.

sa_verse: स चिन्तयानो वैदेहीं कामबाणैः प्रपीडितः। प्रविवेश गृहं रम्यं सीतां द्रष्टुमभित्वरन्॥
en: And brooding over Vaidehi, he, sore pierced by the shafts of Kāma, hastily entered his charming mansion, with the intention of secing Sitā.

sa_verse: स प्रविश्य तु तद्वेश्म रावणो राक्षसाधिपः। अपश्यद् राक्षसीमध्ये सीतां दुःखपरायणाम्॥ अश्रुपूर्णमुखीं दीनां शोकभारावपीडिताम्। वायुवेगैरिवाक्रान्तां मज्जन्ती नावमर्णवे॥
en: And entering that apartment, Rāvana-lord of Rākşasas-saw the distressed Sītā in the midst of the Raksasas, with a tearful countenance, oppressed with a load of grief, like to a bark sinking in the ocean through the violence of the winds; like to a doe separated from the herd of deer, and surrounded by dogs.

sa_verse: मृगयूथपरिभ्रष्टां मृगी श्वभिरिवावृताम्। अधोगतमुखीं सीतां तामभ्येत्य निशाचरः॥ तां तु शोकवशाद् दीनामवशां राक्षसाधिपः। स बलाद् दर्शयामास गृहं देवगृहोपमम्॥ हर्म्यप्रासादसम्बाधं स्त्रीसहस्रनिषेवितम्। नानापक्षिगणैर्जुष्टं नानारत्नसमन्वितम्॥ दान्तकैस्तापनीयैश्च स्फाटिकै राजतैस्तथा। वज्रवैदूर्यचित्रैश्च स्तम्भैईष्टिमनोरमैः॥
en: Coming to Sītā disconsolate in consequence of stress of sorrow, remaining with her head bent down, that ranger of the night, the lord of the Rākşasas, forcibly showed to her that mansion resembling the mansion of the celestials, thick with palaces and lordly piles, inhabited by thousands of females; containing birds of various kinds; furnished with various gems; with beautiful pillars of ivory gold and crystal and silver, studded with diamonds and lapises.

sa_verse: दिव्यदुन्दुभिनिर्घोषं तप्तकाञ्चनभूषणम्। सोपानं काञ्चनं चित्रमारुरोह तथा सह॥
en: Rāvana in company with Şītā ascended the beautiful golden stairs, resounding with the sounds of kettle-drums and embellished with ornaments of burnished gold.

sa_verse: दान्तका राजताश्चैव गवाक्षाः प्रियदर्शनाः। हेमजालावृताश्चासंस्तत्र प्रासादपङ्क्तयः॥
en: And those loftily edifices had excellent windows made of vory and silver, and covered with golden nets.

sa_verse: सुधामणिविचित्राणि भूमिभागानि सर्वशः। दशग्रीवः स्वभवने प्रादर्शयत मैथिलीम्॥ दीर्घिकाः पुष्करिण्यश्च नानापुप्पसमावृताः। रावणो दर्शयामास सीतां शोकपरायणाम्॥
en: The ground all over was decorated with ambrosia and gems. The Ten-headed one in his own mansion showed to Maithilī large tanks and pools covered with various kinds of flowers. (All this) Rāvaņa showed to Sítā overmastered by sorrow.

sa_verse: दर्शयित्वा तु वैदेहीं कृत्स्नं तद्भवनोत्तमम्। उवाच वाक्यं पापात्मा सीतां लोभितुमिच्छया॥
en: And after having showed to Vaidehi the whole of that good lie of mansions, that wicked one, with the intention of tempting Sītā, spoke to her.

sa_verse: दश राक्षसकोट्यश्च द्वाविंशतिरथापराः। वर्जयित्वा जनान्वृद्धान् बालांश्च रजनीचरान्॥ तेषां प्रभुरहं सीते सर्वेषां भीमकर्मणाम्। सहस्रमेकमेकस्य मम कार्यपुरःसरम्॥
en: O Sītā, leaving out old men and boys, I am the lord of thirty two crore of night-rangers of terrible deeds. And a thousand come forward whenever required for any service.

sa_verse: यदिदं राज्यतन्त्रं मे त्वयि सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्। जीवितं च विशालाक्षि त्वं मे प्राणैर्गरीयसी॥
en: If such is my sovereignty, all this is established in you, O large-eyed lady, as well as my life. You are dearer to me than life.

sa_verse: बह्वीनामुत्तमस्त्रीणां मम योऽसौ परिग्रहः। तासां त्वमीश्वरी सीते मम भार्या भव प्रिये॥
en: O Sītā, be you the mistress of those numerous excellent women who are my wives. Dear, be you my wife.

sa_verse: साधु किं तेऽन्यथाबुद्ध्या रोचयस्व वचो मम। भजस्व माभितप्तस्य प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि॥
en: This is for your good. Why should you act otherwise? Do you relish my speech. Do you bend your mind towards me. It behoves you to favour me, who am burning in the heat of desire).

sa_verse: परिक्षिप्ता समुद्रेण लङ्केयं शतयोजना। नेयं धर्षयितुं शक्या सेन्ट्रैरपि सुरासुरैः॥
en: This Larkă measuring an hundred Yojanas girt round by the ocean, is incapable of being harassed by the celestials themselves headed by Indra.

sa_verse: न देवेषु न यक्षेषु न गन्धर्वेषु नर्षिषु। अहं पश्यामि लोकेषु यो मे वीर्यसमो भवेत्॥
en: Neither among the celestials nor Yakşãs nor Gandharvas nor Serpents, find I any one that can match me in prowess.

sa_verse: राज्यभ्रष्टेन दीनेन तापसेन पदातिना। सिं करिष्यसि रामेण मानुषिणाल्पतेजसा॥
en: What will you do with Rāma a human being of short life, poor, of small prowess, practising mendicancy?

sa_verse: भजस्व सीते मामेव भर्ताहं सदृशस्तव। यौवनं त्वध्रुवं भीरु रमस्वेह मया सह॥
en: O Sītā, bend your mind to me. I am a fit husband for you. O timed one, youth is uncertain. Sport with me here.

sa_verse: दर्शने मा कृथाः बुद्धिं राघवस्य वरानने। कास्य शक्तिरिहागन्तुमपि सीते मनोरथैः ॥
en: And, O you of a handsome countenance, do not wish for the sight of Rāghava. O Sītā, what power has he to come hither even in thought?

sa_verse: न शक्यो वायुराकाशे पाशैर्बद्धं महाजवः। दीप्यमानस्य वाप्यग्नेर्ग्रहीतुं विमला: शिखाः॥
en: None can fetter the exceedingly fleet wind in the sky, or hold the bright flame of a burning fire.

sa_verse: त्रयाणामपि लोकानां न तं पश्यामि शोभते। विक्रमेण नयेद् यस्त्वां मद्बाहुपरिपालिताम्॥
en: O beauteous one, in these three worlds I find no one that can by his might carry you away, who are protected by my arms.

sa_verse: लङ्कायाः सुमहद्राज्यमिदं त्वमनुपालय। त्वत्प्रेष्या मद्विधाश्चैव देवाश्चापि चराचरम्॥
en: Do you govern at Lankā this extensive kingdom. The like of me and celestials and all that are mobile and immobile shall be your servants.

sa_verse: अभिषेकजलक्लिन्ना तुष्टा च रमयस्व च। दुष्कृतं यत्पुरा कर्म वनवासेन तद्गतम्॥ यच्च ते सुकृतं कर्म तस्येह फलमाप्नुहि।
en: Laving your limbs with water, do you gratify me. The evil that you had done, has been expiated by your life in the forest: now do you reap the fruit of your good deeds.

sa_verse: इह सर्वाणि माल्यानि दिव्यगन्धानि मैथिलि॥ भूषणानि च मुख्यानि तानि सेव मया सह।
en: Here are garlands furnished with divine fragrance, and, O Maithili, superb ornaments. Enjoy you all those along with me.

sa_verse: पुष्पकं नाम सुश्रोणि भ्रातुर्वैश्रवणस्य मे॥ विमानं सूर्यसंकाशं तरसा निर्जितं रणे। विशालं रमणीयं च तद्विमानं मनोजवम्॥ तत्र सीते मया सार्धं विहरस्व यथासुखम्।
en: O you of shapely hips, the car called Pușpaka, resembling the sun, which (formerly) belonged to my brother Vaiśravaņa, was through my prowess won by me in fight. And vast and beautiful is that car furnished with the speed of the mind. Do you, O Sītā, at your pleasure sport on it along with me.

sa_verse: वदनं पद्मसंकाशं विमलं चारुदर्शनम्॥ शोकार्तं तु वरारोहे न भ्राजति वरानने।
en: Your face stainless and lovely to look at, resembling the lotus, dose not, O you of a comely countenance, O magnificent damsel, appear beautiful in consequence of your being exercised with grief.

sa_verse: एवं वदति तस्मिन् सा वस्त्रान्तेन वराङ्गना॥ पिधायेन्दुनिभं सीता मन्दमश्रूण्यवर्तयत्।
en: When Rāvana had spoken thus, that best of females Sītā muffling up her moon-like countenance with the ends of her cloth, began to shed gentle tears.

sa_verse: ध्यायन्ती तामिवास्वस्थां सीतां चिन्ताहतप्रभाम्॥ उवाच वचनं वीरो रावणो रजनीचरः।
en: Thereat the heroic ranger of the night, Ravana, said to Sita, distressed, sunk in thought, and deprived of her splendour through anxiety,

sa_verse: अलं वीडेन वैदेहि धर्मलोपकृतेन ते॥ आर्षोऽयं देवि निष्पन्दो यस्त्वामभिभविष्यति।
en: O Vaidehi, banish bashfulness, which stands in the way of one's duty. The yearning I feel after you is in consonance with what the sages prescribe.

sa_verse: एतौ पादौ मया स्निग्धौ शिरोभिः परिपीडितौ॥ प्रसादं कुरु मे क्षिप्रं वश्यो दासोऽहमस्मि ते।
en: These tender feet of yours I press upon my heads. Do you speedily show your favour to me. I am your slave (ever) obedient to you.

sa_verse: इमाः शून्या वाचः शुष्यमाणेन भाषिताः॥ न चापि रावणः कांचिन्मूर्खा स्त्री प्रणमेत ह। एवमुक्त्वा दशग्रीवो मैथिली जनकात्मजाम्। कृतान्तवशमापन्नो ममेयमिति मन्यते॥
en: Let not these words of mine spoken by me under the withering influence of love prove fruitless. Rāvana has never bowed his head to any female. Having said this, the Ten-headed one, come under the subjection of the Destroyer, looked upon Maithili, Janaka's daughter (as his own) saying, She is mine.

sa_verse: सा तथोक्ता तु वैदेही निर्भया शोककर्शिता। तृणमन्तरतः कृत्वा रावणं प्रत्यभाषत॥
en: Having been thus addressed, Vaidehī unaffected by fear, although exercised with grief, placing a blade of grass between herself and Rāvana, answered him.

sa_verse: राजा दशरथो नाम धर्मसेतुरिवाचलः। सत्यसंधः परिज्ञातो यस्य पुत्रः स राघवः॥ नामो नाम स धर्मात्मा त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतः। दीर्घबाहुर्विशालाक्षो दैवतं स पतिर्मम ॥
en: There was a king named Dasaratha, the bridge of righteousness, like to a mountain, ever bearing regard towards the truth, and renowned among men, whose son is Rāghava. He is named Rāma, and is righteous-souled and celebrated over the three worlds. he is long-armed, of expansive eyes, like to a celestial—he is my husband.

sa_verse: इक्ष्वाकूणां कुले जातः सिंहस्कन्धो महाद्युतिः। लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा यस्ते प्राणान् वधिष्यति॥ प्रत्यक्षं यद्यहं तस्य त्वया वै धर्षिता बलात्। शयिता त्वं हतः संख्ये जनस्थाने यथा खरः॥
en: Born in the race of the Ikşvākus, the has the shoulders of a lion and is possessed of exceeding effulgence-the same that along with his brother, Laksmana, shall take your life. If you had wronged me forcibly in his presence, you would have slain in battle in Janasthāna even as Khara.

sa_verse: य एते राक्षसाः प्रोक्ता घोररूपा महाबलाः। राघवे निर्विषाः सर्वे सुपर्णे पन्नगा यथा॥
en: All the mighty Rākşasas of grim visages, whom you have extolled (before me), shall be deprived of their venom before Rāma, as serpents are before Suparna,

sa_verse: तस्य ज्याविप्रमुक्तास्ते खराः काञ्चनभूषणाः। शरीरं विधमिष्यन्ति गङ्गाकूलमिवोर्मयः।७।।
en: The shafts decorated with gold shot by the bow-string of Rāghava, shall pierce their bodies, as the waves of the Ganga (beat against) her banks.

sa_verse: असुरैर्वा सुरैर्वा त्वं यद्यवध्योऽसि रावण। उत्पाद्य सुमहद् वैरं जीवंस्तस्य न मोक्ष्यसे ॥
en: Although, O Rāvana, you may be incapable of being slain either by the gods or the Asuras, yet having roused the high hostility of Rāghava, you will not be able to liberate your life.

sa_verse: स ते जीवितशेषस्य राघवोऽन्तकरो बली। पशोडूंपगतस्येव जीवितं तव दुर्लभम्॥
en: The strong Rāghava will compass the end of what remains of your life. Like the life of a beast tied to the sacrificial stake, your own is incapable of being reclaimed.

sa_verse: यदि पश्येत् स रामस्त्वं रोषदीप्तेन चक्षुषा। रक्षस्त्वमद्य निर्दग्धो यथा रुद्रेण मन्मथः॥
en: Should Rāma look at you with eyes aglow with anger, you, O Raksasa, would be consumed even as Manmatha was by, Rudra.

sa_verse: यश्चन्द्रं नभसो भूमौ पातयेन्नाशयेत वा। सागरं शोषयेद् वापि स सीतां मोचयेदिह॥
en: He who is able to bring down the Moon or destroy him, and to drink up the ocean dry, will surely liberate Sītā from here.

sa_verse: गतासुस्त्वं गतश्रीको गतसत्त्वो गतेन्द्रियः। लङ्का वैधव्यसंयुक्ता त्वत्कृतेन भविष्यति ॥
en: Your days are numbered, and auspiciousness has bidden you adieu. You are shorn of strength, and your senses have been dulled. And it is owing to you that Larkā shall be subject to widowhood.

sa_verse: न ते पापमिदं कर्म सुखोदकं भविष्यति। याहं नीता विनाभावं पतिपार्ध्वात् त्वया बलात्॥
en: That you by force have for naught carried me away from the side of my husband-this sinful act can never conduce to your felicity.

sa_verse: स हि देवरसंयुक्तो मम भर्ता महाद्युतिः। निर्भयो वीर्यमाश्रित्य शून्ये वसति दण्डके॥
en: That exceedingly effulgent lord of mine along with my husband's younger brother, summoning up their energy, is fearlessly dwelling in the vacant Dandaka.

sa_verse: स ते वीर्यं बलं दर्पमुत्सेकं च तथाविधम्। व्यपनेष्यति गात्रेभ्यः शरवर्षेण संयुगे॥
en: By means of an arrowy shower in conflict, he will take your prowess and strength, your hauteur and wickedness out of your person,

sa_verse: यदा विनाशो भूतानां दृश्यते कालचोदितः। तदा कार्ये प्रमाद्यन्ति नराः कालवशं गताः॥
en: When urged by the Destroyer, the destruction of creatures is perceptible, then, coming under the sway of the Destroyer, men become careless in their actions.

sa_verse: मां प्रधृष्य स ते कालः प्राप्तोऽयं राक्षसाधम। आत्मनो राक्षसानां च वधायान्तःपुरस्य च॥
en: Having outraged me, you, O worst of Rāks asas your time come; for compassing your own destruction as well as that of the Rākşasas and those dwelling in your inner apartment.

sa_verse: न शक्या यज्ञमध्यस्था वेदिः स्रुग्भाण्डमण्डिता। द्विजातिमन्त्रसम्पूता चण्डालेनावमर्दितुम्॥
en: A Candāla cannot tread the dais reared the midst of a sacrifice beauteous with ladies, and vessels, and sanctified by the twice-born ones.

sa_verse: तथाहं धर्मनित्यस्य धर्मपत्नी दृढव्रता। त्वया स्प्रष्टुं न शक्याहं राक्षसाधम पापिना॥
en: So I, the religiously wedded wife of that one, ever intent on virtue, and (always) firm in my vows, is incapable, you vilest of Raksasas, of being touched by you, a sinner.

sa_verse: क्रीडन्ती राजहंसेन पद्मषण्डेषु नित्यशः। हंसी सा तृणमध्यस्थं कथं द्रक्ष्येत मद्गुकम्॥
en: How can the female that has always ‘sported with her mate amidst lotuses, cast her eyes on a shag staying among rushes.

sa_verse: इदं शरीरं नि:संज्ञं बन्ध वा घातयस्व वा। नेदं शरीरं रक्ष्यं मे जीवितं वापि राक्षस ॥
en: Do you either bind or destroy this body deprived of sensation. This body will I not protect, nor yet this life, O Rāvana;

sa_verse: न तु शक्यमपक्रोशं पृथिव्यां दातुमात्मनः। एवमुक्त्वा तु वैदेही क्रोधात् सुपरुषं वचः॥
en: And I shall not be able to bring blame on myself in this world. having said these harsh words in wrath, Videha's daughter, Janaki, did not there again say anything to Rāvaņa.

sa_verse: रावणं जानकी तत्र पुनर्नोवाच किंचन। सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा परुषं रोमहर्षणम्॥ प्रत्युवाच ततः सीतां भयसंदर्शनं वचः।
en: Hearing Sita's words, harsh and calculated to make one's down stand on end, Rāvana answered her in words tending to excite one's apprehension,

sa_verse: शृणुमैथिलि मद्वाक्यं मासान् द्वादश भामिनि ॥ कालेनानेन नाभ्येषि यदि मां चारुहासिनि। ततस्त्वां प्रातराशार्थं सूदाश्छेत्स्यन्ति लेशशः॥
en: Hear, O Maithili, my words. O damsel if, O you of sweet smiles, within this time you do not turn yourself to me, the cooks shall cut you off in pieces to serve my morning mcal.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा परुषं वाक्यं रावणः शत्रुरावणः। राक्षसीश्च ततः क्रुद्ध इदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Having herself spoken thus, Ravana-challenger of foes-growing exceedingly wroth, addressed the Rākşasis in these words,

sa_verse: शीघ्रमेव हि राक्षस्यो विरूपा घोरदर्शनाः। दर्पमस्यापनेष्यन्तु मांसशोणितभोजनाः॥
en: You frightful Rākşasīs terrible to behold, subsisting on flesh and gore, do you at once crush her pride.

sa_verse: वचनादेव तास्तस्य सुघोरा घोरदर्शनाः। कृतप्राञ्जलयो भूत्वा मैथिली पर्यवारयन्॥
en: As soon as he had said this, those frightful and terrible Rākṣasīs with joined hands encircled Maithili.

sa_verse: स ताः प्रोवाच राजासौ रावणो घोरदर्शनाः। प्रचल्य चरणोत्कर्षैर्दारयन्निव मेदिनीम्॥
en: Then the grim-visaged king Ravana, as if riving the earth by his tread, proceeding a pace or two, said,

sa_verse: अशोकवनिकामध्ये मैथिली नीयतामिति। तत्रेयं रक्ष्यतां गूढं युष्माभिः परिवारिता॥ तत्रैनां तर्जनैोरैः पुनः सान्त्वैश्च मैथिलीम्। आनयध्वं वशं सर्वा वन्यां गजवधूमिव ॥
en: Do you take Maithilī to the wood of Asokas. There surrounding her, do you secretly guard her; and there (sometimes) by storming, and (at other) by means of soft speech, do you all strive to bring Mithila's daughter, like a wild female elephant, under your sway.

sa_verse: इति प्रतिसमादिष्टा राक्षस्यो रावणेन ताः। अशोकवनिकां जग्मुर्मैथिली परिगृह्य तु॥ सर्वकामफलैर्वृक्षैर्नानापुष्पफलैर्वृताम्। सर्वकालमदैश्चापि द्विजैः समुपसेविताम्॥
en: Thus commanded by Rāvana, those Rākşasis taking Maithili along with them, went to the Asoka wood, abounding with trees granting every desire, and filled with various kinds of fruits and flowers, and frequented by fowls fraught with juices at all seasons.

sa_verse: सा तु शोकपरिताङ्गी मैथिली जनकात्मजा। राक्षसीवशमापन्ना व्याघ्रीणां हरिणी यथा॥
en: And as a doe comes under the subjection of tigresses, Janaka's daughter, Maithili, her frame worked up with grief, came under the sway of the Raksasis.

sa_verse: शोकेन महता त्रस्ता मैथिली जनकात्मजा। न शर्म लभते भीरुः पाशबद्धा मृगी यथा॥
en: And like a female deer fast bound by a trap, Janaka's timid daughter Maithilī, agitated by the mighty grief, did not attain respite.

sa_verse: न विन्दते तत्र तु शर्म मैथिली विरूपनेत्राभिरतीव तर्जिता। पतिं स्मरन्ती दयितं च देवरं विचेतनाभूद् भयशोकपीडिता ॥
en: And greatly up-braided by the fierce eyes (of the Raksasis), Maithili did not know repose; and, afflicted with grief and fear, she, remembering her beloved lord along with Lakşmaņa swooned away.

sa_verse: इतः परं प्रक्षिप्तोऽयं सर्गः।। प्रवेशितायां सीतायां लङ्कां प्रति पितामह। तदा प्रोवाच देवेन्द्र परितुष्टं शतक्रतुम्॥
en: Having been thus addressed, Vaidehi unaffected by fear, although exercised with grief, placing a blade of grass between herself and Ravana, answered him.

sa_verse: त्रैलोक्यस्य हितार्थाय रक्षसामहिताय च। लङ्कां प्रवेशिता सीता रावणेन दुरात्मना ॥
en: On Sītā having entered Lankā, the great father addressed the gratified Devendra of an hundred sacrifices, For bringing about the weal of the three worlds, and the woe of the Rākşasas, Sītā has been taken into Lankā by the wickedminded, Rāvaņa.

sa_verse: पतिव्रता महाभागा नित्यं चैव सुस्त्रैधिता। अपश्यन्ती च भतौरं पश्यन्ती राक्षसीजनम्॥ राक्षसीभिः परिवृता भर्तृदर्शनलालसा।
en: Devoted to her husband, the exalted lady always brought up in happiness, not seeing her husband, and seeing (on the other hand) environed by numbers of Räkşasīs,-is hungering after the sight of her lord.

sa_verse: निविष्टा हि पुरी लङ्का तीरे नदनदीपतेः॥ कथं ज्ञास्यति तां रामस्तत्रस्थां तामनिन्दिताम्।
en: The city of Lankā is situated on the shores of the lord of rivers and streams. How can Rāma get a knowledge of that blameless one, staying there?

sa_verse: दुःशं संचिन्तयनिती सा बहुशः परिदुर्लभा ॥ प्राणयात्रामकुर्राणा प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यत्यसंशयम्। स भूयः संशयो जातः सीतायाः प्राणसंक्षये॥
en: Brooding over the various ills she has undergone, that exceedingly rare damsel is passing her days. Surely she will resign her existence. Great is the doubt that has arisen as to Sītā putting a period to her existence.

sa_verse: स त्वं शीघ्रमितो गत्वा सीतां पश्य शुभाननाम्। प्रविश्य नगरी लङ्कां प्रयच्छ हविरुत्तमम्॥
en: Having yourself from here, do you see the fair-faced Sītā. having entered the city of Lankā, Offer excellent clarified butter.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तोऽथ देवेन्द्रः पुरी रावणपालिताम्। आगच्छन्निद्रया सार्ध भगवान् पाकशासनः॥
en: Thus addressed by the reverend chastiser of Pāka, Devendra in company with sleep, approached the city ruled by Rāvana.

sa_verse: निद्रां चोवाच गच्छ त्वं राक्षसान् सम्प्रमोहय। सा तथोक्ता मघवता देवी परमहर्षिता॥ देवकार्यार्थसिद्धयर्थं प्रामोहयत राक्षसान् ।
en: He then addressed sleep, saying. Go you; and stupefy the Räksasas. Thus accosted by Maghavat, that goddess, exceedingly delighted, for securing success to the work of the celestials, covered the Rākşasas with stupor.

sa_verse: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे देवः सहस्राक्षः शचीपतिः॥ आसमाद वनस्थां तां वचनं चेदमब्रवीत् ।
en: In the meanwhile, that god, the thousand-eyed lord of Śacī went to (Sītā) staying in the woods, and spoke to her these words,

sa_verse: देवराजोऽस्मि भद्रं ते इह चास्मि शुचिस्मिते॥ अहं त्वां कार्यनिद्दध्यर्थं राघवस्य महात्मनः। साहाय्यं कल्पयिष्यामि मा शुचो जनकात्मजे ॥
en: I am the sovereign of the celestials. Good betide you! I am here, O you of luminous smiles! For securing success to the work of the magnanimous Rāghava, I will lend my aid to you. Do not, Odaughter of Janaka, grieve.

sa_verse: मत्प्रसादात् समुद्रं स तरिष्यति बलैः सह। मयैवेह च राक्षस्यो मायाय मोहिताः शुभे॥
en: Through my grace, he shall along with his forces cross over the ocean. And, O excellent wench, I have by my supernal power, stupified the Raksasis.

sa_verse: तस्मादन्नमिदं सीते हविष्यान्नहं स्वयम्। स त्वां संगृह्य वैदेहि आगतः सह निद्रया।१४।।
en: And, O Sita, for this reason, I along with sleep, taking these rice, rice boiled in clarified butter, have, O Vaidehi, corne to you.

sa_verse: एतदत्स्यसि मद्धस्तान्न त्वां वाधिष्यते शुभे। क्षुधा तृषा च रम्भोरु वर्षाणामयुतैरपि॥
en: If you partake of these from my hand, you shall never O beauteous one, be afflicted either with hunger or thirst, O you of thighs resembling rambhā (trunks), for years.

sa_verse: एवमुक्ता तु देवेन्द्रमुवाच परिशङ्किता। कथं जानामि देवेन्द्रं त्वामिहस्थं खजापतिम्॥
en: Thus addressed, Sītā, alarmed, said, How can I know you for Devendra, Saci's husband, staying here?

sa_verse: देवलिङ्गानि दृष्टानि रामलक्ष्मणसंनिधौ। तानि दर्शय देवेन्द्र यदि त्वं देवराट् स्वयम्॥
en: By the side of Rāma and Lakşmaņa, I had beheld the signs of the celestials. If, O Devendra, you are yourself the sovereign of the celestials, show those to me.

sa_verse: सीताया वचनं श्रुत्वा तथा चक्रे शचीपतिः। गृथिवीं नास्पृशत् पद्भ्यामनिमेषेक्षणानि च॥ अरजोऽम्बरधारी च नम्लीनकुसुमस्तथा। तं ज्ञात्वा लक्ष्णैः सीता वासवं परिहर्षिता ॥
en: Hearing Sītā's words, the lord of Saçī did accordingly. he did not touch the earth with his feet, and his eyes remained winkless. he bloomed in youth, and the blossoms did not fade on his attire. Thereupon knowing him for Vāsava, Sītā was overjoyed.

sa_verse: उवाच वाक्यं रुदती भगवन् राघवं प्रति। कह भ्रात्रा महाबाहुर्दिष्टयः मे श्रुतिमागतः॥
en: And weeping, she spoke regarding Rāghava, By luck it is that I had heard of that mightyarmed one along with his brother.

sa_verse: यथा मे श्वशुरो राजा यथा च मिथिलाधिपः। सथा त्वमद्य पश्यामि सनाथो मे पतिस्त्वया॥
en: As is my father-in-law, the king, as is the master of Mithilā, so are you (to me), you whom I behold to-day; my husband has now found a protector.

sa_verse: तवाज्ञया च देवेन्द्र पयोभूतमिदं हविः। अशिष्यामि त्वया दत्तं रघूणां कुलवर्घनम्॥
en: And by your command, O Devendra, will I partake of this pāyasa cooked with milk, which has been offered to me, and which shall enhance (the prosperity) of our race.

sa_verse: इन्द्रहस्ताद् गृहीत्वा तत् पायसं सा शुचिस्मिता। न्यवेदयत भर्ने सा लक्ष्मणाय च मैथिली॥
en: Thereupon taking the pāyasa from Indra's hands, that Maithilī of luminous smiles (mentally) offered it to her husband as well as Laksmana.

sa_verse: यदि जीवति मे भर्ता सह भ्रात्रा महाबलः। इदमस्तु तयोर्भक्त्या तदाश्नात् स्वयम्॥
en: If my mighty lord live along with his brother, let this through my reverence for them, be theirs. She then partook of the pāyasa herself.

sa_verse: इतीव तत् प्राश्य हविर्वरानना जहौ क्षुधादुःखसमुद्भवं च तम्। इन्द्रात् प्रवृत्तिमुपलभ्य जानकी काकुत्स्थयोः प्रीतमना बभूव ॥
en: Having thus eaten it, that one of excellent countenance, had her hunger and rising sorrow removed; and attaining a mental tendency from Indra, Janaki grew glad with reference to the Kakutsthas.

sa_verse: स चापि शक्रस्त्रिदिवालयं तदा प्रीतो ययौ राघवकार्यसिद्धये। आमन्त्र्य सीतां स ततो महात्मा जगाम निद्रासहितः स्वमालयम्॥
en: And Sakra also for bringing about the success of Rāghava's work, with a pleased mind, went to the abode of celestials. And greeting Sītā again and again, that high-souled celestial, in company with sleep went back to his own abode.

sa_verse: राक्षसं मृगरूपेण चरन्तं कामरूपिणम्। निहत्य रामो मारीचं तूर्णं पथि न्यवर्तत॥
en: Killing the Rākṣasa, Mārīca assuming shapes at will and ranging in the shape of a deer, Rāma vended speedily his way.

sa_verse: तस्य संत्वरमाणस्य द्रष्टुकामस्य मैथिलीम्। क्रूरस्वनोऽथ गोमायुर्विननादास्य पृष्ठतः॥
en: And as he hurried himself, eager to behold Maithilī, jackals began to howl hideously at his back.

sa_verse: स तस्य स्वरमाज्ञाय दारुणं रोमहर्षणम्। चिन्तयामास गोमायोः स्वरेण परिशङ्कितः॥
en: Hearing their harsh cries, capable of making one's hair stand on end, Rāma struck with fear at the voices of the jackals, became filled with alarm.

sa_verse: अशुभं वत मन्येऽहं गोमायुर्वाशते यथा। स्वस्ति स्यादपि वैदेह्या राक्षसैर्भक्षणं विना ॥
en: Ah! I consider this as inauspicious—that these jackals are crying. Escaping being devoured by the Raksasas, may fair fortune befall Vaidehi!

sa_verse: मारीचेन तु विज्ञाय स्वरमालक्ष्य मामकम्। विक्रुष्टं मृगरूपेण लक्ष्मणः शृणुयाद् यदि॥ स सौमित्रिः स्वरं श्रुत्वा तां च हित्वाथ मैथिलीम्। तयैव प्रहितः क्षिप्रं मत्सकाशमिहैष्यति॥
en: If Laksmana should have heard the cries which Mārica, knowing my voice, and fixing on the means of harming me, uttered in the form of a deer, Saumitrī, hearing that voice, leaving Mithila's daughter and commissioned by herself, must have come near me.

sa_verse: राक्षसैः सहितैनूनं सीताया ईप्सितो वधः। काञ्चनश्च मृगो भूत्वा व्यपनीयाश्रमात् तु माम्॥ दूरं नीत्वाथ मारीचो राक्षसोऽभूच्छराहतः। हा लक्ष्मण हतोऽस्मीति यद्वाक्यं व्याजहार ह॥
en: Surely, the Rākṣasas in a body are desirous of slaying Sitä. Becoming a golden deer, Mārica, having allured me far, transformed himself into a Rākşasa, as soon as he had been struck with my shafts; and exclaimed, 'Ah! Laksmana, slain am I.'

sa_verse: अपि स्वस्ति भवेद् द्वाभ्यां रहिताभ्यां मया वने। जनस्थाननिमित्तं हि कृतवैरोऽस्मि राक्षसैः॥
en: It is doubtless, we having left (Sītā, whether all is well with her. I having raised the hostility of the Räksasas for the sake of Janasthāna;

sa_verse: निमित्तानि च घोराणि दृश्यन्तेऽद्य बहूनि च। इत्येवं चिन्तयन् रामः श्रुत्वा गोमायुनिःस्वनम् ॥ निवर्तमानस्त्वरितो जगामाश्रममात्मवान्।
en: And many and dreadful are the omens I see (around me). Thus reflecting as he heard the howlings of the jackals, the self-possessed Rāma with hasty steps returned to the asylum.

sa_verse: आत्मनश्चापनयनं मृगरूपेण रक्षसा॥ आजगाम जनस्थानं राघवः परिशङ्कितः।
en: Rāghava went back to Janasthāna, alarmed in consequence of his having been drawn away by the Raksasa in the form of a dear.

sa_verse: तं दीनमानसं दीनमासेदुर्मृगपक्षिणः॥ सव्यं कृत्वा महात्मानं घोरांश्च ससृजुः स्वरान्।
en: And birds and beasts approached that highsouled one distressed and depressed in spirit; and staying on his left set up frightful cries.

sa_verse: तानि दृष्ट्वा निमित्तानि महाघोरणि राघवः॥ ततो लक्ष्मणमायान्तं ददर्श विगतप्रभम्। ततो विदूरे रामेण समीयाय स लक्ष्मणः॥
en: As he was witnessing the exceedingly dreadful signs, Rāghava saw Lakşmaņa coming with a lacklustre (countenance); and Lakşmaņa came up to Rāma.

sa_verse: गृहीत्वा च करं सव्यं लक्ष्मणं रघुनन्दनः॥ उवाच मधुरोदर्कमिदं परुषमार्तवत्।
en: And taking Lakşmaņa's left hand, the son of Raghu in extreme distress sweetly spoke these rough words,

sa_verse: अहो लक्ष्मण गौँ ते कृतं यत् त्वं विहाय ताम्॥ सीतामिहागतः सौम्य कच्चित् स्वस्ति भवेदिति।
en: Alas! Laksmana, you have committed a censurable.,act; leaving Sita, O mild one, you have come hither. Is it well with her?

sa_verse: न मेऽस्ति संशयो वीर सर्वथा जनकात्मजा ॥ विनष्टा भक्षिता वापि राक्षसैर्वनचारिभिः।
en: I make no doubt, O hero, but that Janaka's daughter has either been slain or devoured by Rākṣasas ranging the forest.

sa_verse: अशुभान्येव भूयिष्ठं यथा प्रादुर्भवन्ति मे॥ अपि लक्ष्मण सीतायाः सामग्र्यं प्राप्नुयामहे। जीवन्त्याः पुरुषव्याघ्र सुताया जनकस्य वै॥
en: And, considering the many omens that take place before me, O Laksmana, I do not know whether we shall light upon welfare of Janaka's daughter Sita being alive, O best of men.

sa_verse: यथा वै मृगसंघाश्च गोमायुश्चैव भैरवम्। वाशन्ते शकुनाश्चापि प्रदीप्तामभितो दिशम्। अपि स्वस्ति भवेत् तस्या राजपुत्र्या महाबल॥
en: As these multitudes of beasts and these jackals are crying frightfully in the flaming direction*, I do not know, O you of mighty strength, whether it is well with that daughter of the king. i.e. The quarter presided over by the sun, vic., the East.

sa_verse: इदं हि रक्षो मृगसंनिकाशं प्रलोभ्य मां दूरमनुप्रयातम्। हतं कथंचिन्महता श्रमेण स राक्षसोऽभून्द्रियमाण एव॥
en: This Rākşasa, who, wearing the shape of a deer, and, alluring me, had drawn me far, has in some sort been slain by me wiih much ado; and he became a Rāksasa at the time of his death.

sa_verse: मनश्च मे दीनमिहाप्रहृष्टं चक्षुश्च सव्यं कुरुते विकारम्। असंशयं लक्ष्मण नास्ति सीता हृता मृता वा पथि वर्तते वा॥
en: Yet my mind is poor and cheerless; and my left eye throbs. Doubtless, O Laksmana, Sita is not, she is either carried away, or dead, or is wandering on the way. SECITION 58

sa_verse: स दृष्ट्वा लक्ष्मणं दीनं शून्यं दशरथात्मजः। पर्यपृच्छत धर्मात्मा वैदहीमागतं विना॥
en: Seeing Lakşmana cast down, cheerless, and come without Vaidehī, the righteous son of Dasaratha, asked him.

sa_verse: प्रस्थितं दण्डकारण्यं या मामनुजगाम ह। क्व सा लक्ष्मण वैदेही यां हित्वा त्वमिहागतः॥
en: Where, O Laksmana is that Vaidehi, who has followed me to the Dandaka forest, and leaving whom you have come hither?

sa_verse: राज्यभ्रष्टस्य दीनस्य दण्डकान् परिधावतः। क्व सा दुःखसहाया मे वैदेही तनुमध्यमा॥
en: Where is that one of a slender waist, who is the help in trouble of me, deprived of my kingdom, dispirited, and running about the Dandakas?

sa_verse: यां विना नोत्सहे वीर मुहूर्तमपि जीवितुम्। क्व सा प्राणसहाया मे सीता सुरसुतोपमा॥
en: Without whom, O hero, I cannot live for a moment-where is that life's help of me Sītā resembling the daughter of a celestial?

sa_verse: पतित्वममराणां हि पृथिव्याश्चापि लक्ष्मण । विना तां तपनीयाभा नेच्छेयं जनकात्मजाम्॥
en: O Laksmana, without Janaka's daughter (hued) like burning gold, I covet not the sovereignty of the celestials or the earth.

sa_verse: कच्चिज्जीवति वैदेही प्राणैः प्रियतरा मम। कच्चित् प्रव्राजनं वीर न मे मिथ्या भविष्यति॥
en: Is Vaidehī, dearer to me than my life still living? Shall this exile of mine be of no avail?

sa_verse: सीतानिमित्तं सौमित्रे मृते मयि गते त्वयि। कच्चित् सकामा कैकेयी सुखिता सा भविष्यति॥ सपुत्रराज्यां सिद्धार्थां मृतपुत्रा तपस्विनी। उपस्थास्यति कौसल्या कच्चित्सौम्येन कैकयीम्॥
en: O Sumitrā's son, on my dying for Sītā and your returning (to the city). shall Kaikeyi have her desire, and attain felicity; and shall Kausalyā, her son dead, and herself wearing the guise of a female mendicant, humbly wait upon Kaikeyi when she shall have succeeded in obtaining the kingdom for her son?

sa_verse: यदि जीवति वैदेही गमिष्याम्याश्रमं पुनः। संवृत्ता यदि वृत्ता सा प्राणांस्त्यक्ष्यामि लक्ष्मण ॥
en: If Vaidehī live, I will then return to the asylum; but O Lakşmaņa, if that one of excellent character should happen to be dead, I will also renounce my life.

sa_verse: यदि मामाश्रमगतं वैदेही नाभिभाषते। पुरः प्रहसिता सीता विनशिष्यामि लक्ष्मण ॥
en: If, O Laksmana, Vaidehi ever precluding her speech with a smile should not speak to me when I arrive at the asylum, I shall give up my life.

sa_verse: ब्रूहि लक्ष्मण वैदेही यदि जीवति वा न वा। त्वयि प्रमत्ते रक्षोभिर्भक्षिता वा तपस्विनी॥
en: tell me, O Laksmana, whether Do you Videha's daughter live or not; or whether, in consequence of your acting heedlessly, that forlorn wench has been devoured by Raksasas.

sa_verse: सुकुमारी च बाला च नित्यं चादुः :खभागिनी। मद्वियोगेन वैदेही व्यक्तं शोचति दुर्मनाः॥
en: Of a tender frame, and a mere girl, Vaidehi, never having experienced unhappiness being cast down, surely weep for my separation.

sa_verse: सर्वथा रक्षसा तेन जिह्येन सुदुरात्मना। वदता लक्ष्मणेत्युच्चस्तवापि जनितं भयम्॥
en: When that exceedingly wicked Rākşasa cried, Lakşmaņa at the top of his voice, was you also seized with fear?

sa_verse: श्रुतश्च मन्ये वैदेह्या स स्वरः सदृशो मम। त्रस्तया प्रेषितस्त्वं च द्रष्टुं मां शीघ्रमागतः॥
en: And I apprehend that voice resembling mine was heard by Vaidehī; and, despatched by her from fear, you may have come hither swiftly to see* me. Some texts-to rescue me.

sa_verse: सर्वथा तु कृतं कष्टं सीतामुत्सृजता वने। प्रतिकर्तुं नृशंसानां रक्षसां दत्तमन्तरम्॥
en: You have every way acted unwisely in having left Şītā alone in the wood. By this you have afforded opportunity to the cruel Rākşasas to repair the mischief (I have done them).

sa_verse: दुःखिताः खरघातेन राक्षसाः पिशिताशनाः। तैः सीता निहता घोरैर्भविष्यति न संशयः॥
en: The Rākşasas subsisting flesh are aggrieved because of Khara having been slain; and now, without doubt, those terrible ones have slain Sita.

sa_verse: अहोऽस्मि व्यसने मग्नः सर्वथा रिपुनाशन। किं त्विदानीं करिष्यामि शङ्के प्राप्तव्यमीदृशम्॥
en: Alas! absolutely sunk am I in peril, O destroyer of foes. What shall I do now? I fear such an event was appointed for me.

sa_verse: इति सीतां वरारोहां चिन्तयन्नेव राघवः। आजगाम जनस्थानं त्वरया सहलक्ष्मणः॥
en: Thus thinking of Sītā, paragon among women, Rāghava hastily went to Janasthāna in company with Laksmana. on

sa_verse: विगर्हमाणोऽनुजमार्तरूपं क्षुधाश्रमेणैव पिपासया च। विनिःश्वसशुष्कमुखो विषण्णः प्रतिश्रयं प्राप्य समीक्ष्य शून्यम्॥
en: Taking to task his younger brother of distressed visage, Rāma, afflicted with hunger and thirst, and dejected in spirits, sighing heavily with a countenance turned pale, entered the asylum and found it vacant.

sa_verse: स्वमाश्रमं स प्रविगाह्य वीरो विहारदेशाननुसृत्य कांश्चित्। एतत्तदित्येव निवासभूमौ प्रहृष्टरोमा व्यथितो बभूव ॥
en: And entering his own asylum that hero went to the play-grounds (of Sītā) and remembering the sporting ground (of Sītā) in that abode, he was filled with grief and his down stood on end.

sa_verse: अथाश्रमादुपावृत्तमन्तरा रघुनन्दनः। परिपप्रच्छ सौमित्रिं रामो दुःखादिदं वचः॥
en: Then coming out of the hermitage, Raghu's descendant, Rāma, after a while, from grief, spoke these words to the son of Sumitrā,

sa_verse: तमुवाच किमर्थं त्वमागतोऽपास्य मैथिलीम्। यदा सा तव विश्वासाद् वने विरहिता मया॥
en: When confiding myself in you, I had left Maithilī with you in the wood, why then did you go out, leaving her behind?

sa_verse: दृष्ट्वैवाभ्यागतं त्वां मे मैथिली त्यज्य लक्ष्मण । शङ्कमानं महत् पापं यत्सत्यं व्यथितं मनः॥
en: O Laksmana, directly I saw you approach, renouncing Maithilī, my mind, apprehending great wrong, became really aggrieved.

sa_verse: स्फुरते नयनं सव्यं बाहुश्च हृदयं च मे। दृष्ट्वा लक्ष्मण दूरे त्वां सीताविरहितं पथि।४।।
en: O Lakşmaņa, seeing you coming at a distance, renouncing her, my left eye and arm as well as my heart keep throbbing.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु सौमित्रिर्लक्ष्मणः : शुभलक्षणः। भूयो दुःखसमाविष्टो दुःखितं राममब्रवीत्॥
en: Thus accosted, Laksmana having auspicious signs, afflicted with great grief, said to the aggrieved Rāma,

sa_verse: न स्वयं कामकारेण तां त्यक्त्वाहमिहागतः। प्रचोदितस्तयैवोग्रैस्त्वत्सकाशमिहागतः॥
en: I have not come hither, of my own accord, renouncing Sītā; but I have come to you, having been urged thereto by herself with rudeness.

sa_verse: आर्येणेव परिक्रुष्टं लक्ष्मणेति सुविस्वरम्। परित्राहीति यद्वाक्यं मैथिल्यास्तच्छ्रुतिं गतम्॥
en: The cries of O Laksimana, save me, as if uttered by the master, came to the ears of Maithili.

sa_verse: सा तमार्तस्वरं श्रुत्वा तव स्नेहेन मैथिली। गच्छ गच्छेति मामाशु रुदती भयविक्लवा:॥
en: Hearing those distressful accents, Maithili from affection [for you], breaking out into lamentations, and overwhelmed with fear, spoke to me, Off, off.

sa_verse: प्रचोद्यमानेन मया गच्छेति बहुशस्तया। प्रत्युक्ता मैथिली वाक्यमिदं तत् प्रत्ययान्वितम्॥
en: On being repeatedly urged, with Go, answered Maithili in these words, tending to inspire her confidence.

sa_verse: न तत् पश्याम्यहं रक्षो यदस्य भयमावहेत् । निर्वृता भव नास्त्येतत् केनाप्येतदुदाहृतम्॥
en: I do not see such a Rākṣasa, as can excite his fear. Do you desist. These cries do not come from him; but must have been uttered by someone else.

sa_verse: विगर्हितं च नीचं च कथमार्योऽभिधास्यति। त्राहीति वचनं सीते यस्त्रायेत् त्रिदशानपि॥
en: How can he that can rescue the celestials themselves, utter, O Sītā, such a blame-worthy and base word as-save me?

sa_verse: किंनिमित्तं तु केनापि भ्रातुरालम्ब्य मे स्वरम्। विस्वरं व्याहृतं वाक्यं लक्ष्मण त्राहि मामिति ॥
en: Some one for some purpose, assuming my brother's voice, is crying-O Laksmana, save me.

sa_verse: राक्षसेनेरितं वाक्यं त्रासात् त्राहीति शोभने। न भवत्या व्यथा कार्या कुनारीजनसेविता॥
en: O beauteous lady, these words, save memust have been uttered by some Rākşasa from fear. You should not act like a mean woman.

sa_verse: अलं विक्लवतां गन्तुं स्वस्था भव निरुत्सुका। न चास्ति त्रिषु लोकेषु पुमान् यो राघवं रणे॥ जातो वा जायमानो वा संयुगे यः पराजयेत्। अजेयो राघवो युद्धं देवैः शक्रपुरोगमैः॥
en: Do not be over-whelmed; and banish your anxiety. There breathes no person, nor yet shall there be born any one in these three worlds who in the field shall vanquish Rāghava in fight. Rāghava is incapable of being beaten in battle by the very gods headed by Indra.'

sa_verse: एवमुक्ता तु वैदेही परिमोहितचेतना। उवाचाश्रूणि मुञ्चन्ती दारुणं मामिदं वचः॥
en: Thus addressed (by me) Vaidehi, deprived of her sense, shedding tears, spoke to me these cruel words,

sa_verse: भावो मयि तवात्यर्थं पाप एव निवेशितः। विनष्टे भ्रातरि प्राप्तुं न च त्वं मामवाप्स्यसे॥
en: You cherish the vile idea that on your brother perishing, you shall come by me; but me you shall never have.

sa_verse: संकेताद् भरतेन त्वं रामं समनुगच्छसि। क्रोशन्तं हि यथात्यर्थं नैनमभ्यवपद्यसे॥
en: As you do not go to him albeit he is crying loudly (for help), you follow Rāma in consonance with a hint from Bharata.

sa_verse: रिपुः प्रच्छन्नचारी त्वं मदर्थमनुगच्छसि। राघवस्यान्तरं प्रेप्सुस्तथैनं नाभिपद्यसे॥
en: A foe going about in disguise, you follow Rāma for my sake, prying into Rāghava's draw backs; and it is for this that you do not go (to him)?

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु वैदेह्या संरब्धो रक्तलोचनः। क्रोधात् प्रस्फुरमाणोष्ठ आश्रमादभिनिर्गतः॥
en: Thus accosted by Vaidehi, I, with eyes reddened in wrath, and my nether lip swollen in ire, rushed out of the asylum.

sa_verse: एवं ब्रुवाणं सौमित्रिं रामः संतापमोहितः। अब्रवीद् दुष्कृतं सौम्य तां विना त्वमिहागतः॥
en: When Saumitri had spoken thus, Rāma transported by grief; said to Laksmana, O gentle one, you have done wrong in having come out hither without her.

sa_verse: जानन्नपि समर्थं मां रक्षसामपवारणे। अनेन क्रोधवाक्येन मैथिल्या निर्गतो भवान्॥
en: Although you knew (full well) that I was able to withstand the Rākşasas, yet did you sally out at the angry words of Mithila's daughter.

sa_verse: नहि ते परितुष्यामि त्यक्त्वा यदसि मैथिलीम्। क्रुद्धायाः परुषं श्रुत्वा स्त्रिया यत् त्वमिहागतः॥
en: I am not pleased with you that hearing her harsh speech spoken in wrath, you have come hither, leaving Vaidehi behind.

sa_verse: सर्वथा त्वपनीतं ते सीतया यत् प्रचोदितः। क्रोधस्य वशमागम्य नाकरोः शासनं मम॥
en: You have every way done wrong in not acting out my mandate in consequence of being urged by Sītā, and under the influence of indignation.

sa_verse: असौ हि राक्षसः शेते शरेणाभिहतो मया। मृगरूपेण येनाहमाश्रमादपवाहितः॥
en: That Rākṣasa lie low, being wounded by my shafts—that had drawn me away from the asylum wearing the form of a deer.

sa_verse: विकृष्य चापं परिधाय सायकं सलीलबाणेन च ताडितो मया। मार्गी तनुं त्यज्य च विक्लवस्वरो बभूव केयूरधरः स राक्षसः॥
en: I hit him stretching my brow slightly and fixing the shaft on it; when, renouncing his deerform he became a Rākṣasa wearing a bracelet and began to emit distressful shrieks.

sa_verse: शराहतेनैव तदातया गिरा स्वरं ममालम्ब्य सुदूरसुश्रवम्। उपाहृतं तद् वचनं सुदारुणं त्वमागतो येन विहाय मैथिलीम्॥
en: Wounded by my shaft, he, assuming my voice, and in accents capable of being heard from far, uttered those dreadful words fraught with dole, hearing which, you have come hither, renouncing Mithilā's daughter.

sa_verse: भृशमाव्रजमानस्य तस्याधो वामलोचनम्। प्रास्फुरच्चास्खलद् रामो वेपथुश्चास्य जायते॥
en: As Rāma went on, his feet failed him, his left eye to beat, and a trembling came over his frame.

sa_verse: उपालक्ष्य निमित्तानि सोऽशुभानि मुहुर्मुहुः। अपि क्षेमं तु सीताया इति वै व्याजहार ह॥
en: Seeing again and again all these signs, he continually kept on asking (Lakşmaņa), Is it well with Sita?

sa_verse: त्वरमाणो जगामाथ सीतादर्शनलालसः। शून्यामावसथं दृष्ट्वा बभूवोद्विग्नमानसः॥
en: Eager to behold Sītā, he proceeded fast; but finding the abode empty, he was filled with anxiety.

sa_verse: उद्भ्रमन्निव वेगेन विक्षिपन् रघुनन्दनः। तत्र तत्रोटजस्थानमभिवीक्ष्य समन्ततः॥ ददर्श पर्णशालां च सीतया रहितां तदा। श्रिया विरहितां ध्वस्तां हेमन्ते पद्मिनीमिव ॥
en: And proceeding with swiftness, throwing about his limbs, Raghu's son began to survey all around the hut. He then found it empty of Sītā, like to a tank in evil plight and bereft of lotuses-during the winter.

sa_verse: रुदन्तमिव वृक्षैश्च ग्लानपुष्पमृगद्विजम् । श्रिया विहीनं विध्वस्तं संत्यक्तं वनदैवतैः॥ विप्रकीर्णाजिनकुशं विप्रविद्धबृसीकटम्। दृष्ट्वा शून्योटजस्थानं विललाप पुनः पुनः॥
en: And seeing the cottage empty, with its trees as if sorrowing, and its flowers faded, and its beasts and birds sunk in gloom, shorn of grace, worn out, forsaken by the sylvan deities, strewn with deer-skins and Kusa, and twists of Kasa, he wept again and again.

sa_verse: हृता मृता वा नष्टा वा भक्षिता वा भविष्यति। निलीनाप्यथवा भीरुरथवा वनमाश्रिता।।।।। गता विचेतुं पुष्पाणि फलान्यपि च वा पुनः। अथवा पद्मिनी याता जलार्थं वा नदीं गता॥
en: Has the timid one been carried off, or is she dead, or has any one eaten her up, or has she vanished (from the earth), or has she gone to the wood, or has she gone to cull flowers and fruits, or has she gone to the pool for procuring water, or has she repaired to the river?

sa_verse: यत्नान्मृगयमाणस्तु नाससाद वने प्रियाम्। शोकरक्तेक्षणः श्रीमानुन्मत्त इव लक्ष्यते॥
en: Although he searched his beloved one carefully, yet he failed to find her out in the wood-land. And that graceful one with his eyes reddened with grief, seemed like a maniac

sa_verse: वृक्षाद् वृक्षं प्रधावन् स गिरिश्चापि नदीनदम्। बभ्राम विलपरामः शोकपङ्कार्णवप्लुतः॥
en: He rushed from tree to tree, and bewailing being sunk in an ocean of grief traversed all the rivers and mountains.

sa_verse: अस्ति कच्चित्त्वया दृष्ट्वा सा कदम्बप्रिया प्रिया। कदम्ब यदि जानीषे शंस सीतां शुभाननाम्॥ स्निग्धपल्लवसंकाशां पीतकौशेयवासिनीम्। शंसस्व यदि सा दृष्ट्वा बिल्ब बिल्वोपमस्तनी॥
en: O Kadamba, have you seen where is that one fond of Kadamba groves? If know you this do you tell me of Sītā having an auspicious countenance. O Bilva, tell me pray, if you have seen her, wearing silken cloth, resembling cool leaves and having breast like to Bilva fruits.

sa_verse: अथवार्जुन शंस त्वं प्रियां तामर्जुनप्रियाम्। जनकस्य सुता तन्वी यदि जीवति वा न वा॥
en: Or, O Aryunā, she was very fond of you, tell me if live that daughter of Janaka of slender frame.

sa_verse: ककुभः ककुभोरूं तां व्यक्तं जानाति मैथिलीम्। लतापल्लवपुष्पाढ्यो भाति ह्येष वनस्पतिः॥ भ्रमरैरूपगीतश्च यथा द्रुमवरो ह्यसि। एष व्यक्तं विजानाति तिलकस्तिलकप्रियाम्॥
en: This Kakubha know for certain about Maithilī having thighs like to Kakubha. You stands beautifully that Vanaspati being enveloped with creepers flowers and leaves and filled with the hum of Bhramaras. Surely dose this Tilaka know about her who was fond of her.

sa_verse: अशोक शोकापनुद शोकोपहतचेतनम्। त्वन्नामानं कुरु क्षिप्रं प्रियासंदर्शनेन माम्॥
en: O Asoka, who dose remove sorrows, do you make good your name by making me, who am exercised with grief, see instantly my beloved (spouse).

sa_verse: यदि ताल त्वया दृष्टा पक्वतालोपमस्तनी। कथयस्व वरारोहां कारुण्यं यदि ते मयि ॥
en: O Tāla, if you have any pity on me do you tell me whether you have beheld that fair damsel having breast resembling ripe Tāla fruits.

sa_verse: यदि दृष्ट्वा त्वया जम्बो जाम्बूनदसमप्रभा। प्रियां यदि विजानासि निःशङ्कं कथयस्व मे॥
en: Do you tell me without fear, O Jāmbu, if you have seen my dear one resembling in hue the river Jāmbu.

sa_verse: अहो त्वं कर्णिकाराद्य पुष्पितः शोभते भृशम्। कर्णिकारप्रियां साध्वी शंस दृष्टा यदि प्रिया॥
en: O Karnikar, you appear very beautiful with this blossoming flowers, tell me if you have seen my dear devoted wife who was fond of you.

sa_verse: चूतनीपमहासालान् पनसान् कुररांस्तथा। दाडिमानपि तान् गत्वा दृष्ट्वा रामो महायशाः॥ बकुलानथ पुन्नागांश्चन्दनान् केतकांस्तथा। पृच्छन् रामो वने भ्रान्त उन्मत्त इव लक्ष्यते॥
en: Thus the highly famous Rāma asking about Sītā, nearing the various trees such as mangoes, Nipa, Mahaśālā, Panasa, Kurava, Pomegranate, Bakula, Punnaga, Sandal and Ketak began to traverse the forest like a maniac.

sa_verse: अथवा मृगशावाक्षी मृग जानासि मैथिलीम्। मृगविप्रेक्षणी कान्ता मृगीभिः सहिता भवेत्॥
en: Again addressed he the diverse animals-O dear, know you for certain about Janakī having the eyes of a doe; is she engaged in play with the does?

sa_verse: गज सा गजनासोरूय॑दि दृष्ट्वा त्वया भवेत् तां मन्ये विदितां तुभ्यमाख्याहि वरवारण॥
en: O elephant, methinks you do know about the daughter of Janaka having thighs resembling your trunk; pray tell me if you have beheld her.

sa_verse: शार्दूल यदि सा दृष्ट्वा प्रिया चन्द्रनिभानना। मैथिली मम विस्रब्धः कथयस्व न ते भयम्॥
en: O tiger, fearlessly do you relate to me if you have seen my beloved Maithilī, having a countenance resembling Moon.

sa_verse: किं धावसि प्रिये नूनं दृष्टासि कमलेक्षणे। वृक्षराच्छाद्य चात्मानं किं मां न प्रतिभाषसे॥
en: O dear! O you having eyes like to lotuses! why do you fly away? Surely have I seen you. Why do you not address me hiding yourself behind the tree?

sa_verse: तिष्ठ तिष्ठ वरारोहे न तेऽस्ति करुणा मयि। नात्यर्थं हास्यशीलासि किमर्थं मामुपेक्षसे॥
en: Wait, wait, O you fair damsel, you have no compassion for me! Never had you mock me before in this way! Why do you neglect me now?

sa_verse: पीतकौशेयकेनासि सूचिता वरवर्णिनि। धावन्त्यपि मया दृष्ट्वा तिष्ठ यद्यस्ति सौहृदम्॥
en: O exquisitely fair damsel, truly have I found you out from this your yellow silken cloth. I have seen you flying away. Stand if you have any love for me.

sa_verse: नैव सा नूनमथवा हिंसिता चारुहासिनी। कृच्छं प्राप्तं हि मां नूनं यथोपेक्षितुमर्हति॥
en: Or, O you having a sweet smile, you are not she; truly you have been killed or else you would not have neglected me at this time of dire affliction.

sa_verse: व्यक्तं सा भक्षिता बाला राक्षसैः पिशिताशनैः। विभज्याङ्गानि सर्वाणि मया विरहिता प्रिया॥
en: True it is that she has been devoured in my absence by the Rākşasas living on flesh having torn into pieces her limbs.

sa_verse: नूनं तच्छुभदन्तोष्ठं सुनासं शुभकुण्डलम्। पूर्णचन्द्रनिभं ग्रस्तं मुखं निष्प्रभतां गतम्॥
en: Truly has her face, resembling the full-moon, having beautiful teeth a fine nose and white Kundalas, become of pale countenance being brought under the possession of the Rākșasas.

sa_verse: सा हि चन्दनकवर्णाभा ग्रीवा ग्रैवेयकोचिता। कोमला विलपन्त्यास्तु कान्ताया भक्षिता शुभा॥
en: Her neck had the hue of sandal and was adorned with necklace—that beautiful tender neck was eaten up by the Rākşasas, my beloved wife wailing.

sa_verse: नूनं विक्षिप्यमाणौ तौ बाहू पल्लवकोमलौ। भक्षितौ वेपमानाग्रौ सहस्ताभरणाङ्गदौ॥
en: Her arms were tender like leaves and adorned with various ornaments; truly have the Rākşasas eaten them up, shaken as they were, by throwing them here and there.

sa_verse: मया विरहिता बाला रक्षसां भक्षणाय वै। सार्थेनेव परित्यक्ता भक्षिता बहुबान्धवा ॥
en: Alas! did I leave her alone only to be devoured by the Rākşasas? And she has been eaten up like one weak and helpless albeit she has many friends.

sa_verse: हा लक्ष्मण महाबाहो पश्यसे त्वं प्रियां क्वचित्। हा प्रिये व गता भद्रे हा सीतेति पुनः पुनः॥ इत्येवं विलपन् रामः परिधावन् वनाद् वनम्। क्वचिदुद्भ्रमते वेगात् क्वचिद् विभ्रमते बलात्॥
en: O Lakşmaņa, O you of mighty-arms, have you seen where my dear wife is? O dear! O Sītā! where have you gone? bewailing again and again in this strain Rāma began to range the forest. Sometimes leaping, sometimes walking in an uncertain direction, again and again he looked like one void of sense.

sa_verse: क्वचिन्मत्त इवाभाति कान्तान्वेषणतत्परः। स वनानि नदीः शैलान् गिरिप्रस्रवणानि च। काननानि च वेगेन भ्रमत्यपरिसंस्थितः॥
en: And again intent on searching Sītā he furiously engaged in traversing the rivers, mountains, fountains and the woods. he could not wait patiently anywhere.

sa_verse: तदा स गत्वा विपुलं महद् वनं परीत्य सर्वं त्वथ मैथिली प्रति। अनिष्ठिताशः स चकार मार्गणे पुनः प्रियायाः परमं परिश्रमम्॥
en: Entering a vast forest he searched every nook and corner for Maithilī; his desire was not satisfied and he again engaged with great labour in the finding out of his dear spouse.

sa_verse: दृष्ट्वाऽऽश्रमपदं शून्यं रामो दशरथात्मजः। रहितां पर्णशालां च प्रविद्धान्यासनानि च॥ अदृष्ट्वा तत्र वेदेही संनिरीक्ष्य च सर्वशः। उवाच रामः प्राक्रुश्य प्रगृह्य रुचिरौ भुजौ॥
en: Beholding the hermitage and cottage desolate and the seats strewn here and there, Rāma the son of Dasaratha looked around. And finding Sītā nowhere he raised up his beautiful arm and broke out into lamentations.

sa_verse: क्व नु लक्ष्मण वैदेही कं वा देशमितो गता। केनाहृता वा सौमित्रे भक्षिता केन वा प्रिया॥
en: O Laksmana where is Sītā? Where has she gone hence? O Saumitri, who has carried away my dear one or who has devoured her?

sa_verse: वृक्षणावार्य यदि मां सीते हसितुमिच्छसि। अलं ते हसितेनाद्य मां भजस्व सुदुःखितम्॥
en: O Sītā, if wish you to mock me, hiding yourself behind the tree, enough-~-enough it is-console me who am exercised with grief.

sa_verse: यैः परिक्रीडसे सीते विश्वस्तैमूंगपोतकैः। एते हीनास्त्वया सौम्ये ध्यायन्त्यस्राविलेक्षणाः॥
en: O pleasant Sītā, without you these faithful little deer have engaged in meditation being bathed in tears.

sa_verse: कामवृत्तमनार्यं वा मृषावादिनमेव च॥ धिक् त्वामिति परे लोके व्यक्तं वक्ष्यति मे पिता।
en: O shame on you!' For certain shall my father address me with these words relating to my passionate, false and base conduct.

sa_verse: विवशं शोकसंतप्तं दीनं भग्नमनोरथम्॥ मामिहोत्सृज्य करुणं कीर्तिनरमिवानृजुम्। क्व गच्छसि वरारोहे मा मोत्सृज सुमध्यमे॥
en: All my desires have now been baffled and I have lost all control over myself and have been exercised with grief. O fine damsel, O you of slender waist, where do you repair leaving me behind like to fame renouncing a person of vicious nature?

sa_verse: त्वया विरहितश्चाहं त्यक्ष्ये जीवितमात्मनः। इतीव विलपन् रामः सीतादर्शनलालसः॥ न ददर्श सुदुःखार्तो राघवो जनकात्मजाम्।
en: Without you I shall renounce my own life. Being desirous of seeing Sītā, Rāma afflicted with grief began to bewail in this strain, but did not behold the daughter of Janaka.

sa_verse: अनासादयमानं तं सीतां शोकपरायणम्॥ पङ्कमासाद्य विपुलं सीदन्तमिव कुञ्जरम्। लक्ष्मणो राममत्यर्थमुवाच हितकाम्यया॥
en: Being sunk in grief on Sītā's account he became worn out like to an elephant fallen in mud.' Thereat for his well being, Laksmana spoke to him.

sa_verse: मा विषादं महाबुद्धे कुरु यत् मया सह। इदं गिरिवरं वीर बहुकन्दरशोभितम्॥ प्रियकाननसंचारा वनोन्मत्ता च मैथिली। सा वनं वा प्रविष्टा स्यान्नलिनी वा सुपुष्पिताम्॥ सरितं वापि सम्प्राप्ता मीनवजुलसेविताम्। वित्रासयितुकामा वा लीना स्यात्कानने क्वचित् ॥ जिज्ञासमाना वैदेही त्वां मां च पुरुषर्षभ। तस्या ह्यन्वेषणे श्रीमान् क्षिप्रमेव यतावहे ॥ वनं सर्वं विचिनुवो यत्र सा जनकात्मजा।
en: O you of mighty intellect-do not grieve. Do you put forth your endeavours along with me. There is that high hill, O hero, containing many a cave. Maithilī who is fond of ranging the forest and ever delight in beholding the flowery woods, must have entered therein or have gone to the watering-place blooming with flowers and lot''ses. She Las gone to the river abounding in fish and Vañjulas or has hidden herself somewhere in the forest to frighten us and to know, O best of men, how we can search her out. O you of great beauty, let us soon engage in quest of her.

sa_verse: मन्यसे यदि काकुत्स्थ मा स्म शोके मनः कृथाः।।१८ एवमुक्तः स सौहार्दाल्लक्ष्मणेन समाहितः। सह सौमित्रिणा रामो विचेतुमुपचक्रमे॥
en: O Kākutstha, if think you that she is somewhere in this forest, we shall leave no quarter untried. Do you not grieve. After Laks maņa had spoken thus out of fraternal affection, Rāma, with a composed heart, set out along with him in quest of Sītā.

sa_verse: तौ वनानि गिरीश्चैव सरितश्च सरांसि च। निखिलेन विचिन्वन्तौ सीतां दशरथात्मजौ॥ तस्य शैलस्य सानूनि शिलाश्च शिखराणि च। निखिलेन विचिन्वन्तौ नैव तामभिजग्मतुः॥ विचित्य सर्वतः शैलं रामो लक्ष्मणब्रवीत्। . नेह पश्यामि सौमित्रे वैदेही पर्वते शुभाम्।।२२।
en: And searching every nook and corner of the mountains, rivers, ponds, table-lands, hills and summits they found Sītā nowhere. And searching thus all the mountains Rāma spoke to Lakşmaņa saying Behold not I the auspicious Vaidehī on this mountain, O Saumitrī.

sa_verse: ततो दुःखाभिसंतप्तो लक्ष्मणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। विचरन् दण्डकारण्यं भ्रातरं दीप्ततेजसम्॥
en: Ranging the entire forest of Dandaka, Laks mana, sore distressed, spoke to his brother of flaming energy.

sa_verse: प्राप्स्यसे त्वं महाप्राज्ञ मैथिली जनकात्मजाम्। यथा विष्णुर्महाबाहुर्बलिं बद्ध्वा महीमिमाम्॥
en: Surely shall you come by Maithilī the daughter of Janaka like to the mighty armed Viş ņu obtaining this earth after having bound Vāli.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु वीरेण लक्ष्मणेन स राघवः। उवाच दीनया वाचा दुःखाभिहतचेतनः॥
en: Being thus addressed by the heroic Lakşmaņa, Rāghava, greatly afflicted with sorrow, replied in piteous accents .

sa_verse: वनं सुविचितं सर्वं पद्मिन्यः फुल्लपङ्कजाः। गिरिश्चायं महाप्राज्ञ बहुकन्दरनिर्झरः। नहि पश्यामि वेदेही प्राणेभ्योऽपि गरीयसीम्॥
en: O you of mighty intellect I have searched every nook of this forest, this pool abounding in blown lotuses, and this mountain containing many a cave and fountain; but nowhere have I seen Vaidehi dearer than my life.

sa_verse: एवं स विलपन् रामः सीताहरणकर्षितः। दीनः शोकसमाविष्टो मुहूर्तं विह्वलोऽभवत्॥
en: Thus bewailing Rāma, racked with sorrow consequent on Sītā being carried away, became poorly and afflicted with grief and swooned away for sometime.

sa_verse: स विह्वलितसर्वाङ्गो गतबुद्धिर्विचेतनः। विषसादातुरो दीनो निःश्वस्याशीतमायतम्॥
en: He lost his sense and his whole frame was worked with grief. Being greatly anxious and breathless he sighed hot and fast and began to lament.

sa_verse: बहुशः स तु निःश्वस्य रामो राजीवलोचनः। हा प्रियेति विचुक्रोश बहुशो वाष्पगद्गदः॥
en: And sobbing again and again the lotus-eyed Rāma bewailed with his voice choked with the vapour of grief, exclaiming “Ah Sītā!”

sa_verse: तं सान्त्वयामास ततो लक्ष्मणः प्रियवान्धवम्। बहुप्रकारं शोकार्तः प्रश्रितः प्रश्रिताञ्जलिः॥
en: Thereat his dear brother Lakşmaņa, aggrieved, consoled him with joined hands.

sa_verse: अनादृत्य तु तद् वाक्यं लक्ष्मणोष्ठपुटच्युतम्। अपश्यंस्तां प्रियां सीतां प्राक्रोशत् स पुनः पुनः॥
en: But passing by the words dropping from Laks mana's lips Rāma again and again bewailed not beholding his dear Sītā.

sa_verse: सीतामपश्यन् धर्मात्मा शोकोपहतचेतनः। विललाप महाबाहु रामः कमललोचनः॥
en: Not beholding Sītā the virtuous-souled Rāma, of mighty arms, having eyes resembling lotuses, beside himself with grief, lamented in many a way).

sa_verse: पश्यन्निव च तां सीतामपश्यन्मन्मन्थार्दितः। उवाच राघवो वाक्यं विलापाश्रयदुर्वचम्॥
en: Pierced with the shafts of Manmatha, Rāghava, as if beholding Sītā though he actually did not see her, uttered the following piteous accents

sa_verse: त्वमशोकस्य शाखाभिः पुष्पप्रियतरा प्रिये। आवृणोषि शरीरं ते मम शोकविवर्धनी॥
en: O my dear, you delights greatly in flowers. Covering your own Person with Asoka twigs you are increasing my grief.

sa_verse: कदलीकाण्डसदृशौ कदल्या संवृतावुभौ । ऊरू पश्यामि ते देवि नासि शक्ता निगुहितुम्॥
en: Your thighs are like to the trunks of plantain trees and you have hidden your self behind the plantain grove. But I perceive you, O fair one, you are incapable of keeping yourself hidden.

sa_verse: कर्णिकारवनं भद्रे हसन्ती देवि सेवसे। अलं ते परिहासेन मम बाधावहेन वै॥
en: O auspicious one, you have entered smiling the Karņikara grove. Now more with your pastime, O dear one, leading to my death.

sa_verse: विशेषेणाश्रमस्थाने हासोऽयं न प्रशस्यते। अवगच्छामि ते शीलं परिहासप्रियं प्रिये॥ आगच्छ त्वं विशालाक्षि शून्योऽयमुटजस्तव।
en: Moreover it is not proper to sport in this way in a herinitage. I know it full well, O my dear, that you are by nature fond of pastimes. But O you of expansive eyes, this cottage lie desolate, do you came here.

sa_verse: सुव्यक्तं राक्षसैः सीता भक्षिता वा हृतापि वा।७।। न हि सा विलपन्तं मामुपसम्पैति लक्ष्मण। एतानि मृगयूथानि साश्रुनेत्राणि लक्ष्मण ॥ शंसन्तीव हि मे देवीं भक्षितां रजनीचरैः।
en: Evident it is that Sītā has either been devoured by the Rākşasas or carried away by them, and therefore she dose not approach me, O Laks mana, who am thus bewailing. These deer, O Lakşmaņa, with tearful eyes have been as if declaring that Sītā has been devoured by the night-rangers.

sa_verse: हा ममार्ये क्व यातासि हा साध्वि वरवर्णिनि॥ हा सकामाद्य कैकेयी देवि मेऽद्य भविष्यतु ।
en: O chaste one, O you of exquisitely fine hue, O worshipful madam, whither have you repaired? O Sītă, truly has the desire of Kaikeyi been fulfilled to-day.

sa_verse: सीतया सह निर्यातो विना सीतामुपागतः॥ कथं नाम प्रवेक्ष्यामि शून्यमन्तःपुरं मम।
en: I came out with Sītā and shall return home without her. How shall I enter again that inner apartment void of Sitā?

sa_verse: निर्वीर्य इति लोको मां निर्दयश्चेति वक्ष्यति ॥ कातरत्वं प्रकाशं हि सीतापनयने स मे।
en: Surely shall the people blame me as being cruel and destitute of energy. That I have no prowess has already been manifested in the destruction of Sītā.

sa_verse: निवृत्तवनवासश्च जनकं मिथिलाधिपम्॥ कुशलं परिपृच्छन्तं कथं शक्ष्ये निरीक्षितुम्।
en: When the king Janaka shall come to me after my return home from exile, to ask me of my welfare how shall I meet him?

sa_verse: विदेहराजो नूनं मां दृष्ट्वा विरहितं तया॥ सुताविनाशसंतप्तो मोहस्य वशमेष्यति।
en: And surely shall he be overwhelmed with grief on his daughter's account when he shall find me without Sītā.

sa_verse: अथवा न गमिष्यामि पुरीं भरतपालिताम्॥ स्वर्गोऽपि हि तया हीनः शून्य एव मतो मम।
en: Blessed is my father since he is in heaven now. I shall not repair to that city protected by Bharata. Without her even the heaven itself appears to me as desolate.

sa_verse: तन्मामुत्सृज्य हि वने गच्छायोध्यापुरीं शुभाम्॥ न त्वहं तां विना सीतां जीवेयं हि कथंचन।
en: Do you therefore repair to the city of Ayodhyā Icaving me in this forest. By no means shall I breathe without her.

sa_verse: गाढमाश्लिष्य भरतो वाच्यो मद्वचनात् त्वया॥ अनुज्ञातोऽसि रामेण पालयेति वसुंधराम्।
en: Embracing him warmly do you tell Bharata, as instructed by me-Rāma has given you permission to administer this kingdom.

sa_verse: अम्बा च मम कैकेयी सुमित्रा च त्वया विभो॥ कौसल्या च यथान्यायमभिवाद्या ममाज्ञया। रक्षणीया प्रयत्नेन भवता सूक्तचारिणा॥
en: As ordered by me do you with proper respect salute "ll my muchers Kausalyā, Kaikeyi and Sumitrā and protect them all with great care and respectful compliments.

sa_verse: सीतायाश्च विनाशोऽयं मम चामित्रसूदन । विस्तरेण जनन्या मे विनिवेद्यस्त्वया भवेत्॥
en: O destroyer of foes, do you relate at length to my mnother the story of Sītā's destruction.

sa_verse: इति विलपति राघवे तु दीने वनमुपगम्य तया विना सुकेश्या। भयविकलमुखस्तु लक्ष्मणोऽपि व्यथितमना भृशमातुरो बभूव ॥
en: Rāma bewailing thus, being overwhelmed with grief on account of his separation from Sītā having a head of fine hair, Laksmana became of pale countenance and was greatly distressed at heart.

sa_verse: स राजपुत्रः प्रियया विहीनः शोकेन मोहेन च पीड्यमानः। विषादयन् भ्रातरमार्तरूपो भूयो विषादं प्रविवेश तीव्रम्॥
en: That son of a king, stricken as he was with grief consequent upon separation from his dear one, was again overwhelmed with a terrible grief after causing sorrow to his brother,

sa_verse: स लक्ष्मणं शोकवशाभिपन्न शोके निमग्नो विपुले तु रामः। मुष्णं विनिःश्वस्य रुदन् सशोकम्॥
en: Sunk in the abyss of grief, Rāma, sighing hot and weeping piteously, spoke to Laksmana who was equally aggrieved, words worthy of being said on that occasion.

sa_verse: न मद्विधो दुष्कृतकर्मकारी मन्ये द्वितीयोऽस्ति वसुंधरायाम्। शोकानुशोको हि परम्पराया मामेति भिन्दन् हृदयं मनश्च ॥
en: Methinks there is none other on this earth like me, the perpetrator of vicious crimes. My heart or soul is not riven though crushed again and again without respite with a multitude of doleful events.

sa_verse: राज्यप्रणाशः स्वजनैर्वियोगः पितुर्विनाशो जननीवियोगः। मापूरयन्ति प्रविचिन्तितानि॥
en: Coming within the compass remembrance, the loss of my kingdom, the death of my father, the separation of my mother and other kinsmen culminates my grief.

sa_verse: सर्वं तु दुःखं मम लक्ष्मणेदं शान्तं शरीरे वनमेत्य क्लेशम्। सीतावियोगात् पुनरप्युदीर्णं काष्ठरिवाग्निः सहसोपदीप्तः॥
en: Repairing to woods, O Laksmana, in Sita's company my grief was assuaged, nay I did not suffer physical affliction even. Without Sītā these sorrows have grown anew like to fire flaming again by means of fuel.

sa_verse: सा नूनमार्या मम राक्षसेन ह्यभ्याहता खं समुपेत्य भीरुः। अप्यस्वरं सुस्वरविप्रलापा भयेन विक्रन्दितवत्यभीक्ष्णम्॥
en: Truly my wife, timid as she is, has been carried away by a Rākşasa by the ethereal track. Alas! doubtless it is, that one of pleasant accents, wept piteously out of fear many a time and oft.

sa_verse: तौ लोहितस्य प्रियदर्शनस्य सदोचितावुत्तमचन्दनस्य। वृत्तौ स्तनौ शोणितपङ्कदिग्धौ नूनं प्रियाया मम नाभिपातः॥
en: For certain my dear wife's breast round and sprinkled as it was with red sandal paste, was bathed in blood (while devoured by the Rākş asas) but there is no death for me.

sa_verse: तच्छ्लक्ष्णसुव्यक्तमृदुप्रलापं तस्या मुखं कुञ्चितकेशभारम्। रक्षोवशं नूनमुपागताया न भ्राजते राहुमुखे यथेन्दुः॥
en: That countenance the beauty of which was enhanced by a head of curly hair and which used to emit forth tender, soft and clear accents, has become pale, being taken possession of by the Rākşasas like to the Moon almost devoured by Rahu.

sa_verse: तां हारपाशस्य सदोचितान्तां ग्रीवां प्रियाया मम सुव्रतायाः। रक्षांसि नूनं परिपीतवन्ति शून्ये हि भित्त्वा रुधिराशनानि॥
en: Surely have the Räkşasas subsisting on gore drunk her blood in the sky tearing off the neck of my dear one ever devoted to pious observances.

sa_verse: मया विहीना विजने वने सा रक्षोभिरावृत्य विकृष्यमाणा। नूनं विनादं कुररीव दीना सा मुक्तवत्यायतकान्तनेत्रा ॥
en: Surely did that one of beautifully expansive eyes cry aloud poorly like to a hind when she was drawn hither and thither by the Rākşasas encircling her in the forest in my absence.

sa_verse: अस्मिन् मया सार्धमुदारशीला शिलातले पूर्वमुपोपविष्टा। कान्तस्मिता लक्ष्मण जातहासा त्वामाह सीता बहुवाक्यजातम्॥
en: O Lakşmana, sitting at the foot of this hill with me that large-hearted, pious Sītā, of smiling countenance, used to address you on many a topic.

sa_verse: गोदावरीयं सरितां वरिष्ठा प्रिया प्रियाया मम नित्यकालम्। अप्यत्र गच्छेदिति चिन्तयामि नैकाकिनी याति हि सा कदाचित् ॥
en: This is Godāvarī, the best of rivers, my dear wife took delight in her-has she gone there?—But she never goes there alone.

sa_verse: पद्मानना पद्मपलाशनेत्रा पद्मानि वाऽऽनेतुमभिप्रयाता। तदप्ययुक्तं नहि सा कदाचिन् मया विना गच्छति पङ्कजानि॥
en: Or has Janaki having eyes resembling lotuspetals has gone to bring lotuses? But how is that possible, she never goes without me to bring lotuses.

sa_verse: कामं त्विदं पुष्पितवृक्षषण्डं नानाविधैः पक्षिगणैरुपेतम्। मेकाकिनी सातिबिभेति भीरुः॥
en: Has she entered at her pleasure this forest filled with many flowery trees and diverse birds? But that is not possible too-she is timid and fear much to enter alone in this forest.

sa_verse: आदित्य भो लोककृताकृतज्ञ लोकस्य सत्यानृतकर्मसाक्षिन्। मम प्रिया सा क्व गता हृता वा शंसस्व मे शोकहतस्य सर्वम्॥
en: O.Aditya, know you the pious and vicious actions of men; bear you testimony to the truth and untruth of their actions-do you tell me, pray, who am stricken with grief, whither has my dear on repaired, or whether has she been killed?

sa_verse: लोकेषु सर्वेषु न नास्ति किंचिद् यत् तेन नित्यं विदितं भवेत् तत्। शंसस्व वायो कुलपालिनी तां मृता हृता वा पथि वर्तते वा॥
en: O Air, there is nothing on earth which is not within the compass of your vision, do you relate to me whether Sītā preserving the fame of my ancestry, has been killed or carried away or if she wait on the way.

sa_verse: इतीव तं शोकविधेयदेह रामं विसंज्ञं विलपन्तमेव। उवाच सौमित्रिरदीनसत्त्वो न्याय्ये स्थितः कालयुतं च वाक्यम्॥
en: After Rāma had bewailed thus being beside himself with grief, Saumitrī, ever treading the right path and not of poorly mind spoke words worthy of being said on that occasion.

sa_verse: शोकं विसृज्याद्य धृतिं भजस्व सोत्साहता चास्तु विमार्गणेऽस्याः। उत्साहवन्तो हि नरा न लोके सीदन्ति कर्मस्वतिदुष्करेषु॥
en: Do you take heart renouncing your grief and engage with energy of Sita. Persons of high energy are never exhausted on the earth in quest even in the face of arduous works.

sa_verse: इतीव सौमित्रिमुदापौरुषं ब्रुवन्तमार्तं रघुवंशसत्तमः। न चिन्तयामास धृतिं विमुक्तवान् पुनश्च दुःखं महदभ्युपागमत्॥
en: The highly powerful Lakşmaņa having spoken thus being afflicted with grief, Rāma, the best of Raghu's descendants, did not consider that worth pondering over. Renouncing patience he again indulged in excessive grief.

sa_verse: स दीनो दीनया वाचा लक्ष्मणं वाक्यमब्रवीत्। शीघ्रं लक्ष्मण जानीहि गत्वा गोदावरी नदीम्॥ अपि गोदावरी सीता पद्मान्यानयितुं गता।
en: Rāma stricken with grief spoke to Lakşmaņa the following piteous words saying, O Laks mana, do you speedily repair to the river Godaverſ and learn if Sītā has gone there to fetch lotuses.

sa_verse: एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण लक्ष्मणः पुनरेव हि॥ नदी गोदावरी रम्यां जगाम लघुविक्रमः।
en: Being thus addressed by Rāma, the quickpaced Lakşmaņa went to the pleasant stream Godaveri.

sa_verse: तां लक्ष्मणस्तीर्थवती विचित्वा राममब्रवीत्॥ नैनां पश्यामि तीर्थेषु क्रोशतो न शृणोति मे।
en: Reconnoitring full well the river containing many a watering-place Lakşmaņa spoke to Rāma saying, Searched have I all the watering-places but have found her nowhere-anon I cried aloud but she did not hear.

sa_verse: कं नु सा देशमापन्ना वैदेही क्लेशनाशिनी॥ नहि तं वेद्मि वै राम यत्र सा तनुमध्यमा।
en: I cannot trace whither has Vaidehi of slender waist repaired ever assuaging our mental affliction.

sa_verse: लक्ष्मणस्य वचः श्रुत्वा दीनः संतापमोहितः॥ रामः समभिचक्राम स्वयं गोदावरी नदीम्।
en: Hearing Laksmana's words, Rāma, aggrieved and overwhelmed with sorrow repaired in person to the river Godāverī.

sa_verse: स तामुपस्थितो रामः क्व सीतेत्येवमब्रवीत्॥ भूतानि राक्षसेन्द्रेण वधाहेण हृतामपि। न तां शशंशू रामाय तथा गोदावरी नदी।७।।
en: Arriving there cried he where is Şītā? Neither did the world of creatures nor the river Godāvarī apprise Rāma of Sītā's being carried away by the Lord of Rākşasas worthy of being slain.

sa_verse: ततः प्रचोदिता भूतैः शंस चास्मै प्रियामिति। न च सा ह्यवदत् सीतां पृष्टा रामेण शोचता॥ रावणस्य च तद्रूपं कर्मापि च दुरात्मनः। ध्यात्वा भयात् तु वैदेही सा नदी न शशंस ह॥
en: Thinking of the terrible figure and monstrous action of that vicious-souled Råvaņa, that river did not dare relate to him anything about Sītā, albeit appointed by the creatures to relate the story concerning her and accosted by Răma in piteous accents.

sa_verse: निराशस्तु तया नद्या सीताया दर्शने कृतः। उवाच रामः सौमित्रिं सीतादर्शनकर्शितः॥
en: Being thus disappointed by the river in beholding Sītā, Răma racked with her separation spoke to Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: एषा गोदावरी सौम्य किंचिन्न प्रतिभाषते। किं तु लक्ष्मण वक्ष्यामि समेत्य जनकं वचः॥ मातरं चैव वैदेह्या विना तामहमप्रियम्।
