en: O you of auspicious looks, this river Godāvarī dose give no reply. But O Laksmana, returning without her what shall I say to Janaka and Vaidehi's mother?

sa_verse: या मे राज्यविहीनस्य वने वन्येन जीवितः॥ सर्वं व्यपानयच्छोकं वैदेही क्व नु सा गता। ज्ञातिवर्गविहीनस्य वैदेहीमप्यपश्यतः॥
en: Where has that Vaidehi gone who used to assuage my grief who had been deprived of kingdom and living in this forest on wild fruits and vegetables? Nights shall appear too long to me, keeping late liquors being deprived of my kinsmen and relatives and not beholding Vaidehi.

sa_verse: मन्ये दीर्घा भविष्यन्ति रात्रयो मम जाग्रतः। मन्दाकिनी जनस्थानमिमं प्रस्रवणं गिरिम्॥ सर्वाण्यनुचरिष्यामि यदि सीता हि लभ्यते।
en: I can range this Mandakini, this Janasthāna and this Pasravana hill if I can find Sītā there.

sa_verse: एते महामृगा वीर मामीक्षन्ते पुनः पुनः॥ वक्तुकामा इह हि मे इङ्गितान्युपलक्षये।
en: Behold, O hero, the high deer have been casting their looks again and again at men; methinks from their gestures, they intend speaking something to me.

sa_verse: तांस्तु दृष्ट्वा नरव्याघ्रो राघवः प्रत्युवाच ह॥ क्व सीतेति निरीक्षन् वै बाष्पसंरुद्धया गिरा। एवमुक्ता नरेन्द्रेण ते मृगाः सहसोत्थिताः॥ दक्षिणाभिमुखाः सर्वे दर्शयन्तो नभः स्थलम्।
en: Beholding them, Rāghava, the best of men, looked at them and said in accents choked with vapour-Where is Sītā? Being thus addressed by that Lord of men the deer rose up all on a sudden and looked up to the sky facing the south and proceeded to the direction by which Maithili had been carried away.

sa_verse: मैथिली ह्रियमाणा सा दिशं यामभ्यपद्यत ॥ तेन मार्गेण गच्छन्तो निरीक्षन्ते नराधिपम्। येन मार्गं च भूमिं च निरीक्षन्ते स्म ते मृगाः॥ पुनर्नदन्तो गच्छन्ति लक्ष्मणेनोपलक्षिताः। तेषां वचनसर्वस्वं लक्षयामास चेङ्गितम्॥ उवाच लक्ष्मणो धीमाज्येष्ठं भ्रातरमार्तवत्। क्व सीतेति त्वया पृष्टा यदिमे सहसोत्थिताः॥ दर्शयन्ति क्षितिं चैव दक्षिणां च दिशं मृगाः। साधु गच्छावहे देव दिशमेतां च नैर्ऋतीम्॥ यदि तस्यागमः कश्चिदार्या वा साऽथ लक्ष्यते।
en: Moving by that way these deer eyed the Lord of men and again and again fixed their looks upon that way and earth and passed along emitting cries which was marked by Lakṣmaṇa. He marked with attention their movements and cries and spoke to his elder brother like one aggrieved saying-Being accosted by you with—'Where is Sītā?' these deer have stood up all on a sudden and have been pointing to the south and earth-let us therefore proceed in this direction—it may be that we shall either meet with that worshipful madam or find some mementoes concerning her.

sa_verse: बाढमित्येव काकुत्स्थः प्रस्थितो दक्षिणां दिशम्॥ लक्ष्मणानुगतः श्रीमान् वीक्षमाणो वसुंधराम्।
en: Thereat Kākutstha, gifted with supreme beauty, proceeded towards the south being followed by Laksmana and casting his look upon the earth.

sa_verse: एवं सम्भाषमाणौ तावन्योन्यं भ्रातरावुभौ॥ वसुंधरायां पतितपुष्पमार्गमपश्यताम्।
en: While proceeding thus, conversing with each other the two brothers beheld some flowers scattered on the high-way.

sa_verse: पुष्पवृष्टिं निपतितां दृष्ट्वा रामो महीतले॥ उवाच लक्ष्मणं वीरो दुःखितो दुःखितं वचः।
en: Beholding a collection of flowers scattered on earth, Rāma, exceedingly sorry, spoke to Laks mana in piteous accents.

sa_verse: अभिजानामि पुष्पाणि तानीमानीह लक्ष्मण ॥ अपिनद्धानि वैदेह्या मया दत्तानि कानने।
en: O Lakşmaņa, I have come to know that these are the flowers of the forest I gave Vaidehī; with these she decorated her hair.

sa_verse: मन्ये सूर्यश्च वायुश्च मेदिनी च यशस्विनी॥ अभिरक्षन्ति पुष्पाणि प्रकुर्वन्तो मम प्रियम्।
en: Me-thinks the sun, the air and the famed earth have preserved them for my well-being.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा महाबाहुर्लक्ष्मणं पुरुषर्भम्॥ उवाच रामो धर्मात्मा गिरिं प्रस्रवणाकुलम्।
en: Having spoken these words to Lakşmaņa, the best of men, the virtuous-souled Rāma, of mighty arms, addressed the mountain in front of him containing many fountains.

sa_verse: कच्चित् क्षितिभृतां नाथ दृष्टा सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरी॥ रामा रम्ये वनोद्देशे मया विरहिता त्वया।
en: O you the best of mountains, have you beheld in this picturesque forest-land, that exquisitely fine damsel racked with my separation?

sa_verse: क्रुद्धोऽब्रवीद् गिरिं तत्र सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगं यथा॥ तां हेमवर्णां हेमाङ्गी सीतां दर्शय पर्वत। यावत् सानूनि सर्वाणि न ते विध्वंसयाम्यहम्॥ एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण पर्वतो मैथिली प्रति। दर्शयन्निव तां सीतां नादर्शयत राघवे॥
en: Exceedingly wroth he accosted the mountain like to a lion addressing a little deer, saying Show me my graceful Sītā hued like gold before I crush down your summits. Being thus addressed by Rāma on Maithili's account the mountain did not show him Sītā.

sa_verse: ततो दाशरथी राम उवाच च शिलोच्चयम्। मम बाणाग्निनिर्दग्धो भस्मीभूतो भविष्यसि ॥ असेव्यः सर्वतश्चैव निस्तृणद्रुमपल्लवः।
en: Again addressed him Rāma—You shall by the fire of my arrows, be reduced to ashes-your twigs and leaves shall be totally destroyed and no one shall resort to you.

sa_verse: इमां वा सरितं चाद्य शोषयिष्यामि लक्ष्मण॥ यदि नाख्याति मे सीतामद्य चन्द्रनिभाननाम्।
en: O Lakşmaņa, I shall dry up this river Godāvari if it tell me not about Sītā having a moon-like countenance.

sa_verse: एवं प्ररुषितो रामो दिधक्षन्निव चक्षुषा ॥ ददर्श भूमौ निष्क्रान्तं राक्षसस्य पदं महत्। वस्ताया रामकाङ्गिण्याः प्रधावन्त्या इतस्ततः॥ राक्षसेनानुसृप्ताया वैदेयाश्च पदानि तु।
en: Rāma, exceedingly wroth, cast his looks around as if desiring to burn everything with his eyes and beheld footprints of the Räkşasas on the earth as well as those of Sītā moving wildly hither and thither, terrified and desirous to see Rāma, while pursued by the Rākşasas.

sa_verse: स समीक्ष्य परिक्रान्तं सीताया राक्षसस्य च॥ भग्नं धनुश्च तूणी च विकीर्णं बहुधा रथम्। सम्भ्रान्तहृदयो रामः शशंस भ्रातरं प्रियम्।।
en: Beholding these foot marks, the snapped bow, the quiver and the chariot broken into many pieces, Rāma, terrified spoke to his dear brother.

sa_verse: पश्य लक्ष्मण वैदेह्या कीर्णाः कनकविन्दवः। भूषणानां हि सौमित्रे माल्यानि विविधानि च॥
en: Behold O, Laksmana, the remnants of Vaidehi's golden ornaments, strewn hither and thither, and diverse garlands.

sa_verse: तप्तबिन्दुनिकाशैश्च चित्रैः क्षतजबिन्दुभिः। आवृतं पश्य सौमित्रे सर्वतो धरणीतलम्॥
en: Behold O Saumitrī, the earth covered with drops of blood resembling golden drops.

sa_verse: मन्ये लक्ष्मण वैदेही सक्षसैः कामरूपिभिः। भित्वा भित्वा विभक्ता वा भक्षिता वा भविष्यति॥
en: Methinks, O Laksmana, Vaidehi has been devoured by the Rākşasas assuming shapes at will, having sundered her in pieces.

sa_verse: तस्या निमित्तं सीताया द्वयोर्विवदमानयोः। बभूव युद्धं सौमित्रे घोरं राक्षसयोरिह।॥
en: O Saumitrī, there took place a terrible conflict between the Rākṣasas, fighting with each other on Sita's account.

sa_verse: मुक्तामणिचितं चेदं रमणीयं विभूषितम्। धरण्यां पतितं सौम्य कस्य भग्नं महद् धनुः॥
en: O gentle one, whose is this snapped bow lying on the breast of the earth adorned and crested with pearls and diamonds?

sa_verse: राक्षसानामिदं वत्स सुराणामथवापि वा। तरुणादित्यसंकाशं वैदूर्यगुलिकाचितम्॥ विशीर्णं पतितं भूभौ कवचं कस्य काञ्चनम्। छत्रं शतशलाकं च दिव्यमाल्योपशोभितम्॥ भग्नदण्डमिदं सौम्य भूमौ कस्य निपातितम्। काञ्चनोरश्छदाश्चेमे पिशाचवदनाः खराः॥ भीमरूपा महाकायाः कस्य वा निहता रणे।
en: O brother, this belongs either to the celestials or to the Rākşasas. Whose is this golden armour lying shattered on earth, resembling the newly risen sun, in colour and adorned with sapphire? Whose is this umbrella lying broken on earth, containing a hundred rod and adorned with celestial garlands? In whose conflict have these terrible asses, of large proportions, having faces of demon and with breast plates, been killed?

sa_verse: दीप्तपावकसंकाशो द्युतिमान् समरध्वजः॥ अपविद्धश्च भग्नश्च कस्य साङ्ग्रामिको रथः।
en: Whose is this shattered war-car lying upset on the ground and broken flag resembling in lustre the burning gold?

sa_verse: रथाक्षमात्रा विशिखास्तपनीयविभूषणा॥ कस्येमे निहता वाणाः प्रकीर्णा घोरदर्शनाः।
en: Whose are these terrible arrows feathered in gold, measuring four-hundred fingers, lying without blades on earth?

sa_verse: शरावरौ शरैः पूर्णौ विध्वस्तौ पश्य लक्ष्मण ॥ प्रतोदाभीषुहस्तोऽयं कस्य वा सारथिर्हतः।
en: Behold, O Laksmana, these two quivers have been totally spoiled though filled with arrows. whose charioteer is this who has been killed with reins and lash in hands?

sa_verse: पदवी पुरुषस्यैषा व्यक्तं कस्यापि रक्षसः॥ वैरं शतगुणं पश्य मम तैर्जीवितान्तकम्। सुघोरहृदयैः सौम्य राक्षसैः कामरूपिभिः। ५१ ।।
en: These foot-marks must be some Raksasa's. I made these Rākşasas my fatal enemies, assuming shapes at will and of crooked hearts.

sa_verse: हृता मृता वा वैदेही भक्षिता वा तपस्विनी। न धर्मस्त्रायते सीतां ह्रियमाणा महावने॥
en: Poor Sītā must have been either dead, carried away by them or devoured. Virtue did not save her from being carried away in this mighty forest.

sa_verse: भक्षितायां हि वैदेह्यां हृतायामपि लक्ष्मण। के हि लोके प्रियं कर्तुं शक्तः सौम्य ममेश्वराः॥
en: O Lakşmaņa, while virtue did not protect Janakī being devoured or taken away by stealth, what person else gifted with heavenly power, on this earth shall bring about my well-being?

sa_verse: कर्तारमपि लोकानां शूरं करुणवेदिनम्। अज्ञानादवमन्येरन् सर्वभूतानि लक्ष्मण ॥
en: For this it is that people through ignorance disregard the ever kind Almighty-the lord of creatures and the best of the celestials.

sa_verse: मृदुं लोकहिते युक्तं दान्तं करुणवेदिनम्। निर्वीर्य इति मन्यन्ते नूनं मां त्रिदशेश्वराः।५५।।
en: Truly shall the celestials regard me as one devoid of prowess, who am mild-tempered, kind, ever engaged in the welfare of the humanity, and have controlled all my senses.

sa_verse: मां प्राप्य हि गुणो दोषः संवृत्तः पश्य लक्ष्मण। अद्यैव सर्वभूतानां रक्षसामभवाय च॥ संहत्यैव शशिज्योत्स्नां महान् सूर्य इवोदितः। संहत्यैव गुणान् सर्वान् मम तेजः प्रकाशते॥
en: Observe, O Laksmana, obtaining me as the stay these accomplishments have been turned into so many blemishes. Truly shall my prowess manifest itself to-day overshadowing all my other accomplishments for the destruction of the Rākşasas and all created beings like to the rising of the Sun casting the Moon into shade on the day of dissolution.

sa_verse: नैव यक्षा न गन्धर्वा न पिशाचा न राक्षसाः। किंनरा वा मनुष्या वा सुखं प्राप्स्यन्ति लक्ष्मण॥
en: None shall enjoy felicity, O Lakşmaņa,-Yaksas, Gandharvas, Pisacas, Raksasas, Kinnaras, or human beings.

sa_verse: ममास्त्रबाणसम्पूर्णमाकाशं पश्य लक्ष्मण। असम्पातं करिष्यामि ह्यद्य त्रैलोक्यचारिणाम्॥
en: Today shall the welkin be filled up with my arrows. Motionless shall I make all the animals inhabiting the three worlds.

sa_verse: संनिरुद्धग्रहगणमावारितनिशाकरम्। विप्रणष्टानलमरुद्भास्करद्युतिसंवृतम्॥ विनिर्मथितशैलाग्रं शुष्यमाणजलाशयम्। ध्वस्तद्रुमलतागुल्मं विप्रणाशितसागरम्॥ त्रैलोक्यं तु करिष्यामि संयुक्तं कालकर्मणा। न ते कुशलिनी सीतां प्रदास्यन्ति ममेश्वराः॥ अस्मिन् मुहूर्ते सौमित्रे मम द्रक्ष्यन्ति विक्रमम्।
en: I shall arrest the movement of the planets and overshadow the Moon. Stopping the course of the wind and destroying the rays of the Sun and fire I shall envelope the earth with darkness, crush down the summits of the mountains, dry up the pools, blow up the creepers, demolish the Ocean and eradicate the trees. If the celestials do not give me back my Sītā I shall bring about the dissolution of the three worlds which would else have been wrought by time. O son of Sumitrā, instantly shall the celestials headed by Indra, meet with my prowess, if they do not give back my Sītā, ever advancing my welfare.

sa_verse: नाकाशमुत्पतिष्यन्ति सर्वभूतानि लक्ष्मण ॥ मम चापगुणोन्मुक्तैर्बाणजालैर्निरन्तरम्। मर्दितं मम नाराचैध्वस्तभ्रान्तमृगद्विजम्॥ समाकुलममर्यादं जगत् पश्याद्य लक्ष्मण।
en: None shall be able to range the welkin. Behold O Lakşmaņa, being perpetually crushed down by my arrows shot off my bow, the world shall be disturbed and dislodged and the animals and birds shall be confused and destroyed.

sa_verse: आकर्णपूर्णैरिषुभिर्जीवलोकदुरावरैः॥ करिष्ये मैथिलीहेतोरपिशाचमराक्षसम्।
en: Stretching the bow to my ears I shall make the world, for Sita's sake, void of Pisacas and Raks asas with my arrows incapable of being withstood by created beings.

sa_verse: मम रोषप्रयुक्तानां विशिखानां बलं सुराः॥ द्रक्ष्यन्त्यद्य विमुक्तानाममर्षाद् दूरगामिनाम्।
en: To-day shall the celestials behold the power of my arrows coursing a long distance shot through my ire.

sa_verse: नैव देवा न दैतेया न पिशाचा न राक्षसाः॥ भविष्यन्ति मम क्रोधात् त्रैलोक्ये विप्रणाशिते।
en: Three worlds, destroyed on account of my wrath, celestials, Sanavas, Pisacas or Raks asas,-none shall be saved.

sa_verse: देवदानवयक्षाणां लोका ये रक्षसामपि॥ बहुधा निपतिष्यन्ति बाणौधैः शकलीकृताः।
en: The dwellings of the celestials, Asuras, Yaks as and Rākşasas shall fall down sundered by my arrows into diverse pieces.

sa_verse: निर्मर्यादानिमाल्लोकान् करिष्याम्यद्य सायकैः॥ हतां मृतां वा सौमित्रे न दास्यन्ति ममेश्वराः। तथारूपां हि वैदेही न दास्यन्ति यदि प्रियाम्॥ नाशयामि जगत् सर्वं त्रैलोक्यं सचराचरम्। यावद् दर्शनमस्या वै तापयामि च सायकैः॥
en: I shall dislodge the whole world by my arrows. If the celestials do not give me back my Vaidehī dead or carried away or as she was before, I shall destroy the whole world mobile or immobile and disturb all with my arrows until i see her.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वा क्रोधताम्राक्षः स्फुरमाणोष्ठसम्पुटः। वल्कलाजिनमाबद्ध्य जटाभारमबन्धयत्।७२।।
en: Having spoken thus, Rāma, with his eyes reddened with fire and lips swollen, tying fast his bark and deer-skin, braided his matted locks.

sa_verse: तस्य क्रुद्धस्य रामस्य तथाभूतस्य धीमतः। त्रिपुरं जघ्नुषः पूर्वं रुद्रस्येव बभौ तनुः॥
en: Having done this, being exceedingly wroth he looked like Rudra about to destroy Tripura.

sa_verse: लक्ष्मणादथ चादाय रामो निष्पीड्य कार्मुकम्। शरमादाय संदीप्तं घोरमाशीविषोपमम्॥ संदधे धनुषि श्रीमान् रामः परपरञ्जयः। युगान्ताग्निरिव क्रुद्ध इदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereat taking his bow from Lakşmaņa and holding it fast, the effulgent Rāma, the conqueror of foes, fixed flaming arrows to it like so many serpents and said being exercised with ire like to fire on the eve of dissolution.

sa_verse: यथा जरा यथा मृत्युर्यथा कालो यथा विधिः। नित्यं न प्रतिहन्यन्ते सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मण। तथाहं क्रोधसंयुक्तो न निवार्योऽस्म्यसंशयम् । ७६ ।।
en: O Lakşınaņa none shall be able to withstand me, who am inflamed with rage, as debility consequent on old age, death, time, duty are incapable of being averted from their destined ends by the animals.

sa_verse: पुरेव मे चारुदतीमनिन्दितां दिशन्ति सीतां यदि नाद्य मैथिलीम्। सदेवगन्धर्वमनुष्यपन्नगं जगत् सशैलं परिवर्तयाम्यहम्॥
en: I shall bring about a mighty revolution in the world containing the celestials Gandharvas, human beings, Pannagas and the mountains, if I do not get back, in her pristine beauty, my Sītā, the daughter of the King of Mithilā.

sa_verse: तप्यमानं तदा रामं सीताहरणकर्शितम्। लोकानामभवे युक्तं सांवर्तकमिवानलम्॥ वीक्षमाणं धनुः सज्यं निःश्वसन्तं पुनः पुनः। दग्धुकामं जगत् सर्वं युगान्ते च यथा हरम्॥ अदृष्टपूर्वं संक्रुद्धं दृष्ट्वा रामं स लक्ष्मणः। अब्रवीत् प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं मुखेन परिशुष्यता॥
en: Rāma highly aggrieved on account of Sīta's being carried away, addressing himself to destroy the world like to the fire of dissolution and casting his look, sighing again and again, upon the stringed bow like to Mahadeva desirous of burning down the whole world at the time of dissolution, Laksmana, having his countenance dried up, beholding his rage not seen before, began with folded hands.

sa_verse: पुरा भूत्वा मृदुर्दान्तः सर्वभूतहिते रतः। न क्रोधवशमापन्नः प्रकृति हातुमर्हसि ॥
en: Ere this you had been gently, self-controlled and engaged in the welfare of all beings. It dose not behove you now to renounce your natural temper being influenced by ire.

sa_verse: चन्द्रे लक्ष्मीः प्रभा सूर्ये गतिर्वायौ भुवि क्षमा। एतच्च नियतं नित्यं त्वयि चानुत्तमं यशः॥
en: Ever manifested itself in you, glory Par excellence like to splendour in the Moon, lustre in the Sun, motion in the wind and forgiveness in the Earth.

sa_verse: एकस्य नापराधेन लोकान् हन्तुं त्वमर्हसि। ननु जानामि कस्यायं भग्नः सांग्रामिको रथः॥
en: It become you not to devastate the whole world for the crime of an individual being. Methinks for certain, this shattered car must be the property of an individual person, not of many.

sa_verse: केन वा कस्य वा हेतोः सयुगः सपरिच्छदः। खुरनेमिक्षतश्चायं सिक्तो रुधिरबिन्दुभिः॥ देशो निवृत्तसंग्रामः सुघोरः पार्थिवात्मज। एकस्य तु विमर्दोऽयं न द्वयोर्वदतां वर॥ नहि वृत्तं हि पश्यामि बलस्य महतः पदम्। नैकस्य तु कृते लोकान् विनाशयितुमर्हसि॥
en: But I do not know whose is this car with yokes and dresses and what for it has been shattered? Behold, O you the son of a King this terrible spot bathed in blood and raked with hoofs and wheels. Surely here took place a conflict. O you the foremost of those skilled in speech, it appear from these signs that this skirmish did take place with one, not with two. Here are not to be seen the foot-marks of a large army. It therefore dose not behove you to destroy the whole world for one's individual offence.

sa_verse: युक्तदण्डा हि मृदवः प्रशान्ताः वसुधाधिपाः। सदा त्वं सर्वभूतानां शरण्यः परमा गतिः॥
en: Kings, gentle and mild by nature, do always administer punishment, proportionate to the amount of offence. You are always the stay and the best refuge of all animals.

sa_verse: को नु दारप्रणाशं ते साधु मन्येत राघव। सरितः सागराः शैला देवगन्धर्वदानवाः॥ नालं ते विप्रियं कर्तुं दीक्षितस्येव साधवः।
en: Who shall think well, O Raghava, of the destruction of your wife? The celestials, Danavas, Gandharvas, rivers, seas, and mountains-none can act unfriendly by you as the learned priests cannot act improperly towards those initiated by them.

sa_verse: येन राजन् हृता सीता तमन्वेषितुमर्हसि ॥ मद्वितीयो धनुष्पाणिः सहायैः परमर्षिभिः।
en: It is your duty, O king with bow in hand to search out the person who has carried away Sītā, along with me and the devotees.

sa_verse: समुद्रं वा विचेष्यामः पर्वतांश्च वनानि च॥ गुहाश्च विविधा घोराः पद्मिन्यो विविधास्तथा। देवगन्धर्वलोकांश्च विचेष्यामः समाहिताः॥ यावनाधिगमिष्यामस्तव भार्यापहारिणम्। न चेत् साम्ना प्रदास्यन्ति पत्नी ते त्रिदशेश्वराः। कोशलेन्द्र ततः पश्चात् प्राप्तकालं करिष्यसि ॥
en: Explore shall we, with great care, the seas, the forest, the mountains, the fearful caves, the pools and the abodes of the celestials and Gandharvas until we find out the person who has carried away your wife. If the celestials do not return you peacefully your wife, O Lord of Kosala, you shall adopt imeasures, befitting the occasion.

sa_verse: शीलेन साम्ना विनयेन सीतां नयेन न प्राप्स्यसि चेन्नरेन्द्र। महेन्द्रवज्रप्रतिमैः शरौघैः॥
en: You shall then uproot the whole world, O lord of men, with your gold-feathered arrows resembling the thunderbolt of Mahendra, if you do not come by your wife by resorting to good conduct, self-control, lowliness and polity.

sa_verse: तं तथा शोकसंतप्तं विलपन्तमनाथवत्। मोहेन महता युक्तं परिधूनमचेतसम्॥ ततः सौमित्रिराश्वस्य मुहूर्तादिव लक्ष्मणः। रामं सम्बोधयामास चरणौ चाभिपीडयन्॥
en: Rāma bewailing thus like one helpless being stricken with grief, overwhelmed with sorrow and losing control over himself, Lakşmaņa touched his feet and consoling him instantly began.

sa_verse: महता तपसा चापि महता चापि कर्मणा। राज्ञा दशरथेनासील्लब्धोऽमृतमिवामरैः॥
en: By constant asceticism and manifold pious observances king Dasaratha obtained you like to the celestials obtaining ambrosia.

sa_verse: तव चैव गुणैर्बद्धस्त्ववियोगान्महीपतिः। राजा देवत्वमापन्नो भरतस्य यथा श्रुतम्॥
en: As I have heard from Bharata, king Dasaratha died for your separation, attached as he was to you for your accomplishments.

sa_verse: यदि दुःखमिदं प्राप्तं काकुत्स्थ न सहिष्यसे। प्राकृतश्चाल्पसत्वश्च इतरः कः सहिष्यति॥
en: O Kākutstha, if do you not bear patiently this impending peril what little-minded person else shall bear it?

sa_verse: आश्वसिही नरश्रेष्ठ प्राणिनः कस्य नापदः। संस्पृशन्त्यग्निवद् राजन् क्षणेन व्यपयान्ति च॥
en: Compose yourself, O you best of men. Peril overtake everybody like to fire but vanish in no time.

sa_verse: लोकस्वभाव एवैष ययातिर्नहुषात्मजः। गतः शक्रेण सालोक्यमनयस्तं समस्पृशत्॥
en: This is the nature of men. Yayati, the son of king Nahusa, though attained to the state of celestials, was however thrown down for an iniquitous deed.

sa_verse: महर्षिर्यो वसिष्ठस्तु यः पितुर्नः पुरोहितः। अह्ना पुत्रशतं जज्ञे तथैवास्य पुनर्हतम्॥
en: The hundred sons, that had been born to our ancestral priest Vasiştha, were all killed in one day.

sa_verse: या चेयं जगतो माता सर्वलोकनमस्कृता। अस्याश्च चलनं भूमेर्दृश्यते कोशलेश्वर॥
en: O lord of Kosala, even Vasumati, the mother of the world, adored of all beings, meet with misery consequent upon earth-quake.

sa_verse: यौ धर्मों जगतो नेत्रौ यत्र सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम्। आदित्यचन्द्रौ ग्रहणमभ्युपेतौ महाबलौ॥
en: Even the mighty Sun and Moon witness eclipse who are the eyes of the world and the very images of virtue and in whom the whole world is stationed.

sa_verse: सुमहान्त्यपि भूतानि देवाश्च पुरुषर्षभ । न दैवस्य प्रमुञ्चन्ति सर्वभूतानि देहिनः॥
en: O you best of men, what of insignificant beings cased in this frail body, even the mighty creatures and celestials are subject to the influence of destiny.

sa_verse: शक्रादिष्वपि देवेषु वर्तमानौ नयानयौ। श्रूयेते नरशार्दूल न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि॥
en: I have heard, O best of men, even the celestials headed by Indra are subject to happiness or misery. So it dose not behove you to bewail thus.

sa_verse: मृतायामपि वैदेह्यां नष्टायामपि राघव। शोचितुं नार्हसे वीर यथान्यः प्राकृतस्तथा॥
en: O descendant of Raghu, it become you not to lament like an ordinary person even if Janakí is dead or has been carried away.

sa_verse: त्वद्विधा नहि शोचन्ति सततं सर्वदर्शनाः। सुमहत्स्वपि कृच्छ्रेषु रामानिर्विण्णदर्शनाः॥
en: O Rāma, persons, highly experienced and ascertaining right or wrong without being moved, do not lament even in the face of mighty perils.

sa_verse: तत्त्वतो हि नरश्रेष्ठ बुद्ध्या समनुचिन्तय बुद्ध्या युक्ता महाप्राज्ञा विजानन्ति शुभाशुभे॥
en: O you best of men, do you, after due consideration, ascertain what is proper or improper; persons of your vast wisdom are cognizant of the right or wrong by dint of their understanding.

sa_verse: अदृष्टगुणदोषाणामधुराणां तु कर्मणाम्। नान्तरेण क्रियां तेषां फलमिष्टं च वर्तते॥
en: Without proper exercise, actions, of unknown merit and uncertain issue do not bear fruits.

sa_verse: मामेवं हि पुरा वीर त्वमेव बहुशोक्तवान्। अनुशिष्याद्धि को नु त्वामपि साक्षाद् बृहस्पतिः॥
en: O hero, many a time and oft ere this, you had given me the self-same counsel. Who is capable of counselling you who are the very preceptor of the gods?

sa_verse: बुद्धिश्च ते महाप्राज्ञ देवैरपि दुरन्वया। शोकेनाभिप्रसुप्तं ते ज्ञानं सम्बोधयाम्यहम्॥
en: O you of great intellect, even the celestials cannot measure your mental acumen. Greatly benumbed is your wisdom with the slumber of grief, and I am to rouse it.

sa_verse: दिव्यं च मानुषं चैवमात्मनश्च पराक्रमम्। इक्ष्वाकुवृषभावेक्ष्य यतस्व द्विषतां वधे॥
en: O you the best of lkşvākus, do you engage in the destruction of your foes considering well your celestial and human prowess.

sa_verse: किं ते सर्वविनाशेन कृतेन पुरुषर्षभ। तमेव तु रिपुं पापं विज्ञायोद्धर्तुमर्हसि ॥
en: O you best of men, what necessity have you to destroy the whole world? Do you rescue Sītā after finding out your vicious enemy.

sa_verse: पूर्वजोऽप्युक्तमात्रस्तु लक्ष्मणेन सुभाषितम्। सारग्राही महासारं प्रतिजग्राह राघवः॥
en: After Laksmana had spoken these highly sound and pleasant words, Rāma, ever taking to what is sound, accepted them.

sa_verse: स निगृह्य महाबाहुः प्रवृद्धं रोषमात्मनः। अवष्टभ्य धनुश्चित्रं रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereupon that one, of mighty arms, slaking his flaming ire and reclining himself upon his beautiful bow, addressed Laksmana.

sa_verse: किं करिष्यावहे वत्स क्व वा गच्छाव लक्ष्मण। केनोपायेन पश्यावः सीतामिह विचिन्तय॥
en: Do you ponder over, O brother, where shall we repair, what shall we do and by what means shall we come by Sītā?

sa_verse: तं तथा परितापार्तं लक्ष्मणो वाक्यमब्रवीत्। इदमेव जनस्थानं त्वमन्वेषितुमर्हसि॥ राक्षसैर्बहुभिः कीर्णं नानाद्रुमलतायुतम्। सन्तीह गिरिदुर्गाणि निर्दराः कन्दराणि च ॥
en: Whereto Lakşmaņa replied saying to the highly aggrieved Råma, It is proper for you to search this Janasthāna filled with a multitude of Rākṣasas and covered with diverse trees and creepers. Here are many strongholds in the midst of mountains, clefts of rocks, many caves and numerous cavities filled with various animals.

sa_verse: गुहाश्च विविधा घोरा नानामृगगणाकुलाः। आवासाः किंनराणां च गन्धर्वभवनानि च॥ तानि युक्तो मया सार्धं समन्वेषितुमर्हसि। त्वद्विधा बुद्धिसम्पन्ना महात्मानो नरर्षभाः॥ आपत्सु न प्रकम्पन्ते वायुवेगैरिवाचलाः।
en: Many are the abodes here belonging to the Kinnaras and Gandharvas. Do you, along with me, search all these places. Great men, of your calibre, do remain unagitated even in the midst of difficulties like to mountains never shaken by the velocity of the wind.

sa_verse: इत्युक्तस्तद् वनं सर्वं विचचार सलक्ष्मणः॥ क्रुद्धो रामः शरं घोरं संधाय धनुषि क्षुरम्।
en: Hearing these words, Rāma, enraged, fixing sharp and terrible arrows to his bow, began to range the forest with Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: ततः पर्वतकूटाभं महाभागं द्विजोत्तमम्॥ ददर्श पतितं भूमौ क्षतजाद्रं जटायुषम्। तं दृष्ट्वा गिरिशृङ्गाभं रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्॥
en: Thereupon he beheld, fallen on ground, having his person bathed in blood, the king of birds-Jatayu, resembling a mountain peak, and spoke to Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: अनेन सीता वैदेही भक्षति नात्र संशयः। गृध्ररूपमिदं व्यक्तं रक्षो भ्रमति काननम्॥
en: It is clear and beyond all doubt that Vaidehī has been devoured by this Rākşasa, assuming the shape of a vulture and ranging the forest.

sa_verse: भक्षयित्वा विशालाक्षीमास्ते सीतां यथासुखम्। एनं वधिष्ये दीप्ताग्रैः शरघोरैरजिह्मगैः॥
en: This Rākşasa has been reposing at ease after devouring that one of expansive eyes; I shall kill him with terrible straight coursing arrows, having flaming points.

sa_verse: इत्युक्त्वाभ्यपतद् द्रष्टुं संधाय धनुषि क्षुरम्। क्रुद्धो रामः समुद्रान्तां चालयन्निव मेदिनीम्॥
en: Fixing sharpened shafts to his bow, Rāma, enraged, darted towards the vulture, as if moving the sea-girt earth.

sa_verse: तं दीनदीनया वाचा सफेनं रुधिरं वमन्। अभ्यभाषत पक्षी स रामं दशरथात्मजम्॥
en: Vomiting frothy blood Jațāyu, the king of vultures, spoke to Rāma, the son of Dasaratha.

sa_verse: यामोषधीमिवायुष्मन्नन्वेषसि महावने। सा देवी मम च प्राणा रावणेनोभयं हृतम्॥
en: O you of long life, that goddess, whom you have been searching in this vast forest like to Os adhi, and my life have been carried away by Rāvana.

sa_verse: त्वया विरहिता देवी लक्ष्मणेन च राघव। ह्रियमाणा मया दृष्टा रावणेन बलीयसा॥
en: I saw her, O Rāghava, carried away stealthily by the powerful Rāvaņa, in your absence as well as that of Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: सीतामभ्यवपन्नोऽहं रावणश्च रणे प्रभो। विध्वंसितरथच्छत्रः पतितो धरणीतले॥
en: Myself nearing Sītā, for her rescue, O Lord, Rāvaņa was thrown down on earth by me in conflict having his car and umbrella shattered.

sa_verse: एतदस्य धनुर्भग्नमेते चास्य शरास्तथा। अयमस्य रणे राम भग्नः सांग्रामिको रथः॥
en: This is his snapped bow and these are his broken shafts. And this is his war-car, O Rāma shattered in fight.

sa_verse: अयं तु सारथिस्तस्य मत्पक्षनिहतो भुवि। परिश्रान्तस्य मे पक्षौ छित्वा खङ्गेन रावणः॥ सीतामादाय वैदेहीमुत्पपात विहायसम्। रक्षसा निहतं पूर्वं मां न हन्तुं त्वमर्हसि ॥
en: This is his charioteer lying on earth being killed by the velocity of my wings. Having sundered my wings with his dagger, who had been exhausted, Rāvana taking Sītā, rose high up in the welkin. It behove you not to kill me who had been wounded before by the Rākşasa.

sa_verse: रामस्तस्य तु विज्ञाय सीतासक्तां प्रियां कथाम्। गृध्रराजं परिष्वज्य परित्यज्य महद् धनुः॥ निपपातावशो भूमौ रुरोद सहलक्ष्मणः। द्विगुणीकृततापा” रामो धीरतरोऽपि सन्॥
en: Hearing from him pleasant words relating to Sītā, Rāma, leaving aside, instantly, his mighty bow, embraced him, and rolling on earth having lost self-control through grief, began to lament with Lakşmaņa. Though highly composed by nature, he was overwhelmed with doubled grief.

sa_verse: एकमेकायने कृच्छ्रे नि:श्वसन्तं मुहुर्मुहुः। समीक्ष्य दुःखितो रामः सौमित्रिमिदमब्रवीत्॥
en: And beholding Jațāyu, sigh again and again and breathing with difficulty in a helpless plight, Rāma, highly aggrieved, spoke to Lakşmaņa.

sa_verse: राज्यं भ्रष्टं वने वासः सीता नष्टा मृतो द्विजः। ईदृशीयं ममालक्ष्मीर्दहेदपि हि पावकम्॥
en: I have lost my kingdom and have been living in this forest. My Sītā has been carried away and this bird has been killed (on my account). This misfortune of mine can burn even the very fire.

sa_verse: सम्पूर्णमपि चेदद्य प्रतरेयं महोदधिम्। सोऽपि नूनं ममालक्ष्म्या विशुष्येत् सरितां पतिः॥
en: If for assuaging my grief I do enter the mighty ocean, verily shall that misfortune dry up even that lord of rivers.

sa_verse: नास्त्यभाग्यतरो लोके मत्तोऽस्मिन् सचराचरे। येनेयं महती प्राप्ता मया व्यसनवागुरा॥
en: There is none so unfortunate as I, throughout this earth, mobile or immobile, and it is for this bad luck that I have confronted this mighty disaster.

sa_verse: अयं पितुर्वयस्यो मे गृध्रराजो महाबलः। शेते विनिहतो भूमौ मम भाग्यविपर्ययात्॥
en: This mighty king of vultures is our father's friend and he Leith on earth, killed through the evil turn of my fortune.

sa_verse: इत्येवमुक्त्वा बहुशो राघवः सहलक्ष्मणः। जटायुषं च पस्पर्श पितृस्नेहं निदर्शयन्॥
en: Uttering these and various other words, Rāghava, along with Lakşmaņa touched his body manifesting his paternal affection.

sa_verse: निकृत्तपक्षं रुधिरावसिक्तं तं गृध्रराजं परिगृह्य राघवः। क्व मैथिली प्राणसमा गतेति विमुच्य वाचं निपपात भूमौ ॥
en: Embracing the king of vultures, bathed in blood, having its wings cut off, Rāghava, fell on the ground, exclaiming where has Maithili gone like to my life?

sa_verse: रामः प्रेक्ष्य तु तं गृधं भुवि रौद्रेण पातितम्। सौमित्रिं मित्रसम्पन्नमिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥ ममायं नूनमर्थेषु यतमानो विहंगमः। राक्षसेन हतः संख्ये प्राणांस्त्यजति मत्कृते॥ अतिखिन्नः शरीरेऽस्मिन् प्राणो लक्ष्मण विद्यते। तथा स्वरविहीनोऽयं विक्लवं समुदीक्षते॥
en: Beholding Jațāyu fallen on the ground by the terrible Raksasa, Rāma spoke to Laksmana, having compassion for all, saying Verily for my service this bird has breathed its last, being killed by the Raksasa.O Laksmana, its voice has been enfeebled, its vision weakened and its life, greatly exhausted, Leith in a very little proportion in its body.

sa_verse: जटायो यदि शक्नोषि वाक्यं व्याहरितुं पुनः। सीतामाख्याहि भद्रं ते वधमाख्याहि चात्मनः॥
en: May good betide you, O Jatayu; if you are capable of speaking again, do you relate how Sītā has been carried away and you have been killed.

sa_verse: किंन्निमित्तो जहारार्यां रावणस्तस्य किं मया। अपराधं तु यं दृष्ट्वा रावणेन हृता प्रिया॥
en: Why has Rāvaņa taken away by stealth the worshipful Jānki? What offence did I commit by him that he has carried away my dear one?

sa_verse: कथं तच्चन्द्रसंकाशं मुखमासीन्मनोहरम्। सीतया कानि चोक्तानि तस्मिन्काले द्विजोत्तम॥
en: O you best of birds, how looked the moonlike, pleasant countenance of Sītā at the time of her being carried away? What did she speak then?

sa_verse: कथंवीर्यः कथंरूपः किंकर्मा स च राक्षसः। क्व चास्य भवनं तात ब्रूहि मे परिपृच्छतः॥
en: What is the prowess, appearance and action of that Raksasa? Where dose he live, O reverend Sir? Pray tell me, I do ask you.

sa_verse: तमुवीक्ष्य स धर्मात्मा विलपन्तमनाथवत्। वाचा विक्लवया राममिदं वचनमब्रवीत्॥
en: Beholding Rāma, lament like one helpless, the virtuous-souled Jațăyu spoke in faltering accents.

sa_verse: सा हृता राक्षसेन्द्रेण रावणेन दुरात्मना। मायामास्थाय विपुलां वातदुर्दिनसंकुलाम्॥
en: Sītā has been carried away by Rāvana, the lord of Rākşasas, creating a mighty illusion producing wind and showers.

sa_verse: परिकलान्तस्य मे तात पक्षौ छित्वा निशाचरः। सीतामादाय वैदेही प्रयातो दक्षिणामुखः॥
en: O darling, myself being worn out that nightranger, having sundered my wings, fled away with Sītā to the southerly direction.

sa_verse: उपरुध्यन्ति मे प्राणा दृष्टिभ्रमति राघव। पश्यामि वृक्षान् सौवर्णानुशीरकृतमूर्धजान्॥
en: O Rāghava, my life is about to expire, my eye-sight has grown of mistaken perception, I see trees before me made of gold having hair resembling Usira.

sa_verse: येन याति मुहूर्तेन सीतामादाय रावणः। विप्रणष्टं धनं क्षिप्रं तत्स्वामी प्रतिपद्यते॥ विन्दो नाम मुहूर्तोऽसौ न च काकुत्स्थ सोऽबुधत्। झषवद् बडिशं गृह्य क्षिप्रमेव विनश्यति ॥
en: Rāvana has taken away Sītā at a moment when a person regains soon his lost property. O Kākutstha, this moment is called Vindya, which Rāvana has not been able to perceive. (At this moment) the person who takes away (a thing) is soon destroyed like to a fish devouring a hook. This refers to Jatāyu's astrological knowledge. Vindya is derived from the root Vid-to gain. Thus this moment is favourable to the laser and unfavourable to the taker. Hence Rāvana carrying away Sītā at this moment shall meet with destruction.

sa_verse: न च त्वया व्यथा कार्या जनकस्य सुतां प्रति। वैदेह्या रंस्यसे क्षिप्रं हत्वा तं रणमूर्धनि ॥
en: Do not therefore entertain the least doubt about your coming by Janakī. Destroying him at the head of the battle you shall soon sport with Vaidehi.

sa_verse: असम्मूढस्य गृध्रस्य रामं प्रत्यनुभाषतः। आस्यात् सुस्राव रुधिरं म्रियमाणस्य सामिषम्॥
en: Thereupon flesh and gore began to come out of the mouth of Jatayu, the king of vultures, not loosing his sense even while treading the verge of death.

sa_verse: पुत्रो विश्रवसः साक्षाद् भ्राता वैश्रवणस्य च। इत्युक्त्वा दुर्लभान् प्राणान् मुमोच पतगेश्वरः॥
en: Thereupon the king of birds gave up his dear life uttering only-Rāvana is the son of Visravā and brother to Vaisravana (the lord of wealth).

sa_verse: ब्रूहि ब्रूहीति रामस्य ब्रुवाणस्य कृताञ्जलेः। त्यक्त्वा शरीरं गृध्रस्य प्राणा जग्मुर्विहायसम्॥
en: Rāma again and again addressed him with joined palms saying, Do you speak! Do you speak. And instantly Jatayu's vital spark rose up in the sky, leaving his bodily frame.

sa_verse: स निक्षिप्य शिरो भूमौ प्रसार्य चरणौ तथा। विक्षिप्य च शरीरं स्वं पपात धरणीतले ॥
en: Thereupon the king of vultures fell down on the earth by stretching forth his legs, body and head on the ground.

sa_verse: तं गृधं प्रेक्ष्य ताम्राक्षं गतासुमचलोपमम्। रामः सुबहुभिर्दुःखैर्दीनः सौमित्रिमब्रवीत्॥
en: Beholding the vulture dead, of huge proportions, resembling a hill and having red eyes, Rama, aggrieved, spoke piteously to Saumitri.

sa_verse: बहूनि रक्षसां वासे वर्षाणि वसता सुखम्। अनेन दण्डकारण्ये विशीर्णमिह पक्षिणा॥
en: Living happily, for years, in this forest of Dandaka inhabited by the Rākşasas, Jațāyu has, at last, given up his life.

sa_verse: अनेकवार्षिको यस्तु चिरकालसमुत्थितः। सोऽयमद्य हतः शेते कालो हि दुरतिक्रमः॥
en: He lived for a long time, of an uplifted person, and has now laid low on the earth. None can withstand the course of destiny.

sa_verse: पश्य लक्ष्मण गृध्रोऽयमुपकारी हतश्च मे। सीतामभ्यवपन्नो हि रावणेन बलीयसा॥
en: Observe, O Laksmana, this vulture for my benefaction, has been killed by the powerful Rāvaņa in his attempt to rescue Sītā.

sa_verse: गृध्रराज्यं परित्यज्य पितृपैतामहं महत्। मम हेतोरयं प्राणान् मुमोच पतगेश्वरः॥
en: For me, has this Lord of birds, breathed his last, renouncing his large ancestral kingdom.

sa_verse: सर्वत्र खलु दृश्यन्ते साधवो धर्मचारिणः। शूराः शरण्याः सौमित्रे तिर्यग्योनिगतेष्वपि॥
en: In every status of animal creation, the heroic, the righteous and the honest, affording refuge to all, are to be found, even among the birds.

sa_verse: सीताहरणजं दुःखं न मे सौम्य तथागतम्। यथा विनाशो गृध्रस्य मत्कृते च परंतप ॥
en: I do not feel so much affliction, O hero, for Sītā's ravishment as I do for this vulture, who has been killed for me.

sa_verse: राजा दशरथः श्रीमान् यथा मम महायशाः। पूजनीयश्च मान्यश्च तथायं पतगेश्वरः॥
en: Like to the highly famous, effulgent king Dasaratha, this King of birds is worthy of being adored and worshipped by me.

sa_verse: सौमित्रे हर काष्ठानि निर्मथिष्यामि पावकम्। गृध्रराजं दिधक्ष्यामि मत्कृते निधनं गतम्॥
en: O Saumitri, do you bring fuels; I shall produce fire there-with and burn the dead body of this king of birds who has been killed on my account.

sa_verse: नाथं पतगलोकस्य चितिमारोपयाम्यहम्। इमं धक्ष्यामि सौमित्रे हृतं रौद्रेण रक्षसा॥
en: Placing on a funeral pile, I shall cremate, O Saumitrī, the dead body of this king of birds who has been destroyed by the grim-visaged Raksasas.

sa_verse: या गतिर्यज्ञशीलानामाहिताग्नेश्च या गतिः। अपरावर्तिनां या च या च भूमिप्रदायिनाम्॥ मया त्वं समनुज्ञातो गच्छ लोकाननुत्तमान्। गृध्रराज महासत्व संस्कृतश्च मया व्रज॥
en: Being consecrated and commanded by me, do you, O highly powerful king of birds, attain to that excellent state of existence, which is reached by persons ever performing pious observance, by Āhilāgnis by heroes who are not afraid of entering a battle-field and by persons who confer grants of land.

sa_verse: एवमुक्त्वा चितां दीप्तामारोप्य पतगेश्वरम्। ददाह रामो धर्मात्मा स्वबन्धुमिव दुःखितः॥
en: Saying this the virtuous-souled Rāma, afflicted with sorrow, burned the body of the king of birds, placing it on the funeral pile, like to his own kinsman.

sa_verse: रामोऽपि सहसौमित्रिर्वनं गत्वा स वीर्यवान्। स्थूलान् हत्वा महारोहीननुतस्तार तं द्विजम् ॥ रोहिमांसानि चोद्धृत्य पेशीकृत्वा महायशाः। शकुनाय ददौ रामो रम्ये हरितशाद्वले॥
en: Entering the forest with Saumitrī, Rāma gifted with prowess, killed plump high deer and stretched forth grass and twigs for offering oblation to that bird. Taking off the flesh of those high deer and clustering it, Rāma, of great renown, offered it to the vultures in that pleasant forest-land, abounding in green grass.

sa_verse: यत् तत् प्रेतस्य मर्त्यस्य कथयन्ति द्विजातयः। तत् स्वर्गगमनं क्षिप्रं तस्य रामो जजाप ह॥
en: Thereupon for his speedy arrival at the abode of celestials, Rāma recited those Mantras which are being uttered by the twice-born ones.

sa_verse: ततो गोदावरी गत्वा नदी नरवरात्मजौ। उदकं चक्रतुस्तस्मै गृध्रराजाय तावुभौ ॥
en: Afterwards repairing to the river Godāverī the two princes offered water to that kingly vulture.

sa_verse: शास्त्रदृष्टेन विधिना जनं गृध्राय राघवौ। स्नात्वा तौ गृध्रराजाय उदकं चक्रतुस्तदा ॥
en: And offering yater to him according to the prescribed rites of the Śástras, those two descendants of Raghu, after bathing, perforined the Udaka ceremony for that king of vultures. Presentation of water specially to the manes as a religious or obsequal rite.

sa_verse: स गृध्रराजः कृतवान् यशस्करं सुदुष्करं कर्म रणे निपातितः। महर्षिकल्पेन च संस्कृतस्तदा जगाम पुण्यां गतिमात्मनः शुभाम्॥
en: Having been killed in battle for an arduous but glorious work, that king of vultures, consecrated by the ascetic-like Rāma, attained to an excellent state.

sa_verse: कृतोदकौ तावपि पक्षिसत्तम स्थिरां च बुद्धिं प्रणिधाय जग्मतुः। प्रवेश्य सीताधिगमे ततो मनो वनं सुरेन्द्राविव विष्णुवासवौ॥
en: Having performed the Udaka ceremony for that best of birds and considering him in the light of a father they went away and entered the forest in quest of Sītā like to the two best of celestials-Visnu and Vasava. glorious work, that king of vultures, consecrated by the ascetic-like Rāma, attained to an excellent state.
